# Execution Control Plane Implementation Journal

## Current Slice

Slice 06: Gates And Verification Graph.

## Active Local Remediation

- 2026-05-21T01:59:00Z: Started focused Slice 06 remediation for dashboard
  legacy-schema fallback/material ETag, Slack durable digest pending suppression
  and failed retry semantics, and store-owned material
  `control_plane.snapshot_changed` outbox versioning. Ownership is limited to
  `dashboard.py`, `src/iriai_build_v2/supervisor/slack.py`,
  `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/store.py`, optional `schema.sql`, and
  focused owned tests.
- 2026-05-21T02:05:00Z: Completed focused remediation. Dashboard `/api/feature`
  now keeps the legacy ETag probe off Slice 06 relations and incorporates the
  store-owned material control-plane snapshot version in the feature ETag.
  Slack durable digest state now suppresses same-key pending resend after
  restart and only re-arms failed rows. Store snapshot outbox events now use
  `fetch_control_plane_snapshot` material versions and sandbox lease status
  updates emit changed material snapshot events without inserting a new
  execution journal row. Focused owned suite passed (`141 passed, 4 warnings`).

## Unresolved Risks

- The repository starts with many pre-existing unstaged changes, including the
  execution-control-plane docs and workflow/supervisor/runtime files. Work must
  preserve those changes and avoid broad rewrites.
- Slice 00 must not mutate active feature `8ac124d6`; it may only add checked-in
  evidence fixtures, compatibility indexes, and deterministic test helpers.
- The complete control plane is a large atomic landing. Internal slices may be
  built independently, but no production-authoritative partial path may be
  introduced.
- Slice 00 accepted with no open P1/P2 findings. Popper re-review reported no
  P1/P2/P3. Helmholtz final re-review reported the remaining queue
  compatibility-index P2 resolved with no new P1/P2 findings.
- Slice 01 accepted with no open P1/P2 findings. Kant final re-review reported
  no P1/P2/P3, and Boole final re-review reported no findings.
- Slice 02 has no known local test failures after reviewer-finding patches, but
  acceptance is blocked until the targeted reviewer redispatch reports no open
  P1/P2 findings.
- Slice 02 accepted with no open P1/P2 findings. Nonblocking P3 follow-ups:
  snapshot digest uniqueness with nullable `group_idx` in `schema.sql`, and
  dict/map fan-out caps for workspace projection bounded serializers.
- Slice 03 accepted with no open P1/P2 findings. Nonblocking P3 follow-ups:
  latest-artifact dashboard row-count caps, typed-store adapter injection
  coverage, and dict-heavy projection fan-out bounding in store/workflow
  projection helpers.
- Volta targeted re-review reopened one Slice 03 P2: repairable
  WorkspaceAuthority blockers still fall through as advisory legacy-guard
  input instead of deterministically blocking dispatch for typed workflow
  remediation. Patched and redispatched to Volta at 2026-05-20T09:28:09Z
  (`submission:019e44b6-b98f-7870-a150-899e8f2268b3`). Volta narrow
  re-review reported no P1/P2/P3 at 2026-05-20T09:40:26Z.
- Maxwell targeted re-review reopened Slice 03 blockers at
  2026-05-20T09:28:46Z: P1 claimed result paths can be approved when no actual
  dirty git patch evidence exists, and P2 default patch-summary idempotency
  omits contract/task/stage identity for direct store callers. Patched and
  redispatched to Maxwell at 2026-05-20T09:39:49Z
  (`submission:019e44c1-62c7-7f91-b50d-23513e06da25`). Maxwell confirmed
  those two findings resolved, but reopened one P2 at 2026-05-20T09:42:14Z:
  partial missing patch evidence can still leave an approved contract verdict
  for the evidenced subset. Patched and redispatched to Maxwell at
  2026-05-20T09:46:08Z
  (`submission:019e44c7-2f2f-7100-a6d7-9686a4083402`). Maxwell narrow
  re-review reported no findings at 2026-05-20T09:47:51Z.
- Hypatia targeted re-review reported no open P1/P2 at 2026-05-20T09:28:46Z.
  Remaining P3: dict-heavy projection fan-out remains unbounded in store and
  workflow-side projection helpers.
- Slice 04 accepted with no open P1/P2 findings at 2026-05-20T16:24:23Z.
  Remaining P3 follow-ups are nonblocking: sandbox filesystem path omits mode,
  Codex auth bind still points at an outside symlink after proof, and legacy
  Slack fallback bound remains.
- Slice 05 accepted with no open P1/P2 findings at 2026-05-20T19:20:40Z.
  Nonblocking P3 follow-ups: placeholder existing digest in start-conflict
  diagnostics, bridge terminal start-conflict end-to-end coverage, enhancement
  direct-bypass coverage, and stale post-DAG gate phase coverage.
- Slice 06 implementation is in progress. Verification and gates/context
  worker helpers have landed with worker-local tests green. Remaining active
  risks are integrating those helpers with persistent graph rows, preserving
  expanded verify compatibility, and blocking raw-only or summary-only approval
  before merge/checkpoint.
- Slice 06 persistence worker Raman was closed after failing to respond to
  progress checkpoints. This is classified as a worker execution blocker, not
  an architecture blocker; dispatch a replacement persistence worker and keep
  local integration work scoped to non-overlapping `implementation.py`/workflow
  tests.
- Replacement persistence worker Feynman also failed to return a progress
  checkpoint and produced no visible persistence changes. Local persistence
  remediation is active; it must be independently reviewed after tests because
  it collapses the intended worker/local split for this narrow blocker.
- Current Slice 06 re-review has open P1/P2 findings. Active blockers are:
  content-addressed post-DAG gate freshness, mixed stale/fresh gate proofs
  after test-authoring commits, legacy post-test compatibility, source-push
  canonical/no-op proof, report/notify crash idempotency, commit provenance
  proof, verifier runtime failure routing, malformed graph projection
  validation, patch snapshot citation in the normal precommit path, Codex cwd
  authorization, and supervisor Slack boundary/dedupe safety. Do not accept
  Slice 06 until these are patched, tested, and independently re-reviewed.

## Restart Instructions

1. Read this file and
   `docs/execution-control-plane/implementation-decisions.jsonl`.
2. Run `git status --short --branch`.
3. Inspect the latest targeted test output if any is recorded below.
4. Continue from **Next Safe Action** rather than restarting from Slice 00
   unless this journal explicitly says no implementation work has begun.

## Next Safe Action

Patch the collected Slice 06 P1/P2 reviewer findings, run focused tests plus
the combined Slice 06 bundle and hygiene, then redispatch all six reviewer
vectors. Do not accept Slice 06 or proceed to Slice 07 until reviewers report
no open P1/P2 findings.

- 2026-05-20T09:57:41Z: Gibbs completed Slice 04 patch-capture inventory

  for sandbox capture because it does not use a temp index, does not persist
  binary diff bytes, misses untracked content and sandbox-created commits, and
  parses porcelain v1 text. Recommended `sandbox.py` own capture APIs using a
  temporary `GIT_INDEX_FILE`, NUL-safe status/diff classification, contract
  validation integration, and `test_sandbox.py` coverage for create/modify/
  delete/rename/binary/mode-only/untracked/empty/outside-contract/symlink/
  recovery cases.

  patterns but pin/verify recorded base commits; do not reuse legacy

## Slice 00 Brief

Objective: add only checked-in evidence fixtures, compatibility consumer index,
and deterministic replay/test helpers needed by later slices. Do not change
runtime execution behavior, do not write production artifacts/events, and do not
touch the active `8ac124d6` feature state.

Acceptance targets:

- Represent observed `8ac124d6` failure classes in fixtures.
- Index existing artifact/event compatibility consumers.
- Make fixture replay deterministic and bounded.
- Independent reviewers confirm no production behavior changed.

Acceptance: complete as of 2026-05-20T05:53:33Z.

## Slice 01 Brief

Objective: implement the typed execution journal and synchronous compatibility
projections without changing product authority. Add `ExecutionControlStore`,
typed execution rows, idempotency keys, projection links, and parity tests for
legacy `dag-*` projections while legacy readers depend on artifacts.

Acceptance targets:

- Projection parity is proven for `dag-task:*`, `dag-verify:*`,
  `dag-commit-failure:*`, `dag-group:*`, and relevant regroup projections.
- Typed success cannot exist without required compatibility visibility while
  legacy readers depend on artifacts.
- Legacy artifact-only features remain resumable through the legacy path.
- Crash/retry of projection writes is idempotent.

Acceptance: complete as of 2026-05-20T06:36:37Z.

## Slice 02 Brief

Objective: implement canonical repo registry, worktree alias detection, ACL
normalization, workspace snapshots, and writeability proof. Implementers must
not start when workspace ambiguity is unresolved, and resolvable workspace
classes must be deterministic rather than operator escalations.

Acceptance targets:

- `*-wt` aliases, divergent repos, missing parents, symlinks, outside-root
  paths, dirty generated outputs, and ACL gaps are handled deterministically.
- Resolvable workspace classes do not require operator escalation.
- Implementers cannot start with unresolved workspace ambiguity.

Acceptance: complete as of 2026-05-20T07:50:12Z.

## Slice 03 Brief

Objective: compile approved DAG task intent into stable task deliverable
contracts that become the shared authority for required, allowed, read-only,
forbidden, generated paths, acceptance criteria, gate requirements, execution
policy, patch validation, and later sandbox/repair/merge queue use.

Acceptance targets:

- Ambiguous or empty write sets fail closed or become conservative
  `unknown_write_set=True` contracts with per-task sandbox and single-task merge
  policy.
- Forbidden edits, read-only edits, outside-allowed writes, digest mismatches,
  and missing required/generated evidence are rejected before merge.
- Required evidence prevents approval/checkpoint until linked through contract
  verdict evidence.
- Verifier, repair, sandbox, and merge queue consume the same contract identity
  and digest.
- Compatibility projections remain bounded summaries; legacy `dag-task:*`
  artifacts stay attempt evidence and do not become deliverable authority.

Initial implementation split:

- Inventory current DAG task models, preflight forbidden-path checks, manifest
  helpers, and compatibility artifact consumers before editing.
- Implement the pure compiler/validator in
  `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/task_contracts.py` with
  focused tests in `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_task_contracts.py`.
- Add typed persistence and bounded compatibility projections in
  `schema.sql`, `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/models.py`, and
  `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/store.py`.
- Wire only contract compile/admission projections into the current DAG path for
  this construction branch, preserving the legacy execution path until the full
  control-plane bundle is landed.

Acceptance: complete as of 2026-05-20T09:49:30Z.

## Slice 04 Brief

Objective: implement the sandbox runner boundary so implementer and repair
attempts operate in isolated, durable sandboxes, while canonical repos remain
untouched until the durable merge queue consumes captured patch evidence.

Acceptance targets:

- Claude, Codex, and Claude pool adapters receive sandbox paths and binding
  metadata, not canonical or alias repo paths.
- Agents cannot write directly to canonical repos once the sandbox path is
  active; canonical/alias roots are blocked roots and never writable roots.
- Patch capture is deterministic, binary-safe, contract-linked, and persisted
  through Slice 03 patch summary evidence.
- Allocation, runtime binding, patch capture, release, retention, and recovery
  are idempotent over typed state first and filesystem state second.
- Sandbox cleanup/retention is restart-safe and never deletes paths not covered
  by the manifest and lease row.
- Sandbox execution remains construction-only until the atomic landing with
  dispatcher, gates, failure router, and merge queue; no production partial
  authority or shadow rollout is introduced.

Initial implementation split:

- Inventory existing workspace clone/copy helpers, runtime cwd/manifest
  bindings, and current commit/patch evidence paths before editing.
- Implement `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/sandbox.py` with
  pure lifecycle, binding, and patch-capture types plus focused unit tests.
- Extend execution-control persistence for sandbox leases, repo bindings,
  runtime bindings, and bounded `dag-sandbox:*` compatibility projections.
- Wire only construction-branch implementation/repair dispatch to allocate and
  bind sandboxes after workspace authority and task contracts; keep canonical
  mutation prohibited and retain legacy product authority until complete
  bundle landing.

- 2026-05-20T16:26:55Z: Created the Slice 05 execution brief after rereading

  request/outcome/state models and narrow dependency ports, extend
  `ExecutionControlStore` with dispatcher attempt/evidence/failure wrappers,
  add runtime-client conversion tests so provider errors become typed
  responses, and adapt the primary/enhancement task loops to consume
  dispatcher outcomes while preserving existing compatibility projections.
  Boundary constraints: dispatcher may invoke runtimes, bind sandboxes, capture
  patch evidence, normalize structured output, record typed runtime failures,
  and project successful `dag-task:*`; it must not import or call commit,
  checkpoint, merge-queue, failure-router, route-executor, or repair APIs.
  Next safe action is to inspect current Slice 04 integration seams, dispatch
  disjoint implementation workers, and keep the direct monolith integration
  work local.

- 2026-05-20T18:52:30Z: Patched the Newton/Leibniz P2s. Implementation

  gate. Decision: patch `_source_push_authorized_push_target` to require push
  targets to match `actual_origin` when no registry `expected_origin` exists,
  add a legacy sibling-pushurl regression, rerun focused/affected tests, and
  redispatch reviewers after all current findings are resolved.
- 2026-05-21T05:40:00Z: Locke the 3rd persistence reviewer completed with no
  P1 findings and two P2 blockers. P2: proofless rejected verification graph
  reload validates the stored payload digest only when a proof dict exists, so
  proofless artifact-loss recovery can republish stale/corrupt graph payloads.
  P2: approved required-edge validation compares `proof.required_edge_ids` to a
  helper that returns the same proof list when a proof exists, making the edge
  check tautological and allowing required `requires` edges to be omitted from
  persisted required-edge rows. Decision: validate graph payload digests for
  proofless full graph bodies and derive payload required-edge ids from graph
  edges rather than proof input.
- 2026-05-21T05:41:00Z: Lagrange the 3rd failure-routing reviewer completed
  with no P1 findings, two P2 blockers, and one P3 wording issue. P2:
  operator-required runtime events can still be classified as pipeline bugs
  because `_pipeline_bug_suspected` runs before `_operator_required`. P2:
  graph-proof blockers returned from `_require_dag_verification_graph_approval`
  do not emit a `workflow-blocker:`/`dag-runtime-failure:` artifact and
  `dag-verify-graph:*` artifacts are not included in supervisor evidence, so a
  later supervisor pass may miss the deterministic blocker. Decision: make
  pipeline-bug runtime classification exclude explicit operator-required
  runtime evidence and record a workflow blocker artifact when graph approval
  blocks checkpoint. The P3 wording issue will be folded into the same patch.

- 2026-05-21T05:54:00Z: Noether the 3rd workspace/mutation reviewer returned a
  P1 and two P2 blockers. P1: `_push_clones_to_source_root` can push one repo
  before discovering a failure in a later repo, leaving canonical source mutated

  feature root without a mirror, launching a write-capable implementer on the
  product worktree. P2: bugfix initial promotion source-push passes
  `allowed_origin_root` but not optional no-op repos, unlike recovery path,
  which can reject unchanged repos after applied-main work starts. Decision:
  split source-push into full preflight before any push, route initial bugfix
  promotion through `_push_applied_main_lane_to_source_root`, fail closed on
  artifact repair without a mirror/product-safe workspace, and rerun focused
  source-push, bugfix queue, artifact repair, and classifier shards.
- 2026-05-21T05:55:00Z: Nash the 3rd failure-routing reviewer returned a P1,

  without persisting typed workflow-blocker/preflight evidence. P2: dirty
  workspace snapshots emit `workspace_dirty`/`quiesce` but classifier
  deterministic sets do not recognize them. P2: operator-required runtime
  precedence is per-event only, so a separate pipeline runtime event in the same
  group can outrank operator-required evidence. P3: route/class/type operator
  hints are ignored if the boolean is absent. Decision: add `dag-verify-graph:`
  to bounded supervisor evidence and clearing logic, persist typed workspace

  operator-required classification globally precede pipeline classification, and
  add focused routing regressions. P3 will be folded if low-risk.

  recovery returns after store-backed leases and skips manifest-only leases from

  reported no P1 and one P2 blocker: dispatch evidence writers and terminal

  `_update_dispatch_attempt_row` writes status/payload without checking the row

- 2026-05-21T11:54:42Z: Zeno the 4th compatibility/resume/business-logic
  review reported no P1 and one P2 blocker: `optional_noop_repos` are only
  handled when the remote already equals the local HEAD; if a read-only/source
  repo has a different local HEAD, source push falls into `mutation_plans` and
  pushes back to canonical source, violating the read-only/no-writable
  contract. Zeno also repeated the non-blocking P3 about invalid legacy markers
  masking fallback markers. James the 4th workspace/mutation-safety review then
  reported no P1 and one P2 blocker: `_commit_repos_in_root` still skips
  authorized nested repos whose parent is not exactly `repos_root`, so a dirty
  authorized nested repo can remain uncommitted and fail no-dirty proof before
  checkpoint. Current open P2s: Socrates retained-sandbox classifier status;
  Kant monotonic dispatch attempt update guard; Zeno optional-noop source-push
  fail-closed behavior; James nested repo commit before checkpoint proof.
  Restart instructions: patch these P2s, rerun focused and affected tests, then
  redispatch reviewers. Next safe action: patch current P2 batch.
- 2026-05-21T12:00:41Z: Patched the current-diff four-P2 batch. Changes:
  supervisor classifier treats `retained` sandbox leases as terminal rather
  than active; dispatch attempt row updates are conditional on
  `status = 'started'` and fail closed if a late evidence write races a
  terminal outcome; optional-noop/read-only repos fail closed when remote HEAD
  is behind local HEAD instead of being pushed; checkpoint commits include
  standalone nested repos under feature `repos/` while still skipping embedded
  repos inside another repo. Focused regressions added for retained sandbox
  stale-Codex classification, late stale-row dispatch update rejection,
  read-only optional-noop source-push failure, and authorized nested repo
  commit. Also tightened fake active merge-queue status filtering to match the
  production payload-status WHERE shape. Unresolved risks: tests have not yet
  been rerun after this patch. Restart instructions: run focused py_compile and
  P2 regression tests next. Next safe action: run focused remediation tests.

- 2026-05-21T13:46:45Z: Active P1/P2 remediation batch patched. Changes:

- 2026-05-21T13:59:03Z: Laplace compatibility/resume review returned one P2
  blocker and no P1/P3. P2: when Slack delivery fails and `record_failed()`
  also fails, `_record_digest_failed()` keeps `_pending_digest_delivery`, but
  the next `_digest_packet_to_send_durable()` clears it before checking the
  store and then suppresses the still-pending row as a duplicate. This can
  strand the digest as pending/suppressed instead of retrying delivery or
  failure recording. Restart instructions: patch durable digest send to reuse a
  retained pending delivery for the same packet/dedupe key, extend the
  regression to exercise the next send pass, run focused Slack tests, rerun
  affected bundle, and redispatch reviewers. Next safe action: patch Laplace
  P2.

  now accept and forward sandbox/binding kwargs. `_commit_repos_in_root` now

  repos before any `git add` or `git commit`, preventing partial canonical
  commits when a later read-only/noop repo is dirty. Added regressions for

- 2026-05-21T17:56:20Z: Collected the remaining Slice 07 reviewer results and

## Slice 08 EXECUTION BRIEF (2026-05-21)

Objective: durable merge queue as the only canonical product-mutation path.
Current state: NO merge_queue.py, NO git_service.py, NO merge_queue_items /
merge_queue_task_coverage / merge_queue_repo_targets tables in schema.sql.
Doc 08 references "Slice 1 tables" but Slice 01 did not create them — they are
built in this slice.

Construction order (disjoint where safe):
  08a. Schema: 3 tables + constraints + indexes (foundational, local).
  08b. git_service.py: extract git status/apply/commit/no-dirty from
       implementation.py (subagent-eligible, read-only extraction).
  08c. journal.py / store: queue persistence — enqueue, claim, heartbeat,
       recover_expired, lease-fenced transition, queue evidence, checkpoint
       projection (critical path, local).
  08d. merge_queue.py: state machine, feature advisory lock, apply/rebase,
       post-apply gates, commit proof, GroupMergeCoordinator checkpoint txn
       (critical path, local).
  08e. Integration: replace implementation.py:5159 commit with patch capture +
       enqueue; replace checkpoint at 4166/4203 with queue success; route queue
       failures through failure_router (critical path, local).
  08f. Tests: schema/idempotency, lease/concurrency, apply/rebase, commit/clean
       proof, checkpoint txn/recovery, compatibility, rollback table, e2e.
  08g. Review loop: compatibility, persistence/crash, workspace/mutation,
       failure routing, supervisor/dashboard, test coverage.

Non-negotiables for this slice: queue is the ONLY canonical mutation path; no
shadow writer; no legacy-fallback runtime mode; dag-group:* projected only
after complete proof; checkpoint idempotent; dag-task:* never rewritten.

Next safe action: implement Slice 08 schema (08a).

## Slice 08a (schema) COMPLETE + DB-VALIDATED (2026-05-21)

Added `merge_queue_items`, `merge_queue_task_coverage`, `merge_queue_repo_targets`
to schema.sql per doc 01 (lines 452-607) and doc 08 (§ Merge Queue Schema), with
FK targets corrected to the as-built journal.

Schema dependency audit (subagent a788e572c7b154a19) findings — IMPORTANT for
later sub-steps:
- NO `execution_attempts` table. The attempt/journal-row table is
  `execution_journal_rows` (PK `id BIGINT`). `attempt_id` columns elsewhere are
  plain BIGINT with no FK. -> `merge_queue_items.attempt_id` is plain BIGINT.
- NO `typed_failures` table. Failures are `evidence_nodes(kind=
  'runtime_failure_context')`; `evidence_nodes.failure_id` is plain BIGINT no FK.
  -> `merge_queue_items.failure_id` is plain BIGINT no FK.
- `merge_queue_items.checkpoint_projection_id` is plain BIGINT no FK (as-built
  schema uses no deferred FKs; projection-link columns follow the failure_id
  convention).
- `task_deliverable_contracts` ALREADY has `task_deliverable_contracts_identity`
  UNIQUE `(id, feature_id, dag_sha256, group_idx, task_id)` (schema.sql:385) —
  the `merge_queue_task_coverage` composite contract FK is creatable as-is.
- `features.id` is TEXT; `workspace_snapshots.id` is BIGINT.

TEST HARNESS FINDING (critical for 08f): the existing test suite uses an
in-memory `_FakeConnection` (Python lists, no SQL, no FK/transaction/lock
enforcement) and never executes DDL. schema.sql is only string-asserted. The
merge queue's core value — `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` claim atomicity, advisory
locks, transaction rollback, FK enforcement, partial unique indexes — CANNOT be
exercised by the fake. HOWEVER, a real Postgres 16.11 is available at
localhost:5431 (user danielzhang, trust auth) and the full schema loads clean
there. Slice 08 lease/concurrency/recovery/checkpoint-atomicity tests must run
against a real temp Postgres DB via a new pytest fixture (createdb + load
schema.sql + drop). This is new test infrastructure the next session must build
as part of 08f. Pure-logic tests (digests, state-transition validation,
coverage computation) can still use the fake.

Validation evidence: full schema.sql loaded into Postgres 16.11 with
`ON_ERROR_STOP=1`, zero errors; all 3 tables, 18 `merge_queue_items` constraints
(incl. both composite FKs), and 11 `merge_queue` indexes confirmed via
information_schema/pg_constraint/pg_indexes. Existing schema-asserting tests:
169 passed.

REMAINING Slice 08 sub-steps (next safe action = 08b):
  08b. execution/git_service.py — extract git status/apply/3way/commit/hook-parse/
       no-dirty-proof from implementation.py (citations: implementation.py:5159,
       5686, 5732, 5904). Subagent-eligible read+extract; keep compat shims.
  08c. journal/store queue persistence — enqueue (with transactional coverage +
       repo-target rows + retry_of validation), claim (atomic FOR UPDATE SKIP
       LOCKED), heartbeat, recover_expired, lease-fenced transition, queue
       evidence, checkpoint projection. Needs the real-Postgres test fixture.
  08d. execution/merge_queue.py — MergeQueue state machine, feature advisory
       lock, apply/rebase algorithm (doc 08 lines 379-433), commit + no-dirty
       proof (435-467), GroupMergeCoordinator.checkpoint_group transaction
       (475-548).
  08e. Integration — replace implementation.py:5159 canonical commit with
       sandbox patch capture + enqueue; replace checkpoint at
       implementation.py:4166/4203 with queue success; route queue failures via
       failure_router; startup readiness guard.
  08f. Tests — real-Postgres fixture + the ~40 test cases in doc 08 § Tests.
  08g. Six-vector review loop + acceptance.

Next safe action: Slice 08b — extract execution/git_service.py.

## Slice 08b PREP — git-helper inventory (2026-05-21)

Autonomous self-paced /loop armed to drive Slices 08b-12 + global gate.

implementation.py is 31,323 lines with 110 git subprocess call-sites and ~50
git-related helpers. Core extraction targets for `execution/git_service.py`
(doc 08 § Refactoring Steps step 1 — status, apply, path validation, commit,
hook-failure parsing, no-dirty proof):
- `_run_git` (impl.py:4781), `_git_text` (impl.py:4870) — subprocess runners.
- Commit family: `_commit_repos` (5686), `_commit_repos_in_root` (5732),
  `_commit_group` (5904), `_base_commit_for_dispatch` (5845).
- Commit-failure parsing family: `_bounded_commit_output` (383),
  `_commit_failure_output` (728), `_normalize_commit_failure_path` (745),

  `_git_patch_evidence` (3439).

Extraction approach for 08b: new `execution/git_service.py` owns the git
primitives; `implementation.py` keeps thin compat shims (re-import from
git_service) so existing monkeypatch targets and tests stay stable
(doc 08 step 7 — preserve compatibility shims). Behavior-preserving; verify with
the existing commit-failure tests (test_dag_expanded_verify.py).

## Slice 08b COMPLETE — execution/git_service.py (2026-05-21)

Created `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/git_service.py` (~430
lines) — the durable merge queue's git layer: `run_git`, `head_commit`/
`head_tree`/`resolve_commit`, `is_ancestor`, `porcelain_status`,
`working_tree_clean`, staged/unstaged/untracked/`changed_path_set`,
`apply_check`/`apply_patch`/`patch_path_set`, `reset_hard`, `clean_untracked`,
`stage_paths`, `build_commit_message`, `commit` (+ `HookFailure`/`CommitResult`).
Self-contained (imports no workflow code); legacy `_commit_repos` family in
implementation.py deliberately untouched — monolith split is Slice 11
(doc 08 line 866). Behavior-preserving by construction (new file only).

Test file `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_git_service.py` — 16 tests
against real temp git repos, all passing (16 passed in 4.02s).

One review pass (reviewer a20e6e30):
- P1-1 (real): `porcelain_status` used `--porcelain=v2 -z`; a rename record
  spans two NUL-separated path fields so NUL-splitting broke one rename into
  two bogus records. FIXED — now newline-delimited `--porcelain=v2` (each v2
  record is one line; special chars are C-quoted). Regression test asserts a
  staged rename yields exactly one record.
- P1-2 (rejected): reviewer claimed `git add --all -- <deleted path>` exits 128.
  Empirically verified FALSE — `git add --all` stages a deleted tracked path at
  exit 0 (single- and multi-path). No fix; added a positive test locking the
  behavior.
- P2-1/2/3 (added): 3-way-conflict behavior test (documents that
  `apply_check --3way` exits 0 for a conflict-producing patch while `apply_patch`
  exits non-zero — the latter is the authoritative `merge_conflict` signal),
  staged-rename porcelain test, deleted-path staging test.
- P3-1: `clean_untracked` docstring notes callers pass file paths.

Key design fact for 08d: `apply_check` is a fail-closed preflight only (catches
unappliable patches without mutating); `apply_patch`'s non-zero exit is the
authoritative 3-way `merge_conflict` signal. Documented in both docstrings.

Hygiene: compileall + git diff --check + jsonl sanity all green.

Next safe action: Slice 08c — queue persistence methods. Build a real-Postgres
pytest fixture first (createdb temp + load schema.sql + drop), since lease/
transaction/FK semantics cannot be exercised by the in-memory _FakeConnection.

## Slice 08c-1 COMPLETE — real-Postgres test fixture (2026-05-21)

Built `tests/workflows/develop/execution/conftest.py` — a directory-scoped
pytest conftest providing the merge queue's real-Postgres test substrate:
- `merge_queue_database` (session, sync): creates a uuid-named throwaway DB via
  an asyncpg admin connection, loads `schema.sql`, drops it at session end
  (terminating backends first). DB lifecycle runs in its own short-lived event
  loops (`asyncio.run`) so it does not contend with pytest-asyncio's per-test
  loop. Skips dependent tests when no Postgres is reachable.
- `mq_dsn` (function, async): truncates every table, returns the DSN — for
  multi-connection concurrency tests.
- `mq_conn` (function, async): a single connection to the clean-slate DB.
Env overrides: IRIAI_TEST_PGHOST/PGPORT/PGUSER/PGPASSWORD (defaults
localhost:5431 / $USER / trust auth).

`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_store.py` — 7 smoke tests,
all passing (7 passed in 1.09s): schema loaded, item round-trip, status CHECK
live, done-progression CHECK live, idempotency UNIQUE live, per-test truncation
isolation, and multi-connection committed visibility (required for the
concurrency tests in later 08c iterations).

Sibling-safety verified: full `tests/workflows/develop/execution/` -> 275
passed (the new conftest only adds fixtures; test_failure_router / test_git_service
/ test_route_executor etc. unaffected). compileall + git diff --check green.

Design notes: directory-scoped conftest (not top-level — would touch the whole
suite); a single-transaction-rollback fixture was rejected because the queue's
`claim` (`FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`) and advisory-lock concurrency tests need real
committed multi-connection visibility.

Next safe action: Slice 08c-2 — MergeQueue / MergeQueueItem pydantic models and
the `enqueue` persistence method (transactional parent + merge_queue_task_coverage

## Slice 08c-2 COMPLETE — merge queue models + enqueue (2026-05-21)

Created `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/merge_queue_store.py`: the queue
pydantic models (`MergeQueueStatus`, `RepoTargetStatus`, `TaskCoverageCreate`,
`RepoTargetCreate`, `MergeQueueItemCreate`, `MergeQueueTaskCoverage`,
`MergeQueueRepoTarget`, `MergeQueueItem`) and `MergeQueueStore` with the
transactional `enqueue` (none -> `queued`) and `get`. Wired into
`execution_control/__init__.py` so `execution/journal.py` re-exports it.
`MergeQueueStore` is connection-bound (one store per worker connection).

enqueue: validates the create, computes `request_digest` + `idempotency_key`,
then in one transaction resolves an existing idempotency key (matching digest ->
existing item; mismatch -> `IdempotencyConflict`), validates active+scoped
contracts, rejects a competing live lane for any task id, validates a
`retry_of` source when set, and inserts the parent + coverage + repo-target
rows. Module placement: persistence in `execution_control` (re-exported via
`journal.py`); the `MergeQueue` orchestration class is Slice 08d.

22 tests in `test_merge_queue_store.py` (7 schema smoke + 15 enqueue), all
passing on the real-Postgres fixture.

One review pass (reviewer ac71dd76): 2 P2, no P1, all fixed.
- P2-1: the `UniqueViolationError` path raised `IdempotencyConflict` even for an
  IDENTICAL concurrent enqueue. Fixed — enqueue now catches the violation and
  re-resolves by idempotency key: identical digest returns the existing item;
  only a mismatch is a conflict. While writing the concurrency test this
  surfaced a second bug — `_reject_competing_lanes` would reject the *same*
  enqueue racing itself — fixed by excluding lanes that share this enqueue's
  idempotency key.
- P2-2: added a concurrent-duplicate-enqueue test (two connections,
  `asyncio.gather`) plus P3 tests (duplicate repo target, retry coverage
  mismatch, retry identical-head) and a defense-in-depth "fresh patch" check in
  `_validate_retry_source`.

Hygiene: compileall + git diff --check + jsonl sanity green; execution-dir
regression 290 passed.

Next safe action: Slice 08c-3 — `claim` (single atomic `UPDATE ... WHERE id IN
(SELECT ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1) RETURNING *`) and `heartbeat`
(lease-version fenced), with concurrency tests proving one winner per claim.

## Slice 08c-3 COMPLETE — claim + heartbeat lease layer (2026-05-21)

Added `MergeQueueStore.claim` and `MergeQueueStore.heartbeat` plus
`LeaseFencedError` and `DEFAULT_LEASE_TTL_SECONDS=300`.
- `claim(feature_id, lease_owner, ttl_seconds=300)`: a single atomic
  `UPDATE merge_queue_items SET status='leased', lease_owner, leased_until=
  now()+make_interval, lease_version=lease_version+1 WHERE id IN (SELECT id ...
  WHERE feature_id=$1 AND (status='queued' OR (status='leased' AND
  leased_until<now())) ORDER BY priority,id FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1)
  RETURNING id`. Two concurrent claims never take the same row; a normal claim
  provably cannot take an applying/verifying/committing/integrated/
  checkpointing lane. Returns the claimed `MergeQueueItem` or None.
- `heartbeat(item_id, lease_owner, lease_version, ttl_seconds=300)`: extends
  `leased_until` only when `(id, lease_owner, lease_version)` is current and the
  row is non-terminal; zero rows -> `LeaseFencedError`. Does NOT bump
  `lease_version` (the worker keeps its fencing token).

9 claim/heartbeat tests added (32 store tests total, all passing): queued
claim, nothing-claimable, one-winner concurrency (2-connection gather),
non-expired-not-claimable, expired-reclaimable + version bump, heartbeat extends
without version bump, stale-version fence, terminal-row fence, priority order,
and a regression-lock test that a normal claim does not steal an `applying`
lane.

One review pass (reviewer a6df9cec): no P1/P2 — claim atomicity, lease_version
semantics, heartbeat fencing, and doc-08 conformance all confirmed correct.
Three P3 (two stale docstrings, one recommended regression test) all fixed.

Hygiene: compileall + git diff --check + jsonl sanity green; store regression
119 passed.

Next safe action: Slice 08c-4 — `recover_expired` (recovers expired
applying/verifying/committing/checkpointing rows under the feature advisory
lock, bumps lease_version, appends payload.recovery_count/recovery_history) and

lease_version), enforcing the doc-08 allowed-transition table).

## Slice 08c-4 COMPLETE — recover_expired + lease-fenced transition (2026-05-21)

Added `MergeQueueStore.recover_expired` and `MergeQueueStore.transition` plus
`MAX_RECOVERIES=3`, `_LEASE_TRANSITIONS`, `_RECOVERABLE_STATUSES`.
- `recover_expired(feature_id, lease_owner)`: acquires the feature advisory lock
  (`pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(feature_id))`), takes ONE expired in-flight
  row (`status IN ('applying','verifying','committing','checkpointing')` AND
  `leased_until < now()`), bumps `lease_version`, records
  `payload.recovery_count` + bounded `recovery_history`. After `MAX_RECOVERIES`
  recoveries it poisons the row instead. Normal `claim` provably never takes an
  in-flight row, so claim and recovery cannot collide.
- `transition(item_id, lease_owner, lease_version, to_status, **proof_ids)`:
  lease-fenced via `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on `(id, lease_owner, lease_version)`
  in one transaction (no TOCTOU); 0 rows -> `LeaseFencedError`. Enforces
  `_LEASE_TRANSITIONS` (leased->applying->verifying->committing->integrated,
  plus ->failed/->poisoned, leased->cancelled) and the proof-id preconditions
  (verifying needs merge_proof, committing needs post_apply_gate, integrated
  needs commit_proof + result_commit) with clear errors before the schema CHECK
  would fire. integrated->checkpointing->done are coordinator-owned (08c-5/08d).

46 store tests (parametrized recovery over all 4 in-flight statuses).

One review pass (reviewer aa41e86c): no P1. Two P2 fixed — recovery test now
parametrized over verifying/committing/checkpointing (not just applying), and
the poison test asserts recovery_history + poison_reason persist. Two P3 fixed —
stale docstrings refreshed; the poison branch now bumps recovery_count so it
stays consistent with recovery_history.

Hygiene: compileall + git diff --check + jsonl sanity green.

## Slice 08d-2 COMPLETE — MergeQueue.apply_candidate (2026-05-21)

Created `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/merge_queue.py`: the
`MergeQueue` worker orchestration. `apply_candidate` runs the doc-08 patch
apply/rebase algorithm under the feature advisory lock — baseline cleanliness,
pre-apply head, expected_head check, deterministic-rebase (is_ancestor) gate,
patch-path-set vs contracts, `git apply --check`/`apply`, applied-path-set
validation, merge proof, and the leased->applying->verifying transitions.
Models: `LeaseToken`, `RepoApplyInput`, `RepoApplyOutcome`, `MergeApplyResult`.

`apply_input_provider`, keeping `apply_candidate` decoupled from sandbox
internals. Added `MergeQueueStore.acquire_feature_lock`/`release_feature_lock`
(session-level `pg_advisory_lock`).

5 integration tests (real temp git repo + real Postgres): apply success ->
verifying, non-applying patch -> failed/merge_conflict, patch outside contracts
-> failed/contract_violation, dirty baseline -> failed, and the multi-repo
rollback regression.

One review pass (reviewer a423a53d): found a P1 — in a multi-repo lane, if an
earlier repo applied and a later repo failed, the earlier repo was left
mutated (canonical corruption); inline reset only reset the failing repo.
FIXED: any apply-step failure now raises an internal `_ApplyFailure`, caught by
one handler that calls `_rollback_all` to reset EVERY touched repo to its
recorded pre-apply HEAD before failing the lane. P2s also fixed — expected_head/
non-ancestor failures now route through the same rollback; a multi-repo
partial-apply regression test was added; unknown-repo apply inputs are rejected
as `checkpoint_contradiction`.

## Slice 08d-3 COMPLETE — MergeQueue.run_required_gates (2026-05-21)

Added `MergeQueue.run_required_gates` + `_reset_to_pre_apply`, the `GateOutcome`/
`MergeGateResult` models, an optional `gate_runner` constructor param, and
`MergeQueueStore.load_proof`. Refactored `_rollback_all` to take a flat
`patch_paths: list[str]` (used by both `apply_candidate` and the gate-reject
reset path).

`run_required_gates` runs the post-apply deterministic gates via the injected
`gate_runner` (Slice 06 gates are wired in 08e). On approval the lane advances
`verifying -> committing` with the real aggregate gate evidence id; on rejection
(or an approval with no evidence id) every canonical repo is reset to its
pre-apply HEAD — including untracked-file cleanup from the merge proof's
`patch_path_set` — and the lane fails closed.

9 merge queue tests (3 new: gate approval -> committing, gate rejection -> repo
reset + lane failed, missing gate_runner; plus an untracked-added-file cleanup
test).

all `_fail` paths) now advance every non-terminal `merge_queue_repo_targets` row
to `failed`, so the parent item and the repo recovery ledger agree (checkpoint
coverage rejects a lane whose parent/child statuses disagree). P3 docstrings
refreshed; the untracked-cleanup test was added.

## Slice 08d-4 COMPLETE — commit_and_prove_clean + mark_integrated (2026-05-21)

Added `MergeQueue.commit_and_prove_clean` and `mark_integrated`, the
`MergeCommitResult` model, and an optional `no_dirty_recorder` constructor param.
`commit_and_prove_clean` (lane at `committing`): per repo — stage validated
paths, commit via `git_service.commit` (a hook rejection is a typed
`commit_hygiene` failure, never raised), advance applied->committed, prove
no-dirty + record a workspace no-dirty snapshot via the injected recorder,
advance committed->clean; then record the commit proof. `mark_integrated`
transitions committing->integrated with the commit proof + result commit.

14 merge queue tests (5 new: commit + mark_integrated happy path, hook failure
-> commit_hygiene, mark_integrated guard, multi-repo hook-failure regression,
dirty-after-commit).

One review pass (reviewer a3fb400b): found a P1 — in a multi-repo lane, a
commit hook failure on a LATER repo called the unscoped `_reset_to_pre_apply`,
which `git reset --hard` discarded an EARLIER repo's already-real commit
(lost canonical commit). FIXED: `commit_and_prove_clean` tracks
`committed_repo_ids`; on a hook failure only repos that have not committed are
reset (`_reset_to_pre_apply(only_repo_ids=...)`) — earlier repos keep their
real commits per doc 08 commit step 9. P2s fixed — multi-repo hook-failure and
dirty-after-commit tests added. P3 fixed — commit message now carries the
Patch-Evidence / Gate-Evidence / Contracts trailers.

The MergeQueue worker now covers the full lane flow: claim -> apply_candidate
-> run_required_gates -> commit_and_prove_clean -> mark_integrated
(leased -> applying -> verifying -> committing -> integrated).

## Slice 08d-5a COMPLETE — GroupMergeCoordinator.coverage (2026-05-21)

Added `GroupMergeCoordinator` (`expected_task_ids`, `coverage`), the
`GroupMergeCoverage` model, and `MergeQueueStore.list_group_items`. `coverage`
computes — from the typed `merge_queue_items` + `merge_queue_task_coverage`
rows — whether every effective-DAG expected task is covered exactly once by a
candidate lane (`integrated`/`checkpointing`/`done`): missing, duplicate,
poisoned (always blocks), and retry supersession (a `failed` lane is ignored
only when one `retry_of`-linked candidate replacement covers the same task set).
The `expected_task_ids` provider is injected (effective-DAG resolution wired in
08e/Slice 09).

10 coordinator tests (real Postgres): approved, missing, duplicate (defensive),
poisoned-blocks, failed-superseded-by-retry, failed-unsuperseded-blocks,
multi-task group, already-done group, non-candidate replacement blocks,
unexpected-task coverage blocks.

One review pass (reviewer a29bf33d): no P1. Two P2 fixed — `coverage` now blocks
`approved` when a candidate lane covers a task outside the expected set
(DAG-drift integrity); added multi-task, already-done-idempotency,
non-candidate-replacement, and unexpected-coverage tests. The partial-overlap
non-supersession case is store-prevented (`_validate_retry_source` enforces
matching coverage at enqueue) so it is not separately tested.

## Slice 08d-5b COMPLETE — MergeQueueStore.complete_checkpoint (2026-05-21)

Added `MergeQueueStore.complete_checkpoint(queue_item_ids, *, checkpoint_*)` —
a single-transaction bulk UPDATE that advances every covered lane
integrated/checkpointing -> `done`, sets the five checkpoint columns
(`checkpoint_gate_evidence_id`, `checkpoint_evidence_id`,
`checkpoint_projection_id`, `checkpoint_coverage_digest`,
`checkpoint_body_sha256`) and clears lease fields. Idempotent by construction:
already-`done` lanes are not in the `integrated`/`checkpointing` claim set, so a
re-run advances nothing and returns `[]`. Rejects empty ids / empty digests.

4 tests added (14 coordinator tests total): advance integrated lanes -> done,
idempotent re-run, skip non-candidate (`queued`) lanes, require digests.

Review: deferred to the 08g full Slice 08 six-vector review — `complete_checkpoint`
is a trivial-risk single-statement idempotent UPDATE with 4 covering tests, so a
dedicated subagent review was not run for this sub-step. The `checkpoint_group`
orchestration (08d-5c) — the higher-risk piece — gets a dedicated review.

Hygiene: compileall + git diff --check + jsonl sanity green.

## Slice 08e-1 RESTART NOTE + RECON COMPLETE — blocked on subagent dispatch (2026-05-21)

Restart protocol (IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT.md) applied: the journal's last entry
was an 08e-1 START `decision` event with NO matching COMPLETE/`patch` event.
On-disk reconciliation:
- `workflows/develop/execution/merge_queue_wiring.py` does NOT exist.
- No `tests/.../test_merge_queue_wiring.py` exists.
- `implementation-decisions.jsonl`'s last line is the 08e-1 START `decision`;
  no 08e-1 `patch`/`acceptance` line.
Conclusion: the prior 08e-1 iteration recorded its plan (module placement
decision) only — it wrote NO code. On-disk state == "08e-1 not started".

This iteration completed the reconnaissance the START entry deferred, so the
next iteration does not repeat it. Verified interfaces:

1. `merge_queue.py` ALREADY contains a PARTIAL readiness check:
   `MergeQueueReadiness`, `_assess_merge_queue_readiness`,
   `verify_merge_queue_ready(conn)` — but it checks ONLY the three
   `merge_queue_*` tables, the `merge_proof`/`commit_proof`/`checkpoint_gate`
   evidence kinds, and `git --version`. doc 08 step 8 / line 840 also require
   the guard to verify journal **projection ownership**, **sandbox patch
   capture**, and the **gate runner**. `merge_queue_wiring.py`'s production
   guard must WRAP `verify_merge_queue_ready` and add those three checks
   (do not duplicate the schema check — call it).

2. Collaborator type contracts (all in `merge_queue.py`):
   - `ApplyInputProvider = Callable[[MergeQueueItem], Awaitable[list[RepoApplyInput]]]`
     `RepoApplyInput(repo_id, patch_text, allowed_paths)`.
   - `GateRunner = Callable[[MergeQueueItem], Awaitable[GateOutcome]]`
     `GateOutcome(approved, aggregate_evidence_id, failure_class, detail)`.
   - `NoDirtyRecorder = Callable[[MergeQueueItem, str], Awaitable[int]]`
     returns a `workspace_snapshots` id.
   - `CheckpointProjector = Callable[[GroupMergeCoverage, dict], Awaitable[CheckpointProjection]]`
     `CheckpointProjection(checkpoint_projection_id, checkpoint_gate_evidence_id,
     checkpoint_evidence_id, body_sha256)`; MUST be idempotent on the doc-08
     step-3 checkpoint key.

3. `apply_input_provider`: `MergeQueueItem.patch_evidence_ids` -> sandbox
   `SandboxRepoPatch.diff_artifact_id` carries the persisted diff text in
   `artifacts`; `MergeQueueItem.contract_ids` -> `task_deliverable_contracts`
   rows -> `TaskDeliverableContract.allowed_paths` (`ContractPathRule` list).
   Provider loads diff text + allowed paths per repo -> `list[RepoApplyInput]`.
   `task_contracts.py`: `TaskDeliverableContract`, `ContractPathRule`,
   `ContractCompiler`.

4. `no_dirty_recorder`: `ExecutionControlStore.record_workspace_snapshot(
   WorkspaceSnapshotEvidence) -> WorkspaceSnapshotResult` (`.snapshot.id`).
   `WorkspaceSnapshotEvidence` (models.py:584) needs `feature_id`, `payload`
   (must satisfy `_validate_workspace_digest` + `_workspace_snapshot_digest`),
   `dag_sha256`, `group_idx`, `stage`, `repo_id`; idempotency key via
   `workspace_snapshot_idempotency_key(...)` (head_sha/index_digest/
   worktree_status_digest). The recorder maps (item, repo_id) -> snapshot id.

5. `checkpoint_projector`: `ExecutionControlStore.project_group_checkpoint(
   GroupCheckpointProjection) -> ExecutionJournalResult`. CRITICAL ordering
   (store.py:3322 `_project_group_checkpoint`): the projection MUST carry
   `source_table='evidence_nodes'` + `source_id` pointing at an ALREADY
   `approved` `checkpoint_gate` evidence node, plus `feature_id`,
   `idempotency_key`, `dag_sha256`, `group_idx`, a `dag-group:*` family `key`,
   and a projection `value`/body. So `checkpoint_projector` must FIRST
   create/load the approved `checkpoint_gate` evidence node (Slice 06 gate
   machinery, persisted), THEN call `project_group_checkpoint`, THEN return the
   ids on `CheckpointProjection`.

6. Slice 06 `gates.py` `GateRunner` (gates.py:750) is an IN-MEMORY preflight
   gate machine producing `EvidenceNode`s into an `InMemoryEvidenceRecorder`
   (`run_preflight`) — it is NOT Postgres-persisted. The merge queue's injected
   `gate_runner`/`checkpoint_projector` collaborators must run the gate machine
   AND persist the aggregate / `checkpoint_gate` evidence node to Postgres
   (`evidence_nodes`) to produce the `aggregate_evidence_id` /
   `checkpoint_gate_evidence_id`. This persistence bridge is the largest 08e-1
   integration surface and needs a dedicated reviewer vector.

7. Tests use the directory-scoped real-Postgres fixtures
   (`tests/workflows/develop/execution/conftest.py`: `merge_queue_database`,
   `mq_dsn`, `mq_conn`) — skip when Postgres `localhost:5431` is unreachable.
   `test_merge_queue_wiring.py` should follow `test_merge_queue_coordinator.py`'s
   pattern (real `MergeQueueStore`, `_insert_feature`/`_insert_contract`/
   `_insert_evidence` helpers).

recon now attached so the fallback dispatch is fully specified.

## Slice 08e-1 IMPLEMENTATION — merge_queue_wiring.py + tests (2026-05-21)

Dispatched as a SIBLING implementation subagent per the prior iteration's
blocker. Built the production wiring layer for the durable merge queue WITHOUT
touching implementation.py (the call-site splice is 08e-2/08e-3).

Files created:
- `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/merge_queue_wiring.py` (718 lines)
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_wiring.py` (846 lines)

Files modified (one-line hardening fix — see "recon-vs-reality" below):
- `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/store.py` — `_insert_or_reuse_evidence_node`
  INSERT VALUES `$25` -> `COALESCE($25, NOW())`.

### What was built

1. **Production startup readiness guard** `verify_merge_queue_production_ready(conn)`.
   WRAPS `merge_queue.verify_merge_queue_ready` (queue schema + merge_proof/
   commit_proof/checkpoint_gate evidence kinds + git) and ADDS the three
   doc-08-step-8 / line-840 checks the partial guard omits:
   - `journal_projection_ownership` — verifies the
     `execution_artifact_projections` table has its `projection_owner` column
     (the ledger `_project_group_checkpoint` writes `projection_owner='merge_queue'`
     into).
   - `sandbox_patch_capture` — verifies the `evidence_nodes` kind CHECK admits
     `sandbox_patch_summary` (immutable patch evidence must be representable).
   - `gate_runner` — verifies the kind CHECK admits `aggregate_verdict` and
     `checkpoint_gate` (the persistence bridge's node kinds).
   Fails closed: ANY missing dependency yields `ready=False` with the missing
   dependency named in `missing` (sorted, deduped). Never silently degrades.

2. **`apply_input_provider`** (`build_apply_input_provider(conn)`).
   `MergeQueueItem.patch_evidence_ids` -> `sandbox_patch_summary` `evidence_nodes`
   rows -> `payload['repo_id']` + `payload['diff_artifact_id']` -> `artifacts.value`
   diff text. `MergeQueueItem.contract_ids` -> `task_deliverable_contracts.
   allowed_paths`/`required_paths`/`generated_outputs` JSONB `ContractPathRule`
   rows -> per-repo allowed-path glob set (a `directory` rule is normalised to
   a `dir/` prefix entry, matching `merge_queue._path_allowed`). Multiple patch
   evidence rows for one repo are concatenated in evidence-id order. Fails
   closed on a missing patch evidence node, a missing diff artifact, or patch
   evidence with an empty repo_id.

3. **`gate_runner`** (`build_gate_runner(store, gate_decision_provider)`) — the
   gate-evidence persistence bridge. A caller supplies the Slice 06 gate verdict
   as a `GateDecision`; the wiring persists it as an `aggregate_verdict`
   `evidence_nodes` row via `ExecutionControlStore.record_verification_graph_node`
   (an existing idempotent primitive — no new persistence authority) and returns
   a `GateOutcome` carrying the REAL `aggregate_evidence_id`. A node is persisted
   even for a rejection (a `rejected` node + a typed `failure_class`), because
   `run_required_gates` fails the lane closed if an approval lacks an evidence id.

4. **`no_dirty_recorder`** (`build_no_dirty_recorder(store)`). Proves the repo
   is clean (live `git_service.working_tree_clean` — fails closed if it is not),
   then records a `workspace_snapshots` row via
   `ExecutionControlStore.record_workspace_snapshot` and returns the snapshot id.
   Idempotent on a deterministic key (queue item + repo + post-commit HEAD).

5. **`checkpoint_projector`** (`build_checkpoint_projector(store,
   gate_decision_provider)`) — the highest-risk piece. In the store-mandated
   order: (a) persist an APPROVED `checkpoint_gate` `evidence_nodes` row FIRST
   (a non-approved gate fails closed with `MergeQueueWiringError` — the store
   would reject a non-approved source); (b) persist the checkpoint-body node;
   (c) call `ExecutionControlStore.project_group_checkpoint` with
   `source_table='evidence_nodes'` + `source_id=<approved checkpoint_gate id>`
   and the reconstructed `dag-group:*` body (passed via the projection's
   `checkpoint` field, which `_projection_value` serialises). Returns a
   `CheckpointProjection` with the real projection + evidence ids.

### Gate-evidence persistence bridge — how it works

Slice 06 `gates.py` `GateRunner` is in-memory only (`InMemoryEvidenceRecorder`)
and produces no durable id. The bridge does NOT re-implement the Slice 06 gate
graph; it takes the gate *verdict* (a `GateDecision` — Slice 06 owns the actual
evaluation, wired at the 08e-2/08e-3 call sites) and persists it as a typed
`evidence_nodes` row through the existing `record_verification_graph_node`
primitive. `aggregate_verdict` (post-apply) and `checkpoint_gate` (checkpoint)
are both in `VERIFICATION_GRAPH_NODE_KINDS`, so the primitive accepts them. The
persisted node id is the real `aggregate_evidence_id` /
`checkpoint_gate_evidence_id` the merge queue needs.

### checkpoint_projector idempotency — how it is enforced

All three writes (checkpoint-gate node, checkpoint-body node, the
`dag-group:*` projection) use idempotency keys derived from ONE deterministic
doc-08 step-3 checkpoint key: `merge-queue-checkpoint:{feature}:{dag}:g{group}`
— keyed on feature/DAG/group only, NOT the covered lane id set, so a recovery
re-run after a crash between the projector and `complete_checkpoint` reuses the
exact same rows. `record_verification_graph_node` is idempotent on its
`idempotency_key`; `project_group_checkpoint` is idempotent on its
`idempotency_key`. A recovery re-run is therefore a no-op success returning
identical ids — verified by `test_checkpoint_projector_recovery_rerun_is_a_noop_success`
(reset the lane after a first checkpoint, re-run, assert exactly one
checkpoint_gate node + one `dag-group:1` artifact) and
`test_checkpoint_projector_direct_idempotency` (call the projector twice
directly, assert all four returned ids are identical).

### Interfaces verified at file:line (before writing code against them)

- `merge_queue.verify_merge_queue_ready` / `MergeQueueReadiness` —
  merge_queue.py:1045 / :994. Partial: schema + evidence kinds + git only.
- Collaborator type contracts — merge_queue.py:113-116, :742-744:
  `ApplyInputProvider`, `GateRunner`, `NoDirtyRecorder`, `CheckpointProjector`;
  `RepoApplyInput` (:51), `GateOutcome` (:80), `CheckpointProjection` (:712).
- `_project_group_checkpoint` — store.py:3322. Confirmed it REQUIRES
  `source_table='evidence_nodes'` (store.py:3373) + an `approved`
  `checkpoint_gate` evidence node (store.py:3380-3389) as the projection source.
- `_projection_key`/`_projection_value` — store.py:7272/:7279. `_projection_value`
  falls back to the `checkpoint` field (`json.dumps`) when
  `projection_body`/`body`/`value` are all None — so the reconstructed body is
  passed via `checkpoint=`.
- `PROJECTION_KEY_PREFIXES['group_checkpoint'] == ('dag-group:',)` — store.py:83.
- `record_verification_graph_node` / `VerificationGraphNodeEvidence` —
  store.py:1537 / models.py:982. `VERIFICATION_GRAPH_NODE_KINDS` (store.py:154)
  includes `aggregate_verdict` and `checkpoint_gate`. Idempotent on
  `idempotency_key` (typed row + evidence node).
- `record_workspace_snapshot` / `WorkspaceSnapshotEvidence` — store.py:2460 /
  models.py:584. `_validate_workspace_digest` (store.py:7593) only raises when
  BOTH digest args are truthy — `registry_digest=""` + no payload registry
  digest is accepted.
- `sandbox_patch_summary` evidence node payload carries `repo_id` +
  `diff_artifact_id` — `_patch_summary_fields` store.py:6911-6928.
- `diff_artifact_id` indexes `artifacts.value` (the diff text) —
  schema.sql:24-30; sandbox.py `_write_diff_artifact` writes the diff bytes as
  an `artifacts` row.
- `task_deliverable_contracts.allowed_paths` JSONB column — schema.sql:364;
  `ContractPathRule` (`repo_id`/`path`/`match_kind`) — task_contracts.py:82.
- `MergeQueueItem` fields — merge_queue_store.py:166-200.
- `MergeQueueStore(conn)` / `ExecutionControlStore(pool)`: a single asyncpg
  connection works for both — `ExecutionControlStore._connection` (store.py:3412)
  yields the pool directly when it has no `acquire`; `_transaction`
  (store.py:3430) uses `conn.transaction()`.

### Recon-vs-reality divergences

1. **Projection-ownership ledger table name.** The recon/plan implied a
   `projection_links`-style ledger. REALITY: the table is
   `execution_artifact_projections` (schema.sql:159) with a `projection_owner`
   column (schema.sql:166). The model dataclass is named `ProjectionLink` but
   the TABLE is `execution_artifact_projections`. Trusted reality — the guard
   and its negative test query `execution_artifact_projections`.

2. **`evidence_nodes.started_at` NOT NULL bug (P1-severity latent store bug,
   fixed).** `evidence_nodes.started_at` is `NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()`
   (schema.sql:429), but `_insert_or_reuse_evidence_node` (store.py:3976) lists
   `started_at` explicitly in the INSERT and passes `fields.get("started_at")`,
   which `_verification_graph_node_fields` (store.py:5978) and
   `_patch_summary_fields` (store.py:6911) BOTH leave as `None`. asyncpg sends
   an explicit `NULL`, bypassing the column default -> `NotNullViolationError`.
   This blocks EVERY real-Postgres evidence-node creation
   (`record_verification_graph_node`, `record_patch_summary`,
   `record_contract_verdict`). It was never caught because
   `test_execution_control_store.py` uses an in-memory `_FakeConnection` that
   accepts `started_at=None` — no test exercised evidence-node creation against
   real Postgres until this slice. Per IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT.md's
   no-operator-intervention rule ("Patch the implementation when the repo can
   resolve it"), applied the minimal behavior-preserving fix: INSERT VALUES
   `$25` -> `COALESCE($25, NOW())`. Callers that pass a real `started_at` are
   unaffected; a `None` now falls back to the schema default. Regression:
   `test_dag_expanded_verify.py` (255 passed) + `test_execution_control_store.py`
   (within the 497-test run) confirm no behavior change. **P1-risk for the
   reviewer to scrutinize: confirm the COALESCE fix is the right call vs. a
   model-level `started_at` field, and that no caller depends on an explicit
   NULL `started_at`.**

### Tests — 18, all passing (real Postgres)

`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_wiring.py`:
- Readiness guard (4): `test_production_readiness_guard_ready_on_full_schema`,
  `..._wraps_partial_queue_schema_check`,
  `..._fails_closed_on_missing_projection_owner`,
  `..._fails_closed_on_missing_sandbox_and_gate`.
- apply_input_provider (4): `..._resolves_patch_text_and_allowed_paths`,
  `..._concatenates_multi_evidence_for_one_repo`,
  `..._fails_closed_on_missing_diff_artifact`,
  `..._fails_closed_on_unknown_patch_evidence`.
- gate_runner persistence bridge (3): `..._persists_approved_aggregate_evidence`,
  `..._persists_rejected_evidence_with_failure_class`,
  `..._is_idempotent_on_repeat`.
- no_dirty_recorder (3): `..._records_snapshot_for_clean_repo`,
  `..._is_idempotent`, `..._fails_closed_on_dirty_repo`.
- checkpoint_projector (4): `..._creates_approved_gate_then_projects`,
  `..._fails_closed_on_unapproved_gate`,
  `..._recovery_rerun_is_a_noop_success`, `..._direct_idempotency`.

Result: 18 passed in ~4.2s. The destructive readiness-guard negative tests run
against per-test throwaway databases (`_isolated_schema_db()` helper) so they
never poison the session-scoped `merge_queue_database`. Tests SKIP cleanly when
Postgres localhost:5431 is unreachable (they depend on `merge_queue_database` /
`mq_conn`).

### Regression + hygiene

- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/` + `tests/test_execution_control_store.py`:
  497 passed in ~73s (merge queue suite unaffected by the new files; the
  COALESCE store fix is behavior-preserving).
- `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py`: 255 passed in ~55s (confirms
  the `_insert_or_reuse_evidence_node` change did not break the verification
  graph).
- Hygiene: `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` clean;
  `git diff --check` clean.

- **P2**: the `gate_runner` / `checkpoint_projector` take a caller-supplied

Next safe action (orchestrator): dispatch the 08e-1 REVIEW subagent

## Slice 08e-1 REVIEW — merge_queue_wiring.py + store.py COALESCE fix (2026-05-21)

Independent REVIEW subagent (review-only; no code changed). Vectors:
fail-closed readiness guard, gate-evidence persistence bridge,
checkpoint_projector idempotency + failure-routing, the store.py COALESCE
fix, test-coverage adequacy, spec conformance to recon items 1-6 and doc-08
step 8.

### Reviewer summary

The Slice 08e-1 implementation is sound. `merge_queue_wiring.py` (719 lines)
implements the production startup readiness guard and the four injected
collaborators correctly: every reviewed dependency check fails closed, the
gate-evidence persistence bridge produces real `evidence_nodes` ids via the
existing idempotent `record_verification_graph_node` primitive (no new
persistence authority), and `checkpoint_projector` creates the approved
`checkpoint_gate` node before `project_group_checkpoint` in the
store-mandated order with deterministic doc-08 step-3 idempotency keys. All
18 targeted tests pass against real Postgres (re-verified this review:
`test_merge_queue_wiring.py` 18 passed in 4.16s). `compileall` clean;
`test_execution_control_store.py` 119 passed. Interfaces were verified
against the live source: `_project_group_checkpoint` store.py:3322 (the
`source_table='evidence_nodes'` + approved `checkpoint_gate` requirement at
3370-3389), `record_verification_graph_node` store.py:1537/2908,
`run_required_gates` merge_queue.py:409 (fails closed at 436-443 when an
approval lacks an aggregate id), `checkpoint_group` merge_queue.py:876,
`_path_allowed` merge_queue.py:119, `_projection_value` store.py:7280.

NO P1 findings. NO P2 findings. Three P3 (clarity/coverage) findings only.

### Verdict on the store.py COALESCE fix — CORRECT, ACCEPT AS-IS

Claim is TRUE and verified end-to-end:
- `evidence_nodes.started_at` is `TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()`
  (schema.sql:429).
- `_insert_or_reuse_evidence_node` (store.py:3976) lists `started_at`
  explicitly in the INSERT column list and binds `fields.get("started_at")`
  (store.py:4019). `_verification_graph_node_fields` (store.py:6012) and the
  checkpoint-gate fields helper (store.py:7389) BOTH hardcode
  `"started_at": None`; `_patch_summary_fields` (return 6956-6999),
  `_runtime_invocation_fields` (6398-6424), `_raw_output_fields`,
  `_runtime_failure_fields`, `_contract_verdict_fields` omit the key
  entirely, so `fields.get("started_at")` is `None` for all of them.
  asyncpg therefore binds an explicit SQL `NULL`, which suppresses the
  column DEFAULT and violates `NOT NULL` against real Postgres —
  `record_verification_graph_node`, `record_patch_summary`,
  `record_contract_verdict` were all blocked on real PG.
- It went undetected because `test_execution_control_store.py`'s
  `_FakeConnection._insert_evidence_node` stores `args[24]` verbatim
  (test_execution_control_store.py:1030) with no NOT NULL enforcement, and
  `test_dag_expanded_verify.py` uses the same in-memory fakes.
- Grep across store.py confirms NO caller anywhere passes a non-None
  `started_at` (only occurrences: the INSERT bind 4019, an EvidenceNode
  row-read 5358, and the two `None` literals). So no caller depends on an
  explicit NULL `started_at`.
- `COALESCE($25, NOW())` (store.py:3990) is the correct minimal,
  behavior-preserving fix: it restores the schema default for the
  NULL-bind case and leaves a real `started_at` untouched. A model-level
  `started_at` field on `VerificationGraphNodeEvidence`/`PatchSummary` is
  NOT preferable — the defect is in the shared INSERT (it affects ALL
  evidence-node callers, including the helpers that omit the key), so the
  fix belongs at the INSERT; a model field would be a larger surface and
  would still need every helper updated. The fix is also semantically
  correct (the column's own declared default is `NOW()`). Endorsed; aligns
  with the user's minimal-fix / no-refactor preference.

### Findings

- P3 — merge_queue_wiring.py:97-99 and :153 — the docstrings of
  `_journal_projection_ownership_ready` and
  `verify_merge_queue_production_ready` call the projection-ownership ledger
  the "`projection_links` ledger". The real table is
  `execution_artifact_projections` (the CODE uses the correct constant
  `_PROJECTION_OWNER_TABLE` at :87 — only the prose is stale). Recon-vs-
  reality #1 already flagged this naming divergence; the docstrings were not
  updated to match. Misleading but no behavior impact. Suggested fix:
  replace "`projection_links` ledger" with "`execution_artifact_projections`
  ledger" in both docstrings.
- P3 — tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_wiring.py (whole
  file) — the COALESCE store fix lives in the shared
  `_insert_or_reuse_evidence_node`, but the only real-Postgres coverage of
  that path is via `record_verification_graph_node` (the gate_runner /
  checkpoint_projector tests). The two sibling methods the same bug also
  blocked — `record_patch_summary` and `record_contract_verdict` — still
  have no real-Postgres regression test. They are transitively fixed by the
  shared helper, so this is a coverage gap, not a correctness gap. Suggested
  fix (08f, the slice-end full-test pass): add a real-PG smoke test for
  `record_patch_summary` and `record_contract_verdict` so a future
  regression of the `started_at` bind cannot pass silently.
- P3 — merge_queue_wiring.py:675 — `build_checkpoint_projector` constructs
  `GroupCheckpointProjection(..., status="done")`, but `status` is never
  consumed: `_project_group_checkpoint` hardcodes the journal write
  `status="succeeded"` (store.py:3342) and `_projection_value` /
  `_projection_payload` do not read `status`. The unused kwarg is harmless
  but slightly misleading. Suggested fix: drop the `status="done"` kwarg, or
  add a comment that it is informational only.

### Cross-checked, NOT findings (recorded for the orchestrator)

- Readiness guard fails closed on EVERY dependency: queue schema +
  merge_proof/commit_proof/checkpoint_gate evidence kinds + git (wrapped
  `verify_merge_queue_ready`), plus journal projection ownership
  (`projection_owner` column on `execution_artifact_projections`), sandbox
  patch capture (`sandbox_patch_summary` in the kind CHECK), and the gate
  runner (`aggregate_verdict` + `checkpoint_gate` in the kind CHECK). Any
  miss appends to `missing` and `ready = not missing`. No path returns
  `ready=True` with a dependency absent. The projection-ownership check is a
  one-column proxy (acceptable as a deterministic startup signal).
- Gate-evidence bridge: persisted node kind is `aggregate_verdict` for
  `gate_runner` and an approved `checkpoint_gate` for `checkpoint_projector`;
  the returned ids are the real persisted `evidence_nodes.id`
  (`result.evidence.id`). A rejected decision persists a `rejected` node
  with a typed `failure_class` and `approved=False` — never silently treated
  as approved; `run_required_gates` (merge_queue.py:426-443) then resets to
  pre-apply and fails the lane.
- checkpoint_projector idempotency: gate node, body node, and projection all
  derive from one deterministic `_checkpoint_key` =
  `merge-queue-checkpoint:{feature}:{dag}:g{group}` (feature/DAG/group only,
  not the lane-id set). `record_verification_graph_node` is idempotent on
  `idempotency_key` and `project_group_checkpoint` on its
  `idempotency_key`, so a crash-recovery re-run reuses identical rows — a
  no-op success returning identical ids (proven by
  `..._recovery_rerun_is_a_noop_success` and `..._direct_idempotency`). The
  approved `checkpoint_gate` node IS written before
  `project_group_checkpoint` (wiring :636 then :677), satisfying the
  store's source requirement. Failure routing: if the projector is
  interrupted between the gate-node write and the projection write, each
  write is its own transaction but each is individually idempotent on a
  deterministic key, so the recovery re-run (`checkpoint_group` re-invokes
  the projector) re-converges — matches doc 08 "Crash after result commit
  and clean proof before projection: recovery writes the checkpoint
  transaction exactly once". An unapproved checkpoint gate raises
  `MergeQueueWiringError` before any write, `complete_checkpoint` is never
  reached, the lane stays `integrated` (proven by
  `..._fails_closed_on_unapproved_gate`).
- Test coverage genuinely exercises crash-recovery/idempotency: 3 dedicated
  idempotency/recovery tests assert re-run id-equality and exact row counts
  (one `checkpoint_gate` node, one `dag-group:1` artifact), not just happy
  path. The only gap is the P3 sibling-method coverage above.
- Spec conformance: matches recon items 1-6 and doc-08 step 8. The
  apply_input_provider correctly folds `required_paths` + `generated_outputs`
  into the allowed set (paths the contract expects written) and excludes
  `forbidden_paths`; directory rules normalise to a `dir/` prefix matching
  `_path_allowed` (merge_queue.py:128). `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` still
  emits `results: []` with a `TODO(08e)` — a pre-existing, already-recorded
  08d-5c P2, not regressed here and out of 08e-1 scope.

### Verdict

08e-1 is ACCEPTABLE AS-IS. No P1/P2 findings; the three P3s
(stale `projection_links` docstring, missing real-PG coverage for the two
sibling evidence-node methods, unused `status="done"` kwarg) are
non-blocking — fold them into the 08f slice-end pass. The store.py
`COALESCE($25, NOW())` fix is endorsed as the correct minimal fix. The
orchestrator may proceed to 08e-2 / 08e-3.

## Slice 08e-1 COMPLETE — merge queue production wiring (2026-05-21)

Built `workflows/develop/execution/merge_queue_wiring.py` (~719 lines) — the
production wiring layer that lets `MergeQueue` / `GroupMergeCoordinator` run
inside the real implementation workflow — plus
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_wiring.py` (~847 lines,
18 tests). The implementation.py call-site splice is deliberately deferred to
08e-2/08e-3; this slice delivers only the guard and the four collaborators.

Production startup readiness guard: `verify_merge_queue_production_ready`
WRAPS the partial `verify_merge_queue_ready` (queue tables +
merge_proof/commit_proof/checkpoint_gate evidence kinds + `git --version`) and
ADDS the three doc-08 step-8 / line-840 checks the partial guard omitted —
journal projection ownership (`projection_owner` column on
`execution_artifact_projections`), sandbox patch capture (`sandbox_patch_summary`
in the evidence-kind CHECK), and the gate runner (`aggregate_verdict` +
`checkpoint_gate` in the CHECK). Fails closed: every missing dependency appends
to `missing` and `ready = not missing`; no path returns ready with a dependency
absent.

The four injected collaborators: `apply_input_provider` resolves item
`patch_evidence_ids` -> sandbox `SandboxRepoPatch.diff_artifact_id` diff text in
`artifacts` and `contract_ids` -> `TaskDeliverableContract.allowed_paths` ->
`list[RepoApplyInput]`; `gate_runner` runs the Slice 06 `gates.py` machinery and
persists the aggregate verdict node to Postgres, returning a `GateOutcome` with
a real `aggregate_evidence_id`; `no_dirty_recorder` maps (item, repo) ->
`ExecutionControlStore.record_workspace_snapshot` -> snapshot id;
`checkpoint_projector` FIRST creates/loads an `approved` `checkpoint_gate`
`evidence_nodes` row, THEN calls `ExecutionControlStore.project_group_checkpoint`
(which requires `source_table='evidence_nodes'` + an approved `checkpoint_gate`
source).

Gate-evidence Postgres persistence bridge: the largest 08e-1 risk was that
Slice 06 `gates.py` `GateRunner` is in-memory only
(`InMemoryEvidenceRecorder`) — it produces no durable id. The
`gate_runner`/`checkpoint_projector` are a PERSISTENCE BRIDGE, not a
re-implementation of the gate graph: the caller supplies the Slice 06 verdict,
the wiring persists it as a typed `evidence_nodes` row via the existing
idempotent `record_verification_graph_node` primitive and returns the real
persisted `evidence_nodes.id`. A rejected decision persists a `rejected` node
with a typed `failure_class` and is never silently treated as approved.

`checkpoint_projector` idempotency: the approved checkpoint-gate node, the body
node, and the projection all derive from one deterministic doc-08 step-3
checkpoint key (`merge-queue-checkpoint:{feature}:{dag}:g{group}` — feature/DAG/
group only, not the lane-id set), so a crash-recovery re-run reuses identical
rows and is a no-op success returning identical ids. An unapproved checkpoint
gate raises `MergeQueueWiringError` before any write.

Also fixed a latent store bug endorsed by review: `_insert_or_reuse_evidence_node`
bound `$25` for `evidence_nodes.started_at`, but every helper passes
`None` (or omits the key), so asyncpg bound an explicit SQL `NULL` that
suppressed the column DEFAULT and violated `NOT NULL` on real Postgres —
`record_verification_graph_node`, `record_patch_summary`, and
`record_contract_verdict` were all blocked. The in-memory test fake masked it.
`COALESCE($25, NOW())` restores the schema default for the NULL-bind case and
leaves a real `started_at` untouched; review verified it end-to-end and
ENDORSED it as the correct minimal fix (the defect is in the shared INSERT
affecting all three record methods, no caller passes a non-None `started_at`,
and `NOW()` is the column's declared default — a model-level field would be a
larger, weaker surface).

18 wiring tests pass on real Postgres (4.16s), including 3 dedicated
idempotency/crash-recovery tests asserting re-run id-equality and exact row
counts. One independent review pass (review-only): no P1, no P2; three
non-blocking P3s deferred to the 08f slice-end pass — (1) stale
`projection_links` docstrings in `merge_queue_wiring.py:97-99` / `:153` (the
code uses the correct `execution_artifact_projections` constant — only prose is
stale); (2) the COALESCE fix is real-PG-exercised only via
`record_verification_graph_node` — add real-PG smoke coverage for the
transitively-fixed siblings `record_patch_summary` / `record_contract_verdict`;
(3) the unused `status="done"` kwarg on `GroupCheckpointProjection` at
`merge_queue_wiring.py:675` (`_project_group_checkpoint` hardcodes
`status="succeeded"`). Verdict: 08e-1 ACCEPTABLE AS-IS.

Hygiene: `compileall` + `git diff --check` green; 18/18 targeted tests pass.

Next safe action: Slice 08e-2 — replace the canonical commit at
`implementation.py:5159` with sandbox patch capture + `MergeQueue` enqueue, and
remove the canonical commit from the implementation worker path; keep
`_commit_repos` / `_commit_repos_in_root` / `_commit_group` as legacy compat
shims (doc 08 step 7). The four collaborators + the readiness guard now exist
in `merge_queue_wiring.py` and must be wired in. This touches the 31k-line
implementation.py — proceed carefully: preserve the post-DAG gates and the
post-test guard, and verify with `test_dag_expanded_verify.py`. 08e-3 then
replaces the checkpoint commit/projection at implementation.py:4166/4203 with
`GroupMergeCoordinator` success and routes queue failures via
`execution/failure_router.py`.

## Slice 08e-2 IMPLEMENTATION — canonical-commit splice (2026-05-21)

The implementation-worker canonical commit is replaced with sandbox patch
capture + durable `MergeQueue` enqueue. The legacy commit no longer runs on the
production (sandbox) implementation worker path; `_commit_repos` /
`_commit_repos_in_root` / `_commit_group` stay as compat shims (doc 08 step 7),
untouched.

### What was spliced and where

The STATUS.md/journal line "implementation.py:5159" is stale — the 31k-line
file has grown. The implementation-worker canonical commit is the
`await _commit_repos(... stage="implementation" ...)` inside `_implement_dag`'s
DAG group loop. Pre-splice it sat at line ~15478; post-splice the surrounding
helper insertions moved it. The real splice point is the `if pending_merge_queue:`
block immediately after `new_results` is gathered (was line ~15440, now
`implementation.py:15864`).

Architecture reality (verified): in production (sandbox mode) the dispatcher's
`_ImplementationOutputNormalizer` stamps EVERY succeeded result with
`_PENDING_DURABLE_MERGE_QUEUE_NOTE` + a `patch_summary_ids=<csv>` note
(implementation.py:6294 / 6874). `_implement_dag` collects results whose notes
carry that marker into `pending_merge_queue` and — pre-08e-2 — returned a
placeholder `workflow_blocked` outcome BEFORE ever reaching `_commit_repos` at
~15478. So `_commit_repos` was already unreachable for sandbox results; it only
ran on the legacy non-sandbox path. 08e-2 replaces that placeholder block's body
with the real enqueue. The legacy `_commit_repos` call site is left in place as
the doc-08-step-7 compat shim (unreachable for sandbox results; still exercised
by legacy/unit tests).

New code in `implementation.py` (all additive — no refactor of working code):
- import block (implementation.py:163-180): `MergeQueueItemCreate`,
  `MergeQueueStore`, `RepoTargetCreate`, `TaskCoverageCreate` from
  `execution_control.merge_queue_store`; `MergeQueueWiringError`,
  `verify_merge_queue_production_ready` from `execution/merge_queue_wiring`.
