  Import-failure-tolerant (mirrors the Slice 04-07 `try/except ImportError`
  pattern) — all bound to `None` if the Slice 08 modules are absent.
- `from contextlib import asynccontextmanager` added to the stdlib imports.
- `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` — the fail-closed exception.
- `_parse_patch_evidence_ids_from_notes` — extracts the `patch_summary_ids` csv
  the dispatcher writes into result notes; returns sorted+deduped ids (stable
  enqueue request digest).
- `_merge_queue_connection` — an `@asynccontextmanager` that yields an asyncpg
  connection from an `ExecutionControlStore._pool` (or yields the object
  directly when the "pool" is itself a bare connection — the single-connection
  test path). Mirrors `ExecutionControlStore._connection` without depending on
  that private contextmanager.
- `_coerce_jsonb` — asyncpg JSONB (dict or JSON string) -> dict.
- `_resolve_merge_queue_lane_inputs` — per sandbox result: resolves the
  contract, the immutable `sandbox_patch_summary` evidence node payloads (for
  `repo_id` + `base_commit`), the canonical repo dir, and the base commit.
  Fails closed on every gap.
- `_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results` — the splice entry point.
- The splice itself inside `_implement_dag` (implementation.py:15864).

### How the enqueue + sandbox patch capture are wired

The sandbox patch capture ALREADY happened — the dispatcher (`_ImplementationSandboxPort.capture_patch` -> `SandboxRunner.capture_patch` ->
`_record_patch_summary`) captured each repo diff into `artifacts` and recorded a
`sandbox_patch_summary` `evidence_nodes` row whose payload carries `repo_id`,
`base_commit`, `diff_artifact_id` (verified `_patch_summary_fields`,
store.py:6912-6929). 08e-2 consumes that evidence; it does not re-capture.

`_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results` runs in three phases:
1. ONE connection: `verify_merge_queue_production_ready(conn)` (the Slice 08e-1
   readiness guard) then `_resolve_merge_queue_lane_inputs` (reads the
   `sandbox_patch_summary` nodes, resolves repo/base per task).
2. NO connection held: one pre-queue gate evidence node per task via the
   existing `_record_typed_verification_gate_node` (kind `aggregate_verdict`,
   stage `merge_queue_pre_queue:implementation`, status `approved`). The store's
   `enqueue` requires a non-null `pre_queue_gate_evidence_id`; the per-task
   contract verdict was already validated at sandbox capture/promotion time, so
   this node is the durable typed gate attesting that. `_record_typed_
   verification_gate_node` acquires its OWN pool connection — recording it
   between phase 1 and phase 3 (not while a merge-queue connection is held)
   avoids holding two pool connections at once (pool-exhaustion safety).
3. ONE connection: `MergeQueueStore(conn).enqueue(MergeQueueItemCreate(...))`
   per task — ONE queue item per task, `integration_lane="task:{task_id}"`,
   one `TaskCoverageCreate`, one `RepoTargetCreate` (repo_id, absolute canonical
   repo_path, base_commit). A per-task lane is always isolation-safe and the
   store enforces single-task coverage for a `task:` lane — so an
   `unknown_write_set` contract is handled without any special grouping logic.

After a fully successful enqueue the splice writes the `dag-task-pending-merge:*`
markers (resume idempotency) and returns `workflow_blocked` with the enqueued
`merge_queue_items` ids appended. The queue worker (08e-3) drains the lanes;
until the full Slice 08 bundle lands the workflow correctly stays blocked
pending queue drain — 08e-2 is the ENQUEUE side only.

### How the readiness guard fails closed

`verify_merge_queue_production_ready` is called in phase 1; if `ready` is false
the helper raises `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` naming the missing dependencies and
the splice returns a `workflow_blocked` `DagExecutionOutcome` whose failure text
states the legacy canonical commit is disabled and is NOT a fallback. The helper
ALSO fails closed when: the Slice 08 modules are absent; no typed
`ExecutionControlStore` / DB pool is configured; `feature_root` is `None`; a
task has no contract / no persisted contract id / no patch evidence ids; a
patch evidence node is missing, foreign-feature, or wrong-kind; a single task
has cross-repo patch evidence; the patch-evidence repo disagrees with the
contract repo; the canonical repo is not a git repo; or the base commit cannot
be resolved. No path falls back to `_commit_repos` — standing "no silent
degradation" non-negotiable.

### How the post-DAG gates / post-test guard are preserved

The splice replaces ONLY the body of the pre-existing `if pending_merge_queue:`
early-return block — it does not add a new early return and does not reorder
anything. `_verify_and_fix_group` (the post-DAG verify gates incl.
`_require_dag_verification_graph_approval`) and `_record_precommit_contract_
verdicts` run LATER in `_implement_dag` and were already unreachable for sandbox
results via this same early return — that is unchanged. The post-test guard
lives in the separate `post_test_observation.py` phase, which runs only after
the DAG completes (`terminal_state="complete"`); the splice keeps the
`workflow_blocked` terminal state while the queue is pending, so the post-test
guard is untouched. The durable merge queue WORKER (08e-3) runs the post-apply
gates via the Slice 08e-1 `gate_runner`; the verifier still produces gate
evidence — the queue owns commit/checkpoint authority, gates are not bypassed.

### Compat shims

`_commit_repos`, `_commit_repos_in_root`, `_commit_group` are unchanged. They
remain physically in `implementation.py` and are still called by the legacy
non-sandbox path and by direct unit tests
(`test_dag_expanded_verify.py::test_commit_repos_in_root_*`). Production sandbox
results never reach them — the enqueue early-returns first. This is exactly doc
08 step 7 ("preserve compatibility shims for tests during the landing, but
production call sites must not use `_commit_repos` ...").

### Interfaces verified at file:line (verified-not-assumed)

- `MergeQueueStore.enqueue(MergeQueueItemCreate) -> MergeQueueItem` —
  merge_queue_store.py:358; idempotent on `idempotency_key`, raises
  `MergeQueueError`/`IdempotencyConflict`.
- `MergeQueueItemCreate` fields — merge_queue_store.py:110-128
  (`feature_id`, `dag_sha256`, `group_idx`, `base_commit`, `repo_id`,
  `repo_path`, `head_commit`, `contract_ids`, `patch_evidence_ids`,
  `gate_evidence_ids`, `pre_queue_gate_evidence_id`, `integration_lane`,
  `task_coverage`, `repo_targets`, `payload`).
- `TaskCoverageCreate(task_id, contract_id)` — merge_queue_store.py:98-100.
- `RepoTargetCreate(repo_id, repo_path, base_commit, expected_head="")` —
  merge_queue_store.py:103-107.
- `_validate_create` enqueue preconditions — merge_queue_store.py:972-1009:
  requires `feature_id`+`dag_sha256`, `base_commit`, non-null
  `pre_queue_gate_evidence_id`, non-empty `task_coverage`, non-empty
  `patch_evidence_ids`, non-empty `contract_ids`; a `task:` lane must cover
  exactly one task.
- `_validate_contracts` — merge_queue_store.py:1011-1038: each coverage
  contract must be `active` and its `(feature_id, dag_sha256, group_idx,
  task_id)` must match the lane scope. (This drove the 08e-2 test to enqueue
  at the contract's `group_idx`.)
- `verify_merge_queue_production_ready(conn) -> MergeQueueReadiness` —
  merge_queue_wiring.py:144; `MergeQueueReadiness(ready: bool, missing:
  list[str])` — merge_queue.py:994.
- `sandbox_patch_summary` evidence node payload carries `repo_id`,
  `base_commit`, `diff_artifact_id` — store.py `_patch_summary_fields`:6912-6929
  and `record_patch_summary` kind — store.py:2227-2241.
- dispatcher result note format `dispatcher_attempt_id=...;
  patch_summary_ids=<csv>` — implementation.py:6870-6877; pending-merge-queue
  marker — implementation.py:6294-6297.
- `_record_typed_verification_gate_node(... kind, name, stage, dag_sha256,
  group_idx, status, payload, metadata) -> dict` returning
  `{"persisted": bool, "evidence_node_id": int|None, ...}` —
  implementation.py:2745-2811; `aggregate_verdict` is an accepted
  `VERIFICATION_GRAPH_NODE_KINDS` and `record_verification_graph_node` accepts
  a plain dict — store.py:1537-1551.
- `_execution_control_store_for_runner` returns an `ExecutionControlStore`
  bound to a pool (`store._pool`) — implementation.py:2566-2607;
  `ExecutionControlStore._connection` yields the pool directly when it has no
  `acquire` (bare-connection case) — store.py:3411-3428.
- `MergeQueue` worker `apply_candidate` resolves `Path(target.repo_path)` as
  the canonical filesystem path and checks `pre_apply != base_commit` to decide
  rebase — merge_queue.py:181-270 (so `RepoTargetCreate.repo_path` MUST be the
  absolute canonical repo path; `base_commit` is the apply base).

### Design-doc-vs-reality divergences (trust reality; journaled)

1. Doc 08 § Proposed Interfaces lists `MergeQueue.enqueue(...)`. Reality: the
   `MergeQueue` class (merge_queue.py:144) is WORKER-facing only
   (claim/apply/gates/commit); `enqueue` lives on `MergeQueueStore`
   (merge_queue_store.py:358). 08e-2 enqueues via `MergeQueueStore.enqueue`.
2. STATUS.md/journal cite `implementation.py:5159` for the canonical commit and
   `4166/4203` for the checkpoint commit. The 31k-line file has grown well past
   those numbers; line 5159 today is an unrelated dict literal in
   `_repair_contract_for_paths`, and 4166/4203 today are inside
   `_run_workspace_authority_pre_dispatch`. The canonical commit was located by
   reading the surrounding code (the `stage="implementation"` `_commit_repos`
   call in `_implement_dag`'s group loop), as the slice brief instructed.
3. The task brief named `tests/workflows/develop/test_dag_expanded_verify.py`;
   the actual path is `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py` (no
   `develop/` segment). Used the real path.
4. Doc 08 § Tests describes enqueue rejecting empty/duplicate/noncanonical
   repo_targets and empty coverage in the enqueue transaction. The as-built
   `MergeQueueStore._validate_create` only rejects DUPLICATE repo_id /
   duplicate task_id and empty `task_coverage`/`patch_evidence_ids`/
   `contract_ids` — it does NOT reject empty `repo_targets` or validate
   canonical/outside-root repo paths. 08e-2 does not rely on that stricter
   validation: it always supplies exactly one resolved `RepoTargetCreate` per
   lane and resolves the canonical repo dir + base commit itself (fail-closed).
   The stricter repo-target validation, if still wanted, is an 08c/08f gap, not
   an 08e-2 concern — recorded here for the reviewer.

### Tests

Targeted suites — all pass (Postgres `localhost:5431` was UP; the real-Postgres
merge-queue tests RAN, did not skip):
- `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py` — 255 passed.
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/` — 382 passed (includes the 4 new 08e-2
  tests below and the existing 18 merge-queue-wiring + ~106 merge-queue tests).

Behavior-changed existing tests updated as part of 08e-2 (the splice changes the
blocker message on the implementation worker path when no Postgres pool is
configured — the test fakes have none, so the enqueue fails closed instead of
emitting the old placeholder):
- `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py::test_implement_dag_blocks_
  canonical_mutation_until_durable_merge_queue` — assertion `"canonical
  mutation" in failure` -> `"legacy canonical commit is disabled" in failure`.
  Intent preserved: `_commit_repos` and `_verify_and_fix_group` are still
  asserted NOT to run; outcome is still `workflow_blocked`.
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_implementation_workspace_authority_
  adapter.py::_assert_pending_merge_queue_blocker` (shared helper, 9 tests) —
  now accepts EITHER "canonical mutation" (the unchanged resume/marker +
  enhancement blocker family) OR "legacy canonical commit is disabled" (the new
  implementation-worker enqueue-fail-closed family). The enhancement-group
  blocker test at line ~3212 still asserts "canonical mutation" and is
  unchanged — the enhancement path was deliberately NOT spliced (out of 08e-2
  scope; it has its own canonical commit).

New 08e-2 real-Postgres tests in `test_merge_queue_wiring.py` (4, all pass):
- `test_implementation_worker_enqueue_creates_durable_lane` — the splice
  enqueues a real `merge_queue_items` row: `queued`, per-task `task:` lane
  (verified via the idempotency key), real `base_commit`, real persisted
  `aggregate_verdict` pre-queue gate node, one coverage row, one repo-target
  row with the absolute canonical repo path.
- `test_implementation_worker_enqueue_is_idempotent` — re-running the enqueue
  (resume / crash recovery) returns the same item id; exactly one row exists.
- `test_implementation_worker_enqueue_fails_closed_without_store` — no typed
  store -> `_MergeQueueEnqueueError`, no fallback.
- `test_implementation_worker_enqueue_fails_closed_on_foreign_patch` — patch
  evidence from another feature fails the enqueue closed; zero queue rows
  created (full fail-closed, no partial state).

Hygiene: `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` clean;
`git diff --check` clean.

Pre-existing UNRELATED failures (NOT caused by 08e-2, confirmed): running the
broader `tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py` shows 2 failures —
`test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave` and
`test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints`
("Group 0 checkpoint marker is stale or lacks durable proof — re-running" at
implementation.py:15188, the prior-slice resume checkpoint-freshness logic).
ISOLATION PROOF: temporarily reverting the 08e-2 splice block to its pre-08e-2
placeholder body and re-running these two tests still FAILS identically — so
they are pre-existing failures in the uncommitted Slice 00-08e-1 bundle, not
08e-2 regressions. They also lie outside the 08e-2 targeted test set; they
belong to the 08f global gate / a prior-slice fix. Recorded here for the
reviewer.

### P1/P2-risk areas for a reviewer to scrutinize

- P2 (intermediate state): 08e-2 enqueues but the queue worker is not wired
  until 08e-3. Between the two slices a sandboxed feature enqueues a lane and
  then blocks `workflow_blocked` forever (no drainer). This is by design —
  Slice 08 lands atomically and is gated by the Slice 12 atomic-landing switch;
  no production feature runs the partial path. The reviewer should confirm the
  08e-2/08e-3 ordering is acceptable and that 08e-3 wires the worker before any
  enable.
- P2 (per-task lane granularity): 08e-2 enqueues ONE lane per task. Doc 08
  permits multi-task group lanes as an optimization; per-task lanes are always
  isolation-safe and `GroupMergeCoordinator.coverage` collects all integrated
  lanes for a group, so group checkpointing still works. The reviewer should
  confirm per-task lanes are acceptable (vs. one group lane) for the
  multi-task-group case — it is more queue rows but never an isolation
  violation.
- P2 (pre-queue gate semantics): the pre-queue gate node is recorded as an
  `aggregate_verdict` node asserting the per-task contract verdict (validated
  earlier at sandbox capture) approved. It is NOT a fresh re-run of the Slice 06
  gate graph. The store only requires a non-null `pre_queue_gate_evidence_id`;
  the post-apply gates run in the queue worker (08e-1 `gate_runner`). The
  reviewer should confirm an `aggregate_verdict` node is the right kind for the
  pre-queue gate slot and that this is not summary-only approval (the real
  per-task contract verdict + patch evidence already gate the result before it
  reaches `pending_merge_queue`).
- P3 (connection acquisition): `_merge_queue_connection` reads the private
  `ExecutionControlStore._pool`. A public accessor would be cleaner; deferred
  to avoid widening the `ExecutionControlStore` surface in this slice.

## Slice 08e-2 REVIEW — canonical-commit splice (independent, review-only) (2026-05-21)

Independent review subagent, REVIEW-ONLY (no code changed). Target: the 08e-2
splice that replaced the implementation-worker canonical commit with a durable
`MergeQueue` enqueue. Reviewed the working-tree state of `implementation.py`,
the 4 new + 2 updated tests, and the doc-08 Refactoring Steps 4-7 / Persistence
And Artifact Compatibility sections. Targeted suites re-run green:
`test_merge_queue_wiring.py` 22 passed; `test_dag_expanded_verify.py::test_
implement_dag_blocks_canonical_mutation_until_durable_merge_queue` +
`test_implementation_workspace_authority_adapter.py` 44 passed; `compileall` +
`git diff --check` clean.

### Reviewer summary

The splice is correct and fails closed. Vector 1 (canonical commit removed),
Vector 2 (no silent degradation), Vector 3 (gates/guard preservation), Vector 6
(test honesty), and Vector 7 (pre-existing-failure claim) all PASS. One real
P2 was found in Vector 4 (partial-loop-failure recovery). Two P3s. The three
modeling questions are all judged acceptable. Verdict: acceptable after the
single listed P2 is addressed (or explicitly accepted as a known 08e-3/08f
follow-up).

### Findings

- P2 — implementation.py:5328-5396 (`_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results`
  phase 3) + implementation.py:15448-15480 (`_implement_dag` per-task resume
  loop). PARTIAL-ENQUEUE FAILURE IS NOT RECOVERABLE. `MergeQueueStore.enqueue`
  wraps EACH call in its own `async with self._conn.transaction()`
  (merge_queue_store.py:373) — the phase-3 per-task loop is therefore N separate
  committed transactions, not one. If task 3 of 5 fails mid-loop (transient DB
  error, or an `IdempotencyConflict` on one task), tasks 1-2 are durably
  enqueued (`queued`) while the helper raises `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` and the
  feature returns `workflow_blocked`. The `dag-task-pending-merge:*` resume
  markers are written ONLY on the full-success path (implementation.py:15912-
  15917), so after a partial failure they are absent — but the `dag-task:*`
  attempt markers DO exist (projected by the dispatcher at implementation.py:
  7176 before the enqueue). On resume, the per-task loop at implementation.py:
  15448 reaches the first task with a `completed` `dag-task:*` marker carrying
  the durable-merge-queue note and returns `workflow_blocked` at line 15474-
  15480 WITHOUT ever calling `_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results` again.
  Net effect: tasks 1-2 are orphaned in the queue, tasks 3-5 are never
  enqueued, and no resume path will ever enqueue them — the 08e-3 worker drains
  the queue but does not enqueue, and `GroupMergeCoordinator` coverage will then
  see permanently-missing task ids and block the group checkpoint forever. This
  contradicts doc-08 acceptance "Group checkpoint is atomic from the workflow
  perspective" for the multi-task-group case. Probability is low (phase 1
  validates all lanes and phase 2 records all gate nodes BEFORE phase 3, so
  phase 3 only fails on a transient DB fault or a genuine idempotency conflict),
  but the consequence is an unrecoverable stuck feature. SUGGESTED FIX: make the
  phase-3 loop transactionally atomic — open one `conn.transaction()` around the
  whole per-task loop so a mid-loop failure rolls back tasks 1-2 (the per-call
  `enqueue` transactions would nest as savepoints); OR make resume re-drive the
  enqueue for any task whose `dag-task:*` marker exists but which has no
  `merge_queue_items` row, instead of short-circuiting at line 15474.

- P3 — implementation.py:5283-5324 (phase 2) with implementation.py:6669. The
  pre-queue `aggregate_verdict` node is recorded with `status="approved"`
  UNCONDITIONALLY for every lane. For a `completed` sandbox result this is
  truthful — the dispatcher's `capture_patch` sets `validate_contract=True`
  only when `response.status == "completed"` (implementation.py:6669), so the
  per-task contract was validated against the captured patch at sandbox-capture
  time. But a `partial` `ImplementationResult` (a valid non-blocked status —
  models/outputs.py:1027) ALSO carries the durable-merge-queue marker and
  enters `pending_merge_queue`, and for it `validate_contract` is False, so its
  contract was NOT validated at capture. The splice still stamps an `approved`
  pre-queue node for it. Not a safety hole — the queue worker's post-apply gates
  (08e-1 `gate_runner`) are the real enforcement — but the node's `approved`
  claim is not strictly earned for `partial` results. SUGGESTED FIX: either skip
  enqueue / fail closed for `partial`-status results, or carry the actual
  capture-time contract-validation outcome into the pre-queue node status rather
  than hardcoding `approved`.

- P3 — tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_wiring.py:1114. The
  `test_implementation_worker_enqueue_fails_closed_on_foreign_patch` test ends
  with the comment "No queue item was created — the enqueue fully failed
  closed" but has NO assertion verifying zero `merge_queue_items` rows. The
  fail-closed-with-no-partial-state claim is asserted only implicitly (the
  enqueue never reaches phase 3 because the foreign-patch check is in phase 1).
  SUGGESTED FIX: add `assert await mq_conn.fetchval("SELECT count(*) FROM
  merge_queue_items WHERE feature_id = $1", feature_id) == 0`. Also note: the 4
  new tests exercise the `without_store` and `foreign_patch` fail-closed modes
  directly; the readiness-not-ready, wrong-kind-evidence, cross-repo,
  repo/contract-mismatch, non-git-repo, and partial-loop-failure modes are not
  directly covered (all share the uniform `raise _MergeQueueEnqueueError`
  mechanism, so this is a coverage-completeness note, not a correctness gap).

### Verdict on the three modeling questions

1. Per-task lanes (not one group lane): ACCEPTABLE, not a defect. Doc 08
   line 147-152 / line 805 treats a multi-task group lane as an OPTIONAL
   optimization ("most groups still produce one queue item") and REQUIRES a
   per-task split only for contract isolation (`unknown_write_set`). Per-task
   `task:{id}` lanes are always isolation-safe; the store enforces single-task
   coverage for a `task:` lane (merge_queue_store.py:1005-1008); and
   `GroupMergeCoordinator.coverage` aggregates all integrated lanes per group,
   so the single-projection group checkpoint still holds. Per-task lanes cost
   more queue rows but never violate an acceptance criterion. (A P3-flavoured
   note for 08f: it does diverge from the doc's "most groups produce one queue
   item" expectation and could be revisited for queue-row efficiency.)

2. Worker wired in 08e-3, so 08e-2-only features enqueue then block by design:
   ACCEPTABLE. Doc 08 line 12-15 / 824-830 and IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT line 180-
   182 are explicit that this is a CONSTRUCTION ORDER, not a staged production
   rollout, and that the bundle lands atomically behind the Slice 12
   `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` gate. Enqueue-then-block is the correct
   intermediate state for an enqueue-only slice; no production feature runs the
   partial path. Conditional on 08e-3 wiring the queue worker before any
   enablement — which STATUS.md and the journal both commit to.

3. Pre-queue node is an `aggregate_verdict` evidence node, not a fresh Slice 06
   gate-graph re-run: ACCEPTABLE / correct kind. Doc 08 schema line 193 defines
   `pre_queue_gate_evidence_id` as the "aggregate pre-queue approval proof" —
   the doc does not require a fresh gate-graph run; it requires a non-null
   aggregate attestation. `aggregate_verdict` is an accepted
   `VERIFICATION_GRAPH_NODE_KIND`. The real gating happened earlier (per-task
   contract verdict + patch evidence validated at sandbox capture, verified at
   implementation.py:6669/6103), and the post-apply gates run fresh in the
   queue worker. So this is a durable aggregate attestation, not summary-only
   approval. (The one wrinkle — `partial`-status results — is captured as a P3
   above; it does not change the verdict that `aggregate_verdict` is the right
   kind for the pre-queue slot.)

### Verdict on the pre-existing-failures claim

PLAUSIBLE AND CONFIRMED. Both `tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py::test_
implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave` and `::test_implement_dag_resume_
scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints` fail, but NOT because of the 08e-2 splice.
The `_ImplementationOutputNormalizer.normalize` stamps `_PENDING_DURABLE_MERGE_
QUEUE_NOTE` on EVERY validated `ImplementationResult` (implementation.py:6730,
unconditional) — that normalizer is Slice 04/05 code and is wired into every
implementation dispatch. So every successful dispatch result enters
`pending_merge_queue`, and the `if pending_merge_queue:` early-return block
(which existed PRE-08e-2 as a placeholder `workflow_blocked`, per the 08e-2
IMPLEMENTATION entry) returned `workflow_blocked` before 08e-2 ever touched it.
08e-2 only replaced that block's BODY. Reverting the 08e-2 splice body leaves
the pre-08e-2 placeholder, which still returns `workflow_blocked` for the same
results — so the implementer's "revert → identical failure" isolation proof
holds. `test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave` expects the legacy
`terminal_state == "complete"` and was already broken by the pre-08e-2 durable-
merge-queue scaffolding; it belongs to the 08f global gate. NB: the two tests
fail with DIFFERENT mechanisms — test 1 fails at `assert terminal_state ==
"complete"` (got `workflow_blocked`), test 2 fails with `TypeError: object
NoneType can't be used in 'await' expression` at implementation.py:15310
(its `_ensure_task_worktrees` monkeypatch is a sync `lambda` returning `None`
while the production code `await`s it). Both are splice-unrelated and pre-
existing; the journal's single lumped description ("Group 0 checkpoint marker
is stale", line 15188) is imprecise but the substance of the claim is correct.

### Overall verdict

08e-2 is ACCEPTABLE AFTER the listed P2 is addressed: the partial-enqueue
failure path (implementation.py:5328-5396 + 15448-15480) leaves a multi-task
group permanently stuck with orphaned lanes. Make the phase-3 per-task loop one
transaction, OR make resume re-drive the enqueue for un-enqueued tasks. The two
P3s (unconditional `approved` pre-queue node for `partial` results; missing
zero-rows assertion in the foreign-patch test) and the per-task-lane doc
divergence can fold into 08f. Vectors 1/2/3/6/7 and all three modeling
questions pass.


## Slice 08e-2 REMEDIATION + COMPLETE — P2 atomic enqueue + P3a/P3b (2026-05-21)

Remediation of the three Slice 08e-2 REVIEW findings. The P2 is fully fixed;
both P3s are closed. 08e-2 is now COMPLETE.

### P2 FIX (BLOCKING) — partial-enqueue is now atomic and recoverable

`_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results` phase 3 (implementation.py, the
per-task `MergeQueueStore.enqueue` loop) was N independent committed
transactions: `MergeQueueStore.enqueue` wraps each call in its own
`async with self._conn.transaction()` (merge_queue_store.py:373), and the
splice's phase-3 `async with _merge_queue_connection(store) as conn` opened NO
transaction — so a mid-loop failure left tasks 1..N-1 durably enqueued
(orphaned) while the feature blocked, and the resume loop short-circuited at
the first `dag-task:*` marker carrying the durable-merge-queue note WITHOUT
re-driving the enqueue.

Fix: wrap the entire phase-3 per-task loop in ONE outer
`async with conn.transaction()`. asyncpg nests `Connection.transaction()` on an
already-open connection as a SAVEPOINT (verified in `asyncpg/transaction.py`
`Transaction.start`: when `con._top_xact` is already set the inner transaction
emits `SAVEPOINT ...`, not `BEGIN`). So each per-task `enqueue` is now a
savepoint inside the splice's outer transaction:
- A successful per-task `enqueue` releases its savepoint (changes stay in the
  outer transaction, uncommitted).
- A mid-loop failure (transient DB fault, `IdempotencyConflict`, contract
  rejection) is re-raised as `_MergeQueueEnqueueError`, escapes the outer
  `async with conn.transaction()`, which issues `ROLLBACK` — undoing EVERY
  prior lane in the loop. Result: ZERO `merge_queue_items` rows persist.
- The feature then blocks cleanly (`workflow_blocked`), and a resume re-drives
  the whole enqueue from scratch — no orphaned lanes, fail-closed, recoverable.

The existing single-call `enqueue` API is UNCHANGED — savepoint nesting is
automatic; no transaction-aware variant or bulk method was needed (the
preferred "add a new path, don't refactor working code" outcome, achieved with
zero change to `MergeQueueStore`). The `enqueue`-internal
`except asyncpg.UniqueViolationError` idempotent-resolution path still works:
after a savepoint rollback the subsequent `_resolve_existing` query runs inside
the still-valid outer transaction.

Secondary change: the per-lane `_log_feature_event` calls were moved OUT of the
transaction. Successful enqueues are collected into a list inside the
transaction and the `dag_merge_queue_enqueued` events are emitted only AFTER
the transaction commits — so a rolled-back partial enqueue never emits a
spurious "enqueued" event. `_log_feature_event` writes to `runner.feature_store`
(a separate store from the merge-queue pool) and is exception-tolerant; moving
it post-commit also avoids any cross-store entanglement during the transaction.

### P3a OUTCOME (FIXED) — fail closed for a non-`completed` result

`_ImplementationOutputNormalizer.normalize` stamps `_PENDING_DURABLE_MERGE_
QUEUE_NOTE` on EVERY validated `ImplementationResult` (implementation.py:6730,
unconditional — `completed`, `partial`, or `blocked`), while the dispatcher's
`capture_patch` sets `validate_contract=True` only when
`response.status == "completed"` (implementation.py:6669). So a `partial`
result's deliverable contract was never validated against its captured patch,
yet it entered `pending_merge_queue` and got an `approved` pre-queue
`aggregate_verdict` node — stamping `approved` on unvalidated work, a silent
degradation.

Fix (chosen: refuse to enqueue): `_resolve_merge_queue_lane_inputs` now fails
closed with `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` for any result whose `status` is not
`completed`. Rationale per doc 08 § Tests ("Enqueue requires active contracts,
immutable patch evidence, and pre-queue gate evidence") and the standing
"no silent degradation" non-negotiable: the merge queue owns CANONICAL commit
and must only receive fully-completed, contract-validated lanes. A `partial`
result is not a complete deliverable. This is recoverable: the per-task resume
loop only short-circuits a `completed` `dag-task:*` marker
(implementation.py:15473) — a `partial` task falls to the re-run branch and
simply re-runs on the next resume. The `ImplementationResult.status` model
already supports `completed | blocked | partial` (models/outputs.py:1027), so
no model change was needed. A refused `partial` result does not orphan a
`dag-task-pending-merge:*` marker — those are written only AFTER a fully
successful enqueue (the splice's `except _MergeQueueEnqueueError` returns
`workflow_blocked` before the marker write).

### P3b OUTCOME (ALREADY SATISFIED) — zero-rows assertion present

`test_implementation_worker_enqueue_fails_closed_on_foreign_patch` in
`test_merge_queue_wiring.py` ALREADY contains the
`SELECT count(*) FROM merge_queue_items ... == 0` assertion (the test file is
untracked, so the assertion was added after the review-only snapshot was
taken — the review reviewed an earlier state). No change required; verified the
assertion is present and the "No queue item was created" comment is accurate.

### Tests — new + updated

New real-Postgres tests in `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_
wiring.py`:
- `test_implementation_worker_enqueue_partial_failure_rolls_back_all` (P2) — a
  two-task enqueue where task 2's contract is `superseded`. Phase-1 lane
  resolution does not check contract status, so both lanes resolve and both
  pre-queue gate nodes record; task 1 enqueues into a savepoint, task 2's
  `enqueue` raises `MergeQueueError` from `_validate_contracts`, the outer
  transaction ROLLS BACK. Asserts `count(*) merge_queue_items == 0` (task 1 is
  NOT orphaned). Recovery half: flips the contract to `active`, re-runs, asserts
  BOTH lanes enqueue (`count == 2`) — proving fail-closed + recoverable.
- `test_implementation_worker_enqueue_fails_closed_on_partial_result` (P3a) — a
  `partial`-status result carrying the pending-durable-merge-queue note is
  refused with `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` ("not 'completed'"); asserts zero
  `merge_queue_items` rows.

Test helper `_insert_contract` extended with an optional `status` kwarg
(default `"active"`) so the partial-failure test can stage a `superseded`
contract; all existing callers are unaffected.

### Verification — all green

- `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` — clean.
- `git diff --check` — clean.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py -q` — 255 passed.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q` — 384 passed (was
  382; +2 new 08e-2 tests). Postgres `localhost:5431` UP — real-PG tests RAN,
  none skipped.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_wiring.py
  -q` — 24 passed (was 22; +2 new tests).
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py -q` — 2 failed, 25
  passed: ONLY the 2 known pre-existing failures
  (`test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave`,
  `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints`), failure
  mechanisms exactly as the 08e-2 IMPLEMENTATION entry documents (one fails at
  `assert terminal_state == "complete"`, the other `TypeError` on the sync
  `_ensure_task_worktrees` monkeypatch). NO new failures introduced by the
  remediation. These 2 remain 08f global-gate items.

### Files changed in this remediation

- `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py` — P2: phase-3
  loop wrapped in one `conn.transaction()`, event logging deferred post-commit;
  P3a: non-`completed` result fail-closed in `_resolve_merge_queue_lane_inputs`.
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_wiring.py` — 2 new tests;
  `_insert_contract` `status` kwarg.
- `docs/execution-control-plane/implementation-journal.md`,
  `docs/execution-control-plane/implementation-decisions.jsonl` — this entry.

### Outcome

08e-2 is COMPLETE. P2 fixed (atomic all-or-nothing enqueue, no orphaned lanes,
fail-closed, recoverable on resume); P3a fixed (fail closed for non-`completed`
results); P3b already satisfied. No new test failures. STATUS.md advanced to
point the next iteration at Slice 08e-3.


## Slice 08e-3a IMPLEMENTATION — BRIEF (2026-05-21)

Slice 08e-3 (per STATUS.md) is three parts: (1) a queue-worker DRAIN of the
`task:{id}` lanes 08e-2 enqueues; (2) the post-DAG CHECKPOINT splice replacing
`_commit_group`/`dag-group:*` with `GroupMergeCoordinator` checkpoint success;
(3) routing queue failures via `failure_router.py`. Loop discipline mandates ONE
verified sub-deliverable per iteration and this touches the 31k-line
`implementation.py`, so this iteration delivers **08e-3a only — the
queue-worker drain plus the drain-path failure routing**. The post-DAG
checkpoint splice is deferred to 08e-3b (next iteration).

Plan: add a new drain-worker helper to `implementation.py` that builds the
Slice 08e-1 collaborators (`build_apply_input_provider`, `build_gate_runner`,
`build_no_dirty_recorder`) and the Slice 08d `MergeQueue` worker, then drains
every `queued`/expired-`leased` `task:{id}` lane for a feature through the
existing worker methods `claim -> apply_candidate -> run_required_gates ->
commit_and_prove_clean -> mark_integrated`, leaving lanes at `integrated`.
Drain-path apply/gate/commit failures route through the Slice 07
`failure_router` via the existing `_failure_router_for_runner` +
`RouterFailureObservation` pattern already used by `_typed_direct_route_payload`.
This sub-chunk does NOT wire the drain into `_implement_dag`'s control flow and
does NOT touch the checkpoint splice — both are 08e-3b (the invocation point is
intertwined with the checkpoint that consumes `integrated` lanes). The
`_commit_repos`/`_commit_repos_in_root`/`_commit_group` compat shims stay
untouched. Verify: hygiene gate + `test_dag_expanded_verify.py` +
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/` + new real-Postgres drain tests.


## Slice 08e-3a IMPLEMENTATION — COMPLETE (2026-05-21)

Delivered **08e-3a only**: the durable merge queue DRAIN worker plus its
drain-path failure routing. The post-DAG checkpoint splice (the second of
08e-3's three parts) and the wiring of the drain into `_implement_dag`'s
control flow are deferred to **08e-3b** (split rationale below).

### What was built and where (file:line)

All new code is in `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`,
inserted ADDITIVELY right after `_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results`
(08e-2's enqueue helper). `_implement_dag`'s control flow was NOT modified.

* Import block (`implementation.py:158-192`, the Slice 08 `try/except ImportError`
  block) extended with: `MergeQueueError as MergeQueueStoreError`,
  `LeaseFencedError as MergeQueueLeaseFencedError` (from `merge_queue_store`);
  `GateDecision as MergeQueueGateDecision`,
  `build_apply_input_provider as build_merge_queue_apply_input_provider`,
  `build_gate_runner as build_merge_queue_gate_runner`,
  `build_no_dirty_recorder as build_merge_queue_no_dirty_recorder` (from
  `merge_queue_wiring`); `LeaseToken as MergeQueueLeaseToken`,
  `MergeQueue as MergeQueueWorker` (from the `merge_queue` worker module);
  and `git_service as merge_queue_git_service` (from `..execution`). Every new
  name has a matching `= None` fallback in the `except ImportError` arm,
  mirroring the existing 08e-2 imports.
* `_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS` (`implementation.py:~5491`) — the static
  map from a `MergeQueue` worker `failure_class` string to the Slice 07
  `failure_router` `(failure_class, failure_type)` route-table pair. All seven
  mapped pairs were verified present in `failure_router.ROUTE_TABLE` (programmatic
  check, see Verification). `sandbox_isolation`/`worktree_alias`/`acl_workability`
  are intentionally absent — the drain path cannot produce them (they are
  workspace/sandbox classes routed before enqueue).
* `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure(...)` (`implementation.py:~5505`) — routes one
  drain-path queue failure through the Slice 07 router. Mirrors the existing
  `_typed_direct_route_payload` router-usage pattern exactly: resolves the router
  via `_failure_router_for_runner`, builds a typed `RouterFailureObservation`
  (`source="merge_queue"`, `deterministic=True`, a stable
  `failure:{feature}:merge-queue-drain:g{group}:item-{id}:...` idempotency key),
  then `record` → `decide` → `mark_route_started`. An unmapped class falls back
  to `("unknown","unclassified")` (the router quiesces it) — fails closed, never
  drops a failure. Returns a bounded dict (empty when the Slice 07 module is
  absent — the worker's own `_fail` still records the lane terminal, so routing
  being unavailable never loses the typed state).
* `_merge_queue_post_apply_gate_decision(item)` (`implementation.py:~5575`) — the
  deterministic post-apply `GateDecisionProvider` the drain feeds to
  `merge_queue_wiring.build_gate_runner`. For each repo target it runs
  `git diff --check` against the applied-but-uncommitted tree; conflict markers
  or whitespace errors → `GateDecision(approved=False, failure_class="merge_conflict")`,
  else approved. This is a REAL non-redundant check: `apply_candidate`'s own
  `git apply --3way --check` (per `git_service.apply_check`'s docstring) does
  NOT catch conflict markers a 3-way merge leaves behind. Applied-path contract
  scope is NOT re-checked — `apply_candidate` already validates it before the
  lane reaches `verifying`.
* `_MergeQueueDrainResult` (`implementation.py:~5640`) — a small `__slots__`
  result object: `item_id`, `task_ids`, `terminal_status`, `result_commit`,
  `failure_class`, `detail`, `routed_failure`, plus an `integrated` property.
* `_drain_one_merge_queue_lane(runner, feature, worker, item, *, dag_sha256,
  stage)` (`implementation.py:~5705`) — drives ONE claimed (`leased`) lane
  through the Slice 08d `MergeQueue` worker methods in the doc-08 order:
  `apply_candidate` → reload → `run_required_gates` → reload →
  `commit_and_prove_clean` → reload → `mark_integrated`. The row is reloaded
  via `worker.get(item_id)` after each successful step so the next call has the
  fresh lease version and proof ids. Any worker-method failure: the worker has
  already recorded the lane `failed` via its own `_fail`; the local async
  `_fail_result` closure additionally routes the typed `failure_class` via
  `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` and reloads the authoritative terminal
  status. It never mutates canonical repos itself and never falls back to a
  legacy commit.
* `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature(runner, feature, *, dag_sha256,
  stage, lease_owner, max_lanes)` (`implementation.py:~5825`) — the drain
  entrypoint. Builds the four collaborators from one held connection
  (`build_merge_queue_apply_input_provider(conn)`,
  `build_merge_queue_gate_runner(ExecutionControlStore(conn),
  _merge_queue_post_apply_gate_decision)`,
  `build_merge_queue_no_dirty_recorder(ExecutionControlStore(conn))`),
  constructs `MergeQueueWorker(MergeQueueStore(conn), apply_input_provider,
  gate_runner=..., no_dirty_recorder=...)`, then loops up to `max_lanes` (256):
  `queue_store.claim(feature_id, lease_owner)` → `_drain_one_merge_queue_lane`
  → emit a `dag_merge_queue_integrated` or `dag_merge_queue_drain_failed`
  feature event → repeat until `claim` returns `None`. Returns one
  `_MergeQueueDrainResult` per lane in claim order.

### How the drain is invoked and how it drains `task:{id}` lanes

08e-3a delivers the drain as a wired, callable, fully-tested unit;
`_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` is the public entrypoint. It is NOT
yet called from `_implement_dag` — that invocation point is intertwined with
the checkpoint splice (08e-3b reads `integrated` lanes and projects
`dag-group:*`), so wiring the call now would force touching `_implement_dag`'s
control flow for a half-finished feature. The drain loop drains `task:{id}`
lanes by calling `MergeQueueStore.claim` repeatedly: `claim` is a single atomic
`UPDATE ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1 RETURNING` that selects exactly the
`queued` or expired-`leased` rows — i.e. the lanes 08e-2 enqueued. Each claimed
lane runs through the four `MergeQueue` worker methods and ends `integrated`
(committed + no-dirty proof recorded, group checkpoint not yet projected).

### How drain failures route through `failure_router`

Each `MergeQueue` worker method returns a result carrying a `failure_class` on
failure (the worker's `_fail` has already recorded the lane `failed`).
`_drain_one_merge_queue_lane`'s `_fail_result` closure calls
`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure`, which maps the class via
`_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS` and runs the Slice 07
`record/decide/mark_route_started` cycle — e.g. `merge_conflict` →
`(merge_conflict, patch_apply_conflict)` → route action `retry_merge`;
`commit_hygiene` → `(commit_hygiene, commit_hook_failed)` →
`run_commit_hygiene_repair`. A sibling lane's failure is non-fatal: the drain
loop records that lane `failed` (routed) and continues claiming the next lane.

### How the post-DAG gates / post-test guard are preserved

Untouched. `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` adds a new function and is
not on the post-DAG-gate or post-test code path; `_implement_dag`'s control
flow, the six post-DAG gates (`_record_post_dag_gate_proof` &c.), and the
post-test guard are byte-for-byte unchanged. The drain's `run_required_gates`
post-apply gate is a *separate* deterministic per-lane gate (a `git diff
--check`), not the post-DAG verify; the verifier still runs post-DAG in
`execute()`. The `_commit_repos`/`_commit_repos_in_root`/`_commit_group`
doc-08-step-7 compat shims are likewise untouched.

### Interfaces verified at file:line (verified, not assumed)

* `MergeQueue` worker (`workflows/develop/execution/merge_queue.py`):
  `__init__(store, apply_input_provider, gate_runner=None, no_dirty_recorder=None)`
  (:147); `claim` (:161), `get` (:176); `apply_candidate(item, token) ->
  MergeApplyResult` (:181) — `.applied`/`.failure_class`/`.detail`;
  `run_required_gates(item, token) -> MergeGateResult` (:409) —
  `.approved`/`.failure_class`/`.detail`; `commit_and_prove_clean(item, token)
  -> MergeCommitResult` (:489) — `.committed`/`.failure_class`/`.detail`;
  `mark_integrated(item, token, commit_result) -> MergeQueueItem` (:624);
  `LeaseToken.for_item(item)` (:42-48).
* `MergeQueueStore` (`execution_control/merge_queue_store.py`):
  `claim(feature_id, lease_owner, *, ttl_seconds=300) -> MergeQueueItem | None`
  (:514) — atomic `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` claim of `queued`/expired-`leased`;
  `get` (:423). `MergeQueueItem` fields used — `id`, `group_idx`,
  `task_coverage[].task_id`, `repo_targets[].{repo_id,repo_path,status}`,
  `status`, `result_commit`, `merge_proof_evidence_id`,
  `post_apply_gate_evidence_id`, `commit_proof_evidence_id`,
  `checkpoint_projection_id`, `payload` (:166-200). `MergeQueueError` (:55) /
  `LeaseFencedError` (:59).
* `merge_queue_wiring.py`: `build_apply_input_provider(conn)` (:210),
  `build_gate_runner(store: ExecutionControlStore, gate_decision_provider)`
  (:404), `build_no_dirty_recorder(store: ExecutionControlStore)` (:470);
  `GateDecision(approved, failure_class, detail, input_evidence_ids,
  verdict_payload)` frozen dataclass (:311-329); `GateDecisionProvider =
  Callable[[MergeQueueItem], Awaitable[GateDecision]]` (:333).
* `failure_router.py`: `ROUTE_TABLE` (:1379) — programmatically verified to
  contain all seven mapped pairs plus `("unknown","unclassified")`;
  `FailureObservation` fields (:579-593); `FailureRouter.record/decide/
  mark_route_started` (:1506/1525/1572).
* `git_service.run_git(cwd, *args, check=False) -> GitCommandResult` with
  `.ok` (:70); `head_commit` (:114), `working_tree_clean` (:167).
* `implementation.py` existing helpers reused: `_failure_router_for_runner`
  (:547), `_execution_control_store_for_runner` (:2586),
  `_merge_queue_connection` (:5019, the 08e-2 pool→connection contextmanager),
  `_log_feature_event` (:1648), and the `_typed_direct_route_payload`
  router-usage pattern (:667).

### Doc-vs-reality divergences

* Doc 08 § Proposed Interfaces shows `MergeQueue.enqueue` and a
  `GroupMergeCoordinator.checkpoint_group(coverage, token: FeatureLeaseToken)`.
  Reality (and the 08d/08e-1 build): `enqueue` lives on `MergeQueueStore`, not
  `MergeQueue`; `checkpoint_group` takes a `LeaseToken`. The 08e-2 journal
  already recorded the `enqueue` divergence; 08e-3a touches neither (drain only
  uses the four worker methods + `claim`).
* Doc 08 § Refactoring Step 6 lists `checkpoint_contradiction` among the queue
  failure classes to route; the `failure_router` route table spells the
  type-level pair as `(checkpoint_contradiction, checkpoint_after_failed_gate)`
  (the only `checkpoint_contradiction` pair). Mapped accordingly.
* The doc-08 4166/4203 line numbers remain stale (the brief flagged this); the
  drain located its anchors by reading the code, not the literals. No
  literal-line edit was made.

### Split rationale — what 08e-3b / remaining work is

STATUS.md's 08e-3 has THREE parts; this iteration did part (1) only — the
drain worker + drain-path routing — because (a) loop discipline mandates ONE
verified sub-deliverable per iteration, (b) `implementation.py` is 31k+ lines
and the change had to stay surgical/additive, and (c) parts (2)/(3) below are
coupled. Deferred to **08e-3b**:
1. The post-DAG CHECKPOINT splice — replace the legacy `_commit_group` /
   `_record_dag_group_commit_proof` / `dag-group:*` projection path with
   `GroupMergeCoordinator.checkpoint_group` success (consuming the `integrated`
   lanes this drain produces). This is the one that needs `build_checkpoint_
   projector` (deliberately NOT imported in 08e-3a).
2. Wiring `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` into `_implement_dag`'s
   resume/control flow at the right point — coupled to (1) because the drain
   hands `integrated` lanes to the checkpoint, so the invocation point and the
   checkpoint splice land together.
3. Any drain-failure → workflow-outcome translation at that call site (the
   drain returns typed results today; turning a routed `merge_conflict` into a
   `DagExecutionOutcome` belongs with the call-site wiring).

### Tests — 9 new, all real-Postgres

New file `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_drain.py` (9
tests, real Postgres via the `mq_conn` fixture + real on-disk git repos; SKIP
cleanly when Postgres `localhost:5431` is down):
* `test_drain_advances_a_clean_lane_to_integrated` — happy path: a queued lane
  drains to `integrated` with a real canonical commit; checkpoint columns stay
  empty (checkpoint is 08e-3b).
* `test_drain_returns_empty_when_no_lanes_are_claimable`.
* `test_drain_drains_multiple_lanes_for_one_feature` — the loop integrates
  every queued lane.
* `test_drain_routes_a_merge_conflict_through_the_failure_router` — a stale
  patch fails closed, routes `(merge_conflict, patch_apply_conflict)` →
  `retry_merge`, repo untouched (no legacy commit), router row recorded.
* `test_drain_routes_a_commit_hook_failure_as_commit_hygiene` — pre-commit hook
  rejection routes `(commit_hygiene, commit_hook_failed)` →
  `run_commit_hygiene_repair`, repo reset clean.
* `test_drain_continues_after_a_failed_sibling_lane` — a failing lane does not
  strand the clean sibling.
* `test_drain_fails_closed_without_a_typed_store` — `_MergeQueueEnqueueError`,
  no legacy-commit fallback.
* `test_post_apply_gate_decision_approves_a_clean_applied_tree`.
* `test_post_apply_gate_decision_rejects_conflict_markers`.

### Verification — all green

* `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` — clean (exit 0).
* `git diff --check` — clean.
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_drain.py
  -q` — 9 passed (real Postgres up).
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py
  tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q` — 648 passed (was 639 baseline + 9
  new drain tests; Postgres `localhost:5431` up — real-PG tests ran, none
  skipped). ZERO new failures.
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py -q` — 2 failed, 25
  passed: ONLY the 2 known pre-existing failures
  (`test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave` at `assert
  terminal_state == "complete"`; `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_
  regroup_checkpoints` `TypeError` on the sync `_ensure_task_worktrees`
  monkeypatch). NO new failures — these 2 remain 08f items.

### P1/P2-risk areas for a reviewer

* P2-risk — the drain is wired + tested but NOT invoked from `_implement_dag`.
  This is the deliberate 08e-3a/08e-3b split; a reviewer should confirm the
  split is acceptable and that 08e-3b will wire the call site. Until 08e-3b,
  08e-2's enqueued lanes are not yet drained in a live run (they sit `queued`)
  — expected intermediate state of the atomic construction order (doc 08
  §§ 12-15, IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT lines 180-182).
* P2-risk — the deterministic post-apply gate is a `git diff --check` only.
  Doc 08 step 11 says "re-run required deterministic gates against the applied
  canonical state"; 08e-3a treats the conflict-marker check as that gate
  (applied-path contract scope is already enforced by `apply_candidate`). A
  reviewer should confirm no other deterministic post-apply gate is expected
  pre-checkpoint, or that it belongs in 08e-3b's checkpoint gate.
* P3-risk — the drain holds ONE connection for the whole drain loop; the
  per-lane feature advisory lock + lane lease (acquired/released inside the
  worker methods) are what serialize canonical mutation, which is correct, but
  a reviewer may want a `heartbeat` during a long per-lane gate/commit (the
  worker methods do not heartbeat today; the 5-min lease TTL covers a normal
  drain). Not a correctness gap for the current synchronous single-worker
  drain — flagged for the 08g six-vector review.

## Slice 08e-3a REVIEW — durable merge queue DRAIN worker (independent, review-only) (2026-05-21)

Independent review of Slice 08e-3a — the durable merge queue DRAIN worker plus
drain-path failure routing, all ADDITIVE in
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py` after
`_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results`. Reviewed working-tree state (the
whole repo is an uncommitted Slice 00–08e bundle). Review-only: no code changed.

### Reviewer summary

The drain is well-built and surgical. The claim/drive/reload loop is correct,
the lease fencing genuinely prevents double-apply, no path silently falls back
to the legacy `_commit_group`/direct commit, and the 7 failure-class → route
pairs are all present in `failure_router.ROUTE_TABLE`. Three findings: zero P1,
**three P2**, one P3. The two correctness P2s are (a) an uncaught-exception hole
that breaks the drain's stated sibling-isolation contract, and (b) the post-apply
`git diff --check` gate inspecting the wrong tree state (unstaged) when
`apply_candidate`'s `git apply --index` leaves applied content STAGED. The third
P2 is the absence of any `recover_expired` wiring / crash-recovery test for a
lane that crashes after canonical apply starts. None are live hazards today
(the drain is uninvoked — see vector 6), but all must close before 08e-3b wires
the call site. Verdict: **acceptable after the listed P2 fixes**.

### Findings

**P2-1 — `implementation.py:5759` / `:5777` / `:5793` (`_drain_one_merge_queue_lane`)
— a RAISED worker exception aborts the whole drain loop and strands sibling
lanes.** `apply_candidate` / `run_required_gates` / `commit_and_prove_clean` are
called with NO try/except. The `MergeQueue` worker methods only catch their own
`_ApplyFailure`; a `LeaseFencedError` / `MergeQueueError` / asyncpg error raised
from `_store.transition` (`merge_queue.py:240`, `:445`), `acquire_feature_lock`
(`:224`, `:527`), `advance_repo_target` (`:264`, `:329`, `:565`, `:588`), or
`record_merge_proof` (`:348`) PROPAGATES out of the worker method, out of
`_drain_one_merge_queue_lane`, and out of `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature`'s
`for` loop. Only `mark_integrated` is wrapped (`:5810-5817`). Because the worker
methods do NOT heartbeat and `claim` sets a 5-min TTL, a lane whose
gate/commit/hooks exceed 5 minutes hits a fenced `transition` → `LeaseFencedError`
→ the drain aborts. The just-claimed lane is left mid-flight and every still-`queued`
sibling is stranded — directly contradicting the function docstring and the
08e-3a journal claim "a sibling lane's conflict must not strand the others"
(that holds only for *returned* `applied=False` failures, not *raised* ones). Not
P1: no double-apply, no data loss, no silent fallback — the lease fence is the
safety mechanism working correctly, and a re-run re-`claim`s the stranded
siblings; but the per-lane isolation contract is violated for the raised-exception
class, and a transient asyncpg blip on lane 1 strands lanes 2..N.
Suggested fix: wrap each worker-method call in `_drain_one_merge_queue_lane` in a
`try/except (MergeQueueStoreError, MergeQueueLeaseFencedError, Exception)` that
funnels into `_fail_result` (reload the authoritative status — a fenced worker
means a recovery worker now owns the lane), so a raised failure is recorded +
routed like a returned one and the loop continues.

**P2-2 — `implementation.py:5629` (`_merge_queue_post_apply_gate_decision`) — the
post-apply conflict-marker gate runs `git diff --check`, which inspects the
UNSTAGED worktree, but `apply_candidate` stages the applied patch into the
INDEX; the gate therefore misses staged conflict markers.** `apply_candidate`
applies via `git apply --index --3way` (`git_service.py:261`), which STAGES the
applied hunks. The gate then runs `run_git(repo_path, "diff", "--check")` with
NO `--cached` — `git diff --check` compares worktree-vs-index, so for a patch
fully staged with conflict-marker lines (worktree == index) it reports clean and
the gate FALSELY APPROVES. The codebase already knows this: `working_tree_clean`
(`git_service.py:167-174`, the no-dirty proof) deliberately checks BOTH
`git diff --quiet` AND `git diff --cached --quiet`. A genuine 3-way *conflict*
is already caught earlier by `apply_patch`'s non-zero exit inside
`apply_candidate`, so there is no live miss while the drain is uninvoked — but
the gate as the journal advertises it ("catches conflict markers a 3-way merge
leaves behind") does not do so for the staged state `apply_candidate` actually
produces. This is sharper than the implementer's "is `git diff --check` enough"
P2-candidate framing: the gate inspects the wrong tree, not merely a minimal
one. Suggested fix: `git diff --check HEAD` (inspects worktree+index vs HEAD), or
add a second `git diff --cached --check` call.

**P2-3 — `implementation.py:5826` (`_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature`) — no
crash-recovery path: the drain wires `claim` but never `recover_expired`, so a
lane that crashes after canonical apply starts is never recovered.** `claim`'s
predicate (`merge_queue_store.py:538-539`) matches only `queued` OR
expired-`leased` rows — by design it NEVER re-takes an `applying`/`verifying`/
`committing` row. Per doc 08 § Lease Semantics, those states "are recoverable
only through `recover_expired`", and the doc 08 "Rollback And Recovery Table"
mandates a recovery action for each (`applying` → reset to `pre_apply_head` then
reapply/fail; `verifying`/`committing` similar). The drain calls neither
`recover_expired` nor anything equivalent. So if the drain process dies mid-lane
(after `apply_candidate` advanced the lane to `verifying`, before
`mark_integrated`), a re-run of `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature`
`claim`-skips the stuck lane and silently leaves it mid-flight forever. There is
no crash/re-drive test among the 9 (see vector 8). Not P1 only because the drain
is uninvoked (vector 6) so the unrecoverable state cannot occur in production
yet, and the typed row faithfully records the stuck state (no data loss); but it
is a missing safety mechanism + missing safety test that doc 08 explicitly
requires, and 08e-3b MUST wire `recover_expired` (and a crash-recovery test)
before the drain goes live. Suggested fix: 08e-3b adds a `recover_expired` pass
(or documents the recovery owner) and a crash-after-apply/before-mark_integrated
re-drive test.

**P3-1 — `implementation.py:5543-5546` + `:5557` (`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure`)
— a drain `contract_violation` dead-ends at `quiesce` instead of scoped product
repair, because the routed observation carries no `target_paths`/`contract_ids`.**
The drain maps `contract_violation` → `(contract_violation, outside_allowed_paths)`,
which in `ROUTE_TABLE` is `run_product_repair` with `allow_product_repair=True`
(`failure_router.py:1044-1050`, `_route` line 974-980 — `outside_allowed_paths`
∈ `_SCOPED_CONTRACT_PRODUCT_TYPES`). `record()` therefore does NOT raise. But
`decide()` calls `_allows_product_repair` (`failure_router.py:1761-1777`), which
needs `repair_scope["target_paths"]` AND (`target_contract_ids` OR an authorized
source verdict); the drain's observation `payload` only carries
`idempotency_key`/`merge_queue_item_id`/`group_idx`/`stage`/`detail` and
`evidence_ids=[]`, so `_repair_scope` yields no `target_paths` → `decide()`
DOWNGRADES the action to `quiesce` (`:1545`). This is FAIL-CLOSED and safe (no
broad product repair without typed evidence, exactly the Slice 07 non-negotiable
and doc 08 step 6 "not a model repair prompt") — hence only P3, not a P1
swallowed-failure. But a genuine contract escape (the agent wrote files outside
its contract — a real product defect `apply_candidate` detects at
`merge_queue.py:287` / `:320`) cannot be scoped-repaired; it dead-ends at
quiesce. Suggested fix (08e-3b/08f): enrich the routed payload with the
offending paths + lane `contract_ids` so a real contract violation can route to
scoped product repair instead of quiescing.

### Vector-by-vector

1. Drain correctness — PASS. `claim → apply_candidate → reload → run_required_gates
   → reload → commit_and_prove_clean → reload → mark_integrated` matches the doc 08
   / Slice 08d worker contract exactly; the row is reloaded via `worker.get`
   after every step so each call sees the fresh lease version + proof ids; a
   clean lane ends `integrated`. (The raised-exception hole is P2-1, a robustness
   gap, not a step-ordering bug.)
2. Lease / claim races — PASS, no double-apply. Once `apply_candidate` does the
   `leased→applying` transition, `claim`'s `queued`/expired-`leased` predicate can
   never re-take the lane. In the microscopic `claim`→`leased→applying` window a
   second `claim` is theoretically possible, but the first drainer's stale-version
   `leased→applying` transition is then lease-fenced (`LeaseFencedError`) and
   cannot apply — only one drainer ever applies. The reload-between-steps further
   guarantees fresh versions. No TOCTOU double-apply.
3. Crash recovery — GAP (P2-3). `apply_candidate`/`commit_and_prove_clean` are
   idempotent-safe on a re-`claim` of an *expired-`leased`* lane (the baseline-clean
   check fails closed on a dirty residue rather than double-applying), but a lane
   stuck in `applying`/`verifying`/`committing` after a crash is invisible to
   `claim` and the drain never calls `recover_expired` — see P2-3.
4. Failure routing — PASS (with P3-1). All 7 `_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS`
   entries are present in `ROUTE_TABLE` and the `record`/`decide`/
   `mark_route_started` cycle fires; an unmapped class falls back to
   `(unknown,unclassified)` (router quiesces — fail closed). NO path silently
   swallows a failure or falls back to `_commit_group`/direct commit. The
   `contract_violation` mapping resolves to `quiesce` rather than scoped product
   repair for lack of scoped evidence — fail-closed and safe, recorded as P3-1.
5. **The `git diff --check` post-apply gate — VERDICT: a real gap, P2 (P2-2).**
   Doc 08 step 11 requires re-running "required deterministic gates against the
   applied canonical state"; treating a conflict-marker check as that gate is
   defensible scoping (applied-path contract scope is already enforced inside
   `apply_candidate`, and the post-DAG semantic verifier still runs separately).
   The defect is not that the gate is *minimal* — it is that `git diff --check`
   (no `--cached`) inspects the UNSTAGED worktree while `apply_candidate`'s
   `git apply --index` leaves the applied content STAGED, so the gate misses
   staged conflict markers and the one test that "proves" it only exercises an
   unstaged worktree. Fix: `git diff --check HEAD` or add `git diff --cached
   --check`.
6. **The deferred `_implement_dag` invocation — VERDICT: a sound split, NOT a
   half-wired hazard, acceptable.** Confirmed `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature`
   has no call site in `_implement_dag` (only the definition + an internal
   `_drain_one_merge_queue_lane` reference). Doc 08 §§ 12-15 + IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT
   lines 180-182 explicitly sanction slice-by-slice construction with intermediate
   states behind the Slice 12 atomic-landing gate; the whole queue (enqueue side
   included, 08e-2) is already uninvoked-in-production by the same construction
   order. 08e-2's enqueued lanes simply sit `queued` until 08e-3b wires both the
   drain call site and the checkpoint splice that consumes the `integrated` lanes
   — there is no live enqueue-with-no-drainer hazard because the *enqueue* path
   itself is gated. The split is coherent. (Caveat: P2-3 — the crash-recovery
   wiring — MUST land in 08e-3b alongside the call site, not be deferred further.)
7. Gates preserved — PASS. The drain functions add new code, are not on the
   post-DAG-verify or post-test code path, and `_implement_dag`'s control flow is
   byte-for-byte unchanged; the six post-DAG gates and the `post_test_observation.py`
   post-test guard are untouched by 08e-3a. `_commit_repos`/`_commit_repos_in_root`/
   `_commit_group` remain doc-08-step-7 compat shims.
8. Test honesty — MOSTLY HONEST, two gaps. The 9 tests genuinely exercise the
   happy path, multi-lane drain, real `merge_conflict` and `commit_hygiene`
   routing (with router-row assertions and "no legacy commit" assertions),
   sibling-isolation for a *returned* failure, and the no-store fail-closed path.
   Gaps: (a) NO crash / re-drive test for a lane stuck `applying`/`verifying`/
   `committing` (vector 3 / P2-3); (b) `test_post_apply_gate_decision_rejects_
   conflict_markers` writes markers to the UNSTAGED worktree, never the STAGED
   state `apply_candidate` produces, so it does not reproduce the real
   post-`apply_candidate` index state and masks P2-2; (c) no test for a *raised*
   worker exception mid-drain (P2-1) — every failure test uses a *returned*
   `applied=False`.

### Verdict

08e-3a is **acceptable after the listed P2 fixes** (P2-1 raised-exception
sibling-isolation hole; P2-2 post-apply gate inspecting the wrong tree state;
P2-3 missing `recover_expired` crash-recovery wiring + test). Zero P1 — no live
hazard exists while the drain is uninvoked (vector 6 split is sound), no
double-apply (vector 2), no data loss, no silent legacy fallback (vector 4). The
three P2s should be remediated in 08e-3b (P2-3 MUST land with the call-site
wiring) or explicitly accepted as tracked 08e-3b/08f follow-ups before Slice 08
acceptance. P3-1 (contract-violation → quiesce) folds into 08e-3b/08f.


## Slice 08e-3a REMEDIATION + COMPLETE — durable merge queue DRAIN worker P2 fixes (2026-05-21)

Remediation of the three BLOCKING P2 findings from the independent Slice 08e-3a
review. All fixes are ADDITIVE in
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py` (the drain
functions) plus `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_drain.py`.
No working 08d/08e-1/08e-2 code was refactored. The `_implement_dag` drain call
site and the post-DAG checkpoint splice are explicitly NOT in this chunk — they
are 08e-3b.

### P2-1 FIXED — a raised worker exception no longer strands sibling lanes

The Slice 08d `MergeQueue` worker methods catch only their own `_ApplyFailure`;
a `LeaseFencedError` / `MergeQueueError` raised from a lease-fenced
`transition` / `advance_repo_target` / `record_*_proof` (e.g. a lane whose gate
exceeds the 5-min lease TTL → fenced `transition` → `LeaseFencedError`), or any
asyncpg error, PROPAGATES out of the worker method. Before the fix that raised
exception escaped `_drain_one_merge_queue_lane` and aborted the whole drain
loop, stranding every still-`queued` sibling — the docstring's
"a sibling lane's conflict must not strand the others" held only for a
*returned* `applied=False`, not a *raised* exception.

Fix: each of the three worker calls in `_drain_one_merge_queue_lane`
(`apply_candidate`, `run_required_gates`, `commit_and_prove_clean`) is now
wrapped in `try/except (MergeQueueStoreError, MergeQueueLeaseFencedError,
Exception)` that funnels the raised exception into the SAME `_fail_result`
route-and-record path a returned failure uses (typed `checkpoint_contradiction`,
routed through the Slice 07 failure router, authoritative status reloaded via
`worker.get`). The pre-existing step-4 `mark_integrated` guard was broadened
from `(MergeQueueStoreError, MergeQueueLeaseFencedError)` to also catch
`Exception` (an asyncpg error inside the `transition` is not a
`MergeQueueStoreError`). The drain loop therefore always receives a returned
`_MergeQueueDrainResult`, never an escaping exception — a transient fault on
one lane fails only that lane and the loop continues.

Why correct: a raised exception now has identical blast radius to a returned
failure — exactly one lane is failed + routed; siblings are untouched. No
double-apply (the lane's lease fences it), no data loss (the typed row records
the terminal state), no silent legacy fallback.

Proving test: `test_drain_continues_after_a_raised_worker_exception` — drives a
`MergeQueueWorker` subclass whose `apply_candidate` *raises* a real
`MergeQueueError` for one designated lane and delegates to the genuine worker
for the sibling. Asserts the drain visits BOTH lanes, the raised lane ends
routed + failed with `checkpoint_contradiction`, and the clean sibling STILL
integrates (the loop continued). Pre-fix this test would fail — the raised
exception would abort the loop and the sibling would never be drained.

### P2-2 FIXED — the post-apply gate now inspects the STAGED applied state

`_merge_queue_post_apply_gate_decision` ran `git diff --check` with NO
`--cached`. `apply_candidate` applies the patch via `git apply --index`
(`git_service.apply_patch`), which STAGES the applied hunks; `git diff --check`
compares worktree-vs-index, so for content fully staged with conflict-marker
lines it reports clean and the gate FALSELY APPROVES a conflicted merge. The
codebase already knows this — `git_service.working_tree_clean` deliberately
checks BOTH `git diff --quiet` and `git diff --cached --quiet`; doc 08 step 9
confirms the applied path set is read from `git diff --cached --name-only`.

Fix: the gate now runs `git diff --check HEAD` — git diffs HEAD against the
combined staged+unstaged tree, so it catches conflict markers / whitespace
errors in BOTH the staged and unstaged content. One-token change per repo
target; docstring + rejection detail updated to explain why `HEAD` is
load-bearing.

Why correct: `git diff --check HEAD` is the staged-aware equivalent of the
unstaged-only `git diff --check`, mirroring `working_tree_clean`'s two-gate
check. It now inspects the exact index state `apply_candidate` produces.

Proving test: `test_post_apply_gate_decision_rejects_conflict_markers` was
STRENGTHENED — it now `git add`s the conflict-marker file so the marker is
fully STAGED (the exact post-`apply_candidate` state). It asserts BOTH that the
buggy bare `git diff --check` returns 0 (MISSED the staged marker — the masked
bug) AND that the fixed `git diff --check HEAD` returns non-zero, then asserts
the gate rejects with `merge_conflict`. The old test wrote an UNSTAGED marker,
which the buggy gate happened to catch — masking the bug.

### P2-3 FIXED — a crashed-mid-apply lane is now recovered via `recover_expired`

The drain wired `MergeQueueStore.claim` but never `recover_expired`. `claim`'s
predicate matches only `queued` / expired-`leased` rows — by design it NEVER
re-takes a lane stuck in `applying`/`verifying`/`committing`/`checkpointing`
(a lane that crashed mid-canonical-apply). Per doc 08 § Lease Semantics +
the Rollback And Recovery Table those states "are recoverable only through
`recover_expired`". Before the fix such a lane was invisible to every drain
re-run and stranded mid-flight forever.

Fix: a new additive function `_recover_one_crashed_merge_queue_lane` plus a
`recover_expired` pass in the drain loop. The loop now does: `claim` a
claimable lane; when `claim` is exhausted, fall through to
`queue_store.recover_expired(feature_id, lease_owner)`; when both return `None`,
stop. A recovered in-flight lane is driven to a deterministic FAIL-CLOSED
terminal: `_recover_one_crashed_merge_queue_lane` resets every touched
canonical repo to its recorded `merge_queue_repo_targets.pre_apply_head`, cleans
the merge proof's `patch_path_set` residue, proves no-dirty, then transitions
the lane (via its recovery-bumped lease token) to `failed`
(`checkpoint_contradiction`, routed) when the repo resets clean, or to
`poisoned` when a repo has irreconcilable residue (untracked files outside the
patch path set — doc 08 step 7 forbids auto-deleting those). An already-
`poisoned` recovered row (recovery limit exhausted) is reported as-is. This is
the doc Rollback Table's "reset each repo to pre_apply_head … then reapply OR
fail typed" — the deterministic *fail typed* branch (re-applying a recovered
partial apply is not modelled in 08e-3a; a retry goes through a router-created
replacement queue item per the `failed`-row recovery rule). The recovered lane
is logged as a distinct `dag_merge_queue_lane_recovered` feature event.

Why correct: a crashed in-flight lane is now visible to a drain re-run and
driven to a typed terminal with the canonical repo deterministically restored
to its pre-apply HEAD — no stranded lane, no data loss, no double-apply (the
recovery-bumped `lease_version` fences any racing recovery worker), no silent
legacy fallback. `_LEASE_TRANSITIONS` confirms `applying`/`verifying`/
`committing` each permit `→ failed`/`→ poisoned`.

Proving test: `test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_mid_apply` — stages the exact
crash: a lane is claimed, transitioned to `applying` with a recorded
`pre_apply_head`, its patch is left applied (staged via `git apply --index`) in
the canonical repo, and its lease is expired. It first asserts a normal `claim`
returns `None` (the lane is invisible without the recovery pass), then runs the
drain and asserts the lane is recovered, the canonical repo is reset to its
pre-apply HEAD (clean, README restored, no mid-apply residue, NO legacy
commit), the lane is terminal `failed` with `checkpoint_contradiction` routed,
and a SECOND drain re-run finds nothing claimable or recoverable (idempotent —
the failed lane is not re-driven).

### P3-1 — DEFERRED to 08f with rationale

The drain `contract_violation` route maps to `(contract_violation,
outside_allowed_paths)` → `run_product_repair`, but `decide()` downgrades to
`quiesce` because the routed observation carries no `repair_scope`
`target_paths`/`target_contract_ids` (the router's `_allows_product_repair`
requires both). This is FAIL-CLOSED and safe. Enriching the payload was
evaluated: `item.contract_ids` IS cleanly in hand, but the OFFENDING escaped
paths the router needs as `target_paths` exist only inside `apply_candidate`'s
free-text `_ApplyFailure.detail` string ("…outside the lane contracts:
[…]"), NOT as structured output. Threading them out cleanly would require
extending the Slice 08d `MergeApplyResult` contract to carry a structured
escaped-path list — a refactor of working, reviewed 08d worker code, which the
standing no-refactor non-negotiable forbids for a P3. P3s may defer per loop
discipline; this one is DEFERRED to 08f, where structured failure-evidence work
belongs. The fail-closed `quiesce` is the safe interim behavior — a genuine
contract escape stops the lane with typed evidence rather than dead-ending in a
broad product-repair loop.

### Verification — all green

* `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` — clean (exit 0).
* `python -m py_compile tests/.../test_merge_queue_drain.py` — clean.
* `git diff --check` — clean (exit 0).
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_drain.py
  -q` — 11 passed (real Postgres `localhost:5431` up). 9 original + 2 new
  (`test_drain_continues_after_a_raised_worker_exception`,
  `test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_mid_apply`); the P2-2 test
  `test_post_apply_gate_decision_rejects_conflict_markers` was strengthened
  in place.
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py -q` —
  255 passed.
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q` — 395 passed
  (real-PG tests ran, none skipped).
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py -q` — 2 failed,
  25 passed: ONLY the 2 KNOWN pre-existing failures
  (`test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave` at
  `assert terminal_state == "complete"`;
  `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints` `TypeError`
  on the sync `_ensure_task_worktrees` monkeypatch). ZERO new failures.

### Outcome

Slice 08e-3a is COMPLETE. All 3 BLOCKING P2s fixed, each with a test that
genuinely exercises it (pre-fix the new/strengthened tests fail). P3-1 deferred
to 08f with a journaled no-refactor rationale. Targeted suites green; no new
failures vs the 2 known `test_dag_regroup` ones. 08e-3b is next: the post-DAG
CHECKPOINT splice + wiring `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` into
`_implement_dag`.


## Slice 08e-3b IMPLEMENTATION — durable merge queue drain wiring + post-DAG checkpoint splice (2026-05-21)

The final 08e integration sub-slice: it makes the durable merge queue LIVE
end-to-end (enqueue -> drain -> checkpoint). 08e-2 enqueues `task:{id}` lanes;
08e-3a built + tested the drain worker; 08e-3a left the drain with NO call
site, so the enqueued lanes sat `queued` forever and the legacy post-DAG
checkpoint was still the `_commit_group` / `_record_dag_group_commit_proof` /
`_project_dag_group_checkpoint` path. 08e-3b joins them. Two coupled changes,
both in `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`, plus
one new test file. No working 08d / 08e-1 / 08e-2 / 08e-3a code was refactored.

### What changed

(A) **Drain call site wired into `_implement_dag`.** In the
`if pending_merge_queue:` block of `_implement_dag` (the same 08e-2 enqueue
block — `implementation.py:16903`), immediately after the successful
`_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results` enqueue and the per-task pending
marker writes, the block now calls
`_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature(runner, feature, dag_sha256=...,
stage="implementation")` (the call is at `implementation.py:16992`). The drain
claims each `queued` lane the enqueue produced and drives it through the Slice
08d `MergeQueue` worker to `integrated`. A `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` (drain
modules / store unavailable) fails the group closed via the new local
`_merge_queue_group_blocked(...)` helper (typed `workflow_blocked`, never the
legacy commit). Any per-lane apply/gate/commit failure is already recorded
`failed` + routed by the drain itself; the block additionally fails the GROUP
closed when `[r for r in drain_results if not r.integrated]` is non-empty (it
never silently degrades to a legacy commit).

(B) **Post-DAG checkpoint spliced.** A new additive function
`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` (`implementation.py:6259`) replaces the
legacy `_commit_group` / `_record_dag_group_commit_proof` /
`_project_dag_group_checkpoint` path FOR THE QUEUE-DRIVEN FLOW. It builds the
Slice 08d `GroupMergeCoordinator` from one held connection — a
`MergeQueueStore`, a `build_checkpoint_projector(ExecutionControlStore, ...)`
collaborator (Slice 08e-1 wiring), and a deterministic
`ExpectedTaskIdsProvider` (returns the group's task ids) — then runs
`coordinator.coverage(...)` and `coordinator.checkpoint_group(coverage,
token)`. `checkpoint_group` projects the SINGLE `dag-group:{group_idx}`
compatibility artifact via `ExecutionControlStore.project_group_checkpoint`
(`projection_owner='merge_queue'`) behind an approved `checkpoint_gate`
evidence node, and advances every covered lane `integrated -> done`. The
`pending_merge_queue` block calls `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group`
right after the drain (`implementation.py:17048`). On full success it records
handover successes for the pending results (the legacy `_verify_and_fix_group`
does this for the non-queue path — the queue path skips that function), deletes
the now-stale `_pending_merge_queue_marker_key` markers, logs
`dag_merge_queue_group_checkpointed`, refreshes the public exhibit, then
`group_idx += 1; continue` to the next DAG group. The legacy
non-queue path in `_verify_and_fix_group` (the `_commit_group` checkpoint at
`implementation.py:~15330`/`15408`/`15464`) is byte-for-byte UNCHANGED — it
runs only when `pending_merge_queue` is empty.

### Locating the real checkpoint site (doc-vs-reality)

Doc 08 § Refactoring Steps step 5 cites the checkpoint commit at
`implementation.py:4166` / `4203`. Those line numbers are STALE — the file is
now ~33k lines. Located by reading: the real legacy post-DAG checkpoint is the
"Record outcomes + checkpoint" section of `_verify_and_fix_group`
