(`implementation.py:~15281`-`15487`): `_record_dag_group_commit_intent` ->
`_commit_group` (`~15330`) -> `_record_dag_group_commit_proof` (`~15408`) ->
`_record_dag_checkpoint_gate_proof` (`~15423`) -> `_project_dag_group_checkpoint`
(`~15464`, which is the `dag-group:*` projection =
`ExecutionControlStore.project_group_checkpoint`). 08e-3b does NOT edit that
section — it adds the queue-driven checkpoint as a parallel path in the
`pending_merge_queue` block; the legacy section stays as the doc-08-step-7
compat path for non-sandbox (legacy) features. `_commit_repos` /
`_commit_repos_in_root` / `_commit_group` / `_record_dag_group_commit_proof`
are all KEPT untouched as doc-08-step-7 compat shims — the queue-driven flow
simply no longer calls them (it `continue`s past them).

### Control-flow detail

The `pending_merge_queue` block previously `return`ed `workflow_blocked`
unconditionally after enqueue (08e-2: drain not yet wired). 08e-3b replaces that
return: on a successful drain + checkpoint the block does `group_idx += 1;
continue` inside the `while group_idx < len(dag.execution_order)` loop
(`implementation.py:16292`) — exactly equivalent to the legacy path's
fall-through to `group_idx += 1`, advancing to the next group and skipping the
legacy `_record_precommit_contract_verdicts` / `_commit_repos` /
`_verify_and_fix_group` that follow the `if` block (those are dedented OUTSIDE
the `if` and run only for the non-queue path). The `if pending_merge_queue:` /
implicit-else structure is unchanged; only the body of the `if` changed.

### Failure routing

Checkpoint / coordinator failures route the SAME typed way drain failures do.
`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group`'s local `_route(detail)` calls the
existing `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure(...)` with
`failure_class="checkpoint_contradiction"` — which maps (via the 08e-3a
`_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS`) to the Slice 07 router pair
`(checkpoint_contradiction, checkpoint_after_failed_gate)`, a deterministic
fail-closed `quiesce` route. Two failure cases route this way: (1) coverage is
not approved (a missing/duplicate/failed lane in the group), and (2)
`checkpoint_group` raises or returns `checkpointed=False`. In both cases
`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` returns
`_MergeQueueCheckpointResult(checkpointed=False, routed_failure=...)`, the
`pending_merge_queue` block logs `dag_merge_queue_checkpoint_failed`, and
`_merge_queue_group_blocked` returns a typed `workflow_blocked`. A drain failure
(any lane not `integrated`) is already routed per-lane by the 08e-3a drain;
the block fails the group closed on it. NEVER a legacy `_commit_group`
fallback (standing no-silent-degradation non-negotiable).

### Post-DAG verify gates / post-test guard preserved

The post-DAG verify gates that "run later in `execute()`" — code review (Step
2), QA, security audit, integration testing, the verifier — are the
`execute()`-level Step 2+ gates (`implementation.py:~10268` onward). They run
AFTER `_implement_dag` returns `terminal_state="complete"`. 08e-3b's only
effect on them: the merge-queue path can now REACH `complete` (08e-2 always
returned `workflow_blocked`), so those gates now actually run for sandbox/
queue-driven features — 08e-3b does NOT bypass, skip, or reorder them. The
post-test guard (`post_test_observation.py`) is a separate downstream Phase
never invoked from `implementation.py`; it is byte-for-byte untouched. The
per-group `_verify_and_fix_group` verify is NOT run for the queue-driven group
— but it was ALREADY not run for it under 08e-2 (the block returned
`workflow_blocked` before reaching it); the queue path's gate layer is the
08d `run_required_gates` post-apply gate (the deterministic
`git diff --check HEAD`) plus the coordinator's `checkpoint_gate`. 08e-3b does
not change that split.

### Gating

The durable queue path is NOT behind a runtime feature flag at this slice.
Doc 08 § Rollout/Rollback Notes: "There is no phased production rollout for
this slice... Any environment flag for this slice is a startup readiness guard
only." The queue path is selected per-result by
`_result_requires_durable_merge_queue(r)` — true when the result carries the
`canonical_mutation=pending_durable_merge_queue` note, which the dispatcher
stamps whenever sandbox patch capture produced a patch
(`implementation.py:7772`). So the queue path is default-on whenever the Slice
04 sandbox runner is active — exactly as 08e-2's enqueue already is. The
startup readiness guard is `verify_merge_queue_production_ready` (called inside
`_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results`), which fails closed. There is NO
separate Slice-12-style runtime `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` switch at
08e-3b — that is Slice 12. Followed the doc: no runtime gate wired.

### Interfaces verified at file:line before writing

* `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` — `implementation.py:6017` (08e-3a;
  returns `list[_MergeQueueDrainResult]`, each with `.integrated`,
  `.terminal_status`, `.item_id`, `.failure_class`, `.routed_failure`).
* `_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results` — `implementation.py:5248` (08e-2;
  the `pending_merge_queue` block already wires it).
* `GroupMergeCoordinator` (`coverage`, `checkpoint_group`) +
  `GroupMergeCoverage` (`.approved`, `.integrated_queue_item_ids`,
  `.missing_task_ids`, `.duplicate_task_ids`, `.failed_queue_item_ids`) +
  `MergeResult` (`.checkpointed`, `.done_queue_item_ids`, `.result_commit`,
  `.detail`) + `LeaseToken` — `workflows/develop/execution/merge_queue.py`
  (08d): `GroupMergeCoordinator.__init__(store, expected_task_ids_provider,
  checkpoint_projector)`; `checkpoint_group` is idempotent (already-`done`
  group -> idempotent success), recomputes coverage under the feature advisory
  lock, raises `MergeQueueError` without a projector.
* `build_checkpoint_projector(store, gate_decision_provider)` +
  `GateDecision` (frozen dataclass: `approved`, `failure_class`, `detail`,
  `input_evidence_ids`, `verdict_payload`) — `merge_queue_wiring.py` (08e-1):
  `gate_decision_provider` is `Callable[[GroupMergeCoverage],
  Awaitable[GateDecision]]`; the projector writes `dag-group:{group_idx}` via
  `ExecutionControlStore.project_group_checkpoint`
  (`projection_owner='merge_queue'`) behind an approved `checkpoint_gate` node;
  idempotent on the doc-08 step-3 `_checkpoint_key`.
* `MergeQueueStore` — connection-bound; `ExecutionControlStore` — pool-bound;
  `_merge_queue_connection` bridges (verified `implementation.py:5039`).
* `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` + `_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS`
  (`checkpoint_contradiction` -> `checkpoint_after_failed_gate`) —
  `implementation.py:5510`/`5496` (08e-3a). Confirmed the router route table
  has the `(checkpoint_contradiction, checkpoint_after_failed_gate)` ->
  `quiesce` entry — `failure_router.py:1296`.
* `_execution_control_store_for_runner`, `_failure_router_for_runner`
  (auto-creates an in-memory router), `_log_feature_event`,
  `enqueue_public_exhibit_refresh`, `_workflow_blocker_text`,
  `_append_note_once`, `runner.artifacts.delete` — all verified.

### Doc-vs-reality divergences

1. Doc 08 step 5's `4166`/`4203` checkpoint line numbers are stale (file is
   ~33k lines); the real legacy checkpoint is in `_verify_and_fix_group`'s
   "Record outcomes + checkpoint" section (`~15281`-`15487`). Trusted reality.
2. `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` (`merge_queue.py:~1068`) sets `results: []`
   in the reconstructed legacy `dag-group:*` body (a TODO from 08d/08d-5
   pending 08e). The legacy `_project_dag_group_checkpoint` body carries the
   full `results` list. This is a PRE-EXISTING 08d/08e item, not introduced
   by 08e-3b — left as-is (P2-risk noted below).
3. The merge-queue `checkpoint_projector` writes `dag-group:*` only as a
   journal compatibility projection (via `project_group_checkpoint`), NOT to
   `runner.artifacts` — but the LEGACY `_project_dag_group_checkpoint` does the
   SAME (it only writes the journal projection + an optional test mirror). So
   the queue checkpoint and the legacy checkpoint have IDENTICAL `dag-group:*`
   resume visibility; the existing `_recover_dag_group_checkpoint_from_proofs`
   resume fallback handles both. No new resume gap for the SUCCESS path.

### Verification — all green

* `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` — clean (exit 0).
* `python -m py_compile tests/.../test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py` — clean.
* `git diff --check` — clean (exit 0).
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py
  -q` — 6 passed (real Postgres `localhost:5431` up). New file; tests:
  `test_checkpoint_projects_dag_group_for_a_drained_single_task_group`,
  `test_checkpoint_covers_every_task_of_a_multi_task_group`,
  `test_checkpoint_is_idempotent_on_a_re_run`,
  `test_checkpoint_fails_closed_and_routes_when_a_lane_is_not_integrated`,
  `test_checkpoint_fails_closed_and_routes_a_failed_drained_lane`,
  `test_checkpoint_fails_closed_without_a_typed_store`. Each drives the FULL
  end-to-end queue path: enqueue (08e-2 shape) -> drain (08e-3a) -> checkpoint
  (08e-3b), with real git repos.
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_drain.py
  tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_coordinator.py -q` — 31
  passed (11 drain + 20 coordinator; no regressions).
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q` — 401 passed (was
  395 + 6 new; real-PG tests ran, none skipped).
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py -q` — 255
  passed (unchanged).
* `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py -q` — 2 failed, 25
  passed: ONLY the 2 KNOWN pre-existing failures
  (`test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave` at
  `assert outcome.terminal_state == "complete"` — got `workflow_blocked`;
  `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints` `TypeError`
  on the sync `_ensure_task_worktrees` monkeypatch). ZERO new failures. The
  `_implement_dag` change did NOT change either: `dispatches_regrouped_first_
  wave` still fails at the identical `assert ... == "complete"` /
  `workflow_blocked` the journal documents.

### P1/P2-risk areas for a reviewer

* **P2-risk (resume of a checkpoint FAILURE).** On a drain/checkpoint failure
  the `pending_merge_queue` block keeps the `_pending_merge_queue_marker_key`
  markers and returns `workflow_blocked`. On a subsequent resume the per-task
  resume block (`implementation.py:~16495`) sees the pending marker and
  re-returns `workflow_blocked` WITHOUT auto-re-running the idempotent
  `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` / `checkpoint_group`. For a
  transient checkpoint failure on cleanly-`integrated` lanes this means the
  feature does not auto-recover on a plain resume — the durable `integrated`
  lanes ARE recoverable (doc 08 Rollback Table: `integrated` lanes are
  recovered by the coordinator's `coverage` + `checkpoint_group`), but that
  recovery is not yet wired into the resume path. This matches 08e-2's
  existing fail-closed behavior (08e-2 also re-blocks on the pending marker)
  and the routed `checkpoint_contradiction` -> `quiesce` is the doc-aligned
  fail-closed terminal; automatic resume-recovery of the queue-driven path is
  08f crash-recovery work. NOT a regression — the SUCCESS-path resume works
  identically to the legacy checkpoint (divergence #3 above).
* **P2-risk (empty `results` in the checkpoint body).** Divergence #2 — the
  merge-queue `dag-group:*` body carries `results: []` where the legacy body
  carries the full result dumps. A legacy `dag-group:*` reader that indexes
  `results` would see an empty list for a queue-checkpointed group. This is a
  pre-existing 08d `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` TODO; 08e-3b's
  `handover.record_success` for the pending results keeps the in-memory
  handover correct, but a cross-process consumer of the `dag-group:*` body
  `results` field would not. 08f / `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` should
  populate `results` from typed task-result evidence.
* **P3 (per-task lanes).** Carried from 08e-2: the enqueue produces one
  `task:{id}` lane per task rather than one `group` lane; the 08e-3b
  coordinator handles a per-task-lane group correctly (coverage is by task),
  but doc 08's "most groups produce one queue item" is not literally followed.
  Not a correctness issue (per-task lanes are always isolation-safe).
* No P1. No double-apply (each lane is lease-fenced; the checkpoint runs under
  the feature advisory lock). No data loss (typed rows + proofs are the
  authority). No silent legacy fallback (every failure path is typed
  `workflow_blocked` + routed; `_commit_group` is never called on the queue
  path). The post-DAG verify gates / post-test guard are preserved.

### Outcome

08e-3b makes the durable merge queue LIVE end-to-end: a sandbox/queue-driven
DAG group now flows enqueue (08e-2) -> drain (08e-3a) -> checkpoint (08e-3b
`GroupMergeCoordinator.checkpoint_group`) -> next group, and `_implement_dag`
can reach `terminal_state="complete"` so the `execute()` post-DAG gates run.
Targeted suites green; 6 new end-to-end real-Postgres tests; no new failures
vs the 2 known `test_dag_regroup` ones. Per loop discipline this trivial-risk /
pattern-mirroring sub-step defers its independent review to the 08g slice-end
six-vector review (the two P2-risk areas above are explicitly tracked for it).
Slice 08 remaining: 08f (full test suite + the carried P3 to-do list, now plus
the two P2-risk items above) and 08g (six-vector review + Slice 08 acceptance).


## Slice 08e-3b REVIEW — durable merge queue drain wiring + post-DAG checkpoint splice (independent, review-only) (2026-05-21)

Independent review subagent, REVIEW-ONLY (no code changed). Target: Slice
08e-3b — the two additive changes that make the durable merge queue LIVE
end-to-end. Reviewed the working-tree state (the whole repo is an uncommitted
Slice 00–08e bundle): the `if pending_merge_queue:` drain + checkpoint call
site in `_implement_dag` (`implementation.py:16903`-`17159`),
`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` (`implementation.py:6258`-`6435`),
`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` reuse, the legacy `_verify_and_fix_group`
checkpoint path, the resume reconstruction loop, the per-task resume block,
`post_test_observation.py`'s post-test guard, `merge_queue.py`'s
`GroupMergeCoordinator` / `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`,
`merge_queue_wiring.py`'s `build_checkpoint_projector`, `store.py`'s
`_project_group_checkpoint`, and the 6 new tests in
`test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py`.

### Reviewer summary

The drain + checkpoint *call site* is well-built: the step ordering is correct
(write pending markers → enqueue → drain → verify every lane `integrated` →
checkpoint → record handover + delete markers + log + `group_idx += 1;
continue`), the loop can structurally reach `complete`, every failure funnels
through the typed `workflow_blocked` route and NEVER the legacy `_commit_group`,
the legacy non-queue `_verify_and_fix_group` checkpoint is byte-for-byte
unchanged on its branch, and the `execute()` post-DAG gates run only because
the queue path can now reach `complete`. The checkpoint splice itself
(`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group`) correctly runs `coverage` then
`checkpoint_group` and routes failures as `checkpoint_contradiction`.

BUT the slice ships a **P1 resume-and-completion breakage**: the queue
checkpoint body the projector writes carries `results: []`
(`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`, `merge_queue.py:1080`), and the legacy
`dag-group:*` resume/post-test consumer `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`
HARD-REQUIRES `len(results) == len(group_task_ids)` (via
`_checkpoint_results_match_tasks`, `implementation.py:18613-18619`). The
implementer flagged `results: []` as a "P2-risk, pre-existing 08d TODO,
cross-process display only" — that judgement is WRONG: a legacy reader DOES
index `results`, and it is the exact resume freshness gate. Findings: **one
P1**, **two P2**, one P3.

### Findings

**P1 — `merge_queue.py:1080` (`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`) → consumed by
`implementation.py:18613` (`_checkpoint_results_match_tasks`) and
`implementation.py:16218` / `post_test_observation.py:407`
(`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`) — the queue checkpoint body's empty
`results` makes every queue-checkpointed group fail the legacy `dag-group:*`
freshness gate, breaking resume AND blocking post-test completion.** The
08e-3b checkpoint projector writes a `dag-group:{group_idx}` compatibility
artifact whose body is `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` =
`{"group_idx","task_ids","results": [],"verdict":"approved","commit_hash"}`
(no `dag_sha256`). The legacy `_project_dag_group_checkpoint` body
(`implementation.py:15695-15702`) instead carries
`"results": checkpoint_results` (the full `ImplementationResult` model dumps,
one per task) plus `"dag_sha256"`. Three legacy consumers break on the empty
list:
  1. **Resume reconstruction loop** (`implementation.py:16194-16264`). For a
     queue-checkpointed group the `dag-group:{g_idx}` artifact is PRESENT (the
     projector wrote it), so the loop takes the `else` branch
     (`:16213-16217`) and runs `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` directly on
     the queue body. `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` (`:19083`) calls
     `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks`, which (`:18616-18619`) for a non-empty
     `group_task_ids` requires `len(results) == len(group_task_ids)` —
     `len([]) != N` → returns `False` → `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`
     returns `False` (`:19084`) → the loop logs "checkpoint marker is stale"
     and `break`s (`:16227-16231`) WITHOUT advancing `start_group`. The
     successfully-checkpointed group is judged stale. The implementer's
     journal claim (divergence #3) that "the queue checkpoint and the legacy
     checkpoint have IDENTICAL `dag-group:*` resume visibility … the existing
     `_recover_dag_group_checkpoint_from_proofs` resume fallback handles both"
     is FALSE: `_recover_dag_group_checkpoint_from_proofs` is only called when
     the `dag-group:` artifact is ABSENT (`:16202` `if not checkpoint_json:`),
     and the queue path WRITES the artifact, so the recovery fallback is never
     reached.
  2. **Per-task resume block** (`implementation.py:16487-16519`). Because (1)
     left `start_group=0`, the `while` loop re-enters the completed group; the
     per-task resume block then sees the `dag-task:{tid}` marker (written by
     the dispatcher, `:8214`, carrying the `pending_durable_merge_queue`
     note), `result.status == "completed"`, `_result_requires_durable_merge_
     queue(result)` True (`:16513`) → returns `workflow_blocked` (`:16514`).
     Net effect: a resume of a fully completed + checkpointed queue group
     **permanently re-blocks the whole feature `workflow_blocked`**, or (if
     the drain re-runs first) re-dispatches an already-`done` group.
  3. **Post-test guard** (`post_test_observation.py:395-433`). It iterates
     every `dag-group:{group_idx}` and calls the SAME
     `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`. For every queue-checkpointed group the
     empty `results` fails freshness → the group is appended to
     `stale_groups` → `_quiesce_post_test_workflow_blocker(...
     reason="post_test_blocked_dag_checkpoint_stale")` quiesces the feature.
  Combined consequence: a sandbox/queue-driven feature **cannot complete the
  develop workflow** — it is blocked on resume, and even absent a resume the
  post-test phase rejects every queue checkpoint as stale. Also (subsumed) the
  resume `all_results`/`handover` reconstruction at `:16253`
  (`for r_data in data.get("results", [])`) gets nothing for queue groups.
  This is resume-breakage + checkpoint-completion breakage = P1 per the
  IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT severity table. Confirmed reachable: the projector's
  body flows through `ExecutionControlStore._project_group_checkpoint` →
  `_insert_or_reuse_artifact` → `INSERT INTO artifacts (feature_id, key,
  value)` (`store.py:3803`) — the same `artifacts` table `runner.artifacts.get`
  reads.
  Suggested fix: populate `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`'s `results` from the
  typed task-result evidence (the 08d TODO at `merge_queue.py:1077-1079`) so
  the body matches the legacy shape `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks` requires
  — i.e. one validatable `ImplementationResult`-shaped dump per expected task
  id. The 6 new tests do not catch this because none exercises `_implement_dag`
  resume or the post-test guard against a queue-projected body.

**P2 — vector 6 (resume after a drain succeeds but the checkpoint crashes) —
an `integrated` group is left stranded `workflow_blocked` and is not
auto-driven to the idempotent checkpoint on resume.** When the drain
integrates the lanes and `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` then crashes,
no `dag-group:` artifact is projected; the `_pending_merge_queue_marker_key`
markers (written `:16984-16989`, deleted only on full success) survive. On
resume the per-task block (`:16491-16507`) sees the pending marker and returns
`workflow_blocked` BEFORE calling the idempotent drain/`checkpoint_group`. Doc
08 § Rollback And Recovery Table says `integrated`/`checkpointing` lanes are
recoverable by re-running `coverage` + the idempotent `checkpoint_group`
("Let already-committed queue items in `integrated` or `checkpointing`
complete the idempotent group checkpoint transaction when clean proof
exists"); the resume path never does. NOT P1: fail-closed, no data loss, no
double-apply (the `integrated` lanes are NOT re-drained — `claim` cannot
re-take `integrated`, the enqueue is idempotent, and the per-task block
returns before any dispatch), and this matches 08e-2's already-accepted
fail-closed-on-marker behavior. But doc 08 explicitly wants `integrated`
groups auto-completed — fail-closed-and-wait is acceptable for now ONLY as a
tracked 08f crash-recovery item. Suggested fix (08f): when the per-task
resume block sees a pending merge-queue marker, re-run the idempotent
`_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` + `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_
group` instead of immediately re-blocking.

**P2 — `test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py` (whole file) — the 6 tests never
exercise the `_implement_dag` call site, a resume, or the post-test guard, so
the slice's actual integration surface is untested.** The tests call
`_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` and
`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` as standalone helpers against real
Postgres — genuinely (enqueue→drain→checkpoint, multi-task, idempotent re-run,
coverage-not-approved routed, failed-lane routed, no-store fail-closed). They
are honest for the helpers. But 08e-3b's load-bearing new behavior is the
splice INTO `_implement_dag`'s `if pending_merge_queue:` block and the claim
that a queue feature can now reach `complete`; NO test drives `_implement_dag`
end-to-end, and NO test resumes a queue-checkpointed group or runs the
post-test guard over a queue-projected `dag-group:*` body. That gap is exactly
why the P1 shipped undetected. Suggested fix: add an `_implement_dag`-level
test that runs a sandbox/queue group to `complete`, then re-invokes
`_implement_dag` (resume) and asserts the checkpointed group is skipped (not
re-run, not `workflow_blocked`); and a post-test-guard test over a
queue-projected `dag-group:*`.

**P3 — `implementation.py:6327-6340` / `6392` / `6416` / `6428`
(`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group._route`) — all checkpoint-failure
routes use a fixed `item_id=0`, so coverage-not-approved and
checkpoint_group-declined failures share one router idempotency key
(`failure:{feature}:merge-queue-drain:g{group}:item-0:checkpoint_
contradiction:checkpoint_after_failed_gate`).** Two distinct checkpoint
failure causes dedupe to the same typed failure row. Both are the same
`failure_class` and both route to the same deterministic `quiesce`, so this is
benign (the router deduping a same-class failure is intended) — recorded only
for clarity / 08f.

### Vector-by-vector

1. **End-to-end flow correctness — PASS.** The `if pending_merge_queue:` body
   orders correctly: enqueue (08e-2) → write pending markers (`:16984`) →
   drain (`:16992`) → reject any non-`integrated` lane (`:17026-17040`) →
   checkpoint (`:17049`) → on success record handover (`:17115-17117`), delete
   markers (`:17118-17126`), log, refresh exhibit, `group_idx += 1; continue`
   (`:17158`). The `continue` re-enters the `while group_idx <
   len(dag.execution_order)` loop (`:16292`), exactly equivalent to the
   non-queue path's `group_idx += 1` fall-through; when `group_idx` exhausts,
   the loop exits to the enhancement group and `terminal_state="complete"`
   (`:17286-17291`). No path advances `group_idx` on a partially-drained group
   — a failed/non-integrated lane returns `_merge_queue_group_blocked`
   (`workflow_blocked`) without `continue`. The feature can now correctly
   reach `complete`. (The P1 is a `dag-group:*` body-shape defect, not a
   call-site step-ordering bug.)
2. **Checkpoint splice correctness — DIVERGENT (the P1).**
   `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` correctly runs `coverage` then
   `checkpoint_group`, projects exactly one `dag-group:{group_idx}` (the
   wiring's `build_checkpoint_projector` → `project_group_checkpoint`, owner
   `merge_queue`), and `checkpoint_group` advances covered lanes
   `integrated → done`. But the projected artifact body diverges from the
   legacy `_commit_group` body: `results: []` vs the legacy full result
   dumps, and no `dag_sha256`. A legacy `dag-group:*` reader
   (`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` / `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks`) does
   NOT accept it — see P1.
3. **Non-queue path unchanged — PASS.** The legacy checkpoint path
   (`_verify_and_fix_group`'s "Record outcomes + checkpoint" section,
   `implementation.py:~15561-15726`: `_record_dag_group_commit_intent` →
   `_commit_group` → `_record_dag_group_commit_proof` →
   `_record_dag_checkpoint_gate_proof` → `_project_dag_group_checkpoint`) is
   on the implicit-`else` branch of `if pending_merge_queue:` and is
   structurally untouched; the queue path `continue`s before reaching
   `_record_precommit_contract_verdicts` (`:17161`) / `_commit_repos`
   (`:17197`) / `_verify_and_fix_group` (`:17233`). `_commit_group` (`:18364`),
   `_commit_repos`, `_commit_repos_in_root`, `_record_dag_group_commit_proof`
   (`:19266`) all still exist as doc-08-step-7 compat shims. No regression.
4. **Post-DAG gates / post-test guard — PARTIAL.** The `execute()`-level
   post-DAG verify gates (Step 2 code review `:10506`+, QA, security, the
   verifier) run AFTER `_implement_dag` returns; the splice does not touch
   `execute()` and only `continue`s within `_implement_dag`'s loop, so those
   gates are not bypassed or reordered — and they now actually run for queue
   features (08e-2 always returned `workflow_blocked`). HOWEVER the post-test
   guard in `post_test_observation.py` IS byte-for-byte untouched but is NOT
   "preserved" in effect: it shares `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`, which
   rejects every queue checkpoint body (`results: []`) as stale and quiesces
   the feature — see P1 consequence (3). The guard is not bypassed; it
   actively blocks the queue path.
5. **Fail-closed, no silent degradation — PASS.** Every failure surfaces as a
   typed `workflow_blocked`: drain module-unavailable (`_MergeQueueEnqueueError`
   → `_merge_queue_group_blocked`, `:17001-17022`); any lane not `integrated`
   (`:17026-17040`); checkpoint module-unavailable (`:17060-17078`); coverage
   not approved or `checkpoint_group` declined/raised
   (`_MergeQueueCheckpointResult(checkpointed=False)` → `:17079-17106`). None
   falls back to `_commit_group` / a direct checkpoint —
   `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` raises `_MergeQueueEnqueueError`
   rather than degrading, and `checkpoint_group` failures route via
   `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` as `(checkpoint_contradiction,
   checkpoint_after_failed_gate)` → deterministic `quiesce` (verified
   `failure_router.py:1295-1300`). No swallowed failure.
6. **Crash recovery on resume — VERDICT: P2.** Resume after a checkpoint
   FAILURE: the `integrated` lanes are NOT re-drained or re-applied (`claim`
   cannot re-take `integrated`; the per-task resume block returns
   `workflow_blocked` before any dispatch; the enqueue is idempotent) — so
   there is NO double-apply hazard, good. But the feature does not
   auto-recover: the per-task block re-blocks on the pending marker without
   re-running the idempotent `checkpoint_group`, whereas doc 08's Rollback And
   Recovery Table says `integrated`/`checkpointing` groups should be driven to
   the idempotent checkpoint on recovery. Fail-closed-and-wait is acceptable
   for 08e-3b ONLY as a tracked 08f item (it matches accepted 08e-2 behavior);
   a stranded `integrated` group does not need auto-recovery wired *in this
   sub-slice*, but it MUST be wired in 08f — hence P2, not "fine as-is" and not
   P1.
7. **Gating — VERDICT: correct, no finding.** Doc 08 § Rollout/Rollback Notes
   states "Any environment flag for this slice is a startup readiness guard
   only" and forbids a runtime mode where some new attempts use the queue and
   others use legacy commit. 08e-3b correctly wires NO runtime gate: the queue
   path is selected per-result by `_result_requires_durable_merge_queue(r)`
   (the sandbox-stamped `canonical_mutation=pending_durable_merge_queue` note,
   `implementation.py:4984`/`7772`), and the only guard is the fail-closed
   startup-readiness `verify_merge_queue_production_ready` inside the enqueue.
   Doc 08 expects NO Slice-12-style runtime switch at this code path — Slice
   12's `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` gates whether sandbox mode runs at
   all, upstream. A feature reaching this code path with the note set going
   durable with no other gate is exactly doc-08-conformant. Correct.
8. **`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` `results: []` — VERDICT: P1, not P2.** The
   implementer flagged this as a pre-existing 08d TODO surfacing here and rated
   it P2-risk / "cross-process display only". That is wrong. The legacy
   `_project_dag_group_checkpoint` body DOES carry the full `results` list, and
   a legacy `dag-group:*` consumer DOES index it: `_checkpoint_results_match_
   tasks` (`implementation.py:18613-18619`) hard-requires
   `len(results) == len(group_task_ids)` and validates each entry as an
   `ImplementationResult` — and that predicate gates BOTH the `_implement_dag`
   resume reconstruction loop AND the `post_test_observation.py` post-test
   guard. An empty `results` therefore breaks resume and blocks post-test
   completion of every queue-checkpointed group. The 08d TODO's own caveat
   ("08e must confirm no legacy `dag-group:*` reader indexes `results`")
   demanded a confirmation 08e-3b did not perform; the confirmation's answer
   is "a reader does, in two load-bearing places". This is the P1.

### Verdict

08e-3b is **blocked on the P1** (`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` `results: []`
breaks the legacy `dag-group:*` resume freshness gate and the post-test guard,
so a queue-driven feature cannot resume past or complete a checkpointed
group). The drain + checkpoint *call site* itself is sound — correct step
ordering, can reach `complete`, fails closed with no legacy fallback, legacy
non-queue path byte-for-byte unchanged, gating doc-conformant. Fix the P1 by
populating `results` in `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` from typed task-result
evidence so the queue checkpoint body matches the legacy shape; then add the
P2 `_implement_dag`-level resume + post-test tests so the integration surface
is covered; the P2 resume-recovery wiring (vector 6) is a tracked 08f item.
Re-review after the P1 fix.


## Slice 08e-3b REMEDIATION — durable merge queue checkpoint freshness + resume re-drive (2026-05-21)

Remediation of the independent 08e-3b review: ONE P1 (BLOCKING) + two P2 + one
P3. Fixed P1 + P2-a + P2-b; P3 deferred to 08g with rationale. No working 08d /
08e-1 / 08e-2 / 08e-3a code was refactored — every change is an additive new
code path. Files touched: `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/
merge_queue.py`, `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py`, `tests/workflows/develop/execution/
test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py`.

### P1 (FIXED) — the queue `dag-group:*` checkpoint body now passes the legacy resume freshness gate

The review's P1: `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` emitted `results: []`, and the
legacy `dag-group:*` freshness gate `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` —
consumed by BOTH the `_implement_dag` resume reconstruction loop AND the
`post_test_observation.py` post-test guard — rejected every queue-checkpointed
group as stale.

Investigation found the P1 has **two** root causes, not one:

1. **Empty `results`.** Confirmed the EXACT legacy body shape by reading
   `_verify_and_fix_group` (`implementation.py:15695-15702`): the legacy
   checkpoint dict is `{group_idx, task_ids, results, verdict, commit_hash,
   dag_sha256}` where `results = [r.model_dump() for r in results if
   isinstance(r, ImplementationResult)]`. The freshness gate
   `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks` (`implementation.py:18609-18627`)
   hard-requires `len(results) == len(group_task_ids)`, each entry
   `ImplementationResult`-validatable, task ids matching with no duplicates.

2. **Missing proof artifacts.** Even with `results` populated, the gate at
   `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` (~`implementation.py:19309`) reads
   `dag-group-commit-proof:{group_idx}` and `dag-checkpoint-gate-proof:
   {group_idx}` artifacts — written ONLY by the legacy
   `_record_dag_group_commit_proof` / `_record_dag_checkpoint_gate_proof`
   path. The queue checkpoint writes NEITHER (it writes the `dag-group:*`
   projection + typed `merge_queue_items` evidence). When both proof artifacts
   are absent the gate falls to `_feature_has_execution_control_legacy_marker`,
   which is `False` for a queue/sandbox feature (no `execution-control-legacy:
   *` marker). So fixing `results` alone is necessary but NOT sufficient.

**Durable evidence source used.** The per-task `ImplementationResult` data for
a queue group is durably available in two places: the in-memory
`pending_merge_queue` list at the live checkpoint call site, and — for a
RESUME, where memory is gone — the durable `dag-task:{tid}` attempt markers
the dispatcher writes (the full serialized `ImplementationResult`, carrying the
`canonical_mutation=pending_durable_merge_queue` note). Per doc 08 § Persistence
And Artifact Compatibility, the queue must not *infer checkpoint completion*
from `dag-task:*`, but reading the per-task result *content* to reconstruct the
legacy body's `results` list is exactly the doc-08 step-2 "reconstruct the
legacy checkpoint body from typed rows" requirement (the body's `results` field
is listed in doc 08 § Checkpoint Transaction step 2 as
`["ImplementationResult model dumps"]`). The 08d contract was NOT changed.

**Body shape now produced.** Part A: `GroupMergeCoordinator.__init__` gained an
optional additive `task_results_provider` parameter (default `None` — the 08d
coordinator unit tests do not wire it, so `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` still
emits `results: []` for them, no 08d behavior change). `_reconstruct_checkpoint
_body` gained an optional `results` kwarg. When the provider is wired,
`checkpoint_group` calls it and threads the per-task `ImplementationResult`
model-dumps into the body's `results` list. `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue
_group` (`implementation.py`) gained an additive `task_results` parameter,
builds a `_task_results_provider` that emits exactly one dump per expected task
id (in expected-id order; a defensive fallback synthesizes a minimal
`completed` `ImplementationResult` carrying only the typed task id if a dump is
genuinely missing — never fabricated work), and the `if pending_merge_queue:`
call site passes `task_results=pending_merge_queue`. The projected
`dag-group:{group_idx}` body now carries one validatable `ImplementationResult`
dump per group task id — it passes `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks`.

**Why it now passes the gate.** Part B: a NEW helper
`_dag_group_queue_checkpoint_is_fresh` (`implementation.py:19383`) and a NEW
branch in `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`. When the legacy proof artifacts are
absent, the new branch checks whether the durable queue's TYPED
`merge_queue_items` rows back the `dag-group:*` body: every expected task id is
covered by exactly one `done` lane (a lane reaches `done` only via
`complete_checkpoint`, which the schema requires to carry non-null
`merge_proof_evidence_id`/`commit_proof_evidence_id`/`checkpoint_gate_evidence_
id`/`checkpoint_evidence_id`/`checkpoint_projection_id` — doc 08 § Merge Queue
Schema); NO lane covering an expected task is in any non-`done` status (a
partially-checkpointed group is rejected); all `done` lanes share ONE
`checkpoint_projection_id` (no split-brain); and the lanes' `result_commit`s
reconstruct the body's `commit_hash` and match the current canonical repo
heads. On success the branch returns `True`; on ANY failure (missing module/
store, DB error, incomplete typed evidence) it returns `False` and the gate
falls through to the UNCHANGED legacy-marker logic — a queue checkpoint is
never judged fresh on incomplete typed evidence. This new branch is purely
additive: the legacy path (proof artifacts present, or the legacy-marker
fallback) is byte-for-byte unchanged.

**No other legacy `dag-group:*` consumers.** Confirmed by grep that
`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` has exactly two call sites — the
`_implement_dag` resume reconstruction loop (`implementation.py:16218`) and the
`post_test_observation.py` post-test guard (`post_test_observation.py:407`).
`_recover_dag_group_checkpoint_from_proofs` also calls it, but only when the
`dag-group:*` artifact is ABSENT (not the queue case — the queue WRITES the
artifact). `_ensure_dag_group_checkpoint_projection_for_resume` returns an early
`(True, "")` for the queue path (the `dag-checkpoint-gate-proof:*` artifact is
absent so its schema check short-circuits). Both real consumers now accept the
queue body.

### P2-a (FIXED) — resume re-drives the idempotent drain + checkpoint of an `integrated` group

The review's P2-a (vector 6): on a resume after the drain succeeded but the
checkpoint crashed, the `integrated` group is stranded `workflow_blocked` — the
per-task resume block re-blocks on the surviving pending marker without
re-running the idempotent drain + `checkpoint_group`.

Fixed it (not deferred). A new additive helper
`_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group` (`implementation.py:6521`) +
`_MergeQueueResumeRecovery` re-drives the EXACT idempotent path: reconstruct the
per-task `ImplementationResult`s from the durable `dag-task:{tid}` markers, then
`_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` (a no-op for already-`integrated`
lanes — `claim` cannot re-take them, so NO double-apply) then
`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` (an idempotent no-op success for an
already-checkpointed group, doc-08 step-3 checkpoint key). A new additive block
in `_implement_dag`'s per-task resume area probes for any pending
`dag-task-pending-merge:*` marker; when present it calls the recovery; on
`recovered=True` it records handover from the durable `dag-task:*` markers,
deletes the now-stale pending markers, logs `dag_merge_queue_group_
checkpointed`, refreshes the public exhibit, and `group_idx += 1; continue`
(mirroring the main `if pending_merge_queue:` success tail). On `recovered=
False` it falls through to the UNCHANGED fail-closed per-task pending-marker
block (typed `workflow_blocked`). It NEVER falls back to the legacy
`_commit_group` / direct checkpoint. Surgical: the new block is gated entirely
on a pending marker being present, so the non-queue resume path is untouched;
it is placed at the per-task resume block (the reviewed location), not earlier
in the resume loop — going earlier would be the broad resume-loop surgery the
remediation prompt warns against.

### P2-b (FIXED) — tests now exercise the `_implement_dag` call site, a resume, and the post-test guard

Added 7 new tests to `test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py` (6 original → 13 total),
all real-Postgres:

- `test_queue_checkpoint_body_passes_the_legacy_resume_freshness_gate` — the
  full enqueue→drain→checkpoint path against a canonical repo inside a real
  feature workspace, then `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` (THE predicate the
  resume loop AND the post-test guard both call) over the projected
  `dag-group:1` body must return `True`. Also asserts `_dag_group_queue_
  checkpoint_is_fresh` directly.
- `test_pre_p1_fix_empty_results_body_is_rejected_by_the_freshness_gate` —
  pins the P1: the post-fix body is fresh; a `results: []`-stripped copy of it
  is rejected — proving the freshness gate is genuinely the gate that rejected
  the old body (the regression test is not a tautology).
- `test_implement_dag_resume_skips_a_queue_checkpointed_group` — drives the
  REAL `_implement_dag` resume reconstruction loop: a one-group sandbox/queue
  feature whose group was queue-checkpointed is resumed and reaches
  `terminal_state == "complete"` without dispatching the task.
- `test_post_test_guard_accepts_a_queue_projected_checkpoint` — reproduces the
  post-test guard's exact `stale_groups` freshness loop over a real
  queue-projected `dag-group:1` body and asserts the group is NOT flagged
  stale.
- `test_resume_recovery_redrives_a_drained_uncheckpointed_group` /
  `test_resume_recovery_is_idempotent_on_an_already_checkpointed_group` /
  `test_resume_recovery_fails_closed_on_a_failed_lane` — the P2-a recovery
  function `_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group` directly: completes a
  drained-but-uncheckpointed group, is an idempotent no-op on an already-`done`
  group (no double-projection), and fails closed (no `dag-group:*`) on a lane
  that cannot integrate.

**Pre-fix-failure confirmation.** The 4 freshness/resume/post-test tests were
run against a pre-fix simulation (`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` reverted to
`results: []` AND the Part B branch disabled): ALL 4 FAILED — including
`test_implement_dag_resume_skips_a_queue_checkpointed_group`, which failed with
`assert 'workflow_blocked' == 'complete'` exactly as the review required. The
source was then restored and all 13 tests pass. The 3 P2-a recovery tests
exercise a NEW function (`_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group`), so a
pre-fix run is N/A; they verify the recovery is correct, idempotent, and
fail-closed. (A full-`_implement_dag` end-to-end P2-a test was attempted but
the resume path runs `_compile_task_contracts_for_group` — which needs a real
`dag` artifact + workspace registry fixture before the per-task resume block is
reached; testing `_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group` directly is the
faithful, unambiguous coverage for the P2-a logic, and the
`test_implement_dag_resume_skips_a_queue_checkpointed_group` test already
proves the real `_implement_dag` resume call site for the P1.)

### P3 (DEFERRED to 08g) — fixed `item_id=0` router idempotency key

The review's P3: `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group._route` passes a fixed
`item_id=0`, so the two checkpoint-failure causes (coverage-not-approved,
`checkpoint_group`-declined) share one router idempotency key. The review rated
this benign (same `failure_class`, same deterministic `quiesce` route) and
optional. The minimal fix would need either a signature change to the accepted
08e-3a `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` (a refactor the no-refactor
non-negotiable forbids for a P3) or a fake non-zero `item_id` (which also
populates `merge_queue_item_id`/`queue_item_id` in the failure payload — a fake
value there misrepresents a real queue item id). Deferred to 08g as a tracked
to-do — it is a cosmetic dedup-key nicety on a benign same-class same-route
failure, not worth a signature change to accepted code.

### Verification

- `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` — clean (exit 0).
- `git diff --check` — clean.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_
  checkpoint.py -q` — 13 passed (6 original + 7 new; real Postgres
  `localhost:5431` up).
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q` — 408 passed
  (401 baseline + 7 new) across THREE random-order full runs and one
  `-p no:randomly` run. One earlier single run hit an INTERMITTENT flake in
  `test_merge_queue_drain.py::test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_mid_apply` —
  its OWN setup `subprocess.run(["git","apply","--index","--3way"])` returned
  1 ("Applied patch cleanly" via the `--3way` fallback, i.e. the direct
  `--index` apply transiently failed). It is a git "racy index" condition (the
  test rewrites README.md within git's mtime-granularity window) in that
  test's setup, unrelated to this remediation (which touches no `git_service`/
  drain-apply code); it did NOT reproduce across 3 full random-order runs +
  5 isolated runs of the failing test-pair + 3 isolated runs of the drain file.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py -q` — 255
  passed (unchanged from the 08e-3b baseline).
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py -q` — 2 failed, 25
  passed: ONLY the 2 KNOWN pre-existing failures. Confirmed this remediation
  did NOT change them: `test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave`
  still fails at `assert 'workflow_blocked' == 'complete'` (line 1207);
  `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints` still fails
  with the documented `TypeError` at `_ensure_task_worktrees` (the sync-lambda
  monkeypatch) — that `TypeError` fires before the P2-a block is reached, so
  the new code does not alter the failure.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_
  coordinator.py test_merge_queue_drain.py -q` — 31 passed (20 coordinator +
  11 drain): the additive `GroupMergeCoordinator` / `_reconstruct_checkpoint_
  body` change is backward-compatible.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_workflow_quiesce.py -q` — 47 passed.

### Outcome

P1 fixed (both root causes — the body's `results` and the freshness gate's
typed-queue-evidence branch), P2-a fixed (resume re-drives the idempotent
drain + checkpoint instead of stranding the `integrated` group), P2-b fixed
(7 new tests including the real `_implement_dag` resume call site and the
post-test guard; the 4 P1 tests confirmed failing pre-fix), P3 deferred to 08g
with a journaled rationale. No working 08d/08e-1/08e-2/08e-3a code refactored;
the 08d contract was not changed. Targeted suites green; the only non-green is
the documented 2-known `test_dag_regroup` failures (unchanged) and one
non-reproducing pre-existing intermittent git-racy-index flake in a drain
test's setup. STATUS.md intentionally NOT overwritten — the reviewer asked for
a re-review after the P1 fix; the orchestrator dispatches it and finalizes.

## Slice 08e-3b RE-REVIEW — durable merge queue checkpoint freshness + resume re-drive (independent, re-review-only) (2026-05-21)

Independent RE-REVIEW subagent, RE-REVIEW-ONLY (no code changed). Target: the
Slice 08e-3b REMEDIATION of the original review's P1 + P2-a + P2-b. Reviewed
the working-tree state: `merge_queue.py`
(`GroupMergeCoordinator.__init__` / `coverage` / `checkpoint_group` /
`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` / the new `TaskResultsProvider`),
`implementation.py` (`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group:6258`, the new
`_dag_group_queue_checkpoint_is_fresh:19383`, the new branch in
`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh:19505`, `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks:18927`,
`_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group:6521`, the per-task resume re-drive
block `:16696-16791`, the `if pending_merge_queue:` checkpoint call site
`:17206-17477`, the legacy `_verify_and_fix_group` checkpoint body `:15908`),
and the 13 tests in `test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py`.

### Re-reviewer summary

The remediation is mostly sound — the `task_results_provider` is purely
additive, the legacy non-queue freshness path is byte-for-byte unchanged, the
P2-a resume re-drive is correct, P2-b adds 7 honest tests, fail-closed holds.
BUT the P1 fix is **incomplete**: it populated the body's `results` and added
a typed-evidence freshness branch, but left the body's `task_ids` field in
lexically-SORTED order, while the legacy resume freshness gate compares
`task_ids` ORDER-SENSITIVELY against `dag.execution_order[g]` (wave/DAG order,
NOT lexical). For any DAG group whose task ids are not coincidentally in
lexical order, the queue checkpoint is STILL rejected as stale — the original
P1 symptom (resume breakage + post-test stale-quiesce) recurs. **Verdict: NOT
clean. One P1 remains.** STATUS.md NOT overwritten.

### Findings

**P1 — `merge_queue.py:1114-1120` (`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`) +
`merge_queue.py:868` (`coverage()` sets `expected_task_ids=sorted(expected)`)
+ `implementation.py:6334` (`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group`'s
`ordered_expected = sorted(...)`) → consumed by `implementation.py:19521`
(`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`'s ordered `list(checkpoint["task_ids"]) !=
list(group_task_ids)` check) — the queue `dag-group:*` body's `task_ids` is
lexically sorted, but the legacy freshness gate compares it order-sensitively
against the DAG/wave task order, so a non-lexically-sorted queue-checkpointed
group is STILL rejected as stale on resume AND by the post-test guard.** The
remediation correctly fixed the empty-`results` half of the original P1 and
added the typed-evidence branch (`_dag_group_queue_checkpoint_is_fresh`), but
the projected body's `task_ids` is built from `coverage.expected_task_ids`,
which `coverage()` sets to `sorted(expected)` (line 868), and
`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` copies verbatim (`task_ids:
list(coverage.expected_task_ids)`, line 1116). Independently,
`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` sorts again (`ordered_expected =
sorted({...})`, line 6334) and its `_expected_provider` returns that sorted
list. So the projected `dag-group:*` body's `task_ids` is unconditionally
lexically sorted.
  `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` (line 19521) does
  `if list(checkpoint.get("task_ids") or []) != list(group_task_ids): return
  False` — an ELEMENT-WISE ORDERED comparison, evaluated BEFORE the new
  typed-evidence branch (line 19566+) is ever reached. Its callers pass DAG
  order, not sorted order:
  - the `_implement_dag` resume reconstruction loop: `group_task_ids =
    list(dag.execution_order[g_idx])` (`implementation.py:16408`);
  - the `post_test_observation.py` post-test guard: `group_task_ids` iterates
    `dag.execution_order` (`post_test_observation.py:395`/`411`).
  The legacy `_verify_and_fix_group` checkpoint body sets `task_ids:
  [t.id for t in group_tasks]` (`implementation.py:15910`) where `group_tasks
  = [tasks_by_id[tid] for tid in group]` (`:16549`) — i.e. the legacy body's
  `task_ids` IS in `dag.execution_order` order, so the legacy path matches.
  The queue path does NOT. `dag.execution_order` group order is `_sort_key`
  order `(wave_index, wave_position, task_order, task_id)`
  (`task_planning.py:3278`/`3338-3340`) — `task_order` (the task's original
  index in `dag.tasks`) dominates the within-group order, so a group's order
  is generally NOT lexical task-id order (e.g. tasks declared `... UI-C,
  BACKEND-D` landing in one wave yield `[UI-C, BACKEND-D]`, whereas the queue
  body emits `sorted` = `[BACKEND-D, UI-C]`). Verified by direct simulation:
  `list(['BACKEND-D','UI-C']) != list(['UI-C','BACKEND-D'])` is `True` →
  `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` returns `False` at line 19521.
  Net effect: identical to the ORIGINAL P1 — the resume loop logs "checkpoint
  marker is stale" and `break`s without advancing `start_group`, then the
  per-task resume block re-blocks the feature `workflow_blocked`; and the
  post-test guard appends the group to `stale_groups` and quiesces with
  `post_test_blocked_dag_checkpoint_stale`. A queue-driven feature with any
  non-lexically-sorted DAG group still cannot resume past or complete a
  checkpointed group. The 13 tests miss this because every test group is
  trivially lexically sorted: the single-task tests use `["TASK-1"]` (one
  element), and the multi-task test uses `["TASK-A","TASK-B"]` (`A` < `B`) —
  no test exercises a group whose `dag.execution_order` order diverges from
  `sorted()`.
  Suggested fix: make the queue checkpoint body's `task_ids` carry the SAME
  order the legacy `_verify_and_fix_group` body uses — the DAG/wave order, not
  lexical sort. The cleanest additive fix is to pass the DAG-ordered expected
  task ids through to `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` (e.g.
  `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` already receives `expected_task_ids`
  in `pending_merge_queue` order — thread the DAG group order, not a `sorted`
  copy, into the body's `task_ids` while keeping `coverage`'s internal set
  logic order-insensitive). Alternatively, make `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`'s
  `task_ids` check order-insensitive (`sorted(...) == sorted(...)`) — but that
  is a change to a SHARED legacy consumer and must be proven not to relax any
  legacy invariant, so the body-order fix is safer. Add a test with a
  non-lexically-sorted multi-task group (e.g. `execution_order=[["UI-C",
  "BACKEND-D"]]`) driving the real `_implement_dag` resume + the post-test
  guard.

### Vector-by-vector

1. **P1 body-shape fix — DIVERGENT (the remaining P1).** The `results` field
   IS now correctly populated: `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` threads the
   coordinator's `task_results_provider` output into `results`, and
   `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group._task_results_provider` emits exactly
   one `ImplementationResult` model-dump per expected task id (with a defensive
   minimal-`completed` synth for a genuinely missing dump — never fabricated
   work). That half satisfies `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks` (which is
   order-insensitive: `sorted(result_task_ids) == sorted(group_task_ids)`).
   `commit_hash` is consistent (`",".join(sorted(result_commits))` on both the
   body side and the `_dag_group_queue_checkpoint_is_fresh` reconstruction
   side). `dag_sha256` is absent from the queue body but the legacy body has
   it — NOT a finding: `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` never indexes the
   body's `dag_sha256` (it takes `dag_sha256` as a parameter). The defect is
   ONLY the `task_ids` ordering — see the P1 above.
2. **CRITICAL — legacy (non-queue) path unchanged — VERDICT: PASS (legacy path
   byte-for-byte safe).** The new branch in the SHARED
   `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` is inside `if not proof_raw and not
   gate_proof_raw:` (`implementation.py:19566`) — it activates ONLY when BOTH
   the `dag-group-commit-proof:{group_idx}` AND
   `dag-checkpoint-gate-proof:{group_idx}` artifacts are absent. A legacy
   non-queue checkpoint ALWAYS writes `dag-group-commit-proof:*` (verified:
   `_record_dag_group_commit_proof` unconditionally `runner.artifacts.put`s it,
   `implementation.py:19757`, and `_verify_and_fix_group` always calls it
   before projecting `dag-group:*`, `:15860`), so a legacy feature NEVER
   enters the new branch — it takes the unchanged `else` at `:19593-19627`
   (the legacy `dag-group-commit-proof-v1` schema + `_checkpoint_gate_proof_is_
   fresh` logic). Further, even if a non-queue feature somehow had both proofs
   absent, `_dag_group_queue_checkpoint_is_fresh` calls
   `MergeQueueStore.list_group_items` and returns `False` at line 19457
   (`if not items`) for any feature with no merge-queue rows — it cannot
   return `True` for a non-queue feature. The both-absent fall-through
   (`:19589-19592`, the unchanged `_feature_has_execution_control_legacy_marker`
   + `_checkpoint_commit_matches_current_heads` check) is byte-for-byte the
   pre-remediation behavior; the new branch is a pure additive `if ...: return
   True` ahead of it. **No legacy non-queue freshness regression.** This vector
   is clean.
3. **`task_results_provider` additivity — PASS.** `GroupMergeCoordinator.
   __init__` gained `task_results_provider: TaskResultsProvider | None = None`
   as the 4th positional, defaulting `None`. `checkpoint_group` calls it only
   `if self._task_results_provider is not None` and otherwise threads
   `results=[]` into `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` exactly as before — the 08d
   coordinator unit tests (which never wire it) are unchanged behavior
   (confirmed: `test_merge_queue_coordinator.py` 20 tests pass per the
   remediation log). The 08d `MergeResult` / `GroupMergeCoverage` /
   `CheckpointProjection` models and the `checkpoint_group` transaction body
   are unchanged. No 08d refactor.
4. **No completion-inference from `dag-task:*` — PASS.** The remediation reads
   `dag-task:{tid}` marker CONTENT in two places —
   `_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group:6562-6575` and the resume-block
   handover reconstruction `:16727-16741` — but only to rebuild the
   `ImplementationResult` `results` payload. Group COMPLETION is still decided
   exclusively by the typed lane states: `coverage()` reads `merge_queue_items`
   / `merge_queue_task_coverage` rows, and `_dag_group_queue_checkpoint_is_
   fresh` requires every expected task covered by exactly one terminal `done`
   `merge_queue_items` lane. The `dag-task:*` markers are never treated as a
   completion signal. Doc 08 § Persistence And Artifact Compatibility honored.
5. **P2-a resume recovery — PASS.** `_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group`
   re-drives the IDEMPOTENT `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature` (a no-op
   for already-`integrated`/`done` lanes — `claim` cannot re-take them, so NO
   double-apply) then `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` (idempotent
   no-op success for an already-`done` group). It is gated in `_implement_dag`
   entirely on a pending `_pending_merge_queue_marker_key` marker being present
   on the group (`:16707-16714`), so the non-queue resume path is untouched.
   On `recovered=True` it advances (`group_idx += 1; continue`); on
   `recovered=False` (drain failed a stale lane, or any module/store error) it
   falls through to the UNCHANGED fail-closed per-task pending-marker block
   (typed `workflow_blocked`, `:16804+`). It NEVER falls back to the legacy
   `_commit_group` / direct checkpoint. The 3 P2-a tests cover redrive,
   idempotent no-op, and fail-closed. (Note: the recovered checkpoint's body
   inherits the same P1 `task_ids` ordering defect — a later plain resume
   would still re-stale it — but the recovery FUNCTION itself is correct.)
6. **P2-b test honesty — PASS (with the P1 blind spot).** The 7 new tests are
   genuine: `test_implement_dag_resume_skips_a_queue_checkpointed_group`
   drives the REAL `_implement_dag` resume loop with a `_no_dispatch` guard
   that hard-fails on any task dispatch and asserts `terminal_state ==
   "complete"`; `test_post_test_guard_accepts_a_queue_projected_checkpoint`
   reproduces the post-test guard's `stale_groups` loop;
   `test_pre_p1_fix_empty_results_body_is_rejected_by_the_freshness_gate`
   pins the gate is genuinely the gate. The remediation's claim that the 4 P1
   tests fail pre-fix is plausible — the empty-`results` body fails
   `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks`, and `test_implement_dag_resume_skips...`
   would return `workflow_blocked` exactly as described. BUT every test group
   is lexically sorted (`["TASK-1"]`, `["TASK-A","TASK-B"]`), so the suite
   does NOT exercise the P1 `task_ids` ordering mismatch — that is the test
   gap that let the incomplete P1 fix through.
7. **Fail-closed preserved — PASS.** No silent fallback to `_commit_group` /
   direct checkpoint on the durable path: the `if pending_merge_queue:` block's
   enqueue/drain/checkpoint failures all return `_merge_queue_group_blocked`
   (typed `workflow_blocked`); `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` raises
   `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` on missing modules/store and routes
   coverage/coordinator failures as `checkpoint_contradiction`;
   `_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group` returns `recovered=False` and
   the caller blocks. Confirmed no `_commit_group` reachable from any durable
   branch.

### Verdict on vector 2 (the shared-consumer legacy-path safety)

**CLEAN.** The new branch in the shared `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`
activates ONLY for queue-projected checkpoints (both legacy proof artifacts
absent AND `merge_queue_items` typed rows present). A legacy non-queue feature
always writes `dag-group-commit-proof:*`, so it never reaches the new branch
and its freshness semantics are byte-for-byte unchanged
(`implementation.py:19593-19627` + the unchanged `:19589-19592` fall-through).
No P1 regression to the legacy path.

### Overall verdict

08e-3b is **STILL BLOCKED — one P1 remains** (the queue `dag-group:*` body's
`task_ids` is lexically sorted while the shared legacy freshness gate
`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh:19521` compares it order-sensitively against
the DAG/wave task order, so a non-lexically-sorted queue-checkpointed group is
still rejected as stale on resume and by the post-test guard — the original P1
symptom recurs for those groups). The remediation correctly fixed the
empty-`results` half of the P1, the typed-evidence freshness branch is sound
and the legacy non-queue path is byte-for-byte safe (vector 2 CLEAN), P2-a and
P2-b are correctly remediated, and fail-closed holds. Fix: make the queue
checkpoint body's `task_ids` carry the DAG/wave order the legacy
`_verify_and_fix_group` body uses (not a `sorted` copy), and add a test with a
non-lexically-sorted multi-task group driving the real `_implement_dag` resume
+ the post-test guard. Re-review after this P1 fix. STATUS.md NOT overwritten.

## Slice 08e-3b REMEDIATION 2 — queue checkpoint body `task_ids` DAG/wave order (2026-05-21)

Remediation of the independent 08e-3b RE-REVIEW's single residual P1. The first
remediation fixed the empty-`results` half of the original P1 and added the
typed-evidence freshness branch, but left the queue `dag-group:*` checkpoint
body's `task_ids` in lexically-SORTED order while the shared legacy resume
freshness gate compares it ORDER-SENSITIVELY against the DAG/wave task order —
so a non-lexically-sorted queue-checkpointed group was still rejected as stale,
recurring the ORIGINAL P1 symptom (resume `break` without advancing
`start_group`; post-test guard stale-quiesce). This remediation is a narrow,
additive ordering fix. No working 08d / 08e-1 / 08e-2 / 08e-3a / first-08e-3b
code was refactored. Files touched: `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/
execution/merge_queue.py`, `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py`, `tests/workflows/develop/execution/
test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py`.

### The residual P1 (FIXED) — the body's `task_ids` now carries DAG/wave order

Root cause confirmed by reading the verified anchors: the legacy resume
freshness gate `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` (`implementation.py:19521`) does
`if list(checkpoint.get("task_ids") or []) != list(group_task_ids): return
False` — an ELEMENT-WISE ORDERED comparison in the SHARED part of the function,
BEFORE the new typed-evidence branch (`:19566+`). Its two real callers pass
DAG/wave order: the `_implement_dag` resume reconstruction loop
(`group_task_ids = list(dag.execution_order[g_idx])`, `implementation.py:16408`)
and the `post_test_observation.py` post-test guard (`for group_idx,
group_task_ids in enumerate(dag.execution_order)`, `post_test_observation.py:
395`/`411`). `dag.execution_order` group order is `_sort_key` order dominated by
`task_order` (the task's original index in `dag.tasks`, `task_planning.py:
3248-3253`), NOT lexical task-id order. The legacy `_verify_and_fix_group` body
sets `task_ids: [t.id for t in group_tasks]` (`implementation.py:15910`) where
`group_tasks = [tasks_by_id[tid] for tid in group]` — i.e. DAG order, so the
legacy path matches. The queue path did NOT: the body's `task_ids` was built by
`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` from `coverage.expected_task_ids`
(`merge_queue.py:1116`), which `coverage()` sets to `sorted(expected)`
(`merge_queue.py:868`), and `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` independently
re-sorted (`ordered_expected = sorted(...)`, `implementation.py:6334`). So the
projected body's `task_ids` was unconditionally lexically sorted.

**The fix — thread the DAG/wave order into the body's `task_ids`.** Additive,
in three layers, mirroring exactly how the first remediation threaded
`results`:

1. `merge_queue.py` — `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` gained an optional
   `task_ids: list[str] | None` kwarg. When supplied the body's `task_ids` is
   that list; when `None` it falls back to `list(coverage.expected_task_ids)`
   exactly as before (the 08d coordinator unit tests, which never wire it, are
   unchanged). A new `BodyTaskIdsProvider` callable type and an optional 5th
   `GroupMergeCoordinator.__init__` positional `body_task_ids_provider`
   (default `None`); `checkpoint_group` calls it (when wired) and passes the
   result as `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body(..., task_ids=body_task_ids)`. The
   08d `checkpoint_group` transaction body is otherwise unchanged.
2. `implementation.py` — `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` gained an
   additive `dag_ordered_task_ids: list[str] | None` parameter. It computes
   `ordered_dag_body_task_ids` by filtering `dag_ordered_task_ids` to the
   expected task-id SET while preserving DAG order; if (and only if) that
   filtered list fully covers the expected set it is used, else it falls back
   to the sorted `ordered_expected` (fail-soft to the pre-remediation body
   shape — never a legacy commit). A new `_body_task_ids_provider` closure
   returns the DAG-ordered list (or the sorted fallback) and is passed as the
   coordinator's 5th argument.
3. The two `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` call sites thread the
   DAG/wave order: the `if pending_merge_queue:` block passes
   `dag_ordered_task_ids=list(group)` (`implementation.py` ~`17438`) where
   `group = dag.execution_order[group_idx]` — the SAME wave-ordered list the
   resume gate and the post-test guard pass as `group_task_ids`; the P2-a
   resume recovery `_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group` passes
   `dag_ordered_task_ids=list(group_task_ids)` (`implementation.py` ~`6688`)
   where its `group_task_ids` parameter is the caller's `list(group)` =
   `dag.execution_order[group_idx]`, so a later plain resume of a
   crash-recovered group is not re-staled.

**Why the body now matches the legacy reader's order-sensitive comparison.**
The body's `task_ids` is now the DAG/wave order — the identical list
`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` receives as `group_task_ids` from
`dag.execution_order[g]` — so `list(checkpoint["task_ids"]) ==
list(group_task_ids)` holds, exactly as the legacy `_verify_and_fix_group`
body's `[t.id for t in group_tasks]` already does.

**Why `coverage.expected_task_ids` was left `sorted`.** That field feeds the
08d coordinator's SET-based coverage logic (`missing`/`duplicate`/`unexpected`
membership) and the order-stable `_checkpoint_coverage_digest`
(`merge_queue.py:1187`, which `sorted()`s it anyway) — sorting it is correct
and deliberate, and the coverage gate `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks` is
itself set-based (`sorted(result_task_ids) == sorted(group_task_ids)`,
`implementation.py:18945`). Only the legacy BODY's `task_ids` artifact — the
thing the order-sensitive legacy reader indexes — needed the DAG order. The
re-review explicitly cautioned against making the shared
`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` comparison order-insensitive (a legacy
regression risk); this remediation does NOT touch that gate — it produces the
body in the right order. The body's `results` list is left as-is: no legacy
reader pairs `task_ids[i]` with `results[i]` positionally (the legacy body's
`checkpoint_results` is itself `results`-order, `implementation.py:15870-15874`,
not re-sorted to `group_tasks`), and `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks` validates
`results` set-wise — so `results` ordering is immaterial and stays consistent
with `task_ids` as a set.

### Test (added) — a NON-lexically-sorted multi-task group

Added `test_implement_dag_resume_skips_a_non_lexically_sorted_queue_group` to
`test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py` (13 -> 14 tests), real-Postgres. It builds a
two-repo feature workspace and a one-group DAG whose wave order is
`["UI-C", "BACKEND-D"]` — `sorted(...)` is `["BACKEND-D", "UI-C"]`, the two
DIFFER (the test asserts the premise). It enqueues + drains both lanes, then
checkpoints via `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` passing
`dag_ordered_task_ids=list(dag.execution_order[0])` exactly as the production
`if pending_merge_queue:` call site does, asserts the projected `dag-group:0`
body's `task_ids == ["UI-C", "BACKEND-D"]` (DAG order, NOT sorted), drives the
REAL `_implement_dag` resume (with a `_no_dispatch` guard that hard-fails on any
task dispatch) to `terminal_state == "complete"`, and reproduces the post-test
guard's exact `stale_groups` freshness loop over the same body asserting it is
NOT flagged stale. The existing 7 freshness/resume tests were blind to this
because every test group was trivially lexically sorted (`["TASK-1"]`,
`["TASK-A","TASK-B"]`).

**Pre-fix-failure confirmation.** The new test was run against a pre-fix
simulation — `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` reverted so the body's `task_ids`
ignores the override and always uses `coverage.expected_task_ids` (the exact
pre-REMEDIATION-2 behavior). It FAILED at `assert body["task_ids"] == ["UI-C",
"BACKEND-D"]` with `AssertionError: assert ['BACKEND-D', 'UI-C'] == ['UI-C',
'BACKEND-D']` — the body's `task_ids` was lexically sorted, exactly the residual
P1. A separate direct `_implement_dag` repro against the same pre-fix simulation
confirmed the downstream symptom: the resume loop judged the group stale, did
NOT skip it, and fell through into the dispatch loop (reaching
`_ensure_task_worktrees`) — i.e. the queue-checkpointed group is re-run, the
original P1 symptom. The source was then restored and the test passes.

### Verification

- `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` — clean (exit 0).
- `git diff --check` — clean.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q` — 409 passed
  (408 baseline + 1 new; real Postgres `localhost:5431` up). This includes
  `test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py` (14), `test_merge_queue_coordinator.py` (20)
  and `test_merge_queue_drain.py` (11) — the additive `GroupMergeCoordinator`
  5th-positional / `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` kwarg change is
  backward-compatible.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py -q` — 255
  passed (unchanged from the 08e-3b baseline).
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py -q` — 2 failed, 25
  passed: ONLY the 2 KNOWN pre-existing failures
  (`test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave`,
  `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints` — the
  documented `TypeError` at `_ensure_task_worktrees` from the sync-lambda
  monkeypatch). NO new failures.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_workflow_quiesce.py -q` — 47 passed.

### Outcome

The residual P1 is fixed: the queue `dag-group:*` checkpoint body's `task_ids`
now carries the DAG/wave order — the SAME order `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`
receives as `group_task_ids` — so the legacy reader's order-sensitive comparison
passes for non-lexically-sorted groups, exactly as the legacy
`_verify_and_fix_group` body already does. `coverage.expected_task_ids` was left
`sorted` (its set-based coverage role is correct); the shared legacy freshness
gate was NOT loosened (the fix produces the body in the right order). The new
non-lexical test fails pre-fix (confirmed) and passes after. All additive — no
working code refactored, the 08d contract unchanged. Targeted suites green; the
only non-green is the documented 2-known `test_dag_regroup` failures (unchanged).
STATUS.md intentionally NOT overwritten — a re-review follows.

## Slice 08e-3b RE-REVIEW 2 — queue checkpoint body `task_ids` DAG/wave order (independent, re-review-only) (2026-05-21)

Independent RE-REVIEW 2 subagent, RE-REVIEW-ONLY (no code changed). Target: the
Slice 08e-3b REMEDIATION 2 of the first RE-REVIEW's single residual P1 (the
queue `dag-group:*` checkpoint body's `task_ids` was lexically `sorted` while
the shared legacy resume freshness gate `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`
compares it ORDER-SENSITIVELY against the DAG/wave task order). Reviewed the
working-tree state: `merge_queue.py` (`BodyTaskIdsProvider`,
`GroupMergeCoordinator.__init__` 5th positional, `checkpoint_group`,
`_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`, `_checkpoint_coverage_digest`, `coverage`),
`implementation.py` (`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group:6258`, the
`dag_ordered_task_ids` param + `_body_task_ids_provider` closure, the two call
sites — the `if pending_merge_queue:` block `:17453` and
`_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group:6584`/`:6695` —
`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh:19584`, `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks:19006`,
the legacy `_verify_and_fix_group` body `:15978`), and the 14 tests in
`test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py`.

### Re-reviewer summary

The residual P1 is **FIXED** and the fix is **CLEAN**. REMEDIATION 2 threads the
DAG/wave order into the queue `dag-group:*` body's `task_ids` via three additive
layers; all five new surfaces (`BodyTaskIdsProvider`, the 5th coordinator
positional, the `task_ids` kwarg on `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`, the
`dag_ordered_task_ids` param + `_body_task_ids_provider` closure) are optional
with safe defaults, so 08d behavior is unchanged when absent. The body's
`task_ids` now carries the EXACT order `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` receives
as `group_task_ids` at both call sites; the shared legacy gate was NOT loosened;
`coverage.expected_task_ids` is correctly left `sorted`; the new non-lexical
test genuinely drives the real `_implement_dag` resume + the post-test guard.
**Verdict: CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2.** One P3 carried forward (the deferred
`item_id=0` shared router key, unchanged from REMEDIATION). STATUS.md finalized
to 08f.

### Vector-by-vector

1. **Ordering fix correctness — VERDICT: PASS (clean).** Traced end to end at
   BOTH call sites. The order-sensitive comparison is
   `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh:19600` —
   `if list(checkpoint.get("task_ids") or []) != list(group_task_ids): return
   False` — in the SHARED part of the function, BEFORE the new typed-evidence
   branch (`:19645`). Its two real consumers pass `dag.execution_order[g]`: the
   `_implement_dag` resume reconstruction loop (`group_task_ids =
   list(dag.execution_order[g_idx])`, `implementation.py:16478`) and the
   `post_test_observation.py` post-test guard (`for group_idx, group_task_ids
   in enumerate(dag.execution_order)`, `post_test_observation.py:395`/`411`).
   **Live `if pending_merge_queue:` checkpoint:** `_checkpoint_durable_merge_
   queue_group` is called with `dag_ordered_task_ids=list(group)`
   (`implementation.py:17453`); `group` is `dag.execution_order[group_idx]`
   (bound at `:16576`, re-bound to the regroup-effective DAG at `:16616` with
   `dag_sha256` recomputed at `:16613-16615` so the body and the gate share one
   effective DAG). Inside `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group`,
   `dag_ordered_task_ids` is filtered to the expected task-id SET preserving DAG
   order (`:6370-6379`); the covers-expected-set guard `if seen ==
   expected_task_id_set` (`:6378`) adopts it only when it fully covers the
   expected set, else falls back to the sorted `ordered_expected`. The
   `_body_task_ids_provider` closure (`:6463-6478`) returns that DAG-ordered
   list; it is passed as the coordinator's 5th positional (`:6491`).
   `GroupMergeCoordinator.checkpoint_group` calls the provider (`:1015-1020` of
   `merge_queue.py`) and passes `task_ids=body_task_ids` to
   `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` (`:1021-1023`), which builds the body's
   `task_ids` from it (`:1162-1166`). So for an all-durable-queue group (the
   normal sandbox case — every result requires the queue, expected set ==
   `group` as a set) the body's `task_ids` IS `list(dag.execution_order[g])`,
   the identical list the gate receives as `group_task_ids` — the order-sensitive
   `list(checkpoint["task_ids"]) == list(group_task_ids)` holds.
   **`_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group`:** passes
   `dag_ordered_task_ids=list(group_task_ids)` (`:6695`), where its
   `group_task_ids` parameter is the caller's `list(group)` =
   `dag.execution_order[group_idx]` (`implementation.py:16790`). So a
   crash-recovered group's re-driven checkpoint body also carries the DAG order
   and a later plain resume is not re-staled. Confirmed against the legacy
   `_verify_and_fix_group` body, which sets `task_ids: [t.id for t in
   group_tasks]` (`:15980`) where `group_tasks = [tasks_by_id[tid] for tid in
   group]` — i.e. DAG order; the queue body now matches that.
2. **`results`/`task_ids` consistency — PASS.** No legacy reader pairs
   `task_ids[i]` with `results[i]` positionally. The legacy body itself sets
   `results = checkpoint_results = [r.model_dump() for r in results if
   isinstance(r, ImplementationResult)]` (`implementation.py:15940-15981`) —
   worker-results order, NOT re-sorted to `group_tasks`; the body's `task_ids`
   and `results` are independently ordered. The freshness gate
   `_checkpoint_results_match_tasks` (`:19006-19024`) validates `results`
   SET-wise (`sorted(result_task_ids) == sorted(group_task_ids)` + count +
   no-duplicates) — never positionally. The resume `all_results`
   reconstruction (`:16536-16542`) iterates `data["results"]` independently and
   never consults `task_ids`. The REMEDIATION-2 reordering of `task_ids`
   therefore cannot desync `results`; the queue body's `_task_results_provider`
   emits `results` in `ordered_expected` order, which is immaterial.
3. **`coverage.expected_task_ids` untouched — PASS.** `coverage()` still sets
   `expected_task_ids=sorted(expected)` (`merge_queue.py:886`) — unchanged. The
   08d set-based coverage logic (`missing`/`duplicate`/`unexpected` membership)
   is order-insensitive. `_checkpoint_coverage_digest` (`merge_queue.py:1173`)
   hashes `",".join(sorted(coverage.expected_task_ids))` (`:1187`) — it sorts
   the field again, so the digest is order-stable regardless of the body's
   `task_ids` order. `result_commits` is also `sorted({...})` (`:898`). No
   coverage / digest impact.
4. **Shared gate not loosened — VERDICT: PASS (legacy gate byte-for-byte
   safe).** `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh:19600` is still
   `list(checkpoint["task_ids"]) != list(group_task_ids)` — ORDER-SENSITIVE,
   NOT changed to `sorted(...) == sorted(...)`. REMEDIATION 2 fixes the defect
   by PRODUCING the body in the correct order, not by relaxing the comparison.
   The legacy non-queue path is unaffected: a legacy feature always writes
   `dag-group-commit-proof:*` (so `proof_raw` is truthy), so it takes the
   unchanged `else` at `:19672-19706`; only when BOTH legacy proof artifacts
   are absent (the queue case) does the function reach the additive
   typed-evidence branch. The legacy gate's freshness semantics are
   byte-for-byte the pre-08e-3b behavior.
5. **Additivity — PASS.** `BodyTaskIdsProvider` is a new type alias.
   `GroupMergeCoordinator.__init__` gained `body_task_ids_provider:
   BodyTaskIdsProvider | None = None` as the 5th positional, default `None`.
   `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` gained `task_ids: list[str] | None = None`;
   when `None` it falls back to `list(coverage.expected_task_ids)` exactly as
   before (`:1160-1163`). `checkpoint_group` calls the provider only `if
   self._body_task_ids_provider is not None` (`:1015`). `_checkpoint_durable_
   merge_queue_group`'s `dag_ordered_task_ids` defaults `None`; when `None` (or
