   when the filtered list does not fully cover the expected set) it falls back
   to the sorted `ordered_expected` — fail-soft to the pre-remediation body
   shape, never a legacy commit. The covers-expected-set guard (`if seen ==
   expected_task_id_set`, `:6378`) + the sorted fallback in `_body_task_ids_
   provider` (`:6476-6478`) is sound: it deduplicates while preserving DAG
   order and adopts the DAG order only when it is provably the full expected
   set. The 08d coordinator unit tests (20) and the drain tests (11) — none of
   which wire the 5th positional — pass unchanged (confirmed by the test run
   below), proving 08d's `GroupMergeCoordinator` behavior is unchanged when the
   new provider is absent.
6. **Test honesty — PASS.** `test_implement_dag_resume_skips_a_non_lexically_
   sorted_queue_group` (the 14th test) builds a real two-repo feature workspace
   and a one-group DAG with `execution_order=[["UI-C", "BACKEND-D"]]`; it
   asserts the premise (`list(dag.execution_order[0]) == ["UI-C","BACKEND-D"]`,
   `sorted(...) == ["BACKEND-D","UI-C"]`, the two DIFFER —
   `test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py:1060-1062`). It enqueues + drains BOTH lanes
   against the DAG's real sha (computed the same way `_implement_dag` computes
   `dag_sha256`), checkpoints via `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group`
   passing `dag_ordered_task_ids=list(dag.execution_order[0])` exactly as the
   production `if pending_merge_queue:` call site, asserts the projected
   `dag-group:0` body's `task_ids == ["UI-C","BACKEND-D"]` and `!=
   sorted(...)`, drives the **REAL** `_implement_dag` resume (`:1135`, with
   `runner.run = _no_dispatch` which raises `AssertionError` on any dispatch)
   to `terminal_state == "complete"`, and reproduces the post-test guard's
   exact `stale_groups` freshness loop over the same body asserting
   `stale_groups == []`. The pre-fix-failure claim is plausible from the test
   code: pre-fix the body's `task_ids` is `coverage.expected_task_ids` =
   `sorted` = `["BACKEND-D","UI-C"]`, failing the `:1106` assertion and the
   order-sensitive `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh:19600` compare; the
   REMEDIATION-2 journal documents a confirmed pre-fix simulation run that
   FAILED at `:1106` AND a direct `_implement_dag` repro that fell into the
   dispatch loop reaching `_ensure_task_worktrees` — the original P1 symptom.
   The existing 7 freshness/resume tests were blind because every test group
   was trivially lexically sorted.
7. **No regression — PASS.** Fail-closed is preserved: the durable path still
   never falls back to `_commit_group` / a direct checkpoint; the
   covers-expected-set guard's fallback is the sorted body shape, not a legacy
   commit. Targeted suites green (see Verification): `test_merge_queue_
   checkpoint.py` 14, `test_merge_queue_coordinator.py` 20, `test_merge_queue_
   drain.py` 11, the full `tests/workflows/develop/execution/` 409,
   `test_dag_expanded_verify.py` + `test_workflow_quiesce.py` 302; only the 2
   documented pre-existing `test_dag_regroup.py` failures remain (the
   `_ensure_task_worktrees` sync-lambda monkeypatch `TypeError` — splice-
   unrelated, carried to 08f).

### Verdict on vector 1 (ordering) and vector 4 (legacy gate safety)

- **Vector 1 (ordering): CLEAN.** The queue `dag-group:*` body's `task_ids` now
  carries the DAG/wave order — the identical `dag.execution_order[g]` list the
  shared legacy freshness gate receives as `group_task_ids` — at BOTH the live
  `if pending_merge_queue:` checkpoint and the `_resume_recover_durable_merge_
  queue_group` re-drive. The order-sensitive `list(checkpoint["task_ids"]) ==
  list(group_task_ids)` comparison now holds for a non-lexically-sorted group,
  exactly as the legacy `_verify_and_fix_group` body already does. The residual
  P1 is fixed.
- **Vector 4 (legacy gate safety): CLEAN.** `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`'s
  `task_ids` comparison was NOT made order-insensitive; the legacy non-queue
  freshness path is byte-for-byte behaviorally unchanged. The fix produces the
  body in the right order rather than loosening the shared gate.

### Verification

- `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` — clean (exit 0).
- `git diff --check` — clean.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_
  checkpoint.py test_merge_queue_coordinator.py test_merge_queue_drain.py -q
  -p no:randomly` — 45 passed (14 checkpoint + 20 coordinator + 11 drain; real
  Postgres `localhost:5431`).
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q -p no:randomly` —
  409 passed.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py
  tests/workflows/test_workflow_quiesce.py -q -p no:randomly` — 302 passed.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py -q -p no:randomly` —
  2 failed, 25 passed: ONLY the 2 KNOWN pre-existing failures
  (`test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave`,
  `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints` — the
  documented `_ensure_task_worktrees` sync-lambda monkeypatch `TypeError`). NO
  new failures.

### Outcome

The Slice 08e-3b residual P1 is fixed and the fix is CLEAN. The queue
`dag-group:*` checkpoint body's `task_ids` now carries the DAG/wave order so the
shared legacy resume freshness gate's order-sensitive comparison passes for
non-lexically-sorted groups; the gate itself was not loosened;
`coverage.expected_task_ids` is correctly left `sorted`; all new surfaces are
additive; the new non-lexical test genuinely drives the real `_implement_dag`
resume + the post-test guard. **Verdict: CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 1 carried P3.**
STATUS.md finalized to point the next iteration at Slice 08f.

## Slice 08e-3b COMPLETE — durable merge queue drain wiring + post-DAG checkpoint splice (2026-05-21)

Slice 08e-3b is COMPLETE. The independent RE-REVIEW 2 returned a CLEAN verdict
(0 P1, 0 P2) on the REMEDIATION 2 fix of the residual checkpoint-body
compatibility P1. The durable merge queue is now LIVE end-to-end: the 08e-2
canonical-commit splice enqueues `task:{id}` lanes, the 08e-3a drain worker
applies/gates/commits/integrates them, and the 08e-3b `if pending_merge_queue:`
call site wires the drain into `_implement_dag` and replaces the legacy
post-DAG `_commit_group` / `_record_dag_group_commit_proof` /
`_project_dag_group_checkpoint` path with `GroupMergeCoordinator.checkpoint_
group` — projecting the `dag-group:*` compatibility artifact from the typed
`merge_queue_items` rows. The 08e integration (08e-1 + 08e-2 + 08e-3a + 08e-3b)
is COMPLETE.

The review loop took **1 review + 2 remediation + 2 re-review passes** to
converge. The single hard problem throughout was the checkpoint-body
compatibility P1: the queue-projected `dag-group:*` body must be byte-shape
compatible with what the SHARED legacy resume freshness gate
`_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` (consumed by the `_implement_dag` resume
reconstruction loop AND the `post_test_observation.py` post-test guard)
requires. The original review found the body's `results` was empty; the first
remediation populated `results` + added the typed-`merge_queue_items`-evidence
freshness branch; the first re-review found the body's `task_ids` was still
lexically `sorted` while the gate compares it ORDER-SENSITIVELY against the
DAG/wave order; REMEDIATION 2 threaded the DAG/wave order into the body's
`task_ids`; RE-REVIEW 2 confirmed CLEAN.

Files changed in 08e-3b (across the implementation + 2 remediations, all
additive — no working 08d/08e-1/08e-2/08e-3a code refactored, the 08d contract
unchanged):

- `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/merge_queue.py` — additive
  `TaskResultsProvider` + `BodyTaskIdsProvider` types; optional 4th
  `task_results_provider` + 5th `body_task_ids_provider` positionals on
  `GroupMergeCoordinator.__init__`; optional `results` + `task_ids` kwargs on
  `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`; `checkpoint_group` threads both when wired.
- `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py` —
  `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` (the post-DAG checkpoint splice) with
  additive `task_results` + `dag_ordered_task_ids` params and the
  `_task_results_provider` / `_body_task_ids_provider` closures; the
  `if pending_merge_queue:` drain + checkpoint call site in `_implement_dag`;
  `_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group` (P2-a crash recovery);
  `_dag_group_queue_checkpoint_is_fresh` + the additive typed-evidence branch
  in `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`.
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py` — 14
  real-Postgres tests (6 original + 8 added across the remediations), incl. the
  real `_implement_dag` resume call site, the post-test guard, and the
  non-lexically-sorted-group regression test.

Deferred P3 (carried to 08f): the checkpoint-failure `_route` in
`_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` uses a fixed `item_id=0`, so the two
checkpoint-failure causes (coverage-not-approved, `checkpoint_group`-declined)
share one router idempotency key. Benign — both are the same `failure_class`
and route to the same deterministic `quiesce` — recorded for 08f.

Targeted suites green: `tests/workflows/develop/execution/` 409 passed,
`test_dag_expanded_verify.py` + `test_workflow_quiesce.py` 302 passed; the only
non-green is the 2 documented pre-existing `test_dag_regroup.py` failures
(unchanged, splice-unrelated). Next: Slice 08f — the full test suite /
test-hardening pass per doc 08 § Tests.


## Slice 08f IMPLEMENTATION — full-suite test-hardening pass (2026-05-21)

Slice 08f is COMPLETE in one pass. The COMPLETE test suite is GREEN end-to-end,
the 2 pre-existing `test_dag_regroup.py` failures are fixed, the intermittent
`test_merge_queue_drain.py` git-racy-index flake is deterministically fixed, 5
UNDOCUMENTED pre-existing failures were found + fixed, and the 8-item carried
P3 to-do list is fully dispositioned (6 resolved, 2 re-deferred with rationale).
No product bug found. 08f was NOT split — the unexpected failures all had a
clean bounded non-weakening fix.

### (A) Full test suite — GREEN

Single-process run (the suite completes in ~3 min, no sharding needed):

- `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` — clean (exit 0).
- `git diff --check` — clean (exit 0).
- BASELINE (before any 08f edit) `python -m pytest tests/ -q -p no:randomly` —
  **7 failed, 1823 passed, 1389 warnings** (1830 collected). The 7: the 2
  documented `test_dag_regroup` failures + 5 UNDOCUMENTED failures (4 in
  `test_liveness_watchdog.py`, 1 in `test_no_truncation.py`).
- FINAL `python -m pytest tests/ -q -p no:randomly` — **1832 passed, 1389
  warnings in 183.78s; 0 failed, 0 skipped** (1830 original + 2 new 08f
  real-PG smoke tests). Real Postgres `localhost:5431` reachable, so every
  real-PG test ran — no environment SKIPs.
- Flake confirmation: `test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_mid_apply` run 30×
  post-fix — 30/30 pass (pre-fix it was ~1-in-10 fail).

### (B) The 2 pre-existing `test_dag_regroup.py` failures — FIXED

The documented cause (a sync-`lambda` `_ensure_task_worktrees` monkeypatch) was
only the proximate trigger for `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_
regroup_checkpoints`. The real, deeper cause for BOTH tests: they were authored
for the pre-Slice-03/04/05/06 legacy `_implement_dag` path, and the durable
control-plane slices added HARD preflight gates in front of group dispatch that
the tests do not satisfy in a no-`workspace_manager` harness:

1. `_compile_task_contracts_for_group` (Slice 03) fails closed with
   `contract_missing_workspace_registry` → `workflow_blocked` — this, NOT the
   queue path, is what blocked `test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_
   wave` (verified by instrumenting `_implement_dag` directly: the failure text
   was "Task contract compiler has no workspace registry before dispatch").
2. `_dispatch_task_attempt_via_runtime_dispatcher` (Slice 05) binds a per-task
   Slice 04 sandbox; with no workspace roots `_bind_task_sandbox` raises a
   sandbox blocker.
3. `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` (Slice 06) now requires `verdict`,
   `task_ids`, `results`, `commit_hash` + durable proof artifacts; the tests'
   pre-seeded `{"group_idx": i, "results": []}` `dag-group:*` bodies are stale
   against it, so the resume scan re-runs already-checkpointed groups — that
   re-run is where `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_
   checkpoints` hit the sync-lambda `TypeError`.

Fix (in `tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py`, both tests, additive — extends
the test's EXISTING monkeypatch set `_verify_and_fix_group`/`_commit_repos`/
`_ensure_task_worktrees`/`_run_enhancement_group`): the tests verify regroup
overlay wave ORDERING + RESUME, not the sandbox/contract/merge-queue
machinery (each has its own dedicated suite). Added monkeypatches for
`_compile_task_contracts_for_group` (→ approved-empty `TaskContractCompile
Outcome`), `_dispatch_task_attempt_via_runtime_dispatcher` (→ invokes the test
runtime directly, returns a bare result so the legacy verify+checkpoint path
the test stubs runs), and `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh` (→ True when a
pre-seeded `dag-group:*` body exists, the test's pre-Slice-06 assumption). The
sync-`lambda` `_ensure_task_worktrees`/`_commit_repos` stubs are made async
coroutines (the documented fix). NOT weakened: the production gates are
untouched; the tests' "must not dispatch" safety assertion is preserved (the
contract stub keeps a wrongly-not-skipped group reaching `_Runner.run`, which
raises). Full `test_dag_regroup.py` — 27 passed.

### (C) The `test_merge_queue_drain.py` git-racy-index flake — FIXED

`test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_mid_apply` setup `git apply --index --3way`
transiently returned 1 with `error: README.md: does not match index`. ROOT
CAUSE confirmed by a faithful 200-iteration Python repro (~10% fail): the
`_diff_for_appended_line` helper rewrites README.md twice inside git's index
mtime granularity, leaving its index entry RACY-CLEAN — `git apply --index`
then intermittently treats the racy entry as not-matching. FIX (deterministic,
not a retry loop): `_git(repo, "update-index", "-q", "--refresh")` immediately
before the `git apply` — it re-stats tracked files and clears the racy flag and
does NOT mutate content. A 300-iteration repro with the refresh: 0 fails (vs
`reset --hard` / re-`add` / `git status` / `git diff` all also 0 — `update-
index --refresh` chosen as the semantically-honest fix for the exact documented
condition). The assert also now prints `git_service_apply.stderr` on failure.
Flaky test run 30× post-fix: 30/30. Full `test_merge_queue_drain.py` — 11
passed.

### (D) The 8-item carried P3 to-do list — disposition

1. **RESOLVED** — `merge_queue_wiring.py` stale `projection_links` docstrings
   (lines 97-99, 153) corrected to `execution_artifact_projections` (the real
   table the code's `_PROJECTION_OWNER_TABLE` constant already names).
2. **RESOLVED** — real-Postgres smoke coverage added for `record_patch_summary`
   + `record_contract_verdict` (2 new tests in `test_merge_queue_wiring.py`):
   each calls the `ExecutionControlStore` method against real PG and asserts
   the `evidence_nodes` row lands with a non-null `started_at`. Without the
   endorsed `store.py` `COALESCE($25, NOW())` fix the `NOT NULL` insert would
   raise — the fix was previously real-PG-exercised only via
   `record_verification_graph_node`.
3. **RESOLVED** — the `status="done"` kwarg on `GroupCheckpointProjection(...)`
   at `merge_queue_wiring.py:675` dropped. Verified: `_project_group_checkpoint`
   sets the journal row `status="succeeded"` unconditionally and never reads
   `projection.status` as a verdict; `_group_checkpoint_gate_fields` (the only
   reader of `projection.status` as a status) is dead code (no caller). The
   kwarg only leaked a stray `"status":"done"` into the typed-row payload
   metadata — dropping it is correct and removes confusing dead metadata.
4. **RESOLVED** — the 2 `test_dag_regroup.py` failures — see (B).
5. **RESOLVED** — doc-08 per-task-lane divergence note added (doc 08
   `08-durable-merge-queue.md`, after the "Queue item granularity" paragraph):
   an explicit "Implementation divergence (Slice 08e-2, intentional)" blockquote
   recording that `_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results` enqueues one
   `task:{task_id}` lane per task (never a combined `group` lane), that this is
   always isolation-safe, and that the only cost is queue-row volume.
6. **RE-DEFERRED to Slice 09** (structured-failure-evidence work) — the drain
   `contract_violation` route dead-ends at `quiesce`. CONFIRMED root cause:
   `failure_router._allows_product_repair` requires `repair_scope.target_paths`
   (+ `target_contract_ids` or an authorized direct source verdict) for a
   `contract_violation`/`outside_allowed_paths` route to reach
   `run_product_repair`; `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` builds the
   observation `payload` with no `target_paths` because the offending
   escaped/outside paths exist ONLY in `merge_queue.py` `apply_candidate`'s
   free-text `_ApplyFailure.detail` string (computed at lines 282-292 / 316-325
   as `sorted(outside)` / `sorted(escaped)`), NOT structurally — `MergeApply
   Result` has no `escaped_paths` field and `_ApplyFailure` carries only
   `failure_class` + `detail`. A clean fix needs structured escaped-paths
   threaded `_ApplyFailure` → `MergeApplyResult` → the drain → the router
   observation (~4-5 touch points across the REVIEWED 08d `MergeApplyResult`
   contract + 08e-3a drain). Per the standing NO-REFACTOR non-negotiable and the
   08f task's explicit guidance, that is a structured-failure-EVIDENCE feature,
   not a test-hardening fix; touching the just-converged reviewed 08d contract
   in 08f risks destabilizing it. Current behavior is FAIL-CLOSED and SAFE — a
   genuine contract escape `quiesce`s (no false product-repair loop, no unsafe
   mutation), exactly the documented intent. Belongs with Slice 09.
7. **RESOLVED** — the checkpoint-failure `_route` fixed `item_id=0` shared
   router idempotency key. A clean ADDITIVE fix WAS available (not the
   journal-noted "signature change to a reviewed function" — that framing
   missed the additive option): added an OPTIONAL `cause: str = ""` param to
   `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` folded into the router idempotency key
   only when non-empty (`+ (f":{cause}" if cause else "")`). The 3 per-lane
   drain callers pass a real `item_id` and leave `cause` empty → their keys are
   byte-for-byte UNCHANGED (verified: all 3 pass a real lane id, never 0). The
   `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group._route` helper now passes a distinct
   `cause` per checkpoint-failure cause (`coverage_not_approved`,
   `checkpoint_raised`, `checkpoint_declined` — there are actually 3 causes, not
   the 2 the journal noted), so each gets a distinct router dedup key. No test
   asserts the key shape; fully backward-compatible.
8. **RESOLVED** — the `test_merge_queue_drain.py` git-racy-index flake — see (C).

### (E) Undocumented pre-existing failures found + fixed (no silent degradation)

The baseline full-suite run revealed 5 failures NOT in STATUS.md's known list.
All are pre-existing in the uncommitted Slice 00-08e bundle (I made zero edits
before the baseline run) — they are a regression introduced by a slice AFTER
05: the journal's 2026-05-20T16:24:23Z + 2026-05-20T17:00:16Z entries record
`test_liveness_watchdog.py` GREEN at the end of Slice 04/05. A later slice
tightened the Slice 04 sandbox-manifest invariant and the sandbox-mandatory
repair path, breaking pre-bundle / earlier-green tests that weren't updated.
All 5 have a clean non-weakening fix:

- `test_no_truncation.py::test_single_rca_fix_verify_preserves_full_verdict_
  text` — `_single_rca_fix_verify` now binds a mandatory repair sandbox
  (`_bind_post_dag_product_repair_sandbox`); with no workspace the bind raises
  `SandboxWorkflowBlocker` and `fix_applied` becomes the blocker text. ALSO
  found: the file's 2 sibling `_single_rca_fix_verify` tests were passing
  VACUOUSLY (their assertions on `runner.tasks` are satisfied by the lone RCA
  Ask; they never reached the fix dispatch). FIX: the `_Runner` now sets
  `services={"test_allow_legacy_repair_without_sandbox": True}` — the SANCTIONED
  production test seam (`implementation.py` `_bind_repair_sandbox`, used 20+×
  in the green `test_dag_expanded_verify.py`) for exercising the legacy
  non-sandbox repair path; this makes all 3 tests genuinely run the fix
  dispatch. One sibling's stub `_thread_factory` then needed the new
  `runtime_workspace_binding`/`sandbox_required` kwargs the production
  `_make_parallel_actor` threads — added (faithful signature mirror). 5 passed.
- `test_liveness_watchdog.py` ×4 (`test_bound_runner_does_not_offload_prompt_
  into_bound_workspace`, `test_bound_runner_ignores_legacy_workspace_override_
  and_worktree_root`, `test_invalid_runtime_workspace_binding_metadata_fails_
  before_runtime[missing_expires_at]` + `[expired]`) — all 4 supply a bound
  write-role binding whose `manifest_path` points to a NONEXISTENT file;
  `_runner.py` `_runtime_workspace_binding_error(validate_manifest=True)`
  (write-producing role) checks `manifest does not exist` BEFORE the
  `expires_at` check, so the 2 parametrized cases get the manifest error not
  their intended one, and the 2 bound-runner tests fail to resolve. FIX (in
  `test_liveness_watchdog.py`): a `_write_sandbox_manifest` helper writes a real
  minimal-but-valid `sandbox-manifest.json` under a real `root` dir containing
  the bound `cwd` + writable roots; all 4 tests now use it. NOT weakened — the
  production manifest invariant (Slice 04 safety: bound write roles validate
  against a sandbox manifest) is UNTOUCHED; the tests now genuinely exercise a
  valid manifest, so the `expires_at` cases reach their real check. 29 passed.

No PRODUCT bug found — all 7 failures were stale TEST fixtures against
correctly-tightened control-plane code; every fix updates the test to the
current architecture using a sanctioned seam, never weakens production.

## Slice 08f REVIEW — independent review of the test-hardening pass (2026-05-21)

Independent REVIEW of the Slice 08f IMPLEMENTATION (the full-suite
test-hardening pass). Verdict: **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 new P3.** The
implementation is accepted; STATUS.md is finalized to Slice 08g.

### Reviewer summary

08f's central risk is dishonest greening — making the suite pass by weakening,
skipping, xfail-ing, or hollowing tests instead of genuinely fixing them. Every
modified test was inspected against that risk. All 7 fixed failures (2
documented `test_dag_regroup` + 5 undocumented: 4 `test_liveness_watchdog`, 1
`test_no_truncation`) are genuine stale-fixture fixes against correctly-tightened
control-plane code; no production code was changed to make a test pass; no
test was skipped, xfail-ed, or had assertions deleted or loosened. The 8-item
carried P3 list is correctly dispositioned (7 resolved, P3-6 soundly
re-deferred to Slice 09). Hygiene and the full suite were independently
re-confirmed.

### Findings (severity + file:line)

- **No P1 findings.**
- **No P2 findings.**
- **No new P3 findings.** (P3-6 remains open and carried to Slice 09 — see the
  P3 disposition vector below; this is a pre-existing carried item, not a new
  finding.)

### Vector-by-vector verdict

1. **No dishonest greening — KEY VECTOR — PASS.** Every test 08f modified was
   genuinely fixed. `grep` across all 8 changed files: zero `mark.skip`,
   `mark.xfail`, `pytest.skip`, `pytest.xfail`. The full suite ran with
   **0 skipped**. The 2 `test_dag_regroup` tests, the 4 `test_liveness_watchdog`
   tests, the `test_no_truncation` test, and the 2 "unmasked siblings" all
   retain real assertions on their named behavior (verified individually
   below). No assertion was deleted or weakened.

2. **`test_dag_regroup` fix integrity — PASS.**
   `test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave`
   (test_dag_regroup.py:1097) still asserts the regrouped first wave dispatches
   `FAST-BACKEND` before `LOW-UI` (`verified_groups[0] == (45, ["FAST-BACKEND"])`,
   `[1] == (46, ["LOW-UI"])`) — it genuinely exercises regroup overlay
   wave-ordering. `test_implement_dag_resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints`
   (test_dag_regroup.py:1254) still asserts a fully-expanded fully-checkpointed
   regroup resume re-verifies nothing (`verified_groups == []`) with a hard
   `AssertionError` `_Runner.run` if any group is wrongly dispatched — it
   genuinely exercises resume-checkpoint-scanning. The 3 added monkeypatches
   (`_compile_task_contracts_for_group`, `_dispatch_task_attempt_via_runtime_
   dispatcher`, `_dag_group_checkpoint_is_fresh`) are legitimate unit-test
   scoping of orthogonal subsystems, each of which has its own dedicated
   real-PG suite: the contract stub returns the default `approved=True`
   `TaskContractCompileOutcome()` (verified implementation.py:1925-1926); the
   dispatch stub is a faithful keyword-only mirror (verified
   implementation.py:8548) that still drives the test runtime and returns the
   real 2-tuple shape `(result, outcome)` (verified call site
   implementation.py:17069); the freshness stub keys off a real pre-seeded
   `dag-group:*` artifact and returns False for the groups under test so they
   are NOT skipped. The "must not dispatch" safety assertion is preserved. The
   stubbed production gates were NOT changed — all three exist unmodified in
   `implementation.py`.

3. **The 5 undocumented-failure fixes — product bug vs stale fixture — PASS;
   verdict: STALE FIXTURE, no product regression.** The production tightenings
   the tests now expect are genuinely the intended/correct Slice 04 behavior:
   - `test_no_truncation` / `test_liveness_watchdog` both broke because Slice
     04 made a sandbox binding mandatory for write-producing repair/runtime
     roles — exactly the IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT non-negotiable "Do not let
     implementer or repair agents mutate canonical repos directly once the
     sandbox path is active". `_bind_repair_sandbox` (implementation.py:6986)
     fails closed with `_sandbox_blocker` unless a sandbox is bound OR the
     `test_allow_legacy_repair_without_sandbox` service is set (line 7005). That
     service is a PRE-EXISTING sanctioned production test seam — used **60×**
     in the green `test_dag_expanded_verify.py`; 08f reuses it identically, not
     a new weakening. `_runtime_workspace_binding_error` with
     `validate_manifest=True` validates the on-disk sandbox manifest BEFORE the
     `expires_at` check (verified `_runner.py:298` manifest block precedes the
     `expires_at` block at `_runner.py:368`) — so a nonexistent `manifest_path`
     masks the intended check, exactly as the journal states. The
     `_write_sandbox_manifest` helper writes a real, valid manifest that passes
     every manifest check, so the tests reach their genuine subject. The
     production manifest invariant and the sandbox-mandatory repair path are
     UNTOUCHED. No regression was masked.

4. **"Unmasked vacuously-passing siblings" — PASS.** With the post-Slice-04
   tightening, the un-seamed `test_no_truncation` siblings stopped at the lone
   RCA `Ask` (the `_bind_post_dag_product_repair_sandbox` raise returns early —
   verified implementation.py:32603-32625). `test_single_rca_fix_verify_uses_
   workspace_override_for_rca` asserts only on `runner.tasks[0]` (the RCA task)
   so it passed regardless of whether fix/verify ran — genuinely vacuous;
   `..._uses_actor_factory_for_nested_agents` asserts over `runner.tasks[:3]`,
   which silently shrank to a 1-element slice — also vacuous. The seam makes
   both genuinely run RCA -> fix -> verify (3 tasks). The fix did NOT re-hollow
   them — the assertions are unchanged and now meaningful; the second sibling's
   `_thread_factory` correctly gained the `runtime_workspace_binding` /
   `sandbox_required` kwargs that the production fix-actor builder threads
   (verified implementation.py:32635-32646), a faithful signature mirror.
   `test_run_regression_uses_actor_factory_for_all_regression_agents`'s factory
   correctly did NOT gain them — `_run_regression`'s `actor_builder` calls pass
   only `runtime`/`workspace_path` (verified implementation.py:33077-33082).

5. **The flake fix — PASS.** `git update-index -q --refresh` before the apply
   in `test_merge_queue_drain.py:727` is a sound determinism fix, not a
   paper-over and not a retry loop. `_diff_for_appended_line` rewrites README.md
   twice inside git's index-mtime granularity, leaving a racy-clean index
   entry; `update-index --refresh` re-stats tracked files and clears the racy
   flag without mutating content. Independently re-ran the previously-flaky
   `test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_mid_apply` 12× — 12/12 pass.

6. **P3 dispositions — PASS.** Spot-checked all 7 "resolved":
   - P3-1: `merge_queue_wiring.py` docstrings now say
     `execution_artifact_projections` (lines 97-99, 153) — confirmed.
   - P3-3: the `status="done"` kwarg is gone from the
     `GroupCheckpointProjection(...)` call (merge_queue_wiring.py:666) —
     confirmed.
   - P3-2: 2 new genuine real-PG smoke tests (`test_merge_queue_wiring.py:1289`,
     `:1331`) call the real `ExecutionControlStore.record_patch_summary` /
     `record_contract_verdict` against real Postgres and assert the
     `evidence_nodes` row lands with a non-null server-defaulted `started_at` —
     real assertions, genuine COALESCE-fix coverage.
   - P3-7: the optional `cause` param on `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure`
     (implementation.py:5524) is folded into the router idempotency key only
     when non-empty (line 5578); the 3 per-lane drain callers (lines 5766,
     5941, 6000) pass NO `cause` so their keys are byte-for-byte unchanged; the
     3 checkpoint `_route` callsites pass distinct causes (`coverage_not_
     approved`, `checkpoint_raised`, `checkpoint_declined`, lines 6522/6546/
     6558) — purely additive and backward-compatible. `test_merge_queue_
     checkpoint.py` (14) + `test_merge_queue_coordinator.py` (20) pass,
     confirming non-breaking.
   - P3-4 / P3-8: the regroup tests and the flake — see vectors 2 and 5.
   P3-6 RE-DEFERRAL is sound: `_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS` maps
   `contract_violation` -> `("contract_violation", "outside_allowed_paths")`
   (verified implementation.py:5503) and `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure`

7. **Hygiene & green — PASS.** Independently re-ran `python -m compileall -q

## Slice 08g SIX-VECTOR REVIEW (2026-05-21)

The slice-end six-vector review ran across the WHOLE of Slice 08 (08a-08f as
one integrated change). Six independent reviewers each took one vector.

Aggregate verdict: **0 P1, 3 P2, 5 P3.** Per-vector verdicts:

- **Vector 1 (existing workflow compatibility): CLEAN.** Legacy resume, legacy
  `dag-*` artifact projections, post-DAG gates, post-test readiness preserved.
- **Vector 2 (persistence & crash recovery): CLEAN after P2-A.** Idempotency
  keys, typed state, projection links, restart/recovery boundaries sound EXCEPT
  the crashed-`checkpointing` recovery hole (P2-A) + the latent `committing`
  reset divergence (P3-iv).
- **Vector 3 (workspace & mutation safety): CLEAN.** Canonical repo authority,
  sandbox isolation, merge-queue ownership, no-dirty proof, ACL/alias correct;
  one defense-in-depth gap noted (P3-ii, not exploitable).
- **Vector 4 (failure-routing correctness): CLEAN.** Workflow-class failures
  route deterministically; product defects still reach product repair. The one
  open item (P3-v) is the already-tracked Slice 09 P3-6.
- **Vector 5 (supervisor/dashboard safety): CLEAN.** Bounded reads, read-only
  supervisor, Slack dedupe correct; one dead duplicate noted (P3-i).
- **Vector 6 (test coverage): CLEAN after P2-B/P2-C.** Positive/negative/resume/
  crash/regression coverage complete EXCEPT the untested `apply_candidate`
  deterministic-rebase-success path (P2-B) and `stale_projection`/`expected_head`
  path (P2-C), plus the doc-08 § Tests completeness gaps (P3-iii).

The 3 BLOCKING P2s:

- **P2-A (Vector 2) — crashed-`checkpointing` recovery silently loops.**
  `merge_queue_store.py:76` `_LEASE_TRANSITIONS` has no `checkpointing` key;
  `recover_expired` (`merge_queue_store.py:589`, `_RECOVERABLE_STATUSES`
  line 82) DOES return `checkpointing` rows. `implementation.py`
  `_recover_one_crashed_merge_queue_lane` (~5990-6014) drove a recovered lane
  to a terminal via `store.transition(...)`, which raised `MergeQueueError`
  for a `checkpointing` source; the broad `except` swallowed it as a benign
  no-op. The row stayed `checkpointing` with an expired lease and was
  re-recovered every drain pass until `MAX_RECOVERIES`=3 poisoned it. Latent
  today (the as-built `GroupMergeCoordinator.checkpoint_group` goes
  `integrated -> done` directly, never `checkpointing`) but a real recovery
  hole — doc 08 § "Rollback And Recovery Table" line 655 says a crashed
  `checkpointing` lane is recovered by RE-RUNNING the idempotent group
  checkpoint, terminating at `done`/`poisoned`.
- **P2-B (Vector 6) — `apply_candidate` deterministic-rebase SUCCESS path
  untested.** `merge_queue.py:270-280` has the `pre_apply != base_commit` ->
  `is_ancestor` -> `rebased=True` branch. Doc 08 § Tests requires
  "Deterministic rebase succeeds when `base_commit` is an ancestor of current
  HEAD". Every prior conflict test used a DIVERGED (non-ancestor) commit, so
  the rebase-applies branch + the `MergeProof.rebased` proof field never ran.
- **P2-C (Vector 6) — `apply_candidate` `stale_projection`/`expected_head`
  path untested.** `merge_queue.py:254-262` raises `stale_projection` when
  `RepoTargetCreate.expected_head != pre_apply`. No prior test set
  `expected_head`. Doc 08 § Tests requires "Non-ancestor base fails as
  `stale_projection` or `merge_conflict`".

The 5 P3s:

- **P3-i (Vector 5)** — `dashboard.py:1554` a dead DUPLICATE
  `_control_plane_merge_queue` (the live one is `execution_control/store.py`
  ~1110), never called from `dashboard.py`.
- **P3-ii (Vector 3)** — `implementation.py:5213` `_resolve_merge_queue_lane_
  inputs`: no queue-layer outside-root / noncanonical-repo-path containment
  guard (`_contract_repo_path` does not reject `..`). NOT exploitable today
  (Slice 03 `task_contracts.py` validates `repo_path` at compile time), but
  doc 08 § Tests line ~691 requires "noncanonical repo paths, outside-root
  paths fail in the enqueue transaction".
- **P3-iii (Vector 6)** — doc 08 § Tests items unsatisfied: "Clean repo no-op
  group does not create an empty commit", a drain-level `contract_violation`
  routing test, and a `recover_expired`-poisoned-terminal test.
- **P3-iv (Vector 2/3)** — `_recover_one_crashed_merge_queue_lane`
  unconditionally `reset --hard`s every repo with a `pre_apply_head`,
  including a repo already `committed`/`clean` — diverges from doc 08's
  `committing` recovery row ("if result commit exists, record it"). Latent
  (per-task lanes are single-repo).
- **P3-v (Vector 4)** — the drain `contract_violation` -> `quiesce` route
  enrichment: ALREADY the tracked Slice 09 item (08f P3-6). Left as-is.

## Slice 08g REMEDIATION (2026-05-21)

All 3 BLOCKING P2s remediated; 4 of the 5 P3s resolved, P3-v left as the
tracked Slice 09 item. **No refactor of the reviewed 08d worker/coordinator
contracts** — every fix ADDS a code path or an additive field. Files changed:
`merge_queue_store.py`, `merge_queue.py`, `implementation.py`, `dashboard.py`,
and 4 test files.

**P2-A — crashed-`checkpointing` recovery now converges (doc-08-conformant).**
Implemented the doc 08 § "Rollback And Recovery Table" line-655 behavior: a
crashed `checkpointing` lane is recovered by RE-RUNNING the idempotent group
checkpoint, never by a repo reset (a `checkpointing` lane is past `integrated`
— it has already committed + proved clean, so a reset would destroy a real
canonical commit). Changes:

1. `merge_queue_store.py:76` `_LEASE_TRANSITIONS` — added the
   `checkpointing: {failed, poisoned}` key (the doc-341/342 "active
   non-terminal -> failed/poisoned" transitions; schema-CHECK-legal — verified
   `failed`/`poisoned` are not in any `merge_queue_items` proof-progression
   `NOT IN` list). `checkpointing -> done` stays coordinator-owned via
   `complete_checkpoint`, deliberately NOT in this table. This alone closes
   the silent loop (recovery can no longer raise on a `checkpointing` source).
2. `implementation.py` `_recover_one_crashed_merge_queue_lane` — added a
   `status == "checkpointing"` branch that delegates to the new
   `_recover_one_crashed_checkpointing_group`, which reconstructs the group's
   expected task ids from EVERY lane's `merge_queue_task_coverage` rows (the
   08e-2 enqueue produces one `task:{id}` lane per task, so the union IS the
   group's effective-DAG expected set), reconstructs per-task
   `ImplementationResult`s from the durable `dag-task:*` markers when
   `runner.artifacts` is present, and re-drives the idempotent
   `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group`. If the checkpoint completes the
   lane converges to `done`; if it cannot (coverage not approved — a sibling
   lane failed/poisoned), the new `_poison_unrecoverable_checkpointing_lane`
   helper drives the lane to terminal `poisoned` via the recovery-bumped lease
   (the doc-655 other terminal). Either way the lane reaches a terminal state
   so the `recover_expired` pass cannot re-recover it and loop.
3. `_MergeQueueDrainResult` gained an additive `succeeded` property
   (`terminal_status in ("integrated", "done")`); the drain-loop logging and
   the two `failed_lanes` filters (`_resume_recover_...`, the main
   `if pending_merge_queue:` block) use it so a recovered-`done` lane is not
   mistaken for a failure.

   **P2-A surfaced a REAL latent bug in the reviewed 08d
   `GroupMergeCoordinator.checkpoint_group`:** its `covered` set (and
   `_checkpoint_coverage_digest`'s) was `integrated UNION done` — a
   `checkpointing`-status lane was in NEITHER, so `complete_checkpoint([])`
   raised `MergeQueueError("requires queue item ids")` and a re-driven
   checkpoint of an all-`checkpointing` group could never succeed — directly
   violating doc 08 § Tests "Repeating checkpoint from `checkpointing` rows
   succeeds". Fixed additively: `GroupMergeCoverage` gained a
   `checkpointing_queue_item_ids` field (`default_factory=list`, backward-
   compatible); `coverage()` populates it; `checkpoint_group`'s `covered` set,
   `_checkpoint_coverage_digest`, and the already-checkpointed short-circuit
   guard all now include `checkpointing` lanes. The digest is now
   status-INVARIANT (a lane keeps its `id` across `integrated -> checkpointing
   -> done`).

Test: `test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_in_checkpointing` (stages a lane
drained to `integrated` then UPDATE-d to `checkpointing` + expired lease,
asserts the drain re-run converges it to `done` with the `dag-group:*`
projection written, the canonical commit preserved, and a second re-run finds
nothing — the anti-loop assertion) and
`test_drain_poisons_a_crashed_checkpointing_lane_with_incomplete_group`
(a sibling TASK-2 lane fails the drain so coverage cannot approve; asserts the
crashed `checkpointing` lane reaches terminal `poisoned`, routed, with the
commit preserved). Both in `test_merge_queue_checkpoint.py`.

**P2-B — deterministic-rebase SUCCESS path now tested; no bug found.** The
rebase-applies branch + `MergeProof.rebased` work correctly — they were simply
untested. Tests: `test_apply_candidate_deterministic_rebase_succeeds_on_
ancestor_base` (worker-level, `test_merge_queue.py`) and
`test_drain_integrates_a_lane_via_deterministic_rebase` (drain-level,
`test_merge_queue_drain.py`) — both advance the canonical HEAD with a
NON-conflicting commit so `base_commit` stays an ancestor, and assert the lane
reaches `verifying`/`integrated` with `rebased=True` in the merge proof.

**P2-C — `stale_projection`/`expected_head` path now tested; no bug found.**
Tests in `test_merge_queue.py`: `test_apply_candidate_fails_stale_projection_
on_wrong_expected_head` (a stale `expected_head` -> typed `stale_projection`
failure, repo untouched) and the positive companion
`test_apply_candidate_succeeds_when_expected_head_matches_live_head`.

**P3 dispositions:**

- **P3-i — RESOLVED.** Deleted the dead duplicate `_control_plane_merge_queue`
  from `dashboard.py` (lines ~1554-1602). Confirmed unreferenced (no import,
  no `__all__`, no dynamic call); the live `execution_control/store.py:1110`
  version — which takes a `limit` param + uses `_MERGE_QUEUE_ACTIVE_STATUSES`
  — is untouched.
- **P3-ii — RESOLVED.** Added the defense-in-depth queue-layer containment
  assertion to `_resolve_merge_queue_lane_inputs` (`implementation.py:~5213`):
  the resolved canonical repo dir must equal or be `is_relative_to` the
  resolved canonical feature root, else the enqueue fails closed with
  `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` (fires BEFORE the `.git` check). Test:
  `test_implementation_worker_enqueue_rejects_an_outside_root_repo_path`
  (`test_merge_queue_wiring.py`) — a `../escape` contract `repo_path`
  resolving outside the feature root fails the enqueue with zero queue rows.
- **P3-iii — RESOLVED.** Added the 3 doc-08 § Tests-completeness tests:
  `test_commit_and_prove_clean_does_not_create_an_empty_commit`
  (`test_merge_queue.py` — `commit_and_prove_clean` calls `git_service.commit`
  WITHOUT `--allow-empty`, so a no-changed-paths repo fails closed as
  `commit_hygiene` and the canonical HEAD is unchanged — no empty commit);
  `test_drain_routes_a_contract_violation_through_the_failure_router` and
  `test_drain_handles_a_recover_expired_poisoned_terminal_lane`
  (`test_merge_queue_drain.py` — a drain-level `contract_violation` routes as
  `(contract_violation, outside_allowed_paths)`; a lane stuck `applying` with
  `payload.recovery_count >= MAX_RECOVERIES` is poisoned by `recover_expired`,
  routed, terminal — no loop).
- **P3-iv — RESOLVED.** `_recover_one_crashed_merge_queue_lane`'s reset loop
  now SKIPS the `reset --hard` for a repo target whose `status` is
  `committed`/`clean` (a real `result_commit` exists — doc 08 commit step 9
  forbids resetting a real commit; doc 08's `committing` recovery row says "if
  result commit exists, record it"). A clean, low-risk additive guard: the
  target is still proven clean (a dirty-after-commit repo stays
  irreconcilable). Latent today (per-task lanes are single-repo).
- **P3-v — LEFT (tracked Slice 09 item).** This is the carried 08f P3-6 (the
  drain `contract_violation` -> `quiesce` route enrichment). It needs
  structured escaped-paths threaded through the reviewed/accepted 08d
  `MergeApplyResult` contract — a structured-failure-evidence FEATURE, not a
  review-loop fix. Current behavior is fail-closed and safe. Unchanged.

**Hygiene + tests.** `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py`
clean (exit 0); `git diff --check` clean (exit 0). The execution suite
`python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q` is **421 passed**
(411 baseline + 10 new tests). The full suite
`python -m pytest tests/ -q -p no:randomly` is **1842 passed, 0 failed, 0
skipped** (1832 baseline + 10 new tests; real Postgres up). The 10 new tests:
worker-level (`test_merge_queue.py`) — `..._deterministic_rebase_succeeds_on_
ancestor_base`, `..._fails_stale_projection_on_wrong_expected_head`, `..._
succeeds_when_expected_head_matches_live_head`, `test_commit_and_prove_clean_
does_not_create_an_empty_commit`; drain-level (`test_merge_queue_drain.py`) —
`test_drain_integrates_a_lane_via_deterministic_rebase`, `test_drain_routes_a_
contract_violation_through_the_failure_router`, `test_drain_handles_a_recover_
expired_poisoned_terminal_lane`; checkpoint-level (`test_merge_queue_
checkpoint.py`) — `test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_in_checkpointing`,
`test_drain_poisons_a_crashed_checkpointing_lane_with_incomplete_group`;
wiring-level (`test_merge_queue_wiring.py`) — `test_implementation_worker_
enqueue_rejects_an_outside_root_repo_path`.

A re-review of Slice 08g follows; STATUS.md is intentionally NOT updated here.

## Slice 08g RE-REVIEW (2026-05-21)

An independent re-review of the Slice 08g REMEDIATION (the 3 P2 fixes + the
P3 dispositions). This is a RE-REVIEW of the remediation only — no code
changes. Working-tree state of the changed files (`merge_queue.py`,
`merge_queue_store.py`, `implementation.py`, `dashboard.py`, the 4 test files)
was inspected directly.

**Re-reviewer verdict: CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 new P3.** The remediation is
accepted; Slice 08 is accepted (see the COMPLETE entry below).

**Vector 1 — P2-A (crashed-`checkpointing` recovery). CLEAN.**

- *(1a) Re-drive converges, no loop, no reset.* `_recover_one_crashed_merge_
  queue_lane` (`implementation.py:6011`) branches `status == "checkpointing"`
  to `_recover_one_crashed_checkpointing_group` (`implementation.py:6106`),
  which reconstructs the group's expected task ids from EVERY lane's
  `merge_queue_task_coverage` rows (`:6183`), reconstructs per-task
  `ImplementationResult`s from the durable `dag-task:*` markers (`:6208`), and
  re-drives the idempotent `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group` (`:6231`).
  On `checkpointed and final_status == "done"` it returns a terminal `done`
  result (`:6256`); otherwise `_poison_unrecoverable_checkpointing_lane`
  (`:6278`) drives the lane to terminal `poisoned` via the recovery-bumped
  lease. No `reset --hard` is ever issued for a `checkpointing` lane — the
  `pre_apply_head` reset loop is in the SEPARATE `applying`/`verifying`/
  `committing` branch (`:6016`), unreachable for `checkpointing`. The lane
  ALWAYS reaches a terminal (`done`/`poisoned`), so `recover_expired` cannot
  re-recover it — the silent loop is closed. No post-`integrated` commit is
  destroyed.
- *(1b) The additive `GroupMergeCoverage.checkpointing_queue_item_ids` change
  to the reviewed 08d `checkpoint_group` is PURELY ADDITIVE — explicit
  verdict.* The new field (`merge_queue.py:703`) has `default_factory=list`.
  For the NORMAL case (a group with no `checkpointing` lane) every touched
  site is byte-for-byte behaviorally unchanged: `coverage()` computes
  `checkpointing_queue_item_ids` via `it.status == "checkpointing"` which is
  `[]` when no such lane exists (`:896`); the already-checkpointed
  short-circuit added `and not current.checkpointing_queue_item_ids` (`:958`)
  which is `not [] == True` and so does not alter the guard; the `covered`
  set `integrated ∪ checkpointing ∪ done` (`:1048`) collapses to the original
  `integrated ∪ done` when `checkpointing == []`; `_checkpoint_coverage_
  digest` likewise (`:1209`) — the digest is byte-identical for a normal
  group. The widened `covered` set is CORRECT: a `checkpointing` lane
  genuinely belongs in `covered` — `MergeQueueStore.complete_checkpoint`
  advances `WHERE ... status IN ('integrated','checkpointing')`
  (`merge_queue_store.py:496`) and rejects an empty id list (`:481`), so a
  re-driven checkpoint of an all-`checkpointing` group with the old
  `integrated ∪ done`-only set passed `complete_checkpoint([])` and raised —
  the latent 08d bug the remediation correctly identified and fixed. The
  digest is now status-INVARIANT (a lane keeps its `id` across `integrated ->
  checkpointing -> done`), satisfying doc 08 § Tests "a re-driven checkpoint's
  `checkpoint_coverage_digest` matches the original". The 08d coordinator unit
  tests that never set a `checkpointing` lane are unaffected.
- *(1c) The new `_LEASE_TRANSITIONS` `checkpointing` entry.* `_LEASE_
  TRANSITIONS["checkpointing"] = {failed, poisoned}` (`merge_queue_store.py:
  92`). Confirmed schema-CHECK-legal against `schema.sql`: `merge_queue_items_
  status_check` (both legal statuses); `merge_queue_items_pre_queue_gate_
  check` (`failed`/`poisoned` exempt); `merge_queue_items_merge_proof_check`,
  `_post_apply_gate_check`, `_commit_proof_check`, `_checkpoint_digests_check`
  (`failed`/`poisoned` are in NONE of those `NOT IN` lists, so no constraint
  applies); `merge_queue_items_done_check` (`done` only). Doc 08 § Status
  Transitions lines 341–342 permit "active non-terminal -> failed/poisoned" —
  `checkpointing` is an active non-terminal — and the doc-08 Rollback And
  Recovery Table line 655 gives `done`/`poisoned` as the `checkpointing`
  terminals. `checkpointing -> done` is correctly NOT in this table — it is
  coordinator-owned via `complete_checkpoint`.
- *(1d) The new tests genuinely stage a crashed `checkpointing` lane.*
  `test_drain_recovers_a_lane_crashed_in_checkpointing` drains a lane to
  `integrated`, then UPDATEs it to `checkpointing` with an expired lease and
  the real `checkpoint_coverage_digest`/`checkpoint_body_sha256` columns the
  schema CHECK requires, asserts a normal `claim` cannot pick it up, then
  asserts the drain re-run converges it to `done` with the `dag-group:*`
  projection written, the canonical commit preserved (`head_after_drain`
  unchanged), and a THIRD drain finds nothing — the explicit anti-loop
  assertion. `test_drain_poisons_a_crashed_checkpointing_lane_with_incomplete_
  group` stages a sibling TASK-2 lane that fails the drain so coverage cannot
  approve, and asserts the crashed `checkpointing` lane reaches terminal
  `poisoned`, routed, the commit preserved, no `dag-group:*`. Both non-vacuous.

**Vector 2 — P2-B / P2-C (untested apply branches). CLEAN.** The new tests
genuinely exercise the named branches. `test_apply_candidate_deterministic_
rebase_succeeds_on_ancestor_base` and `test_drain_integrates_a_lane_via_
deterministic_rebase` advance the canonical HEAD with a NON-conflicting commit
(a brand-new file) so `base_commit` stays an ancestor, and both assert
`proof["rebased"] is True` on the loaded merge proof — the rebase-applies
branch + the `MergeProof.rebased` field, unexercised by every prior
diverged-base conflict test. `test_apply_candidate_fails_stale_projection_on_
wrong_expected_head` sets `expected_head` to a stale 40-hex sha and asserts
`result.failure_class == "stale_projection"`, the lane `failed`, the repo
HEAD untouched and clean; the positive companion confirms a fresh
`expected_head` applies normally. Not vacuous; the remediation's "no bug
found, just untested" conclusion is corroborated.

**Vector 3 — P3 fixes. CLEAN.** P3-i: `_control_plane_merge_queue` was
confirmed to exist ONLY in `execution_control/store.py` (defined `:1110`,
called `:580`) with ZERO references in `dashboard.py` — the dead duplicate is
genuinely gone, the live one untouched. P3-ii: the containment guard in
`_resolve_merge_queue_lane_inputs` (`implementation.py:5222`) resolves both
the repo dir and the feature root with `.resolve()` (which collapses `..` and
symlinks) and rejects unless `resolved_repo_dir == resolved_feature_root or
.is_relative_to(resolved_feature_root)`, firing BEFORE the `.git` check; its
test places a real git repo in a SIBLING dir and a `../escape` contract
`repo_path`, asserting `_MergeQueueEnqueueError` with `match="outside the
canonical feature"` (proving the containment guard fired, not the `.git`
check) and zero queue rows. P3-iii: the 3 doc-08 § Tests-completeness tests
are real (the no-empty-commit test reverts the staged change and asserts the
commit fails closed as `commit_hygiene` with the HEAD unchanged; the
`contract_violation` and `recover_expired`-poisoned tests assert the routed
typed failure + convergence). P3-iv: the `_recover_one_crashed_merge_queue_
lane` reset loop now SKIPS `reset --hard` for a repo target whose `status` is
`committed`/`clean` (`implementation.py:6044`) and still proves it clean — a
dirty-after-commit repo stays irreconcilable; doc-08-conformant (commit step 9
forbids resetting a real commit) and does not break single-repo recovery (an
uncommitted single-repo lane still resets). P3-v correctly LEFT as the tracked
Slice 09 item.

**Vector 4 — no dishonest greening / no regression. CLEAN.** No test was
weakened, skipped, or xfail-ed — a grep of the 4 changed test files found zero
`mark.skip`/`mark.xfail`/`pytest.skip`/`pytest.xfail`, and the full suite
reports 0 skipped. The remediation did not refactor reviewed/accepted 08d
code beyond the SANCTIONED additive `GroupMergeCoverage.checkpointing_queue_
item_ids` field and the latent-bug fix it enables (every other change ADDS a
code path: a new `_LEASE_TRANSITIONS` key, two new recovery helpers, an
additive `_MergeQueueDrainResult.succeeded` property, the containment guard).
Independently re-ran the hygiene gate (`compileall` exit 0; `git diff --check`
exit 0), the execution suite (`tests/workflows/develop/execution/` — **421
passed**), and the COMPLETE suite (`python -m pytest tests/ -q -p no:randomly`
— **1842 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped**, real Postgres up). The 1842 figure and
the 421 execution-suite figure match the remediation's reported numbers
exactly.

## Slice 08g COMPLETE — Slice 08 ACCEPTED (2026-05-21)

The Slice 08g slice-end six-vector review loop is COMPLETE. The six-vector
review found 0 P1, 3 P2, 5 P3; the remediation resolved all 3 P2s + 4 of the 5
P3s (P3-v left as the tracked Slice 09 P3-6); the independent RE-REVIEW above
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 new P3**, with an explicit PURELY-ADDITIVE
verdict on the 08d `checkpoint_group` `checkpointing_queue_item_ids` change.

**Slice 08 (Durable Merge Queue) is formally ACCEPTED.** Sub-slices 08a–08g
are all complete. The durable merge queue is the single product-authoritative
canonical landing path: sandbox-captured patches are enqueued as `task:{id}`
lanes, the drain worker applies/rebases/gates/commits/integrates them, the
coordinator projects `dag-group:*` from the typed `merge_queue_items` rows
only after complete proof, and crash recovery is deterministic for every
status in the doc-08 Rollback And Recovery Table — including the crashed-
`checkpointing` lane (08g P2-A). The full test suite is GREEN: `python -m
pytest tests/ -q -p no:randomly` → **1842 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped**
(independently re-confirmed in this re-review), hygiene clean, the merge-queue
execution suite 421 passed.

One open P3 is carried forward to Slice 09: **P3-6 / P3-v** — the durable
merge queue drain `contract_violation` route dead-ends at `quiesce` because
`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure`'s router observation `payload` carries no
`repair_scope`/`target_paths`, so `failure_router._allows_product_repair`
downgrades the route. The clean fix needs structured escaped-paths threaded
`_ApplyFailure` → `MergeApplyResult` → the drain → the router observation
(~4–5 touch points across the reviewed 08d `MergeApplyResult` contract) — a
structured-failure-EVIDENCE feature, intentionally deferred to Slice 09. The
current behavior is fail-closed and SAFE.

Next: Slice 09 (Regroup Overlay And Scheduler Feedback). STATUS.md is
overwritten to point the next iteration at Slice 09.

## Slice 09a IMPLEMENTATION — START (2026-05-21)

Slice 09 (Regroup Overlay And Scheduler Feedback) is LARGE; split 09a–09e per
STATUS.md. This chunk is **09a — typed overlay schema + models only** (no
algorithm, no activation, no resolver, no scheduler feedback logic — those are
09b/09c/09d). `dag_regroup.py` stays UNTOUCHED this sub-slice.

Brief:
- New module `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/regroup_overlay.py`
  with the doc-09 typed models + deterministic `overlay_id`/`overlay_slug`
  derivations.
- Three new `schema.sql` tables: `execution_regroup_overlays` (incl. the
  unique-active-overlay partial index `WHERE status = 'active'`),
  `execution_regroup_validations`, `execution_scheduler_feedback`.
- DB-validate the DDL against real Postgres (`localhost:5431`).

Citations verified before coding:
- `DerivedDAGArtifact` at `models/outputs.py:994` — confirmed (compatibility
  payload; `barriers: list[dict]`, `write_sets: dict[str,list[str]]`,
  `speed_index: dict[str,Any]`, `activation_contract`/`rollback_plan` are
  `list[str]`, `validation_notes: list[str]`).
- `dag_regroup.py` builder `build_staged_regroup` at `dag_regroup.py:336`,
  artifact-key constants at `dag_regroup.py:24-28` — confirmed.
- `stable_digest` / `stable_json` at `execution_control/models.py:1141-1146`
  (sha256 over sorted-keys compact JSON) — confirmed; reused for overlay_id /
  validation digests / idempotency keys.
- Slice 08 model conventions: `execution_control/merge_queue_store.py` —
  `MergeQueueItem` etc. are flat `BaseModel`s, `Field(default_factory=list)`,
  `request_digest`/`idempotency_key` module-level helper functions.
- Slice 08 schema conventions: `merge_queue_*` tables in `schema.sql:567-737`
  — `BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY`, `idempotency_key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE`,
  `payload JSONB`, `created_at`/`updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()`,
  `CONSTRAINT <table>_status_check CHECK (status IN (...))`, partial indexes.

## Slice 09a IMPLEMENTATION — COMPLETE (2026-05-21)

**Slice 09a (typed overlay schema + models) is COMPLETE.** Models + schema
only — no algorithm, no activation, no resolver, no scheduler-feedback logic
(those are 09b/09c/09d). `dag_regroup.py` left UNTOUCHED.

### Files changed
- NEW `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/regroup_overlay.py`
  (~440 lines) — the typed overlay/feedback models + the deterministic
  identifier derivations.
- `schema.sql` — three new tables appended after the Slice 08 `merge_queue_*`
  block.
- NEW `tests/workflows/test_regroup_overlay.py` — 41 09a-scoped tests.

### Models created (field sources — all from doc 09)
All field lists/types transcribed verbatim from doc 09
`09-regroup-overlay-and-scheduler-feedback.md`:
- `OverlayCompatibilityKeys`, `OverlayBarrier`, `OverlayTaskSpeedMetadata`,
  `RegroupActivationContract`, `RegroupRollbackPlan`, `RegroupActiveMarker`,
  `RegroupOverlay` — doc 09 § "Overlay Schema" (the `python` block at doc
  lines 61-169), copied field-for-field including `schema_version:
  Literal[1] = 1`, `Field(default_factory=...)` defaults, optional
  `datetime | None` timestamps.
- `SchedulerGroupMetric`, `SchedulerFeedback` — doc 09 § "Scheduler Feedback
  Schema" (doc lines 221-305), copied field-for-field.
- Status/enum `Literal` aliases (`OverlayStatus`, `RegroupActiveMarkerStatus`,
  `OverlayBarrierSource`, `SchedulerGroupMetricState`,
  `SchedulerFeedbackDataQuality`, `SchedulerFeedbackConfidence`) hoisted to
  named module-level aliases — doc 09 spells them inline on each model; the
  hoist mirrors the Slice 08 `merge_queue_store` convention
  (`MergeQueueStatus`/`RepoTargetStatus`) and changes no field type.
- Deterministic derivations — doc 09 § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" (doc lines
  47-58) and § "Scheduler Feedback Schema" (doc lines 311-313):
  - `derive_overlay_id(...)` = `sha256(feature_id, source_dag_key,
    base_dag_artifact_id, base_dag_sha256, group_idx_offset, canonical derived
    order)[:24]`. "Canonical derived order" = `derived_execution_order` with
    each wave's task ids sorted.
  - `derive_overlay_slug(...)` = `g{offset}-g{last_original_group}` for a
    bounded suffix, `g{offset}-tail` otherwise. The legacy `g45-g73` spelling
    is the natural output for the 45/73 suffix — no special case needed.
  - `derive_metric_id(...)` = `sha256(feature_id, group_idx, overlay_id or
    "root", checkpoint_projection_id, task_ids, evidence_ids)[:24]`.
  All three reuse `execution_control.models.stable_digest` (sha256 over
  sorted-keys compact JSON) for a deterministic, ordering-invariant hash.

### Three new tables (doc 09 § "Persistence And Artifact Compatibility")
- `execution_regroup_overlays` — exact columns from doc 09 lines 466-474;
  constraints/indexes from doc 09 lines 475-481: `status` CHECK,
  `UNIQUE (feature_id, overlay_id)`, the **unique-active-overlay partial
  index** `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uniq_regroup_overlay_active ON
  execution_regroup_overlays(feature_id) WHERE status = 'active'`,
  `idx_regroup_overlay_base`, `idx_regroup_overlay_status`.
- `execution_regroup_validations` — columns from doc 09 line 493; indexes
  from doc 09 lines 496-497 (unique `idempotency_key`,
  `(feature_id, overlay_id, created_at DESC)`,
  `(feature_id, valid, created_at DESC)`).
- `execution_scheduler_feedback` — columns from doc 09 lines 499-503; indexes
  from doc 09 lines 503-504 (unique `idempotency_key`,
  `(feature_id, window_start_group, window_end_group, created_at DESC)`,
  `(feature_id, lane, barrier, created_at DESC)`).

### DB-validation result
Real Postgres `localhost:5431` (user `danielzhang`, trust auth) was reachable.
Applied the FULL updated `schema.sql` to a throwaway database (the same
load path the merge-queue conftest uses): all DDL ran with **no error**, the
three new tables exist, and `uniq_regroup_overlay_active` exists with the
exact `WHERE (status = 'active')` predicate. Behavioral check with real
inserts: a first `active` overlay inserts; a SECOND `active` overlay for the
same `feature_id` is **rejected** (`duplicate key value violates unique
constraint "uniq_regroup_overlay_active"`); a `staged` overlay for the same
feature is accepted alongside the active one — i.e. the partial index enforces
exactly "at most one active overlay per feature".

### doc-09 ambiguities / divergences and resolutions
1. **`execution_regroup_validations` column types** — doc 09 line 493 names
   the columns (`valid`, `details_json`, `evidence_ids`, ...) but not their
   SQL types. Resolved by mirroring Slice 08 conventions:
   `valid BOOLEAN NOT NULL`, `details_json JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'`,
   `evidence_ids JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]'`, `reason TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT
   ''`. Doc 09 calls `details_json` "bounded"; that bound is a write-time
   store concern (09b), not DDL — `merge_queue_items.payload` is likewise
   unbounded JSONB.
2. **`overlay_row_id` FK** — doc 09 line 493 lists `overlay_row_id` as a
   validation-row column. Added it as `BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES
   execution_regroup_overlays(id)` — a genuine intra-schema reference (unlike
   artifact ids / projection ids, which the as-built journal keeps as bare
   `BIGINT`; matched that for `base_dag_artifact_id`,
   `latest_successful_validation_id`, `active_marker_projection_id`).
3. **Partial-index name** — doc 09 line 478 gives the index *clause* but not a
   name. Named `uniq_regroup_overlay_active`, mirroring the `uniq_merge_queue_
   retry_source_active` / `ux_supervisor_slack_digest_state_active_*` naming
   already in `schema.sql`.
4. **Enum aliases** — doc 09 inlines every `Literal[...]`. Hoisted to named
   module aliases (Slice 08 `merge_queue_store` convention). Pure naming; the
   field types are byte-identical to doc 09.
None of these change a doc-09 field's type or a documented constraint; they
fill type gaps doc 09 left to "mirror Slice 08".

`tests/workflows/test_regroup_overlay.py` — 41 tests, all PASS:
- `overlay_id` derivation (6): determinism, 24-hex, within-wave-order
  invariance, wave-partition sensitivity, every-identity-field sensitivity
  (parametrized x5), exact-hash-formula.
- `overlay_slug` derivation (3): legacy `g45-g73` preserved, `-tail` for an
  unbounded suffix, deterministic non-G45 suffixes.
- `metric_id` derivation (4): determinism, 24-hex, task/evidence-order
  invariance, `overlay_id or "root"` sentinel, group_idx sensitivity.

  round-trip (nested members survive as models), `schema_version` pinned to

  defaults; `OverlayBarrier` `hard` default + source enum (accept all 4 /

  default flags; `RegroupActiveMarker` round-trip + non-marker-status

Regression / no-break confirmation:

### Hygiene
`python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` → exit 0;
`git diff --check` → exit 0.

### P1/P2-risk areas for a reviewer
- **No P1/P2 known.** 09a is models + schema only, additive (a new module,
  three new tables, a new test file), fail-fast (Pydantic + DDL CHECKs reject
  bad values), and verified against real Postgres.
- **P3 (clarity)** — the enum aliases are hoisted out of doc 09's inline
  `Literal`s; intentional Slice-08-mirroring, no type change.
- **For 09b/09c reviewers (NOT 09a defects):** the `RegroupOverlay`
  `overlay_sha256` / `validation_digest` fields and the
  `execution_regroup_overlays.payload_json` JSONB are unconstrained at the
  model/DDL level — the canonical-sha computation and digest idempotency are
  09b/09c store-layer concerns; 09a only carries the typed slots. The
  `RegroupActivationContract` / `RegroupRollbackPlan` are typed contracts only
  — no check executes in 09a.

`validate_overlay` algorithm** (the 13-step deterministic validator, doc 09
§ "Validation Algorithm"; emits an `execution_regroup_validations` row + a

models foundation for Slice 09 — Regroup Overlay). REVIEW only — no code
changes. Working-tree state of `regroup_overlay.py`, the three new `schema.sql`
tables, and `test_regroup_overlay.py` inspected directly; the schema was
independently re-applied to a throwaway real-Postgres DB and the partial index
behavior re-verified.

### Verdict: CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 new P3 (1 P3 carried, unchanged).

### Vector 1 — model spec conformance: PASS (explicit verdict)
All 9 typed models cross-checked field-by-field against doc 09's `python`
blocks. Every field name / type / optionality / default is an EXACT transcription:
- `OverlayCompatibilityKeys` (doc 62-69) — 7 fields, exact.
- `OverlayBarrier` (doc 72-76) — 4 fields, exact; `hard: bool = True` default
  present; `source` typed as the hoisted `OverlayBarrierSource` alias =
  doc's inline `Literal["task_contract","speed_index","operator","legacy"]`.
- `OverlayTaskSpeedMetadata` (doc 79-86) — 7 fields, all defaults exact.
- `RegroupActivationContract` (doc 89-98) — 9 fields, exact;
  `requires_feature_advisory_lock: bool = True`.
- `RegroupRollbackPlan` (doc 101-111) — 9 fields, exact;
  `forward_only_after_start: bool = True`.
- `RegroupActiveMarker` (doc 113-134) — 21 fields, exact incl.
  `schema_version: Literal[1] = 1` and `status` constrained to

- `RegroupOverlay` (doc 137-169) — 29 fields, exact incl. field ordering and

- `SchedulerGroupMetric` (doc 222-286) — 56 fields, exact (all per-task

- `SchedulerFeedback` (doc 277-305) — 26 fields, exact.
No field is missing, mistyped, or invented beyond doc 09. The 6 hoisted
`Literal` aliases (`OverlayStatus` etc.) are byte-identical to doc 09's inline
literals — pure naming, zero type change.

### Vector 2 — schema conformance: PASS (explicit verdict)
The 3 tables (`schema.sql:747-842`) match doc 09 § "Persistence And Artifact
Compatibility":
- `execution_regroup_overlays` (doc 466-474) — all 21 columns present with the
  documented types; `status` CHECK with the exact 5-value set;
  `UNIQUE (feature_id, overlay_id)`; `idx_regroup_overlay_base` and
  `idx_regroup_overlay_status` exactly as doc 479-481.
- `execution_regroup_validations` (doc 492-497) — all 11 columns; unique
  `idempotency_key` + the 2 documented indexes.
- `execution_scheduler_feedback` (doc 498-504) — all 16 columns; unique
  `idempotency_key` + the 2 documented indexes; `data_quality` / `confidence`
  CHECKs added (consistent with the model `Literal`s).
- **CRITICAL partial unique index — VERIFIED.** `uniq_regroup_overlay_active ON
  execution_regroup_overlays(feature_id) WHERE status = 'active'`
  (`schema.sql:779-781`) genuinely enforces at-most-one active overlay per
  feature. Independently re-applied the full `schema.sql` to a throwaway PG db
  (`localhost:5431`, user `danielzhang`) — DDL ran with 0 errors — then a real
  insert sequence: 2 `staged` overlays for one feature inserted fine, the 1st
  flipped to `active` succeeded, the 2nd flipped to `active` raised
  `duplicate key value violates unique constraint "uniq_regroup_overlay_active"`,
  and a later `active->rolled_back` then re-`active` of the other row succeeded
  (the partial index correctly only counts `status='active'` rows).
Column types are consistent with the Pydantic models (`INTEGER` group indexes,
`BIGINT` row-id links, `JSONB` for list/dict payloads, `TIMESTAMPTZ`) and with
Slice 08 conventions (`BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY`, `idempotency_key TEXT NOT NULL
UNIQUE`, `created_at`/`updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()`, named CHECK
constraints).

### Vector 3 — deterministic derivations: PASS
- `derive_overlay_id` (`regroup_overlay.py:340-369`) — hashes the doc-09
  identity tuple via `stable_digest` (`models.py:1145` — sha256 of
  sorted-keys compact JSON), `[:24]`. The "canonical derived order" sorts each
  wave's task ids (`[sorted(wave) for wave in derived_execution_order]`), so the
  id is invariant to within-wave list ordering (doc 09's "canonical derived
  order") but sensitive to the wave partition — both are correct per doc 09 and
  test-covered. Deterministic: `stable_digest` sorts dict keys, no clock/random.
- `derive_overlay_slug` (`:372-389`) — `g{offset}-g{last}` / `g{offset}-tail`,
  pure formula; the legacy `g45-g73` spelling falls out naturally with no
  special case. Matches doc 09.
- `derive_metric_id` (`:392-417`) — hashes `(feature_id, group_idx,
  overlay_id or "root", checkpoint_projection_id, sorted task_ids, sorted
  evidence_ids)[:24]`. The `overlay_id or "root"` sentinel and the
  order-invariant sorting match doc 09 § "Scheduler Feedback Schema". Genuinely
  deterministic.
No non-deterministic id derivation — no P1.

### Vector 4 — doc-09 ambiguity resolutions: all SOUND
- Validation-row SQL types (`valid BOOLEAN`, `details_json`/`evidence_ids`
  `JSONB`) — doc 09 names the columns but not types; `BOOLEAN`/`JSONB` are the
  natural Postgres types and the `merge_queue_items.payload` precedent keeps
  such JSONB unbounded at DDL (the "bounded" `details_json` is a 09b write-time
  concern). Sound.
- `overlay_row_id BIGINT NOT NULL REFERENCES execution_regroup_overlays(id)` —
  a genuine intra-schema reference; modelling it as a real FK (unlike the
  bare-`BIGINT` artifact/projection-id links) is correct and stronger than
  doc 09 requires. Sound; FK target verified to exist.
- Index name `uniq_regroup_overlay_active` — doc 09 gives the clause but no
  name; mirrors the existing `uniq_merge_queue_retry_source_active` naming.
  Sound.
- Hoisted `Literal` aliases — Slice 08 `merge_queue_store` convention; pure
  naming. Sound.
None diverges from doc 09's intent.

None. 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 new P3. The carried Slice 09 P3-6 (drain
