## 2026-05-22 — Slice 09d-2 IMPLEMENTATION

Slice 09d-2 — the conservative cap computation + the typed `SchedulerFeedback`
+ the `review:dag-sizing:{feature_id}:{window}` projection — is implemented and
verified. This is the LAST Slice 09 implementation sub-slice (09e is the
slice-end six-vector review). It consumes 09d-1's
`build_scheduler_group_metrics` output.

NEW sibling leaf module
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/scheduler_sizing.py`. NOT
`scheduler_metrics.py` (09d-1 is review-CLEAN; the no-refactor discipline keeps
it untouched) and NOT `regroup_overlay.py` (the 09a model-only module the same
discipline protects) — the same sibling-leaf pattern as 09b-2 / 09c-1 / 09c-2 /
09d-1. The cap-computation vs. feedback-emission was NOT split further: they
are tightly coupled (the `SchedulerFeedback` IS the typed carrier of the cap
output) and the whole thing is a bounded formula-transcription + emission
chunk, done in one verified pass.

### (A) The conservative cap computation — every formula VERBATIM from doc 09

doc-09 source for every threshold is doc 09 § "Scheduler Metrics And Cap Rules"
(== § "Adaptive Sizing Data Flow" steps 5-6). Nothing invented or rounded:

- `policy_cap` (`compute_policy_cap`) — doc 09 step 1, the four tiers VERBATIM:
  - "unknown writes or high-risk barriers cap at 4" → `POLICY_CAP_UNKNOWN_OR_
    HIGH_RISK = 4`
  - "backend or multi-repo work caps at 6" → `POLICY_CAP_BACKEND_OR_MULTI_REPO
    = 6`
  - "isolated UI/document work caps at 10" → `POLICY_CAP_ISOLATED_UI_OR_
    DOCUMENT = 10`
  - "test-only and perf lanes cap at 14" → `POLICY_CAP_TEST_OR_PERF = 14`
  Evaluated most-conservative-first (a window that is both unknown-write and a
  test lane caps at 4 — the riskier tier wins). An UNCLASSIFIED lane/barrier
  conservatively falls to the 4-cap so a mis-tagged lane can never widen.
- `evidence_cap` (`compute_evidence_cap`) — doc 09 step 4 VERBATIM:
  `evidence_cap = floor(12h_checkpoint_budget / hours_per_task_p75)`, clamped
  to `[4, policy_cap]`. `CHECKPOINT_BUDGET_HOURS = 12.0`. `None` when there is
  no p75 (no completed samples) — the caller then falls back to the policy
  cap.
- The reduction triggers (`compute_cap_decision`) — doc 09 steps 2/3/6:
  - step 2: "Require at least two completed samples with evidence ids for the
    lane/barrier before widening above the current cap" → `MIN_SAMPLES_TO_WIDEN
    = 2`; a candidate strictly above `current_cap` with `< 2` completed samples
    *with evidence ids* is clamped to `min(current_cap, candidate)`.
  - step 6: "If sample count is below two, data is stale, ... keep the current
    cap or reduce to 4" → stale data likewise clamps the widen.
  - step 3: "Reduce to current cap or 4 when workflow repair, product repair,
    commit failure, or merge conflict rates exceed the global completed
    baseline by more than 10 percent" → `RATE_REGRESSION_THRESHOLD = 0.10`; a
    window per-task rate `> baseline * 1.10` reduces to `min(current_cap, 4)`.
  The result is never below `MIN_CAP = 4`.
- The global post-change baseline (`_GlobalBaseline`) — doc 09 § "Adaptive
  Sizing Data Flow" step 4: "Keep global post-change baseline metrics for
  comparison." Built from ALL completed metrics (the feature-wide norm); the
  per-task rates come straight off 09d-1's typed `*_per_task` metric fields.
- The post-cap dependency / hard-barrier / write-set / mapping validators
  (`build_candidate_waves` / `_can_join_wave`) — doc 09 step 5: they run "AFTER
  cap selection" and "may shrink or reject a candidate wave; metrics may not
  override them." `build_candidate_waves` produces recommended waves by
  topological order (doc 09 step 7); `cap` is the upper bound ONLY — a wave is
  SHRUNK below it when a validator objects: hard-barrier mix (step 9),
  same-wave write-set overlap after path canonicalization (step 10), an
  `unknown_write` task or a missing-write-set-contract task scheduled alone
  (step 7), incomplete merged-original-group write-set coverage (step 7). An
  unsatisfiable remaining graph (cycle / unreachable dep) fails fast with
  `SchedulerSizingError`; `compute_scheduler_feedback` catches it and surfaces
  NO candidate waves (the cap decision still holds) rather than crash the
  emission.

### (B) The typed `SchedulerFeedback` + the review-projection emission

- `compute_scheduler_feedback` is the PURE computation (no DB / no clock / no
  write). It returns an `AdaptiveSizingResult` carrying the typed
  `SchedulerFeedback` (the 09a model — every field populated), the `CapDecision`,
  and the `CandidateWave` list. The completed-throughput aggregates
  (`hours_per_task_p50/p75`, `tasks_per_hour`, `queue_wait_p75_h`, the four
  per-task rates, `verify_cost_per_task`) are computed from COMPLETED groups
  ONLY — doc 09 § "Adaptive Sizing Data Flow" step 2 ("Active and incomplete
  groups are included for status but excluded from completed-throughput
  averages"); 09d-1's `completed` flag is the gate. Percentiles use a fixed
  nearest-rank rule (deterministic — no interpolation, no estimator ambiguity).
  Lane/barrier aggregation: doc 09 step 4 — `barrier:{barrier}` when ≥ 2
  completed samples, else `lane:{lane}`, else `policy` (the modal lane/barrier
  across the completed metrics, ties broken by sorted name).
- `feedback_id` — `_derive_feedback_id`: `sha256` over the sorted-keys
  `(feature_id, window_start/end_group, lane, barrier, overlay_id or "root",
  sorted metric_ids)` tuple, in the SAME deterministic style as the 09a
  `derive_overlay_id` / `derive_metric_id`. `generated_at` is DELIBERATELY
  EXCLUDED — re-running adaptive sizing over the identical metric set yields
  the identical `feedback_id` (→ the 09b store idempotency key collides → an
  idempotent no-op re-insert). `generated_at` is an explicit caller-supplied
  parameter so a test can pin it.
- `persist_scheduler_feedback` — writes EXACTLY ONE
  `execution_scheduler_feedback` row through the 09b
  `RegroupOverlayStore.insert_scheduler_feedback` (idempotent on
  `scheduler_feedback_idempotency_key`).
- `project_sizing_review` — writes ONE `review:dag-sizing:{feature_id}:
  {window}` `artifacts` row (`{window}` = `g{start}-g{end}`). The body carries
  the typed `SchedulerFeedback` payload + the candidate-wave recommendation +
  an EXPLICIT `"executable": false` / `"is_active_marker": false` stamp.
  Idempotent on identical body (reuse the existing row).
- `run_adaptive_sizing` — the end-to-end flow: 09d-1's
  `build_scheduler_group_metrics` (steps 1-3, a pure read path) →
  `compute_scheduler_feedback` (steps 4-7, pure) → `persist_scheduler_feedback`
  + `project_sizing_review` (step 8). `persist=False` performs NO write.

### How the NEVER-writes-an-active-marker safety property is guaranteed

09d-2's ENTIRE persistence surface is exactly TWO writes, and only when
`persist=True`: one `execution_scheduler_feedback` row (through the 09b store)
and one `review:dag-sizing:*` `artifacts` row. It is structurally impossible
for 09d-2 to write an active marker:

- `persist_scheduler_feedback` calls ONLY `RegroupOverlayStore.
  insert_scheduler_feedback` — that 09b method inserts into
  `execution_scheduler_feedback` and nothing else (its own docstring: "this
  store never writes a `dag-regroup-active:*` marker").
- `project_sizing_review` runs ONE `INSERT INTO artifacts` with a key that is
  ALWAYS `review:dag-sizing:{feature_id}:{window}` — never `dag-regroup-
  active:*` / `dag-regroup:*` / `dag-regroup-rollback:*`.
- `run_adaptive_sizing` calls ONLY those two + 09d-1's read-only metric build.
  It never imports / calls `activate_overlay`, never inserts an
  `execution_regroup_overlays` row, never writes an `events` row.
- The `review:dag-sizing:*` key is NEVER consumed by `RegroupOverlayResolver`
  (the resolver reads only the typed overlay row + `dag-regroup-active:*` /
  `dag-regroup:*`) — `test_run_adaptive_sizing_review_key_never_consumed_by_
  resolver` proves it: after `run_adaptive_sizing` emits a review artifact, the
  resolver still returns `has_typed_overlay=False` / `applied=False`.
- The cap recommendation can affect dispatch ONLY by being converted into a
  new `RegroupOverlay` and passing the full 13-step `validate_overlay` +
  `activate_overlay` (doc 09 § "Validation Algorithm": "Metrics can only
  produce a recommendation artifact. The recommendation must be converted into
  a new overlay and pass the same validation path before activation").
  `test_run_adaptive_sizing_recommendation_needs_validate_overlay` asserts NO
  `execution_regroup_overlays` row + NO active marker exist after the flow.

`test_run_adaptive_sizing_writes_only_feedback_and_review` snapshots `count(*)`
of 7 tables + 5 artifact-key families before/after and asserts: +1
`execution_scheduler_feedback`, +1 `review:dag-sizing:*` artifact, ZERO active
markers / canonical / rollback artifacts / overlay rows / events rows, and the
typed evidence tables untouched.

### How `data_quality` degrades on flagged metrics

`_resolve_data_quality` reads 09d-1's `data_quality_flags` off the CONTRIBUTING
metrics (the completed metrics, the sizing inputs — or the whole status window
when zero completed). doc 09 § "Scheduler Feedback Schema": missing evidence
links "force `SchedulerFeedback.data_quality` away from `sufficient`." The
deterministic, conservative rule:

- No contributing metric → `insufficient`.
- ANY metric carries `missing_projection_lineage` → `stale` (doc 09 § "Adaptive
  Sizing Data Flow" step 3: "stale projection lineage sets `data_quality=
  "stale"`"). Stale is the strongest signal — it wins.
- Otherwise ANY metric carries any flag: EVERY metric flagged → `insufficient`;
  some but not all → `mixed`.
- No flag anywhere → `sufficient`.

A `stale` window also BLOCKS the widen in `compute_cap_decision` (the `stale`
arg is `data_quality == "stale"`), so a flagged window stays conservative.
`confidence` is `low` whenever `data_quality != "sufficient"` or `< 2`
completed samples.

### Interfaces / citations verified at file:line

- `SchedulerGroupMetric` / `SchedulerFeedback` (every field) — 09a
  `regroup_overlay.py:226` / `:289`.
- `RegroupOverlay` (`group_idx_offset` / `derived_execution_order` /
  `write_sets` / `barriers` (`OverlayBarrier.hard` / `barrier_id` /
  `task_ids`) / `speed_index` (`OverlayTaskSpeedMetadata.semantic_lane` /
  `.barrier` / `.unknown_write`) / `remaining_dependency_edges`) —
  `regroup_overlay.py`.
- `build_scheduler_group_metrics(conn, *, feature_id, base_dag, overlay,
  write_sets)` / `effective_execution_order_for_overlay(base_dag, overlay)` /
  `SchedulerMetricsError` — 09d-1 `scheduler_metrics.py:1051` / `:1205` / `:120`.
- `RegroupOverlayStore.insert_scheduler_feedback` (idempotent on
  `scheduler_feedback_idempotency_key`; inserts ONLY into
  `execution_scheduler_feedback`) / `SchedulerFeedbackRecord` /
  `RegroupOverlayStore(conn)` — 09b `regroup_overlay_store.py:730` / `:148` /
  `:329`.
- `RegroupOverlayResolver(store)` / `resolve(feature_id, group_idx) ->
  RegroupOverlayResolution` (fields `has_typed_overlay` / `applied` /
  `effective_execution_order` / `quiesce_reason`) — 09c-2
  `regroup_overlay_resolver.py:225` / `:228` / `:95`.
- `stable_digest` / `stable_json` — `execution_control/models.py:1141-1146`.
- `INSERT INTO artifacts (feature_id, key, value)` — mirrors
  `regroup_overlay_activation.py:594` `_insert_artifact`.
- `ImplementationDAG` / `ImplementationTask` (`id` / `dependencies` /
  `files`) — `models/outputs.py:984` / `:942`.
- `execution_scheduler_feedback` table + its `data_quality` / `confidence`
  CHECK constraints — `schema.sql` Slice-09 section.
- `execution_journal_rows.status` CHECK (`started` / `succeeded` / `failed` /
  `cancelled` / `incomplete`) — `schema.sql:53`.

### doc-09 ambiguities + resolutions

1. **The four `policy_cap` tiers name no concrete barrier ids / lane names.**
   doc 09 step 1 says "high-risk barriers", "backend ... work", "isolated
   UI/document work", "test-only and perf lanes" but the typed
   `OverlayTaskSpeedMetadata` carries only a free-text `barrier` /
   `semantic_lane`. Resolution: classify by matching documented substrings
   (`_HIGH_RISK_BARRIER_MARKERS` / `_TEST_OR_PERF_LANE_MARKERS` /
   `_ISOLATED_UI_OR_DOCUMENT_LANE_MARKERS` / `_BACKEND_LANE_MARKERS`), most-
   conservative tier first, and fall an UNRECOGNIZED lane/barrier to the 4-cap.
   Sound because doc 09 says caps are "conservative" — a mis-tagged lane must
   never widen above the safe minimum; a reviewer-watch item below.
2. **No `feedback_id` formula.** doc 09 gives explicit `overlay_id` /
   `metric_id` formulas but not `feedback_id`. Resolution: mint it in the SAME
   `sha256`-over-sorted-keys style, EXCLUDING `generated_at` so a re-run is
   idempotent. Sound — it matches the 09a derivation discipline + the doc-09
   projection-idempotency intent.
3. **No `confidence` formula.** doc 09 carries `confidence` on the model with
   no rule. Resolution: `low` when data is not sufficient or `< 2` samples;
   `high` when sufficient + `>= 4` samples; `medium` otherwise. Conservative
   and deterministic.
4. **The percentile estimator for `p50` / `p75`.** doc 09 names `p50` / `p75`
   but does not pin an estimator. Resolution: nearest-rank (no interpolation) —
   the simplest fully deterministic choice; it never invents an intermediate
   value (conservative-by-construction).
5. **doc 09 step 3 says "Reduce to current cap OR 4" — which?** Resolution:
   `min(current_cap, 4)` (the more conservative reading — a regression should
   not be able to leave the cap *higher* than 4 when `current_cap > 4`).
6. **doc 09 step 6: "Never widen from a window whose completed groups omit
   typed merge/checkpoint proof."** By 09d-1's construction a `completed=True`
   metric ALWAYS has the full merge/checkpoint proof (09d-1 only sets
   `completed=True` for a fully-proven checkpoint), so the only remaining
   doc-09 staleness trigger is the projection-lineage signal — `stale` is
   driven by `data_quality == "stale"`. Confirmed against 09d-1's
   completed-group identity.
7. **`current_cap` source.** 09d-2 takes `current_cap` as a caller-supplied
   parameter (the cap currently in force, the baseline the recommendation
   widens from / narrows to) rather than inferring it. doc 09 § "Scheduler
   Feedback Schema" carries `current_cap` as a model field; the caller (09e's
   facade conversion / the future scheduler wiring) owns supplying it. 09d-2
   does not assume any particular legacy cap source — `dag_regroup.py`'s legacy
   `recommend_adaptive_sizing` is left untouched.

The carried P3-7 / P3-8 / P3-D are confirmed for 09d-2: 09d-2 writes only the
`execution_scheduler_feedback` row + the `review:dag-sizing:*` artifact — it
NEVER writes a `dag-regroup-validation:*` artifact, so it cannot collide with
the 09b validation key (P3-7); it reads the typed overlay's own `speed_index` /
`barriers` / `write_sets` and the optional Slice 3 `write_sets` param, and does
NOT assume the validator already enforced live Slice 3 contracts — the
candidate-wave validators are 09d-2's own recommendation-time checks, not a
substitute for `validate_overlay` (P3-8); 09d-2 is offset-agnostic — it takes
whatever `group_idx_offset` the overlay carries and makes NO any-offset-
dispatch claim (P3-D). The new 09d-1 P3-E / P3-G are noted: 09d-2's
`verify_cost_per_task` weighting reads 09d-1's `verify_cost_units` (the typed
verification-cost sum from the overlay speed index — NOT the overlapping
`verify_count`), so the P3-E `verify_count`/`gate_evidence_ids` overlap does
not skew 09d-2's cap (P3-E should be confirmed clean by 09e); the P3-G
`merge_conflicts` upward bias only ever SHRINKS the 09d-2 cap (a higher
conflict rate → the step-3 reduction fires; never a widen) — conservative-safe.

### Tests + results

NEW `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_scheduler_sizing.py` — **45 tests,
all green** (real Postgres for persistence + end-to-end, pure for the cap
computation; SKIPs cleanly when `localhost:5431` is down). Test names by area:

- policy_cap tiers — `test_policy_cap_unknown_write_is_4`,
  `test_policy_cap_high_risk_barrier_is_4`,
  `test_policy_cap_backend_lane_is_6`, `test_policy_cap_multi_repo_is_6`,
  `test_policy_cap_isolated_ui_document_is_10`,
  `test_policy_cap_test_or_perf_is_14`,
  `test_policy_cap_unclassified_lane_falls_to_conservative_4`,
  `test_policy_cap_riskier_tier_wins`.
- evidence_cap formula + clamp — `test_evidence_cap_formula_is_floor_12h_over_
  p75`, `test_evidence_cap_clamps_down_to_policy_cap`,
  `test_evidence_cap_none_when_no_p75`, `test_checkpoint_budget_is_12h`.
- reduction triggers — `test_cap_widen_blocked_below_two_samples`,
  `test_cap_widen_allowed_with_two_samples`,
  `test_cap_widen_blocked_when_stale`,
  `test_cap_reduced_when_rate_exceeds_baseline_by_more_than_10pct`,
  `test_cap_no_regression_when_rate_within_10pct`,
  `test_cap_never_below_minimum`, `test_cap_narrowing_always_allowed`.
- post-cap validators shrinking AND a widened wave being rejected —
  `test_candidate_waves_shrink_on_hard_barrier_mix`,
  `test_candidate_waves_shrink_on_write_set_overlap`,
  `test_candidate_waves_unknown_write_task_scheduled_alone`,
  `test_candidate_waves_missing_contract_task_scheduled_alone`,
  `test_candidate_waves_respect_dependency_topology`,
  `test_candidate_waves_rejects_cap_below_minimum`,
  `test_candidate_waves_widen_when_all_validators_pass`.
- data_quality forced off sufficient — `test_data_quality_sufficient_when_no_
  flags`, `test_data_quality_stale_on_missing_projection_lineage`,
  `test_data_quality_insufficient_when_every_metric_flagged`,
  `test_data_quality_mixed_when_some_metrics_flagged`,
  `test_data_quality_flag_forces_off_sufficient_blocks_widen`,
  `test_data_quality_insufficient_when_no_completed_groups`.
- typed SchedulerFeedback shape + determinism — `test_feedback_is_typed_
  scheduler_feedback`, `test_feedback_id_is_deterministic_and_clock_
  independent`, `test_feedback_aggregates_by_barrier_with_two_samples`,
  `test_feedback_rejects_empty_feature_id`,
  `test_feedback_rejects_current_cap_below_minimum`.
- emits ONLY a SchedulerFeedback row + the review projection, NEVER an active
  marker — `test_persist_scheduler_feedback_writes_one_feedback_row`,
  `test_project_sizing_review_writes_review_artifact`,
  `test_run_adaptive_sizing_writes_only_feedback_and_review` (the 7-table +
  5-artifact-family count snapshot), `test_run_adaptive_sizing_dry_run_writes_
  nothing`, `test_run_adaptive_sizing_review_key_never_consumed_by_resolver`,
  `test_run_adaptive_sizing_recommendation_needs_validate_overlay`,
  `test_run_adaptive_sizing_widens_end_to_end_with_full_lineage`,
  `test_run_adaptive_sizing_with_overlay_uses_offset_window`.

Regression: `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q` → **637
passed** (592 prior + 45 new 09d-2; 0 failed, 0 regressions).
`python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py tests/workflows/test_
regroup_overlay.py -q` → **68 passed** (the legacy regroup compatibility suite
— 27 + 41 — stays GREEN; `dag_regroup.py` UNTOUCHED).

### Hygiene

`python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` → OK.
`git diff --check` → clean.

### P1 / P2-risk areas for the 09e reviewer

- **P3-risk (lane/barrier classification by substring).** `compute_policy_cap`
  classifies a free-text lane/barrier by matching documented substrings (doc 09
  names the four tiers but no concrete ids). The fail-soft direction is
  conservative — an unrecognized lane falls to the 4-cap — so a mis-match can
  only ever shrink the cap, never widen it unsafely. 09e should confirm the
  marker lists are reasonable for the project's actual lane vocabulary (or
  decide whether the lane→tier map should be an explicit overlay-carried field
  rather than a substring heuristic). NOT a P1/P2 — it cannot produce an unsafe
  widen.
- **P3-risk (the nearest-rank percentile).** `p50` / `p75` use nearest-rank
  (no interpolation). With a very small completed-sample set (the common case)
  this is the conservative deterministic choice; 09e may confirm it matches
  doc 09's intent.
- No P1/P2 identified. The cap recommendation is structurally inert — it cannot
  reach dispatch without `validate_overlay` + `activate_overlay`; the
  pure-read-path + two-write surface is proven by the count-snapshot test; the
  determinism (clock-independent `feedback_id`, sorted keys, nearest-rank) is
  tested; `dag_regroup.py` is untouched; the legacy regroup suite is green.

Slice 09 status: 09a + 09b + 09b-2 + 09c + 09d-1 + **09d-2 COMPLETE** (pending
the slice-end review); 09e next — the slice-end six-vector review + Slice 09
acceptance + the `dag_regroup.py` facade conversion.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 09d-2 REVIEW

Independent review of Slice 09d-2 — the conservative cap computation + the
typed `SchedulerFeedback` + the `review:dag-sizing:{feature_id}:{window}`
projection. REVIEW ONLY — no code changes. Review target: the working-tree
state of `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/scheduler_sizing.py`
(NEW, 1729 lines) and `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_scheduler_
sizing.py` (NEW, 1640 lines, 45 tests), cross-checked against doc 09 §§
"Scheduler Metrics And Cap Rules" / "Scheduler Feedback Schema" / "Adaptive
Sizing Data Flow".

### Reviewer summary

Slice 09d-2 is CLEAN. Every doc-09 cap formula/threshold is transcribed
verbatim; the post-cap validators run strictly after cap selection and only
ever shrink/reject; the persistence surface is structurally exactly two writes
(one `execution_scheduler_feedback` row + one `review:dag-sizing:*` artifact)
and it is impossible for 09d-2 to reach dispatch without a new overlay +
`validate_overlay` + `activate_overlay`. 45 new tests pass (1.54s); the 90-test
regroup + 09d-1 regression suite (27 dag_regroup + 41 regroup_overlay + 22
scheduler_metrics) stays GREEN; `dag_regroup.py` / `scheduler_metrics.py` /
the 09a `regroup_overlay.py` are UNTOUCHED (`git status` shows no `M`).
Verdict: 0 P1, 0 P2, 2 non-blocking P3 carried for 09e (P3-I + P3-J).

### Vector 1 — doc-09 cap-formula conformance — PASS

Every formula/threshold cross-checked against doc 09 § "Scheduler Metrics And
Cap Rules" (== § "Adaptive Sizing Data Flow" steps 5-6); none diverges:

- `policy_cap` risk tiers (`compute_policy_cap`, scheduler_sizing.py:308-372) —
  `POLICY_CAP_UNKNOWN_OR_HIGH_RISK=4` / `POLICY_CAP_BACKEND_OR_MULTI_REPO=6` /
  `POLICY_CAP_ISOLATED_UI_OR_DOCUMENT=10` / `POLICY_CAP_TEST_OR_PERF=14`
  (scheduler_sizing.py:178-184) match doc-09 step 1 verbatim
  (unknown-write/high-risk→4, backend/multi-repo→6, isolated-UI/document→10,
  test/perf→14). Tiers evaluated most-conservative-first (the riskier tier
  wins) — verified at :337-372.
- `evidence_cap = floor(12h / hours_per_task_p75)` clamped `[4, policy_cap]`
  (`compute_evidence_cap`, :375-403): `raw = math.floor(CHECKPOINT_BUDGET_HOURS
  / hours_per_task_p75)` then `max(MIN_CAP, min(raw, policy_cap))` —
  `CHECKPOINT_BUDGET_HOURS=12.0` (:191), `MIN_CAP=4` (:188). Exact.
- The three reduction triggers (`compute_cap_decision`, :425-563):
  (a) sample < 2 — `MIN_SAMPLES_TO_WIDEN=2` (:200); a candidate strictly above
  `current_cap` with `sample_count < 2` is clamped to `min(current_cap,
  candidate)` (:506-512). (b) stale data — clamps identically (:500-505).
  (c) repair/commit/merge-conflict rate > global baseline + 10% —
  `RATE_REGRESSION_THRESHOLD=0.10` (:196); `_GlobalBaseline.rate_regressions`
  flags `window_rate > baseline_rate * (1.0 + 0.10)` (:676); a regression
  reduces to `min(current_cap, MIN_CAP)` (:534). All match doc-09 steps 2/3/6.
- `min(current_cap, 4)` for "reduce to current cap or 4" — the more
  conservative reading (a regression never leaves the cap above 4); sound.
- The candidate cap is `min(policy_cap, evidence_cap)` (:487) — the smaller
  ceiling wins (conservative). Result clamped to `>= MIN_CAP` (:552).

### Vector 2 — post-cap validator ordering — PASS

The dependency / hard-barrier / write-set / mapping validators run strictly
AFTER cap selection and may only SHRINK or REJECT; no metric-driven path widens
past them. Confirmed:

- `compute_scheduler_feedback` computes `cap_decision = compute_cap_decision(
  ...)` FIRST (:1348-1355), THEN calls `build_candidate_waves(cap=cap_decision.
  recommended_cap, ...)` (:1360-1366). The cap is passed as the upper-bound
  parameter only.
- Inside `build_candidate_waves` the `cap` is used solely as `if len(wave) >=
  cap: break` (:885) — it can only STOP a wave growing, never force a task in.
  Every task added beyond the seed must pass `_can_join_wave` (:887-897).
- `_can_join_wave` (:926-1018) rejects on: unknown-write/missing-contract
  (:955-962), hard-barrier mix (:964-985), same-wave write-set overlap after
  path canonicalization (:987-1004), incomplete merged-group coverage
  (:1006-1016). Each returns `(False, reason)` — a REJECT/SHRINK, never a
  widen. There is no code path where a metric or cap value re-admits a task a
  validator rejected.
- `build_candidate_waves` is documented and verified as a pure in-memory
  function: it returns `CandidateWave` objects and mutates nothing — no DAG
  write, no overlay write, no marker. A widened candidate wave still has to
  become a new `RegroupOverlay` and pass the full 13-step `validate_overlay`.

### Vector 3 — NEVER-writes-an-active-marker — PASS

09d-2's ENTIRE persistence surface is exactly one `SchedulerFeedback` row + one
`review:dag-sizing:{feature_id}:{window}` artifact, and only when
`persist=True`. Confirmed structurally:

- `persist_scheduler_feedback` (:1515-1535) calls ONLY `store.insert_scheduler_
  feedback(feedback)`. The 09b `RegroupOverlayStore.insert_scheduler_feedback`
  (regroup_overlay_store.py:730) inserts into `execution_scheduler_feedback`
  and nothing else (verified: its sole `INSERT` targets that table).
- `project_sizing_review` (:1538-1621) runs ONE `INSERT INTO artifacts` with
  `key = _sizing_review_key(...)` which is ALWAYS `review:dag-sizing:
  {feature_id}:g{start}-g{end}` (:1500-1512) — never `dag-regroup-active:*` /
  `dag-regroup:*` / `dag-regroup-rollback:*`. The body carries an explicit
  `"executable": false` + `"is_active_marker": false` stamp (:1579-1581).
- `run_adaptive_sizing` (:1627-1729) calls ONLY `build_scheduler_group_metrics`
  (09d-1, read-only) + `persist_scheduler_feedback` + `project_sizing_review`.
  It does NOT import or call `activate_overlay`, inserts no `execution_regroup_
  overlays` row, writes no `events` row, writes no typed-attempt / evidence /
  merge-queue row.
- The `review:dag-sizing:*` key is NOT consumed by `RegroupOverlayResolver` —
  confirmed by `grep`: `regroup_overlay_resolver.py` references only
  `dag-regroup-active:{slug}` / `dag-regroup:{slug}`, never `review:`.
- The cap recommendation cannot reach dispatch on its own — doc 09
  § "Validation Algorithm" is honored: a recommendation must be converted into
  a new overlay and pass `validate_overlay` + `activate_overlay`.
- Test honesty: `test_run_adaptive_sizing_writes_only_feedback_and_review`
  (test_scheduler_sizing.py:1289-1384) seeds two FULLY completed groups
  (typed attempts + 5 evidence nodes + a fully-proven `merge_queue_items` lane
  + a `dag-group:{n}` artifact), snapshots `count(*)` of 7 tables + 5
  artifact-key families before/after, and asserts +1 feedback / +1 review
  artifact / 0 active-marker / 0 canonical / 0 rollback / 0 overlay-rows /
  0 events / typed-evidence tables unchanged. Non-vacuous and exact.

### Vector 4 — `data_quality` degradation — PASS

`_resolve_data_quality` (:1118-1166) reads 09d-1's `data_quality_flags` off the
contributing metrics. The rule matches the brief verbatim: no contributing
metric → `insufficient`; any `missing_projection_lineage` → `stale` (wins);
else any flag with EVERY metric flagged → `insufficient`, some → `mixed`; no
flag → `sufficient`. A `stale` window also blocks the widen — `compute_
scheduler_feedback` sets `stale = data_quality == "stale"` (:1346) and passes
it to `compute_cap_decision`. The four `missing_*` flag names are exactly those
09d-1 emits (scheduler_metrics.py:938-949). Tested by 6 dedicated tests.

### Vector 5 — Determinism — PASS

`feedback_id` (`_derive_feedback_id`, :1038-1071) is `stable_digest` over a
sorted-keys identity dict with `metric_ids` pre-sorted and `generated_at`
EXCLUDED — `test_feedback_id_is_deterministic_and_clock_independent` proves a
7-day clock shift yields the identical id. The nearest-rank percentile
(`_percentile_nearest_rank`, :406-422) sorts its input and indexes
`ceil(pct*n)` clamped `[1,n]` — order- and float-stable, no interpolation.
`_select_lane_and_barrier` (:1074-1115) breaks modal ties by sorted name.
`build_candidate_waves` sorts eligible tasks by `(original_group, task_id)`
and emits sorted `task_ids` — deterministic. The `project_sizing_review` body
is `json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True)`.

### Vector 6 — doc-09 ambiguity resolutions — all SOUND

(1) substring lane→tier classification, unrecognized → 4-cap: SOUND — the
fail-soft direction is conservative; a mis-tag can only shrink, never widen
unsafely (see P3-I). (2) minted `feedback_id` (sha256-over-sorted-keys,
clock-excluded): SOUND — matches the 09a derivation discipline + the doc-09
projection-idempotency intent. (3) `confidence` band (low/medium/high by
data-quality + sample count): SOUND — conservative and deterministic.
(4) nearest-rank percentile: SOUND — doc 09 pins no estimator; nearest-rank
never invents an intermediate value (see P3-J). (5) "reduce to current cap or
4" → `min(current_cap, 4)`: SOUND — the strictly more conservative reading.
(6) `current_cap` as a caller parameter: SOUND — doc 09 carries `current_cap`
as a model field; the caller owns the cap in force; 09d-2 makes no legacy-cap
assumption and leaves `dag_regroup.py` untouched. None is a divergence.

### Vector 7 — Additivity & test honesty — PASS

`dag_regroup.py`, `scheduler_metrics.py` (09d-1), the 09a `regroup_overlay.py`
are all untouched (`git status --short` shows no `M`; only the expected `??`).
The 45 tests are not vacuous: each of the 8 `policy_cap` tier tests asserts a
concrete cap constant; the `evidence_cap` tests check the floor formula + the
`[4,policy_cap]` clamp both directions; the reduction-trigger tests cover
sample<2 / stale / rate>baseline+10% with hand-built `_GlobalBaseline`s; the
post-cap-validator tests prove a wave SHRINKS on hard-barrier mix / write-set
overlap / unknown-write / missing-contract AND widens when all validators pass;
the `data_quality` tests cover sufficient/stale/insufficient/mixed/zero-
completed; the writes-nothing-but-two property is the 7-table+5-artifact-family
count snapshot. `verify_cost_per_task` reads 09d-1's `verify_cost_units`-based
field (NOT the P3-E `verify_count`/`gate_evidence_ids` overlap), and `verify_
cost_per_task` is reported on the feedback but never gates a cap decision — so
P3-E does not skew the 09d-2 cap. The P3-G `merge_conflicts` upward bias only
ever shrinks the 09d-2 cap (conservative-safe).

### Findings

- **P3-I (non-blocking, carried to 09e)** — `compute_policy_cap` classifies a
  free-text `OverlayTaskSpeedMetadata` `barrier` / `semantic_lane` by matching
  documented substrings (`_HIGH_RISK_BARRIER_MARKERS` etc.,
  scheduler_sizing.py:214-254). doc 09 names the four tiers but no concrete
  ids. The fail-soft direction is conservative (an unrecognized lane → the
  4-cap), so a mis-match can only ever shrink the cap, never widen it
  unsafely — NOT a P1/P2. 09e should confirm the marker lists fit the
  project's real lane vocabulary, or decide whether the lane→tier map should
  be an explicit overlay-carried field rather than a substring heuristic.
- **P3-J (non-blocking, carried to 09e)** — `p50` / `p75` use nearest-rank
  with no interpolation (`_percentile_nearest_rank`, :406-422). With a very
  small completed-sample set (the common case) this is the conservative
  deterministic choice; 09e may confirm it matches doc 09's intent.
- The carried P3-7 / P3-8 / P3-D are confirmed clean for 09d-2: it writes no
  `dag-regroup-validation:*` artifact (no P3-7 key collision); it reads the
  overlay's own `speed_index` / `barriers` / `write_sets` + the optional Slice
  3 `write_sets` param and does NOT assume `validate_overlay` enforced live
  Slice 3 contracts — the candidate-wave validators are 09d-2's own
  recommendation-time checks, not a substitute for the 13-step validator
  (P3-8); it is offset-agnostic (P3-D). The 09d-1 P3-E / P3-G are confirmed
  not to affect the 09d-2 cap (above).

### Verdict

Slice 09d-2 is CLEAN — **0 P1, 0 P2, 2 non-blocking P3 (P3-I + P3-J)** — across
all 7 review vectors. Vector 1 (cap-formula conformance): PASS — every doc-09
formula/threshold transcribed verbatim. Vector 2 (post-cap validator ordering):
PASS — the validators run strictly after cap selection and only shrink/reject;
no metric-driven widen-past-validators path exists. Vector 3 (never-writes-an-
active-marker): PASS — the persistence surface is structurally exactly one
`SchedulerFeedback` row + one `review:dag-sizing:*` artifact; 09d-2 never
imports `activate_overlay`, never writes an overlay/marker/event row, and the
`review:dag-sizing:*` key is never consumed by `RegroupOverlayResolver`.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 09d-2 COMPLETE

Slice 09d-2 — the conservative cap computation + the typed `SchedulerFeedback`

ACCEPTED. The independent Slice 09d-2 REVIEW returned CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2,
2 non-blocking P3 (P3-I + P3-J) — across all 7 vectors, with explicit PASS on
vector 1 (doc-09 cap-formula conformance — every `policy_cap` tier /
`evidence_cap` formula / reduction trigger transcribed verbatim), vector 2
(post-cap validator ordering — the dependency / hard-barrier / write-set /
mapping validators run strictly after cap selection and only shrink/reject),
and vector 3 (never-writes-an-active-marker — the persistence surface is

`review:dag-sizing:*` artifact; no `activate_overlay` / overlay-row / marker /
event write; the `review:dag-sizing:*` key is never consumed by

The new sibling leaf module `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/
scheduler_sizing.py` carries the conservative cap computation
(`compute_policy_cap` / `compute_evidence_cap` / `compute_cap_decision` /

(`compute_scheduler_feedback`), the persistence (`persist_scheduler_feedback`

/ `scheduler_metrics.py` / the 09a `regroup_overlay.py` are UNTOUCHED.

**Slice 09d (09d-1 the `SchedulerGroupMetric` builder + 09d-2 the cap
computation + the `SchedulerFeedback` emission) is COMPLETE.** All Slice 09
implementation sub-slices — 09a (typed schema + models), 09b (the store),

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 09e-1 IMPLEMENTATION

Slice 09e-1 — the `dag_regroup.py` CLI/review **facade conversion**, the
surgical first chunk of doc 09 § "Refactoring Steps" item 1 (*"Keep
`dag_regroup.py` as a CLI/review facade that delegates to the new module"*).

### What changed (the delegation map)

Two surgical, behavior-preserving edits to
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/dag_regroup.py`:

1. **Added the module docstring** that formally declares `dag_regroup.py` is
   the CLI/review *facade* per doc 09 § "Refactoring Steps" item 1, names the
   typed substance extracted across 09a–09d into
   `workflows/develop/execution/` (`regroup_overlay*.py` + `scheduler_*.py`),
   and records the 09e-1 ↔ 09e-2 SPLIT decision (below).

2. **Converted the legacy `g45-g73` artifact-key constants from opaque string
   literals into a delegation to the typed module.** `dag_regroup.py` now
   imports `derive_overlay_slug` from `.execution.regroup_overlay` (09a) and
   computes `_LEGACY_OVERLAY_SLUG = derive_overlay_slug(group_idx_offset=45,
   last_original_group=73)`; `DRAFT_KEY` / `CANONICAL_KEY` / `ACTIVE_KEY` /
   `ROLLBACK_KEY` / `OBSERVATION_KEY` are now f-strings over that derived slug.
   `DEFAULT_FROM_GROUP` / `DEFAULT_TO_GROUP` were hoisted above the key block
   (the slug now depends on them). The facade thus *derives* its compatibility
   keys through the typed overlay module — the typed `derive_overlay_slug` is
   the single source of truth for the slug spelling — instead of hard-coding
   them. The formula yields the **byte-identical** `g45-g73` suffix for the
   `group_idx_offset == 45` / `last_original_group == 73` case (verified:
   `derive_overlay_slug(45, 73) == "g45-g73"`; every key string is identical
   to its prior literal).

No other code changed. `command_draft` / `command_validate` /
`command_activate` / `command_rollback` / `command_status` /
`command_analyze_sizing` / `command_recommend_sizing`, the builders
(`build_staged_regroup` / `build_speed_index` / `validate_candidate`), and the
review collectors (`collect_sizing_metrics` / `identify_process_improvements` /
`recommend_adaptive_sizing`) keep their EXACT prior behavior.

### How CLI / review behavior is preserved

The conversion changes only how five key-string *constants* are spelled — from
literal to a derived f-string that resolves to the identical bytes. Every CLI
command, builder, and review collector reads those same constants and is
otherwise untouched, so observable behavior (artifact keys written, JSON
payloads, event types, return dicts, raised errors) is unchanged. The 27-test
`tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py` legacy compatibility suite — which drives
`command_activate` / `command_rollback` / `command_status` against the
artifact-table `_FakeConn`, and asserts exact reason codes from
`_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` — is the behavior-preservation safety
net and stays GREEN with zero test edits.

### SPLIT decision (09e-1 covered vs. 09e-2 deferred)

doc 09 § "Refactoring Steps" item 1's facade conversion is **multi-part**, so
per the loop-discipline split rule it was SPLIT:

- **09e-1 (this entry, COMPLETE)** — the key-derivation delegation + the facade
  docstring. Surgical, behavior-preserving, fully verified.
- **09e-2 (DEFERRED)** — rewiring `command_activate` / `command_rollback` onto
  the *async, typed* `activate_overlay` / `rollback_overlay` (09c-1) over the
  `execution_regroup_overlays` tables. This is doc 09 items 7/8 and is
  INSEPARABLE from item 1's full facade: a fully-delegating `command_activate`
  must build a typed `RegroupOverlay` and call `activate_overlay`, which needs
  a `DerivedDAGArtifact` → typed `RegroupOverlay` conversion layer that does
  not exist yet. That rewire NECESSARILY changes CLI behavior — it writes typed
  overlay rows, not only `artifacts` — and would break the 27-test `_FakeConn`
  suite (no typed-overlay tables), which the task's CRITICAL CONSTRAINT forbids
  during this conversion. It is therefore a separate later iteration.

Doc 09 item 3 ("move `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update`'s regroup-specific
checks into the overlay validator") is, per the task prompt, a delegation not a
re-implementation — and it is **already substantively satisfied**: the 13-step
`validate_overlay` (09b-2) IS the typed reimplementation of those checks.
`_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` itself must REMAIN as the
`DerivedDAGArtifact`-shaped sync validator because (a) the typed resolver at
`implementation.py:16939` calls it on a `DerivedDAGArtifact`, (b)
`dag_regroup.validate_candidate` (`dag_regroup.py:494`) calls it, (c) the
27-test suite asserts its exact reason codes. Confirmed structurally:
`validate_overlay`'s `_coerce_typed_overlay`
(`regroup_overlay_validation.py:207-221`) explicitly **rejects a bare
`DerivedDAGArtifact`** with `dag_regroup_overlay_not_typed` — the typed
validator cannot consume the legacy artifact model, so no sync→async delegation
of `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` is possible without the 09e-2
conversion layer. 09e-1 leaves `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` and the
3 `_implement_dag` call sites + the `_resolve_active_regroup_*` functions
untouched.

### Interfaces / citations verified at file:line before editing

  group)` — `workflows/develop/execution/regroup_overlay.py:372-389`; returns
  `g{offset}-g{last_original_group}` (or `g{offset}-tail`). Verified

  "g45-g73"`.
- `regroup_overlay.py` imports — only `pydantic` + `execution_control.models.

- `dag_regroup.py` legacy key constants prior literals — `dag_regroup.py:24-31`
  (now 64-74): `DRAFT_KEY="review:dag-regroup-draft:g45-g73"`,
  `CANONICAL_KEY="dag-regroup:g45-g73"`, `ACTIVE_KEY="dag-regroup-active:
  g45-g73"`, `ROLLBACK_KEY="dag-regroup-rollback:g45-g73"`,
  `OBSERVATION_KEY="dag-regroup-observation:g45-g73"`. All five byte-identical

  `_barrier_for_task`, `semantic_lane_for_task` only) + `test_dag_regroup.py`;
  the CLI commands run only via `main()` / `_run()`. None affected.
- `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` — `implementation.py:24924`; 3 call

  `regroup_overlay_validation.py:209-221`.
- The 3 `_implement_dag` regroup call sites — `implementation.py:17056` /

### doc-09 ambiguities + resolutions

  layer absent from 09a–09d and would break the 27-test safety net — so 09e-1
  delivers the verifiable behavior-preserving subset (key-derivation

  recorded in both journals.
- **Ambiguity**: doc 09 item 3 — is moving `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_
  update`'s regroup checks a re-implementation? **Resolution**: per the task
  prompt it is a delegation, and `validate_overlay` (09b-2) already IS the
  typed reimplementation; `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` stays because
  the typed validator structurally rejects `DerivedDAGArtifact`
  (`dag_regroup_overlay_not_typed`). No 09e-1 change.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 09e-1 REVIEW

Independent REVIEW of the Slice 09e-1 `dag_regroup.py` facade conversion (the
`2026-05-22 — Slice 09e-1 IMPLEMENTATION` entry above). REVIEW only — no code
changes. Working-tree state of `dag_regroup.py`,
`execution/regroup_overlay.py`, `regroup_overlay_validation.py`, and
`tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py` inspected directly; the targeted suites
re-run against real Postgres (`localhost:5431`).

### Reviewer summary

The delivered 09e-1 change is a **two-edit, behavior-preserving facade
conversion**: (1) a module docstring formally declaring `dag_regroup.py` the
doc-09-item-1 CLI/review facade and recording the 09e-1 ↔ 09e-2 split; (2) the
five legacy `g45-g73` artifact-key constants converted from opaque string
literals to f-strings over `_LEGACY_OVERLAY_SLUG = derive_overlay_slug(
group_idx_offset=45, last_original_group=73)` (the typed 09a derivation), with
`DEFAULT_FROM_GROUP` / `DEFAULT_TO_GROUP` hoisted above the key block. The
delivered change is CLEAN — **0 P1, 0 P2** — across all four review vectors.
The implementer's SPLIT-WITHIN-A-SPLIT (deferring the CLI rewire to 09e-2) is a
sound *engineering* decision but, per vector 3 below, the deferred CLI rewire
**IS a required Slice-09 refactoring step** and must be tracked as the next
sub-slice — Slice 09 is not yet complete.

### Findings

- **Vector 1 — delivered-change correctness — PASS (0 P1/P2).** All five
  derived keys verified BYTE-IDENTICAL to their prior literals by direct
  execution (`dag_regroup.py:82-86`): `DRAFT_KEY ==
  "review:dag-regroup-draft:g45-g73"`, `CANONICAL_KEY == "dag-regroup:g45-g73"`,
  `ACTIVE_KEY == "dag-regroup-active:g45-g73"`, `ROLLBACK_KEY ==
  "dag-regroup-rollback:g45-g73"`, `OBSERVATION_KEY ==
  "dag-regroup-observation:g45-g73"`. `derive_overlay_slug`
  (`regroup_overlay.py:372-389`) is the unconditional formula
  `f"g{group_idx_offset}-g{last_original_group}"` (no special case), which for
  `(45, 73)` yields exactly `"g45-g73"` — a changed key would have orphaned
  every legacy `dag-regroup:*` artifact (would-be-P1); it did not. The new
  `dag_regroup.py:66` import `from .execution.regroup_overlay import
  derive_overlay_slug` is CYCLE-FREE: `regroup_overlay.py` imports only
  `pydantic` + `execution_control.models.stable_digest` and does NOT import
  `dag_regroup.py` (verified `python -c "import ...dag_regroup"` succeeds;
  `compileall` clean).
- **Vector 2 — behavior preservation — PASS.** `command_draft` /
  `command_validate` / `command_activate` / `command_rollback` /
  `command_status` / `command_analyze_sizing` / `command_recommend_sizing`,
  the builders, and the review collectors are otherwise untouched — the
  conversion changes only how five constants are *spelled*.
  `tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py` (27) stays GREEN with **zero test
  edits** (`git diff --stat` empty). `test_dag_regroup.py:1420` still asserts
  the literal string `"dag-regroup-active:g45-g73 is missing"` and passes —
  independent confirmation the derived `ACTIVE_KEY` is byte-identical.
- **Vector 3 — doc-09 § "Refactoring Steps" conformance — THE KEY VECTOR.**
  Item-by-item enumeration of doc 09 § "Refactoring Steps":
  - **Item 1** ("Extract `regroup_overlay.py` … Keep `dag_regroup.py` as a
    CLI/review facade **that delegates to the new module**") — PARTIALLY DONE.
    The extraction (09a–09d) is done. The facade conversion is only PARTIALLY
    delivered: 09e-1 delegates the *key constants* and adds the facade
    docstring, but `command_activate` (`dag_regroup.py:2065`) / `command_
    rollback` (`:~2280`) still run the legacy `DerivedDAGArtifact` /
    artifact-table path — they do NOT delegate to the typed `activate_overlay`
    / `rollback_overlay`. A facade "that delegates" is not satisfied by
    constant-spelling delegation alone.
  - **Item 2** ("Replace hard-coded G45-G73 constants in dispatch resolution
    with `RegroupOverlayResolver.resolve`") — DONE in 09c-2 (not a 09e item).
  - **Item 3** ("Move `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` regroup-specific
    checks into the overlay validator and call it from artifact repair/update
    validation, **CLI activation**, and resolver safety checks") — DEFERRED /
    NOT DONE for the CLI-activation clause. The 13-step `validate_overlay`
    (09b-2) is indeed the typed reimplementation of those checks, and
    `_coerce_typed_overlay` (`regroup_overlay_validation.py:209-221`)
    structurally rejects a bare `DerivedDAGArtifact` with
    `dag_regroup_overlay_not_typed` — so `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update`
    legitimately REMAINS as the `DerivedDAGArtifact`-shaped sync validator for
    the legacy resolver/`validate_candidate`/repair call sites. BUT item 3 also
    explicitly requires the overlay validator be called **from CLI
    activation** — and `command_activate` still calls the legacy
    `validate_candidate` → `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update`, not
    `validate_overlay`. So item 3 is satisfied *for the existing typed call
    sites* but NOT for the CLI-activation clause; the CLI-activation clause
    rides on the same 09e-2 rewire as item 1.
  - **Items 4, 5, 6** — done in 09a/09b/09c/09d (typed rows + projections;
    typed-attempt sizing; typed `SchedulerFeedback`). Not 09e items.
  - **Item 7** ("Wire activation to an explicit operator/control-plane action
    that converts an approved recommendation or staged derived DAG into
    `RegroupOverlay`, validates it under lock, writes canonical/projection rows
    atomically, and emits a typed activation event") — `activate_overlay`
    (09c-1) IMPLEMENTS the typed action, but `dag_regroup.py`'s `command_
    activate` CLI surface is NOT wired to it — it remains the legacy
    artifact-table activation. The "wire" is incomplete at the CLI.
  - **Item 8** ("Wire rollback to the overlay store …") — same: `rollback_
    overlay` (09c-1) exists, `command_rollback` is not wired to it.
  - **Item 9** (resolver observation path) — `project_regroup_observation` /
    the resolver are 09c work, not 09e.
  - **Item 10** (targeted regression tests) — runs in the slice-end review.
  **REFACTORING-STEPS ITEMS REMAINING:** the *CLI-delegation remainder of item
  1*, the *CLI-activation clause of item 3*, and the *CLI wiring of items 7 &
  8* — all one coherent chunk: rewiring `command_activate` / `command_rollback`
  onto the typed `activate_overlay` / `rollback_overlay`, with the
  `DerivedDAGArtifact` → `RegroupOverlay` conversion layer, and routing CLI
  activation through `validate_overlay`.
- **VERDICT ON STEP 2 (the key judgment).** The deferred CLI rewire IS A
  REQUIRED part of doc 09's Slice-09 refactoring steps — it is **not** out of
  scope and **not** already satisfied. The deciding citations: doc 09
  § "Refactoring Steps" **item 1** mandates `dag_regroup.py` be "a CLI/review
  facade **that delegates to the new module**" — a facade whose
  `command_activate` / `command_rollback` still run a parallel legacy
  artifact-table activation/rollback path is, by the plain meaning of
  "delegates", not yet that facade; and **item 3** explicitly enumerates
  "**CLI activation**" among the three call sites that must invoke the moved
  overlay validator. Therefore deferring the CLI rewire leaves Slice 09's
  refactoring steps INCOMPLETE, and the deferral must be tracked as a REQUIRED
  next sub-slice (**Slice 09e-1b**), not an optional follow-on. The
  implementer's *engineering* rationale for the split is nonetheless sound —
  the rewire genuinely needs a `DerivedDAGArtifact` → `RegroupOverlay`
  conversion layer that does not exist in 09a–09d, genuinely changes CLI
  behavior (it writes `execution_regroup_overlays` rows), and a behavior change
  legitimately permits *updating* (not weakening) the `test_dag_regroup.py`
  `_FakeConn` suite — so doing it as a separate verified sub-slice is correct
  loop discipline; what is NOT correct is treating Slice 09 as ready for its
  six-vector review/acceptance before 09e-1b lands.
- **Vector 4 — additivity of risk — PASS.** The change is a constant
  re-spelling + a docstring. Regression confirmed: `tests/workflows/develop/
  execution/` + `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py` = **892 passed**
  (637 + 255), and `tests/workflows/test_dag_regroup.py` +
  `tests/workflows/test_regroup_overlay.py` = **68 passed** — 0 regressions,
  exactly matching the implementer's reported counts. `compileall` clean.

### P3 (non-blocking) — confirmed

- **P3 (09e-1, implementer-flagged) — CONFIRMED non-blocking.** `dag_regroup.py`
  now imports `derive_overlay_slug` from `.execution.regroup_overlay`; the
  import is cycle-free today and `regroup_overlay.py` is a true leaf (pydantic
  + `stable_digest` only). A future edit making `regroup_overlay.py` import
  `dag_regroup.py` would create a cycle — the new-module-mirrors-helpers
  convention (recorded in the 09e-1 decisions) already guards this direction.
- **P3-D (carried) — unchanged, not a new regression.** The 3 `_implement_dag`
  regroup call sites remain gated on the legacy `dag-regroup-active:g45-g73`
  marker; the typed `RegroupOverlayResolver` is in practice reachable only for
  an offset-45 overlay. 09e-1 did not re-gate them (correctly out of its
  scope); the call-site re-gating belongs with the 09e-1b CLI rewire.

### Verdict

CLEAN on the delivered 09e-1 change — **0 P1, 0 P2**, 1 confirmed non-blocking
P3 (the import-direction watch) + carried P3-D. Vector-3 verdict: the deferred
CLI rewire IS a required Slice-09 refactoring step (doc 09 § "Refactoring Steps"
items 1, 3, 7, 8). 09e-1 is finalized COMPLETE; the next safe action is **Slice
09e-1b** — the `dag_regroup.py` CLI rewire — NOT the slice-end six-vector
review, which now follows 09e-1b.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 09e-1 COMPLETE

Slice 09e-1 (the `dag_regroup.py` CLI/review facade conversion — the
key-derivation delegation + the facade docstring) is COMPLETE. The independent
Slice 09e-1 REVIEW (entry above) returned CLEAN — **0 P1, 0 P2**, 1 confirmed
non-blocking P3 (the `regroup_overlay.py` import-direction watch) + carried
P3-D — across all four review vectors, with explicit PASS on vector 1 (the five
`g45-g73` artifact keys are BYTE-IDENTICAL to their prior literals — verified by
direct execution — and the new `dag_regroup.py → .execution.regroup_overlay`
import is cycle-free) and vector 2 (`test_dag_regroup.py` 27 GREEN with zero
test edits; `test_dag_regroup.py:1420` still asserts the literal `g45-g73`
key). Regression GREEN: 892 passed (`tests/workflows/develop/execution/` 637 +
`tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py` 255) + 68 passed
(`test_dag_regroup.py` 27 + `test_regroup_overlay.py` 41); `compileall` clean.

**Slice 09 is NOT yet complete.** Per the REVIEW's vector-3 verdict, doc 09
§ "Refactoring Steps" items 1 ("a CLI/review facade that delegates"), 3 ("call
it from CLI activation"), 7, and 8 require the `dag_regroup.py` CLI
`command_activate` / `command_rollback` to be rewired onto the typed
`activate_overlay` / `rollback_overlay` — 09e-1 delivered only the
behavior-preserving constant-delegation chunk and DEFERRED that rewire. The
deferred rewire is a REQUIRED Slice-09 refactoring step, tracked as **Slice
09e-1b** (the next safe action). The slice-end six-vector review + the carried
P3-6…P3-J confirmations + formal Slice 09 acceptance (Slice 09e-2) follow
09e-1b.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 09e-1b CHUNK A — conversion layer + command_activate / command_rollback rewire (CHECKPOINT)

**Split decision.** Slice 09e-1b (6 parts) is large — parts 1-5 touch
`dag_regroup.py` + the 31k-line `implementation.py`. Per the loop-discipline
"one verified sub-deliverable per iteration" rule it is split into two
journaled checkpoints:

- **Chunk A (this entry)** — parts 1, 2, 3, 4 (the `DerivedDAGArtifact` →
  typed `RegroupOverlay` conversion layer + the `command_activate` /
  `command_rollback` rewire + doc-09 item-3's CLI-activation clause) **and**
  part 6's `test_dag_regroup.py` updates for the rewired CLI commands. These
  are inseparable: rewiring `command_activate` breaks the legacy `_FakeConn`
  activation tests the same instant, so the rewire + its tests must land
  together.
- **Chunk B (next)** — part 5 (the P3-D `_implement_dag` call-site re-gating
  in `implementation.py`). It is independent of `dag_regroup.py` (it touches
  only the 3 call sites + their gating), self-contained, and verifiable on its
  own; doing it as the second chunk keeps each chunk a coherent verified unit.

### Part 1 — the `DerivedDAGArtifact` → typed `RegroupOverlay` conversion layer

`derived_artifact_to_regroup_overlay(candidate, base_dag, *, feature_id,
reason="")` added to `dag_regroup.py` (after `_effective_execution_order`), with
`RegroupOverlayConversionError` (a fail-fast `RuntimeError` subtype). It is the
deterministic CLI-side inverse of 09c-1's `build_canonical_projection`: it lifts
a staged/canonical `DerivedDAGArtifact` regroup candidate + the base DAG into
the full 09a typed `RegroupOverlay` — `overlay_id` / `overlay_slug` / the
execution orders / `original_to_new_group_mapping` (int-keyed, transcribed from
the candidate's str-keyed map) / `barriers` (legacy `id`/`hard`/`task_ids`
dicts → typed `OverlayBarrier`, `source="speed_index"`) / `write_sets` /
`speed_index` (the legacy `{"tasks": {...}}` block → `OverlayTaskSpeedMetadata`
per task) / `activation_contract` / `rollback_plan` / `compatibility_keys` /
`overlay_sha256` / `validation_digest`. It computes NO scheduling — a pure
transcription. KEY DESIGN POINTS verified at file:line:

- `task_definition_fingerprints` are computed by reusing
  `regroup_overlay_validation._task_definition_fingerprint` — the SAME formula
  `validate_overlay` step 5 compares against — over the BASE DAG task
  definitions (the derived tasks ARE the base tasks re-waved), so a converted
  overlay's fingerprints match by construction; step 5 then re-derives them
  from the base DAG and confirms.
- `remaining_dependency_edges` are derived from the BASE DAG —
  `base_by_id[tid].dependencies ∩ base_suffix_ids` — i.e. exactly what
  `validate_overlay` step 6 compares to. (Consequence, recorded as a reviewer
  note: tampering only the *candidate's* `dag.tasks[].dependencies` is silently
  corrected by the converter — a genuine-invalid-overlay test must tamper a
  field the converter transcribes verbatim, e.g. `original_to_new_group_
  mapping`; the 09e-1b test `test_activate_fails_closed_when_overlay_fails_
  validation` does exactly that.)
- `overlay_sha256` is stamped by reusing
  `regroup_overlay_validation._canonical_overlay_sha`; it is a single-pass
  fixed point because `_canonical_overlay_sha` excludes BOTH `overlay_sha256`
  and `activation_contract.required_overlay_sha256` from the hashed body. The
  contract's `required_overlay_sha256` is set to the same canonical sha so
  `validate_overlay` step 11 (`required_overlay_sha256 == overlay_sha256`)
  passes. This mirrors EXACTLY how the 09c-1 activation test's `_valid_overlay`
  helper builds a validatable overlay (`test_regroup_overlay_activation.py`
  `_valid_overlay`). `validation_digest` is left a deterministic placeholder
  (`"0"*64`) — `record_validation` owns it, and the 09b store's
  `_validate_overlay_for_insert` only requires it be non-empty.
- `overlay_slug` = `derive_overlay_slug(group_idx_offset=offset,
  last_original_group=len(base_dag.execution_order)-1)` (09a). For a base DAG
  shaped with suffix 45..73 this yields the legacy `g45-g73`; for the 49-group
  `_base_dag()` test fixture it yields `g45-g48` — the genuine generalization.
- `overlay_id` = `derive_overlay_id(...)` (09a) over the candidate identity.
- Fail-fast: `RegroupOverlayConversionError` on a missing `group_idx_offset`,
  an out-of-range offset, or a derived task id absent from the base suffix.

### Part 2 — `command_activate` rewire (doc 09 items 1, 7)

`command_activate` no longer runs the legacy `conn.transaction()` +
`pg_advisory_xact_lock` artifact-table flip. New flow: (a) load the
`DerivedDAGArtifact` candidate + the live root DAG; (b) fail-fast staleness
guard on `base_dag_artifact_id`/`base_dag_sha256` (a human-legible early
message — `validate_overlay` step 2 ALSO rejects a stale base structurally);
(c) the CLI-only `--skip-safety` DB/worktree budget gate, preserved as a
fail-fast facade concern; (d) lift the candidate to a typed `RegroupOverlay`
via the conversion layer; (e) persist the staged overlay row via
`RegroupOverlayStore.insert_overlay` (idempotent on overlay identity); (f) run
the 13-step `validate_overlay` under `store.acquire_feature_lock` (doc 09 item
3's CLI-activation clause — see part 4); (g) call the typed `activate_overlay`
(09c-1). The DB-row-presence checks the legacy facade ran by hand
(`dag-group:{checkpointed}` exists, `dag-group:{offset}` absent, "group work
started", "active marker exists") are SUBSUMED by `activate_overlay`'s
in-transaction `_run_forbidden_set_check` + its `get_active_overlay` check —
NOT re-implemented. `OverlayConflictNeedsFreshOverlay` and
`RegroupActivationRejected` are both re-raised as `RuntimeError` carrying the
deterministic reason, so the CLI surface is a uniform fail-closed `RuntimeError`
(and `main()`'s generic-`Exception` `{"ok": false}` handler still applies).

### Part 3 — `command_rollback` rewire (doc 09 item 8)

`command_rollback` no longer writes a legacy `rolled_back` artifact by hand. It
looks up the single `active` typed overlay via `RegroupOverlayStore.get_active_
overlay` (fail-fast `RuntimeError` when none) and routes to the typed
`rollback_overlay` (09c-1) — which holds the feature advisory lock + one
`conn.transaction()`, cross-checks the active marker against the typed row,
re-runs the doc-09 not-started forbidden-set check, and writes the `rolled_back`
typed status + a `status="rolled_back"` active marker + a typed
`dag_regroup_overlay_rolled_back` event ONLY before the first derived wave
starts (afterward it rejects fail-closed; the active marker is left untouched).
`RegroupRollbackRejected` is re-raised as `RuntimeError` carrying the reason.

### Part 4 — doc 09 item 3's CLI-activation clause

`command_activate` runs `validate_overlay(overlay, OverlayValidationContext(...),
store, activation_check=False, persist=True)` EXPLICITLY under the feature
advisory lock before `activate_overlay`. This is the literal "the moved
regroup-specific checks are called from … CLI activation": the CLI activation
safety checks are routed through the typed 13-step validator; a rejection
raises a `RuntimeError` and NOTHING activates (fail-closed). `activate_overlay`
re-runs `validate_overlay` internally too — the explicit pass gives the operator
a precise rejection reason before any state change, and the re-run is the
doc-09 design (idempotent). `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` itself is
UNTOUCHED — it remains the `DerivedDAGArtifact`-shaped sync validator for the
legacy resolver / `dag_regroup.validate_candidate` / artifact-repair call sites;
`validate_overlay`'s `_coerce_typed_overlay` structurally rejects a bare
`DerivedDAGArtifact` (`dag_regroup_overlay_not_typed`), so the two validators do
not collide. The unused `_advisory_lock_key` import was removed (the typed path
uses `store.acquire_feature_lock`'s session-level `pg_advisory_lock`).

### How the CLI now fails closed on a bad overlay

A CLI activation whose overlay fails `validate_overlay` CANNOT activate: step
(f)'s explicit `validate_overlay` returns `valid=False` and `command_activate`
raises `RuntimeError("candidate validation failed: …")` BEFORE `activate_
overlay` is ever called — and even if that pass were bypassed, `activate_
overlay` (09c-1) step 4 re-runs `validate_overlay` inside its own transaction
and `RegroupActivationRejected`s on `not validation.valid`, rolling the whole
transaction back. The test `test_activate_fails_closed_when_overlay_fails_
validation` proves it: a tampered `original_to_new_group_mapping` (group 48
dropped) → `dag_regroup_mapping_group_coverage_mismatch` → 0 `active`
`execution_regroup_overlays` rows + 0 `dag-regroup:g45-g48` artifacts written.
`test_activate_rejects_when_boundary_checkpoint_missing` proves the boundary
checkpoint constraint (no `dag-group:44` → `dag_regroup_boundary_checkpoint_
missing` → 0 active rows).

### Part 6 (Chunk A subset) — `test_dag_regroup.py` updates for the CLI rewire

The rewire is a GENUINE behavior change — `command_activate` / `command_rollback`
now write `execution_regroup_overlays` typed rows + the canonical/rollback/
active-marker projections in ONE transaction under the advisory lock, not only
`artifacts` rows. The 27-test `_FakeConn` suite has no typed-overlay tables, so
the 4 legacy CLI activation/rollback tests are UPDATED to run against a REAL
Postgres database (the typed path's correctness is in Postgres
transaction/FK/advisory-lock semantics the in-memory fake cannot exercise).
This is the doc-09 § "Tests" expectation ("extend the existing regroup tests
where compatibility behavior is already covered") and is an ALLOWED
behavior-change update. It is NOT a weakening — every safety-check, the
not-started boundary, and the reason codes are still asserted, now against the
real typed transaction, and typed-row assertions are ADDED. Concretely:

- New real-Postgres fixtures (`regroup_database` session-scoped /
  `regroup_dsn` function-scoped truncating) added to `test_dag_regroup.py`,
  mirroring `tests/workflows/develop/execution/conftest.py` (that conftest is
  directory-scoped and not visible to `tests/workflows/`). They SKIP cleanly
  when Postgres is unreachable, so the suite stays green offline. A
  `_patch_real_pool` helper makes `dag_regroup.create_pool` open a pool over
  the test DB; `_RealArtifacts` (pool-backed, exposes `_pool`) lets the typed
  resolver build a `RegroupOverlayStore` over the same DB.
- `test_activate_writes_restart_safe_artifacts` → split/renamed to
  `test_activate_writes_typed_overlay_and_compatibility_projections`: KEEPS the
  legacy "canonical/rollback/active-marker artifacts exist" assertions; ADDS
  the typed `execution_regroup_overlays` row (`status='active'`), the typed
  `dag_regroup_overlay_activated` event, the recorded `execution_regroup_
  validations` row, and the typed-resolver-applies-it assertion.
- `test_activate_rejects_existing_active_marker` →
  `test_activate_rejects_a_different_overlay_over_active_suffix`: the legacy
  test rejected on an `ACTIVE_KEY` *artifact* being present; the rewired CLI
  rejects because `activate_overlay`'s `get_active_overlay` finds a DIFFERENT
  `active` typed overlay row (`dag_regroup_other_overlay_active`). The "cannot
  re-activate over an already-active suffix" coverage is preserved.
- `test_rollback_blocks_after_regrouped_first_wave_task_started`: KEPT (same
  name, same intent — rollback rejects after a regrouped first-wave task
  started); UPDATED to seed a real `dag-task:FAST-BACKEND` artifact and assert
  the typed `rollback_overlay` rejects `dag_regroup_first_wave_task_started`
  and the typed overlay row STAYS `active` (no partial rolled-back projection).
- `test_rollback_writes_rolled_back_marker_before_g45_starts` →
  `…before_first_wave_starts`: KEEPS the legacy "the latest ACTIVE_KEY artifact
  has status rolled_back" assertion; ADDS the typed-row (`status='rolled_back'`)
  and typed-event (`dag_regroup_overlay_rolled_back`) assertions.
- NEW tests ADDED (genuine new behavior the rewire introduces):
  `test_activate_g45_g73_shape_keeps_legacy_projection_key` (a 74-group base
  DAG → the legacy `g45-g73` projection keys are preserved for that exact
  suffix — the legacy-compatibility assertion for the one shape the literal
  applies to); `test_activate_fails_closed_when_overlay_fails_validation`;
  `test_activate_rejects_when_boundary_checkpoint_missing`;
  `test_activate_re_activation_of_active_overlay_is_rejected` (the typed
  `activate_overlay` requires a `staged` row — re-activation raises
  `dag_regroup_overlay_not_staged`, the documented 09c-1 contract asserted by
  `test_regroup_overlay_activation.py:388`; this is NOT the doc-09 "Edge Cases"
  idempotent-accept — the 09e-1b CLI test matches the ACTUAL typed 09c-1
  behavior, it does not re-litigate 09c-1); `test_rollback_rejects_when_no_
  active_overlay`. The unused `_ArtifactsFromConn` helper class was removed
  (it bridged only the old fake-conn activate test). `_FakeConn` / `_FakePool`
  STAY — `test_status_reports_rollback_blocked_without_dag_group_45`
  (`command_status` is NOT rewired) and all 22 pure-function tests keep the
  fast `_FakeConn` path unchanged.

### doc-09 ambiguities + resolutions

1. **doc 09 says a `DerivedDAGArtifact` is "generated FROM the typed overlay"
   — there is no documented INVERSE.** doc 09 § "Refactoring Steps" item 1
   requires the CLI facade to delegate to the typed module, and items 7/8
   require `command_activate`/`command_rollback` to drive the typed
   `activate_overlay`/`rollback_overlay`, whose input is a typed `RegroupOverlay`
   — yet the CLI's draft input is still a `DerivedDAGArtifact`
   (`build_staged_regroup` / operator-authored). RESOLUTION: a deterministic
   `DerivedDAGArtifact` → `RegroupOverlay` conversion layer is the missing
   adapter. STATUS.md "Next safe action" part 1 names exactly this; the Slice
   09e-1 facade docstring already pre-recorded the split. The adapter is a pure
   transcription that adds NO checks — `validate_overlay` remains the sole gate.
2. **doc 09 § "Edge Cases" says "Existing active overlay for the same suffix:
   idempotently accept only if the overlay id and sha match" — but 09c-1's
   `activate_overlay` rejects `dag_regroup_overlay_not_staged` on a re-activation
   of an already-`active` overlay.** RESOLUTION: 09c-1's actual implemented +
   reviewed contract (`test_regroup_overlay_activation.py:388` asserts the
   `not_staged` rejection) is authoritative — 09e-1b wires the CLI onto the
   typed path AS BUILT and does not re-litigate the 09c-1 design. The 09e-1b
   CLI test (`test_activate_re_activation_of_active_overlay_is_rejected`)
   asserts the `not_staged` rejection, the genuine behavior. (The doc-09
   "idempotent accept" is a same-overlay re-stage-then-validate path; a closed
   loop here would be a 09c-scope change, out of 09e-1b's CLI-rewire scope —
   flagged for the 09e-2 six-vector review as a doc-vs-impl note, NOT a P1/P2:
   the `not_staged` rejection is strictly MORE conservative / fail-closed.)
3. **doc 09 item 3 keeps `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` AND adds the
   overlay validator at "CLI activation".** RESOLUTION (already pre-decided in
   STATUS.md part 4 + the 09b-2 env facts): `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_
   update` STAYS as the `DerivedDAGArtifact`-shaped sync validator (legacy
   resolver / `validate_candidate` / artifact-repair call sites);
   `command_activate` routes its CLI activation safety checks through the typed
   `validate_overlay`. `validate_overlay`'s `_coerce_typed_overlay` structurally
   rejects a bare `DerivedDAGArtifact`, so the two validators have disjoint
   inputs and cannot collide. UNTOUCHED — verified `_validate_derived_dag_
   artifact_update` has no edits.

- `git diff --check` — clean.

  **32 passed** (was 27; the rewired CLI suite — UPDATED, not weakened — runs
  against real Postgres at `localhost:5431`, reachable for this run).
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/test_regroup_overlay.py tests/workflows/
  develop/execution/test_regroup_overlay_{activation,validation,resolver,store}

### P1/P2-risk areas for a reviewer (Chunk A)

- The conversion layer derives `remaining_dependency_edges` + fingerprints from
  the BASE DAG, not the candidate's `dag.tasks`. This is CORRECT (the derived
  tasks ARE the base tasks re-waved, and `validate_overlay` steps 5/6 compare
  to the base DAG) and is the SAME thing `build_canonical_projection` does
  (`dag.tasks` copied byte-for-byte from the base). A reviewer should confirm
  that an operator who hand-authors a draft `DerivedDAGArtifact` whose
  `dag.tasks` MUTATE a task definition is still caught: yes — the converter's
  fingerprints come from the base DAG, the candidate's mutated task is dropped,
  so the converted overlay's fingerprints DON'T match a mutated task and step 5
  would only reject if the *converter's own* fingerprint disagreed — but since
  the converter always uses the base, a mutated `dag.tasks` is silently ignored
  rather than rejected. The mutation is harmless because the typed activation's
  `build_canonical_projection` ALSO copies base tasks byte-for-byte, so a
  mutated draft `dag.tasks` never reaches execution. Still — flagged as a P3
  watch for 09e-2: the converter could additionally assert the candidate's
  `dag.tasks` match the base task definitions, to fail-fast on a malformed
  hand-authored draft instead of silently normalizing it.
- `command_activate` acquires `store.acquire_feature_lock` for the explicit
  `validate_overlay`, releases it, then `activate_overlay` RE-acquires the same
  session-level `pg_advisory_lock` on the SAME connection. `pg_advisory_lock` is
  re-entrant within a session and the acquire/release/acquire is strictly
  sequential — no deadlock — but a reviewer should confirm there is no path
  where the release is skipped (it is in a `finally`).
- Chunk A does NOT yet close P3-D — a typed overlay at `group_idx_offset != 45`
  is reachable via the CLI now, but `_implement_dag`'s 3 call sites still gate
  the resolver on the legacy `dag-regroup-active:g45-g73` artifact. **Chunk B
  closes this.** Until Chunk B lands, a non-45 typed overlay activated via the
  rewired CLI is resolvable ONLY when the dispatch group probe happens to be
  reached — i.e. the gap P3-D describes is unchanged by Chunk A.

**Next safe action: Slice 09e-1b Chunk B** — the P3-D `_implement_dag`
call-site re-gating (`implementation.py:17049` / `:17152` / `:17196`), then the
hygiene gate + the targeted suites.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 09e-1b CHUNK B — P3-D `_implement_dag` call-site re-gating (COMPLETE)

Slice 09e-1b Chunk B delivers part 5 — the P3-D call-site re-gating in
`implementation.py` — completing all 6 parts of Slice 09e-1b.

### Part 5 — the P3-D `_implement_dag` regroup call-site re-gating

**The P3-D gap.** The 3 `_implement_dag` regroup call sites were gated on the
legacy `dag-regroup-active:g45-g73` artifact marker (`active_marker =
artifacts.get(DAG_REGROUP_ACTIVE_KEY)`, `DAG_REGROUP_ACTIVE_KEY =
"dag-regroup-active:g45-g73"`), so the typed `RegroupOverlayResolver` was in
practice reachable ONLY for an offset-45 overlay. Slice 09e-1b Chunk A's
`command_activate` rewire makes a typed overlay activatable at ANY
`group_idx_offset` — `activate_overlay` writes `dag-regroup-active:{slug}` where
the slug is derived from the overlay's offset/last-group (e.g.
`dag-regroup-active:g3-g4`), NOT the literal `g45-g73`. So a typed overlay at
offset != 45 never set `active_marker` and the 3 gates never fired → the
resolver was unreachable for it. Chunk B closes this.

**The re-gating (all in `_implement_dag`, `implementation.py`).** A new bounded
read-only helper `_typed_regroup_active_overlay_offset(runner, feature_id) ->
int | None` (added right after `_typed_regroup_active_overlay_probe`) returns
the `group_idx_offset` of the single `active` `execution_regroup_overlays` row
(the `uniq_regroup_overlay_active` partial unique index guarantees at most one),
or `None` when there is no active typed overlay / the Slice 09 modules or an
execution-control store are unavailable / a transient probe error (an error is
treated as "not present" so a DB blip cannot block dispatch — the typed
resolver's own fail-closed path still quiesces if a typed overlay genuinely
exists but cannot be proven safe). It is purely ADDITIVE — the existing
`_typed_regroup_active_overlay_probe` (bool) is left untouched (no-refactor
discipline).

`_implement_dag` now, right after fetching `active_marker`, computes three
values:

- `typed_regroup_offset` = `await _typed_regroup_active_overlay_offset(runner,
  feature.id)` — the active typed overlay's offset, or `None`.
- `regroup_offset` = `typed_regroup_offset if typed_regroup_offset is not None
  else DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP` — the EFFECTIVE offset.
- `regroup_in_play` = `bool(active_marker) or typed_regroup_offset is not None`
  — a legacy marker OR a typed overlay is present.

The 3 call sites are re-gated:

1. **Top-of-`_implement_dag`** (was `if active_marker:`) → `if regroup_in_play:`.
   The boundary-checkpoint probe `g45_checkpoint` (`dag-group:45`) is renamed
   `regroup_boundary_checkpoint` and reads `dag-group:{regroup_offset}`; the
   probe group is `regroup_offset + 1 if regroup_boundary_checkpoint else
   regroup_offset` (was `DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP +/0`).
2. **Resume-past-offset** (was `start_group > DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP and
   active_marker`) → `start_group > regroup_offset and regroup_in_play`.
3. **Dispatch-loop-reaches-offset** (was `group_idx == DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP
   and active_marker`) → `group_idx == regroup_offset and regroup_in_play`.

Also re-gated: the resume-freshness `accepted_dag_sha256s` line
`if regroup_overlay_applied and g_idx < DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP:` →
`g_idx < regroup_offset` — the prefix groups before the EFFECTIVE offset keep
the base-DAG sha (correct for an overlay whose prefix is `[0, regroup_offset)`).

**How non-overlay behavior is preserved EXACTLY** (verified by reading + by
the targeted suites):

- **No regroup at all** (no typed overlay, no legacy marker) →
  `typed_regroup_offset=None`, `regroup_in_play=False`, `regroup_offset=45`. All
  3 gates require `regroup_in_play` → SKIPPED. The `accepted_dag_sha256s` line
  requires `regroup_overlay_applied` (set ONLY inside a gate that did not run)
  → stays `[]`. Byte-for-byte identical to pre-09e-1b.
- **Legacy `g45-g73` marker, no typed overlay** → `typed_regroup_offset=None`
  (the probe found no `active` typed row), `regroup_in_play=True`,
  `regroup_offset=45`. Identical to pre-09e-1b (`active_marker` truthy, the
  effective offset is the legacy 45). The wrapper
  `_resolve_active_regroup_before_group_dispatch` runs the typed resolver
  first; with no typed row it returns `has_typed_overlay=False` and falls
  through to `_resolve_active_regroup_legacy_marker` — the UNCHANGED legacy
  G45-G73 path. `_resolve_active_regroup_legacy_marker` keeps its own
  `DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP` / `regroup_offset_mismatch` check (it is the
  no-typed-overlay fallback and is intentionally offset-45-scoped — a non-45
  typed overlay is handled by the typed resolver, never this path).
- **Typed overlay at any offset O** → `regroup_in_play=True`,
  `regroup_offset=O`. The 3 gates fire at the right group; the resolver's Step
  C (`if group_idx < group_idx_offset: no-typed-overlay`) and the call sites'
  group_idx now agree, so the typed overlay is resolvable at offset O.
- **Old install / no execution-control store** —
  `_typed_regroup_active_overlay_offset` returns `None` (the `RegroupOverlay
  Resolver is None or RegroupOverlayStore is None` guard, and the
  `_execution_control_store_for_runner(runner) is None` guard) → `regroup_
  offset` falls back to 45 → legacy behavior preserved.

`DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP` (45) / `DAG_REGROUP_ACTIVE_KEY` / `DAG_REGROUP_
CANONICAL_KEY` constants are NOT removed — they remain the legacy-marker
fallback and are still the constant used by `_resolve_active_regroup_legacy_
marker`. No monkeypatch target moved; the 3 call sites' `(effective_dag,
regroup_failure, observation)` tuple handling is unchanged.

### Part 6 (Chunk B subset) — `test_dag_regroup.py` P3-D verification tests

Two real-Postgres tests ADDED (the P3-D re-gating is a genuine
behavior-broadening change — a non-45 typed overlay was previously unreachable
from `_implement_dag` and is now reachable):

- `test_typed_overlay_at_non_45_offset_resolves` — activates a typed overlay at
  `group_idx_offset = 3` (a 5-group DAG, suffix [3,4]) via the rewired
  `command_activate`; asserts the slug is `g3-g4` (NOT `g45-g73`), the legacy
  `dag-regroup-active:g45-g73` artifact was NEVER written (the regroup is "in
  play" SOLELY because the typed overlay row exists), `_typed_regroup_active_
  overlay_offset` returns `3` (so `_implement_dag`'s gates would now fire:
  `regroup_in_play=True`, `regroup_offset=3`), and the typed resolver
  (`_resolve_active_regroup_before_group_dispatch`) applies the overlay at
  group 3 — the component the re-gated call sites reach. The root DAG is never
  overwritten (the effective order is the base prefix [0,3) + the derived
  suffix).
- `test_typed_overlay_offset_probe_none_without_overlay` — confirms
  `_typed_regroup_active_overlay_offset` returns `None` when no typed overlay is
  active, so `regroup_in_play` is `False` and `regroup_offset` falls back to
  the legacy `DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP` — the `_implement_dag` regroup gates are
  skipped exactly as before (the non-overlay-preservation proof).

The existing `_implement_dag` regroup tests in `test_dag_regroup.py`
(`test_implement_dag_dispatches_regrouped_first_wave`, `test_implement_dag_
resume_scans_expanded_regroup_checkpoints`) use a `_FakeConn`-style artifacts
store with NO execution-control store, so `_typed_regroup_active_overlay_offset`
returns `None` → `regroup_offset=45` → those tests still exercise the legacy
`dag-regroup-active:g45-g73` marker path unchanged (they pass — the legacy
path is byte-for-byte preserved).
