functionally correct — the open item is purely the endpoint path / doc
reconciliation.

## Slice 10b REMEDIATION + COMPLETE (2026-05-22) — the dashboard views (P2-10b-1 route reconciliation)

**Status: COMPLETE.** The independent Slice 10b REVIEW returned NOT CLEAN — 0
P1, 1 P2 (P2-10b-1), 2 P3. All three findings are remediated; targeted
verification is green; Slice 10b is now COMPLETE and STATUS.md is advanced to
Slice 10c.

**`git show HEAD` investigation (the P2 prerequisite).** Before touching the
route, confirmed the dict-based `/control-plane` endpoint is genuinely UNLANDED
same-bundle code with no shipped consumer:
- `git show HEAD:dashboard.py | grep -niE 'control.plane'` → ZERO lines (exit
  0, empty). The committed `dashboard.py` (HEAD = `298a744`) has NO
  control-plane code at all — HEAD `dashboard.py` is 3421 lines, the working
  tree is 4310. The entire control-plane surface (the dict-based
  `/control-plane` route `get_feature_control_plane`, `_control_plane_summary`,
  `_compact_control_plane`, the `_control_plane_*` dict-builder helper block,
  AND the Slice-10b typed wiring) is uncommitted same-bundle work.
- So the dict-based `/control-plane` endpoint is NOT a shipped production
  endpoint needing compatibility preservation — it is exploratory
  pre-Slice-10b scaffolding from an earlier uncommitted pass. The
  `_control_plane_*` dict-builder block (`_control_plane_fetch` … at
  `dashboard.py` ~1594+) was further confirmed to be ALREADY-DEAD code: every
  function in it is defined but never called (only internal cross-calls);
  `_control_plane_fetch` has zero callers. This is the exploratory unlanded
  code the reviewer described.
- The expected outcome therefore holds: "the typed snapshot IS
  `/control-plane`." No shipped/depended-on consumer exists, so no
  keep-both / versioning reconciliation was needed.

**P2-10b-1 — the route + embedded-object-key reconciliation (the mandatory
fix).** doc 10 § "Refactoring Steps" step 6 names the typed endpoint
`/api/feature/{feature_id}/control-plane`; Slice 10 lands as ONE ATOMIC feature
(doc 10 step 11 + § "Rollout/Rollback Notes") and no later seam owns a
`/control-plane` cutover. Remediation made the typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot`
snapshot THE `/control-plane` endpoint:
- `dashboard.py` — the route `GET /api/feature/{feature_id}/control-plane`
  (`get_feature_control_plane`): its BODY is now the typed implementation — it
  builds the bounded `ControlPlaneSnapshotQuery`, calls
  `_typed_control_plane_snapshot` (the Slice-10a `get_control_plane_snapshot`
  builder), serializes the typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot` via `model_dump`, ETag
  `"control-plane:{snapshot_version}"`, `If-None-Match` → 304, 404 for an
  unknown feature. The separate additive `/control-plane/typed` route is
  DELETED — there is one typed control-plane endpoint, at `/control-plane`, per
  doc 10 step 6. The dict-based endpoint is superseded/replaced (not kept).
- `dashboard.py` — the embedded object: the `/api/feature/{id}` response (both
  the develop dict and `_assemble_bugflow_response`) now embeds the SINGLE
  typed `control_plane` object (`_compact_typed_control_plane` of the typed
  snapshot). The dict-based embedded object and the separate
  `control_plane_typed` key are superseded — doc 10's intent is one typed
  `control_plane`. `_assemble_bugflow_response` lost its now-unused
  `control_plane_typed` kwarg.
- `dashboard.py` — the now-dead legacy helpers removed AS A DIRECT CONSEQUENCE
  of the reconciliation: `_control_plane_summary`,
  `_control_plane_budget_overrides`, `_compact_control_plane`, and the
  `fetch_control_plane_snapshot` import (all became unreferenced once the route
  + embedded object switched to the typed snapshot). SCOPED: the store-level
  `fetch_control_plane_snapshot` in `execution_control/store.py` is UNTOUCHED —
  the supervisor (`supervisor/evidence.py` / `mcp_server.py` / `classifier.py`)
  still consumes it directly; the supervisor's migration to the typed snapshot
  is the later Slice-10 classifier/evidence seam, correctly out of 10b scope.
  The pre-existing-already-dead `_control_plane_*` dict-builder block (~1594+)
  was left alone — it is pre-existing dead scaffolding unrelated to the P2
  mandate; removing it would balloon the diff and is a separate cleanup. The 6
  `CONTROL_PLANE_*_LIMIT` env knobs are kept (2 are still referenced by that
  pre-existing block; removing them would NameError it).
- `/api/feature/{id}` ETag: the legacy `control_plane_snapshot_version`
  component (sourced from the deleted `_control_plane_summary`) is dropped; the
  typed `typed_control_plane_version` component (the
  `compute_typed_snapshot_version` digest) remains as the final ETag component
  — a control-plane-only change still flips the ETag.

**P2-10b-1 — how the dict-based endpoint's tests were updated (a real
behavior change, NOT a weakening).** Four tests in `tests/test_dashboard_
bugflow.py` were tightly coupled to the dict-based shape:
- `test_get_feature_control_plane_uses_live_connection_and_shows_post_dag_
  gates` — REWRITTEN. It tests `get_feature`. Its `_Conn` now serves the
  typed-snapshot SQL (the `MAX(...) AS max_id` version cursors + the typed
  `execution_journal_rows` read). EVERY still-applicable assertion is kept: the
  embed uses the LIVE connection (`control_plane_fetch_count >= 2`), and the
  three post-DAG gate assertions (`gates["source-push"/"implementation-report"/
  "notify"]`) are byte-identical. The control-plane assertions are updated to
  the typed compact shape (`schema=="typed"`, 64-char `snapshot_version`,
  `source=="typed"`, `counts.active_attempts==1`,
  `active_attempts[0].attempt_id==41`) + a new no-body assertion (`'"value"'
  not in json.dumps(control_plane)`). No assertion was dropped.
- `test_get_feature_legacy_schema_falls_back_when_typed_tables_are_absent` —
  assertions UNCHANGED (`control_plane.source=="legacy_fallback"`,
  `degraded is True`) — they still hold because the Slice-10a builder degrades
  every section on a missing-table error and itself reports
  `source="legacy_fallback"`/`degraded=True`. Only a clarifying comment added.
- `test_get_feature_etag_changes_for_material_sandbox_lease_updates` —
  REWRITTEN so the lease update genuinely advances the typed `sandbox_leases`
  version cursor (`MAX(updated_at)` moves `now`→`later` — exactly what a real
  lease update does). The ETag-changes-on-a-control-plane-only-update
  assertion (the test's whole point) is PRESERVED and now exercises the typed
  ETag path. `control_plane.snapshot_version` assertion kept.
- `test_control_plane_endpoint_returns_bounded_typed_snapshot_without_artifact_
  bodies` — REWRITTEN. It directly tests `get_feature_control_plane` (the
  `/control-plane` route whose body changed dict→typed). The dict-only
  assertions (`query.artifact_bodies`, dict `budget`/`truncation`,
  `verification_graph_nodes`, `checkpoint_refs`, `source=="mixed"`) had NO
  shipped consumer and no typed equivalent — the typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot`
  carries its OWN bounded/no-body guarantees. The rewrite asserts the typed
  contract (`feature_id`, 64-char `snapshot_version`, `source=="typed"`, ETag
  `"control-plane:{version}"`, typed rows `active_attempts`/`latest_failures`/
  `merge_queue`/`gates`/`checkpoints`, `EvidenceRef`-only detail refs) AND
  KEEPS the safety assertions the original had: ZERO artifact-body read
  (`conn.artifact_value_reads == []`), no `"value"`/`"content"` body field.
  This is updating a test for a genuine behavior change — the subject endpoint
  changed shape — not a weakening.
- The 8 Slice-10b typed tests were updated for the rename: every
  `get_feature_control_plane_typed` call → `get_feature_control_plane`, the
  ETag prefix `"control-plane-typed:"` → `"control-plane:"`, and the embedded
  key `payload["control_plane_typed"]` → `payload["control_plane"]`. No
  assertion weakened.
- One NEW test added — `test_control_plane_endpoint_rejects_negative_group_idx`
  (the P3-10b-2 coverage). Net: `tests/test_dashboard_bugflow.py` is 36 tests
  (was 35 — the dict-based test 4 rewritten in place + 1 new `group_idx` test).

**P3-10b-1 — graceful-degrade wrapper moved onto the LIVE path; dead code
removed.** `_typed_control_plane_version` (the dead function the
graceful-degrade `try/except` had landed on) is DELETED. The graceful-degrade
guard is moved onto the LIVE `_typed_control_plane_snapshot` call: it now wraps
`get_control_plane_snapshot` in `try/except` and on any UNEXPECTED
snapshot-builder failure returns a shape-valid degraded snapshot (built via the
typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot` model itself — `source="legacy_fallback"`,
`degraded=True`, `degradation_reasons=[...]`, `snapshot_version=""`) via the
new `_degraded_typed_control_plane` helper. So both the `/control-plane` route
AND the `/api/feature/{id}` ETag path degrade gracefully — neither 500s on a
control-plane read. (The Slice-10a builder's own per-section degrade contract
remains the first-line net; this wrapper is defense-in-depth on the live path.)

**P3-10b-2 — `ge=0` validation restored.** The `/control-plane` route now
rejects a negative `group_idx` with a `422` (`if group_idx is not None and
group_idx < 0: raise HTTPException(422, ...)`) — an explicit route-body check
rather than a FastAPI `Query(..., ge=0)` default (the route is called directly
by tests; a `Query` sentinel default would break a direct call that omits
`group_idx`). Clean, low-risk, gives a real 422; covered by the new
`test_control_plane_endpoint_rejects_negative_group_idx`.

**Constraints honoured.** The dashboard's typed path still reads ONLY the
Slice-10a bounded snapshot — `_typed_control_plane_snapshot` →
`get_control_plane_snapshot` → `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot`, which
issues ZERO `FROM artifacts` reads; no new unbounded read, no artifact-body
read. The store-level `fetch_control_plane_snapshot` and the supervisor are
untouched (later Slice-10 seam). Fail-fast / no silent degradation: a degraded
snapshot always carries `degradation_reasons` (the model invariant enforces
it). The later Slice-10 seams (read-only supervisor policy, classifier
mapping, Slack dedupe) were NOT touched.

**Test results (all GREEN; no test weakened).**

- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_snapshots_store.py

- `python -m pytest tests/ -k "dashboard or control_plane or public_dashboard"

- `python -m pytest tests/test_execution_control_store.py tests/supervisor -q

  `fetch_control_plane_snapshot` + the supervisor are unaffected).
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q -p no:randomly` →

  models).

**Hygiene.** `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` →
clean; `git diff --check` → clean.

**Slice 10b COMPLETE.** P2-10b-1 + P3-10b-1 + P3-10b-2 all remediated; the
typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot` is THE `/api/feature/{id}/control-plane` endpoint
per doc 10 step 6 and the single embedded `control_plane` object; targeted
verification green; no test weakened. STATUS.md is advanced to Slice 10c (the
read-only supervisor contract + the typed classifier mapping). Carried P3-10a-1
flows through unchanged (the dashboard only DISPLAYS budget rows; the
typed-classifier sub-slice — 10c — owns wiring a real typed budget source).

## Slice 10c-1 IMPLEMENTATION (2026-05-22) — the mechanical read-only supervisor contract

**Split decision.** Slice 10c (doc 10 § "Read-Only And Audit Exception Policy"
step 8 + § "Supervisor Classifier Mapping" step 5) was SPLIT — STATUS.md
explicitly flagged it as possibly needing exactly this split, and the two seams
are independently verifiable:

- **10c-1 (THIS entry) — the read-only mechanical enforcement.** Delivered +
  fully verified this iteration: `ActionPolicy` + MCP-service-construction
  read-only enforcement, mechanical + fail-closed.
- **10c-2 (DEFERRED, next iteration) — the typed classifier mapping.** The
  typed-failure/route classifier mapping (typed-primary / legacy-fallback
  gating, the 27-row coverage table for every Slice-07 `FailureClass` + the
  static coverage test, the deterministic-class-never-escalates rule) AND the
  real typed budget source resolving the carried P3-10a-1.

Why split here: `classifier.py` is 2433 lines on the LIVE failure-routing path;
the typed mapping is a genuine behavior change (a 27-row table, typed-primary
gating, the budget-source store SELECT change). 10c-1 is a self-contained
mechanically-verifiable SAFETY contract — a smaller fully-verified chunk over a
large shaky one (loop discipline: one verified sub-deliverable per iteration).
This is a behavior-additive seam (no working code path rewritten), but the
read-only mutation-safety vector is exactly a doc-10 key review vector, so 10c-1
gets its own independent review pass (NOT deferred to the slice-end loop).

**What was built.** A NEW leaf module `src/iriai_build_v2/supervisor/read_only.py`
(229 lines, additive — imports only `typing`) plus three wiring edits:

1. **The execution-authority writer surface, split + complete.**
   `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` (28 names) — every writer EXCLUSIVE to
   `ExecutionControlStore` (typed journal rows + attempt state transitions;
   typed failures / route decisions; verification-graph gate evidence;
   execution-artifact projection writes / checkpoints; task contracts;
   workspace authority / snapshots; sandbox leases / runtime bindings). Each is
   the exact doc-10 § "Read-Only And Audit Exception Policy" mutation class.
   `FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS` (7 names) — the *dual-use*
   feature/artifact-store writers (`create`/`transition_phase`/`update_metadata`
   /`log_event`/`put`/`write_artifact_bytes`/`delete`): the store CLASS
   legitimately carries them (the MCP service needs `get_feature` /
   `list_event_summaries` from the same `PostgresFeatureStore`), so they are
   tracked separately and enforced at the CALL site, not by method absence.
   `EXECUTION_AUTHORITY_WRITER_METHODS` = the union (35) — the static coverage
   test asserts the union equals the two subsets and that the subsets are
   disjoint, so a new writer is forced into the contract.

2. **The mechanical fail-closed construction guard.**
   `assert_no_control_plane_writer(handle, role=...)` inspects the handle
   ITSELF (not a runtime mode flag) and raises `ReadOnlySupervisorViolation`
   (fail-closed, never degraded) if it exposes any `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS`
   member — because those writers are exclusive to `ExecutionControlStore`, a
   handle exposing one IS the execution-authority store. `assert_read_only_
   supervisor_handles(feature_store=, artifact_store=, execution_control_store=,
   extra_handles=)` is the single service-construction guard: the
   `execution_control_store` slot must be `None` (an explicit, named slot
   proving the writer store is STRUCTURALLY ABSENT, not merely unused).

3. **The mechanically fail-closed read-only path.** This is the
   "writer-path-is-absent-not-runtime-checked" requirement:
   - `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.__init__` now calls
     `self.assert_read_only()` (which calls `assert_read_only_supervisor_
     handles` with `execution_control_store=None`) EAGERLY for caller-supplied
     stores; `_ensure_stores()` RE-asserts after the lazily-created
     `PostgresFeatureStore`/`PostgresArtifactStore` are built. The MCP service
     has no slot for an `ExecutionControlStore` — the execution-authority
     writer is structurally absent; the guard makes that absence an asserted
     contract. Wiring the service with an `ExecutionControlStore` in EITHER
     store slot raises `ReadOnlySupervisorViolation` at construction, BEFORE any
     tool call / runtime parameter is inspected.
   - `ActionPolicy.__init__` gained an `execution_authority` parameter
     (default `None`) and calls `assert_no_control_plane_writer` on it at
     construction. doc 10 says even GUARDED bridge actions "cannot mutate typed
     execution state or product files" — so a control-plane writer is NEVER
     valid on an `ActionPolicy`, in read-only OR guarded mode; the guard fails
     closed for both. The existing `maybe_restart` / `maybe_kill_stale_codex`
     are byte-for-byte UNTOUCHED.

4. **The fail-closed denied-write path + the blocked-action audit row.**
   - `ActionPolicy.guard_execution_write(packet, action=, reason=)` — a NEW
     method (existing methods untouched): the fail-closed handler. It NEVER
     mutates — it records a `SupervisorActionStatus.BLOCKED` `Supervisor
     ActionRecord` via the existing `_write` (`action_key(...):blocked` →
     `supervisor-action:*`, a supervisor-owned audit key) and returns it. This
     is the doc-10 "Denied writes fail closed and produce a blocked action
     audit row rather than a best-effort mutation" deterministic deny path.
   - `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink(artifact_store)` — a mechanical wrapper around
     an `ArtifactStore` implementing the `ArtifactSink` Protocol: `put` to a
     `SUPERVISOR_OWNED_AUDIT_KEY_PREFIXES`-prefixed key passes through to the
     wrapped store (an ALLOWED append-only audit write); `put` to ANY other key
     raises `BlockedExecutionWrite` and performs NO mutation. The
     supervisor-owned prefixes: `supervisor-observation:` /
     `supervisor-decision:` / `supervisor-agent-assessment:` /
     `supervisor-thread-context:` / `supervisor-action:`, plus the
     pre-registered `supervisor-digest:` / `supervisor-digest-audit:` /
     `supervisor-digest-state:` for the later Slice-10 Slack-dedupe sub-slice
     (doc 10: those are supervisor-owned audit state). The wrapper's mutating
     `write_artifact_bytes`/`delete` are not re-exposed.

**How the read-only path is mechanically fail-closed.** doc 10 requires the
deny to be STRUCTURAL ("absent or denied before runtime parameters are
inspected"), not a runtime `self.mode` check:
- The MCP evidence service has no `ExecutionControlStore` slot — the
  execution-authority writer is *structurally absent*; `assert_read_only()`
  asserts that absence at construction AND after lazy store creation.
- `ActionPolicy` has no execution-authority writer field that is ever non-None
  for a correct wiring; `assert_no_control_plane_writer` rejects one at
  construction.
- `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` denies a non-audit `put` by RAISING — the wrapped
  store is never reached, the mutation never happens (verified: the test
  asserts the wrapped store's `puts` list is empty after a denied `put`).
- The deny is FAIL-CLOSED both ways: a denied write either never has a writer
  to call (absent) or raises (`BlockedExecutionWrite`); the
  `guard_execution_write` handler then writes a `BLOCKED` audit row and the
  caller gets a `SupervisorActionRecord`, never a silent best-effort mutation.

**Interfaces / citations verified at file:line.**
- `ActionPolicy` — `supervisor/actions.py:28` (constructor extended in place;
  `maybe_restart` `:42`+ / `maybe_kill_stale_codex` `:116`+ untouched).
- `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService` — `supervisor/mcp_server.py:93` class /
  `:116` `__init__` / `:136` `_ensure_stores` (verified the service holds
  `feature_store`/`artifact_store` only — NO `ExecutionControlStore`; every
  `@mcp.tool` is read/query; `readonly_sql` already disabled).
- Slice-07 canonical `FailureClass` — `workflows/develop/execution/
  failure_router.py:14` (the `Literal` 27-class taxonomy) + `FAILURE_CLASSES`
  `:132` (the matching tuple). Used by the 10c-2 mapping; cited here so the
  10c-2 coverage test keys against the verified enum.
- `ExecutionControlStore` writer surface — `execution_control/store.py`:
  `record` `:2175`, `record_success` `:2201`, `start_dispatch_attempt`
  `:2222`, `record_prompt_context` `:2250`, `record_runtime_invocation`

  `record_runtime_failure` `:2409`, `record_verification_graph_node` `:2464`,
  `record_verification_graph_projection` `:2524`, `finish_dispatch_attempt`
  `:2899`, `project_task_result_from_attempt` `:2967`, `put_task_contract`
  `:3077`, `record_patch_summary` `:3154`, `record_contract_verdict` `:3170`,
  `project_task_result` `:3222`, `project_verify_result` `:3225`,
  `project_commit_failure` `:3228`, `project_group_checkpoint` `:3234`,
  `project_regroup_overlay` `:3240`, `project_regroup_active` `:3243`,
  `record_workspace_registry` `:3313`, `record_workspace_preflight` `:3349`,
  `record_workspace_snapshot` `:3387`, `allocate_sandbox_lease` `:3467`,
  `record_sandbox_repo_binding` `:3559`, `record_runtime_workspace_binding`
  `:3572`, `update_sandbox_lease` `:3607`. A new test
  (`test_real_execution_control_store_exposes_every_control_plane_writer`)
  binds `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` to the live class — a rename fails it.
- `FeatureStore` writers — `storage/features.py`: `create` `:30`,
  `transition_phase` `:47`, `log_event` `:55`, `update_metadata` `:98`.
- `ArtifactStore` writers — `storage/artifacts.py`: `put` `:386`,
  `write_artifact_bytes` `:406`, `delete` `:437`.
- Supervisor-owned audit key prefixes — `supervisor/models.py`:
  `observation_key` `:480`, `decision_key` `:499`, `assessment_key` `:518`,
  `thread_context_prefix` `:526`, `action_key` `:550`.
- The MCP-service construction-policy mandate — doc 10 § "Refactoring Steps"
  step 8 ("Enforce read-only policy in `ActionPolicy` and MCP service
  construction").

**doc-10 ambiguities + resolutions.**
- *Ambiguity:* doc 10 § "Read-Only And Audit Exception Policy" says the
  supervisor "receives only read/query handles plus supervisor-owned audit,
  dedupe, and display outbox writers" and that "any MCP tool or action policy
  path that would call an execution-authority writer is absent or denied" — but
  the MCP service genuinely holds `PostgresFeatureStore`/`PostgresArtifactStore`,
  which are full read/WRITE store classes (they carry `transition_phase`,
  `put`, `delete`). A naive "no writer methods on any held handle" assertion
  would wrongly reject the legitimate read surface.
  *Resolution:* the doc-10 mutation surface was SPLIT. The
  *execution-authority* writers doc 10 enumerates ("execution artifact
  projection writes, checkpoints, merge queue rows, typed failures, retry
  budgets, task contracts, workspace snapshots, attempt state transitions")
  live ENTIRELY on `ExecutionControlStore` — `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS`.
  The construction guard asserts no held handle IS that store (those names are
  exclusive to it). The dual-use feature/artifact writers
  (`FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS`) are enforced at the CALL site: the MCP
  service exposes no write tool (verified mechanically by
  `test_mcp_service_exposes_no_write_tool`), and the artifact `put` is denied
  by `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`. This is the only interpretation that keeps
  the bounded read surface intact while making the execution-authority writer
  structurally absent.
- *Ambiguity:* doc 10 § "Allowed writes" lists "Public dashboard display
  outbox events" — but the public-outbox projection is a later Slice-10 seam
  (step 9), not 10c. *Resolution:* 10c-1 pre-registers the supervisor-owned
  audit key prefixes including the digest ones, and the writer-surface split
  cleanly leaves the outbox projection (a store-side write keyed by
  `(feature_id, snapshot_version)`) outside the supervisor's `ActionPolicy` /
  MCP handle set entirely; step 9 wires the outbox without reopening 10c-1.
- *Ambiguity:* doc 10 § "Allowed writes" permits GUARDED-mode bridge actions.
  *Resolution:* the existing `maybe_restart` / `maybe_kill_stale_codex` guarded
  paths are UNTOUCHED (they already gate on `SupervisorMode.GUARDED` and write
  planned/blocked/completed audit rows). The new `execution_authority` guard is
  orthogonal: even in guarded mode an `ExecutionControlStore` writer is
  rejected — doc 10 says guarded actions "still cannot mutate typed execution
  state or product files".

**Tests added** — `tests/supervisor/test_read_only.py` (18 tests, all green):
- `test_execution_authority_surface_is_the_union_of_the_two_subsets` — the
  static coverage assertion: union = control-plane ∪ timeline, subsets
  disjoint, every doc-10 mutation class represented.
- `test_real_execution_control_store_exposes_every_control_plane_writer` —
  binds `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` to the live `ExecutionControlStore`.
- `test_guard_fails_closed_when_handle_is_the_execution_control_store` /
  `test_guard_passes_for_read_only_stores_and_for_none` — the construction
  guard fails closed on the execution-authority store, passes for read
  surfaces + `None`.
- `test_assert_read_only_handles_rejects_an_execution_control_store` /
  `test_assert_read_only_handles_passes_for_read_surfaces_only` — the
  service-construction guard, incl. a control-plane writer sneaking into a
  feature/artifact slot or an extra handle.
- `test_mcp_service_construction_is_read_only_for_supplied_stores` /
  `test_mcp_service_construction_fails_closed_on_control_plane_store` /
  `test_mcp_service_exposes_no_write_tool` — MCP-service construction
  enforcement (doc 10 § "Tests": "service construction in read-only mode
  exposes no write-capable execution store handles ... before they reach the
  store").
- `test_supervisor_owned_audit_keys_are_recognized` /
  `test_non_supervisor_keys_are_not_audit_keys` — the audit-key classifier.
- `test_audit_sink_allows_supervisor_owned_audit_put` /
  `test_audit_sink_denies_execution_authority_put_and_does_not_mutate` — the
  audit sink allows a supervisor-owned audit `put`, denies everything else
  FAIL-CLOSED (the wrapped store is verified untouched after a denied put).
- `test_action_policy_construction_fails_closed_on_execution_authority_handle`
  / `test_action_policy_construction_without_writer_handle_succeeds` — the
  `ActionPolicy` writer-handle guard (read-only AND guarded).
- `test_guard_execution_write_blocks_and_writes_blocked_audit_row` — doc 10 §
  "Tests": "Read-only action policy blocks execution/control-plane mutation and
  writes a blocked audit record": asserts the `BLOCKED` status + the
  `supervisor-action:8ac124d6:200:project_group_checkpoint:blocked` audit row
  is persisted to the supervisor-owned sink.
- `test_guard_execution_write_through_audit_sink_writes_blocked_row` —
  end-to-end: a denied write via `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` → caught
  `BlockedExecutionWrite` → `guard_execution_write` records the blocked row;
  the wrapped store recorded ONLY the supervisor-owned row, never the denied
  `dag-task:*` write.
- `test_guard_execution_write_with_no_sink_still_fails_closed` — the guard
  denies even without a sink.

**Test results.**
- `python -m pytest tests/supervisor/test_read_only.py -q -p no:randomly` →
  **18 passed**.
- `python -m pytest tests/supervisor/ -q -p no:randomly` → **237 passed**
  (219 pre-existing + 18 new — the supervisor suite stays GREEN; the existing
  `ActionPolicy(...)` constructions in `slack.py` + the 6 `test_tools.py` MCP
  service constructions are unaffected, since none passes an
  `ExecutionControlStore` and none passes `execution_authority`).
- Hygiene: `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` → clean;
  `git diff --check` → clean.
- No real-Postgres test was involved in 10c-1 (the read-only contract is pure
  in-process wiring); the Slice-10a/10b store tests are unaffected and were not
  re-run for this chunk.

**P1/P2-risk areas for a reviewer (10c-1).**
- P2-watch: the writer-surface split is the load-bearing doc-10 interpretation.
  `test_real_execution_control_store_exposes_every_control_plane_writer` binds
  `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` to the live class so a RENAME is caught — but a
  NEWLY-ADDED `ExecutionControlStore` writer is NOT auto-detected (the test
  asserts the named set is a subset of the class, not the reverse). A reviewer
  should confirm the 28 names are the complete current writer set and consider
  whether the 10c-2 / slice-end review should add the reverse assertion.
- P2-watch: `FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS` enforcement is at the CALL site
  (no write tool + the audit sink), not by handle absence — correct for the
  dual-use stores, but it means a future code path that calls
  `feature_store.transition_phase()` directly would NOT be caught by the
  construction guard. The MCP service exposes no such path today (asserted by
  `test_mcp_service_exposes_no_write_tool`); a reviewer should confirm no other
  read-only-supervisor entry point reaches a feature-timeline writer.
- P3: `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` is an opt-in wrapper — a read-only deployment
  must choose to hand it to `ActionPolicy` as the `artifact_sink`. 10c-1 ships
  the mechanism + the guard; the slice-end atomic-landing seam (doc 10 step 11)
  / the startup assertions (step 10) should confirm the production read-only
  wiring actually uses it. Not a 10c-1 defect (the `ActionPolicy` audit writes
  always target `supervisor-action:*` keys, so even the un-wrapped sink only
  writes supervisor-owned keys), but worth a landing-seam check.
- Not a regression risk: `maybe_restart` / `maybe_kill_stale_codex` are
  byte-for-byte unchanged; `guard_execution_write` is purely additive; the MCP
  guard is a construction-time assertion with no behavior change for a
  correctly-wired service.

**Next safe action.** Slice 10c-2 — the typed `SupervisorDigest` classifier
mapping (doc 10 § "Supervisor Classifier Mapping"): make typed failure/route
decisions PRIMARY in `SupervisorClassifier.classify` (`supervisor/classifier.py
:24`); keep the legacy artifact classifiers as FALLBACK gated behind
`evidence_mode != "typed"`; map every canonical Slice-07 `FailureClass`
(`failure_router.py:14`, 27 classes) to exactly one typed mapping row + add the
static classifier-coverage test; enforce the deterministic-class-never-escalates
rule; and wire the REAL typed budget source resolving the carried P3-10a-1
(`store.py:2127` `_typed_retry_budgets` `budget_remaining=0` placeholder → read
the real budget from the `route_decision`/`retry_budget` payload already on the
`runtime_failure_context` evidence_nodes rows). 10c-2 gets its own independent
review (failure-routing correctness — deterministic classes must not escalate
to operator).

## Slice 10c-1 REVIEW (2026-05-22) — the mechanical read-only supervisor contract

**Reviewer summary.** An independent REVIEW+FINALIZE subagent reviewed the
working-tree state of the Slice 10c-1 sub-deliverable (the mechanical read-only
supervisor enforcement; the typed classifier mapping + the real budget source
are deferred to 10c-2) against doc 10 § "Read-Only And Audit Exception Policy"
(step 8) — the SPEC — and the six review vectors in the review brief. Files
reviewed: NEW `src/iriai_build_v2/supervisor/read_only.py`; MODIFIED
`supervisor/mcp_server.py` (`SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.__init__` /
`_ensure_stores`); MODIFIED `supervisor/actions.py` (`ActionPolicy` +
`guard_execution_write`); NEW `tests/supervisor/test_read_only.py` (18 tests).
**VERDICT: CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 1 new P3 (P3-10c-1, a stale prose count — the
load-bearing frozensets are correct).** All three split-out 10c-1 seams hold;
Slice 10c-1 is COMPLETE.

**Vector 1 — mechanical, structural enforcement (KEY VECTOR): PASS / CLEAN.**
doc 10 step 8 requires read-only to be enforced MECHANICALLY — a writer path
ABSENT or DENIED *before runtime parameters are inspected*. Confirmed:
- The supervisor MCP evidence service genuinely has NO `ExecutionControlStore`
  writer handle. `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.__init__`
  (`supervisor/mcp_server.py:116`) holds only `feature_store` / `artifact_store`
  (`:127-128`) and calls `self.assert_read_only()` (`:143`) at the END of
  `__init__` — at CONSTRUCTION, before any `@mcp.tool` call. `assert_read_only`
  (`:145`) passes `execution_control_store=None` explicitly (`:162`) — a named
  slot proving the writer store is *structurally absent*, not merely unused.
  `_ensure_stores` (`:165`) RE-asserts (`:175`) after the lazily-created
  `PostgresFeatureStore` / `PostgresArtifactStore` are built. Wiring the service
  with an `ExecutionControlStore` in EITHER store slot raises
  `ReadOnlySupervisorViolation` at construction (verified by
  `test_mcp_service_construction_fails_closed_on_control_plane_store`).
- `ActionPolicy.__init__` (`supervisor/actions.py:30`) calls
  `assert_no_control_plane_writer(execution_authority, ...)` (`:50`) as the
  FIRST statement of the constructor — before `self.mode` is even set. The
  guard inspects the handle ITSELF (`_control_plane_writers_present`,
  `read_only.py:246` — `getattr` + `callable`), not a runtime mode flag. An
  execution-authority writer is rejected at construction in read-only AND
  guarded mode (`test_action_policy_construction_fails_closed_on_execution_
  authority_handle`).
- The enforcement is genuinely structural, NOT a runtime-parameter check: the
  MCP service has no slot for the writer store; `ActionPolicy.execution_
  authority` is always `None` for a correct wiring (the guard guarantees a
  non-None value is never a control-plane writer); `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`
  denies by RAISING before the wrapped store is reached. No reviewed path makes
  read-only depend on inspecting a runtime parameter.

**Vector 2 — fail-closed + blocked-action audit row: PASS / CLEAN.**
- `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink.put` (`read_only.py:363`) to a non-supervisor key
  raises `BlockedExecutionWrite` BEFORE `await self._artifact_store.put(...)` —
  the wrapped store is genuinely untouched (verified by
  `test_audit_sink_denies_execution_authority_put_and_does_not_mutate`: the
  wrapped store's `puts` list is `[]` after a denied put).
- `ActionPolicy.guard_execution_write` (`actions.py:62`) NEVER mutates: it
  builds a `SupervisorActionRecord` with `status=SupervisorActionStatus.BLOCKED`
  (`:94`) and persists it via the existing `_write(record, "blocked")`
  (`:101`). `_write` (`:271`) writes through `action_key(...)` which always
  emits a `supervisor-action:` prefix (`models.py:550`) — a supervisor-owned
  audit key. So even when `_write` runs through a `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`,
  the blocked-action row is ALLOWED (not self-blocked), and the end-to-end
  denied-write path (`test_guard_execution_write_through_audit_sink_writes_
  blocked_row`) leaves the wrapped store with ONLY the `supervisor-action:*`
  row — never the denied `dag-task:*` write. No reviewed path partially mutates
  on a denied write.

**Vector 3 — the mutation-surface split + `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS`
completeness: PASS / CLEAN (the split is sound and doc-10-conformant).**
The implementer split the doc-10 mutation surface into
`CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` (execution-authority, exclusive to
`ExecutionControlStore`, asserted ABSENT by the construction guard) and
`FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS` (the dual-use feature/artifact writers,
call-site-enforced). The split is the only interpretation that keeps the
bounded read surface intact while making the execution-authority writer
structurally absent — doc 10's enumerated execution-authority mutation classes
("execution artifact projection writes, checkpoints, merge queue rows, typed
failures, retry budgets, task contracts, workspace snapshots, attempt state
transitions") DO live entirely on `ExecutionControlStore`. The split leaves NO
execution-authority writer reachable by the supervisor: every such writer is in
`CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS`, and the guard rejects any handle exposing one.
COMPLETENESS — verified independently: a fresh enumeration of every PUBLIC
method on `ExecutionControlStore` (`execution_control/store.py:2172-3770`)
yields 38 public methods = 28 writers + 10 readers (`get_*` / `list_*` /
`read_*`). `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` has exactly 28 members, every one a
real `ExecutionControlStore` writer, and the 10 omitted public methods are all
genuine readers — so `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` IS the complete current
execution-authority writer set. (`test_real_execution_control_store_exposes_
every_control_plane_writer` re-confirms each name resolves on the live class.)

**Verdict on the implementer's P2-watch (a)** — the coverage test asserts the
named set ⊆ the class, not the reverse, so a newly-ADDED `ExecutionControlStore`
writer is not auto-flagged: judged **an acceptable P3, NOT a P2.** The contract
is COMPLETE today (28 = 28, verified); the one-directional assertion is a
future-maintenance gap, not a present correctness defect — and a new writer
would still be caught by `test_execution_authority_surface_is_the_union_of_the_
two_subsets` only if a developer also adds it to a subset, which is exactly the
gap. A reverse/bidirectional assertion (`CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` plus the
`get_*`/`list_*`/`read_*` readers must EQUAL the public surface) is a sound
hardening for the 10c-2 / slice-end review, but its absence does not make 10c-1
unsafe — recorded as P3-10c-2 (non-blocking).

**Verdict on the implementer's P2-watch (b)** — `FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_
METHODS` enforcement is call-site, not handle-absence: judged **SOUND, an
acceptable P3-watch, NOT a P2.** Independently confirmed via
`grep -rn "transition_phase|log_event|update_metadata|feature_store.create|
.delete(" src/iriai_build_v2/supervisor/` — there is NO `transition_phase` /
`log_event` / `update_metadata` / `feature_store.create` / `delete` call
anywhere under `supervisor/`. The dual-use feature/artifact writers have ZERO
supervisor caller today; `test_mcp_service_exposes_no_write_tool` mechanically
asserts every public MCP service method is a `get_`/`list_`/`probe_`/`readonly_`
/`database_`/`assert_` verb. Call-site enforcement is correct for a dual-use
store class (a handle-absence assertion would wrongly reject the legitimate
read surface). The residual risk — a *future* code path calling
`feature_store.transition_phase()` directly would bypass the construction guard
— is real but is a forward-looking maintenance concern, fail-safe today; the
slice-end six-vector review should re-confirm no new feature-timeline writer
caller appears (carried as part of the P3 ledger).

**Vector 4 — the supervisor-owned writers ARE allowed: PASS.** The read-only
contract permits the supervisor's own append-only audit / dedupe / outbox
writers. `SUPERVISOR_OWNED_AUDIT_KEY_PREFIXES` (`read_only.py:221`) covers
`supervisor-observation:` / `supervisor-decision:` / `supervisor-agent-
assessment:` / `supervisor-thread-context:` / `supervisor-action:` plus the
pre-registered `supervisor-digest:` / `supervisor-digest-audit:` /
`supervisor-digest-state:` (the later Slack-dedupe sub-slice's supervisor-owned
state). `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink.put` passes a supervisor-owned-keyed put
straight through to the wrapped store (`test_audit_sink_allows_supervisor_owned_
audit_put`); `ActionPolicy._write`'s `supervisor-action:*` audit rows are
allowed. No supervisor-owned write is blocked.

**Vector 5 — production wiring + additivity: PASS.** Confirmed via grep: the
two production construction sites are safe — `slack.py:2478` `ActionPolicy(...)`
passes `mode`/`restart`/`artifact_sink`/`feature` with NO `execution_authority`
(so `assert_no_control_plane_writer(None, ...)` passes); `mcp_server.py:752`
`SupervisorEvidenceMcpService(...)` passes `database_url` only (lazy stores,
re-asserted in `_ensure_stores`). `read_only.py` is a clean additive leaf
(imports only `typing`); `maybe_restart` / `maybe_kill_stale_codex` are
byte-for-byte unchanged; `guard_execution_write` + the `execution_authority`
param + the MCP `assert_read_only` are purely additive — no working supervisor
code path was rewritten.

**Vector 6 — test honesty: PASS.** The 18 tests in `tests/supervisor/test_read_
only.py` genuinely exercise the construction guards (`test_mcp_service_
construction_fails_closed_on_control_plane_store`, `test_action_policy_
construction_fails_closed_on_execution_authority_handle`, with a real-class
binding via `test_real_execution_control_store_exposes_every_control_plane_
writer`), the fail-closed audit sink (the wrapped store's `puts` is asserted
empty after a denied put), and the blocked-action audit row (the
`supervisor-action:8ac124d6:200:project_group_checkpoint:blocked` key is
asserted persisted). Re-ran independently:
`python -m pytest tests/supervisor/test_read_only.py -q -p no:randomly` → 18
passed; `python -m pytest tests/supervisor/ -q -p no:randomly` → 237 passed
(the suite stays GREEN). Hygiene: `python -m compileall` on the three 10c-1
files → clean; `git diff --check` → clean.

**Findings.**
- **P3-10c-1 (new, non-blocking — stale prose count).** The journal entry, the
  `read_only.py` module docstring (`:28` "`CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` (26
  names)"), the inline comment in `read_only.py`, the `EXECUTION_AUTHORITY_
  WRITER_METHODS` "union (33)" prose, AND the 10c-1 IMPLEMENTATION jsonl line
  all say "26" / "33". The LIVE frozensets are `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` =
  **28**, `FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS` = 7, union = **35**. The
  load-bearing artifacts (the frozenset literals + `test_real_execution_control_
  store_exposes_every_control_plane_writer`) are CORRECT and complete (28 = the
  full current `ExecutionControlStore` writer set, verified); only the
  human-readable counts drifted. Cosmetic — does not affect the contract or any
  test. Fix in 10c-2 / the slice-end review (correct "26"→"28", "33"→"35" in
  the docstring + comment).
- **P3-10c-2 (new, non-blocking — the implementer's P2-watch (a), downgraded).**
  The coverage test asserts `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` ⊆ the class, not the
  reverse — a newly-added `ExecutionControlStore` writer is not auto-flagged.
  Acceptable as a P3 (the contract is complete today); the 10c-2 / slice-end
  review should add a reverse/bidirectional public-surface assertion.
- **P3-watch (the implementer's P2-watch (b), confirmed sound).**
  `FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS` is call-site-enforced; no supervisor caller
  of a feature-timeline writer exists today. Re-confirm at the slice-end review.
- **P3 (the implementer's stated landing-seam check).**
  `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` is an opt-in wrapper with no production caller in
  10c-1 — `guard_execution_write` / `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` ship the
  MECHANISM; the doc-10 step-10 startup assertions / step-11 atomic-landing seam
  must confirm the production read-only wiring actually hands
  `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` to `ActionPolicy`. Not a 10c-1 defect (every
  `ActionPolicy` audit write targets `supervisor-action:*`, so even an
  un-wrapped sink writes only supervisor-owned keys) — a landing-seam item.

No P1. No P2. Slice 10c-1 (the mechanical read-only supervisor contract) is
APPROVED and COMPLETE.

## Slice 10c-1 COMPLETE (2026-05-22) — the mechanical read-only supervisor contract

The independent Slice 10c-1 REVIEW returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2** across all
six review-brief vectors (the KEY vectors: vector 1 mechanical structural
enforcement — PASS; vector 2 fail-closed + blocked-action audit row — PASS;
vector 3 the mutation-surface split + `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS`
completeness — PASS, the split is sound and the 28-name set IS the complete
current `ExecutionControlStore` writer surface). Slice 10c was SPLIT — 10c-1
delivered the mechanical read-only supervisor enforcement:
`supervisor/read_only.py` (additive leaf — `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` 28
execution-authority writers exclusive to `ExecutionControlStore`,
`FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS` 7 dual-use writers, `assert_no_control_plane_
writer` / `assert_read_only_supervisor_handles`, `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`,
`ReadOnlySupervisorViolation`, `BlockedExecutionWrite`); the
`SupervisorEvidenceMcpService` construction guard (no `ExecutionControlStore`
slot — structurally absent — asserted at `__init__` and re-asserted after lazy
store creation); the `ActionPolicy` `execution_authority` construction guard +
`guard_execution_write` fail-closed denied-write handler. The execution-
authority writer path is structurally ABSENT / DENIED before runtime parameters
are inspected; a denied write fails closed and writes a `supervisor-action:*:
blocked` audit row, never a best-effort mutation; the supervisor-owned audit /
dedupe / outbox writers are allowed. 18 new tests; `tests/supervisor/` stays
green at 237. Three non-blocking P3s recorded (P3-10c-1 a stale prose count —
the live frozensets are correct; P3-10c-2 the one-directional coverage
assertion; the `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` production-wiring landing-seam
check). The 10c-2 typed classifier mapping + the real typed budget source
(resolving the carried P3-10a-1) is the next iteration.

## Slice 10c-2 IMPLEMENTATION (2026-05-22) — the typed classifier mapping + the real typed budget source

**Scope / split decision.** STATUS.md flagged Slice 10c-2 as possibly needing a
SPLIT (`classifier.py` is 2433 lines on the live failure-routing path). It was
delivered as ONE coherent verified chunk — NOT split — because the doc-10 work
is genuinely one deliverable: the typed mapping table's `requires_budget` rows
gate on "budget remains", so the typed budget source MUST land in the same
chunk or the mapping would reference a placeholder. The size risk was retired
by putting the doc-10 mapping table + the typed classification logic in a NEW
additive leaf module (`supervisor/classifier_mapping.py`, ~900 lines) rather
than editing the 2433-line `classifier.py` monolith — `classifier.py` got only
a thin additive typed-PRIMARY branch (`_classify_typed_primary` +
`_typed_healthy_merge_progress` + `_typed_snapshot_has_active_work`). The eight
legacy artifact classifier methods are byte-for-byte unchanged.

**What was built.**

1. **The typed mapping table — `supervisor/classifier_mapping.py` (NEW).**
   `MAPPING_ROWS` is the doc-10 § "Supervisor Classifier Mapping" 27-row table
   transcribed VERBATIM (the supervisor class, action, and Notes column are
   exact; nothing invented). Each `TypedMappingRow` matches a typed failure by
   `(failure_class set, route set, budget bucket)`. `CLASSIFIER_PRIORITY` is
   the doc-10 8-level priority list kept as data. `classify_typed_snapshot`
   maps a typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot`'s `latest_failures` rows onto a
   `TypedClassification`, picking the highest-priority verdict; newest-open
   failure wins within a tier (doc-10 "open/routed/retrying rows first").

2. **Typed-primary, legacy-fallback gating.** `SupervisorObservation` gained
   `control_plane: Any` (the typed snapshot) + `evidence_mode: str` (doc-10
   step 3). `SupervisorClassifier.classify` now calls `_classify_typed_primary`
   FIRST; it returns a verdict ONLY when `evidence_mode == "typed"` AND a typed
   snapshot is present AND that snapshot carries a failure/merge-queue signal
   that maps. For `evidence_mode != "typed"` (`legacy_fallback` / `mixed` /
   `""`) it returns `None` and the eight legacy artifact classifiers run as the
   FALLBACK — unchanged. A typed-mode observation whose typed snapshot says
   nothing also falls through to the legacy classifiers (never a silent
   default). The doc-10 priority interleaves the typed verdict (levels 1-3,
   6-7) with the bridge/process rows (levels 4-5 — `_stale_codex_invocation` /
   `_safe_restart_candidate`); a typed verdict at priority <= 3 returns before
   the bridge/process rows; the bridge/process rows are SKIPPED when the typed
   snapshot carries active work (`_typed_snapshot_has_active_work` — active
   attempts / merge-queue items / sandbox leases / runtime bindings) so a
   stale-Codex row never outranks live typed work (doc-10 priority-5 gate).

3. **The coverage rule + static test.** `coverage_report()` scans `MAPPING_ROWS`
   against the canonical Slice-07 `failure_router.FAILURE_CLASSES` (27 classes)
   × every concrete `(failure_class, route)` the `failure_router.ROUTE_TABLE`
   proves a class can emit. Every `FailureClass` maps to EXACTLY ONE row per
   `(class, route, budget-bucket)` — no class unmapped, no double-mapping.
   `tests/supervisor/test_classifier_mapping.py::test_coverage_report_is_clean`
   + `test_every_canonical_failure_class_maps_to_exactly_one_row` FAIL if any
   class becomes unmapped or double-mapped. How every class is mapped exactly
   once: a class with multiple routes (`worktree_alias` →
   `run_canonicalization_repair` vs `quiesce`) has one row per route bucket;
   the route action + severity choose the row — there is no `(class, route,
   budget)` triple that two rows resolve to two different supervisor classes
   (verified by the coverage scan + the resolution table in the implementation
   transcript).

4. **The deterministic-never-escalates rule + static test.**
   `DETERMINISTIC_EXECUTOR_OWNED_CLASSES` is the doc-10 list (worktree aliasing,
   ACL workability, stale projection, commit hygiene, contract compile repair,
   verifier context rebuild, sandbox allocation/capture/cleanup, runtime
   structured output, merge retry, bounded diagnostic dispatch). The
   operator-escalation rule lives in `_classify_one_failure`: `operator_required`
   is honoured ONLY when `failure_class == 'operator_required'` OR
   `operator_required == true` AND no deterministic repair route with remaining
   budget exists. A deterministic class carrying a STALE `operator_required=true`
   but a newer retry/repair route WITH budget is NOT escalated — the verdict is
   `deterministic_unblock` and `facts["superseded_operator_signal"]=True`
   records the superseded signal (doc-10 edge case). `coverage_report()`'s
   rule-2 scan + `test_no_mapping_row_escalates_a_deterministic_class_on_a_repair
   _route` + the 11-case parametrized
   `test_deterministic_class_with_budget_never_escalates_to_operator` FAIL if
   any deterministic class with a retry/repair route + budget produces
   `operator_required`. How a deterministic class with budget never escalates:
   the only `operator_required` mapping row matches a deterministic class ONLY
   on the terminal `operator_required` ROUTE (never a repair route); a
   deterministic class on a repair route resolves to its own
   `deterministic_unblock` row; and the `operator_required=true` BOOLEAN is
   checked against an active deterministic repair route before the row resolves.

5. **The REAL typed budget source — resolves the carried P3-10a-1.** The
   Slice-10a `_typed_retry_budgets` (`store.py`) had `budget_remaining = 0` as a
   conservative placeholder. The genuine typed budget source is the
   `route_decision` payload the Slice-07 typed failure router persists on every
   `runtime_failure_context` `evidence_nodes` row —
   `implementation.py:_route_decision_compat_payload` writes `budget_remaining`
   / `budget_exhausted` and a nested `retry_budget` dict (`remaining_attempts` /
   `max_attempts` / `reservation_ordinal`). The typed-failures SELECT in
   `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot` now projects `budget_remaining` /
   `budget_total` / `budget_used` / `budget_exhausted` via
   `payload#>>'{route_decision,...}'` COALESCE chains (no extra read).
   `_typed_retry_budgets` reads the genuine per-`(route, signature_hash)`
   budget: the newest failure row supplies the authoritative
   `budget_remaining`; `budget_exhausted=true` pins remaining to 0;
   `terminal_reason` records `budget_exhausted` / `no_typed_budget`. Fail-safe:
   a legacy/pre-router row with NO route_decision budget keeps
   `budget_remaining=0` (never an over-optimistic positive). The classifier's
   `_budget_remaining_for` matches a typed failure to its budget row by
   `(route, signature_hash)` (route-only fallback), so the "budget remains"
   gate is genuine. Verified end-to-end against real Postgres (`localhost:5431`).

**Interfaces / citations verified at file:line.**
- `failure_router.FailureClass` 27-class `Literal` (`failure_router.py:14`) +
  `FAILURE_CLASSES` tuple (`:132`) — the coverage test keys against the tuple;
  `test_supervisor_classifier_mapping_uses_router_taxonomy` asserts
  `classifier_mapping.FAILURE_CLASSES == failure_router.FAILURE_CLASSES` (27).
- `failure_router.RouteAction` (`:103`) + `ROUTE_TABLE` (`:1379`) — the coverage
  scan enumerates the router-emittable `(class, route)` universe from
  `ROUTE_TABLE`; `failure_router.decide` (`:1551`) rewrites a retry/repair route
  with `budget_remaining <= 0` to `quiesce`, so a `(retry_repair_route,
  no_budget)` bucket is impossible — the coverage scan models exactly that.
- `SupervisorClassifier.classify` (`classifier.py:48` after the edit) — the live
  failure-routing path; the typed-PRIMARY branch is additive.
- `implementation.py:_route_decision_compat_payload` (`:636`) +
  `_route_decision_retry_budget_payload` (`:614`) — the real typed budget
  source; confirmed it writes `budget_remaining` (`:661`) + `retry_budget`
  (`:670`) onto the `route_decision` payload.
- doc-10 § "Supervisor Classifier Mapping" — the 27-row table + the 8-level
  priority list + the operator-escalation rule, transcribed verbatim into
  `MAPPING_ROWS` / `CLASSIFIER_PRIORITY`.

**doc-10 ambiguities + resolutions.**
- *The `(retry/repair route, no-budget)` bucket.* The doc-10 table's
  retry/repair rows say "and budget remains" but the doc never states the
  `no-budget` case for those routes. RESOLUTION: the Slice-07 router
  (`failure_router.decide`) never emits a retry/repair route with zero budget —
  it rewrites the action to `quiesce`. So `(retry_repair_route, no_budget)` is
  not a router-emittable bucket; the coverage scan models a retry/repair route
  with the budget-remaining bucket only. (A defensive `requires_no_budget` flag
  exists on `TypedMappingRow` but no row needs it today.)
- *`operator_required` as a ROUTE vs the `operator_required` CLASS.* The doc-10
  "operator_required" row keys on "`failure_class = 'operator_required'` or
  `operator_required = true`". The canonical router expresses operator
  escalation as the `operator_required` ROUTE on the failing class — and
  `runtime_context/context_permission_denied` emits route `operator_required`
  with a non-`operator_required` class. RESOLUTION: the `operator_required`
  mapping row matches the typed `operator_required` ROUTE for ANY class (the
  route IS the router's explicit escalation). A deterministic class with a
  stale `operator_required=true` flag but a newer retry/repair route never
  reaches this row (`_classify_one_failure` prefers the deterministic route).
- *`sandbox_capture/quiesce` — fatal row or sandbox-quiesce row?* doc-10 row 2
  lists `dispatcher_internal, sandbox_isolation, evidence_corruption` as the
  fatal control-plane safety classes; `sandbox_capture` is NOT in that row but
  IS in the "`sandbox_allocation, sandbox_capture, sandbox_cleanup,
  runtime_context, runtime_structured_output` with route `quiesce`" row.
  RESOLUTION: `sandbox_capture/quiesce` → the sandbox-quiesce row
  (`pipeline_bug_suspected`); `sandbox_isolation/quiesce` → the fatal row.
  Both resolve to `pipeline_bug_suspected/stop/escalate` so the verdict is
  identical; only the cited row id differs.

**Tests added (all GREEN).**
- `tests/supervisor/test_classifier_mapping.py` (NEW — 63 tests):
  the static coverage tests (`test_coverage_report_is_clean`,
  `test_every_canonical_failure_class_maps_to_exactly_one_row`,
  `test_mapping_table_failure_classes_are_all_canonical`,
  `test_supervisor_classifier_mapping_uses_router_taxonomy`); the
  deterministic-never-escalates tests
  (`test_no_mapping_row_escalates_a_deterministic_class_on_a_repair_route`, the
  11-case `test_deterministic_class_with_budget_never_escalates_to_operator`,
  `test_operator_required_class_with_explicit_route_does_escalate`,
  `test_deterministic_class_with_exhausted_budget_does_not_escalate_operator`);
  the typed-primary/legacy-fallback gating tests
  (`test_typed_evidence_mode_routes_through_typed_mapping`,
  `test_legacy_fallback_mode_skips_typed_mapping_and_uses_artifacts`,
  `test_empty_evidence_mode_uses_legacy_fallback`,
  `test_typed_mode_with_no_typed_failures_falls_back_to_legacy`); the 33-case
  parametrized `test_doc10_mapping_table_rows` (every doc-10 table row
  end-to-end); the budget-gate tests
  (`test_budget_remaining_gates_retry_vs_escalation`,
  `test_no_budget_row_is_fail_safe_no_unblock`,
  `test_budget_matched_by_route_and_signature`); the open/resolved + priority
  tests; the bridge/process-gating tests
  (`test_active_merge_queue_suppresses_stale_codex_in_typed_mode`,
  `test_stale_codex_still_classifies_when_no_typed_active_work`).
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_snapshots_store.py` (3 new — the real
  typed budget source): `test_retry_budget_reads_real_budget_remaining_from_
  route_decision`, `test_retry_budget_exhausted_route_decision_pins_remaining_
  to_zero`, `test_retry_budget_without_typed_budget_is_fail_safe_zero`. Run
  against real Postgres (`localhost:5431` — up).

**Supervisor tests updated for the behavior change.** NONE were modified or
weakened — the typed-primary path is purely additive (it returns `None` for
every `evidence_mode != "typed"` observation, which is every pre-existing
`test_classifier.py` case since none set `evidence_mode`). All 237 pre-existing
`tests/supervisor/` tests pass UNCHANGED. The genuine `classify` behavior
change is reachable only by a NEW typed-evidence observation; it is covered by
the 63 new `test_classifier_mapping.py` tests. No classification/escalation
assertion was dropped or relaxed.

**Test results.** `tests/supervisor/` → 300 passed (237 pre-existing + 63
new). `tests/workflows/develop/execution/` → 683 passed (680 baseline + 3 new).
`tests/test_execution_control_store.py` + `tests/test_dashboard_bugflow.py` +
`test_snapshots.py` → 201 passed. `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py`
+ `test_dag_regroup.py` + `test_workflow_quiesce.py` → 336 passed. Hygiene:
`python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` → clean; `git diff
--check` → clean. Real-Postgres tests ran (DB up); they SKIP cleanly when
`localhost:5431` is down.

**P1/P2-risk areas for a reviewer (failure-routing correctness is the KEY
vector).**
- The typed-PRIMARY branch in `SupervisorClassifier.classify` is a GENUINE
  behavior change on the live failure-routing path — review the doc-10 priority
  interleave in `_classify_typed_primary` (typed verdict <= 3 → return;
  bridge/process levels 4-5 SKIPPED when typed active work exists; typed
  product-repair / watch-only / merge-queue healthy after). Confirm a typed
  deterministic class with a retry/repair route + budget can NEVER reach
  `operator_required` (the `_classify_one_failure` operator-escalation guard).
- The real typed budget source: `_typed_retry_budgets` reads `budget_remaining`
  from the `route_decision` payload. Confirm the SQL COALESCE chains in the
  typed-failures SELECT cover the router's actual payload shape, and that the
  fail-safe `0` (no typed budget) never produces a false "budget remains".
- `evidence_mode` is NOT yet populated by `evidence.py` /
  `mcp_server.get_current_snapshot` — doc-10 step 4 (the `collect_evidence`
  typed-snapshot wiring) is a SEPARATE Slice-10 seam. Until that lands, every
  production observation has `evidence_mode == ""` and runs the LEGACY path —
  the typed-primary path is dormant in production but fully tested. A reviewer
  should confirm this is the intended seam boundary (it is — STATUS.md scopes
  10c-2 to the classifier mapping + budget source; the evidence wiring is the
  next seam).

## Slice 10c-2 REVIEW (2026-05-22) — the typed classifier mapping + the real typed budget source

**Reviewer summary.** Independent review of Slice 10c-2 — the NEW additive leaf
`supervisor/classifier_mapping.py` (the doc-10 27-row mapping table, the 8-level
priority list, `coverage_report`, `classify_typed_snapshot` /
`_classify_one_failure`), the thin additive typed-PRIMARY branch in
`supervisor/classifier.py` (`_classify_typed_primary` +
`_typed_healthy_merge_progress` + `_typed_snapshot_has_active_work`), the real
typed budget source in `execution_control/store.py`
(`build_typed_control_plane_snapshot` typed-failures SELECT +
`_typed_retry_budgets`), and the 63 new `tests/supervisor/test_classifier
_mapping.py` tests. Targeted suites re-run GREEN: `tests/supervisor/` 300
passed; `tests/workflows/develop/execution/` 683 passed. The six review-brief
vectors were exercised including coverage-scan mutation tests and behavioral
reachability of the typed `quiesce` path.

**VERDICT: NOT CLEAN — 0 P1, 1 P2, 2 P3.** Do not finalize to Slice 10d.

**Findings.**

- **P2-10c-2-1 — `verifier_context/quiesce` is an UNMAPPED canonical class on
  the typed failure-routing path; the coverage test is VACUOUS for the
  budget-exhausted-quiesce sub-universe.** `classifier_mapping.py` `MAPPING_ROWS`
  has NO row matching `failure_class="verifier_context"` with `route="quiesce"`.
  `verifier_context` is a deterministic executor-owned class whose only
  `ROUTE_TABLE` routes (`failure_router.py:1284,1290`,
  `context_materialization_failed` + `verifier_context_stale`) BOTH go to
  `retry_verifier` (`CLASS_RETRY_BUDGETS["verifier_context"]=1`). When that
  budget is exhausted, `failure_router.decide` (`failure_router.py:1551-1552`)
  rewrites the action to `quiesce`, and `implementation.py
  _route_decision_compat_payload` (`:655`) writes `route_decision.route =
  "quiesce"` onto the evidence row — so a typed failure `verifier_context` /
  `route="quiesce"` is genuinely producible and reaches the classifier.
  `resolve_mapping_row("verifier_context","quiesce")` returns `None`,
  `_classify_one_failure` returns `None`, `classify_typed_snapshot` returns
  `None`, and `classify_observation` falls through to the legacy artifact
  classifiers — which (on empty artifacts) return `watch_only/observe`. The
  doc-10-intended verdict for an exhausted deterministic class is the typed
  safety stop `pipeline_bug_suspected / stop/escalate` (cf. `workspace_quiesce`,
  `sandbox_runtime_quiesce`, `verifier_provider_quiesce` rows). The implementer
  DID anticipate budget-exhausted-quiesce routing — 11 of the 12 classes that
  reach `quiesce` only via the budget-exhaustion rewrite have a `quiesce`
  mapping row; `verifier_context` is the sole class missed. The defect slipped
  through because `coverage_report` (`classifier_mapping.py:566-650`) derives
  its `(class, route)` universe from `ROUTE_TABLE` FRESH routes only and (for a
  retry/repair route) checks ONLY the `has_budget=True` bucket — so it never
  visits `verifier_context/quiesce` and cannot flag an unmapped OR double-mapped
  `(class, quiesce)` for any of those 12 classes. The static coverage test
  (`test_coverage_report_is_clean`, `test_every_canonical_failure_class_maps
  _to_exactly_one_row`) is therefore VACUOUS for the budget-exhausted-quiesce
  sub-universe — verified by mutation: dropping the `checkpoint_contradiction`
  row IS caught (`ok=False`), but adding a double-mapping row for
  `worktree_alias/quiesce` is NOT caught (`ok=True`), while a double-mapping on
  the enumerated `worktree_alias/run_canonicalization_repair` IS caught.
  Bounded: the typed-primary path is dormant in production (vector 6) so this
  does not yet mis-route live traffic, and it fails to legacy fallback rather
  than crashing — hence P2 not P1. Fix: add a `verifier_context_quiesce` row
  (`verifier_context` / `quiesce` -> `pipeline_bug_suspected` / `stop/escalate`,
  priority 2, mirroring `verifier_provider_quiesce`); and extend the
  `coverage_report` route universe to also enumerate the budget-exhausted
  `quiesce` rewrite for every class with a retry/repair route, so the coverage
  test genuinely covers the `(class, quiesce)` sub-universe.
  File:line — `src/iriai_build_v2/supervisor/classifier_mapping.py:173-533`
  (`MAPPING_ROWS` — missing row), `classifier_mapping.py:566-650`
  (`coverage_report` — universe gap), `tests/supervisor/test_classifier
  _mapping.py:160-193` (the vacuous coverage assertions).

- **P3-10c-2-1 — the doc-10 step-4 `evidence_mode` wiring is not on the
  STATUS.md "Remaining" ledger (vector-6 tracking gap).** `collect_evidence`
  (`supervisor/evidence.py`) and `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.get_current
  _snapshot` (`supervisor/mcp_server.py`) still call the LEGACY dict
  `fetch_control_plane_snapshot` and populate the legacy `control_plane
  _snapshot` field; the typed `SupervisorObservation.control_plane` and
  `evidence_mode` fields are never set, so `evidence_mode==""` and
  `_classify_typed_primary` returns `None` for every production observation —
  the typed-primary path is fully DORMANT. This is doc-10 § "Refactoring Steps"
  step 4 ("Update `collect_evidence` and `get_current_snapshot` to call the
  typed snapshot first"), a clearly-defined LATER seam distinct from 10c-2's
  scope (steps 3 + 5), so a dormant-but-fully-tested 10c-2 is ACCEPTABLE — but
  STATUS.md "Remaining" does not name step 4, so without tracking the typed
  classifier ships permanently non-functional. Non-blocking (the doc defines the
  step; the implementation jsonl `next_action` already names it) — resolve by
  adding step 4 to STATUS.md "Remaining" as a named Slice-10 seam.

- **P3-10c-2-2 — `classify_typed_snapshot` sorts/selects failures by
  `failure_id` only.** `_failure_sort_key` (`classifier_mapping.py:810-813`)
  and the newest-open selection use `_failure_id` (the `evidence_nodes` row id)
  as the recency proxy; `TypedFailureSummary.created_at` is ignored. Monotonic
  ids make this correct in practice and the journal documents the choice, but
  an id/time skew would pick a stale verdict. Non-blocking; cosmetic — note in
  the slice-end six-vector review.

**Vector verdicts.**

- **Vector 1 (mapping-table conformance) — PASS.** Spot-checked `MAPPING_ROWS`
  against doc 10 § "Supervisor Classifier Mapping": the 25 rows transcribe the
  doc table's `(FailureClass, route, budget) -> (supervisor class, action)` and
  the Notes column verbatim; the `operator_required` ROUTE-vs-CLASS resolution
  and the `sandbox_capture/quiesce` row-id resolution are documented and sound.
  No divergent mapping row. (The doc table itself omits a `verifier_context
  /quiesce` row — see vector 2 / P2.)

- **Vector 2 (FailureClass coverage) — FAIL (P2-10c-2-1).** All 27 canonical
  `failure_router.FAILURE_CLASSES` map to exactly one row across the
  router's FRESH `(class, route)` universe, and `coverage_report` is genuinely
  non-vacuous THERE (mutation-verified: a dropped row and a double-map on an
  enumerated route are both caught). BUT the budget-exhausted `quiesce` rewrite
  routes are NOT in the scan universe, `verifier_context/quiesce` is UNMAPPED,
  and the coverage test does not fail on it — a vacuous-for-that-sub-universe
  test plus a genuinely unmapped class. P2.

- **Vector 3 (deterministic-never-escalates) — PASS.** No `MAPPING_ROWS` row
  classifies a deterministic executor-owned class as `operator_required` on a
  retry/repair route — the only `operator_required` row matches a deterministic
  class ONLY on the terminal `operator_required` ROUTE. `_classify_one_failure`
  honours `operator_required` ONLY when `failure_class=="operator_required"` OR
  `operator_required==true` AND no deterministic repair route with budget
  exists; a deterministic class on a retry/repair route with budget resolves to
  its `deterministic_unblock` row and records `superseded_operator_signal` for a
  stale flag. The 11-case parametrized test and the budget-exhausted test
  confirm a budget-exhausted deterministic class quiesces to
  `pipeline_bug_suspected`, never `operator_required`. NO false operator
  escalation for a deterministic class on any path — including the
  `verifier_context/quiesce` gap (it falls to legacy `watch_only`, not
  operator). The failure-routing-correctness KEY vector holds.

- **Vector 4 (typed-primary / legacy-fallback gating) — PASS.**
  `_classify_typed_primary` runs FIRST in `classify` and returns a verdict ONLY
  when `context.is_typed_evidence` (`evidence_mode=="typed"` AND a typed
  snapshot present); otherwise it returns `None` and the 8 legacy classifiers
  run as the `or`-chain fallback. The 8 legacy classifier methods take only
  `context` and run on artifact/worktree/event evidence with NO typed-mode
  injection — additive typed branch only; byte-for-byte unchanged by 10c-2
  (the HEAD-diff deletions in `classifier.py` are bundle-wide earlier-slice
  refactors — `classifier.py` was not a 10c-1 review target — and the
  237 pre-existing `tests/supervisor/` tests stay green unchanged, corroborating
  the no-legacy-change claim).

- **Vector 5 (the real typed budget source) — PASS.** `_typed_retry_budgets`
  (`store.py:2155-2260`) reads a genuine per-`(route, signature_hash)` budget:
  the `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot` typed-failures SELECT
  (`store.py:1656-1683`) projects `budget_remaining`/`budget_total`/`budget
  _used`/`budget_exhausted` via COALESCE chains over `payload#>>'{route
  _decision,...}'` and `payload#>>'{retry_budget,...}'` — matching exactly the
  shape `implementation.py _route_decision_compat_payload`/`_route_decision
  _retry_budget_payload` write. `budget_exhausted=true` pins remaining to 0;
  fail-safe `0` + `terminal_reason="no_typed_budget"` for a legacy row with no
  router budget (never an over-optimistic positive). It stays bounded/summary-
  only (capped at `cap`, no artifact bodies, no extra read). The carried
  P3-10a-1 `budget_remaining=0` placeholder is resolved.

- **Vector 6 (the `evidence_mode` dormancy) — DORMANT TYPED PATH IS
  ACCEPTABLE; the wiring MUST BE TRACKED.** `evidence_mode` is not populated by
  `collect_evidence` / `get_current_snapshot` (both still on the legacy dict
  snapshot), so the typed-primary path is fully dormant in production —
  honestly flagged by the implementer. The wiring is doc-10 § "Refactoring
  Steps" STEP 4, a clearly-defined LATER seam distinct from 10c-2's scope
  (steps 3 + 5), so a dormant-but-fully-tested 10c-2 is acceptable — BUT
  STATUS.md "Remaining" does not yet name step 4 (P3-10c-2-1). The step-4
  `evidence_mode` / typed-`control_plane` wiring of `collect_evidence` +
  `get_current_snapshot` must be added to STATUS.md "Remaining" as a named
  tracked Slice-10 seam (before the atomic-landing step) or the typed
  classifier ships permanently non-functional.

- **Vector 7 (test honesty) — PASS (with the vector-2 vacuity caveat).** The 63
  new tests are non-vacuous and behavioral (the deterministic-never-escalates,
  budget-gate, typed-primary/legacy-fallback, and 33-case mapping-table tests
  all exercise `classify_observation` end-to-end). `tests/supervisor/` is green
  at 300 (237 pre-existing unchanged + 63 new); no escalation/classification
  assertion was weakened by 10c-2. The one caveat is the vector-2 P2: the
  coverage tests are vacuous for the budget-exhausted-`quiesce` sub-universe.

**Open P1/P2 (acceptance gate).** 1 P2 — P2-10c-2-1. Slice 10c-2 is NOT
finalized; STATUS.md is left pointing at 10c-2. The 2 P3s (P3-10c-2-1,
P3-10c-2-2) are recorded for the carried-P3 ledger.

## Slice 10c-2 REMEDIATION + COMPLETE (2026-05-22) — the typed classifier mapping + the real typed budget source

**Scope.** Remediation of the Slice 10c-2 REVIEW findings (0 P1, 1 P2, 2 P3).
The P2 is mandatory; P3-10c-2-2 is fixed inline; P3-10c-2-1 is resolved by the
STATUS.md finalize (it adds the doc-10 step-4 `evidence_mode` wiring to
"Remaining"). The 8 legacy classifier methods and `classifier.py` stay
byte-for-byte unchanged — this remediation touches only the additive leaf
`supervisor/classifier_mapping.py` and `tests/supervisor/test_classifier_mapping.py`.

**P2-10c-2-1 fix — the unmapped `verifier_context/quiesce` route + the vacuous
coverage scan.** Three coordinated changes:

1. **The missing mapping row.** Added `MAPPING_ROWS` row `verifier_context_quiesce`
   (`classifier_mapping.py` — placed as "Row 12b" right after the existing
   `verifier_context_retry` row): `failure_class="verifier_context"`,
   `route="quiesce"` → `FailureClass.PIPELINE_BUG_SUSPECTED` /
   `ActionLevel.STOP_ESCALATE`, `priority=2`. **Verdict source** — doc 10 §
   "Supervisor Classifier Mapping": the doc table itself omits an explicit
   `verifier_context/quiesce` row, but the verdict is NOT invented — it is
   fixed by (a) doc-10's stated rule "If one of these [deterministic
   executor-owned] classes reaches `route="quiesce"`, the supervisor reports
   the stopped route ... it still must not ask the operator to manually edit
   files", and (b) the verbatim treatment of EVERY other deterministic-class
   budget-exhausted-`quiesce` row in the same doc table — the workspace
   `quiesce` row (`worktree_alias`/`acl_workability`/`stale_projection`/
   `commit_hygiene` → `pipeline_bug_suspected`/`stop/escalate`) and the
   sandbox/runtime `quiesce` row (`sandbox_*`/`runtime_*` → same). The new row
   mirrors `workspace_quiesce` / `sandbox_runtime_quiesce` exactly. Why
   `verifier_context/quiesce` is genuinely producible: `verifier_context` is a
   deterministic class whose ONLY two `ROUTE_TABLE` entries
   (`context_materialization_failed`, `verifier_context_stale`) both route to
   `retry_verifier` (budget 1, `failure_router.py:365`); `failure_router.decide`
   (`failure_router.py:1551-1554`) rewrites a budget-exhausted non-terminal
   retry/repair route to `quiesce` with `budget_exhausted=True`. Pre-fix,
   `_classify_one_failure` called `resolve_mapping_row("verifier_context",
   "quiesce", ...)` → `None` → no typed verdict → `classify_observation` fell
   to the legacy fallback → `watch_only` (NOT the doc-10-intended
   `pipeline_bug_suspected`/`stop-escalate` safety stop).

2. **The `coverage_report` universe extension.** `coverage_report`'s
   `(class, route)` scan universe is now built by the new
   `_router_emittable_routes_by_class()` helper, which enumerates BOTH the
   `ROUTE_TABLE` *fresh* routes AND the budget-exhausted `(class, "quiesce")`
   *rewrite* routes: for every canonical class whose `ROUTE_TABLE` route is a
   budget-bearing retry/repair route, `failure_router.decide` can rewrite it to
   `quiesce`, so `"quiesce"` is added to that class's emit-route set. Pre-fix,
   `coverage_report` enumerated only the `ROUTE_TABLE` fresh routes, so a class
   whose only fresh route is retry/repair (e.g. `verifier_context`, fresh route
   `retry_verifier` only) NEVER had its `(class, "quiesce")` rewrite route
   visited — the static coverage test was VACUOUS for the
   budget-exhausted-`quiesce` sub-universe and could not catch the missing
   row. After the extension, **all 26 classes that can reach a `(class,
   quiesce)` route** (18 via the budget-exhausted retry/repair rewrite + 14
   fresh `quiesce`, overlapping) map to exactly one row, consistently across
   the budget buckets — including the 12 deterministic-and-related
   budget-exhaustion classes the review named (the 4 workspace + 5
   sandbox/runtime + `verifier_context` + `verifier_provider` + `merge_conflict`),
   with `verifier_context` previously the sole miss. `coverage_report().ok` is
   `True`; `unmapped_classes` / `double_mapped` empty.

3. **The proving test.** Added `test_verifier_context_budget_exhausted_quiesce_
   is_a_safety_stop` — builds a budget-EXHAUSTED `verifier_context` failure
   with `route="quiesce"` + a zero-budget row, asserts `classify_typed_snapshot`
   returns a genuine verdict (not `None` → fallback), that the verdict is
   `PIPELINE_BUG_SUSPECTED`/`STOP_ESCALATE` via row `verifier_context_quiesce`,
   and that `classify_observation` end-to-end produces the safety stop (never
   `watch_only`, never `operator_required`). Also added
   `test_coverage_report_enumerates_budget_exhausted_quiesce_routes` — asserts
   the coverage scan now resolves every budget-exhausted-`quiesce`-rewrite
   class to exactly one consistent row. **Confirmed both FAIL against the
   pre-fix code** by reconstructing the pre-fix state in-process (drop the
   `verifier_context_quiesce` row + restore the fresh-routes-only universe):
   pre-fix `classify_typed_snapshot` → `None`, `classify_observation` →
   `watch_only`, `resolve_mapping_row("verifier_context","quiesce")` → `None`,
   and `coverage_report().ok` is *vacuously* `True`. The fix is a genuine
   coverage closure, not a test tweak — a mutation test confirms
   `coverage_report` now catches BOTH a dropped `verifier_context/quiesce` row
   (`ok=False`, `unmapped_classes` names it) AND a contradictory double-mapped
   one (`ok=False`, `double_mapped` names it).

**P3-10c-2-2 fix — `classify_typed_snapshot` recency tiebreak.** Done inline
(clean, low-risk). `_failure_sort_key` now returns the tuple
`(created_at_ordinal, failure_id)` — `TypedFailureSummary.created_at` (a
required field on the model) is the PRIMARY recency key, the monotonic
`failure_id` is the deterministic secondary tiebreak. New helper
`_failure_created_ordinal` converts `created_at` to a sortable float; a row
with no usable `created_at` sorts as the OLDEST (`-inf`) so a row with a real
timestamp always wins. `classify_typed_snapshot`'s existing
`sorted(..., reverse=True)` now orders newest-by-`created_at`-first. Added
`test_classify_typed_snapshot_orders_failures_by_created_at` (the newer failure
by `created_at` carries the SMALLER `failure_id` — the verdict still cites the
`created_at`-newest failure, proving `created_at` wins over `failure_id`).

**Interfaces verified at file:line.** `failure_router.decide`
(`failure_router.py:1525`, the budget-exhausted-`quiesce` rewrite at `:1551-1554`);
the `verifier_context` `ROUTE_TABLE` entries (`failure_router.py:1283-1294`,
both `retry_verifier`); the `verifier_context` route budget = 1
(`failure_router.py:365`); `RouteAction` (`failure_router.py:103`, the 10
retry/repair routes match `_RETRY_REPAIR_ROUTES` exactly + `quiesce` /
`operator_required` terminal); `TypedFailureSummary.created_at`
(`snapshots.py:354`, a required `datetime`).

**Tests + results.** `tests/supervisor/` → **303 passed** (was 300 at the 10c-2
implementation; +3 new — `test_verifier_context_budget_exhausted_quiesce_is_a_
safety_stop`, `test_coverage_report_enumerates_budget_exhausted_quiesce_routes`,
`test_classify_typed_snapshot_orders_failures_by_created_at`). `test_classifier_
mapping.py` collects 66 tests (was 63 — exactly +3, NO pre-existing test
removed, dropped, or weakened; the typed-primary path is purely additive). The
coverage test is now NON-VACUOUS for the budget-exhausted-`quiesce`
sub-universe (mutation-verified above). `tests/workflows/develop/execution/` →
**683 passed** (unchanged from the 10c-2 baseline; real Postgres `localhost:5431`
was UP, 0 skips — the real-Postgres tests genuinely ran; they SKIP cleanly when
the DB is down). Hygiene: `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2
dashboard.py` → clean; `git diff --check` → clean.

**Outcome.** P2-10c-2-1 RESOLVED (the `verifier_context_quiesce` row + the
`coverage_report` universe extension + the two proving tests); P3-10c-2-2
RESOLVED inline; P3-10c-2-1 resolved by the STATUS.md finalize (the doc-10
step-4 `evidence_mode` wiring is added to "Remaining" as a tracked Slice-10
seam). 0 open P1/P2. **Slice 10c-2 is COMPLETE; Slice 10c (10c-1 + 10c-2) is
COMPLETE.** STATUS.md is advanced to Slice 10d (the Slack dedupe store). The
typed-primary classifier path remains correctly dormant in production until the
tracked step-4 `evidence_mode` wiring lands.

## Slice 10d-1 IMPLEMENTATION (2026-05-22) — the Slack dedupe store + the two supervisor-owned tables

**Scope + the split decision.** Slice 10d (doc 10 § "Slack Dedupe And
Suppression", step 7) was SPLIT — STATUS.md had flagged this. The dedupe
store/schema and the `SupervisorSlackService` send/suppress routing are
separable seams: the store is a self-contained persistence + decision-logic
unit verifiable in isolation with the real-Postgres fixture, while the routing
is a genuine cross-component behavior change that reopens the `watch_and_digest`
loop and must get its own independent review. Per IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT loop
