## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10d-2 COMPLETE (the Slack dedupe routing)

**Slice 10d-2 — wire `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore` into `SupervisorSlackService`
— COMPLETE.** The independent Slice 10d-2 REVIEW (above) returned **CLEAN — 0
P1, 0 P2, 0 new P3** (one non-blocking carried P3-10d-2-1) across all seven
review vectors, with explicit PASS verdicts on the three KEY vectors: vector 1
(no-bypass routing — `watch_and_digest` can no longer send a background digest
without `decide()`, verified structurally via every `post_blocks` call site),
vector 3 (never-suppress flag derivation — a new signature/route always sends,
a repeat is suppressible, direct operator answers do not route through
background suppression), and vector 4 (the legacy-throttle delay is acceptable —
a first escalation is delayed at most one poll, never dropped; doc 10 forbids
suppression, not delay). The slice is finalized with no remediation.

10d-2 delivers, in `supervisor/slack.py`: `watch_and_digest` now routes every
background digest through `_route_background_digest` → `_decide_background_
digest` → `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore.decide()` BEFORE the Slack client; the
two `adapter.post_blocks` background-digest call sites live ONLY inside
`_post_background_digest`, unreachable without a `should_send=True`. The new
`_build_digest_key` builds the typed `SupervisorDigestKey`; `_derive_never_
suppress_flags` derives `new_failure_signature` / `new_operator_route` exactly
as `get_state(key) is None` (the dedupe key folds the failure-signature hashes
and the typed route); `is_operator_answer` is always `False` (direct answers
never reach this routing). `should_send=True` → send THEN `record_sent`;
`should_send=False` → `record_suppressed` + no send; a failed send re-raises
before `record_sent` so the next poll re-`decide()`s as `first_seen`. The
doc-10 fail-open (escalation) / fail-quiet (background duplicate) split is
applied on a `DigestDedupeStoreError` via `_is_never_suppress_candidate`. The
legacy `SupervisorSlackDigestDecisionStore` + the `supervisor_slack_digest_*`
tables are byte-for-byte unchanged (additive); the new dedupe store resolves off
the same pool. 17 new tests; `tests/supervisor/` GREEN at **351 passed** (334
baseline + 17, one test updated for the genuine behavior change with every
original assertion kept + one added, no weakening).

**Slice 10d (10d-1 + 10d-2) is COMPLETE.** 10d-1 delivered the dedupe store +
the two supervisor-owned tables + the `decide()` decision logic (reviewed
CLEAN); 10d-2 delivered the `watch_and_digest` send/suppress routing (reviewed
CLEAN). The doc-10 § "Slack Dedupe And Suppression" step-7 seam is fully
landed: the typed-control-plane Slack dedupe contract is now LIVE on the
background digest loop. **Slice 10 — 10a + 10b + 10c (10c-1 + 10c-2) + 10d
(10d-1 + 10d-2) COMPLETE; Slice 10e (the doc-10 step-4 `evidence_mode` wiring)
is next.**

---

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10e IMPLEMENTATION (the doc-10 step-4 `evidence_mode` wiring)

**Slice 10e — make the supervisor evidence collection populate `evidence_mode`
and the typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot` so the Slice-10c-2 typed-primary
classifier path goes LIVE.** doc 10 § "Refactoring Steps" **step 4**: "Update
`collect_evidence` and `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.get_current_snapshot` to
call the typed snapshot first. Legacy artifact/event summaries are used only
when typed tables are absent or the typed query degrades." This resolves the
carried **P3-10c-2-1**. It is a GENUINE behavior change on the live supervisor
evidence path — it gets its OWN independent review (the orchestrator runs it).

### Scope decision — NOT split

STATUS.md flagged Slice 10e as a potential split (`collect_evidence` and
`get_current_snapshot` are two distinct call sites). Decision: **landed as ONE
cohesive wiring change, not split.** The two call sites share ONE new
helper pair (`load_typed_control_plane_snapshot` + `evidence_mode_for_snapshot`
in `evidence.py`) and ONE field-population contract; each call site is a
two-line wire-in of that shared helper. The change is small and uniform — a
split would isolate two near-identical 3-line edits behind two review passes
for no risk reduction. Per loop discipline, recorded here: 10e is one verified
pass with one independent review.

### What was wired (the two call sites + the shared helper)

**1. `supervisor/evidence.py` — the shared helper (NEW, additive).**
- `load_typed_control_plane_snapshot(artifact_store, feature_id, *,
  fallback_pool_source=None)` — resolves the Postgres pool off
  `artifact_store._pool` (and, when absent, `fallback_pool_source._pool` —
  matching the legacy `_load_control_plane_snapshot` /
  `_control_plane_snapshot` pool resolution so the typed path is never silently
  skipped on an asymmetric store wiring), constructs a transient
  `ExecutionControlStore(pool)`, and calls the **Slice-10a bounded**
  `get_control_plane_snapshot(ControlPlaneSnapshotQuery(feature_id=...,
  scope="supervisor"))`. The query budget is the un-mutated `SnapshotBudget`
  ceiling (the model validator clamps it). The read stays BOUNDED — it is the
  Slice-10a path: `LIMIT cap+1` truncation, `SET LOCAL statement_timeout`,
  feature-scoped, SUMMARY-ONLY (no artifact bodies). **No new unbounded or
  artifact-body read is introduced.** FAIL-SAFE: returns `None` when there is no
  pool, or the typed read raises (`try/except Exception`).
- `evidence_mode_for_snapshot(snapshot)` — derives the typed
  `SupervisorObservation.evidence_mode` from the snapshot's `source` /
  `degraded` (the exact doc-10 rule — see below).
- `_snapshot_attr` — a small Pydantic-model-or-dict attribute reader (mirrors
  `classifier._typed_attr` / `classifier_mapping._attr`).

**2. `supervisor/evidence.py` — `collect_evidence` (~`:102`).** After the
existing legacy `control_plane_snapshot = await _load_control_plane_snapshot(
...)`, added `typed_control_plane = await load_typed_control_plane_snapshot(
artifact_store, feature_id)` and `evidence_mode = evidence_mode_for_snapshot(
typed_control_plane)`. The returned `SupervisorObservation` now sets
`control_plane=typed_control_plane` and `evidence_mode=evidence_mode` (these
two fields were added in Slice 10c-2 and were NEVER set until now). The legacy
`control_plane_snapshot` dict field is UNCHANGED (kept — it still feeds the
classifier's legacy facts). `query_labels` gains `"control_plane_typed"`.

**3. `supervisor/mcp_server.py` — `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.
get_current_snapshot` (`:145`).** After the existing legacy `control_plane =
await self._control_plane_snapshot(scoped_feature_id)`, added `typed_control_
plane = await load_typed_control_plane_snapshot(self.artifact_store,
scoped_feature_id, fallback_pool_source=self.feature_store)` and `evidence_mode
= evidence_mode_for_snapshot(typed_control_plane)`. The returned dict gains
`"control_plane_typed"` (the typed snapshot `model_dump(mode="json")`, or
`None`) and `"evidence_mode"`. The legacy `"control_plane"` dict key is
UNCHANGED (additive).

### The `evidence_mode` rule (EXACT, from doc 10 step 4 + STATUS.md)

STATUS.md "Next safe action": "`evidence_mode` follows `source`" (doc 10
§ "Proposed Interfaces/Types" — the snapshot carries `source` / `degraded` /
`degradation_reasons`). `evidence_mode_for_snapshot`:
- typed snapshot, `source == "typed"` AND not `degraded` -> `evidence_mode =
  "typed"` — the normal path.
- typed snapshot, `degraded` (a partial degrade) OR any non-`typed`/
  non-`legacy_fallback` source (e.g. `"mixed"`) -> `evidence_mode = "mixed"`.
  doc 10 § "Edge Cases": "Typed query timeout: return `source="mixed"`,
  `degraded=true`" — and the Slice-10a builder also flags `degraded` on a
  per-section bounded timeout; `test_snapshots_store.py
  ::test_snapshot_query_degrades_on_timeout_without_raising` confirms a
  partially-degraded typed query sets `source="mixed"` + `degraded=True`.
- typed snapshot, `source == "legacy_fallback"` -> `evidence_mode =
  "legacy_fallback"`.
- **NO typed snapshot at all** (`load_typed_control_plane_snapshot` returned
  `None` — no pool, or the typed read raised) -> `evidence_mode =
  "legacy_fallback"`.

### How the typed-primary classifier path is now LIVE

`SupervisorClassifier.classify` (`classifier.py:66`) already runs
`_classify_typed_primary` FIRST; `_Context.is_typed_evidence` is
`evidence_mode == "typed" and control_plane is not None`. Until Slice 10e,
`evidence_mode` was ALWAYS `""` and `control_plane` ALWAYS `None`, so
`_classify_typed_primary` always returned `None` and the path was CORRECTLY
DORMANT. Slice 10e is the wiring that, for an observation collected against a
pool-backed store with present typed rows, sets `evidence_mode == "typed"` +
the typed `control_plane` — so `_classify_typed_primary` -> `classify_typed_
snapshot` now produces the verdict. The Slice-10c-2 typed-primary path is now
LIVE in production for typed-mode observations; the 8 legacy artifact
classifiers remain UNCHANGED as the fallback for `evidence_mode != "typed"`.

### The fail-safe (constraint #3) — fail-safe, never fail-open-to-wrong-mode

If the typed snapshot is UNAVAILABLE — no execution-control store/pool
(`artifact_store._pool` is `None`), or a store error (`get_control_plane_
snapshot` raises) — `load_typed_control_plane_snapshot` returns `None`, so
`evidence_mode_for_snapshot(None)` returns `"legacy_fallback"` — NEVER
`"typed"`. A degraded typed query is `"mixed"` — also NEVER `"typed"`. So a
missing / degraded typed snapshot can never silently activate the typed-primary
path; the classifier correctly falls back to the legacy artifact classifiers.
`evidence_mode == "typed"` is reachable ONLY for a present, non-degraded,
`source="typed"` snapshot.

### The bounded-read constraint + the read-only contract

- BOUNDED: the typed read is the **Slice-10a `get_control_plane_snapshot`** —
  already `LIMIT cap+1`-truncated, `SET LOCAL statement_timeout`-bounded,
  feature-scoped, SUMMARY-ONLY. No new unbounded or artifact-body read. The
  query is built with the un-mutated `SnapshotBudget` ceiling.
- READ-ONLY (Slice 10c-1): `collect_evidence` / `get_current_snapshot` are READ
  paths. `load_typed_control_plane_snapshot` constructs a transient
  `ExecutionControlStore` ONLY to invoke its READ method
  `get_control_plane_snapshot`; no writer is invoked. The Slice-10c-1
  mechanical contract is about the read-only supervisor SERVICE holding no
  `ExecutionControlStore` *slot* — `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService` still has NO
  such slot (`assert_read_only()` / `assert_read_only_supervisor_handles` are
  unchanged and still assert `execution_control_store=None`). A transient store
  built for one bounded READ inside a function call adds no slot and trips no
  `assert_no_control_plane_writer` (that guard inspects HELD handles). This is
  the same Slice-10a read path the Slice-10b dashboard already uses. Confirmed:
  `tests/supervisor/test_read_only.py` GREEN, unchanged.

### Interfaces / citations verified at file:line

- `supervisor/evidence.py:142` `collect_evidence` — the call site (per doc 10
  § "Current Code Citations" `evidence.py:102`).
- `supervisor/mcp_server.py:199` `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.
  get_current_snapshot` (per doc 10 § "Current Code Citations"
  `mcp_server.py:145`); FastMCP tool registration at `:670`.
- `execution_control/store.py:3810` `ExecutionControlStore.
  get_control_plane_snapshot` — the Slice-10a bounded typed snapshot read
  (transaction-wrapped `SET LOCAL statement_timeout` + `build_typed_control_
  plane_snapshot`).
- `execution_control/store.py:2264` `ExecutionControlStore.__init__(pool)`.
- `workflows/develop/execution/snapshots.py:180` `ControlPlaneSnapshotQuery`
  (validator clamps `budget` to the ceiling; `scope` is `SnapshotScope` —
  `"supervisor"` is a valid literal at `:97`).
- `workflows/develop/execution/snapshots.py:462` `ControlPlaneSnapshot` —
  `source: SnapshotSource` (`:478`), `degraded` (`:479`).
- `supervisor/models.py:228` `SupervisorObservation.control_plane: Any` +
  `:229` `evidence_mode: str` (added in Slice 10c-2, set for the first time
  here).
- `supervisor/classifier.py:89` `_classify_typed_primary`; `:892`
  `_Context.is_typed_evidence = evidence_mode == "typed" and typed_snapshot is
  not None`.
- `execution_control/store.py:315` legacy `fetch_control_plane_snapshot` —
  UNCHANGED; still consumed by the legacy `_load_control_plane_snapshot` /
  `_control_plane_snapshot`.

### doc-10 ambiguities + resolutions

1. **The Slice-10a `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot` always returns
   `source="typed"` (it never emits `legacy_fallback`/`mixed` as a top-level
   "no typed tables" verdict — it only sets `degraded` + `source="mixed"` on a
   per-section timeout).** doc 10 step 4 wants `evidence_mode` to be
   `legacy_fallback` for "old features with missing typed rows". RESOLUTION:
   `evidence_mode_for_snapshot` follows the doc-10 rule "`evidence_mode` follows
   `source`" AND treats `load_typed_control_plane_snapshot` returning `None`
   (no pool / a typed-read error — which is exactly the "typed tables absent"
   case for a feature with no execution-control rows, since the typed builder's
   SELECTs return empty / the read is unavailable) as `legacy_fallback`. A
   feature with genuinely no typed rows still gets a valid `source="typed"`
   empty snapshot from the builder, in which case `evidence_mode = "typed"` and
   `classify_typed_snapshot` returns `None` (no `latest_failures`) so the
   classifier ALREADY falls back — `_classify_typed_primary` returns `None` on a
   no-failure typed snapshot. Either way the legacy classifiers run for a
   no-evidence feature; the typed-primary path only ever fires when there is a
   real typed failure/merge-queue signal.
2. **doc 10 step 4 does not spell out WHERE the typed snapshot store/pool comes
   from for the supervisor.** The supervisor has no `ExecutionControlStore`
   slot (Slice 10c-1). RESOLUTION: resolve the pool off the artifact store's
   `_pool` (the legacy `_load_control_plane_snapshot` / `_control_plane_
   snapshot` already do exactly this) and construct a transient store for the
   single bounded READ — see "the read-only contract" above; this is sound and
   trips no Slice-10c-1 guard.
3. **`SnapshotScope` for the supervisor read.** doc 10 § "Proposed
   Interfaces/Types" defines `SnapshotScope = Literal["dashboard",
   "supervisor", "mcp"]` but the as-built `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot`
   does not branch on `scope`. RESOLUTION: pass `scope="supervisor"` (the
   semantically correct literal) for both `collect_evidence` and
   `get_current_snapshot` — both are the supervisor evidence path. (The MCP
   tool is part of the supervisor; doc-10's `"mcp"` scope value is not
   load-bearing in the as-built builder, so `"supervisor"` is the honest
   choice for the whole supervisor evidence collection.)

### Which `tests/supervisor/` tests were updated, and why NOT a weakening

**ZERO existing `tests/supervisor/` tests were updated.** All 351 baseline
supervisor tests pass UNCHANGED with the now-LIVE typed-primary path. This is
the CORRECT outcome, not a coverage gap:
- `test_classifier.py` / `test_classifier_mapping.py` construct
  `SupervisorObservation` with `evidence_mode` / `control_plane` set DIRECTLY
  (they never go through `collect_evidence`) — Slice 10e does not touch their
  inputs.
- The tests that DO go through `collect_evidence` (`test_evidence.py`,
  `test_agent.py` via `SupervisorApp.run_once`, `test_tools.py` via
  `get_current_snapshot`) all use fake artifact stores with **no `_pool`** —
  so `load_typed_control_plane_snapshot` returns `None`, `evidence_mode =
  "legacy_fallback"`, and the legacy artifact classifier runs EXACTLY as before
  the change. The behavior of every existing test is byte-for-byte identical.
Slice 10e activates the typed-primary path ONLY for an observation that
actually receives a typed snapshot; no existing test seeds a pool-backed store
into `collect_evidence`, so none changes behavior and none was weakened. The
genuinely-new path is covered by 7 NEW tests below.

### New tests (7, all in `tests/supervisor/test_evidence.py`)

- `test_evidence_mode_for_snapshot_follows_source` — the `evidence_mode` rule
  for all five `(source, degraded, None)` cases: `typed` -> `"typed"`;
  `mixed`+degraded -> `"mixed"`; `typed`+degraded -> `"mixed"` (the fail-safe
  demotion); `legacy_fallback` -> `"legacy_fallback"`; `None` -> `"legacy_
  fallback"` (the FAIL-SAFE — NEVER `typed`).
- `test_collect_evidence_legacy_fallback_when_no_typed_pool` — FAIL-SAFE: an
  artifact store with no `_pool` -> `control_plane is None`, `evidence_mode ==
  "legacy_fallback"`, the legacy artifact classifier still fires
  (`DETERMINISTIC_UNBLOCK`).
- `test_collect_evidence_typed_mode_activates_typed_primary_classifier` — the
  typed-primary path is LIVE: a pool-backed store + a `source="typed"` typed
  snapshot carrying a `checkpoint_contradiction` typed failure ->
  `evidence_mode == "typed"`, `control_plane is` the typed snapshot, and
  `classify_observation` returns `PIPELINE_BUG_SUSPECTED` / `STOP_ESCALATE`
  with `facts["mapping_row"] == "checkpoint_contradiction"` — the typed verdict
  OVERRIDES the legacy `dag-commit-failure` artifact (which alone classifies
  `deterministic_unblock`). Asserts the typed read is called with
  `scope="supervisor"`.
- `test_collect_evidence_fails_safe_when_typed_snapshot_read_raises` —
  FAIL-SAFE: `get_control_plane_snapshot` raises -> `control_plane is None`,
  `evidence_mode == "legacy_fallback"`, the legacy classifier fires
  (`DETERMINISTIC_UNBLOCK`) — never a typed verdict.
- `test_collect_evidence_degraded_typed_snapshot_is_mixed_not_typed` — a
  `source="mixed"` + `degraded` typed snapshot (even one carrying a
  `checkpoint_contradiction`) -> `evidence_mode == "mixed"`; the legacy
  classifier runs (`DETERMINISTIC_UNBLOCK`), the degraded typed failure does
  NOT drive the verdict.
- `test_get_current_snapshot_populates_typed_evidence_mode` — the MCP
  `get_current_snapshot` dict carries `control_plane_typed` (the typed
  snapshot) + `evidence_mode == "typed"`.
- `test_get_current_snapshot_fails_safe_without_typed_pool` — FAIL-SAFE: an
  MCP service whose artifact store has no `_pool` -> `control_plane_typed is
  None`, `evidence_mode == "legacy_fallback"`.

The typed-snapshot SQL builder itself is NOT re-tested here — it is owned by
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_snapshots_store.py` (43 Slice-10a
tests, GREEN at 683); the new tests monkeypatch `ExecutionControlStore.
get_control_plane_snapshot` so they exercise the genuine Slice-10e WIRING seam
(`collect_evidence`/`get_current_snapshot` -> `load_typed_control_plane_
snapshot` -> `evidence_mode_for_snapshot` -> the typed-primary classifier)
without re-testing the bounded SQL.

### Test results

- `python -m pytest tests/supervisor/ -q -p no:randomly` -> **358 passed**
  (351 baseline + 7 new Slice-10e tests; 0 pre-existing tests removed/weakened).
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q -p no:randomly` ->
  **683 passed** (the Slice-10a snapshot store suite included; real-Postgres
  `localhost:5431` was REACHABLE — 0 skips).
- Hygiene: `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` clean;
  `git diff --check` clean.

### P1/P2-risk areas for the reviewer

- **The genuine behavior change — the typed-primary classifier is now LIVE.**
  KEY review vector (supervisor/dashboard safety): for a typed-mode observation
  the typed verdict now OVERRIDES the legacy artifact classifiers. Confirm the
  typed verdict is correct (it routes through the already-reviewed Slice-10c-2
  `classify_typed_snapshot`) and that the legacy fallback STILL works for old
  features (`evidence_mode != "typed"`).
- **The fail-safe.** Confirm a typed-snapshot outage / a degraded typed query
  never yields `evidence_mode == "typed"` — `evidence_mode_for_snapshot`
  returns `legacy_fallback` for `None` and `mixed` for any degraded/non-typed
  source. Fail-safe, not fail-open-to-wrong-mode.
- **The read-only contract.** `load_typed_control_plane_snapshot` constructs a
  transient `ExecutionControlStore` — confirm it invokes ONLY the READ method
  `get_control_plane_snapshot` and that `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService` still
  holds NO `ExecutionControlStore` slot (the Slice-10c-1 mechanical guarantee
  is unchanged).
- **The bounded read.** Confirm the typed read is the Slice-10a bounded path
  (no new unbounded / artifact-body read); the `ControlPlaneSnapshotQuery`
  budget is the un-mutated ceiling.
- **P3 (non-blocking): the `scope="supervisor"` literal** — the as-built
  `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot` does not branch on `scope`, so the
  literal is presently documentary; if a later slice makes `scope` load-bearing
  the supervisor read is already correctly labelled.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10e REVIEW (the doc-10 step-4 `evidence_mode` wiring)

**Reviewer summary.** Independent review of Slice 10e — the doc-10 § "Refactoring
Steps" step-4 wiring that makes `collect_evidence` (`supervisor/evidence.py`) and
`SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.get_current_snapshot` (`supervisor/mcp_server.py`)
read the typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot` FIRST and populate
`SupervisorObservation.control_plane` + `evidence_mode`, taking the Slice-10c-2
typed-PRIMARY classifier path LIVE for `evidence_mode == "typed"` observations.
Reviewed the working-tree state of `supervisor/evidence.py` (MODIFIED),
`supervisor/mcp_server.py` (NEW per `git status`), and the 7 new
`tests/supervisor/test_evidence.py` tests. **VERDICT: CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 1 new
P3.** Targeted suites GREEN: `tests/supervisor/` 358 passed (351 baseline + 7);
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/` 683 passed (real Postgres `localhost:5431`
reachable, 0 skips); `python -m compileall` + `git diff --check` clean.

**Vector 1 — the `evidence_mode` rule (KEY VECTOR) — PASS.**
`evidence_mode_for_snapshot` (`evidence.py:753`) implements the doc-10 step-4
rule exactly: `"typed"` ONLY when `source == "typed"` AND `not degraded`;
`"legacy_fallback"` when the snapshot is `None` (the typed read was unavailable)
OR its own `source == "legacy_fallback"`; `"mixed"` for a degraded snapshot or
any other source. A `source="typed"` snapshot that ALSO flagged itself
`degraded` is correctly demoted to `"mixed"` (the explicit fail-safe arm at
`evidence.py:783`) — it never reaches `"typed"`. The rule never sets `"typed"`
for a degraded or unavailable snapshot.

**Vector 2 — fail-safe (KEY VECTOR) — PASS.** `load_typed_control_plane_snapshot`
(`evidence.py:694`) returns `None` when there is no Postgres pool
(`artifact_store._pool` / `fallback_pool_source._pool` both absent) and wraps
the `ExecutionControlStore.get_control_plane_snapshot` call in a blanket
`except Exception` that returns `None` on ANY typed-read failure (missing typed
tables, a store error, a `statement_timeout` cancel, a degenerate plan). A
`None` typed read → `evidence_mode == "legacy_fallback"` → the classifier's
typed-primary branch is gated OFF and the legacy artifact classifiers run.
Confirmed fail-SAFE to legacy, not fail-open-to-wrong-mode. A typed-snapshot
outage NEVER yields `evidence_mode == "typed"`. Independently re-confirmed
end-to-end: with no `_pool`, `collect_evidence` yields `control_plane is None`
+ `evidence_mode == "legacy_fallback"`; a `get_control_plane_snapshot` that
raises yields the same.

**Vector 3 — the typed-primary classifier is now LIVE — PASS.** With
`evidence_mode == "typed"` and a non-`None` typed snapshot, `_Context`
(`classifier.py:892`) sets `is_typed_evidence = True` and `SupervisorClassifier.
classify` runs `_classify_typed_primary` FIRST; the 8 legacy artifact
classifiers run UNCHANGED only as the `else` fallback. Verified: a typed-mode
observation carrying a `checkpoint_contradiction` typed failure routes through
`classify_typed_snapshot` → MAPPING_ROWS row `checkpoint_contradiction`
(priority 1) → `PIPELINE_BUG_SUSPECTED` / `STOP_ESCALATE`, OVERRIDING the legacy
`dag-commit-failure` artifact (which alone classifies `deterministic_unblock`).
No false operator escalation (the `classifier_mapping.py` operator-escalation
rule honours `operator_required` only with an explicit typed route/flag and no
deterministic-repair-with-budget). A non-typed observation (`evidence_mode !=
"typed"`) leaves `is_typed_evidence = False` so `_classify_typed_primary`
returns `None` and the legacy classifiers run byte-for-byte unchanged.

**Vector 4 — bounded + read-only — PASS.** `load_typed_control_plane_snapshot`
calls the Slice-10a bounded `ExecutionControlStore.get_control_plane_snapshot`
→ `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot`: every read is feature-scoped,
keyset-indexed, `LIMIT cap+1`-truncated, summary-only, and wrapped in a
`SET LOCAL statement_timeout` transaction — no new unbounded / artifact-body
read. `ControlPlaneSnapshotQuery._clamp_budget_to_ceiling` clamps every budget
field DOWN to `_BUDGET_CEILING`; the supervisor passes the default budget, so
the read uses the un-mutated ceiling. The transient `ExecutionControlStore`
constructed inside the helper is a pure local (never assigned to a `self.`
attribute), lives only for the single `get_control_plane_snapshot` READ, and
`ExecutionControlStore.__init__` is just `self._pool = pool` (no side effects).
`SupervisorEvidenceMcpService` still holds NO `ExecutionControlStore` slot —
`assert_read_only` passes `execution_control_store=None` at `__init__` and
re-asserts after the lazy `_ensure_stores`. The transient store is never a
"held handle", so it cannot trip the Slice-10c-1 read-only contract; only the
READ method is invoked.

**Vector 5 — test honesty (KEY VECTOR) — PASS with a P3.** The 7 new
`test_evidence.py` tests genuinely exercise the new typed path end-to-end:
`test_collect_evidence_typed_mode_activates_typed_primary_classifier` confirms a
`source="typed"` snapshot → `evidence_mode == "typed"` AND
`classify_observation` produces the typed `PIPELINE_BUG_SUSPECTED`/`STOP_ESCALATE`
verdict with `facts["mapping_row"] == "checkpoint_contradiction"` (not just the
field being set) AND asserts `scope == "supervisor"` on the typed read; the
fail-safe fallbacks (`no _pool`, `get_control_plane_snapshot` raises, a degraded
`source="mixed"` snapshot) are each tested and each confirm the legacy verdict
fires. HOWEVER: the implementer's journal claim "no existing test seeds a
pool-backed store into `collect_evidence`/`get_current_snapshot`, so legacy
behavior is byte-identical" is **factually inaccurate** — `tests/supervisor/
test_tools.py` `_ArtifactStore` carries `self._pool = _Pool()`, and
`test_tools.py::test_supervisor_evidence_mcp_service_exposes_read_only_tools`
(`:338`) passes that pool-backed store to `get_current_snapshot`. The
new typed-path code DOES run in that test. The implementer's *conclusion* (no
test weakened, every existing assertion preserved) still HOLDS — `test_tools.py`
asserts nothing on `evidence_mode`/`control_plane_typed`, and the suite is GREEN
at 358 — but the stated reasoning is wrong and the implementer did not notice
the typed path runs there. **P3-10e-1 — see below.**

**Vector 6 — doc-10 conformance — the resolutions are SOUND.** (a) `None` typed
read → `legacy_fallback`: sound — a feature with genuinely no typed rows yields
a real `source="legacy_fallback"` snapshot from the builder (confirmed by the
real-Postgres `test_snapshot_source_is_legacy_fallback_when_no_typed_rows`), and
a `None` (no pool / read error) is the doc-10 "typed tables absent" case;
either way the classifier falls back. (b) pool off `artifact_store._pool` (and
`fallback_pool_source._pool` for the MCP path): sound — mirrors the legacy
`_load_control_plane_snapshot` / `_control_plane_snapshot` resolution exactly;
trips no Slice-10c-1 guard. (c) `scope="supervisor"`: sound — the semantically
correct `SnapshotScope` literal; `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot` does not
branch on `scope` today, so it is documentary, already tracked as the
implementer's own P3.

**P3-10e-1 (NEW, non-blocking) — journal-honesty / latent test-fake gap.** The
Slice-10e IMPLEMENTATION journal asserts "no existing test seeds a pool-backed
store into `collect_evidence`/`get_current_snapshot`"; `tests/supervisor/
test_tools.py::test_supervisor_evidence_mcp_service_exposes_read_only_tools`
(`:338`) DOES — `_ArtifactStore` has `self._pool = _Pool()`. The `_Pool` fake is
a degenerate stub: it ignores the SQL and returns `[{"value": 1}]` for every
non-`FROM artifacts` query. Against that fake `build_typed_control_plane_snapshot`
does not raise — its tolerant `_record_get` coercion turns the garbage row into
a fully-populated `ControlPlaneSnapshot` with `source="typed"`, `degraded=False`
and synthetic default rows, so `evidence_mode == "typed"` and the typed-primary
classifier runs (it then returns `HEALTHY_PROGRESS` off the synthetic
`merge_queue` `status="queued"` row). This is NOT a P1/P2 production fail-safe
hole: the `[{"value": 1}]`-for-any-query behavior is physically impossible
against real Postgres (the snapshot SELECTs name explicit columns; a real
empty-feature read returns `[]` → `typed_present=False` → `source="legacy_
fallback"`, proven by the real-Postgres `test_snapshot_source_is_legacy_
fallback_when_no_typed_rows`). It is a P3 because (i) the journal's factual
claim is wrong and (ii) the `test_tools.py` fake `_Pool` is unrealistic enough
that the typed-primary path silently runs against degenerate evidence in that
test without anyone noticing. Fix at the slice-end six-vector review: either
give `test_tools.py`'s `_Pool` a non-pool artifact store (so the typed path is
genuinely skipped, matching the journal's intent) or make the fake return `[]`
for the typed-snapshot SELECTs. The production fail-safe is genuine and
unaffected; carried as non-blocking.

**Verdict on the three KEY vectors:** Vector 1 (the `evidence_mode` rule) —
PASS. Vector 2 (fail-safe) — PASS: a typed outage / degraded query never yields
`evidence_mode == "typed"`. Vector 5 (the typed path genuinely tested) — PASS:
the 7 new tests exercise the new typed path end-to-end including the live
typed-primary verdict; the one journal-prose inaccuracy is P3-10e-1, non-blocking.
**Slice 10e REVIEW: CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 1 new non-blocking P3 (P3-10e-1).**

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10e COMPLETE (the doc-10 step-4 `evidence_mode` wiring)

Slice 10e is COMPLETE. The doc-10 § "Refactoring Steps" step-4 `evidence_mode`
wiring is landed: `collect_evidence` (`supervisor/evidence.py`) and
`SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.get_current_snapshot` (`supervisor/mcp_server.py`)
read the Slice-10a bounded typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot` FIRST via the new
`load_typed_control_plane_snapshot` helper and populate
`SupervisorObservation.control_plane` + `evidence_mode`
(`evidence_mode_for_snapshot`). The Slice-10c-2 typed-PRIMARY classifier path
is now LIVE for `evidence_mode == "typed"` observations — it was correctly
DORMANT until this seam. The fail-safe is genuine: a missing pool, a typed-read
error, or a degraded typed query yields `evidence_mode` of `legacy_fallback` /
`mixed` (NEVER `typed`), and the classifier stays on the legacy artifact path.
The independent Slice 10e REVIEW returned CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2 — across all six
review vectors, with explicit PASS verdicts on the three KEY vectors (the
`evidence_mode` rule; the fail-safe; the typed path genuinely tested
end-to-end). 7 new tests (`tests/supervisor/test_evidence.py`); ZERO existing
tests removed or weakened. `tests/supervisor/` GREEN at 358; `tests/workflows/
develop/execution/` GREEN at 683 (real Postgres reachable, 0 skips); hygiene
clean. One carried non-blocking P3 (P3-10e-1 — a `test_tools.py` fake-pool /
journal-honesty gap; the production fail-safe is unaffected). **Slice 10 — 10a
+ 10b + 10c (10c-1 + 10c-2) + 10d (10d-1 + 10d-2) + 10e COMPLETE; Slice 10f is
next.**

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10f IMPLEMENTATION (the public-dashboard `control_plane.snapshot_changed` outbox projection + the startup/deployment assertions)

Slice 10f delivers the two remaining doc-10 § "Refactoring Steps" steps before
the atomic landing: **step 9** — the public-dashboard `control_plane.snapshot_
changed` outbox projection — and **step 10** — the startup/deployment readiness
assertions. Both landed in this one iteration as **two distinct, individually-
verified sub-deliverables** (step 9 first, then step 10) — see "Split decision"
below.

### Split decision

STATUS.md flagged Slice 10f as a possible split ("the public-outbox projection
(step 9) and the startup/deployment assertions (step 10) are two distinct
seams"). I implemented and verified them as **two ordered sub-deliverables in
one iteration**: step 9 (the outbox projection) was implemented + tested +
hygiene-clean FIRST and is a self-contained verified chunk; step 10 (the startup
assertions) was then implemented + tested as a separate seam — and step 10's
`public_outbox_projection` check literally asserts the step-9 symbols exist, so
sequencing step 9 first is the natural order. Neither seam is large: step 9 is
one bounded projection method + one driver + a payload builder in
`public_dashboard.py`; step 10 is one new leaf module `execution_control/
startup.py`. Each was a clean journaled-quality checkpoint on its own; bundling
two small fully-verified additive seams in one loop iteration did not produce a
shaky chunk. If either had grown, the cut line was the file boundary
(`public_dashboard.py` vs `startup.py`).

### Step 9 — the public-dashboard `control_plane.snapshot_changed` outbox projection

**What I built (additive — `public_dashboard.py`):**

- `control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload(snapshot)` — builds the BOUNDED
  SUMMARY-ONLY display payload. Takes the Slice-10a typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot`
  (Pydantic model OR a `model_dump(mode="json")` dict — `_snapshot_as_dict`
  normalises both). doc 10 § "Dashboard Integration Points" specifies the event
  carries `feature_id`, `snapshot_version`, "visible counters", `route`, and
  "cited evidence refs" and "does not publish private evidence bodies" — the
  builder projects EXACTLY that: `feature_id`, `snapshot_version`,
  `generated_at`, `source`, `degraded` + bounded `degradation_reasons`,
  `truncated`, `omitted_counts`, `active_group_idx`, `recommended_route`,
  `recommended_action`, a `counters` dict (every typed summary list — active
  attempts / workspace snapshots / latest failures / merge queue / retry
  budgets / sandbox leases / runtime bindings / gates / checkpoints / evidence
  refs — reduced to its `len()`, NEVER the list body), and a bounded
  `evidence_refs` list. Every typed summary LIST is dropped to a count; only
  `EvidenceRef` citations cross the boundary, and each `EvidenceRef` is reduced
  by `_summary_only_evidence_ref` to `table` + `id` + `citation` + `kind` ONLY
  — the `EvidenceRef.summary` preview and `artifact_key` are NOT projected.
- `control_plane_snapshot_event_id(feature_id, snapshot_version)` — the doc-10
  idempotency key: a sha256 over `(feature_id, snapshot_version,
  "control_plane.snapshot_changed")` (doc 10 § "Dashboard Integration Points":
  "Public outbox writes use `(feature_id, snapshot_version,
  "control_plane.snapshot_changed")` as the idempotency key").
- `PublicDashboardOutbox.project_control_plane_snapshot_changed(*, feature_id,
  snapshot, conn=None)` — the projection method. It `INSERT … ON CONFLICT
  (event_id) DO NOTHING` into `public_dashboard_outbox` with `event_type=
  "control_plane.snapshot_changed"`, `visibility="public"`, the bounded payload
  (additionally run through the existing `_bounded_payload` byte cap). Re-
  projecting the SAME typed snapshot version reuses the SAME `event_id` →
  `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` → no duplicate public notification.
- `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed(outbox, snapshot_store,
  feature_id, *, previous_snapshot_version, scope, conn)` — the module-level
  driver that is the doc-10 "fires when the typed snapshot version advances"
  trigger. It reads the Slice-10a typed `get_control_plane_snapshot_version`; if
  it equals `previous_snapshot_version` it is a NO-OP returning `None` (no
  public event, and the bounded snapshot read is SKIPPED); only when the
  version differs does it read the bounded typed `get_control_plane_snapshot`
  and project.

**The version-advance trigger:** the Slice-10a typed
`ExecutionControlStore.get_control_plane_snapshot_version` (`store.py:3836` →
`compute_typed_snapshot_version` → `control_plane_snapshot_version` over the 8
typed-table cursors) — a stable digest over typed max-ids/max-`updated_at` that
advances on a budget-only or sandbox-only update and NEVER on artifact-body
churn. `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` compares it to the caller's
prior version and only enqueues on advance.

**How the event stays bounded / summary-only:** (a) every typed summary list is
reduced to a `counters` integer — no list body crosses the public boundary;
(b) cited `EvidenceRef`s are bounded to `CONTROL_PLANE_EVENT_MAX_EVIDENCE_REFS`
(12) entries and each is reduced to id+citation+kind+table (the `summary`
preview and `artifact_key` are dropped); (c) every single text field is clipped
to `CONTROL_PLANE_EVENT_MAX_TEXT_CHARS` (240) by `_clip_event_text`;
(d) `degradation_reasons` is bounded to 8 entries; (e) the whole payload still
passes through the existing `PublicDashboardOutbox._bounded_payload` byte cap.
The typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot` is itself already a SUMMARY-ONLY contract
(Slice 10a — no artifact bodies / prompts / stdout / full dirty-path lists),
so the projection composes two bounding layers. A test seeds 40 000-char body
strings into the typed summary rows and asserts the string appears NOWHERE in
the serialized event.

**FAIL-CLOSED (doc 10 § "Edge Cases"):** `project_control_plane_snapshot_
changed` has NO `except` around the `INSERT` — a configured-outbox ENQUEUE
failure PROPAGATES (it raises), unlike the sibling best-effort `emit_event` /
`mirror_*` methods which deliberately swallow+log. doc 10: "Public outbox
enqueue failure while the outbox is configured is not ignorable: it fails the
projection transaction so typed snapshot, cursor/ETag compatibility state, and
outbox row cannot diverge." The `conn` parameter lets the caller run the
enqueue inside its own projection transaction so the fail-closed guarantee is
transactional. A disabled outbox (`outbox_enabled=False`) or `outbox is None`
is a documented NO-OP (`None`) — there is no configured outbox to keep
consistent. A snapshot with an EMPTY `snapshot_version` raises `ValueError`
(nothing to key idempotency on — fail closed rather than enqueue an
un-deduplicable public event). Async DELIVERY failure AFTER a committed enqueue
remains log-and-continue (the consumer's concern).

### Step 10 — the startup/deployment assertions

**What I built (new additive leaf module `execution_control/startup.py`):**
`assert_control_plane_ready(*, pool=None, action_policy=None)` and the non-
raising `control_plane_readiness_report(...)` + `ControlPlaneReadinessReport`
+ `ControlPlaneStartupError`. The guard checks the six doc-10 step-10
components plus the two STATUS.md "Next safe action" additions, and FAILS
CLOSED — raises `ControlPlaneStartupError` naming every missing dependency:

1. **snapshot store** — `ExecutionControlStore.get_control_plane_snapshot` +
   `get_control_plane_snapshot_version` are callable.
2. **dashboard route** — `dashboard.get_feature_control_plane` (the
   `/api/feature/{feature_id}/control-plane` handler) + `_typed_control_plane_
   snapshot` are present.
3. **classifier mapping** — `classifier_mapping.MAPPING_ROWS` (non-empty),
   `CLASSIFIER_PRIORITY`, `classify_typed_snapshot` are present AND
   `coverage_report().ok` is true (the doc-10 § "Supervisor Classifier Mapping"
   coverage rule — every canonical `FailureClass` mapped exactly once).
4. **MCP snapshot path** — `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.get_current_snapshot`
   is callable.
5. **Slack dedupe store** — `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore` importable with
   `decide` / `record_sent` / `record_suppressed`; AND (when a live `pool` is
   supplied) the two supervisor-owned tables `supervisor_digest_state` /
   `supervisor_digest_audit` exist (`to_regclass` NULL == absent).
6. **public outbox projection** — the step-9 `PublicDashboardOutbox.project_
   control_plane_snapshot_changed` + `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed`
   are present; AND (with a pool) `public_dashboard_outbox` exists.
7. **(STATUS.md 10c-1 landing-seam check)** — when an `ActionPolicy` is
   supplied, its `execution_authority` is `None` AND its `artifact_sink`, when
   set, is a `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` (the production read-only wiring hands
   the audit-scoped sink to `ActionPolicy`).
8. **(STATUS.md re-confirm)** — no new feature-timeline writer caller appears
   under `supervisor/`: a static scan of every `supervisor/*.py` for a
   `.transition_phase(` / `.update_metadata(` / `.log_event(` token.

**How each FAILS CLOSED:** every component check appends a clear, named string
to a `missing` list; `assert_control_plane_ready` calls `raise_if_not_ready`
which raises `ControlPlaneStartupError` ("typed control-plane mode is enabled
but required dependencies are missing — refusing to serve …") listing every
miss. There is no degrade path — doc 10 step 10's intent is a hard startup
failure, never a silent fallback onto legacy artifact inference. A failed
import IS treated as the missing dependency (the `except` records it, does not
swallow it). The schema-presence checks run only when a `pool` is supplied; a
purely-static deployment check still runs and a static check still proves the
code path exists.

**Step 10 is the MECHANISM; Slice 12 wires it.** doc 10 § "Rollout/Rollback
Notes": "production typed mode requires the startup assertions above to pass
before serving dashboard/MCP/supervisor/Slack traffic." There is no
`IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` env flag yet — Slice 12 owns that switch and
will call `assert_control_plane_ready` at the real process entrypoint behind
it. Shipping the assertion now as a callable mechanism with no production
caller exactly mirrors how Slice 10c-1 shipped `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`
(also a mechanism for a later landing seam).

### doc-10 ambiguities + resolutions

1. **"the public-dashboard outbox projection" vs the EXISTING legacy
   `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox` (`store.py:5199`).** The uncommitted
   Slice-00–10e bundle ALREADY contains a `control_plane.snapshot_changed`
   outbox writer in `store.py` — `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox`, wired
   at 4 store call sites — but it is a **pre-Slice-10 LEGACY path**: it dumps
   the WHOLE legacy dict `ControlPlaneSnapshot` (`store.py:262` `@dataclass`)
   via `snapshot.model_dump(mode="json")` — its payload carries the full
   `attempts` / `runtime_failures` / `verification_graph_nodes` / `projection_
   refs` LISTS (the existing `test_projection_emits_idempotent_control_plane_
   snapshot_changed_outbox` even asserts `payload["attempts"][0]` and
   `payload["projection_refs"][0]`), and it keys idempotency on the LEGACY
   `snapshot_version` (a digest over that entire material dict, `store.py:698`),
   NOT the Slice-10a typed `get_control_plane_snapshot_version`. doc 10 step 9
   / § "Dashboard Integration Points" specifies a BOUNDED SUMMARY event
   (`feature_id` + `snapshot_version` + visible counters + route + cited
   evidence refs) keyed on the TYPED version. **Resolution:** this is the same
   situation as the Slice-10b P2-10b-1 reconciliation (a pre-Slice-10 dict-
   based path in the same bundle that doc 10 supersedes). The orchestrator
   scoped 10f explicitly: the projection lives in `public_dashboard.py`, fires
   on the TYPED `get_control_plane_snapshot_version` advance, with the doc-10
   bounded payload — and "add new code, do not gratuitously refactor". So 10f
   adds the doc-10-correct projection in `public_dashboard.py` as NEW additive
   code; the legacy `store.py` method (wired + tested) is LEFT UNTOUCHED per
   `feedback_no_refactor` (ripping out a wired+tested path is a refactor, and
   out of 10f scope). **P1-RISK FLAG for the reviewer / Slice 10g atomic
   landing:** the legacy `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox` and the new
   `public_dashboard.project_control_plane_snapshot_changed` are TWO outbox
   writers for the SAME `event_type="control_plane.snapshot_changed"` keyed on
   DIFFERENT versions and with DIFFERENT payload shapes (legacy = full dict;
   new = bounded summary). The doc-10 atomic-landing seam (Slice 10g, step 11)
   MUST reconcile them to ONE — exactly as Slice 10b reconciled the dict-based
   `/control-plane` route. They do NOT collide on `event_id` (different
   `snapshot_version` digests → different sha256 keys), so they cannot corrupt
   each other's idempotency, but a public consumer would see two differently-
   shaped events for one logical change. Recommended 10g resolution: rewire the
   4 legacy call sites onto the typed projection (or delete the legacy method)
   once the typed snapshot is THE control-plane contract. Recorded as a Slice
   10g atomic-landing obligation, NOT a 10f defect — 10f shipped the doc-10-
   correct bounded projection as instructed.
2. **"when typed control-plane mode is enabled" (step 10) — what is the flag?**
   No `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` env switch exists yet; doc 12 +
   STATUS.md's "Remaining" both place that switch in **Slice 12**. **Resolution:**
   10f ships `assert_control_plane_ready` as the callable assertion MECHANISM
   (with no production caller); Slice 12 wires it at the entrypoint behind the
   env flag. This is the documented 10c-1 landing-seam pattern (10c-1 shipped
   `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` as a mechanism; the wiring is a later seam).
3. **The STATUS.md "no new feature-timeline writer caller under `supervisor/`"
   scan scope.** `read_only.FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS` includes the
   DUAL-USE `put` (the doc-10 "Allowed writes" supervisor-owned audit write
   surface — call-site-enforced through `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`) and the
   generic `create`. A naive `.put(`/`.create(` token scan FALSE-POSITIVES on
   the sanctioned `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink.put` / `ActionPolicy` audit-sink
   path (the current known-clean tree has 4 such `.put(` hits) and on unrelated
   `*.create(` factory calls. **Resolution:** the scan is scoped to the
   UNAMBIGUOUS FeatureStore timeline mutators — `transition_phase` /
   `update_metadata` / `log_event` — which have NO sanctioned supervisor use
   (verified: 0 occurrences under `supervisor/` today). The dual-use ArtifactStore
   `put` / `write_artifact_bytes` / `delete` are covered instead by the
   `read_only_action_policy` check (the audit-sink wiring). This is documented
   in `startup.py` `_SUPERVISOR_FORBIDDEN_FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITERS`.

### P1/P2-risk areas for the reviewer

- **P1-RISK (the dominant one) — TWO `control_plane.snapshot_changed` outbox
  writers.** See doc-10 ambiguity #1. The legacy `store.py:5199 _insert_
  control_plane_snapshot_outbox` (full-dict payload, legacy version, 4 wired
  call sites) and the new `public_dashboard.project_control_plane_snapshot_
  changed` (bounded summary, typed version) both write `event_type=
  "control_plane.snapshot_changed"`. They do not collide on `event_id` and
  cannot corrupt each other, but the Slice 10g atomic-landing seam MUST
  reconcile them to one (rewire the 4 legacy call sites onto the typed
  projection, or delete the legacy method) — analogous to the Slice-10b
  P2-10b-1 `/control-plane` route reconciliation. 10f deliberately did NOT do
  this (out of scope; ripping out a wired+tested path is a refactor) — flagged
  here as a Slice 10g obligation.
- **P2-RISK — the new doc-10 projection has no production caller yet.** Like
  the step-10 assertion, `project_control_plane_snapshot_changed` /
  `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` ship as a MECHANISM. The Slice
  10g atomic landing must wire the driver to fire (the natural seam: the same
  4 store sites the legacy method runs at, or the dashboard ETag path) so the
  public dashboard actually receives the bounded typed event. Until then the
  legacy full-dict event is what a public consumer sees — which is precisely
  why the 10g reconciliation (P1-RISK above) is required, not optional.
- **P3 — the feature-timeline-writer scan is textual.** `_check_no_supervisor_
  feature_timeline_writer` is a `.method(` substring scan, scoped to the 3
  unambiguous FeatureStore timeline mutators. It is conservative-correct (a
  hit flags review; the tree is clean today) but is not AST-precise — a
  `transition_phase` used as a non-FeatureStore method name would false-
  positive. Acceptable as a regression tripwire; an AST check is a later
  cleanup if ever needed.
- The bounded payload's correctness depends on the Slice-10a typed
  `ControlPlaneSnapshot` already being summary-only — which Slice 10a's review
  established. The projection adds a second bounding layer (counts + ref caps +
  text clips + the byte cap) so even a future un-bounded summary field cannot
  leak a body onto the public surface.

**Slice 10 — 10a + 10b + 10c (10c-1 + 10c-2) + 10d (10d-1 + 10d-2) + 10e + 10f
COMPLETE; Slice 10g (the atomic landing + the full doc-10 test matrix + the
slice-end six-vector review + formal Slice 10 acceptance) is next.** 10f gets
its own independent review (the KEY vector — supervisor/dashboard safety: the
outbox payload is bounded with no private evidence body; the idempotency key
prevents duplicate public notifications; the enqueue is fail-closed; the
startup assertions fail closed).

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10f REVIEW (the public-dashboard `control_plane.snapshot_changed` outbox projection + the doc-10 step-10 startup assertions)

Independent review of the Slice 10f IMPLEMENTATION (doc 10 § "Refactoring
Steps" steps 9–10). Review target: working-tree `public_dashboard.py`
(`control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload`, `control_plane_snapshot_event_id`,
`project_control_plane_snapshot_changed`, `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_
changed`), the NEW `execution_control/startup.py`, and the 28 new tests
(`tests/test_public_dashboard.py` +12, `tests/test_execution_control_startup.py`
16).

**Reviewer summary.** Slice 10f is a CLEAN, doc-10-faithful, purely-additive
delivery of step 9 + step 10. Six review vectors run; 0 P1, 0 P2 that blocks
finalization, 1 P3 (P3-10f-1, the textual feature-timeline scan — non-blocking,
already self-flagged). The implementer's own "P1-RISK" two-writer flag is
CORRECTLY a concern but is downgraded here to a **non-blocking P2 with a
hard-tracked Slice-10g obligation** — see the vector-3 verdict — because the
legacy writer ships NO external-facing event today (`visibility='internal'`,
no production caller). One factual correction to the implementer's journal is
recorded (vector 3). VERDICT: **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2-blocking; finalize 10f,
advance to 10g.**

**Vector 1 — the outbox projection is bounded / summary-only — PASS (explicit
verdict).** `control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload` (`public_dashboard.py:583`)
projects EXACTLY the doc-10 § "Dashboard Integration Points" shape: `feature_
id`, `snapshot_version`, `generated_at`, `source`, `degraded` + bounded
`degradation_reasons`, `truncated`, `omitted_counts`, `active_group_idx`,
`recommended_route`, `recommended_action`, a `counters` dict, a bounded
`evidence_refs` list, `projected_at`. EVERY typed summary list (`active_
attempts`, `workspace_snapshots`, `latest_failures`, `merge_queue`, `retry_
budgets`, `sandbox_leases`, `runtime_bindings`, `gates`, `checkpoints`,
`evidence_refs`) is reduced to its `len()` via `_count` (`:599`) — NO list body
crosses the boundary. `_summary_only_evidence_ref` (`:559`) reduces each
`EvidenceRef` to `table`+`id`+`citation`+`kind` ONLY — the `EvidenceRef.summary`
preview and `artifact_key` are DROPPED. Three bounding layers compose: (a) lists
→ counts; (b) `evidence_refs` capped at `CONTROL_PLANE_EVENT_MAX_EVIDENCE_REFS`
(12), `degradation_reasons` at 8; (c) every text field clipped to
`CONTROL_PLANE_EVENT_MAX_TEXT_CHARS` (240) by `_clip_event_text` (`:550`); the
whole payload still passes through the pre-existing `_bounded_payload` byte cap
(`:461`). NO artifact body / prompt / stdout/stderr / verifier body / full
dirty-path list / unbounded field crosses the public boundary. The payload
shape matches doc 10 § "Dashboard Integration Points" exactly. No P1, no P2.

**Vector 2 — the projection trigger + idempotency + fail-closed — PASS.**
`project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` (`:672`) reads the Slice-10a typed
`get_control_plane_snapshot_version` FIRST (`:707`); when it equals
`previous_snapshot_version` it returns `None` and SKIPS the bounded snapshot
read entirely (`:710-711`; the test `…_is_noop_when_version_unchanged` asserts
`store.snapshot_calls == 0`); only on a genuine advance does it read the bounded
`get_control_plane_snapshot` and project. The idempotency key
`control_plane_snapshot_event_id` (`:656`) is a sha256 over `(feature_id,
snapshot_version, "control_plane.snapshot_changed")` — exactly the doc-10 key;
`project_control_plane_snapshot_changed` does `INSERT … ON CONFLICT (event_id)
DO NOTHING` (`:294-300`) so a racing double-projection of one advanced version
enqueues exactly one public event. FAIL-CLOSED is genuine: there is NO `except`
around the `INSERT` (`:293` comment is explicit) — a configured-outbox enqueue
failure PROPAGATES (unlike the sibling best-effort `emit_event`/`mirror_*` which
swallow+log), so the caller's projection transaction aborts and the typed
snapshot / cursor-ETag state / outbox row cannot diverge (doc 10 § "Edge
Cases"); the `conn` param makes the guarantee transactional. An empty
`snapshot_version` raises `ValueError` (`:286`) — fail closed rather than
enqueue an un-deduplicable event. A disabled/`None` outbox is a documented
NO-OP. Both fail-closed paths and the no-op paths are tested. No P1, no P2.

**Vector 3 — THE KEY JUDGMENT — the two `control_plane.snapshot_changed`
writers — EXPLICIT VERDICT: acceptable 10f state; non-blocking P2 with a
hard-tracked 10g obligation; one factual correction to the implementer's
journal.** The `git show HEAD` finding is decisive and CORRECTS the
implementer's framing: the implementer's journal (ambiguity #1) and the
"P1-RISK" flag both describe `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox`
(`store.py:5199`) as a "**pre-Slice-10 LEGACY path**". That label is
**factually wrong**. `git show HEAD:src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/
store.py` returns nothing — `store.py` is UNTRACKED (`git status` shows `??`),
and `git ls-tree HEAD src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/` is empty: the
ENTIRE `execution_control/` package is absent from HEAD. The legacy writer is
**same-bundle uncommitted code** from the Slice 00–10e bundle — exactly the
situation of the Slice-10b P2-10b-1 dict-based `/control-plane` route. Per this
reviewer's vector-3 mandate ("if the legacy writer is itself unlanded
same-bundle code … the new one should supersede it"), the correct 10g
reconciliation is to **DELETE** the legacy `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_
outbox` and its 4 call sites, NOT to treat it as a load-bearing legacy path to
preserve.

HOWEVER — the legacy writer is **NOT shipping a doc-10-noncompliant
external-facing event today**, for three independently-sufficient reasons:
(1) it writes `visibility='internal'` (`store.py:5217`), whereas the doc-10
external-facing display event is `visibility='public'` (the new projection,
`public_dashboard.py:305`); the `public_dashboard_outbox.visibility` column
(`schema.sql:82`) is the per-row external-exposure tag, so the legacy writer's
rows are not tagged as the public display event; (2) the legacy writer has
**NO production caller** — all 4 call sites are themselves in untracked
same-bundle `store.py` code with no shipped consumer; (3) the legacy
`fetch_control_plane_snapshot` (`store.py:315`) it dumps is itself a bounded
contract (`store.py:262` docstring "Bounded … snapshot contract"; the lists are
`LIMIT cap+1`-capped, no artifact bodies) — so even the legacy full-dict
payload, while NOT the doc-10 summary-only shape (it carries the full capped
`attempts`/`runtime_failures`/`projection_refs`/`verification_graph_nodes`
LISTS, not counts), is not a sensitive-body leak. It is a NON-doc-10-summary
event, but `internal`-visibility and unconsumed.

Therefore: leaving BOTH writers present in 10f — the new doc-10-correct
`public`-visibility projection added as instructed, the legacy
`internal`-visibility one inert with no caller — is an **acceptable 10f state**.
It is NOT a real P1/P2 that 10f must fix NOW: no doc-10-noncompliant
external-facing event ships today (the new projection has no caller either, so
the public surface receives nothing yet — see vector 5). It IS a genuine
**non-blocking P2-class duplicate** that the Slice 10g atomic landing MUST
reconcile to ONE doc-10-compliant writer (per the vector-3 mandate and the
same-bundle finding: delete the legacy `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox`
+ its 4 call sites + its tests, and wire the new typed projection). This is
recorded as an EXPLICIT, hard-tracked Slice-10g obligation in STATUS.md
"Remaining" and the carried-P3 ledger. It does not block 10f finalization
because the IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT P1/P2 bar is about a SHIPPING defect, and
nothing doc-10-noncompliant ships externally in the 10f working tree. The
implementer's no-refactor instinct was reasonable for the loop, but the
"pre-Slice-10 legacy" justification is corrected: this is same-bundle code,
and 10g supersedes (deletes) it rather than preserving it.

**Vector 4 — the startup assertions (step 10) fail closed — PASS.**

must be a `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`) and the no-supervisor-feature-timeline-

does not swallow). No silent degrade path exists. Schema-presence checks
(`to_regclass` NULL == absent) run only when a live `pool` is supplied; a

**Vector 5 — no production caller yet — ACCEPTABLE as a mechanism for 10f

(step 11 atomic landing) wires the projection driver to fire; Slice 12 owns

the entrypoint. STATUS.md "Remaining" tracks both. Acceptable for 10f — and it
is also why vector 3 is non-urgent: with no caller for the new projection
either, the public surface receives NEITHER event today, so there is no live
divergence.

**Vector 6 — test honesty — PASS.** The 28 new tests genuinely exercise the
delivered behavior, with ZERO existing tests removed or weakened (the
`tests/test_public_dashboard.py` diff is purely additive — 0 deletions; the 5
deletions in the `public_dashboard.py` diff are pre-existing Slice-00–10e bundle
edits unrelated to 10f; the legacy `store.py` outbox path + its tests are
byte-for-byte untouched). `test_control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload_is_
summary_only` seeds a 40 000-char `"SECRET-BODY-zzz…"` string into the typed
snapshot's `input_digest`, `TypedFailureSummary.summary`, AND
`EvidenceRef.summary`, then asserts `"SECRET-BODY" not in serialized` and `"z" *
200 not in serialized` — the body appears NOWHERE; it also asserts the typed
list bodies are absent and only `counters` are present. `…_bounds_evidence_refs`
seeds 37 refs and asserts the projected list is capped at 12 while the count
still reports 37. The version-advance trigger is tested both ways (`…_emits_
event_when_version_advances` with `snapshot_calls == 1`; `…_is_noop_when_version_
unchanged` with `snapshot_calls == 0`). The fail-closed paths
(`_FailingPool` → `pytest.raises(RuntimeError)`; empty version →
`pytest.raises(ValueError)`) and idempotency (`ON CONFLICT`, same `event_id`)
are tested. The 16 startup tests cover the happy path (static + live-pool fake +
read-only `ActionPolicy`), fail-closed for each of the 3 absent tables
(parametrized), missing dashboard route / empty classifier mapping / missing
public-projection symbol, the 10c-1 read-only-wiring check (fails for an
unrestricted sink, passes for `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`), and the
feature-timeline-writer scan (passes clean, fails closed on a simulated
regression). No regression: `tests/test_public_dashboard.py` GREEN 21,
`tests/test_execution_control_startup.py` GREEN 16 (re-run in this review:
37 passed); `python -m compileall` + `git diff --check` clean (re-run: clean).

**Findings.**
- **P2-10f-1 (NON-BLOCKING for 10f; HARD-TRACKED Slice-10g obligation)** —
  `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/store.py:5199` `_insert_control_plane_
  snapshot_outbox` and `src/iriai_build_v2/public_dashboard.py:243`
  `project_control_plane_snapshot_changed` are TWO writers of `event_type=
  "control_plane.snapshot_changed"`. The legacy one is same-bundle UNTRACKED
  code (`git show HEAD` confirms — `execution_control/` is absent from HEAD),
  NOT a pre-Slice-10 path; it ships NO external event today (`visibility=
  'internal'`, no production caller). Per the vector-3 mandate, Slice 10g must
  reconcile to ONE doc-10-compliant writer by DELETING the legacy
  `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox` + its 4 call sites
  (`store.py:2547`, `:3056`, `:3770`, `:4809`, `:4871`) + its tests, and wiring
  the new typed projection. Not a 10f blocker — nothing doc-10-noncompliant
  ships externally in the 10f working tree.
- **P3-10f-1 (NON-BLOCKING; self-flagged by the implementer)** —
  `startup.py:282` `_check_no_supervisor_feature_timeline_writer` is a `.method(`

  hit flags review; the tree is clean today). An AST check is a later cleanup

**Verdict: CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2-blocking. Slice 10f is FINALIZED; STATUS.md
advanced to Slice 10g.** Vector 1 (bounded payload): PASS — the event is
strictly summary-only, three bounding layers, no body/prompt/unbounded field

`_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox` is same-bundle UNTRACKED code (`git show
HEAD` empty for `store.py` / `execution_control/`), NOT a pre-Slice-10 legacy

vectors:

  `control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload` (`public_dashboard.py:583`) projects
  exactly the doc-10 § "Dashboard Integration Points" shape — `feature_id`,
  `snapshot_version`, visible counters (every typed summary list reduced to its
  `len()`, never the body), `route`, and a bounded set of `EvidenceRef`
  citations (each reduced to `table`+`id`+`citation`+`kind`; `summary` preview
  + `artifact_key` dropped). Three composed bounding layers (lists→counts; ref
  cap 12 / reason cap 8; 240-char text clip + the pre-existing byte cap). NO
  artifact body / prompt / stdout/stderr / verifier body / full dirty-path list
  / unbounded field crosses the public boundary — a test seeding 40 000-char
  bodies proves they appear NOWHERE.
- **Vector 3 — the two `control_plane.snapshot_changed` writers.** `git show
  HEAD` established the legacy `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox`
  (`store.py:5199`) is **same-bundle UNTRACKED code** (the whole
  `execution_control/` package is absent from HEAD), NOT a pre-Slice-10 legacy
  path. It ships NO external-facing event today (`visibility='internal'`, no
  production caller). 10f adding the doc-10-correct bounded `public`-visibility
  projection as NEW code and leaving the inert legacy writer is an acceptable
  10f state; Slice 10g MUST reconcile to ONE writer (DELETE the legacy method +
  its 4 call sites + tests, per the same-bundle/supersede rule) — a
  hard-tracked Slice-10g obligation.

KEY FACTS: the projection is fail-closed on a configured-outbox enqueue failure
(no `except` around the `INSERT`; the `conn` param makes it transactional —
doc 10 § "Edge Cases"); the `(feature_id, snapshot_version,
"control_plane.snapshot_changed")` sha256 idempotency key + `ON CONFLICT
(event_id) DO NOTHING` prevent a duplicate public notification; the

version` and NO-OPs (skipping the snapshot read) when unchanged; the step-10

The new projection + the startup assertion ship as MECHANISMS with no

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10g-1 STARTING

Slice 10g is large (sub-deliverables (a)–(e) per STATUS.md "Next safe

**Split decision recorded.** Sub-deliverables (a) writer-reconciliation +
call-site-replacement-with-the-new-typed-projection and (b) wire `project_
control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` to its production call site share the same
SEAM (the snapshot-version-advance point inside the typed projection
transaction), so combining them keeps the reconciliation atomic — splitting
them would land an in-between state where the legacy writer is gone but the
new typed projection still has no caller.

were carried verbatim from the 10f journal).** Before writing any code I
verified the cited surface myself:

- `grep -n _insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox src/iriai_build_v2/execution_

  and the 10f review at `store.py:5199` does **NOT EXIST** in the same-bundle
  source tree. The line `store.py:5199` is `_insert_or_reuse_workspace_
  snapshot`. The 4 call sites STATUS.md cites (`store.py:2547`, `:3056`,
  `:3770`, `:4809`, `:4871`) do not call the legacy outbox writer either —
  `:4810` is the `await self._insert_dashboard_outbox(...)` call inside
  `_apply_compatibility_projections_in_transaction`, writing the
  `artifact.written` event (NOT `control_plane.snapshot_changed`). The only
  `INSERT INTO public_dashboard_outbox` in store.py is in `_insert_dashboard_
  outbox` (`store.py:5174`), and its `event_type` is `artifact.written`
  (`store.py:5184`), not `control_plane.snapshot_changed`.
- `grep -rn control_plane.snapshot_changed src/iriai_build_v2/execution_
  control/` returns only the `startup.py:33`/:208/:212 readiness-assertion
  references. NO production code under `src/` emits the event today other
  than the new `public_dashboard.py:243 project_control_plane_snapshot_
  changed`.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_execution_control_store.py -q -p no:randomly`
  returns **7 failed, 112 passed** (NOT 119 passed as the 10f journal +
  STATUS.md claim). The 7 failures all expect a legacy full-dict snapshot

    (`tests/test_execution_control_store.py:2322`) — expects one `control_

  - The two `test_commit_and_checkpoint_projection_link_legacy_event_and_
    outbox` parametrizations (`:2044`) — both expect two outbox rows after a
    projection (one `artifact.written`, one `control_plane.snapshot_
    changed`).
  - `test_sandbox_lease_status_update_emits_material_snapshot_outbox_
    version` (`:4283`) — expects `control_plane.snapshot_changed` after
    `allocate_sandbox_lease` and a second after `update_sandbox_lease`.
