    `test_dispatch_runtime_failure_updates_snapshot_retry_budget_and_outbox`
    + `test_dispatch_runtime_failure_snapshot_normalizes_max_attempt_budget`
    — expect `control_plane.snapshot_changed` rows after
    `record_runtime_failure`/`finish_dispatch_attempt`.

  Every failing test asserts a **full-dict payload** (`payload["attempts"][
  0]…`, `payload["projection_refs"]`, `payload["runtime_failures"]`,
  `payload["retry_budgets"]`, `payload["sandbox_snapshots"]`,
  `payload["source"] == "typed"`, `payload["query"]["artifact_bodies"] is
  False`). That is the doc-10 **NON-compliant** shape: full bounded lists
  rather than the doc-10 § "Dashboard Integration Points" summary-only
  `counters` / `len()` shape that the new
  `control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload` projects. They also expect
  `event_id` = `control-plane-snapshot:{FEATURE_ID}:{snapshot_version}:
  control_plane.snapshot_changed`, NOT the new sha256-keyed
  `control_plane_snapshot_event_id`.

  These tests are the **same-bundle test surface for a writer that was
  never written**. Per the same-bundle/supersede rule and the prompt's
  P2-10f-1 directive ("DELETE the legacy `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_
  outbox` + its 4 call sites + its tests, and wire the new typed
  projection"), the correct 10g-1 action is to **DELETE** the orphan
  outbox-assertion blocks (NOT the broader behavioral assertions in the
  same test functions — those test orthogonal contracts like
  `transaction_rollbacks`, `typed_rows`, route normalization, payload
  recovery evidence — and must be preserved per the no-weaken rule).

**The seam (sub-deliverable (b)).** The natural snapshot-version-advance
seam is the projection-link write at the tail of
`_apply_compatibility_projections_in_transaction` (`store.py:4810`-`4863`):
every typed projection completes a `(feature_id, snapshot_version)` advance,
so calling `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` AFTER the projection
link insert (still inside the same `conn` transaction so the fail-closed
property holds end-to-end) is the doc-10-correct firing point. The previous
snapshot_version is whatever the store has already projected; the new
`project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` reads
`get_control_plane_snapshot_version` itself and no-ops when unchanged —
making "previous" track-keeping unnecessary at the caller (the helper is
self-checking).

**Targeted scope of changes (deletive + additive, NO refactor).** The
implementation subagent will:

1. Surgically REMOVE the outbox-assertion blocks (only) from the 7 failing
   tests in `tests/test_execution_control_store.py`. Every other assertion in
   each test stays byte-for-byte intact. The non-outbox assertions test
   distinct behavior (typed_rows mutation, projection links, route
   normalization, `attempt_payload`, `transaction_rollbacks`, etc.) and MUST
   NOT be weakened.
2. Wire `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` to its production seam.
   The seam is `_apply_compatibility_projections_in_transaction` in
   `store.py` (the projection-write transaction): after the projection-link
   insert succeeds for each projection, call
   `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed(self.public_dashboard_outbox,
   self, feature_id, previous_snapshot_version=None, scope="dashboard",
   conn=conn)` so a doc-10-compliant `control_plane.snapshot_changed`
   public-visibility event is enqueued whenever the typed snapshot version
   advances (and a no-op when it didn't). The helper is itself idempotent on
   `(feature_id, snapshot_version)` so a race-prone double-call still
   enqueues exactly one event. The store needs a `_public_dashboard_outbox`
   handle (or None) on `ExecutionControlStore`; when `None`, the helper
   no-ops (this is the documented disabled-outbox path).
3. Add NEW positive tests covering the wiring: a `_FakeConnection`-backed
   test that asserts after `_call_projection(store, "project_group_
   checkpoint", projection)` runs, the `conn.public_dashboard_outbox` row
   for `event_type=="control_plane.snapshot_changed"` exists AND has the
   doc-10-compliant SUMMARY-only `control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload`
   shape (counters via `len`, no full lists, bounded evidence_refs); the
   `event_id` matches `control_plane_snapshot_event_id(feature_id,
   snapshot_version)`; idempotency (same projection twice → exactly one
   outbox row); no-op when outbox is None. These tests are the **doc-10-
   correct** projection coverage that replaces the deleted legacy-shape
   assertions.
4. NO touch of `_insert_dashboard_outbox` (the `artifact.written` writer
   for compatibility projections) — that's an orthogonal artifact event,
   not a control-plane snapshot event.

**Targeted tests to run.**

- `python -m pytest tests/test_execution_control_store.py -q -p no:randomly`
  must be **all-green**.
- `python -m pytest tests/test_public_dashboard.py -q -p no:randomly` must
  remain green (regression-protect the bounded summary-only payload + the
  helper).
- `python -m pytest tests/test_execution_control_startup.py -q -p
  no:randomly` must remain green (the startup readiness assertion still
  resolves the projection symbol).
- `python -m pytest tests/supervisor/ -q -p no:randomly` must remain green.
- `python -m pytest tests/workflows/develop/execution/ -q -p no:randomly`
  must remain green.

**Files expected to change.**

- `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/store.py` — the
  `_apply_compatibility_projections_in_transaction` wiring (additive: one
  call at the end of the per-projection loop body) + an
  `_public_dashboard_outbox` constructor parameter / attribute (additive).
  NOTHING DELETED in `store.py` — there is no legacy writer to delete.
- `tests/test_execution_control_store.py` — surgical assertion deletions in
  the 7 failing test bodies (only the outbox-assertion blocks; every other
  assertion preserved) + new positive tests covering the doc-10-correct
  shape.
- (Possibly) `dashboard.py` / a wiring point — only if the store's existing
  constructor wiring needs the outbox handle threaded through. The
  implementer must verify whether `ExecutionControlStore` is constructed
  with a `PublicDashboardOutbox` already (the artifact.written writer in
  `_insert_dashboard_outbox` writes raw SQL; it does NOT go through
  `PublicDashboardOutbox`, so the store probably doesn't have an outbox
  handle yet).

**Hard rules I'm binding to the implementer.**

- No silent degrade. If the outbox handle is not available, the wiring is
  a documented no-op (the helper already returns None for outbox=None);
  this is the documented disabled-outbox path, not a degrade.
- No full rewrites. Surgical edits only.
- No truncation of test assertions; only DELETION of the explicit
  legacy-outbox assertion blocks (and only when the test's other
  assertions remain meaningful).
- File:line citations required for every code path touched.
- The fail-closed property of `project_control_plane_snapshot_changed`
  (no `except` around the INSERT, the `conn` makes it transactional) must
  be preserved — the caller MUST pass `conn=conn` so the enqueue lives
  inside the projection transaction and a failure rolls back the
  projection write atomically with the outbox enqueue.

The implementer subagent returns a concise diff summary + the targeted
tests run + the assertion-deletion delta per test + any open questions.
The reviewer then six-vectors the diff before STATUS.md is moved.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10g-1 PAUSED (nesting-limit error, returned to orchestrator)

**Outcome of this loop iteration: PAUSED before any source edits, by design.**
The Slice 10g-1 loop worker (this agent) discovered at the dispatch step that
it has no subagent-dispatch tool available in its tool set — the available
tools are Bash, Edit, Read, Write, ToolSearch, and various MCP tools, but no
Task / general-purpose subagent dispatcher. Per the loop-worker prompt's
explicit instruction ("If you hit a nesting limit ('cannot dispatch own
subagent'), STOP and return the error to the orchestrator — it will fall
back to dispatching the implementer/reviewer as sibling subagents"), this
loop iteration stops here without source edits.

**WHAT THIS ITERATION CONTRIBUTED (still valuable for the orchestrator's
next dispatch).** The "Slice 10g-1 STARTING" entry above records FILE:LINE
VERIFICATION that corrects two material drifts in the carried context:

1. **The legacy writer at `store.py:5199` does NOT exist.** STATUS.md and
   the Slice 10f review/journal repeatedly cite a legacy
   `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox` at `store.py:5199` with 4 call
   sites (`:2547`, `:3056`, `:3770`, `:4809`, `:4871`). Verified
   independently in this loop:
   - `grep -n _insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox src/iriai_build_v2/
     execution_control/store.py` returns NOTHING.
   - `store.py:5199` is `_insert_or_reuse_workspace_snapshot` (the workspace
     snapshot writer, NOT a control_plane outbox writer).
   - The ONLY `INSERT INTO public_dashboard_outbox` in store.py is in
     `_insert_dashboard_outbox` (`store.py:5174`), and its `event_type` is
     `artifact.written` (`store.py:5184`), NOT `control_plane.snapshot_
     changed`.
   - `grep -rn control_plane.snapshot_changed src/iriai_build_v2/execution_
     control/` returns only `startup.py:33/:208/:212` readiness-assertion
     references; no production emitter under `src/`.

   The 10f-journal's framing of `_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox` as
   "same-bundle UNTRACKED code" was already a correction of the 10f-
   implementer's earlier framing — but the writer was NEVER ACTUALLY
   WRITTEN. The 10f review's vector-3 analysis (and the carried P2-10f-1
   ledger entry) referenced a method that does not exist in the tree.

2. **`tests/test_execution_control_store.py` is NOT at "119 passed".** The
   journal/STATUS.md baseline (`tests/test_execution_control_store.py is
   119 passed.`) is wrong. Verified independently in this loop:
   - `python -m pytest tests/test_execution_control_store.py -q -p
     no:randomly` returns **7 failed, 112 passed**.
   - All 7 failures expect a legacy full-dict `control_plane.snapshot_
     changed` writer in store.py that does not exist. They assert
     `payload["attempts"][0]…`, `payload["projection_refs"]`,
     `payload["runtime_failures"]`, `payload["retry_budgets"]`,
     `payload["sandbox_snapshots"]`, `payload["source"] == "typed"`,
     `payload["query"]["artifact_bodies"] is False`, and `event_id =
     "control-plane-snapshot:{FEATURE_ID}:{snapshot_version}:
     control_plane.snapshot_changed"`.
   - That is the doc-10 **NON-compliant** full-dict shape, NOT the doc-10
     summary-only-counters shape the new `public_dashboard.
     control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload` projects. The tests describe
     a writer that was never built.
   - The failing tests: `test_projection_emits_idempotent_control_plane_
     snapshot_changed_outbox` (`tests/test_execution_control_store.py:
     2322`); the two `test_commit_and_checkpoint_projection_link_legacy_
     event_and_outbox` parametrizations (`:2044`); `test_sandbox_lease_
     status_update_emits_material_snapshot_outbox_version` (`:4283`);
     `test_dispatch_runtime_failure_is_typed_resumable_evidence`,
     `test_dispatch_runtime_failure_updates_snapshot_retry_budget_and_
     outbox`, `test_dispatch_runtime_failure_snapshot_normalizes_max_
     attempt_budget`.

**THE CORRECT 10g-1 SCOPE (for the orchestrator's next dispatch).** Given
the corrected state, the P2-10f-1 reconciliation is **purely test-surface
deletion + production-call-site wiring**; there is no legacy writer in
`store.py` to delete. Specifically the next implementer should:

(a) **Surgically remove ONLY the outbox-assertion blocks** from the 7
    failing tests above. Every other assertion in each test body
    (`transaction_rollbacks`, `typed_rows`, route normalization,
    `attempt_payload.route_decision`, `failure.evidence.payload.
    legacy_route`, recovery-evidence persistence, `conn.artifacts == []`,
    etc.) tests ORTHOGONAL contracts and MUST stay byte-for-byte. The
    no-weaken rule applies to safety/boundary/reason-code coverage — NOT
    to assertions that describe a writer that was never built.

(b) **Wire `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` into the production
    seam.** The natural seam is the tail of
    `ExecutionControlStore._complete_missing_projections` (`store.py:
    4754`), AFTER the per-projection loop body completes a projection link
    insert (lines `:4810`-`:4863`). Call the helper inside the same `conn`
    transaction so the fail-closed property holds end-to-end. The helper
    is self-checking (reads `get_control_plane_snapshot_version` itself
    and no-ops when unchanged), so the caller does not have to track
    `previous_snapshot_version` — passing `previous_snapshot_version=None`
    makes the helper always read-and-compare (which works because the
    helper compares against `current_version == previous_version` and an
    empty string-typed comparison means it will always project when called
    at the projection seam).

    Wait — re-reading `public_dashboard.py:710`: `if current_version and
    current_version == (previous_snapshot_version or ""): return None`.
    With `previous_snapshot_version=None`, the comparison is
    `current_version == ""`, which is False, so it always projects. That
    is the correct semantic when the caller does not track previous
    versions — every typed projection commit DOES advance the version
    (the projection-link insert advances the typed cursor), and the
    helper's INSERT-then-ON-CONFLICT idempotency on `(feature_id,
    snapshot_version)` makes a race-double still write exactly one event.

    The `ExecutionControlStore` needs an optional `public_dashboard_
    outbox` attribute (default None). When None, the helper no-ops — this
    is the documented disabled-outbox path. The store's constructor takes
    only `pool` today (`store.py:2264`); the implementer should add an
    optional `public_dashboard_outbox: PublicDashboardOutbox | None =
    None` parameter (additive).

(c) **Add NEW positive tests** covering the doc-10-correct shape: a
    `_FakeConnection`-backed `_store(pool, public_dashboard_outbox=outbox)`
    construction; assert that after a projection call the outbox row for
    `event_type=="control_plane.snapshot_changed"` exists and has the
    summary-only `control_plane_snapshot_changed_payload` shape (counters
    via `len`, no full lists, bounded `evidence_refs`); assert the
    `event_id` matches `control_plane_snapshot_event_id(feature_id,
    snapshot_version)`; idempotency (same projection twice → exactly one
    outbox row); no-op when `public_dashboard_outbox is None`.

(d) **Update `_insert_outbox` in `tests/test_execution_control_store.py:
    580`** to handle BOTH the 4-arg existing `artifact.written` SQL and
    the new 6-arg `control_plane.snapshot_changed` SQL (event_id,
    feature_id, event_type, schema_version, visibility, payload). Today
    it assumes args[0..3] only; the new INSERT in `public_dashboard.py:
    294-307` has 6 args.

below in the verify section.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10g-1 COMPLETE

Slice 10g-1 (the P2-10f-1 reconciliation + wiring `project_control_plane_
snapshot_if_changed` to its production call site) is **COMPLETE** and reviewed
CLEAN after one review + remediation + re-review cycle.

**Two-stage review pattern.** The FIRST independent review of the Slice 10g-1
implementation returned **0 P1, 1 P2, 2 P3**:

- **P2-10g-1 (cross-connection stale snapshot-version read).** The new wiring at
  the tail of `_complete_missing_projections` calls
  `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` inside the projection
  transaction; the helper computed the snapshot version digest via the Slice-10a
  store's `get_control_plane_snapshot_version`, which until 10g-1 ALWAYS opened
  a fresh pooled connection. Under Postgres READ-COMMITTED isolation, the
  cross-connection version read returned the **pre-transaction** digest — the
  caller's just-inserted typed rows (`execution_attempts`, `typed_failures`,
  `failure_route_budgets`, etc.) were invisible. That stale digest collides
  with the `event_id` that an earlier projection on the same feature already
  enqueued, and the `INSERT … ON CONFLICT (event_id) DO NOTHING` silently
  swallowed the new state transition's `control_plane.snapshot_changed`
  emission. The result would be doc-10-noncompliant — state transitions that
  advance the typed cursor would NOT notify the dashboard or the supervisor's
  classifier digest. This is the doc 10 § "Edge Cases" contract violation the
  fail-closed property was supposed to prevent.
- **P3-10g-1-1 (degenerate post-deletion stub).** After the surgical assertion
  removal, `test_projection_emits_idempotent_control_plane_snapshot_changed_
  outbox` had no remaining assertions on the new doc-10-correct shape — it
  was a vacuous stub. Idempotency is properly covered by the NEW positive
  `…_is_idempotent_on_repeat` test, so the stub adds no coverage.
- **P3-10g-1-2 (misnamed test).** After the assertion removal,
  `test_sandbox_lease_status_update_emits_material_snapshot_outbox_version`
  no longer tested an outbox emission — its kept body asserted only the
  in-place typed-row edit + lease_version + typed_rows constraint. The
  remaining assertions describe a behavior the new name no longer matches.

**REMEDIATION.** The fix is the textbook keyword-only `conn` threading through
both Slice-10a readers + a conditional pass in the helper:

- `ExecutionControlStore.get_control_plane_snapshot` (`store.py:3822-3863`) and
  `ExecutionControlStore.get_control_plane_snapshot_version`
  (`store.py:3865-3907`) both gained a keyword-only `conn: Any | None = None`
  parameter. When `conn is None` the method opens a pooled connection as
  before (unchanged behavior for the dashboard, supervisor, and MCP routes,
  which have nothing uncommitted to miss). When `conn is not None` the method
  executes on the caller's connection directly — NO pool acquire — so the
  read sees the caller's uncommitted typed inserts. `_set_local_statement_
  timeout` still applies in both branches so a degenerate plan cannot stall
  the caller.
- `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` (`public_dashboard.py:672-752`)
  now passes `conn=conn` to BOTH the version read and the bounded snapshot
  read when (and only when) `conn is not None`. The conditional pass keeps the
  `tests/test_public_dashboard.py` fakes — which do not accept `conn` — working
  byte-for-byte while preserving the fix in every production path that runs
  inside a projection transaction.
- The wiring in `_complete_missing_projections` (`store.py:4917-4943`) now
  passes `conn=conn` end-to-end so the property holds atomically with the
  projection-link insert.
- The degenerate stub `test_projection_emits_idempotent_control_plane_
  snapshot_changed_outbox` was DELETED (idempotency is covered by the new
  positive `…_is_idempotent_on_repeat` test).
- `test_sandbox_lease_status_update_emits_material_snapshot_outbox_version`
  was RENAMED to `test_sandbox_lease_status_update_edits_typed_row_in_place`
  to match the assertions that survived the outbox-block removal (lease
  status + `lease_version` + the typed_rows constraint).
- Two new proof tests were added:
  - `test_project_control_plane_snapshot_changed_uses_caller_conn_for_version_
    read` — spy-based proof that the helper threads `conn` into BOTH Slice-10a
    reads (`get_control_plane_snapshot_version` and `get_control_plane_
    snapshot`) when invoked from inside a transaction.
  - `test_get_control_plane_snapshot_version_with_caller_conn_skips_pool` —
    store-level proof: a `_Pool` that increments `acquire_count` on every
    `acquire()` confirms `pool.acquire_count == 0` when `conn=...` is
    supplied. The fix is structural, not behavioral — the pool is physically
    never touched.

**RE-REVIEW VERDICT.** The independent re-review returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0
P2, 0 new P3**. The fix is genuine: when `conn is not None`, neither store
reader acquires a new pool connection (verified by the pool-acquire-count
test); the wiring at `store.py:4933-4943` always passes `conn=conn` in
production; the helper's conditional pass at `public_dashboard.py:717-747`
preserves the fix in every production path.

**WHAT LANDED — source.** Two source files touched, both additive:

- `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/store.py`:
  - `TYPE_CHECKING` import + lazy `PublicDashboardOutbox` type-only import
    so the constructor type-annotates without a runtime import cycle.
  - `ExecutionControlStore.__init__` (`:2267-2283`) gained a keyword-only
    `public_dashboard_outbox: PublicDashboardOutbox | None = None`
    parameter (default `None`, the documented disabled-outbox no-op path).
    Stored as `self._public_dashboard_outbox`.
  - `get_control_plane_snapshot` (`:3822-3863`) gained the keyword-only
    `conn: Any | None = None`. When `conn is None`, opens a pooled connection
    + transaction as before. When `conn is not None`, executes on the
    caller's connection directly — no pool acquire — so the bounded snapshot
    read participates in the caller's transaction (the P2-10g-1 fix).
    `_set_local_statement_timeout` still applies in both branches.
  - `get_control_plane_snapshot_version` (`:3865-3907`) gained the same
    keyword-only `conn` parameter with the same semantics.
  - `_complete_missing_projections` (`:4917-4943`): the new wiring sits at
    the tail of the per-projection loop, inside the same `conn` transaction
    as the projection-link insert. Guarded by `if self._public_dashboard_
    outbox is not None` (the configured-as-disabled path is explicit, not a
    silent degrade). Passes `conn=conn` end-to-end.
- `src/iriai_build_v2/public_dashboard.py`:
  - `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` (`:672-752`) now passes
    `conn=conn` to both `snapshot_store.get_control_plane_snapshot_version`
    and `snapshot_store.get_control_plane_snapshot` when `conn is not None`.
    The conditional pass at `:717-747` preserves the fix in all production
    paths while keeping the `tests/test_public_dashboard.py` fakes (which do
    not accept `conn`) working byte-for-byte.

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** All edits are in
`tests/test_execution_control_store.py`; `tests/test_public_dashboard.py`,
`tests/test_execution_control_startup.py`, `tests/supervisor/`, and
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/` are byte-for-byte untouched.

- `_insert_outbox` fake (`:580-622`) was extended to dispatch by arity — the
  legacy 4-arg path still handles the existing `artifact.written` `fetchrow`
  SQL; a NEW 6-arg path handles the `control_plane.snapshot_changed`
  `execute` SQL (event_id, feature_id, event_type, schema_version,
  visibility, payload).
- `_select_outbox` predicate (`:1414`) was extended to recognize the
  64-char sha256 idempotency key produced by `control_plane_snapshot_event_
  id`, in addition to the legacy `artifact-write:` / `control-plane-snapshot:`

- **Surgical assertion removal** (NOT entire tests) in 5 unique tests (the 7
  originally-failing list collapsed across parametrizations):
  - `test_commit_and_checkpoint_projection_link_legacy_event_and_outbox`
    (both parametrizations, `:2078`): removed only the `snapshot_outbox` /

  - `test_sandbox_lease_status_update_edits_typed_row_in_place` (`:4720`,

  - `test_dispatch_runtime_failure_is_typed_resumable_evidence` (`:5698`):

- **NEW positive tests (5 total)** covering the doc-10-correct shape:
  - `test_project_control_plane_snapshot_changed_wires_doc10_summary_outbox`

**P2-10f-1 RESOLUTION + corrected framing.** Slice 10g-1 resolves P2-10f-1,
but the resolution is NOT "deleted a legacy writer" — it is "removed test
assertions that described a writer that was never built, and wired the
doc-10-correct projection at the production seam". The original P2-10f-1
framing (DELETE `store.py:5199 _insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox` + its
4 call sites) was based on a misreading of the same-bundle source. `grep -n
_insert_control_plane_snapshot_outbox src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/
store.py` returns nothing; `store.py:5199` is `_insert_or_reuse_workspace_
snapshot`; the only `INSERT INTO public_dashboard_outbox` in `store.py` is
in `_insert_dashboard_outbox` (`:5174`) writing `event_type='artifact.
written'`. The 7 originally-failing tests were asserting a writer that was
never built. With the surgical assertion-block removal + the production
wiring of `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed`, the tree now has
exactly ONE doc-10-compliant `control_plane.snapshot_changed` writer — the
public-outbox projection — and that single writer is invoked atomically
inside every typed projection transaction. Future maintainers reading the
ledger should know the resolution sequence: the 10f review correctly flagged
a duplicate-writer obligation; the 10g-1 PAUSED file:line verification
discovered the legacy writer cited in that obligation did not exist; the
10g-1 implementation removed the test-surface remnants of the never-built
writer and wired the doc-10-correct projection in its place.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10g-2 COMPLETE

Slice 10g-2 (the full doc-10 test matrix — the atomic-feature end-to-end
scenario + the startup/deployment guard test against real Postgres) is
**COMPLETE** and reviewed CLEAN on the first review pass — **0 P1, 0 P2, 1
non-blocking new P3 (P3-10g-2-1, carried)**. The slice is purely TEST surface:
**NO source edits**. Two new test files, both real-Postgres-backed, both
driving the REAL doc-10 components (no test fakes for the load-bearing
pieces). The two doc 10 § "Tests" deliverables called out in STATUS.md "Next
safe action" both land in this slice.

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** Two new files; zero source edits; zero existing
tests removed or weakened.

- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_doc10_atomic_scenario.py` (NEW, 973
  lines, **ONE comprehensive scenario test** `test_doc10_atomic_feature_
  scenario` at `:507`). The doc 10 § "Tests" atomic-feature end-to-end
  scenario, against the real Postgres fixture (`tests/workflows/develop/
  execution/conftest.py`'s `merge_queue_database` + `mq_conn`). The scenario
  is structured as **seven phases** chained inside the single test body so
  every consumer sees the same evolving cursor state:
  - **Phase 1 — Seed.** Raw inserts populate all **eight cursor tables**
    (`execution_attempts`, `typed_failures`, `failure_route_budgets`,
    `merge_queue_items`, `evidence_nodes`, `workspace_snapshots`,
    `sandbox_leases`, `runtime_workspace_bindings`). The logical → physical
    table-name mapping is documented inline at `:524-533` so future
    maintainers can trace each seed row to its doc-10 § "Cursor Tables"
    entry. The seed drives the typed cursor (and therefore the
    `control_plane_snapshot_version` digest) into a known initial state.
  - **Phase 2 — Outbox projection.** Constructs a real
    `PublicDashboardOutbox(outbox_enabled=True)` and a real
    `ExecutionControlStore` (with the outbox wired in via the Slice-10g-1
    `public_dashboard_outbox=` kwarg); drives
    `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` end-to-end. Asserts:
    exactly **one** outbox row after the first call; **idempotency on
    repeat** at the same `snapshot_version` (the
    `control_plane_snapshot_event_id` sha256 + the
    `ON CONFLICT (event_id) DO NOTHING` clamp do their job);
    `visibility="public"`; the **bounded summary-only payload shape** (every
    typed list is reduced to `len()`, no `*_list` keys in the payload,
    `evidence_refs` is bounded at ≤ 12, the projected payload is ≤ 250 KB).
  - **Phase 3 — Dashboard routes.** Drives the REAL
    `dashboard.get_feature_control_plane` and `dashboard.get_feature` route
    handlers (NOT a JSON-only sanity check; the actual route callable that
    `aiohttp` would dispatch). Asserts the **typed snapshot shape**
    (`schema == "typed"`, `source == "typed"`, the `ControlPlaneSnapshot`
    Pydantic model round-trips); the `/api/feature/{id}` ETag **folds the
    typed snapshot version** (the value participates in the ETag
    computation, observable by mutating the snapshot version and comparing
    the new ETag); the embedded `control_plane` object in
    `/api/feature/{id}` is the **compact typed embedding** (`schema ==
    "typed"`, `source == "typed"`) — proving the Slice-10b reconciliation
    landed end-to-end.
  - **Phase 4 — Supervisor MCP + typed-PRIMARY classifier.** Drives the REAL
    `SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.get_current_snapshot` (the Slice-10e
    typed-first MCP path). Asserts `evidence_mode == "typed"` (NOT
    `legacy_fallback` or `mixed`). Then drives the REAL
    `SupervisorClassifier.classify` (the Slice-10c-2 thin typed-PRIMARY
    branch) — asserts it returns a verdict and `verdict.facts["mapping_
    row"]` is a Slice-10c-2 typed-PRIMARY mapping name (proving the
    classifier ran the typed branch, not the legacy artifact fallback).
  - **Phase 5 — Slack dedupe round-trip.** Drives the REAL
    `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore.decide` against real Postgres for the
    three-call pattern doc 10 § "Slack Dedupe And Suppression" mandates: a
    new key → `first_seen` send; the same key at the same version →
    `suppressed_duplicate` (no Slack send, a `record_suppressed` audit
    row); a new escalation route on the same signature →
    `material_change` (the never-suppress arm — escalation is never
    silenced). Asserts the `supervisor_digest_state` + `supervisor_digest_
    audit` row mutations transactionally.
  - **Phase 6 — Snapshot-version advance lock-in.** Inserts a SECOND typed
    failure row, re-drives the projection, and asserts the snapshot
    version **ADVANCES** → the second projection produces a **DISTINCT
    outbox event** (different `event_id` because
    `control_plane_snapshot_event_id` folds the new
    `snapshot_version`) → asserts `outbox_count == 2` (no event silently
    dropped). This phase is the END-TO-END lock for the structural
    Slice-10g-1 P2-10g-1 fix: the second projection's version read sees
    the just-inserted typed row (the keyword-only `conn` threading works
    inside the real-Postgres transaction), the `ON CONFLICT (event_id) DO
    NOTHING` clamp does NOT swallow the new event because the event_id
    correctly folds the advanced version, and both events land in the
    outbox.
  - **Phase 7 — Cross-consumer agreement after the state transition.**
    Re-drives the dashboard route AND the MCP `get_current_snapshot`
    AFTER the Phase-6 state transition. Asserts all consumers (the
    dashboard, the MCP, the projection) **agree on the same NEW snapshot
    version**, and that the unique-version count equals the outbox-event
    count. This locks the doc 10 § "Acceptance Criteria" property that
    state transitions advancing the typed cursor cause every doc-10
    consumer to observe the new state consistently.
- `tests/supervisor/test_startup_real_postgres.py` (NEW, 446 lines, **12
  tests**). The doc 10 § "Tests" startup/deployment guard against real
  Postgres (`tests/supervisor/conftest.py`'s `supervisor_pg_database`):
  - **PASS path (1 test):** `test_assert_control_plane_ready_real_postgres_

  - **FAIL-CLOSED paths (parametrized + named, 8 distinct components, 11

    7. `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore.decide` absent

    8. Public-outbox projection missing
       (`test_real_postgres_fails_closed_when_public_outbox_projection_

    9. Non-`ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` for `ActionPolicy` — the 10c-1

       (`test_real_postgres_fails_closed_for_non_audit_scoped_artifact_

    10. Feature-timeline-writer scan regression
        (`test_real_postgres_fails_closed_when_supervisor_module_writes_

  - **Schema-cleanliness sanity (1 test):** `test_real_postgres_schema_
    check_passes_after_schema_drop_rollback` (`:430`) — confirms the
    asyncpg transaction-rollback pattern used by the parametrized table-
    drop tests genuinely yields a fresh schema state per test.
  - **Every FAIL-CLOSED test asserts the error message NAMES the failed
    component** (so the operator can identify what to fix without
    grepping the source). Schema mutations use asyncpg transaction

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 10g-2 review returned **CLEAN — 0

- Comprehensive seeding (all 8 cursor tables; both legacy projection

- Real components, not test fakes, for every load-bearing piece (the REAL

  the REAL `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore.decide`, the REAL
  `assert_control_plane_ready`, the REAL `PublicDashboardOutbox.project_
  control_plane_snapshot_changed`).
- NO source edits (`git diff` for `src/` and `dashboard.py` is empty for the
  10g-2 chunk).
- NO existing test weakened (the pre-existing `tests/workflows/develop/
  execution/` suite goes 683 → 684, and the pre-existing `tests/supervisor/`
  suite goes 358 → 370; the only delta is +1 / +12 NEW tests).
- Targeted regression GREEN (see counts below).
- Snapshot version ADVANCES multiple times within the scenario, locked
  end-to-end with the outbox-event-count assertion (Phase 6).
- Test honesty: real Postgres is reachable; 0 skips on the real-Postgres
  tests; no test silently passes by short-circuiting.
- FAIL-CLOSED error messages name components (the operator can fix without
  source-diving).

**NEW non-blocking P3 (carried to slice-end 10g-3):**

- **P3-10g-2-1** (Slice 10g-2) — pre-existing `dashboard.py` develop-workflow
  substring SQL bug. `dashboard.py:561-590` uses `substring(value from 1 for
  CASE WHEN ... THEN $2 ELSE $3 END)` which fails under asyncpg's prepared-
  statement parameter-typing rules: `UndefinedFunctionError: function
  pg_catalog.substring(text, integer, text) does not exist`. The `$2` / `$3`
  parameters get typed as `text` by the asyncpg protocol, but `substring
  (value from 1 for <int>)` requires `integer`. The bug **pre-dates Slice
  10** and is in the develop-workflow branch (the bugfix-v2 branch at
  `dashboard.py:530-558` uses the simpler `substring(value from 1 for $2)`
  which works because there is only ONE parameter to type). The 10g-2
  implementer worked around the bug at the test-fixture level by seeding
  `workflow_name='bugfix-v2'` in the atomic scenario, which routes the
  feature through the bugfix-v2 branch. CRITICALLY, the doc-10 step-6
  embedding contract is **still genuinely exercised**: the embedded
  `control_plane` object in `/api/feature/{id}` is built by the SAME
  `_compact_typed_control_plane` helper in both branches (the call sites
  are `dashboard.py:677` for bugfix-v2 and `dashboard.py:1133` for develop-
  workflow), so the typed embedding is end-to-end covered through the
  bugfix-v2 branch. The bug is therefore **NOT a 10g-2 blocker** — it is a
  pre-existing bug in `dashboard.py` flagged as a carried P3. Recommended
  fix at the slice-end 10g-3 six-vector review: either split the develop-
  branch SQL into two queries (one for each `CASE` arm) OR wrap each
  parameter in an explicit `CAST($N AS integer)`.

**TARGETED REGRESSION (reviewer's re-runs).** All targeted suites GREEN
post-edit:

- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_doc10_atomic_scenario.py`: **1
  passed** (the single comprehensive scenario test).
- `tests/supervisor/test_startup_real_postgres.py`: **12 passed**.

  NO existing test weakened or removed).

  edits).
- `tests/test_public_dashboard.py`: **21 passed** (unchanged; no edits).
- `tests/test_execution_control_startup.py`: **16 passed** (unchanged; no

  `git diff --check` clean.
- 0 skips on the real-Postgres tests (the `merge_queue_database` +
  `supervisor_pg_database` fixtures both reach `localhost:5431`).

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 10 ACCEPTED

**Slice 10 (Supervisor And Dashboard Integration) is formally ACCEPTED.**
Sub-slices 10a + 10b + 10c (10c-1 + 10c-2) + 10d (10d-1 + 10d-2) + 10e + 10f
+ 10g-1 + 10g-2 + 10g-3 are all complete.

The slice-end SIX-VECTOR review (described below) ran across the WHOLE of
Slice 10 (10a + 10b + 10c-1 + 10c-2 + 10d-1 + 10d-2 + 10e + 10f + 10g-1 +
10g-2 as one integrated change) and returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2 — on ALL
SIX vectors**. The aggregate P3 count was ~5 non-blocking carried items
(P3-10e-1, P3-V6-1/P3-10c-1 ledger drift, P3-V6-2/P3-10c-2 reverse subset
assertion gap, P3-10d-2-1, P3-10f-1, P3-10g-2-1, P3-V2 `ControlPlaneSnapshot`
name reuse). Per `IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT.md` § "Acceptance Gate", Slice 10
acceptance is gated on 0 P1 / 0 P2, which holds. Per the user's
correctness/quality preference, the three CHEAP P3s (P3-10e-1, P3-V6-1,
P3-V6-2/P3-10c-2) were remediated within this 10g-3 finalizer pass; the
remaining P3s are documented and deferred with rationale (below).

Slice 10 moved supervisor and dashboard visibility from artifact-body
inference onto a single TYPED `ControlPlaneSnapshot` contract end-to-end. It
landed as ONE ATOMIC feature (doc 10 § "Rollout/Rollback Notes" — no phased
production rollout, no consumer-by-consumer enablement switch): the typed
snapshot store + ~13 models (10a); the dashboard `/api/feature/{id}/control-
plane` route + the embedded compact `control_plane` object + the
typed-snapshot-version-folded ETag on `/api/feature/{id}` (10b); the
mechanical read-only supervisor contract — `supervisor/read_only.py`'s
split writer surface (28-name `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` + 7-name
`FEATURE_TIMELINE_WRITER_METHODS` + the 35-element union), the construction
guards, `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink`, and `ActionPolicy.guard_execution_write`
(10c-1); the typed `SupervisorClassifier.classify` mapping table + the real
typed budget source resolving carried P3-10a-1 (10c-2); the Slack digest
dedupe store + the two supervisor-owned tables + the never-suppress-an-
escalation arm (10d-1); the `SupervisorSlackService` send/suppress routing
through `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore.decide` (10d-2); the doc-10 step-4
`evidence_mode` wiring resolving P3-10c-2-1 (10e); the public-dashboard
`control_plane.snapshot_changed` outbox projection + the doc-10 step-10
`assert_control_plane_ready` startup guard (10f); the P2-10f-1 reconciliation
+ the keyword-only `conn` threading through both Slice-10a readers
(resolving the within-slice-raised P2-10g-1 cross-connection stale-version
read) + the wiring of `project_control_plane_snapshot_if_changed` at the
production seam (10g-1); the full doc-10 § "Tests" matrix — the atomic-
feature end-to-end seven-phase scenario test + the 12-test startup/
deployment guard test, both against real Postgres (10g-2); and the slice-end
six-vector review + the three cheap-P3 remediations + formal acceptance
(10g-3, this entry).

### Slice 10g-3 SIX-VECTOR REVIEW (2026-05-22)

Six independent review subagents each took one vector across the WHOLE of
Slice 10 (10a + 10b + 10c-1 + 10c-2 + 10d-1 + 10d-2 + 10e + 10f + 10g-1 +
10g-2 as one integrated change), mirroring the 08g / 09e-2 pattern.

Aggregate verdict: **0 P1, 0 P2.** ALL SIX vectors returned CLEAN. Per-
vector verdicts:

- **Vector 1 — Doc-10 acceptance criteria: CLEAN, 0 P3.** Every one of
  the 9 doc-10 § "Acceptance Criteria" bullets maps onto a verified
  component + a passing test, with no silent degrade onto legacy artifact
  inference. The key evidence is the 10g-2 atomic-feature scenario test
  (`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_doc10_atomic_scenario.py:507`,
  7 phases against real Postgres) end-to-end exercising every consumer of
  the typed `ControlPlaneSnapshot` shape against the SAME evolving cursor
  state, plus the 12-test startup-guard surface (`tests/supervisor/test_
  startup_real_postgres.py`) covering 8 distinct doc-10 components in 11
  FAIL-CLOSED paths with operator-named error messages.
- **Vector 2 — Source structure and additivity: CLEAN, 2 P3.** All Slice-10
  source edits are ADDITIVE: the legacy dict-based
  `fetch_control_plane_snapshot` (`store.py:315`), the legacy
  `@dataclass ControlPlaneSnapshot` (`store.py:262`), the 8 legacy
  classifier methods on `SupervisorClassifier`, and the legacy
  `SupervisorSlackDigestDecisionStore` are BYTE-FOR-BYTE UNCHANGED. The
  five new modules (`workflows/develop/execution/snapshots.py`,
  `supervisor/classifier_mapping.py`, `supervisor/read_only.py`,
  `supervisor/digest_dedupe.py`, `execution_control/startup.py`) all live
  as leaf modules with no inbound prod calls outside their wired seams.
  Two non-blocking P3s: **P3-10g-2-1** (pre-existing `dashboard.py:561-590`
  develop-workflow substring SQL bug, PRE-DATES Slice 10 — DEFER) and the
  `ControlPlaneSnapshot` name reuse between the legacy dataclass at
  `store.py:262` and the new typed Pydantic class in `snapshots.py` (a
  non-blocking maintainability smell — the two never coexist in the same
  call frame; DEFER).
- **Vector 3 — Test surface and coverage honesty: CLEAN, 1 P3.** No
  existing test was weakened across the whole of Slice 10. The 10g-1
  remediation deleted ONE degenerate stub (`…_emits_idempotent_control_
  plane_snapshot_changed_outbox`) and RENAMED one mismatched-name test
  (`…_emits_material_snapshot_outbox_version` → `…_edits_typed_row_in_
  place`); 5 outbox-only assertion blocks were SURGICALLY narrowed with
  every orthogonal contract assertion kept byte-for-byte (verified by the
  10g-1 re-review). Real-Postgres tests reach `localhost:5431` with 0
  skips. Every new test exercises a REAL production component (not a
  fake) for its load-bearing assertion; every FAIL-CLOSED test names its
  component in the error message. One non-blocking P3: **P3-10e-1** —
  `tests/supervisor/test_tools.py:54` `_Pool.fetch` returns `[{"value":
  1}]` for any non-`FROM artifacts` query. Cosmetic, one-line fix — taken
  in this 10g-3 remediation pass (R1 below).
- **Vector 4 — Fail-closed and edge-case handling: CLEAN, 0 new P3.**
  Every doc-10 § "Edge Cases" row maps onto a concrete safeguard + a
  passing test. Degraded typed read → `evidence_mode` of `legacy_fallback`
  or `mixed`, never `typed` (`evidence.py:730-750`, the `except Exception
  return None` fail-safe; verified by `test_supervisor_evidence_mode_is_
  legacy_fallback_on_typed_read_error`). Missing pool → same. `ON CONFLICT
  (event_id) DO NOTHING` event-drop prevention → the 10g-1 `conn`-
  threading fix + the structural `test_get_control_plane_snapshot_version_
  with_caller_conn_skips_pool` + the end-to-end 10g-2 Phase 6 outbox-count
  assertion. Escalation never-suppress → `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore.
  decide`'s `material_change` arm + the 10g-2 Phase 5 third-call
  assertion. The carried **P3-10d-2-1** (legacy in-process throttle can
  DELAY a first stop/escalate by up to one `min_digest_interval_seconds`
  poll — never a DROP, since the parked packet is re-emitted via
  `pending_due` on the next poll and `decide()`'s never-suppress arm then
  fires) is RE-CONFIRMED BENIGN.
- **Vector 5 — Cross-component contract integrity: CLEAN, 0 new P3.** The
  dashboard (`get_feature_control_plane` `:1109` + the embedded
  `control_plane` at `:1133` for the develop branch / `:677` for the
  bugfix-v2 branch, both feeding `_compact_typed_control_plane`), the MCP
  (`SupervisorEvidenceMcpService.get_current_snapshot`), the public-outbox
  projection (`PublicDashboardOutbox.project_control_plane_snapshot_
  changed`), the classifier (`SupervisorClassifier.classify` → the typed-
  PRIMARY branch when `evidence_mode == "typed"`), and the dedupe store
  (`SupervisorDigestDedupeStore.decide` against `control_plane_snapshot_
  version`) ALL agree on the same `ControlPlaneSnapshot` Pydantic shape +
  the same `control_plane_snapshot_version` digest. The 10g-2 Phase 7
  assertion verifies a single version advance is observed identically by
  every consumer (unique-version count == outbox-event count).
- **Vector 6 — Documentation and ledger accuracy: CLEAN, 2 P3.** The
  STATUS.md ↔ journal ↔ decisions JSONL ↔ in-source docstring file:line
  cites all agree on test counts and the carried-P3 ledger. Two non-
  blocking P3s: **P3-V6-1** — ledger drift in `implementation-journal.md`
  (`:20165`, `:20177`, `:20388`) and `implementation-decisions.jsonl`
  (line 1065) where the prose says "26 names" / "union (33)" but the live
  frozensets are 28 / 7 / 35; `supervisor/read_only.py` itself is already
  clean. Cosmetic — taken in this 10g-3 remediation pass (R2 below).
  **P3-V6-2 / P3-10c-2** — `test_real_execution_control_store_exposes_
  every_control_plane_writer` (`tests/supervisor/test_read_only.py:169-
  180`) asserts only the FORWARD direction (`CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS`
  ⊆ the class); a NEWLY-ADDED `ExecutionControlStore` writer is not auto-
  flagged. Add the REVERSE assertion — taken in this 10g-3 remediation
  pass (R3 below).

### 10g-3 remediations applied

Three cheap-P3 fixes applied within this 10g-3 finalizer (TEST + DOC
SURFACE ONLY, NO production source edits):

- **(R1) P3-10e-1 — `tests/supervisor/test_tools.py:54` `_Pool.fetch`
  cosmetic fix.** Changed the fake to return `[]` (instead of `[{"value":
  1}]`) for ANY query that does NOT match the existing `FROM artifacts`
  carve-out. The `[]` return correctly emulates an empty real-Postgres
  feature → `evidence_mode == "legacy_fallback"`, which matches the
  documented Slice-10e production behavior (`evidence.py` `load_typed_
  control_plane_snapshot` returns a `source="legacy_fallback"` snapshot,
  never spuriously upgrades a degenerate fake to `"typed"`). The inline
  comment documents the contract. NO test assertion broke (none of the
  test_tools.py assertions depended on the `[{"value": 1}]` row shape).
  `tests/supervisor/test_tools.py` 9 passed unchanged.
- **(R2) P3-V6-1 — Ledger drift fix.** Edited the three flagged journal
  occurrences and the JSONL line 1065 in lockstep: `"26 names"` → `"28
  names"` (`implementation-journal.md:20165`, `:20388`; jsonl line 1065);
  `"union (33)"` → `"union (35)"` (`implementation-journal.md:20177`;
  jsonl line 1065). The narrative description of the P3 itself at
  `implementation-journal.md:20577-20586` is LEFT INTACT as historical
  record of the drift. JSONL still parses (1086 lines, all valid JSON).
- **(R3) P3-V6-2 / P3-10c-2 — REVERSE subset assertion.** Added a NEW
  test `test_control_plane_writer_methods_set_matches_execution_control_
  store_public_surface` in `tests/supervisor/test_read_only.py` (sibling
  of the existing forward-direction test). The new test inspects
  `ExecutionControlStore` via `inspect.getmembers`, classifies the 38
  public methods into READERS (anything starting with the known read-only
  prefixes `get_`/`list_`/`read_`) and writers (everything else), and
  asserts the symmetric difference between the writer set and
  `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` is empty. The test docstring documents
  inline WHY the inverse-classifier approach (READER prefixes) is
  preferred over deriving a hard-coded WRITER prefix set (a future writer
  adopting a new verb like `commit_…` or `seal_…` would silently bypass a
  hard-coded WRITER prefix list, but is automatically caught by the
  READER-prefix inverse). A new reader prefix (e.g. `query_…`) would
  trigger a clear failure with the violating method names, forcing a
  DELIBERATE update of the reader-prefix tuple. `tests/supervisor/test_
  read_only.py` 19 passed (was 18 + 1 new).

### DEFERRED P3s (rationale)

- **P3-10g-2-1** — Pre-existing `dashboard.py:561-590` develop-workflow
  `substring(value from 1 for CASE WHEN ... THEN $2 ELSE $3 END)` SQL bug
  that fails under asyncpg's prepared-statement parameter-typing rules
  (`UndefinedFunctionError: function pg_catalog.substring(text, integer,
  text) does not exist`). The bug PRE-DATES Slice 10. The bugfix-v2 branch
  at `dashboard.py:530-558` uses the simpler `substring(value from 1 for
  $2)` which works; the develop-branch `CASE WHEN` form types `$2`/`$3`
  as `text` while `substring(... from 1 for <int>)` requires `integer`.
  The doc-10 step-6 embedding contract IS still genuinely exercised by
  the 10g-2 scenario because the embedded `control_plane` object is built
  by the SAME `_compact_typed_control_plane` helper in both branches.
  **DEFERRED** because: (a) it is not a Slice-10 regression — it pre-
  dates Slice 10; (b) it lives in the develop-workflow branch which is
  itself the target of the Slice 11 `implementation.py` monolith split,
  and is the natural fold-in point; (c) the recommended fix (split the
  develop-branch SQL into two queries OR wrap each parameter in
  `CAST($N AS integer)`) is straightforward but lives best alongside the
  Slice 11 develop-branch refactor or as a maintenance pass. Fold into
  Slice 11 or a maintenance pass.
- **P3-10d-2-1** — Legacy in-process throttle `supervisor/slack.py:2213`
  `_digest_packet_to_send` can DELAY (NEVER drop) a first `stop/escalate`
  by up to one `min_digest_interval_seconds` poll when it arrives inside
  the throttle window. **DEFERRED** because: (a) it is a DELAY, not a
  DROP — the parked packet is re-emitted via `pending_due` on a later
  poll and `decide()`'s never-suppress arm then fires; (b) doc 10
  § "Slack Dedupe And Suppression" forbids SUPPRESSING a first
  escalation, not DELAYING it; (c) collapsing the legacy throttle into
  `decide()` is OPTIONAL cleanup, not a correctness fix. Considered
  benign at slice-end; non-blocking.
- **P3-10f-1** — `execution_control/startup.py:282`
  `_check_no_supervisor_feature_timeline_writer` is a `.method(`
  substring scan (scoped to the 3 unambiguous FeatureStore timeline
  mutators), not AST-precise. **DEFERRED** because: (a) the scan is
  CONSERVATIVE-CORRECT — a hit flags review; (b) the tree is clean today
  (the no-supervisor-feature-timeline-writer property holds); (c) an AST
  check is an optional cleanup if a future false-positive ever appears.
- **P3-V2 `ControlPlaneSnapshot` name reuse** — The legacy
  `@dataclass ControlPlaneSnapshot` at `store.py:262` and the new typed
  Pydantic `ControlPlaneSnapshot` in
  `workflows/develop/execution/snapshots.py` share a name. **DEFERRED**
  because: (a) the two are never imported into the same call frame and
  the call sites are unambiguous in context; (b) renaming the legacy one
  would touch the byte-for-byte-untouched legacy dict path; (c) it is a
  non-blocking maintainability smell. Optional cleanup, not a
  correctness fix.

### doc-10 § "Acceptance Criteria" coverage (9 of 9 delivered + tested)

1. **Supervisor and dashboard agree with executor route decisions.** ✅
   The dashboard reads the Slice-10a `ControlPlaneSnapshot.failures` /
   `merge_queue` / `gates` from the same store the MCP +
   classifier read; the 10g-2 Phase 7 cross-consumer agreement assertion
   proves a single advance is observed identically by every consumer.
2. **Supervisor does not ask operator to manually fix ACL / worktree
   alias / stale projection / commit-only classes.** ✅ The typed
   `SupervisorClassifier.classify` mapping (`classifier_mapping.py`
   `MAPPING_ROWS`) maps every reachable Slice-07 `FailureClass` onto a
   classification with a `recommended_action` other than "ask the
   operator"; the 10c-2 coverage test asserts every reachable class
   maps exactly once.
3. **Dashboard shows queue/sandbox/gate state without broad artifact
   hydration.** ✅ The Slice-10a `get_control_plane_snapshot` is bounded
   (`LIMIT cap+1` + `SET LOCAL statement_timeout`), feature-scoped, and
   SUMMARY-ONLY (no `value`/`content`/`payload`-body / prompt / stdout-
   stderr column ever selected, asserted by
   `test_snapshot_serialization_has_no_body_fields`). The dashboard's
   typed path reads ONLY this bounded snapshot — zero `FROM artifacts`
   reads on the typed `/api/feature/{id}/control-plane` route.
4. **Slack noise is reduced through signature-based dedupe.** ✅
   `SupervisorDigestDedupeStore.decide` returns `first_seen` /
   `suppressed_duplicate` / `material_change` for the three doc-10
   patterns; the 10g-2 Phase 5 assertion drives the real store against
   real Postgres for the three-call pattern.
5. **Supervisor read-only means no executor/control-plane mutation.** ✅
   The Slice-10c-1 `supervisor/read_only.py` mechanical contract: the
   28-name `CONTROL_PLANE_WRITER_METHODS` is structurally absent on the
   MCP service (asserted at `__init__` + after lazy store creation), on
   `ActionPolicy` (`execution_authority` writer is rejected at
   construction in read-only AND guarded mode), and on the
   `ReadOnlyAuditArtifactSink` (denies any non-supervisor-owned-prefix
   put by RAISING — no best-effort mutation). Bounded audit / dedupe /
   outbox writes ARE allowed by name-prefix.
6. **Snapshot APIs are typed, bounded, versioned, and shared by
   dashboard, MCP, and Slack digest generation.** ✅ Same Pydantic
   `ControlPlaneSnapshot` class (`snapshots.py`); same
   `control_plane_snapshot_version` digest (`compute_typed_snapshot_
   version`); all three consumers read via the same `get_control_plane_
   snapshot` (with the keyword-only `conn` Slice-10g-1 fix for inside-
   transaction reads).
7. **Legacy fallback is visibly degraded and never outranks typed
   route/checkpoint state.** ✅ `evidence_mode_for_snapshot`
   (`evidence.py:753`) returns `"legacy_fallback"` / `"mixed"` / `"typed"`
   transparently; the classifier's typed-PRIMARY branch fires ONLY for
   `evidence_mode == "typed"`; the 8 legacy artifact classifiers run as
   the FALLBACK; the snapshot's `degraded=True` / `degradation_reasons`
   are surfaced to every consumer.
8. **Every background Slack send/suppress decision is auditable by
   snapshot version, dedupe key, reason, and cited evidence refs.** ✅
   `supervisor_digest_state` + `supervisor_digest_audit` tables persist
   every decision; the audit row carries `snapshot_version` +
   `dedupe_key` + `decision` + `reason` + bounded `EvidenceRef`
   citations; the 10g-2 Phase 5 assertion verifies the state + audit row
   shape against real Postgres.
9. **The feature lands atomically: schema, snapshot store, dashboard,
   supervisor, Slack dedupe, public outbox, and tests merge together.**
   ✅ Slice 10 is ONE working-tree bundle: `schema.sql` (the two
   supervisor-owned tables added by 10d-1, the
   `public_dashboard_outbox` table by 10f), the snapshot store
   (`snapshots.py` + `store.py` additions), the dashboard
   (`dashboard.py` typed route + ETag wiring), the supervisor
   (`read_only.py` + `classifier_mapping.py` + `digest_dedupe.py`), the
   public-outbox projection (`public_dashboard.py` `project_control_
   plane_snapshot_changed`), the startup guard (`execution_control/
   startup.py`), and the full test matrix (the 10g-2 atomic-feature
   scenario + the 12-test startup guard) all merge as ONE atomic feature.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11 STARTING + Slice 11a STARTING

### Slice 11 sub-slice plan

Slice 11 splits `workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py` (~34K lines)
into the maintainable module set defined by `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md` § "Proposed Module Boundaries" + § "Boundary review
checklist". Per doc 11 § "Refactoring Steps" the extraction order is
strictly dependency-driven: types/adapters before any module that needs
them; git + journal before queue/checkpoint authority; workspace,
contracts, sandbox, dispatcher, gates, verification, router/repair
establish typed evidence before canonical mutation moves; merge queue
comes late because it becomes the only product-authoritative mutation
path; regroup overlay + control plane come after those owners are
stable. The sub-slice plan below mirrors doc 11 § "Refactoring Steps"
1-14 but uses the slice-letter convention used in prior slices (Slice
11a, 11b, ...) and adds an explicit slice-end six-vector review +
acceptance step (mirroring 08g / 09e-2 / 10g-3) at the tail.

Sub-slice plan (dependency-ordered, each extraction PR is refactor-only
unless explicitly the final atomic-landing step):

- **11a — extract `execution/types.py`** (THIS SUB-SLICE). Lowest-risk
  first extraction: cross-module Pydantic / dataclass request/outcome
  types + idempotency-key helper functions + typed failure enums + the
  module-level constants that participate in those type contracts. NO
  behavior change; the shim re-exports every moved name from
  `implementation.py` so every existing import + monkeypatch target
  resolves to the same object. Doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts"
  row 1.
- **11b — extract `execution/git_service.py`** (a shortest-independent
  helper that already has a near-complete external facade — partially
  extracted in Slice 08b). Owns git status/add/commit, hook-failure
  parsing, dirty-state proof, bounded command output. Doc 11 §
  "Boundary-level API contracts" row 12.
- **11c — extract `execution/task_contracts.py`** facade work (deliverable
  contract compilation + write-set authority + validation) — the existing
  Slice-03 module gains the implementation-side facade methods doc 11
  row 5 requires.
- **11d — extract `execution/sandbox.py`** facade work (sandbox
  lifecycle + immutable patch evidence + runtime workspace binding).
  Doc 11 row 6.
- **11e — extract `execution/dispatcher.py`** facade work (runtime
  invocation boundary + retry idempotency + runtime failure typing —
  the Slice-05 module gains the facade contract doc 11 row 7 requires).
- **11f — extract `execution/gates.py`** facade work (deterministic
  preflight gates + raw-gate ordering + stale-context blockers + gate
  evidence shape). Doc 11 row 8.
- **11g — extract `execution/verification.py`** facade work (model
  verifier orchestration + expanded lenses + bounded verify context +
  verifier outcome typing). Doc 11 row 9.
- **11h — extract `execution/repair.py`** facade work (`RouteExecutor`
  + `RepairService` — owning concrete `RepairRequest`/`RepairOutcome` +
  `RetryRequest`/`RetryOutcome` execution for product / contract /
  canonicalization / workspace / commit-hygiene / dispatcher-retry /
  verifier-retry / merge-retry / sandbox-capture-retry / cleanup
  routes). Doc 11 row 10.
- **11i — extract `execution/failure_router.py`** facade work (typed
  failure taxonomy + retry budgets + deterministic route selection +
  quiesce/escalation decisions — Slice-07 was the contract, 11i is the
  implementation-side facade). Doc 11 row 11. **P3-6 FOLD-IN
  CANDIDATE.**
- **11j — extract `execution/merge_queue.py`** facade work (the
  product-authoritative `MergeQueueService` — enqueue / claim /
  apply_patch / run_post_apply_gates / commit_and_checkpoint /
  recover_expired). Doc 11 row 13. **P3-6 FOLD-IN PRIMARY OWNER.**
- **11k — extract `execution/regroup_overlay.py`** facade work
  (derived-DAG overlay validation + active marker resolution +
  activation + rollback-before-first-wave + scheduler safety checks).
  Doc 11 row 14.
- **11l — extract `execution/post_dag_gates.py`** facade work
  (post-DAG code review / security / test authoring / QA / integration
  / final verifier / source push / report / notification — feature-
  level business-gate orchestration after effective DAG completion).
  Doc 11 row 15.
- **11m — extract `execution/post_test_guard.py`** facade work
  (`PostTestReadinessGuard.assert_ready(feature_id)` — effective-DAG
  completion + post-DAG-gate completion + no-active-control-plane-work
  before `PostTestObservationPhase` collects observations). Doc 11 row
  16.
- **11n — slice-end SIX-VECTOR review + formal Slice 11 ACCEPTANCE.**
  Mirror the 08g / 09e-2 / 10g-3 pattern: dispatch six PARALLEL
  vector-review subagents across the WHOLE of Slice 11 (V1 doc-11 §
  "Acceptance Criteria" all bullets; V2 source structure +
  refactor-only / no-behavior-change additivity; V3 test honesty +
  shim coverage + new-module direct tests; V4 fail-closed edges + no
  hidden cross-module shortcuts; V5 cross-component contract integrity
  + monkeypatch targets preserved + import compatibility; V6 docs /
  ledger / journal accuracy). Remediation pass if any vector is NOT
  CLEAN; then re-review; then formal acceptance.

Each sub-slice is one extraction PR per doc 11 § "Refactoring Steps".
Each PR has a shim audit + a four-question § "How To Use This Map"
answer + a minimum proof bundle (direct module test + shim smoke test
+ targeted regression). Tests follow the doc 11 § "Tests" table.

### P3-6 fold-in plan

P3-6 (the durable-merge-queue drain `contract_violation` → `quiesce`
route fix) was re-deferred from Slice 09 because the fix legitimately
reopens the Slice-08 `MergeApplyResult` contract (it threads
structured `_ApplyFailure.escaped_paths` → `MergeApplyResult` → the
08e-3a drain → the router observation `payload` so
`failure_router._allows_product_repair` no longer downgrades the
route from `contract_violation` to `quiesce`). The reopening is
legitimate inside Slice 11 because Slice 11 owns the merge-execution
refactor (Slice-11 § "Refactor Map" row 13 `merge_queue.py` is the
owner of the apply-failure surface), so the P3-6 fix lands as part
of the merge-queue extraction.

**Primary owner:** Slice 11j (`execution/merge_queue.py`) extends
`MergeApplyResult` with a structured `escaped_paths: list[str]` field
(or a typed sub-object) at the moment the apply-time `_ApplyFailure`
is observed, and threads it through the 08e-3a drain into the typed
failure observation payload (`repair_scope` / `target_paths`). The
`merge_queue.py` extraction PR carries the contract change because
the moved module owns the producer side of `MergeApplyResult`.

**Consumer side:** Slice 11i (`execution/failure_router.py`) — the
router's `_allows_product_repair` (or its equivalent typed observation
gate) is the consumer; it reads the new structured field and no
longer downgrades the route when escaped_paths is non-empty. The 11i
PR carries the matching consumer-side change.

**Test coverage:** the new contract field requires direct tests on
both sides (one on the 11j producer side proving the escaped-paths
threading; one on the 11i consumer side proving the route is
preserved when the typed payload carries the escaped-paths field) +
a regression test against the previous fail-closed behavior. The
existing `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py` cases that
expected the `quiesce` downgrade need typed-payload coverage that
proves the new route is `contract_violation` (with the escaped-paths
typed evidence) — the safety direction is unchanged (still
fail-closed-or-safer); the change converts a deferred fail-closed
`quiesce` into a precise `contract_violation` repair route.

**Ordering note:** 11i lands before 11j in the sub-slice plan above
(failure_router is the consumer; merge_queue is the producer). The
P3-6 contract change is therefore added in 11j AND consumed by an
update inside the SAME PR's 11i compatibility shim, OR the consumer
update is split into an 11j follow-on (with 11i's typed contract
already in place). Decision: the contract reopening + the producer
+ consumer update land as a SINGLE atomic chunk inside Slice 11j (the
merge-queue extraction) so the `MergeApplyResult` change is applied
end-to-end in one chunk. 11i lands first as a pure refactor-only
extraction with its existing route-decision logic unchanged; 11j
then carries (a) the merge-queue extraction itself, (b) the
`MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` field, and (c) the router-consumer
update (one or two lines inside `_allows_product_repair`) within the
SAME chunk.

### Slice 11a STARTING

Sub-deliverable: extract pure-data types from
`workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py` into a new module
`workflows/develop/execution/types.py`, leaving compatibility shims
(re-exports) in `implementation.py` so every existing import + every
monkeypatch target keeps resolving to the same object. NO behavior
change. The new module owns:

- Cross-module value objects: `DagExecutionOutcome`,
  `RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding`, `CommitRepoOutcome`, `WorkflowCommitError`,
  `CommitFailureLocation`, `CommitForbiddenPathMatch`,
  `DagDirectRepairRoute`, `DagAuthorityGateOutcome`, `PlannedBugGroup`,
  `PlannedBugDispatch`, `DagTaskDriftRoute`,
  `DagContradictionResolution`, `DagContradictionResolutionValidation`,
  `DagContradictionHandoffOutcome`, `WorktreeRegistryRepo`,
  `WorktreeRegistry`, `WorkspaceAuthorityCompatibilityOutcome`,
  `TaskContractCompileOutcome`, `TaskContractCommitGuardOutcome`,
  `_RepoNeed`, `_MergeQueueEnqueueError`, `_MergeQueueDrainResult`,
  `_MergeQueueCheckpointResult`, `_MergeQueueResumeRecovery`,
  `DagVerifyLensSpec`, `WorktreeAliasPathInfo`,
  `DagTaskReconcileOutcome`, `DagTaskSpecReconcileOutcome`.
- Typed failure-class signal: `SandboxWorkflowBlocker` +
  `_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER`.
- Bounded-output / serialization helper for the type contracts:
  `_bounded_commit_output`, `COMMIT_FAILURE_OUTPUT_LIMIT`.

Out-of-scope (left in `implementation.py`):

- `DagArtifactClosureScan` — its `target_refs()` method depends on
  `_dedupe_preserving_order`, a private helper defined in
  `implementation.py` with many other call sites. Moving the helper too
  would broaden the extraction scope into non-type code. The class
  stays for a later sub-slice (likely 11j or a dedicated 11
  refactor-only follow-up that moves the dedupe helper).
- All classes with real method bodies / behavior — the prompt-
  builder / runtime-client / sandbox-port / output-normalizer /
  journal-ports / `ImplementationPhase` — these are behavior modules,
  not types.
- Private routing constants (`_COMMIT_HYGIENE_ROUTE`,
  `_MANIFEST_FORBIDDEN_CLEANUP_ROUTE`, `_DAG_AUTHORITY_*`) — these are
  not part of a TYPE contract; they are constants used by routing
  helper functions in `implementation.py`. The one `_DAG_AUTHORITY_
  SEMANTIC_ROUTE` value that serves as a default for
  `DagAuthorityGateOutcome.route` is inlined as a literal in the new
  types.py default (byte-for-byte the same value) so the constant in
  `implementation.py` is left for the routing helpers and we don't
  duplicate it.
- The `DAG_REGROUP_*` artifact-key constants — these are domain
  routing keys, not type contracts; they already cross modules via
  the existing `post_test_observation.py` import path. Leaving them
  in `implementation.py` keeps the sub-slice scope tight.

Compatibility shim in `implementation.py`: a single import block at
the head of `implementation.py` re-imports every moved name from
`workflows.develop.execution.types` so every legacy import
(`from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import
DagExecutionOutcome` etc.) and every monkeypatch target
(`monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module, "WorkflowCommitError",
...)`) continues to resolve to the same object.

Targeted-test plan: a new test file
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_types_extraction.py` asserts
each moved name imported through the OLD path is `is` the same
object as the import through the NEW path, smoke-tests
instantiation of representative types, and asserts that the
`DagAuthorityGateOutcome.route` default value still equals
`"semantic_verify_needed"` (the inlined literal).

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11a COMPLETE

Slice 11a (extract `execution/types.py` from
`workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`) is **COMPLETE** and reviewed
CLEAN on the first review pass — **0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3**. The chunk delivers
the lowest-risk first extraction in the Slice-11 plan: pure-data types
move; no behavior moves; every legacy import + every monkeypatch target
keeps resolving to the same object via the re-export shim. The chunk is
the template every later 11.x extraction follows.

**WHAT LANDED — source.** One new module, one modified module; both
edits are mechanical extraction + re-export (no behavior change).

- **NEW** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/types.py` (613
  lines, 19,479 bytes). Owns the canonical definitions for the 29 moved
  type classes (24 dataclasses + 3 Pydantic `BaseModel`s + 3
  `RuntimeError` subclasses) + the 2 supporting module-level constants
  (`COMMIT_FAILURE_OUTPUT_LIMIT`, `_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER`) +
  the 1 supporting helper (`_bounded_commit_output`). The module's
  import set is stdlib + Pydantic + `iriai_compose` types +
  `....models.outputs` — minimal, and crucially NO import from
  `implementation.py` or any phase module (compatibility arrows always
  point IN, never out, per doc 11 § "How To Use This Map"). The 29
  classes are: `DagExecutionOutcome`, `RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding`,
  `SandboxWorkflowBlocker`, `CommitRepoOutcome`, `WorkflowCommitError`,
  `CommitFailureLocation`, `CommitForbiddenPathMatch`,
  `DagDirectRepairRoute`, `DagAuthorityGateOutcome`, `PlannedBugGroup`,
  `PlannedBugDispatch`, `DagTaskDriftRoute`,
  `DagContradictionResolution`, `DagContradictionResolutionValidation`,
  `DagContradictionHandoffOutcome`, `WorktreeRegistryRepo`,
  `WorktreeRegistry`, `WorkspaceAuthorityCompatibilityOutcome`,
  `TaskContractCompileOutcome`, `TaskContractCommitGuardOutcome`,
  `_RepoNeed`, `_MergeQueueEnqueueError`, `_MergeQueueDrainResult`,
  `_MergeQueueCheckpointResult`, `_MergeQueueResumeRecovery`,
  `DagVerifyLensSpec`, `WorktreeAliasPathInfo`,
  `DagTaskReconcileOutcome`, `DagTaskSpecReconcileOutcome`.
- **MODIFIED**
  `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`
  (line count: 34272 → 33863; net delta -409 lines). The moved class
  bodies + the moved constants + the moved helper were removed in
  place; the shim re-export block was inserted at `:321-354`:
  `from ..execution.types import (...)` listing all 29 moved names + the
  2 constants + the 1 helper. The shim block sits among the other
  intra-package imports near the top of the module; the comment
  preamble at `:315-320` documents the contract ("the canonical
  definitions live in `workflows/develop/execution/types.py`; this
  shim re-exports every moved name so every existing
  `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import X`
  and every `monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module, "X", ...)` keeps
  resolving to the SAME object as the canonical definition in
  `execution/types.py`"). Nothing else in `implementation.py` was
  edited — every method body, every routing function, every
  `ImplementationPhase` stage is byte-for-byte preserved.

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** One new file, purely additive. No existing
test was edited or removed.

- **NEW** `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_types_extraction.py`
  (322 lines, 40 tests). The doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" four-
  question contract is locked in code:
  - **Parametrized shim identity (29 tests).** For every name in the
    `MOVED_TYPE_NAMES` list, asserts
    `getattr(implementation_module, X) is getattr(types_module, X)` —
    proving the shim re-exports the SAME object, not a copy. The `is`
    identity (rather than equality) is the structural property that
    keeps `monkeypatch.setattr` working: a patched attribute on
    `implementation_module` is the SAME binding the canonical module
    serves.
  - **Constant + helper identity (3 tests).**
    `COMMIT_FAILURE_OUTPUT_LIMIT`, `_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER`,
    and `_bounded_commit_output` each get an explicit identity
    assertion (the constants are immutable so `is`-checks reduce to
    equality; the helper is a single function object that must be
    pinned).
  - **Canonical `__module__` rebinding (1 test).** Asserts the
    canonical class object's `__module__` is
    `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.types` (not
    `…phases.implementation`) — proving the definition genuinely moved
    rather than being re-aliased from the old module.
  - **7 representative smoke / contract tests** covering cross-cutting
    behavioral invariants the moved types embody:
    1. `DagExecutionOutcome.__iter__` order — the tuple-unpack contract
       the executor relies on.
    2. `CommitRepoOutcome.to_dict` bounded-output round-trip — proves
       `_bounded_commit_output` participates in the serialization
       contract correctly across the move.
    3. `WorkflowCommitError.failed_outcomes` attribute + RuntimeError
       inheritance — the typed-exception contract (`raise … from ` and
       `isinstance(..., RuntimeError)` still work for the legacy
       exception handlers in `implementation.py`).
    4. `DagAuthorityGateOutcome.route` default equals the literal
       `"semantic_verify_needed"`. The literal in `types.py` is byte-
       for-byte equal to the value of `implementation._DAG_AUTHORITY_
       SEMANTIC_ROUTE` at `:455`; the test asserts the equality
       directly so a future drift between the inlined literal and the
       routing constant cannot pass silently.
    5. `SandboxWorkflowBlocker` marker idempotency — the typed-blocker
       signal carries the marker prefix and can be reconstructed
       round-trip.
    6. `WorktreeRegistry` / `WorktreeRegistryRepo` Pydantic round-trip
       — the Pydantic models serialize and deserialize without loss
       (proves `model_dump` / `model_validate` work after the move).
    7. `_MergeQueueDrainResult.succeeded` two-value contract — the
       boolean property collapses the drain-outcome enum into a single
       value the orchestrator branches on.
    Plus a `DagContradictionResolution` Pydantic-defaults smoke test
    (proves the model's optional fields default correctly after the
    move, since contradictions are an emergent-from-defaults type).

**DOC-11 FOUR-QUESTION CONTRACT (per reviewer).**

- **Q1 — What behavior moved?** **NONE.** This is a pure type /
  constant / helper extraction. Every method body, every routing
  function, every `ImplementationPhase` stage stays in
  `implementation.py`. The new module owns DEFINITIONS only.
- **Q2 — What shims still work?** **All 29 types + 2 constants + 1
  helper.** Re-exported at `implementation.py:321-354`. Every existing
  `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import X`
  resolves to the SAME object as
  `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.types import X` —
  proven by the 40 parametrized identity assertions. No
  `monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module, "X", ...)` target moves
  (every existing target name keeps the same binding location).
- **Q3 — What targeted tests prove the new facade + the shim?**
  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_types_extraction.py` —
  40 tests as above (29 parametrized identity + 3 constant/helper
  identity + 1 canonical `__module__` rebinding + 7 behavioral smoke
  tests).
- **Q4 — Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only class / dataclass / enum
  / Pydantic-model bodies + 2 constants + 1 small bounded-output
  helper moved. The single inlined literal `"semantic_verify_needed"`
  in `DagAuthorityGateOutcome.route`'s default is byte-for-byte equal
  to `implementation._DAG_AUTHORITY_SEMANTIC_ROUTE` at `:455` and is
  asserted equal by a smoke test. No control-flow change. No new
  validation. No new field. The PR is mechanical extraction + shim
  preservation.

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11a independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3** on the first pass. All six
vectors PASS + all four doc-11 questions are answered satisfactorily.
Reviewer specifics:

- **V1 (doc-11 acceptance criteria for an extraction PR).** All four
  doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions are answered above; the
  extraction order constraint (types first) is satisfied; the
  compatibility-arrow direction (extracted module never imports back
  into `implementation.py`) is verified by inspection of

  29 types in detail (`DagExecutionOutcome`, `CommitRepoOutcome`,
  `DagAuthorityGateOutcome`, `WorktreeRegistry`,
  `_MergeQueueDrainResult`) — every body is byte-for-byte equal to its
  pre-extraction form modulo whitespace/indent.
- **V3 (test honesty + shim coverage).** All 40 tests are real
  assertions (no `pytest.skip`, no `pass`-stubs); the identity
  assertions genuinely fail if the shim is removed (verified by the
  reviewer briefly mutating the shim block and re-running the test
  file — every parametrized case failed as expected).
- **V4 (fail-closed edges).** The moved types include three
  `RuntimeError` subclasses (`WorkflowCommitError`,
  `_MergeQueueEnqueueError`, `SandboxWorkflowBlocker`); each still
  inherits from `RuntimeError`, each still carries its typed payload
  fields, and each still works under `raise … from …`.
- **V5 (cross-component contract).** No other workflow module was
  modified for source — only `implementation.py`. Imports from the
  new module are confined to the shim at `implementation.py:321-354`;
  the `__init__.py` files in `workflows/develop/execution/` and
  `workflows/develop/phases/` were not edited.
- **V6 (docs / ledger).** This journal entry + the JSONL acceptance
  line + the STATUS.md pointer advance constitute the V6 surface for
  11a; the reviewer verified the journal entry's claims match the
  on-disk state.

The reviewer also explicitly verified the LEFT-BEHIND types are
genuinely behavior-bearing (not arbitrary deferrals):
`DagArtifactClosureScan.target_refs()` depends on

classes carry real method bodies and would broaden the extraction
into non-type code. These will move in a later sub-slice (most
likely 11j or a dedicated dedupe-helper extraction).

**Slice 11 sub-slice plan (recap).** Per the BEFORE-chunk journal
entry above, the dependency-ordered plan is: 11a types (DONE) → 11b
