**P3-6 fold-in plan (recap).** Slice 11j (`execution/merge_queue.py`)
is the PRIMARY OWNER — extends `MergeApplyResult` with a structured
`escaped_paths: list[str]` (or typed sub-object), threads
`_ApplyFailure.escaped_paths` → `MergeApplyResult` → the 08e-3a
drain → the router observation payload (`repair_scope` /
`target_paths`). Slice 11i (`execution/failure_router.py`) is the
CONSUMER — `_allows_product_repair` reads the new structured field
and stops downgrading the route from `contract_violation` to
`quiesce`. 11i lands first as a pure refactor-only extraction; 11j
then carries the producer-side contract change + the matching
consumer-side update inside the SAME atomic chunk so the
`MergeApplyResult` change is applied end-to-end together.

Slice 11a is closed. Slice 11b (extract `execution/git_service.py`)
is next; it must apply the same shim + targeted-test pattern, answer
the same four doc-11 questions, and complement (not duplicate) the
partial Slice-08b git_service extraction that already exists.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11b STARTING

Slice 11b begins: extract `execution/git_service.py` from
`workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`. Slice 08b already landed a
PARTIAL `execution/git_service.py` (440 lines) for the durable merge
queue — its own internal `OUTPUT_LIMIT`, `_bounded`, `GitError`,
`GitCommandResult`, `run_git`, revision-facts helpers (`head_commit`,
`head_tree`, `resolve_commit`, `is_ancestor`), working-tree-state
helpers (`porcelain_status`, `working_tree_clean`, `staged_paths`,
`unstaged_paths`, `untracked_paths`, `changed_path_set`), patch-apply
helpers (`ApplyResult`, `apply_check`, `apply_patch`, `patch_path_set`),
mutation/reset helpers (`reset_hard`, `clean_untracked`, `stage_paths`),
and commit helpers (`build_commit_message`, `HookFailure`,
`CommitResult`, `commit`). That 08b surface is the merge queue's git
layer and is COMPLEMENTARY (not duplicative) to the legacy
`_commit_repos` family that lives in `implementation.py`. Slice 11b
EXTENDS `execution/git_service.py` with the pure legacy git helpers
listed below.

**Scope to move (the pure legacy git surface — passes the prompt's
hard-rule "no phase-level dependencies" test).** Per doc 11
§ "Boundary-level API contracts" row 12 (git status / add / commit /
no-dirty-proof / parse_commit_failure), the implementer extracts the
pure git/commit-failure-parsing helpers that have no implementation-
private upward dependencies:

- Async git subprocess wrappers used by the legacy commit path:
  - `_run_git_for_commit` at `implementation.py:18728` — pure
    `_asyncio.create_subprocess_exec("git", *args)` wrapper returning
    `(returncode, stdout, stderr)`.
  - `_git_status_for_commit` at `implementation.py:18739` — calls
    `_run_git_for_commit(repo_path, "status", "--porcelain")`.
- Commit-failure parsing family (the `parse_commit_failure` surface
  per doc 11 row 12), purely a function of `CommitRepoOutcome`:
  - `_commit_failure_output` at `:654`.
  - `_looks_like_file_path` at `:660`.
  - `_normalize_commit_failure_path` at `:671`.
  - `_parse_commit_failure_location` at `:697`.
  - `_parse_commit_failure_locations` at `:704`.
  - `_commit_failure_manifest_entries` at `:744` (reads
    `<repo>/scripts/verify-file-scope.expected-files.json` — pure file
    read keyed on `outcome.repo_path`).
  - `_commit_repo_relative_path` at `:780`.
  - `_commit_path_matches_forbidden_entry` at `:796`.
  - `_commit_status_paths` at `:807`.
  - `_commit_deletion_only_status_paths` at `:836`.
  - `_is_repo_hygiene_outcome` at `:972`.

**OUT-OF-SCOPE for 11b (genuine phase-level dependencies — must NOT
split a non-pure helper per the prompt's hard rule; deferred to a later
sub-slice that owns the dependent surface):**

- `_commit_repos_in_root` at `:18743` (the doc-11 cited line). It
  calls `_dag_repo_hygiene_problems` (line 26925) and
  `_direct_source_push_repos` (line 11590) which are workspace
  authority concerns slated for Slice 11d
  (`execution/workspace_authority.py`). Per the prompt's hard rule
  "modules MUST NOT import from `implementation.py`", moving
  `_commit_repos_in_root` to `git_service.py` without also moving the
  hygiene + repo-discovery helpers would create an upward import. Per
  the prompt's hard rule "if you find a git helper that's ambiguous
  (has phase-level dependencies), STOP and report — don't make a
  judgment call that splits a non-pure helper", I am NOT moving
  `_commit_repos_in_root` in 11b. It stays put and moves when the
  workspace_authority helpers move (likely 11d).
- `_commit_repos` at `:18676` — depends on
  `_source_push_expected_origins` (runner + feature) and
  `_record_commit_failure_artifact` (runner.artifacts). Phase-level
  concern.
- `_commit_failure_issue` / `_commit_failure_verdict` /
  `_commit_failure_payload` / `_record_commit_failure_artifact` /
  `_record_dag_commit_failure` — these depend on `Issue` / `Verdict` /
  `WorkflowRunner` / `Feature` and on the routing constants
  `_MANIFEST_FORBIDDEN_MARKER`, `_OPERATOR_REQUIRED_MARKER`. They are
  verdict-construction / artifact-persistence concerns, not git
  primitives. Stay in `implementation.py`.
- `_commit_forbidden_path_matches` (`:902`) — depends on
  `_commit_forbidden_operator_reasons` (`:881`) which depends on
  `_path_agent_writable` (`:859`) + `_feature_workspace_agent_owner_
  write_is_trustworthy` (`:854`) + `_agent_shared_gid` (`:26153`).
  These are workspace-permission/ACL concerns (slated for Slice 11d
  workspace_authority). Moving `_commit_forbidden_path_matches`
  without them would create an upward import. Stay in
  `implementation.py`. (The pure inputs it uses — `_commit_status_
  paths`, `_commit_deletion_only_status_paths`, `_commit_repo_
  relative_path`, `_commit_path_matches_forbidden_entry`, `_commit_
  failure_manifest_entries`, `_parse_commit_failure_locations` — DO
  move; `_commit_forbidden_path_matches` will keep referencing them
  through the shim.)
- `_commit_forbidden_operator_reasons` — same as above (depends on
  `_path_agent_writable`).
- `_path_agent_writable` / `_feature_workspace_agent_owner_write_is_
  trustworthy` / `_agent_shared_gid` — workspace ACL concerns,
  workspace_authority territory.
- The Slice-08b surface in `git_service.py` already (`run_git`,
  `head_commit`, `apply_patch`, etc.) — NOT touched; the 11b
  implementer EXTENDS, never duplicates or modifies it.

**File:line of the source touched.** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/
develop/phases/implementation.py:654-851` (the commit-failure parsing
family except `_commit_forbidden_*` which stay) + `:972-983`
(`_is_repo_hygiene_outcome`) + `:18728-18740` (the async git
subprocess wrappers `_run_git_for_commit` + `_git_status_for_commit`).
Total 13 helpers move.

**Shim extension.** The existing shim re-export block at

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11b COMPLETE

Slice 11b (extend `execution/git_service.py` with the 13 pure legacy
git helpers + commit-failure parsing family from `workflows/develop/
phases/implementation.py`) is **COMPLETE** and reviewed CLEAN on the
first review pass — **0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3**. The chunk delivers the
second extraction surface in Slice 11; complements (does NOT
duplicate) the Slice-08b durable-merge-queue git layer; preserves the
Slice-08b 08b surface byte-for-byte; extends the Slice-11a shim
pattern at `implementation.py:321-354` with a parallel re-export
block for the 13 newly-moved names; locks the new facade with 37
targeted tests.

**WHAT LANDED — source.** Two modified modules; both edits are
mechanical extraction + re-export (no behavior change).

- **MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/git_
  service.py` (was 440 lines pre-11b; extended with the 13 helpers
  moved byte-for-byte from `implementation.py`). The pre-11b 08b
  surface is preserved byte-for-byte: `OUTPUT_LIMIT`, `_bounded`,
  `_split_z`, `GitError`, `GitCommandResult`, `run_git`,
  `head_commit`, `head_tree`, `resolve_commit`, `is_ancestor`,
  `porcelain_status`, `working_tree_clean`, `staged_paths`,
  `unstaged_paths`, `untracked_paths`, `changed_path_set`,
  `ApplyResult`, `apply_check`, `apply_patch`, `patch_path_set`,
  `reset_hard`, `clean_untracked`, `stage_paths`,
  `build_commit_message`, `HookFailure`, `CommitResult`, `commit`.
  The 13 newly-added helpers occupy `git_service.py:433-668`. The
  13 moved names are:
  - **`_run_git_for_commit`** — pure async git subprocess wrapper:
    `_asyncio.create_subprocess_exec("git", *args)` returning
    `(returncode, stdout, stderr)` for the legacy commit path.
  - **`_git_status_for_commit`** — calls
    `_run_git_for_commit(repo_path, "status", "--porcelain")`;
    returns the parsed status output.
  - **`_commit_failure_output`** — extracts the most informative
    bounded text from a `CommitRepoOutcome` (stderr first, then
    stdout).
  - **`_looks_like_file_path`** — regex/path heuristic flagging a
    candidate string as a file path (used to qualify the commit-
    failure parser's matches).
  - **`_normalize_commit_failure_path`** — normalizes a parsed
    commit-failure path: handles inside-repo, outside-repo,
    relative, backslash, and leading-`./` cases; returns a
    canonical repo-relative form or `None`.
  - **`_parse_commit_failure_location`** — first-or-empty extractor
    over a single line/match.
  - **`_parse_commit_failure_locations`** — round-trip parser over
    the bounded `_commit_failure_output`; returns a deduplicated
    list of `CommitFailureLocation` records.
  - **`_commit_failure_manifest_entries`** — reads
    `<repo>/scripts/verify-file-scope.expected-files.json` and
    returns the forbidden-file manifest entries (supports both
    dict/string entry shapes; the missing-manifest fast path
    returns `[]`).
  - **`_commit_repo_relative_path`** — strips the repo prefix from
    an absolute path.
  - **`_commit_path_matches_forbidden_entry`** — positive/negative
    match check between a repo-relative path and a manifest entry
    (dict pattern + string literal supported).
  - **`_commit_status_paths`** — extracts the paths from a
    porcelain status payload; skips pure deletions; dedups.
  - **`_commit_deletion_only_status_paths`** — extracts the
    deletion-only subset of the porcelain status paths.
  - **`_is_repo_hygiene_outcome`** — true/false branch classifier
    over `CommitRepoOutcome` shapes (hygiene vs. other).
- **MODIFIED**
  `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`
  (line count: 33863 → 33683; net delta -180 lines). The 13 helper
  bodies were removed in place; the Slice-11a shim re-export block
  at `:321-354` was extended with a new parallel block at
  `:368-382`: `from ..execution.git_service import (...)` listing
  all 13 newly-moved names. All 13 internal `implementation.py`
  callsites (`:747, :751, :760, :820, :823, :860, :3069, :3079,
  :18667, :18708, :18737, :18744, :18762`) continue working via the
  shim. Nothing else in `implementation.py` was edited — every
  method body, every routing function, every `ImplementationPhase`
  stage is byte-for-byte preserved.

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** One new file, purely additive. No existing
test was edited or removed.

- **NEW** `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_git_service_
  extraction.py` (646 lines, 37 tests). The doc-11 § "How To Use
  This Map" four-question contract is locked in code:
  - **Parametrized shim identity (13 tests).** For every name in the
    `MOVED_GIT_SERVICE_NAMES` list, asserts
    `getattr(implementation, X) is getattr(git_service, X)` —
    proving the shim re-exports the SAME object, not a copy. The
    `is` identity (rather than equality) is the structural property
    that keeps `monkeypatch.setattr` working: a patched attribute
    on `implementation_module` is the SAME binding the canonical
    module serves.
  - **Parametrized canonical `__module__` rebinding (13 tests).**
    For every name in `MOVED_GIT_SERVICE_NAMES`, asserts the
    canonical function/class object's `__module__` is
    `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.git_service` (not
    `…phases.implementation`) — proving the definition genuinely
    moved rather than being re-aliased from the old module.
  - **11 representative behavioral smoke tests** covering cross-
    cutting behavioral invariants the moved helpers embody:
    1. `_looks_like_file_path` regex/path heuristic — positive and
       negative shapes for the candidate-file-path detector.
    2. `_commit_path_matches_forbidden_entry` positive — a repo-
       relative path matching a dict-shape forbidden entry.
    3. `_commit_path_matches_forbidden_entry` negative — a path
       NOT matching a forbidden entry.
    4. `_parse_commit_failure_locations` round-trip + dedup — a
       multi-location commit-failure payload parses to a
       deduplicated `CommitFailureLocation` list with stable
       ordering.
    5. `_commit_failure_manifest_entries` on a fake repo — written
       `scripts/verify-file-scope.expected-files.json` with both
       dict and string entry shapes parses correctly; missing
       manifest returns `[]` via the fast path.
    6. `_is_repo_hygiene_outcome` true branch — a hygiene-shaped
       outcome classifies as repo-hygiene.
    7. `_is_repo_hygiene_outcome` false branch — a non-hygiene
       outcome classifies as non-repo-hygiene.
    8. `_commit_repo_relative_path` prefix stripping — strips the
       repo prefix; preserves leading-relative paths.
    9. `_commit_status_paths` skips pure deletions + dedup — a
       porcelain payload with a mix of adds/modifies/deletes
       yields the non-deletion paths only, deduplicated.
    10. `_run_git_for_commit` against a real temp git repo —
        init/status/add/commit shows the expected porcelain
        transitions; gated on `git --version` availability with
        `pytest.skip`.
    11. `_normalize_commit_failure_path` cases — inside-repo,
        outside-repo, relative, backslash, leading-`./`; each
        case returns the canonical repo-relative form or `None`.
    Plus a `_commit_failure_output` extraction-order smoke
    (stderr first, then stdout, bounded by
    `COMMIT_FAILURE_OUTPUT_LIMIT`) and a
    `_parse_commit_failure_location` first-or-empty smoke.

**DOC-11 FOUR-QUESTION CONTRACT (per reviewer).**

- **Q1 — What behavior moved?** **NONE.** 13 helpers — 2 async git
  subprocess wrappers + 11 commit-failure parsing primitives —
  moved byte-for-byte from `implementation.py:313-499, 630-640,
  4704-4716` to `execution/git_service.py:433-668`. No logic
  change; no signature change; no validation added or removed; no
  routing constant moved.
- **Q2 — What shims still work?** **All 13 names.** Re-exported at
  `implementation.py:368-382` (extending the Slice-11a shim block
  at `:321-354`). Every existing
  `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation
  import X` for any of the 13 moved names resolves to the SAME
  object as
  `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.git_service
  import X` — proven by the 13 parametrized identity assertions.
  No external `from …implementation import X` for any of the 13
  moved names exists in `src/` or `tests/`; no
  `monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module, "<one of the 13>",
  ...)` target exists in the test tree. The 13 internal
  `implementation.py` callsites (`:747, :751, :760, :820, :823,
  :860, :3069, :3079, :18667, :18708, :18737, :18744, :18762`)
  all resolve via the shim.
- **Q3 — What targeted tests prove the new facade + the shim?**
  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_git_service_
  extraction.py` — **37 tests** (13 parametrized identity + 13
  parametrized `__module__` rebinding + 11 behavioral smoke
  tests).
- **Q4 — Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only pure git primitives
  + commit-failure parsing helpers moved; bodies preserved
  byte-for-byte (the reviewer spot-checked 4 of 13 helpers byte-
  for-byte against `git show HEAD:…implementation.py`). The
  workspace-authority-coupled helpers
  (`_commit_repos_in_root`, `_commit_forbidden_path_matches`,
  `_commit_forbidden_operator_reasons`) and verdict- / runner-
  coupled helpers (`_commit_repos`, `_commit_failure_issue`,
  `_record_commit_failure_artifact`) correctly stay in
  `implementation.py` — they have phase-level dependencies that
  would either create an upward import from `git_service.py` to
  `implementation.py` (forbidden) or broaden 11b's scope into
  workspace-authority territory (slice 11d). The "left behind"
  judgment is the prompt hard rule "if you find a git helper
  that's ambiguous (has phase-level dependencies), STOP and
  report — don't make a judgment call that splits a non-pure
  helper" applied consistently.

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11b independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3** on the first pass. All six
vectors PASS + all four doc-11 questions are answered
satisfactorily. Reviewer specifics:

- **V1 (doc-11 acceptance criteria for an extraction PR).** All
  four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions are answered
  above; the extraction order constraint (types first via 11a,
  then git_service in 11b) is satisfied; the compatibility-arrow
  direction (extracted module never imports back into
  `implementation.py`) is verified by inspection of
  `execution/git_service.py`'s import set — stdlib + `pathlib` +
  `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.types` only; no
  phase-module dependency.
- **V2 (source additivity + no-behavior-change).** Reviewer spot-
  checked 4 of 13 helpers byte-for-byte against `git show HEAD:
  …implementation.py` (`_parse_commit_failure_locations`,
  `_commit_failure_manifest_entries`, `_normalize_commit_
  failure_path`, `_run_git_for_commit`); every body is byte-for-
  byte equal to its pre-extraction form modulo whitespace/indent.
- **V3 (test honesty + shim coverage).** All 37 tests are real
  assertions (no `pytest.skip` except the conditional
  `git --version` gate on `_run_git_for_commit`; no `pass`-stubs);
  the identity assertions genuinely fail if the shim is removed;
  the `__module__` assertions genuinely fail if the moved name is
  re-aliased rather than re-imported. The reviewer verified the
  shim covers all 13 names by reading
  `implementation.py:368-382` and matching against
  `MOVED_GIT_SERVICE_NAMES`.
- **V4 (fail-closed edges).** The async subprocess wrapper
  `_run_git_for_commit` returns a structured `(returncode,
  stdout, stderr)` tuple; the conditional `git --version` gate
  in the smoke test ensures the test SKIPS cleanly (not fails)
  when `git` is missing. The commit-failure parser family is
  pure (no I/O except `_commit_failure_manifest_entries` which
  fast-paths a missing manifest to `[]`). No exception swallowing.
- **V5 (cross-component contract).** No other workflow module was
  modified for source — only `implementation.py` (the 13 helper
  removals + the extended shim block) and `git_service.py` (the
  13 helper additions). Imports from `git_service.py` are
  confined to (a) the shim at `implementation.py:368-382` and
  (b) the new test file's identity assertions; no other consumer
  imports the moved names. The minimal import set on the new
  surface is `re`, `pathlib`, `asyncio`, and the in-module
  `CommitFailureLocation` / `CommitRepoOutcome` types (from
  `execution/types.py`).
- **V6 (docs / ledger).** This journal entry + the JSONL
  acceptance line + the STATUS.md pointer advance constitute the
  V6 surface for 11b; the reviewer verified the journal entry's
  claims match the on-disk state.

- `_commit_failure_issue` / `_commit_failure_verdict` /
  `_commit_failure_payload` / `_record_commit_failure_artifact` /
  `_record_dag_commit_failure` — depend on `Issue` / `Verdict` /
  `WorkflowRunner` / `Feature` and the routing constants
  `_MANIFEST_FORBIDDEN_MARKER`, `_OPERATOR_REQUIRED_MARKER`,
  `_MANIFEST_FORBIDDEN_CLEANUP_ROUTE`, `_REPO_HYGIENE_ROUTE`,
  `_COMMIT_HYGIENE_ROUTE`, `_NORMAL_VERIFY_ROUTE`. These are
  verdict-construction / artifact-persistence concerns, not git
  primitives — they STAY in `implementation.py` until a future
  routing/repair extraction (likely **Slice 11h**) owns the
  verdict-construction surface.

**Slice 11 sub-slice plan (recap).** Per the Slice 11 STARTING
journal entry, the dependency-ordered plan is: 11a types (DONE) →
11b git_service (DONE) → 11c task_contracts → 11d sandbox → 11e
dispatcher → 11f gates → 11g verification → 11h repair → 11i
failure_router → **11j merge_queue (P3-6 fold-in PRIMARY OWNER)** →
11k regroup_overlay → 11l post_dag_gates → 11m post_test_guard →
11n slice-end SIX-VECTOR review + formal Slice 11 ACCEPTANCE.
**Next: 11c task_contracts.** Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API
contracts", the 11c implementer extracts the task-contract
compile/commit-guard surface (the `TaskContractCompileOutcome` and
`TaskContractCommitGuardOutcome` consumers + the build/check
helpers that have no phase-level dependencies); applies the same
shim + targeted-test pattern; answers the four doc-11 questions.

Slice 11b is closed. Slice 11c (extract `execution/task_contracts.
py`) is next; it must apply the same shim + targeted-test pattern
as 11a + 11b, answer the same four doc-11 questions, and
complement (not duplicate) any task-contract surface already in
`execution/`.



## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11c STARTING

The third extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the task-contract pure-helper surface — is
beginning. Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/task_contracts.py`, the module owns "Required/forbidden
path contracts, write-set authority, deliverable validation." The
Slice-03 ContractCompiler + ContractGroupCompileRequest +
TaskDeliverableContract + ContractVerdict + PatchSummary surface
already lives in `execution/task_contracts.py` (2194 lines as of
pre-11c). Slice 11c EXTENDS that module with the pure data-shape
helpers that currently live in `implementation.py` — projection-key
formatters and contract-key sanitization helpers — without dragging
in any runner-/feature-/verdict-/Issue-/I-O-coupled orchestrators.

**SCOPE TO MOVE (3 pure helpers).** Each helper is byte-for-byte
self-contained: only stdlib (`re`) is needed; no runner/feature/
verdict/Issue/`_model_json_dict`/`_workspace_authority_jsonable`/
`_dedupe_preserving_order` dependencies. The three:

1. `_task_contract_projection_key(task_id: str) -> str` —
   `implementation.py:2483-2484`. Returns the legacy artifact key
   `"dag-task-contract:{task_id}"` used by the contract projection
   path. Pure string format; no dependency on any
   `implementation.py` helper.
2. `_safe_contract_key_fragment(value: str) -> str` —
   `implementation.py:2779-2780`. Sanitizes an arbitrary string
   into a safe `[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+` artifact-key fragment, falling
   back to `"unknown"` when the result is empty. Uses only
   `re.sub` from the stdlib `re` module.
3. `_contract_stage_sandbox_id(group_idx: int, stage: str, repo_id:
   str) -> str` — `implementation.py:2783-2787`. Composes the
   pre-commit sandbox-id format
   `"canonical-precommit-g{group_idx}-{stage}-repo-{repo_id}"`,
   delegating to `_safe_contract_key_fragment` for sanitization.
   Depends only on `_safe_contract_key_fragment` (which is also
   moved in this slice — cluster moves together).

**SCOPE LEFT BEHIND (deferred — each with its named dependency
class).** Every other task-contract helper in `implementation.py`
has at least one dependency that violates the move criteria:

- `_task_contract_projection_summary` (`:2487`) — depends on
  `_model_json_dict` (a generic Pydantic/dataclass conversion
  utility, 35 callers in `implementation.py`). Moving
  `_model_json_dict` is out of scope for 11c (it's not task-
  contract-specific; moving 35 callsites broadens the surface).
  Deferred until a later utility-cluster slice or a future
  task_contracts extension that owns `_model_json_dict`.
- `_task_contract_digest` (`:2526`) — same `_model_json_dict`
  dependency. Deferred.
- `_task_contract_id` (`:2532`) — same `_model_json_dict`
  dependency. Deferred.
- `_task_contract_prompt_block` (`:2598`) — same. Deferred.
- `_contract_repo_id` (`:2768`) — same. Deferred.
- `_contract_repo_path` (`:2772`) — same. Deferred.
- `_contract_verdict_projection_key` (`:2790`) — uses
  `_model_json_dict`, `_contract_repo_id`,
  `_contract_stage_sandbox_id`. The first two are
  `_model_json_dict`-coupled and deferred; the third (the moved
  helper) cannot drag them. Deferred.
- `_contract_relative_observed_path` (`:2944`) — uses
  `_contract_repo_path` which depends on `_model_json_dict`.
  Deferred.
- `_contract_rule_matches_observed_path` (`:2973`) — uses
  `_model_json_dict` + `_contract_relative_observed_path`.
  Deferred.
- `_contract_guard_results_for_repair` (`:2999`) — uses
  `_contract_rule_matches_observed_path` (deferred) +
  `ImplementationResult` (importable). Deferred until the chain
  it depends on moves.
- `_dedupe_contract_items` (`:2870`) — uses
  `_workspace_authority_jsonable` (which is a workspace-authority
  helper slated for Slice 11d). Deferred to 11d.
- `_combined_contract_for_repo` (`:2882`) — uses
  `_dedupe_contract_items` + `_model_json_dict`. Deferred.
- `_result_paths_for_contracts` (`:2926`) — uses
  `_dedupe_preserving_order` (a generic shared utility, NOT in
  `types.py`; not task-contract-specific) +
  `_contract_repo_id`. Deferred.
- `_reported_paths_by_repo` (`:2952`) — uses `_contract_repo_id`
  (deferred) + `_contract_relative_observed_path` (deferred).
  Deferred.
- `_parse_git_status_patch_paths` (`:3034`) — uses
  `_dedupe_preserving_order`. Conceptually this is a git-status
  parser, not a task-contract concern; it likely belongs in
  `git_service.py` (Slice 11b's owner) in a future extension.
  Deferred.
- `_contract_workspace_snapshot` (`:3093`) — uses
  `WorkspaceAuthoritySnapshot` (a `Feature`-coupled snapshot
  builder) and reads `getattr(feature, "id", "")`. Phase-level.
  Deferred (likely Slice 11d sandbox or 11d workspace_authority).
- `_task_contract_ids_for_dispatch` (`:7390`) — uses
  `_task_contract_id` (deferred). Deferred.
- `_contract_runtime_roots` (`:4523`) — pure data extraction; no
  `_model_json_dict` dependency but is also a small helper. Could
  be moved in a future extension along with
  `_task_contract_ids_for_dispatch`. Deferred for tight 11c scope.
- `_persist_task_contracts` (`:2544`), `_task_contract_projection_
  matches` (`:2575`), `_compile_task_contracts_for_group`
  (`:2660`), `_task_contract_approved_verdict_exists` (`:2804`),
  `_completed_task_marker_has_current_lineage` (`:2844`),
  `_record_contract_patch_summary` (`:3123`),
  `_record_contract_verdict_projection` (`:3220`),
  `_record_precommit_contract_verdicts` (`:3294`) — all genuine
  PHASE-LEVEL: each takes `WorkflowRunner` + `Feature`, performs
  I-O via `runner.artifacts.get/put` or
  `store.put_task_contract/.record_patch_summary/.record_contract_
  verdict`, and orchestrates the persisted projection/transaction.
  These belong in a `journal.py`-mediated or `control_plane.py`-
  mediated extraction, not in pure `task_contracts.py`. Deferred
  per the prompt hard rule "if you find a task-contract helper
  that's ambiguous (has phase-level dependencies via `feature`,
  `runner`, `verdict`, `Issue`), LEAVE IT BEHIND and document the
  deferral."

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11c COMPLETE

Slice 11c (extend `execution/task_contracts.py` with the 3 pure
task-contract projection-key + sanitization helpers from `workflows/
develop/phases/implementation.py`) is **COMPLETE** and reviewed CLEAN
on the first review pass — **0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3**. The chunk delivers
the third extraction surface in Slice 11; complements (does NOT
duplicate) the Slice-03 `ContractCompiler` + request/response models
+ verdict types already in `execution/task_contracts.py`; preserves
the Slice-03 surface byte-for-byte; extends the Slice-11a +
Slice-11b shim pattern with a parallel Slice-11c re-export block;
locks the new facade with 10 targeted tests.

**WHAT LANDED — source.** Two modified modules; both edits are
mechanical extraction + re-export (no behavior change). The
implementer correctly chose to EXTEND the pre-existing
`execution/task_contracts.py` (2194 lines pre-11c from Slice 03 —
the side-effect-free contract compiler surface) rather than create
a new module; the doc-11 row for `execution/task_contracts.py`
("Required/forbidden path contracts, write-set authority,
deliverable validation") names a single module that owns this
domain, so an additive extension is the structurally-correct
choice.

- **MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/
  task_contracts.py` (was 2194 lines pre-11c from Slice 03; now
  2224 lines / 85190 bytes). The 3 helpers are appended at
  `:2172-2197` (the Slice-11c block; placed after the existing
  `_raise_worktree_alias_compile` at `:2162` and before the
  existing `__all__` at `:2199`). The pre-11c Slice-03 surface is
  preserved byte-for-byte: `AcceptanceCriterionSpec`,
  `ContractCompileError`, `ContractCompileRequest`,
  `ContractCompiler`, `ContractExecutionPolicy`,
  `ContractGroupCompileRequest`, `ContractPathRule`,
  `ContractVerdict`, `GateCommandSpec`, `PatchSummary`,
  `RequiredEvidenceSpec`, `TaskDeliverableContract`,
  `VerificationGateSpec`, `JsonValue`, `PathIntent`,
  `PathMatchKind`, `GateKind`, `EvidenceRequirementKind`,
  `WriteSetMode`, `SandboxIsolationMode`, `MergeAdmissionMode`.
  `__all__` extended with the 3 new names. The 3 moved helpers
  are:
  - **`_task_contract_projection_key(task_id: str) -> str`** —
    returns the legacy artifact-key format
    `"dag-task-contract:{task_id}"` used by the contract
    projection path. Pure one-liner; no dependency on any
    `implementation.py` helper.
  - **`_safe_contract_key_fragment(value: str) -> str`** —
    sanitizes an arbitrary string into a safe `[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+`
    artifact-key fragment via `re.sub(r"[^A-Za-z0-9_.-]+", "-",
    str(value or "").strip()).strip("-") or "unknown"`, with the
    `"unknown"` fallback when the result is empty. Uses only
    `re` from the stdlib.
  - **`_contract_stage_sandbox_id(group_idx: int, stage: str,
    repo_id: str) -> str`** — composes the pre-commit sandbox-id
    format `"canonical-precommit-g{group_idx}-{stage}-repo-
    {repo_id}"`, delegating to `_safe_contract_key_fragment` for
    sanitization of `stage` and `repo_id`. Depends only on the
    cluster-mate `_safe_contract_key_fragment` (which is also
    moved in this slice).
- **MODIFIED**
  `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`
  (line count: 33683 → 33686; net delta +3 lines — the 3 helper
  bodies were removed in place and the new shim block is the only
  net-positive addition, so the count is approximately
  conservation-of-bytes). The Slice-11a shim block at `:321-354`
  and the Slice-11b shim block at `:368-382` are UNCHANGED; a NEW
  parallel Slice-11c block was inserted at `:383-400`:
  `from ..execution.task_contracts import (_contract_stage_
  sandbox_id, _safe_contract_key_fragment, _task_contract_
  projection_key)`. The 7 internal `implementation.py` callsites
  and the 1 external attribute access at
  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_implementation_
  workspace_authority_adapter.py:1517` (which references
  `implementation_module._task_contract_projection_key`)
  continue resolving identically. Nothing else in
  `implementation.py` was edited — every method body, every
  routing function, every `ImplementationPhase` stage is
  byte-for-byte preserved.

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** One new file, purely additive. No
existing test was edited or removed.

- **NEW** `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_task_contracts_
  extraction.py` (238 lines, **10 tests**). The doc-11 § "How To
  Use This Map" four-question contract is locked in code:
  - **Parametrized shim identity (3 tests).** For every name in
    the moved-names list, asserts `getattr(implementation, X) is
    getattr(task_contracts, X)` — proving the shim re-exports
    the SAME object, not a copy. The `is` identity (rather than
    equality) is the structural property that keeps
    `monkeypatch.setattr` working: a patched attribute on
    `implementation_module` is the SAME binding the canonical
    module serves.
  - **Parametrized canonical `__module__` rebinding (3 tests).**
    For every moved name, asserts the canonical function
    object's `__module__` is `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.
    execution.task_contracts` (not `…phases.implementation`) —
    proving the definition genuinely moved rather than being
    re-aliased from the old module.
  - **4 behavioral smoke tests** covering cross-cutting
    behavioral invariants the moved helpers embody:
    1. `_task_contract_projection_key` artifact-key format pin
       — asserts the literal string output
       `"dag-task-contract:{task_id}"` for representative
       task-id inputs.
    2. `_safe_contract_key_fragment` normalization + fallback
       (8 cases) — covers the empty/whitespace/`None`-string
       inputs (fallback to `"unknown"`), the
       `[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+`-preserving cases, the
       leading/trailing-dash strip, and the special-character
       replacement.
    3. `_contract_stage_sandbox_id` canonical-precommit
       composition (4 cases) — covers the
       `"canonical-precommit-g{n}-{stage}-repo-{repo}"`
       composition across representative `(group_idx, stage,
       repo_id)` tuples, including the unsafe-character
       sanitization via the cluster-mate helper.
    4. Cluster ownership pin — asserts the 3 names land in the
       canonical `execution/task_contracts.py` module and not
       in any other `execution/` sibling (`types.py`,
       `git_service.py`).

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11c independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3** on the first pass. All six
vectors PASS + all four doc-11 questions are answered
satisfactorily. The reviewer additionally judged the NARROW 11c
scope as **ACCEPTABLE**: the deferred utility helpers (the 35-
caller generic `_model_json_dict`, the workspace-authority
`_workspace_authority_jsonable`, the generic `_dedupe_preserving_
order`) cannot move without broadening the surface beyond the
prompt's "do not touch other source files" hard rule; the
structurally-correct sequencing is utility-cluster sub-slice
first → 11c' (task-contract helpers depending on utilities) →
11d (workspace-authority cluster). The narrow 11c is a
defensible, surgical extraction that locks the simplest 3
pure-string helpers via the now-standard shim + targeted-test
pattern while preserving the prompt hard rule.

Reviewer specifics:

- **V1 (doc-11 acceptance criteria for an extraction PR).** All
  four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions are answered
  above; the extraction order constraint (types first via 11a,
  then git_service via 11b, then task_contracts via 11c) is
  satisfied; the compatibility-arrow direction (extracted
  module never imports back into `implementation.py`) is
  verified by inspection of `execution/task_contracts.py`'s
  import set — stdlib `re` and the in-module Slice-03 surface
  only; no phase-module dependency on the new 3-helper
  addition.
- **V2 (source additivity + no-behavior-change).** Reviewer
  spot-checked the 3 helper bodies byte-for-byte against
  `git show HEAD:…implementation.py`; every body is byte-for-
  byte equal to its pre-extraction form modulo whitespace/
  indent.
- **V3 (test honesty + shim coverage).** All 10 tests are real
  assertions (no `pytest.skip`; no `pass`-stubs); the identity
  assertions genuinely fail if the shim is removed; the
  `__module__` assertions genuinely fail if the moved name is
  re-aliased rather than re-imported. The reviewer verified the
  shim covers all 3 names by reading `implementation.py:383-400`
  and matching against the test file's `MOVED_NAMES`.
- **V4 (fail-closed edges).** Each moved helper is a pure
  string-formatting function; no I-O, no exceptions, no
  fail-closed branches. The `_safe_contract_key_fragment`
  fallback path (`"unknown"` when the cleaned input is empty)
  is structurally captured by the 8-case smoke test.
- **V5 (cross-component contract).** Only `implementation.py`
  (the 3 helper removals + the extended shim block) and
  `task_contracts.py` (the 3 helper additions + the `__all__`
  extension) were modified for source. Imports from
  `task_contracts.py` are confined to (a) the shim at
  `implementation.py:383-400` and (b) the new test file's
  identity assertions; no other consumer imports the moved
  names. The minimal import set on the new surface is the
  pre-existing module's `re` (already imported) plus the
  Slice-03 types.
- **V6 (docs / ledger).** This journal entry + the JSONL
  acceptance line + the STATUS.md pointer advance constitute
  the V6 surface for 11c; the reviewer verified the journal
  entry's claims match the on-disk state.

The reviewer also explicitly confirmed the narrow-scope choice
is defensible — the deferred clusters each have a NAMED
non-pure dependency (the 35-caller `_model_json_dict`, the
workspace-authority `_workspace_authority_jsonable`, the
generic `_dedupe_preserving_order`, or a `Feature`-coupled
helper) that justifies keeping them in `implementation.py`
until the owning utility-cluster sub-slice or workspace-
authority sub-slice (11d) lands.

  broader develop-workflow regression bundle).
- `tests/supervisor/`: **371 passed** (unchanged from Slice
  11b; Slice 11c did not edit supervisor code or tests).
- `tests/test_execution_control_store.py +
  tests/test_public_dashboard.py +
  tests/test_execution_control_startup.py`: **160 passed**
  (unchanged; no edits to these surfaces).

**Hygiene gate status.**

- `git diff --check` → CLEAN.

**"Left behind" list (deferred — each with its owning sub-slice
or required precondition).**

- **`_model_json_dict`-coupled cluster (11 helpers)** —
  `_task_contract_projection_summary` (`:2487`),
  `_task_contract_digest` (`:2526`), `_task_contract_id`
  (`:2532`), `_task_contract_prompt_block` (`:2598`),
  `_contract_repo_id` (`:2768`), `_contract_repo_path`
  (`:2772`), `_contract_verdict_projection_key` (`:2790`),
  `_contract_relative_observed_path` (`:2944`),
  `_contract_rule_matches_observed_path` (`:2973`),
  `_contract_guard_results_for_repair` (`:2999`),
  `_task_contract_ids_for_dispatch` (`:7390`). All depend
  (directly or transitively) on `_model_json_dict`, a generic
  35-caller Pydantic/dataclass conversion utility that is NOT
  task-contract-specific. Deferred to a utility-cluster sub-
  slice or to a future `types.py` expansion that takes
  ownership of `_model_json_dict`. Required precondition:
  utility-cluster sub-slice that owns `_model_json_dict`.
- **`_workspace_authority_jsonable`-coupled cluster (2
  helpers)** — `_dedupe_contract_items` (`:2870`),
  `_combined_contract_for_repo` (`:2882`). Both depend on
  `_workspace_authority_jsonable`, which is a workspace-
  authority helper. Deferred to **Slice 11d**
  (`execution/workspace_authority.py`).
- **`_dedupe_preserving_order`-coupled cluster (3 helpers)** —
  `_result_paths_for_contracts` (`:2926`),
  `_reported_paths_by_repo` (`:2952`),
  `_parse_git_status_patch_paths` (`:3034`). All depend on
  `_dedupe_preserving_order`, a generic shared utility used
  across the monolith — not task-contract-specific. Deferred
  to a utility-cluster sub-slice. (`_parse_git_status_patch_
  paths` conceptually belongs in `git_service.py` as a future
  extension.)
- **`Feature`-coupled `_contract_workspace_snapshot`
  (`:3093`)** — depends on `WorkspaceAuthoritySnapshot` (a
  `Feature`-coupled snapshot builder) and reads
  `getattr(feature, "id", "")`. Phase-level. Deferred to
  **Slice 11d** (sandbox or workspace_authority,
  whichever owns the `Feature`-snapshot surface).
- **Persistence/orchestration family (8 async helpers)** —
  `_persist_task_contracts` (`:2544`), `_task_contract_
  projection_matches` (`:2575`), `_compile_task_contracts_
  for_group` (`:2660`), `_task_contract_approved_verdict_
  exists` (`:2804`), `_completed_task_marker_has_current_
  lineage` (`:2844`), `_record_contract_patch_summary`
  (`:3123`), `_record_contract_verdict_projection` (`:3220`),
  `_record_precommit_contract_verdicts` (`:3294`). Each takes
  `WorkflowRunner` + `Feature`, performs I-O via
  `runner.artifacts.get/put` or
  `store.put_task_contract / .record_patch_summary /
  .record_contract_verdict`, and orchestrates the persisted
  projection/transaction. Genuinely PHASE-LEVEL. STAYS in
  `implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule against
  splitting non-pure helpers (a future `journal.py`- or
  `control_plane.py`-mediated extraction may own them; not in
  scope for the current 11c → 11n plan).

**Slice 11 sub-slice plan (recap).** Per the Slice 11 STARTING
journal entry, the dependency-ordered plan is: 11a types (DONE)
→ 11b git_service (DONE) → 11c task_contracts (DONE) → 11d
sandbox → 11e dispatcher → 11f gates → 11g verification → 11h
repair → 11i failure_router → **11j merge_queue (P3-6 fold-in
PRIMARY OWNER)** → 11k regroup_overlay → 11l post_dag_gates →
11m post_test_guard → 11n slice-end SIX-VECTOR review + formal
Slice 11 ACCEPTANCE. **Next: 11d sandbox** (per
`docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md` § "Proposed
Module Boundaries" — `execution/sandbox.py` is the next surface
after `task_contracts.py`). Some deferred 11c clusters could
naturally fold into 11d if 11d's surface claims them (notably
the `_contract_workspace_snapshot` Feature-coupled helper and
the `_workspace_authority_jsonable`-coupled
`_dedupe_contract_items` + `_combined_contract_for_repo`).

Slice 11c is closed. Slice 11d (extract `execution/sandbox.py`)
is next; it must apply the same shim + targeted-test pattern
as 11a + 11b + 11c, answer the same four doc-11 questions, and
complement (not duplicate) any sandbox surface already in
`execution/`.



## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11d STARTING

The fourth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the sandbox-lifecycle pure-helper surface —
is beginning. Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/sandbox.py`, the module owns "Sandbox lifecycle,
immutable patch evidence, runtime workspace binding." The Slice-04
`SandboxRunner` + `SandboxSpec` + `SandboxLease` +
`RuntimeWorkspaceBinding` + `PatchCaptureResult` + `SandboxRepoPatch`
+ the six `Sandbox*Error` subclasses are already in
`execution/sandbox.py` (2338 lines as of pre-11d) — that is the
canonical home for the Slice-04 sandbox-runner surface. Slice 11d
EXTENDS that module with the pure data-shape / format / lifecycle
helpers that currently live in `implementation.py` and depend only
on stdlib + `RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding` (already in `types.py`) +
`ImplementationTask` (already importable from `models.outputs`) +
the cluster-mate moved helpers — without dragging in any runner-/
feature-/store-instance-/contract-compiler-coupled orchestrators.

**SCOPE TO MOVE (6 pure helpers).** Each helper is byte-for-byte
self-contained: only stdlib (`json`, `os`, `pathlib.Path`) +
`SandboxWorkflowBlocker` (already in `types.py`) +
`RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding` (already in `types.py`) +
`ImplementationTask` (already in `models.outputs`) +
`_write_context_text` (cross-package from `_common._helpers`) is
needed; no runner / feature / store / contract-compiler / verdict /
Issue / phase-level helper dependencies. The six:

1. `_sandbox_blocker(message: str, *, task_id: str | None = None)
   -> SandboxWorkflowBlocker` — `implementation.py:4544-4545`.
   Factory wrapping `SandboxWorkflowBlocker(message,
   task_id=task_id)`. Pure two-liner; the only dependency is the
   typed exception class already in `types.py`. Used by EVERY other
   moveable helper as the canonical raise-site.
2. `_is_terminal_sandbox_attempt_blocker(message: str) -> bool` —
   `implementation.py:6520-6526`. String-matching predicate over
   the canonical "terminal sandbox lease" / "retained sandbox
   evidence requires/cannot" markers raised by the Slice-04
   `SandboxRunner.allocate` path. Pure 7-liner; no dependency.
3. `_sandbox_manifest_for_binding(binding: RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding)
   -> dict[str, Any]` — `implementation.py:6529-6536`. Reads the
   `sandbox-manifest.json` from `binding.lease.root` via
   `Path(...).read_text` + `json.loads`; raises `_sandbox_blocker`
   (which is also moved in this slice — cluster moves together) on
   any failure. Pure 8-liner; depends only on stdlib `json` + `Path`
   + the cluster-mate `_sandbox_blocker` + the typed
   `RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding`.
4. `_repair_repo_id_for_sandbox(group_tasks: list[ImplementationTask],
   contracts_by_task_id: dict[str, Any] | None, *, feature_root:
   Path | None, ws_path: str | None) -> str` —
   `implementation.py:6539-6560`. Pure repo-id derivation: scans
   `group_tasks` for a contract repo_id / task.repo_path fallback,
   else derives from `ws_path.relative_to(feature_root)`, else
   `"repo"`. No I-O, no runner/feature attribute access.
5. `_sandbox_prompt_context_dir(context_base: Path, *, task_id:
   str, context_segment: str) -> Path` —
   `implementation.py:11992-12019`. Pure dir-validation: resolves
   `context_base / ".iriai-context" / context_segment`, refuses
   symlinked roots/dirs, refuses paths that escape the context
   root. Raises `_sandbox_blocker` (cluster-mate) on every refusal.
   No runner/feature/store dependency.
6. `_exclude_sandbox_prompt_context_from_capture(context_base: Path,
   *, context_segment: str) -> None` —
   `implementation.py:12022-12042`. Appends `/.iriai-context/{
   segment}/` to `.git/info/exclude` to keep prompt-context side
   files from leaking into capture. Uses `_write_context_text` from
   `_common._helpers` (a sibling-package helper, NOT phase-level).

**SCOPE LEFT BEHIND (deferred — each with its named non-pure
dependency class).** Every other sandbox helper in
`implementation.py` has at least one dependency that violates the
move criteria:

- `_write_sandbox_settings(feature_root: Path)` (`:4328`) —
  feature-scaffold helper that writes `.claude/settings.json` +
  `CLAUDE.md` to each repo worktree under `feature_root`. Uses the
  module-level `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` at `:412` and
  logs via `logger.info(...)`. Conceptually a feature-creation /
  worktree-scaffold concern (NOT a sandbox-lifecycle primitive). The
  `logger` binding is implementation.py-namespaced; moving it would
  retarget the logger. Deferred (likely belongs in a future
  `workspace_authority.py` / feature-creation slice).
- `_sandbox_runtime_name(runtime: str | None, runner: WorkflowRunner
  | None = None)` (`:4488`) — depends on
  `_runtime_instance_name_for_hint(runner, runtime)` at `:4477`,
  which reads `runner.secondary_runtime` / `runner.agent_runtime`.
  Genuinely phase-level (runner attribute access). Also accessed
  via attribute lookup at `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.
  py:64-65` (`implementation_module._sandbox_runtime_name`); the
  shim already keeps the legacy import working — but the helper is
  NOT a pure sandbox primitive. STAYS in `implementation.py` per
  the prompt hard rule against splitting non-pure helpers.
- `_repair_sandbox_required(runner: WorkflowRunner, feature_root:
  Path | None) -> bool` (`:6580`) — reads `runner.services` to
  detect a workspace manager. Phase-level (runner instance attribute
  access). STAYS.
- `_sandbox_git_bytes(repo_path: Path, args: list[str], ...)`
  (`:6869`) — async git subprocess wrapper. Pure stdlib but
  conceptually `git_service.py` territory (Slice 11b's owner), NOT
  a sandbox primitive. Slice 11b moved 13 git-service helpers but
  did NOT include this async wrapper because it was not in the
  contract-failure parsing family; moving it now would broaden the
  Slice-11b surface. Deferred (likely a future `git_service.py`
  extension or a Slice-11b' follow-up).
- `_bind_repair_sandbox` (`:6770`), `_promote_sandbox_capture_to_
  feature_worktree` (`:6897`), `_validate_sandbox_capture_against_
  contract` (`:7039`), `_capture_and_promote_sandbox_patch`
  (`:7187`), `_bind_task_sandbox` (`:7274`), `_bind_post_dag_
  product_repair_sandbox` (`:32339`) — six async orchestrators that
  each take `runner: WorkflowRunner` + `feature: Feature`, perform
  sandbox-runner instance construction with `_execution_control_
  store_for_runner(runner)`, call into `_validate_sandbox_capture_
  against_contract` (which in turn calls `ContractCompiler.validate_
  patch` + `_contract_workspace_snapshot` + `_record_contract_patch_
  summary` + `_record_contract_verdict_projection` — all phase-
  level). Genuinely PHASE-LEVEL (the sandbox port IS phase-level
  because it adapts the runner+feature+store to the Slice-04
  `SandboxRunner` instance). STAYS in `implementation.py` per the
  prompt hard rule. Per the prompt edge-case note: "If you find a
  sandbox helper that's ambiguous (has phase-level dependencies via
  `feature`, `runner`, `store` instance methods), LEAVE IT BEHIND
  and document the deferral." Each of these orchestrators meets
  that ambiguity bar — they exist to adapt phase-level state to the
  pure `SandboxRunner` primitive. The Slice-04 `SandboxRunner` is
  the pure primitive; these are its callers.
- `_ImplementationSandboxPort` class (`:7699`) — adapter class
  wrapping `_bind_task_sandbox` + `_capture_and_promote_sandbox_
  patch` to satisfy the `RuntimeDispatcher` sandbox-port protocol.
  Holds `runner` + `feature` + `task_contract` + `task` state as
  instance attributes. Genuinely phase-level (constructor takes
  runner+feature+task_contract+task). STAYS.
- `_build_task_prompt_with_optional_sandbox_context` (`:12045`) —
  uses `_build_task_prompt` (a phase-level prompt builder that
  pulls in `_task_contract_prompt_block` which is `_model_json_dict`-
  coupled). The function itself uses `_sandbox_prompt_context_dir`
  + `_exclude_sandbox_prompt_context_from_capture` (cluster-mates
  being moved). Deferred until `_build_task_prompt` cluster can
  move (depends on the `_model_json_dict` utility-cluster sub-slice
  AND on `_task_contract_prompt_block` which is `_model_json_dict`-
  coupled per the 11c deferral). The moved cluster-mate helpers
  resolve via the shim, so this function continues to work after
  the move.

Per the prompt edge-case note, the sandbox port (the
`_ImplementationSandboxPort` class + the 6 phase-level
orchestrators) IS phase-level because it adapts the runner+feature
+store to the pure sandbox primitive (the Slice-04 `SandboxRunner`).
This is the structurally-correct boundary: the pure data-shape /
format / lifecycle helpers move; the adapter / port / orchestrator
class stays. The narrow 11d is therefore a defensible, surgical
extraction that locks 6 pure helpers via the now-standard shim +
targeted-test pattern while preserving the prompt hard rule.

**DOC-11 FOUR-QUESTION CONTRACT (preview).**

- **Q1 What behavior moved?** NONE — 6 pure helpers
  (`_sandbox_blocker`, `_is_terminal_sandbox_attempt_blocker`,
  `_sandbox_manifest_for_binding`, `_repair_repo_id_for_sandbox`,
  `_sandbox_prompt_context_dir`,
  `_exclude_sandbox_prompt_context_from_capture`) moved byte-for-
  byte; no signature, no body, no string literal, no return-value
  change.
- **Q2 What shims still work?** All 6 names re-exported at
  `implementation.py:401-{end-of-block}` (the new Slice-11d block,
  parallel to the Slice-11a / 11b / 11c blocks at `:321-400`)
  resolve to the SAME object as the canonical definition in
  `execution/sandbox.py`. The legacy `from iriai_build_v2.
  workflows.develop.phases.implementation import X` for any of the
  6 moved names continues to work via the shim.
- **Q3 What targeted tests prove the new facade + shim?**
  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_sandbox_extraction.py` —
  6 parametrized identity + 6 parametrized `__module__` rebinding
  + 3+ behavioral smoke tests.
- **Q4 Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only pure sandbox-lifecycle
  string/format/IO helpers moved; bodies preserved byte-for-byte.
  The much larger sandbox-port / orchestrator family
  (`_bind_repair_sandbox`, `_promote_sandbox_capture_to_feature_
  worktree`, `_validate_sandbox_capture_against_contract`,
  `_capture_and_promote_sandbox_patch`, `_bind_task_sandbox`,
  `_bind_post_dag_product_repair_sandbox`, the
  `_ImplementationSandboxPort` class) is genuinely PHASE-LEVEL
  (depends on `WorkflowRunner` + `Feature` + typed store
  transactions + `ContractCompiler` validation + the
  `_contract_workspace_snapshot` + projection / verdict-recording
  family) and CORRECTLY stays in `implementation.py` per the
  prompt hard rule against splitting non-pure helpers. The
  `_write_sandbox_settings` feature-scaffold helper is deferred
  because it logs through the implementation.py-namespaced module
  logger; it's not a sandbox-lifecycle primitive. The
  `_sandbox_runtime_name` + `_repair_sandbox_required` runner-
  attribute readers stay because they depend on
  `_runtime_instance_name_for_hint(runner)` and `runner.services`.
  The `_sandbox_git_bytes` async wrapper belongs in
  `git_service.py` (Slice 11b's owner), not `sandbox.py`. The
  `_build_task_prompt_with_optional_sandbox_context` depends on
  `_build_task_prompt` (which is `_model_json_dict`-coupled per
  the 11c deferral chain).



## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11d COMPLETE

Slice 11d (extend `execution/sandbox.py` with the 6 pure sandbox-
lifecycle helpers from `workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`)
is **COMPLETE** and reviewed CLEAN on the first review pass —
**0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3**. The chunk delivers the fourth extraction surface
in Slice 11; complements (does NOT duplicate) the Slice-04
`SandboxRunner` + `SandboxSpec` + `SandboxLease` + `RuntimeWorkspace
Binding` + `PatchCaptureResult` + `SandboxRepoPatch` + the six
`Sandbox*Error` subclasses already in `execution/sandbox.py`;
preserves the Slice-04 surface byte-for-byte; extends the Slice-11a
+ Slice-11b + Slice-11c shim pattern with a parallel Slice-11d
re-export block; locks the new facade with 19 targeted tests.

**WHAT LANDED — source.** Two modified modules; both edits are
mechanical extraction + re-export (no behavior change). The
implementer correctly chose to EXTEND the pre-existing
`execution/sandbox.py` (2338 lines pre-11d from Slice 04 — the
canonical home for the sandbox-runner / lease / patch-capture
primitive surface) rather than create a new module; the doc-11 row
for `execution/sandbox.py` ("Sandbox lifecycle, immutable patch
evidence, runtime workspace binding") names a single module that
owns this domain, so an additive extension is the structurally-
correct choice.

- **MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/
  sandbox.py` (was 2338 lines pre-11d from Slice 04; now 2468 lines;
  net delta +130). The 6 helpers are appended at `:2346-2443` (the
  Slice-11d block; preceded by a Slice-11d header banner at
  `:2334-2343` documenting the extension and placed after the
  existing Slice-04 surface). The pre-11d Slice-04 surface is
  preserved byte-for-byte: `SandboxRunner`, `SandboxSpec`,
  `SandboxLease`, `RuntimeWorkspaceBinding`, `PatchCaptureResult`,
  `SandboxRepoPatch`, the six `Sandbox*Error` subclasses, the typed
  manifest readers, and the lease/allocate path. Imports extended
  for the 6 new helpers: `SandboxWorkflowBlocker` +
  `RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding` from the sibling `types` module (3
  new sibling imports), `ImplementationTask` from the cross-package
  `models.outputs`, and `_write_context_text` from the cross-package
  `_common._helpers`. `__all__` extended with the 6 new names. The
  6 moved helpers are:
  - **`_sandbox_blocker(message: str, *, task_id: str | None = None)
    -> SandboxWorkflowBlocker`** — typed-exception factory wrapping
    `SandboxWorkflowBlocker(message, task_id=task_id)`. The canonical
    raise-site every other moveable helper delegates to. Pure two-
    liner; only dependency is the typed exception class already in
    `types.py`.
  - **`_is_terminal_sandbox_attempt_blocker(message: str) -> bool`** —
    string-marker predicate matching the canonical "terminal sandbox
    lease" / "retained sandbox evidence requires…" / "retained sandbox
    evidence cannot…" markers raised by the Slice-04 `SandboxRunner.
    allocate` path. Pure 7-liner; no dependency.
  - **`_sandbox_manifest_for_binding(binding: RuntimeSandboxTask
    Binding) -> dict[str, Any]`** — JSON-read of `sandbox-manifest.
    json` from `binding.lease.root` via `Path(...).read_text` +
    `json.loads`; raises `_sandbox_blocker` on every IO/parse failure
    with the canonical "sandbox manifest unreadable for task {task_
    id}" message. Pure 8-liner; depends only on stdlib `json` +
    `Path` + the cluster-mate `_sandbox_blocker` + the typed
    `RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding`.
  - **`_repair_repo_id_for_sandbox(group_tasks, contracts_by_task_id,
    *, feature_root, ws_path)`** — pure repo-id derivation with a
    4-level precedence: (1) `contracts_by_task_id[task.id].repo_id`,
    (2) `task.repo_path`, (3) `Path(ws_path).relative_to(feature_
    root).parts[0]`, (4) literal `"repo"`. No I/O, no runner/feature
    attribute access; only operates on the passed-in typed
    structures + stdlib `Path`.
  - **`_sandbox_prompt_context_dir(context_base: Path, *, task_id:
    str, context_segment: str) -> Path`** — symlink/escape-validating
    context-dir resolver. Resolves `context_base / ".iriai-context"
    / context_segment`; refuses symlinked roots/dirs; refuses paths
    that escape the context root via `resolve()` containment check;
    raises `_sandbox_blocker` (cluster-mate) on every refusal with
    a task-id-tagged message. No runner/feature/store dependency.
  - **`_exclude_sandbox_prompt_context_from_capture(context_base:
    Path, *, context_segment: str) -> None`** — idempotent
    `.git/info/exclude` append: writes `/.iriai-context/{segment}/`
    to the repo's git-exclude file via `_write_context_text` from
    `_common._helpers`, keeping prompt-context side files from
    leaking into capture. Idempotent (re-running is a no-op).
- **MODIFIED**
  `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`
  (line count: 33686 → 33613; net delta -73 lines). The 6 helper
  bodies were removed in place at their pre-11d source locations
  (`:4544-4545, :6520-6526, :6529-6536, :6539-6560, :11992-12019,
  :12022-12042`); the Slice-11a shim block at `:321-354`, the
  Slice-11b shim block at `:368-382`, and the Slice-11c shim block
  at `:383-400` are UNCHANGED; a NEW parallel sibling Slice-11d
  block was inserted at `:401-427`: a header banner at `:401-419`
  documenting the extraction + the boundary contract + the deferred
  port surface, followed by the re-import at `:420-427`:
  `from ..execution.sandbox import (_exclude_sandbox_prompt_
  context_from_capture, _is_terminal_sandbox_attempt_blocker, _
  repair_repo_id_for_sandbox, _sandbox_blocker, _sandbox_manifest_
  for_binding, _sandbox_prompt_context_dir)`. Nothing else in
  `implementation.py` was edited — every method body, every routing
  function, every `ImplementationPhase` stage is byte-for-byte
  preserved; the 6 phase-level sandbox-PORT orchestrators
  (`_bind_repair_sandbox`, `_promote_sandbox_capture_to_feature_
  worktree`, `_validate_sandbox_capture_against_contract`,
  `_capture_and_promote_sandbox_patch`, `_bind_task_sandbox`,
  `_bind_post_dag_product_repair_sandbox`) + the
  `_ImplementationSandboxPort` adapter class + the runner-coupled
  `_write_sandbox_settings` / `_sandbox_runtime_name` / `_repair_
  sandbox_required` / `_sandbox_git_bytes` / `_build_task_prompt_
  with_optional_sandbox_context` remain in place per the prompt
  hard rule (deferred per named ownership — see "Left behind" list
  below).

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** One new file, purely additive. No existing
test was edited or removed.

- **NEW** `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_sandbox_
  extraction.py` (527 lines, **19 tests**). The doc-11 § "How To
  Use This Map" four-question contract is locked in code:
  - **Parametrized shim identity (6 tests).** For every name in
    the moved-names list, asserts `getattr(implementation, X) is
    getattr(sandbox, X)` — proving the shim re-exports the SAME
    object, not a copy. The `is` identity (rather than equality)
    is the structural property that keeps `monkeypatch.setattr`
    working: a patched attribute on `implementation_module` is the
    SAME binding the canonical module serves.
  - **Parametrized canonical `__module__` rebinding (6 tests).**
    For every moved name, asserts the canonical function object's
    `__module__` is `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.
    sandbox` (not `…phases.implementation`) — proving the
    definition genuinely moved rather than being re-aliased from
    the old module.
  - **7 behavioral smoke tests** covering cross-cutting behavioral
    invariants the moved helpers embody:
    1. `_sandbox_blocker` typed-blocker construction — asserts
       the returned object is a `SandboxWorkflowBlocker` carrying
       the message + the passed `task_id` (positive and None
       cases).
    2. `_is_terminal_sandbox_attempt_blocker` terminal-marker
       matching — exercises the canonical "terminal sandbox
       lease" / "retained sandbox evidence requires" / "retained
       sandbox evidence cannot" markers positive AND negative
       (non-terminal text returns False).
    3. `_sandbox_manifest_for_binding` happy path — writes a real
       `sandbox-manifest.json` to a temp lease root and asserts
       round-trip dict equality.
    4. `_sandbox_manifest_for_binding` error paths — asserts a
       missing manifest AND a malformed-JSON manifest both raise
       `SandboxWorkflowBlocker` carrying the binding's task_id.
    5. `_repair_repo_id_for_sandbox` 4-precedence — exercises
       each of the four precedence levels (contract.repo_id;
       task.repo_path fallback; `ws_path.relative_to(feature_
       root)` derivation; the literal `"repo"` floor).
    6. `_sandbox_prompt_context_dir` refusal cases — asserts a
       symlinked context root, a symlinked target dir, and a path
       that escapes the context root all raise
       `SandboxWorkflowBlocker` with a task-id-tagged message.
    7. `_exclude_sandbox_prompt_context_from_capture` idempotent
       git-exclude append — writes once, asserts the segment
       lands in `.git/info/exclude`; re-runs and asserts the
       file does not gain duplicates (the idempotent invariant).
    Plus a cluster-ownership pin asserting the 6 names land in
    the canonical `execution/sandbox.py` module and not in any
    other `execution/` sibling.

**DOC-11 FOUR-QUESTION CONTRACT (per reviewer).**

- **Q1 — What behavior moved?** **NONE.** 6 pure sandbox-lifecycle
  helpers (`_sandbox_blocker`, `_is_terminal_sandbox_attempt_
  blocker`, `_sandbox_manifest_for_binding`, `_repair_repo_id_
  for_sandbox`, `_sandbox_prompt_context_dir`, `_exclude_sandbox_
  prompt_context_from_capture`) moved byte-for-byte from
  `implementation.py:4544-4545, :6520-6526, :6529-6536,
  :6539-6560, :11992-12019, :12022-12042` to `execution/sandbox.
  py:2346-2443`. The reviewer spot-checked 3 of 6 helper bodies
  against `git show HEAD:…implementation.py`; every body is byte-
  for-byte equal to its pre-extraction form modulo whitespace/
  indent. No logic change; no signature change; no validation
  added or removed; no routing constant moved; no string-literal
  change.
- **Q2 — What shims still work?** **All 6 names.** Re-exported at
  `implementation.py:401-427` (a parallel sibling block of the
  Slice-11a shim at `:321-354`, the Slice-11b shim at `:368-382`,
  and the Slice-11c shim at `:383-400`). Every existing `from
  iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import X`
  for any of the 6 moved names resolves to the SAME object as
  `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.sandbox import
  X` — proven by the 6 parametrized identity assertions. No
  external legacy importer or `monkeypatch.setattr(
  implementation_module, "<one of the 6>", ...)` target exists in
  `src/` or `tests/` for any of the 6 names — the shim is
  preventive (locked-in by the standard 11.x pattern) rather than
  consumer-facing. Internal `implementation.py` callsites of all
  6 moved names continue resolving identically via the shim
  (identity-equivalent).
- **Q3 — What targeted tests prove the new facade + the shim?**
  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_sandbox_extraction.py`
  — **19 tests** (6 parametrized identity + 6 parametrized
  `__module__` rebinding + 7 behavioral smoke tests: typed-
  blocker construction; terminal-marker matching; manifest
  happy path + missing + malformed; repo_id 4-precedence;
  symlink/escape refusals; idempotent git-exclude append;
  cluster ownership pin). Locks both the structural identity
  and the canonical behavior of each moved helper.
- **Q4 — Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only pure sandbox-
  lifecycle data-shape / format / IO-validation helpers moved;
  bodies preserved byte-for-byte. The 6 phase-level sandbox-PORT
  async orchestrators (`_bind_repair_sandbox`, `_promote_sandbox_
  capture_to_feature_worktree`, `_validate_sandbox_capture_
  against_contract`, `_capture_and_promote_sandbox_patch`,
  `_bind_task_sandbox`, `_bind_post_dag_product_repair_sandbox`)
  + the `_ImplementationSandboxPort` adapter class + the runner-
  / feature- / logger- / generic-utility-coupled siblings
  (`_write_sandbox_settings`, `_sandbox_runtime_name`,
  `_repair_sandbox_required`, `_sandbox_git_bytes`, `_build_
  task_prompt_with_optional_sandbox_context`) correctly stay in
  `implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule against splitting
  non-pure helpers (each one has a named non-pure dependency on
  `WorkflowRunner` / `Feature` / `_execution_control_store_for_
  runner` / `ContractCompiler.validate_patch` / `_contract_
  workspace_snapshot` / `_record_contract_patch_summary` /
  `_record_contract_verdict_projection` / the implementation.py-
  namespaced module logger / a generic-utility helper).

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11d independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3** on the first pass. All six
vectors PASS + all four doc-11 questions are answered
satisfactorily. The reviewer additionally judged the sandbox
PRIMITIVE/PORT split as **ACCEPTABLE**: a structurally-honest
separation, not work-dodging. The pure sandbox-lifecycle primitives
(blocker factory, terminal-attempt classifier, manifest reader,
repo-id derivation, prompt-context dir validator + exclude writer)
move into `execution/sandbox.py` alongside the existing Slice-04
`SandboxRunner` primitive; the phase-level sandbox PORT (the
`_ImplementationSandboxPort` adapter class + the 6 async
orchestrators) stays in `implementation.py` because that port
exists to adapt phase-level state (`runner` + `feature` + `store` +
`contract_compiler`) to the pure `SandboxRunner` primitive — which
is the structurally-correct boundary per doc 11 § "Boundary-level
API contracts" (the named API for `execution/sandbox.py` is
`SandboxRunner.prepare`, `bind_runtime`, `capture_patch`, `discard`
— the PRIMITIVE; the adapter that satisfies the
`RuntimeDispatcher` sandbox-port protocol from
`implementation.py`'s runner+feature state is by definition
phase-level). The narrow 11d is therefore a defensible, surgical
extraction that locks 6 pure helpers via the now-standard shim +
targeted-test pattern while preserving the prompt hard rule.

Reviewer specifics:

- **V1 (doc-11 acceptance criteria for an extraction PR).** All
  four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions are answered
  above; the extraction order constraint (types via 11a, then
  git_service via 11b, then task_contracts via 11c, then sandbox
  via 11d) is satisfied; the compatibility-arrow direction
  (extracted module never imports back into `implementation.py`)
  is verified by inspection of `execution/sandbox.py`'s import
  set — stdlib `json` + `os` + `pathlib.Path`, the sibling
  `execution.types` module (`SandboxWorkflowBlocker`,
  `RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding`), the cross-package `models.
  outputs.ImplementationTask`, and the cross-package
  `_common._helpers._write_context_text`. No upward import from
  `execution/sandbox.py` into `implementation.py` or any phase
  module.
- **V2 (source additivity + no-behavior-change).** Reviewer
  spot-checked 3 of 6 helper bodies byte-for-byte against
  `git show HEAD:…implementation.py`; every body is byte-for-
  byte equal to its pre-extraction form modulo whitespace/indent.
  The Slice-04 surface in `execution/sandbox.py` (the
  `SandboxRunner` + 5 typed records + 6 `Sandbox*Error` classes)
  is preserved byte-for-byte; the 6 new helpers occupy a fresh
  block at `:2346-2443` separated by the Slice-11d header
  banner at `:2334-2343`.
- **V3 (test honesty + shim coverage).** All 19 tests are real
  assertions (no `pytest.skip`; no `pass`-stubs); the identity
  assertions genuinely fail if the shim is removed; the
  `__module__` assertions genuinely fail if the moved name is
  re-aliased rather than re-imported; the behavioral smoke
  assertions exercise real temp filesystem state for the
  manifest reader, the prompt-context dir validator, and the
  idempotent git-exclude append. The reviewer verified the shim
  covers all 6 names by reading `implementation.py:401-427` and
  matching against the test file's `MOVED_NAMES`.
- **V4 (fail-closed edges).** Each pure helper's refusal path
  is structurally exercised: `_sandbox_blocker` produces a
  typed `SandboxWorkflowBlocker` (not a generic `Exception`);
  `_sandbox_manifest_for_binding` raises `SandboxWorkflowBlocker`
  on a missing manifest AND on malformed JSON (the test asserts
  the typed class + the task-id propagation); `_sandbox_prompt_
  context_dir` refuses symlink roots, symlink dirs, and escape
  paths (three separate assertion blocks); `_exclude_sandbox_
  prompt_context_from_capture` is idempotent (re-running does
  not duplicate the exclude line). No exception swallowing in
  any helper.
- **V5 (cross-component contract).** Only `implementation.py`
  (the 6 helper removals + the extended shim block) and
  `sandbox.py` (the 6 helper additions + the `__all__`
  extension + the 3 new sibling imports) were modified for
  source. Imports from `sandbox.py` are confined to (a) the
  shim at `implementation.py:401-427` and (b) the new test
  file's identity assertions; no other consumer imports the
  moved names. The minimal import set on the new surface is
  the pre-existing module's stdlib imports plus the three new
  sibling-module imports (`types.SandboxWorkflowBlocker`,
  `types.RuntimeSandboxTaskBinding`, `models.outputs.
  ImplementationTask`) + the cross-package `_common._helpers.
  _write_context_text`.
- **V6 (docs / ledger).** This journal entry + the JSONL
  acceptance line + the STATUS.md pointer advance constitute
  the V6 surface for 11d; the reviewer verified the journal
  entry's claims match the on-disk state.

The reviewer also explicitly confirmed the sandbox PRIMITIVE/PORT
split is defensible — the phase-level orchestrators each have a
named non-pure dependency (`runner`+`feature`+`contract_compiler`
or a runner-instance attribute reader or the implementation.py-
namespaced module logger or a generic-utility coupling) that
justifies keeping them in `implementation.py` until the owning
sub-slice or utility-cluster sub-slice lands.

**TARGETED REGRESSION (reviewer's re-runs).** All targeted suites
GREEN post-edit:

- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_sandbox_extraction.py`:
  **19 passed** (the new file).
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/`: **790 passed** (was 771 +
  19 new extraction tests; no existing test weakened or removed).
- `tests/workflows/`: **1553 passed** (was 1534 + 19 new; the
  broader develop-workflow regression bundle).
- `tests/supervisor/`: **371 passed** (unchanged from Slice 11c;
  Slice 11d did not edit supervisor code or tests).
- `tests/test_execution_control_store.py +
  tests/test_public_dashboard.py +
  tests/test_execution_control_startup.py`: **160 passed**
  (unchanged; no edits to these surfaces).

**Hygiene gate status.**

- `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py` →
  CLEAN.
- `git diff --check` → CLEAN.
- JSONL parse (`python -c "import json; [json.loads(l) for l
