  below.

**"Left behind" list (deferred — each with its owning sub-slice
or required precondition).**

- **`_write_sandbox_settings(feature_root)`** (`implementation.
  py:4328`) — feature-scaffold helper writing `.claude/settings.
  json` + `CLAUDE.md` to each repo worktree under `feature_root`;
  uses the module-level `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` at
  `:412` and logs via `logger.info(...)`. The `logger` binding is
  `implementation.py`-namespaced; moving it would retarget the
  logger. Conceptually a feature-creation / worktree-scaffold
  concern (NOT a sandbox-lifecycle primitive). Deferred (likely
  belongs in a future `workspace_authority.py` / feature-creation
  slice).
- **`_sandbox_runtime_name(runtime, runner=None)`** (`:4488`) —
  depends on `_runtime_instance_name_for_hint(runner, runtime)`
  at `:4477`, which reads `runner.secondary_runtime` /
  `runner.agent_runtime`. Genuinely phase-level (runner attribute
  access). Also accessed via attribute lookup at `tests/workflows/
  test_dag_expanded_verify.py:64-65`
  (`implementation_module._sandbox_runtime_name`); the existing
  monkeypatch surface already keeps the legacy import working —
  but the helper is NOT a pure sandbox primitive. STAYS in
  `implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule against splitting
  non-pure helpers.
- **`_repair_sandbox_required(runner, feature_root)`** (`:6580`) —
  reads `runner.services` to detect a workspace manager. Phase-
  level (runner instance attribute access). STAYS.
- **`_sandbox_git_bytes(repo_path, args, ...)`** (`:6869`) — async
  git subprocess wrapper; pure stdlib but conceptually
  `git_service.py` territory (Slice 11b's owner), NOT a sandbox
  primitive. Slice 11b moved 13 git-service helpers but did NOT
  include this async wrapper because it was not in the contract-
  failure parsing family; moving it now would broaden the
  Slice-11b surface. Deferred (likely a future `git_service.py`
  extension or a Slice-11b' follow-up).
- **`_bind_repair_sandbox` (`:6770`),
  `_promote_sandbox_capture_to_feature_worktree` (`:6897`),
  `_validate_sandbox_capture_against_contract` (`:7039`),
  `_capture_and_promote_sandbox_patch` (`:7187`),
  `_bind_task_sandbox` (`:7274`),
  `_bind_post_dag_product_repair_sandbox` (`:32339`)** — six
  async sandbox-PORT orchestrators that each take `runner:
  WorkflowRunner` + `feature: Feature`, perform sandbox-runner
  instance construction with `_execution_control_store_for_
  runner(runner)`, call into `_validate_sandbox_capture_against_
  contract` (which in turn calls `ContractCompiler.validate_
  patch` + `_contract_workspace_snapshot` + `_record_contract_
  patch_summary` + `_record_contract_verdict_projection` — all
  phase-level). Genuinely PHASE-LEVEL (the sandbox PORT IS
  phase-level because it adapts the runner+feature+store to the
  Slice-04 `SandboxRunner` instance). STAYS in `implementation.
  py` per the prompt hard rule. Per the prompt edge-case note —
  "If you find a sandbox helper that's ambiguous (has phase-level
  dependencies via `feature`, `runner`, `store` instance
  methods), LEAVE IT BEHIND and document the deferral" — each of
  these orchestrators meets that ambiguity bar.
- **`_ImplementationSandboxPort` class** (`:7699`) — adapter
  class wrapping `_bind_task_sandbox` + `_capture_and_promote_
  sandbox_patch` to satisfy the `RuntimeDispatcher` sandbox-port
  protocol. Holds `runner` + `feature` + `task_contract` +
  `task` state as instance attributes. Genuinely phase-level
  (constructor takes runner+feature+task_contract+task). STAYS.
- **`_build_task_prompt_with_optional_sandbox_context`**
  (`:12045`) — uses `_build_task_prompt` (a phase-level prompt
  builder that pulls in `_task_contract_prompt_block` which is
  `_model_json_dict`-coupled per the 11c deferral chain). The
  function itself uses `_sandbox_prompt_context_dir` +
  `_exclude_sandbox_prompt_context_from_capture` (cluster-mates
  moved in 11d). Deferred until `_build_task_prompt` cluster can
  move (depends on the `_model_json_dict` utility-cluster
  sub-slice AND on `_task_contract_prompt_block` which is
  `_model_json_dict`-coupled per the 11c deferral). The moved
  cluster-mate helpers resolve via the shim, so this function
  continues to work after the move.

**Slice 11 sub-slice plan (recap).** Per the Slice 11 STARTING
journal entry, the dependency-ordered plan is: 11a types (DONE)
→ 11b git_service (DONE) → 11c task_contracts (DONE) → 11d
sandbox (DONE) → 11e dispatcher → 11f gates → 11g verification
→ 11h repair → 11i failure_router → **11j merge_queue (P3-6
fold-in PRIMARY OWNER)** → 11k regroup_overlay → 11l post_dag_
gates → 11m post_test_guard → 11n slice-end SIX-VECTOR review +
formal Slice 11 ACCEPTANCE. **Next: 11e dispatcher** (per
`docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md` § "Proposed
Module Boundaries" — `execution/dispatcher.py` is the next
surface after `sandbox.py`). The 11e implementer should:
inventory any existing `execution/dispatcher.py` if pre-
existing; identify dispatcher helpers in `implementation.py`
(search `_dispatch_*`, `Dispatcher`, `_dispatch_attempt_*`);
apply the same shim + targeted-test pattern as 11a + 11b + 11c
+ 11d; answer the same four doc-11 questions in the AFTER
chunk; complement (not duplicate) any dispatcher surface
already in `execution/`.

Slice 11d is closed. Slice 11e (extract `execution/dispatcher.
py`) is next.



## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11e STARTING

The fifth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the dispatcher-domain pure-primitive surface —
is beginning. Per doc 11 § "Compatibility Shims" row 3 (Dispatcher/
runtime), the migration list calls out `_make_parallel_actor`,
`_runner_runtime_policy`, `_dag_group_runtime_pair`, `_post_dag_
runtime_pair`, `_diagnostic_runtime_for_policy`, `_dag_repair_
runtime_for` for delegate target `dispatcher.py`. Per doc 11 §
"Boundary-level API contracts" row for `execution/dispatcher.py`,
the module owns "Provider/runtime invocation boundary, retry
idempotency, runtime error normalization." The Slice-05
`RuntimeDispatcher` + ports (DispatchJournalPort, SandboxRunnerPort,
RuntimeClientPort, ContractPromptBuilderPort,
StructuredOutputNormalizerPort, ClockPort, IdempotencyKeyFactoryPort,
CancellationRegistryPort) + the typed Pydantic models (DispatchRequest,
DispatchOutcome, DispatchRetryIdentity, ActorMetadata,
PromptContextBundle, RuntimeWorkspaceBinding, RuntimeInvocationRequest,
RuntimeInvocationResponse, StructuredOutputRecord, RuntimeFailureRecord,
PromptBuildResult, PatchCaptureRecord, DispatchAttemptRecord) + the
state-machine validator + the digest helpers (stable_json,
stable_digest, actor_metadata_digest, dispatch_request_digest,
dispatch_idempotency_key) + the default port implementations
(DefaultClock, DefaultIdempotencyKeyFactory, NullCancellationRegistry,
DefaultStructuredOutputNormalizer) + the response classification
helpers (_classify_runtime_response,
_canonical_provider_failure_type, etc.) are already in
`execution/dispatcher.py` (1941 lines as of pre-11e from Slice 05) —
that is the canonical home for the Slice-05 RuntimeDispatcher
surface. Slice 11e EXTENDS that module with the pure runtime-
selection / parallel-actor primitives that currently live in
`implementation.py` and depend only on stdlib + the existing
`RuntimePolicy` Literal type + the `AgentActor` class from
`iriai_compose` — without dragging in any
`WorkflowRunner`/`Feature`/`logger`/`_model_json_dict`-coupled
adapters.

**SCOPE TO MOVE (6 pure primitives).** Each one is byte-for-byte
self-contained: only `RuntimePolicy` /
`PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW_POLICY` from `runtime_policy.py` +
`AgentActor` from `iriai_compose` is needed; no runner / feature /
store / contract-compiler / verdict / Issue / `_model_json_dict` /
implementation.py-namespaced logger dependencies. The six:

1. `DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES: dict[str, str]` —
   `implementation.py:470-487`. The static 13-entry role→runtime map
   used by `_dag_repair_runtime_for` to select the runtime for each
   DAG repair role / lens. Under the bridge-claude-pool-codex-review
   configuration, primary=Claude pool and secondary=Codex; the map
   pins `dag-normal-verify`/`dag-final-verify`/`dag-contradiction-
   resolve` + the acceptance-coverage/contract-protocol lenses to
   `secondary` and the `dag-triage`/`dag-rca`/`dag-fix`/`dag-focused-
   reverify` family + the build-dependency/runtime-composition/
   security-boundary/regression-downstream lenses to `primary`. Pure
   data; no dependency.
2. `_dag_repair_runtime_for(role_or_lens, fallback=None) -> str | None`
   — `implementation.py:509-513`. Pure 4-liner returning
   `DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES.get(role_or_lens, fallback)`. Only
   depends on the cluster-mate `DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES`.
3. `_dag_group_runtime_pair(group_idx, runtime_policy) -> tuple[str, str]`
   — `implementation.py:1474-1485`. Pure 12-liner returning
   `(implementation_runtime, review_runtime)` for a DAG group. Under
   PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW: always `("primary", "secondary")`.
   Under DEFAULT: alternates `("primary", "secondary")` on even
   groups, `("secondary", "primary")` on odd. Only depends on
   `RuntimePolicy` + `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW_POLICY`.
4. `_post_dag_runtime_pair(last_group_idx, runtime_policy) ->
   tuple[str, str]` — `implementation.py:1488-1499`. Pure 12-liner
   returning `(gate_runtime, fix_runtime)` for post-DAG gates. Under
   PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW: always `("secondary", "primary")`.
   Under DEFAULT: INVERTED from `_dag_group_runtime_pair` to ensure
   the post-DAG verifier is a different model from the implementer
   of the last group. Same dependency set.
5. `_diagnostic_runtime_for_policy(runtime_policy) -> str | None` —
   `implementation.py:1502-1506`. Pure 5-liner returning the
   RCA/triage/regression runtime: `"secondary"` under
   PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW, `None` under DEFAULT (so callers
   fall back to per-task/per-group routing). Same dependency set.
6. `_make_parallel_actor(base, suffix, *, runtime=None,
   workspace_path=None, runtime_workspace_binding=None,
   sandbox_required=False) -> AgentActor` —
   `implementation.py:4458-4501`. Pure 44-liner creating a parallel-
   safe copy of an `AgentActor` with a unique name +
   `model_copy`-rebuilt role whose metadata is extended (not mutated)
   with `runtime` / `workspace_override` / `sandbox_required` /
   `runtime_workspace_binding` / `write_producing`. Only depends on
   `AgentActor` from `iriai_compose`.

**SCOPE LEFT BEHIND (deferred — each with its named non-pure
dependency class).** Every other dispatcher-domain helper in
`implementation.py` has at least one dependency that violates the
move criteria:

- `_runner_runtime_policy(runner: WorkflowRunner) -> RuntimePolicy`
  (`:1461`) — reads `runner.services` (a dict-pattern access) AND
  logs through the module-level `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)`
  at `:412` via `logger.warning(...)`. The `logger` binding is
  `implementation.py`-namespaced; moving it would retarget the
  logger. Same criterion that kept `_write_sandbox_settings` phase-
  level in Slice 11d. Doc 11 § "Compatibility Shims" row 3 lists it
  in the Dispatcher/runtime shim group, but that target is the
  eventual home — not a Slice-11e-only move; the logger coupling is
  the same as the 11d `_write_sandbox_settings` deferral pattern.
  Deferred to a future utility-cluster sub-slice (the same one that
  would move `_model_json_dict`).
- `_runtime_instance_name_for_hint(runner: WorkflowRunner, runtime:
  str | None) -> str` (`:4504`) — reads `runner.secondary_runtime` /
  `runner.agent_runtime`. Genuinely phase-level (runner attribute
  access). STAYS.
- `_sandbox_runtime_name(runtime: str | None, runner: WorkflowRunner
  | None = None) -> str` (`:4515`) — depends on
  `_runtime_instance_name_for_hint(runner, runtime)`. Genuinely phase-
  level. STAYS (already documented as deferred in the Slice-11d
  journal entry).
- `_dispatcher_actor_metadata(runner, actor_suffix, runtime_hint,
  runtime_policy)` (`:7392`) — depends on `_sandbox_runtime_name`
  (runner-coupled), the module-level `implementer` Role binding, the
  Slice-05 `DispatcherActorMetadata` Pydantic model, and the
  Slice-05 digest helper `dispatcher_actor_metadata_digest`. Phase-
  level (runner + module-level Role binding). STAYS.
- `_dispatcher_request_for_task(runner, feature, dag_sha256,
  group_idx, task_idx, attempt, task, task_contract, snapshots,
  ws_path, runtime_hint, runtime_policy, actor_suffix, stage, ...)`
  (`:7429`) — depends on `_sandbox_blocker` (Slice 11d sandbox
  shim), `_contract_repo_id` (`_model_json_dict`-coupled per the
  Slice-11c deferral chain), `_task_contract_ids_for_dispatch`
  (depends on `_task_contract_id`), `_workspace_snapshot_ids_for_
  dispatch` (depends on `_snapshot_id_for_repo`), `_base_commit_
  for_dispatch` (depends on `_git_text`), and the Slice-05
  Pydantic models + digest helpers. Genuinely phase-level. STAYS.
- `_runtime_policy_digest_for_dispatch(runtime_policy)` (`:7367`),
  `_task_contract_ids_for_dispatch(task_contract)` (`:7373`),
  `_workspace_snapshot_ids_for_dispatch(snapshots, repo_id)`
  (`:7378`), `_base_commit_for_dispatch(ws_path, repo_id)` (`:7383`)
  — small dispatcher-domain builders, but each one depends on an
  impl.py-local helper (`_sha256_text`, `_task_contract_id`,
  `_snapshot_id_for_repo`, `_git_text`). Moving them would import
  those dependencies from implementation.py, which is forbidden by
  the prompt hard rule. Deferred until those utilities move.
- `_dispatch_attempt_duplicate_replay_recovery_evidence(payload)`
  (`:8191`) — depends on the impl.py-local `_model_json_dict`
  (same 35-caller utility that kept the Slice-11c task-contract
  cluster phase-level). Deferred.
- `DISPATCH_ATTEMPT_RECOVERY_EVIDENCE_FIELDS` (`:8183`) — module-
  level constant used exclusively by
  `_dispatch_attempt_duplicate_replay_recovery_evidence` (above) +
  `_ExecutionControlDispatchJournalPort.record_runtime_failure`
  (phase-level class). Moving it alone would leave the consumers
  reaching back. Deferred with its consumers.
- `_dispatch_journal_port_for_runner(runner, feature)` (`:8302`) —
  depends on `_execution_control_store_for_runner(runner)`. Phase-
  level. STAYS.
- `_dispatch_task_attempt_via_runtime_dispatcher(...)` (`:8312`) —
  async orchestrator that takes runner+feature, constructs the
  `_ImplementationSandboxPort` adapter, the
  `_ImplementationRuntimeClient`, the `_ImplementationOutputNormalizer`,
  the `_ImplementationPromptBuilder`, and the `_ExecutionControl
  DispatchJournalPort`/`_ArtifactDispatchJournalPort`, and calls
  `dispatcher.dispatch(request)`. Genuinely phase-level (the
  dispatcher PORT lives here). STAYS in `implementation.py` per the
  prompt hard rule.
- `_ImplementationSandboxPort` (`:7679`),
  `_ImplementationRuntimeClient` (`:7604`),
  `_RuntimeStartedRunnerProxy` (`:7652`),
  `_ImplementationPromptBuilder` (`:7519`),
  `_ImplementationOutputNormalizer` (`:7794`),
  `_ExecutionControlDispatchJournalPort` (`:7855`),
  `_ArtifactDispatchJournalPort` (`:8216`) — six adapter classes /
  ports that hold `runner` + `feature` + `task_contract` + `task`
  state as instance attributes and adapt phase-level state to the
  Slice-05 `RuntimeDispatcher` primitive. They ARE the dispatcher
  PORT; the Slice-05 `RuntimeDispatcher` is the dispatcher
  PRIMITIVE. The PORT/PRIMITIVE split is the structurally-correct
  boundary per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" — the named
  API for `execution/dispatcher.py` is `ExecutionDispatcher.dispatch
  _task`/`resume_task`/`record_runtime_failure` (the PRIMITIVE); the
  adapter classes that satisfy these ports from `implementation.py`'s
  runner+feature state are by definition phase-level. STAYS.

Per the prompt edge-case note, the dispatcher PORT (the six adapter
classes + the `_dispatch_task_attempt_via_runtime_dispatcher`
orchestrator + the builders that close over runner+feature) IS phase-
level because it adapts phase-level state to the pure
`RuntimeDispatcher` primitive (the Slice-05 surface). This is the
structurally-correct boundary: the pure runtime-selection / parallel-
actor primitives move; the adapter / port / orchestrator classes
stay. The narrow 11e is therefore a defensible, surgical extraction
that locks 6 pure primitives via the now-standard shim + targeted-
test pattern while preserving the prompt hard rule.

**DOC-11 FOUR-QUESTION CONTRACT (preview).**

- **Q1 What behavior moved?** NONE — 6 pure primitives
  (`DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES`, `_dag_repair_runtime_for`,
  `_dag_group_runtime_pair`, `_post_dag_runtime_pair`,
  `_diagnostic_runtime_for_policy`, `_make_parallel_actor`) moved
  byte-for-byte; no signature, no body, no string literal, no
  return-value change.
- **Q2 What shims still work?** All 6 names re-exported at
  `implementation.py:{428-end-of-block}` (the new Slice-11e block,
  parallel to the Slice-11a / 11b / 11c / 11d blocks at `:321-427`)
  resolve to the SAME object as the canonical definition in
  `execution/dispatcher.py`. The pre-existing external importers
  (`tests/workflows/test_runtime_policy.py:7-11` for

  access; `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py:15913` for
  `_make_parallel_actor` attribute access) continue resolving via
  the shim.

  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_dispatcher_extraction.py`
  — 6 parametrized identity + 5 parametrized `__module__` rebinding
  + 8 behavioral smoke tests (19 total).
- **Q4 Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only pure dispatcher-domain
  primitives moved; bodies preserved byte-for-byte. The much larger
  dispatcher-PORT surface (the 6 adapter classes + the orchestrator
  + the builders that close over runner+feature+task_contract +
  `_dispatch_journal_port_for_runner` + `_runner_runtime_policy`)
  is genuinely PHASE-LEVEL (depends on `WorkflowRunner` + `Feature`
  + the typed store + the impl.py-namespaced logger + the impl.py-
  local `_model_json_dict` / `_task_contract_id` /
  `_snapshot_id_for_repo` / `_git_text` / `_sha256_text` /
  `_contract_repo_id` / `_sandbox_blocker` helpers) and CORRECTLY
  stays in `implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule against
  splitting non-pure helpers. `_runner_runtime_policy` is deferred
  because it reads `runner.services` AND logs through the
  implementation.py-namespaced module logger; the
  `_dispatch_attempt_duplicate_replay_recovery_evidence` helper +
  the `DISPATCH_ATTEMPT_RECOVERY_EVIDENCE_FIELDS` constant are
  deferred because the helper depends on the impl.py-local
  `_model_json_dict` utility (same criterion that kept the
  `_model_json_dict`-coupled task-contract cluster in Slice 11c).



## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11e COMPLETE

Slice 11e (extend `execution/dispatcher.py` with the 6 pure
dispatcher-domain primitives from `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py`) is **COMPLETE** and reviewed CLEAN on the first
review pass — **0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3**. The chunk delivers the fifth
extraction surface in Slice 11; complements (does NOT duplicate) the
Slice-05 `RuntimeDispatcher` + 13 Pydantic models + 8 Protocol ports
+ state-machine validator + digest helpers + default port
implementations + response classification helpers already in
`execution/dispatcher.py`; preserves the Slice-05 surface byte-for-
byte; extends the Slice-11a + Slice-11b + Slice-11c + Slice-11d shim
pattern with a parallel Slice-11e re-export block; locks the new
facade with 19 targeted tests.

**WHAT LANDED — source.** Two modified modules; both edits are
mechanical extraction + re-export (no behavior change). The
implementer correctly chose to EXTEND the pre-existing
`execution/dispatcher.py` (1941 lines pre-11e from Slice 05 — the
canonical home for the Slice-05 `RuntimeDispatcher` + 13 Pydantic
dispatch models + 8 Protocol ports + the state-machine validator +
the digest helpers + the default port implementations + the response
classification helpers) rather than create a new module; the doc-11
row for `execution/dispatcher.py` ("Provider/runtime invocation
boundary, retry idempotency, runtime error normalization") names a
single module that owns this domain, so an additive extension is the
structurally-correct choice.

- **MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/
  dispatcher.py` (was 1941 lines pre-11e from Slice 05; now 2093
  lines; net delta +152). The 6 primitives are appended at
  `:1940-2045` (the Slice-11e block; placed after the existing
  Slice-05 surface ending with `_to_jsonable` at `:1899`). The
  pre-11e Slice-05 surface is preserved byte-for-byte: the
  `RuntimeDispatcher` orchestrator at `:768`, the 13 Pydantic
  dispatch models at `:107-394` (`DispatchRequest`,
  `DispatchOutcome`, `DispatchRetryIdentity`, `ActorMetadata`,
  `PromptContextBundle`, `RuntimeWorkspaceBinding`,
  `RuntimeInvocationRequest`, `RuntimeInvocationResponse`,
  `StructuredOutputRecord`, `RuntimeFailureRecord`,
  `PromptBuildResult`, `PatchCaptureRecord`,
  `DispatchAttemptRecord`), the 8 Protocol ports at `:404-524`
  (`DispatchJournalPort`, `SandboxRunnerPort`, `RuntimeClientPort`,
  `ContractPromptBuilderPort`, `StructuredOutputNormalizerPort`,
  `ClockPort`, `IdempotencyKeyFactoryPort`,
  `CancellationRegistryPort`), the state-machine validator, the
  digest helpers (`stable_json`, `stable_digest`,
  `actor_metadata_digest`, `dispatch_request_digest`,
  `dispatch_idempotency_key`), the default port implementations
  (`DefaultClock`, `DefaultIdempotencyKeyFactory`,
  `NullCancellationRegistry`, `DefaultStructuredOutputNormalizer`),
  and the response classification helpers
  (`_classify_runtime_response`,
  `_canonical_provider_failure_type`, etc.). Imports extended for
  the 6 new primitives: `AgentActor` from `iriai_compose` +
  `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW_POLICY` + `RuntimePolicy` from
  `....runtime_policy`. `__all__` extended with the 6 new names.
  The 6 moved primitives are:
  - **`DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES: dict[str, str]`** — the static
    13-entry role→runtime map used by `_dag_repair_runtime_for` to
    select the runtime for each DAG repair role / lens. Under the
    bridge-claude-pool-codex-review configuration, primary=Claude
    pool and secondary=Codex; the map pins
    `dag-normal-verify`/`dag-final-verify`/`dag-contradiction-
    resolve` + the acceptance-coverage/contract-protocol lenses to
    `secondary` and the `dag-triage`/`dag-rca`/`dag-fix`/`dag-
    focused-reverify` family + the build-dependency/runtime-
    composition/security-boundary/regression-downstream lenses to
    `primary`. Pure data; no dependency beyond stdlib.
  - **`_dag_repair_runtime_for(role_or_lens, fallback=None) -> str
    | None`** — pure 4-liner returning
    `DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES.get(role_or_lens, fallback)`. Only
    depends on the cluster-mate `DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES`.
  - **`_dag_group_runtime_pair(group_idx, runtime_policy) ->
    tuple[str, str]`** — pure 12-liner returning
    `(implementation_runtime, review_runtime)` for a DAG group.
    Under `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW`: always
    `("primary", "secondary")`. Under `DEFAULT`: alternates
    `("primary", "secondary")` on even groups,
    `("secondary", "primary")` on odd. Only depends on
    `RuntimePolicy` + `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW_POLICY`.
  - **`_post_dag_runtime_pair(last_group_idx, runtime_policy) ->
    tuple[str, str]`** — pure 12-liner returning
    `(gate_runtime, fix_runtime)` for post-DAG gates. Under
    `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW`: always
    `("secondary", "primary")`. Under `DEFAULT`: INVERTED from
    `_dag_group_runtime_pair` to ensure the post-DAG verifier is a
    different model from the implementer of the last group. Same
    dependency set.
  - **`_diagnostic_runtime_for_policy(runtime_policy) -> str |
    None`** — pure 5-liner returning the RCA/triage/regression
    runtime: `"secondary"` under `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW`,
    `None` under `DEFAULT` (so callers fall back to per-task/per-
    group routing). Same dependency set.
  - **`_make_parallel_actor(base, suffix, *, runtime=None,
    workspace_path=None, runtime_workspace_binding=None,
    sandbox_required=False) -> AgentActor`** — pure 44-liner
    creating a parallel-safe copy of an `AgentActor` with a unique
    name + `model_copy`-rebuilt role whose metadata is extended
    (not mutated) with `runtime` / `workspace_override` /
    `sandbox_required` / `runtime_workspace_binding` /
    `write_producing`. The 7-parameter extended signature was
    already in the working tree pre-11e (the function had been
    extended in the bundle before the move); the 11e move did not
    modify the function body. Only depends on `AgentActor` from
    `iriai_compose`.
- **MODIFIED**
  `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`
  (line count: 33613 → 33565; net delta -48 lines). The 6 primitive
  definitions were removed in place at their pre-11e source
  locations (`DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES` at `:470-487`,
  `_dag_repair_runtime_for` at `:509-513`, `_dag_group_runtime_
  pair`/`_post_dag_runtime_pair`/`_diagnostic_runtime_for_policy`
  at `:1474-1506`, `_make_parallel_actor` at `:4458-4501`); the
  Slice-11a shim block at `:321-354`, the Slice-11b shim block at
  `:368-382`, the Slice-11c shim block at `:383-400`, and the
  Slice-11d shim block at `:401-427` are UNCHANGED; a NEW parallel
  sibling Slice-11e block was inserted at `:428-463`: a header
  banner documenting the extraction + the boundary contract + the
  deferred PORT surface, followed by the re-import
  `from ..execution.dispatcher import (DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES,
  _dag_group_runtime_pair, _dag_repair_runtime_for,
  _diagnostic_runtime_for_policy, _make_parallel_actor,
  _post_dag_runtime_pair)`. Nothing else in `implementation.py` was
  edited — every method body, every routing function, every
  `ImplementationPhase` stage is byte-for-byte preserved; the 7
  dispatcher-PORT adapter classes (`_ImplementationSandboxPort`,
  `_ImplementationRuntimeClient`, `_RuntimeStartedRunnerProxy`,
  `_ImplementationPromptBuilder`, `_ImplementationOutputNormalizer`,
  `_ExecutionControlDispatchJournalPort`,
  `_ArtifactDispatchJournalPort`) + the 5 phase-level helpers
  (`_runner_runtime_policy`, `_runtime_instance_name_for_hint`,
  `_dispatcher_actor_metadata`, `_dispatcher_request_for_task`,
  `_dispatch_attempt_duplicate_replay_recovery_evidence`) + the
  dispatcher orchestrator
  `_dispatch_task_attempt_via_runtime_dispatcher` remain in place
  per the prompt hard rule (deferred per named ownership — see
  "Left behind" list below).

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** One new file, purely additive. No existing
test was edited or removed.

- **NEW** `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_dispatcher_
  extraction.py` (525 lines, **19 tests**). The doc-11 § "How To
  Use This Map" four-question contract is locked in code:
  - **Parametrized shim identity (6 tests).** For every name in
    the moved-names list, asserts `getattr(implementation, X) is
    getattr(dispatcher, X)` — proving the shim re-exports the SAME
    object, not a copy. The `is` identity (rather than equality)
    is the structural property that keeps `monkeypatch.setattr`
    working: a patched attribute on `implementation_module` is the
    SAME binding the canonical module serves.
  - **Parametrized canonical `__module__` rebinding (5 tests).**
    For every moved callable (the 5 functions; the dict constant
    has no `__module__`, which is the expected behavior for a
    `dict` instance — reasonable per V3 review), asserts the
    canonical function object's `__module__` is
    `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.dispatcher` (not
    `…phases.implementation`) — proving the definition genuinely
    moved rather than being re-aliased from the old module.
  - **8 behavioral smoke tests** covering cross-cutting behavioral
    invariants the moved primitives embody:
    1. `DAG_REPAIR_ROLE_RUNTIMES` canonical shape — asserts the
       map is a 13-entry `dict[str, str]` with the canonical
       primary/secondary partition matching the doc-05 routing
       table.
    2. `_dag_repair_runtime_for` table lookup + fallback —
       exercises the positive lookup for every key in the map
       AND the fallback path for an unknown role.
    3. `_dag_group_runtime_pair` per-group pair selector under
       `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW` — always
       `("primary", "secondary")`.
    4. `_dag_group_runtime_pair` per-group pair selector under
       `DEFAULT` — alternates `("primary", "secondary")` on even
       groups, `("secondary", "primary")` on odd.
    5. `_post_dag_runtime_pair` per-stage pair selector under
       `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW` — always
       `("secondary", "primary")`.
    6. `_post_dag_runtime_pair` per-stage pair selector under
       `DEFAULT` — INVERTED from `_dag_group_runtime_pair` (so
       the post-DAG verifier is a different model from the
       implementer of the last group).
    7. `_diagnostic_runtime_for_policy` RCA/triage selector —
       returns `"secondary"` under
       `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW`, `None` under `DEFAULT`.
    8. `_make_parallel_actor` parallel-safe `AgentActor` copy
       factory — exercises every branch: default (no runtime,
       no workspace, no binding); explicit `runtime`; explicit
       `workspace_path`; explicit `sandbox_required=True` only;
       typed `runtime_workspace_binding` with `model_dump`; dict-
       form `runtime_workspace_binding`; empty-cwd fallback
       semantics. Asserts the returned actor's `name` is unique,
       role metadata is extended (not mutated), and the original
       `base` actor is unchanged.
    Plus a cluster-ownership pin asserting the 6 names land in
    the canonical `execution/dispatcher.py` module and not in any
    other `execution/` sibling, AND a shim-block completeness
    probe verifying the Slice-11e re-export at
    `implementation.py:456-463` covers exactly the 6 moved names.

- **Q4 — Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only pure dispatcher-

  PORT surface stays in `implementation.py` per the prompt hard

  `_dispatch_task_attempt_via_runtime_dispatcher` takes
  runner+feature, constructs all 7 adapter classes, and calls
  `dispatcher.dispatch(request)` — genuinely phase-level. The
  7 adapters ARE the dispatcher PORT; the Slice-05
  `RuntimeDispatcher` is the dispatcher PRIMITIVE.

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11e independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3** on the first pass. All six
vectors PASS + all four doc-11 questions are answered
satisfactorily. The reviewer additionally judged the dispatcher
PRIMITIVE/PORT split as **ACCEPTABLE** — mirroring the Slice-11d
sandbox precedent. The pure runtime-selection / parallel-actor
primitives move into `execution/dispatcher.py` alongside the
existing Slice-05 `RuntimeDispatcher` primitive; the phase-level
dispatcher PORT (the 7 adapter classes + the orchestrator + the 5
phase-level builders/helpers) stays in `implementation.py`
because that port exists to adapt phase-level state (`runner` +
`feature` + `store` + the implementation.py-namespaced logger +
the `_model_json_dict`-coupled chain) to the pure
`RuntimeDispatcher` primitive — which is the structurally-correct
boundary per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" (the named
API for `execution/dispatcher.py` is
`ExecutionDispatcher.dispatch_task` / `resume_task` /
`record_runtime_failure` — the PRIMITIVE; the adapters that
satisfy these ports from `implementation.py`'s runner+feature
state are by definition phase-level). The narrow 11e is
therefore a defensible, surgical extraction that locks 6 pure
primitives via the now-standard shim + targeted-test pattern
while preserving the prompt hard rule.

Reviewer specifics:

- **V1 (doc-11 acceptance criteria for an extraction PR).** All
  four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions are answered
  above; the extraction order constraint (types via 11a, then
  git_service via 11b, then task_contracts via 11c, then sandbox
  via 11d, then dispatcher via 11e) is satisfied; the
  compatibility-arrow direction (extracted module never imports
  back into `implementation.py`) is verified by inspection of
  `execution/dispatcher.py`'s import set — only the existing
  Slice-05 imports plus the three new sibling-module imports
  (`AgentActor` from `iriai_compose`,
  `PRIMARY_IMPL_SECONDARY_REVIEW_POLICY` + `RuntimePolicy` from
  `....runtime_policy`). No upward import from
  `execution/dispatcher.py` into `implementation.py` or any
  phase module.
- **V2 (source additivity + no-behavior-change).** Reviewer
  spot-checked the 6 primitive bodies byte-for-byte against
  `git show HEAD:…implementation.py`; every body is byte-for-
  byte equal to its pre-extraction form modulo whitespace/
  indent. The Slice-05 surface in `execution/dispatcher.py`
  (the `RuntimeDispatcher` orchestrator at `:768`, the 13
  Pydantic models at `:107-394`, the 8 Protocol ports at
  `:404-524`) is preserved byte-for-byte; the 6 new primitives
  occupy a fresh block at `:1940-2045` after the existing
  `_to_jsonable` at `:1899`.
- **V3 (test honesty + shim coverage).** All 19 tests are real
  assertions (no `pytest.skip`; no `pass`-stubs); the identity
  assertions genuinely fail if the shim is removed; the 5
  `__module__` assertions genuinely fail if the moved name is
  re-aliased rather than re-imported (the dict constant has no
  `__module__` which is reasonable — `dict` instances don't
  carry a defining module, only the type does); the behavioral
  smoke assertions exercise the canonical-map shape, the role-
  lookup fallback, the per-group/post-DAG pair selectors under
  both policies, the diagnostic-runtime selector, and the 7
  branches of `_make_parallel_actor`. The reviewer verified the
  shim covers all 6 names by reading `implementation.py:456-463`
  and matching against the test file's `MOVED_NAMES`.
- **V4 (fail-closed edges).** Each pure primitive's defensive
  path is structurally exercised: `_dag_repair_runtime_for`
  falls back to the caller-supplied default for an unknown
  role; `_dag_group_runtime_pair` and `_post_dag_runtime_pair`
  inversion under DEFAULT is asserted (so a different model
  always verifies); `_make_parallel_actor`'s empty-cwd fallback
  and the role-metadata-extension-not-mutation invariant are
  pinned. No exception swallowing in any primitive.
- **V5 (cross-component contract).** Only `implementation.py`
  (the 6 primitive removals + the extended shim block) and
  `dispatcher.py` (the 6 primitive additions + the `__all__`
  extension + the 3 new sibling imports) were modified for
  source. Imports from `dispatcher.py` are confined to (a) the
  shim at `implementation.py:456-463` and (b) the new test
  file's identity assertions; no other consumer imports the
  moved names directly from the new module. The external
  importers (`post_test_observation.py`, `bugfix_v2/queue.py`,
  `test_runtime_policy.py`, `test_dag_expanded_verify.py`)
  continue resolving via the `implementation.py` shim
  identity-equivalent.
- **V6 (docs / ledger).** This journal entry + the JSONL
  acceptance line + the STATUS.md pointer advance constitute
  the V6 surface for 11e; the reviewer verified the journal
  entry's claims match the on-disk state.

The reviewer also explicitly confirmed the dispatcher
PRIMITIVE/PORT split is defensible — the 7 adapter classes + the
orchestrator + the 5 phase-level builders/helpers each have a
named non-pure dependency (`runner` + `feature` instance state, or
the impl.py-namespaced module logger, or the impl.py-local
`_model_json_dict` utility chain, or a runner-instance attribute
reader) that justifies keeping them in `implementation.py` until
the owning sub-slice or utility-cluster sub-slice lands. This
mirrors the Slice-11d sandbox PORT precedent exactly.

sandbox (DONE) → 11e dispatcher (DONE) → 11f gates → 11g

four doc-11 questions in the AFTER chunk; complement (not
duplicate) any gate surface already in `execution/`.

Slice 11e is closed. Slice 11f (extract `execution/gates.py`) is
next.






## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11f STARTING

The sixth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the deterministic gate-routing primitive
surface — is beginning. Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts"
row 9 for `execution/gates.py`: "`GateRunner.run_preflight`,
`run_raw_gate`, `run_checkpoint_gate`. Deterministic preflight
gates, raw-gate ordering, stale-context blockers, gate evidence
shape." The Slice-06 `GateRunner` + `GateRequest` + the 22 typed
gate models + `ContextPackageBuilder` + `InMemoryEvidenceRecorder`
+ the gate validators + the bounded-context reader are already in
`execution/gates.py` (1354 lines pre-11f from Slice 06) — that is
the canonical home for the Slice-06 deterministic gate primitive
surface. Slice 11f EXTENDS that module with the pure DAG-authority
gate-routing primitives + 2 small pure gate-key/proof helpers
currently in `implementation.py` that depend only on stdlib +
`models.outputs` (`Verdict`, `Issue`, `ImplementationResult`)
without dragging in any
`WorkflowRunner`/`Feature`/`logger`/`_model_json_dict`-coupled
adapters or `_is_dag_task_artifact_key`/`_safe_dag_task_artifact_refs`
(impl.py-local helpers).

**SCOPE TO MOVE (9 pure primitives).** Each one is byte-for-byte
self-contained; only stdlib + `models.outputs` is needed; no
runner/feature/store/logger/`_model_json_dict` dependencies.

1. `_DAG_AUTHORITY_SEMANTIC_ROUTE = "semantic_verify_needed"` —
   `implementation.py:548`. Routing constant used by
   `_dag_authority_path_problem_route` to select the semantic-
   verify-needed route.
2. `_DAG_AUTHORITY_DB_TASK_RESULT_ROUTE = "db_task_result_drift"` —
   `implementation.py:549`. Routing constant for db_task_result
   drift.
3. `_DAG_AUTHORITY_TASK_SPEC_PROJECTION_ROUTE =
   "task_spec_projection_drift"` — `implementation.py:550`.
   Routing constant for task_spec projection drift.
4. `_DAG_AUTHORITY_SOURCE_ARTIFACT_ROUTE =
   "source_dag_artifact_drift"` — `implementation.py:551`.
   Routing constant for source artifact drift.
5. `_DAG_AUTHORITY_PRODUCT_WORKSPACE_ROUTE = "product_workspace_drift"`
   — `implementation.py:552`. Routing constant for product
   workspace drift.
6. `_DAG_AUTHORITY_REPO_BLOCKER_ROUTE = "repo_or_permission_blocker"`
   — `implementation.py:553`. Routing constant for the repo /
   permission blocker route.
7. `_dag_authority_preflight_key(group_idx: int, retry_label: str)
   -> str` — `implementation.py:29385-29389`. Pure 5-liner
   returning `f"dag-repair-preflight:g{group_idx}:retry-{retry_label}"`.
   No dependencies; pure f-string formatter.
8. `_dag_authority_path_problem_route(problems: list[dict[str, Any]],
   artifact_only: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> tuple[str, str]` —
   `implementation.py:29414-29456`. Pure routing classifier
   returning `(route, reason)` based on dict shape inspection
   only. Depends solely on the 6 route constants above + stdlib
   `str()`.
9. `_dag_authority_blocked_verdict(group_idx, retry, *, route,
   reason, target_refs, detail) -> Verdict` —
   `implementation.py:29486-29515`. Pure factory constructing a
   blocking `Verdict` with a single `Issue` concern. Depends only
   on `Verdict` + `Issue` from `models.outputs`.
10. `_dag_authority_synthetic_result(group_idx, retry, report)
    -> ImplementationResult` — `implementation.py:29532-29547`.
    Pure factory constructing a synthetic `ImplementationResult`
    for the authority-gate repair record. Depends only on
    `ImplementationResult` from `models.outputs` + stdlib `json`.
11. `_dag_authority_reconcile_target_coverage(reconcile_report:
    dict[str, Any], target_refs: list[str]) -> dict[str, Any]` —
    `implementation.py:29571-29599`. Pure dict-shape transform
    computing target/covered/missing refs + skipped + blockers
    + complete bit. No dependencies beyond stdlib.
12. `_post_dag_gate_proof_key(gate_name: str) -> str` —
    `implementation.py:8537-8538`. Pure 1-liner returning
    `f"dag-gate-proof:{gate_name}"`. No dependencies.
13. `_notify_gate_proof_extra_from_delivery(delivery: dict[str, Any])
    -> dict[str, Any]` — `implementation.py:9206-9210`. Pure
    dict transform extracting `delivery_id` + `notification_sha256`
    from a delivery record. No dependencies.

That counts as 6 route constants + 7 pure functions = **13 names
total**.

**SCOPE LEFT BEHIND (deferred — each with its named non-pure

Per the prompt edge-case note, the inventory found 13 pure
primitives (6 routing constants + 7 pure functions) that can
move byte-for-byte. The much larger gate-domain PORT surface (the
async runner+feature-coupled orchestrators and the
`_execution_control_store_for_runner`-coupled `_record_*` /
`_*_is_fresh` helpers) is genuinely PHASE-LEVEL and stays in
`implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule against splitting
non-pure helpers. The narrow 11f is therefore a defensible,
surgical extraction that locks 13 pure primitives via the
now-standard shim + targeted-test pattern while preserving the
prompt hard rule. This mirrors the Slice-11d sandbox precedent
and the Slice-11e dispatcher precedent — the pure routing /
factory / projection primitives move into `execution/gates.py`
alongside the existing Slice-06 `GateRunner` primitive; the
phase-level gate PORT (the async runner-coupled orchestrators
+ the `_execution_control_store_for_runner`-coupled `_record_*`
/ `_*_is_fresh` helpers) stays in `implementation.py` because
it adapts phase-level state to the pure gate primitives.

**DOC-11 FOUR-QUESTION CONTRACT (preview).**

- **Q1 What behavior moved?** NONE — 13 pure primitives (6
  routing constants + 7 pure functions) moved byte-for-byte;
  no signature, no body, no string literal, no return-value
  change.
- **Q2 What shims still work?** All 13 names re-exported at
  the new Slice-11f block (parallel to the Slice-11a / 11b /
  11c / 11d / 11e blocks at `:321-463`) resolve to the SAME
  object as the canonical definition in `execution/gates.py`.
- **Q3 What targeted tests prove the new facade + shim?**
  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_gates_extraction.py`
  — 13 parametrized identity + 7 parametrized `__module__`
  rebinding + behavioral smoke tests.
- **Q4 Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only pure gate-domain
  routing primitives + 2 small pure gate-key/proof helpers
  moved; bodies preserved byte-for-byte. The larger gate-PORT
  surface (the async runner+feature-coupled orchestrators,
  the `_execution_control_store_for_runner`-coupled
  `_record_*` / `_*_is_fresh` helpers, the
  `_is_dag_task_artifact_key`-dependent
  `_dag_authority_applied_dag_task_updates` /
  `_dag_authority_task_refs_from_path_problems`) is
  genuinely PHASE-LEVEL and CORRECTLY stays in
  `implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11f COMPLETE

Slice 11f (extend `execution/gates.py` with 6 pure DAG-authority
routing constants + 7 pure gate-routing/key/proof helpers from
`workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`) is **COMPLETE** and
reviewed CLEAN — **0 P1, 0 P2, 1 non-blocking P3 (P3-11f-1,
RESOLVED in this finalizer pass)**. The chunk delivers the sixth
extraction surface in Slice 11; complements (does NOT duplicate)
the Slice-06 `GateRunner` + `GateRequest` + 22 typed gate models +
`InMemoryEvidenceRecorder` + `ContextPackageBuilder` already in
`execution/gates.py`; preserves the Slice-06 surface byte-for-byte;
extends the Slice-11a + Slice-11b + Slice-11c + Slice-11d +
Slice-11e shim pattern with a parallel Slice-11f re-export block;
locks the new facade with 34 targeted tests.

**WHAT LANDED — source.** Two modified modules; both edits are
mechanical extraction + re-export (no behavior change). The
implementer correctly chose to EXTEND the pre-existing
`execution/gates.py` (1354 lines pre-11f from Slice 06 — the
canonical home for the Slice-06 `GateRunner` at `:756` +
`GateRequest` at `:82` + the 22 typed gate models +
`InMemoryEvidenceRecorder` at `:468` + `ContextPackageBuilder` at
`:521`) rather than create a new module; the doc-11 row for
`execution/gates.py` ("Deterministic preflight gates, raw-gate
ordering, stale-context blockers, gate evidence shape") names a
single module that owns this domain, so an additive extension is
the structurally-correct choice.

- **MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/
  gates.py` (was 1354 lines pre-11f from Slice 06; now 1561
  lines; net delta +207). The 6 routing constants land at
  `:1373-1378`; the 7 pure helpers land at `:1381-1522`; the
  Slice-06 surface (`GateRunner` at `:756`, `GateRequest` at
  `:82`, the 22 typed gate models, `InMemoryEvidenceRecorder` at
  `:468`, `ContextPackageBuilder` at `:521`) is preserved byte-
  for-byte. New imports: stdlib `json` + `ImplementationResult` /
  `Issue` / `Verdict` from `models.outputs`. NO import from
  `implementation.py` or any phase module (compatibility arrow
  points IN, never OUT). `__all__` extended with the 13 new
  names. The 13 moved primitives:
  - **6 routing constants** (`gates.py:1373-1378`):
    `_DAG_AUTHORITY_SEMANTIC_ROUTE = "semantic_verify_needed"`,
    `_DAG_AUTHORITY_DB_TASK_RESULT_ROUTE =
    "db_task_result_drift"`,
    `_DAG_AUTHORITY_TASK_SPEC_PROJECTION_ROUTE =
    "task_spec_projection_drift"`,
    `_DAG_AUTHORITY_SOURCE_ARTIFACT_ROUTE =
    "source_dag_artifact_drift"`,
    `_DAG_AUTHORITY_PRODUCT_WORKSPACE_ROUTE =
    "product_workspace_drift"`,
    `_DAG_AUTHORITY_REPO_BLOCKER_ROUTE =
    "repo_or_permission_blocker"`. Pure string constants; no
    dependencies.
  - **`_dag_authority_preflight_key(group_idx, retry_label)
    -> str`** (`gates.py:1381`) — pure f-string formatter
    returning `"dag-repair-preflight:g{group_idx}:retry-
    {retry_label}"`. No dependencies.
  - **`_dag_authority_path_problem_route(problems, artifact_
    only) -> tuple[str, str]`** (`gates.py:1388`) — pure routing
    classifier returning `(route, reason)` based on dict shape
    inspection only across all 6 routes + the "no path
    problems" / "no deterministic artifact-only problem" edge
    cases. Depends solely on the 6 cluster-mate route constants
    + stdlib `str()`.
  - **`_dag_authority_blocked_verdict(group_idx, retry, *,
    route, reason, target_refs, detail) -> Verdict`**
    (`gates.py:1433`) — pure factory constructing a blocking
    `Verdict` with a single `Issue` concern; emits the
    canonical `"(none)"` placeholder when `target_refs` is
    empty. Depends only on `Verdict` + `Issue` from
    `models.outputs`.
  - **`_dag_authority_synthetic_result(group_idx, retry, report)
    -> ImplementationResult`** (`gates.py:1465`) — pure factory
    constructing a synthetic `ImplementationResult` for the
    authority-gate repair record; handles unknown-status
    fallback by clamping non-blocked statuses to the report
    text without altering the structured outcome. Depends only
    on `ImplementationResult` from `models.outputs` + stdlib
    `json`.
  - **`_dag_authority_reconcile_target_coverage(reconcile_
    report, target_refs) -> dict[str, Any]`**
    (`gates.py:1483`) — pure dict-shape projection computing
    target/covered/missing refs + skipped + blockers + the
    `complete` bit. No dependencies beyond stdlib.
  - **`_post_dag_gate_proof_key(gate_name) -> str`**
    (`gates.py:1514`) — pure 1-liner returning
    `f"dag-gate-proof:{gate_name}"`. No dependencies.
  - **`_notify_gate_proof_extra_from_delivery(delivery)
    -> dict[str, Any]`** (`gates.py:1518`) — pure dict
    transform extracting `delivery_id` + `notification_sha256`
    from a delivery record. No dependencies.
- **MODIFIED**
  `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`
  (line count: 33565 → 33503; net delta -62 lines). The 13
  primitive definitions were removed in place at their pre-11f

  import `from ..execution.gates import
  (_DAG_AUTHORITY_DB_TASK_RESULT_ROUTE,

  _DAG_AUTHORITY_REPO_BLOCKER_ROUTE,
  _DAG_AUTHORITY_SEMANTIC_ROUTE,
  _DAG_AUTHORITY_SOURCE_ARTIFACT_ROUTE,
  _DAG_AUTHORITY_TASK_SPEC_PROJECTION_ROUTE,
  _dag_authority_blocked_verdict,
  _dag_authority_path_problem_route,
  _dag_authority_preflight_key,
  _dag_authority_reconcile_target_coverage,
  _dag_authority_synthetic_result,
  _notify_gate_proof_extra_from_delivery,
  _post_dag_gate_proof_key)`. Nothing else in `implementation.py`
  was edited — every method body, every routing function, every
  `ImplementationPhase` stage is byte-for-byte preserved; the
  phase-level gate PORT surface (the 4 async runner+feature-
  coupled orchestrators `_attempt_dag_authority_gate_repair` /

  `_record_dag_authority_gate` + the 2 `_is_dag_task_artifact_

  / `_dag_authority_task_refs_from_path_problems` + the

  `_typed_verification_gate_node_is_fresh` family + the

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** One new file, purely additive. No
existing test was edited or removed.

- **NEW** `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_gates_
  extraction.py` (635 lines, **34 tests**). The doc-11 § "How
  To Use This Map" four-question contract is locked in code:
  - **Parametrized shim identity (13 tests).** For every name in
    the moved-names list (the 6 routing constants + 7 helpers),
    asserts `getattr(implementation, X) is getattr(gates, X)` —
    proving the shim re-exports the SAME object, not a copy.
    The `is` identity (rather than equality) is the structural
    property that keeps `monkeypatch.setattr` working: a patched
    attribute on `implementation_module` is the SAME binding the
    canonical module serves.
  - **Parametrized canonical `__module__` rebinding (7 tests).**
    For every moved callable (the 7 functions; the 6 string
    constants have no `__module__`, which is the expected
    behavior for `str` instances — reasonable per V3 review),
    asserts the canonical function object's `__module__` is
    `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.gates` (not
    `…phases.implementation`) — proving the definition genuinely
    moved rather than being re-aliased from the old module.
  - **14 behavioral smoke tests** covering cross-cutting
    behavioral invariants the moved primitives embody: the 6
    routing-constant values (each canonical string verified
    against doc-05); the `_dag_authority_preflight_key`
    formatter; the `_dag_authority_path_problem_route` classifier
    across all 6 routes + the no-path-problems / no-
    deterministic-artifact-only-problem edge cases; the
    `_dag_authority_blocked_verdict` factory shape including the
    empty `target_refs` "(none)" placeholder; the
    `_dag_authority_synthetic_result` shape + the unknown-status
    fallback path; the `_dag_authority_reconcile_target_
    coverage` dict-shape projection; the `_post_dag_gate_proof_
    key` formatter; the `_notify_gate_proof_extra_from_
    delivery` transform; a cluster-ownership pin asserting the
    13 names land in the canonical `execution/gates.py` module
    and not in any other `execution/` sibling; a shim-block
    13-name completeness probe verifying the Slice-11f re-export
    at `implementation.py:464-511` covers exactly the 13 moved
    names; AND a back-import guard asserting `execution/gates.
    py` never imports from `implementation.py` (the
    compatibility arrow points IN, never OUT).

**DOC-11 FOUR-QUESTION CONTRACT (per reviewer).**

- **Q1 — What behavior moved?** **NONE.** 13 pure primitives
  (6 routing constants + 7 helpers — `_dag_authority_preflight_
  key`, `_dag_authority_path_problem_route`, `_dag_authority_
  blocked_verdict`, `_dag_authority_synthetic_result`,
  `_dag_authority_reconcile_target_coverage`, `_post_dag_gate_
  proof_key`, `_notify_gate_proof_extra_from_delivery`) moved
  byte-for-byte from `implementation.py` to
  `execution/gates.py:1373-1378` (constants) +
  `:1381-1522` (helpers). No logic change; no signature change;
  no validation added or removed; no routing string-literal
  change; the artifact key strings are byte-identical (`"dag-
  repair-preflight:g{n}:retry-{label}"`, `"dag-gate-proof:
  {gate_name}"`).
- **Q2 — What shims still work?** **All 13 names.** Re-exported
  at `implementation.py:464-511` (a parallel sibling block of
  the Slice-11a shim at `:321-354`, the Slice-11b shim at
  `:368-382`, the Slice-11c shim at `:383-400`, the Slice-11d
  shim at `:401-427`, and the Slice-11e shim at `:428-463`).
  Every existing `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.
  implementation import X` for any of the 13 moved names
  resolves to the SAME object as `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.
  develop.execution.gates import X` — proven by the 13
  parametrized identity assertions. The pre-existing external
  readers continue resolving via the shim:
  - `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/post_test_
    observation.py:52` (production importer of one or more of

  - `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_workflow_quiesce.py`
    (references DAG-authority routing constants via

  - `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_dag_expanded_

- **Q3 — What targeted tests prove the new facade + the shim?**
  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_gates_extraction.py`
  — **34 tests** (13 parametrized identity + 7 parametrized
  `__module__` rebinding + 14 behavioral smoke tests covering
  the 6 routing-constant canonical values; the preflight-key
  formatter; the path-problem-route classifier across all 6

- **Q4 — Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only pure gate-domain
  routing primitives + 2 small pure gate-key/proof helpers
  moved; bodies preserved byte-for-byte. The phase-level gate
  PORT surface stays in `implementation.py` per the prompt
  hard rule: (a) the 4 async runner+feature-coupled

  `_dag_authority_load_preflight_report`,
  `_record_dag_authority_gate`, and the typed-verification-

  `_is_dag_task_artifact_key`-coupled helpers
  (`_dag_authority_applied_dag_task_updates`,
  `_dag_authority_task_refs_from_path_problems`) depend on the
  impl.py-local `_is_dag_task_artifact_key` + `_safe_dag_task_
  artifact_refs` utilities (these are themselves pure but
  cannot be moved without broadening the surface beyond the
  prompt hard rule "modules MUST NOT import from

  gate_node` / `_typed_verification_gate_node_is_fresh` family +

  `_post_dag_gate_tree_digest` family + the

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11f independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 1 non-blocking P3 (P3-11f-1)** on
the first pass. All six vectors PASS + all four doc-11 questions
are answered satisfactorily. The reviewer additionally judged
the gate PRIMITIVE/PORT split as **ACCEPTABLE** — mirroring the
Slice-11d sandbox precedent + the Slice-11e dispatcher precedent
exactly. The pure gate-routing primitives move into
`execution/gates.py` alongside the existing Slice-06 `GateRunner`
primitive; the phase-level gate PORT (the 4 async runner+feature-
coupled orchestrators + the 2 `_is_dag_task_artifact_key`-
coupled helpers + the recorder-coupled / `_get_feature_root`-
coupled / merge-queue-PORT-coupled families) stays in
`implementation.py` because that port exists to adapt phase-level
state (`runner` + `feature` + `store` + the impl.py-local
`_is_dag_task_artifact_key` utility + the async-subprocess shell-
out chain) to the pure `GateRunner` primitive — which is the
structurally-correct boundary per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API
contracts" (the named API for `execution/gates.py` is
`GateRunner.run_preflight` / `run_raw_gate` / `run_checkpoint_
gate` — the PRIMITIVE; the adapters that satisfy these ports
from `implementation.py`'s runner+feature+store state are by
definition phase-level). The narrow 11f is therefore a
defensible, surgical extraction that locks 13 pure primitives
via the now-standard shim + targeted-test pattern while
preserving the prompt hard rule.

**P3-11f-1 (RESOLVED in this finalizer pass).** The reviewer
flagged a stale comment in `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/
execution/types.py:226-232` near the `DagAuthorityGateOutcome.
route` field default. The comment described the per-type
default as "MUST equal `implementation.py`'s
`_DAG_AUTHORITY_SEMANTIC_ROUTE` constant" — but the constant now
lives in `execution/gates.py` (moved here by Slice 11f). The
inlined literal value `"semantic_verify_needed"` is byte-
identical to the constant in its new canonical home, so no
behavior changes; only the comment was stale. The finalizer
pass updated the comment to point to `execution/gates.py`
(`types.py:226-233`) — purely a comment-only edit; the field
default literal value is byte-for-byte unchanged. Verified by
re-running `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_types_
extraction.py + tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_gates_
extraction.py -q -p no:randomly --tb=no` → **74 passed** post-
edit (40 types + 34 gates).

Reviewer specifics:

- **V1 (doc-11 acceptance criteria for an extraction PR).** All
  four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions are answered
  above; the extraction order constraint (types via 11a, then
  git_service via 11b, then task_contracts via 11c, then
  sandbox via 11d, then dispatcher via 11e, then gates via
  11f) is satisfied; the compatibility-arrow direction
  (extracted module never imports back into `implementation.
  py`) is verified by the back-import guard test in
  `test_gates_extraction.py`. `execution/gates.py`'s import
  set is the existing Slice-06 set plus the 4 new sibling-
  module imports (stdlib `json` + `ImplementationResult` /
  `Issue` / `Verdict` from `models.outputs`); no `from
  ..phases.implementation` import.
- **V2 (source additivity + no-behavior-change).** Reviewer
  spot-checked the 13 primitive bodies byte-for-byte against
  `git show HEAD:…implementation.py`; every body is byte-for-
  byte equal to its pre-extraction form modulo whitespace/
  indent. The Slice-06 surface in `execution/gates.py` (the
  `GateRunner` at `:756`, `GateRequest` at `:82`, the 22 typed
  gate models, `InMemoryEvidenceRecorder` at `:468`,
  `ContextPackageBuilder` at `:521`) is preserved byte-for-
  byte; the 6 routing constants land at `:1373-1378` and the 7
  helpers at `:1381-1522`.
- **V3 (test honesty + shim coverage).** All 34 tests are real
  assertions (no `pytest.skip`; no `pass`-stubs); the 13
  identity assertions genuinely fail if the shim is removed;
  the 7 `__module__` assertions genuinely fail if the moved
  name is re-aliased rather than re-imported (the 6 string
  constants have no `__module__` because `str` instances don't
  carry a defining module — only the type does, which is
  reasonable per V3 review); the 14 behavioral smoke
  assertions exercise the routing constant values, the path-
  problem-route classifier across every route + edge case,
  the blocked-verdict shape including the empty-target_refs
  "(none)" placeholder, the synthetic-result fallback, the
  reconcile-target-coverage projection, the proof-key
  formatters, the delivery-record transform, the cluster
  ownership pin, the 13-name shim-block completeness probe,
  and the back-import guard. The reviewer verified the shim
  covers all 13 names by reading `implementation.py:464-511`
  and matching against the test file's `MOVED_NAMES`.
- **V4 (fail-closed edges).** Each pure primitive's defensive
  path is structurally exercised: the path-problem-route
  classifier returns `(_DAG_AUTHORITY_SEMANTIC_ROUTE, "no_
  path_problems")` on the empty-problems edge case;
  `_dag_authority_blocked_verdict` emits the canonical
  "(none)" placeholder when `target_refs` is empty (so the
  blocking `Verdict` is never silently lossy);
  `_dag_authority_synthetic_result`'s unknown-status fallback
  clamps non-blocked statuses to the report text without
  altering the structured outcome. No exception swallowing in
  any primitive.
- **V5 (cross-component contract).** Only `implementation.py`
  (the 13 primitive removals + the extended shim block) and
  `gates.py` (the 13 primitive additions + the `__all__`
  extension + the 4 new imports) were modified for source.
  Imports from `gates.py` are confined to (a) the shim at
  `implementation.py:497-511` and (b) the new test file's
  identity assertions; no other consumer imports the moved
  names directly from the new module. The external readers
  (`post_test_observation.py:52`, `test_workflow_quiesce.py`,
  `test_dag_expanded_verify.py`,
  `test_types_extraction.py:222`) continue resolving via the
  `implementation.py` shim identity-equivalent.
- **V6 (docs / ledger).** This journal entry + the JSONL
  acceptance line + the STATUS.md pointer advance + the
  P3-11f-1 in-finalizer resolution constitute the V6 surface
  for 11f; the reviewer verified the journal entry's claims
  match the on-disk state.

The reviewer also explicitly confirmed the gate PRIMITIVE/PORT
split is defensible — the 4 async runner+feature-coupled
orchestrators + the 2 `_is_dag_task_artifact_key`-coupled
helpers + the recorder-coupled / `_get_feature_root`-coupled /
merge-queue-PORT-coupled families each have a named non-pure

refs` utilities, or the `InMemoryEvidenceRecorder` instance, or

them in `implementation.py` until the owning sub-slice or

sandbox PORT + the Slice-11e dispatcher PORT precedents exactly.

  the `_record_dag_authority_gate` writer + the

  phase-level (runner + feature + store). STAYS.
- **`_dag_authority_load_preflight_report(runner, feature,
  group_idx, retry_label)`** — reads a preflight report from

  recorder-coupled. STAYS.

  recorder writer that persists DAG-authority gate outcomes;
  runner + recorder-coupled. STAYS.
- **`_dag_authority_applied_dag_task_updates(...)`** — depends
  on `_is_dag_task_artifact_key` (impl.py-local pure utility
  that is the same 50+-caller utility that kept several other

  sub-slice lands.
- **`_dag_authority_task_refs_from_path_problems(problems)`** —

  Deferred.

  coupled writer + freshness check. STAYS.
- **`_merge_queue_post_apply_gate_decision(...)`** — depends on

- **`_post_dag_gate_tree_digest(feature, ...)`** — depends on
  `_get_feature_root` + an async-subprocess `git rev-parse

- **`_record_post_dag_gate_proof(...)` /
  `_record_dag_checkpoint_gate_proof(...)` /
  `_checkpoint_gate_graph_projection_is_fresh(...)` /
  `_checkpoint_gate_proof_is_fresh(...)`** — recorder-coupled
  artifact writers + freshness checks. STAYS.

**Slice 11 sub-slice plan (recap).** Per the Slice 11 STARTING
journal entry, the dependency-ordered plan is: 11a types (DONE)
→ 11b git_service (DONE) → 11c task_contracts (DONE) → 11d
sandbox (DONE) → 11e dispatcher (DONE) → 11f gates (DONE) →
11g verification → 11h repair → 11i failure_router → **11j
merge_queue (P3-6 fold-in PRIMARY OWNER)** → 11k regroup_overlay
→ 11l post_dag_gates → 11m post_test_guard → 11n slice-end
SIX-VECTOR review + formal Slice 11 ACCEPTANCE. **Next: 11g
verification** (per `docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-
map.md` § "Proposed Module Boundaries" — `execution/
verification.py` is the next surface after `gates.py`). The 11g
implementer should: inventory any existing `execution/
verification.py` if pre-existing; identify verification helpers
in `implementation.py` (search `_verification_*`, `_verify_*`,
`semantic_verify_*`); apply the same shim + targeted-test
pattern as 11a + 11b + 11c + 11d + 11e + 11f; answer the same
four doc-11 questions in the AFTER chunk; complement (not
duplicate) any verification surface already in `execution/`.

Slice 11f is closed. Slice 11g (extract `execution/
verification.py`) is next.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11g STARTING

The seventh extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the verification-graph primitive surface — is
beginning. Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/verification.py`: "`VerificationService.verify_group`,
`run_lenses`, `load_bounded_context`. Model verifier orchestration,
expanded lenses, bounded verify context, verifier outcome typing."
The pre-11g verification-graph orchestration surface (the
`VerificationGraphAttempt`, `EvidenceNode`, `EvidenceEdge`,
`EvidenceRef`, `BoundedQuery`, `ReadBudgetReport`,
`VerifierNodeResult`, `VerifierCompatibilityLinks`,
`VerificationNodeOutcome`, `MergedConcern`,
`MergedVerificationVerdict`, `AggregateBuildResult`,
`AggregateVerdict`, `GraphApprovalProof`, `VerificationGraph`,
`TypedFailure`, `GraphApprovalError`, `GraphIdempotencyConflict`
types + `normalize_raw_verifier_result`,
`normalize_lens_verifier_result`,
`merge_verdicts_deterministically`, `build_aggregate_verdict`,
`build_graph_approval_proof`, `map_verifier_failure`,
`stable_digest` factories) is already in
`execution/verification.py` (1314 lines pre-11g) — that is the
canonical home for the pure verification-graph primitive surface.
Slice 11g EXTENDS that module with 19 pure verification-domain
helpers currently in `implementation.py` that depend only on stdlib
+ `models.outputs`
(`Verdict`/`Issue`/`Gap`/`ImplementationResult`/`ImplementationTask`)
+ the cluster-mate `stable_digest` (re-exposed under the impl.py-
local rename `verify_graph_stable_digest`) +
`dispatcher_stable_digest` (imported from
`execution/dispatcher.py` with an `ImportError` fallback for
symmetry with the pre-11g impl.py-local try/except) +
`DagVerifyLensSpec` from `.types` + `to_str` from `iriai_compose`
— WITHOUT dragging in any
`WorkflowRunner`/`Feature`/`logger`/`_json_object_from_text`/
`_is_approved`/`_issue_dedupe_key`/`_gap_dedupe_key`-coupled
adapters or the env-coupled `_dag_expanded_verify_enabled` /
`_dag_verify_lens_specs` / `_dag_verify_required_lens_slugs`
helpers.

**SCOPE TO MOVE (19 pure helpers).** Each one is byte-for-byte
self-contained; only stdlib + cluster-mates + the canonical
exports from sibling modules are needed; no runner/feature/store/
logger/io dependencies.

1. `_dag_verify_stage_from_projection_key(projection_key) -> str` —
   `implementation.py:12031`. Pure 3-liner that splits the
   projection key on `:` (maxsplit=2) and returns the stage portion
   iff the prefix is `dag-verify` and arity is 3; otherwise
   `"unknown"`. No dependencies.
2. `_dag_verify_graph_artifact_key(group_idx, stage) -> str` —
   `implementation.py:12036`. Pure 1-liner returning
   `f"dag-verify-graph:g{group_idx}:{stage}"`. No dependencies.
3. `_pydantic_json(value) -> object` — `implementation.py:12040`.
   Pure 4-liner: dispatches to `value.model_dump(mode="json")` if
   callable, else `to_str(value)`. Depends on `to_str` from
   `iriai_compose`.
4. `_synthetic_verification_verdict_id(projection_key, verdict,
   *, suffix="") -> int` — `implementation.py:12047`. Pure factory
   computing a deterministic int from
   `digest_fn({projection_key, suffix, verdict})` where `digest_fn`
   is `verify_graph_stable_digest or dispatcher_stable_digest` with
   sha256 fallback. Depends on cluster-mates
   `verify_graph_stable_digest` + `dispatcher_stable_digest` +
   `_pydantic_json` + stdlib `hashlib` + `to_str`.
5. `_dag_verify_graph_payload_covers_projection(payload_text, key)
   -> bool` — `implementation.py:12152`. Pure predicate over JSON-
   parsed payload + projection_key. Depends on cluster-mate
   `_dag_verify_graph_payload_for_projection`.
6. `_dag_verify_graph_payload_has_durable_projection(payload)
   -> bool` — `implementation.py:12204`. Pure dict-shape
   predicate. No dependencies.
7. `_dag_verify_graph_digest(value) -> str` —
   `implementation.py:12214`. Pure digest with
   `verify_graph_stable_digest or dispatcher_stable_digest`
   fallback chain + sha256 last resort. Same cluster-mate
   dependency set as 4.
8. `_dag_verify_graph_projection_metadata(result) -> dict[str, Any]`
   — `implementation.py:12225`. Pure attribute/dict walker over a
   `result.row` + `result.projection_links` + `result.graph` /
   `result.evidence_graph` instance shape. No dependencies.
9. `_dag_verify_graph_payload_without_durable(payload)
   -> dict[str, Any]` — `implementation.py:12290`. Pure JSON
   round-trip that strips `durable_projection`. Stdlib only.
10. `_dag_verify_graph_store_payload_digest(payload) -> str` —
    `implementation.py:12299`. Pure 1-liner composing
    `_dag_verify_graph_digest(_dag_verify_graph_payload_without_
    durable(payload))`. Cluster-mate dependencies.
11. `_dag_verify_graph_payload_digest_for_proof(payload) -> str` —
    `implementation.py:12303`. Pure variant of 10 that also strips
    `proof.proof_digest` and `proof.graph_payload_digest` before
    digesting. Cluster-mate dependencies.
12. `_dag_verify_graph_int(value) -> int | None` —
    `implementation.py:12312`. Pure try/except int conversion. No
    dependencies.
13. `_dag_verify_graph_edge_ids(value) -> list[str]` —
    `implementation.py:12319`. Pure normalizer composing
    `_dag_verify_graph_int` per element. Cluster-mate dependency.
14. `_dag_verify_graph_durable_metadata_from_reload(result, verified)
    -> dict[str, Any]` — `implementation.py:12329`. Pure walker
    composing `_dag_verify_graph_projection_metadata` + the
    `verified["graph"]` + `verified["required_edges"]` +
    `verified["projection_links"]` dict shapes. Cluster-mate
    dependency.
15. `_dag_verify_graph_payload_for_projection(payload_text, key)
    -> dict[str, Any] | None` — `implementation.py:12858`. Pure
    JSON parser + shape predicate. Stdlib only.
16. `_dag_verify_graph_ref(value) -> dict[str, Any]` —
    `implementation.py:12873`. Pure attribute/payload-dict walker
    extracting a stable set of id/digest fields. No dependencies.
17. `_dag_verify_graph_lineage_payload(*, feature_id,
    projection_key, dag_sha256, group_idx, stage, tasks=None,
    results=None, contracts_by_task_id=None,
    workspace_snapshots=None) -> dict[str, Any]` —
    `implementation.py:12907`. Pure lineage builder composing
    `_dag_verify_graph_ref` + `_dag_verify_graph_digest`. Depends
    on cluster-mates + `ImplementationTask` + `ImplementationResult`
    from `models.outputs`.
18. `_prefix_lens_issue(spec, issue) -> Issue` —
    `implementation.py:29932`. Pure 1-line factory returning
    `issue.model_copy(update={"description": f"[{spec.label} Lens]
    {issue.description}"})`. Depends on `DagVerifyLensSpec` from
    `.types` + `Issue` from `models.outputs`.
19. `_prefix_lens_gap(spec, gap) -> Gap` —
    `implementation.py:29938`. Pure 1-line factory mirroring 18 for
    `Gap`. Depends on `DagVerifyLensSpec` + `Gap`.

**SCOPE LEFT BEHIND (deferred — each with its named non-pure
dependency class).** Every other verification-domain helper in
`implementation.py` has at least one dependency that violates the
move criteria:

- `_dag_verify_required_lens_slugs()` (`:12065`) — depends on
  `_dag_expanded_verify_enabled()` (env-flag scan) +
  `_dag_verify_lens_specs()` (Role-binding-coupled — depends on
  the module-level `verifier`, `security_auditor`,
  `regression_tester` Role bindings). Both are impl.py-local
  utilities. Deferred until a future utility-cluster sub-slice
  moves them.
- `_dag_verify_read_budget_for_projection(...)` (`:12071`) —
  depends on the impl.py-local rename aliases
  `VerifyReadBudgetReport`, `VerifyBoundedQuery`,
  `ImplementationResult` + cluster-mate `_dag_verify_graph_digest`
  + `_dag_verify_graph_ref`. The cluster-mates ARE in the move set;
  the impl.py-local rename aliases would need to be re-exposed in
  `verification.py` to preserve byte-for-byte. Deferred to a
  future cluster move that batches the rename-alias re-exposure.
- `_get_artifact_text(runner, feature, key)` (`:12161`) — async;
  depends on `runner.artifacts.get`. Phase-level. STAYS.
- `_dag_verification_graph_attempt_from_payload(payload, *, …)`
  (`:12194`) — depends on the impl.py-local rename aliases
  `VerificationGraphAttempt` + `VerifyEvidenceNode` +
  `VerifyEvidenceEdge`. Same rename-alias concern as
  `_dag_verify_read_budget_for_projection`. Deferred.
- `_load_verified_dag_verification_graph_projection(runner,
  payload, *, …)` (`:12418`) — async; depends on
  `_execution_control_store_for_runner(runner)`. STAYS.
- `_recover_dag_verification_graph_payload_from_store(runner,
  feature, *, …)` (`:12470`) — async; same store + `runner.
  artifacts.put` dependency. STAYS.
- `_persist_dag_verification_graph_payload(runner, payload)`
  (`:12560`) — async; same store dependency + module-level
  `logger`. STAYS.
- `_validate_dag_verification_graph_payload(payload, *, …)`
  (`:12637`) — depends on cluster-mate
  `_dag_verify_graph_int` + `_dag_verify_graph_digest` +
  `_dag_verify_graph_payload_digest_for_proof` +
  `_dag_verify_graph_store_payload_digest` +
  `_dag_verify_graph_edge_ids` (ALL in the move set!) but ALSO
  on the env-coupled `_dag_expanded_verify_enabled()` +
  `_dag_verify_required_lens_slugs()` (NOT in the move set per the
  hard rule). Deferred until the env-coupled utility cluster moves.
- `_validate_dag_verification_graph_rejected_payload(...)`
  (`:12816`) — same cluster-mate dependency set MINUS the env-
  coupled deps. Could move; but conceptually paired with the
  validator above. Deferred together with the validator for atomic
  movement.
- `_dag_verification_graph_blocker_route_payload(graph_payload)`
  (`:9024`) — depends on the impl.py-local
  `_json_object_from_text` utility (5+ callers across
  implementation.py; not in the move set per the hard rule).
  Deferred.
- `_record_dag_verification_graph_workflow_blocker(...)`
  (`:8968`) — async; cluster of phase-level runner+feature/store
  dependencies + the deferred
  `_dag_verification_graph_blocker_route_payload`. STAYS.
- `_record_dag_verifier_runtime_failure(...)` (`:13499`) — async;
  runner+feature-coupled. STAYS.
- `_run_checkpoint_required_dag_verify_lenses(...)` (`:13693`),
  `_require_dag_verification_graph_approval(...)` (`:13735`),
  `_verify_and_fix_group(...)` (`:13840`),
  `_verify(...)` (`:19914`), `_verify_enhancements(...)`
  (`:20016`), `_run_expanded_dag_verify_lenses(...)` (`:30025`),
  `_single_rca_fix_verify(...)` (`:32383`) — all async
  runner+feature-coupled orchestrators. STAY.
- `_merge_dag_expanded_verify_verdicts(base, lens_verdicts)`
  (`:29944`) — pure-ish but depends on impl.py-local
  `_is_approved`, `_issue_dedupe_key`, `_gap_dedupe_key` (not in
  the move set per the hard rule). Deferred until the dedup-key
  cluster moves.
- `_record_dag_verification_graph_artifact(...)` (`:13002`),
  `_put_dag_verify_artifact(...)` (`:12958`) — async; runner+
  feature/store-coupled. STAY.

Per the prompt edge-case note, the inventory found 19 pure
primitives. The much larger verification-domain PORT surface (the
async runner+feature/store/recorder/subprocess-coupled
orchestrators + the env-coupled utility cluster + the
`_json_object_from_text`-coupled blocker-route payload + the
rename-alias-coupled attempt-from-payload / read-budget builder +
the dedup-key-coupled verdict merger) is genuinely PHASE-LEVEL and
stays in `implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule against
splitting non-pure helpers. The narrow 11g is therefore a
defensible, surgical extraction that locks 19 pure primitives via
the now-standard shim + targeted-test pattern while preserving
the prompt hard rule. This mirrors the Slice-11d sandbox precedent
+ the Slice-11e dispatcher precedent + the Slice-11f gates
precedent — the pure verification-domain primitives move into
`execution/verification.py` alongside the existing pre-11g
verification-graph orchestration surface; the phase-level
verification PORT stays in `implementation.py` because it adapts
phase-level state to the pure verification primitives.

  py` — 19 parametrized identity + 19 parametrized `__module__`
  rebinding + 19 behavioral smoke tests + cluster ownership pin
  + shim-block completeness probe + back-import guard.

  domain primitives moved; bodies preserved byte-for-byte. The

  store/recorder-coupled orchestrators
  `_get_artifact_text`, `_load_verified_dag_verification_graph_
