## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11g COMPLETE

Slice 11g (extend `execution/verification.py` with 19 pure
verification-domain helpers from `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py`) is **COMPLETE** and reviewed CLEAN — **0 P1,
0 P2, 0 P3**. The chunk delivers the seventh extraction surface in
Slice 11; complements (does NOT duplicate) the pre-11g 1314-line
verification-graph orchestration surface already in
`execution/verification.py` (the `VerificationGraphAttempt` +
17 sibling Pydantic models + `stable_digest` + the
`normalize_raw_verifier_result` / `normalize_lens_verifier_result`
/ `merge_verdicts_deterministically` /
`build_aggregate_verdict` / `build_graph_approval_proof` /
`map_verifier_failure` factory family); preserves the pre-11g
surface byte-for-byte; extends the Slice-11a + Slice-11b +
Slice-11c + Slice-11d + Slice-11e + Slice-11f shim pattern with a
parallel Slice-11g re-export block; locks the new facade with
60 targeted tests.

**WHAT LANDED — source.** Two modified modules; both edits are
mechanical extraction + re-export (no behavior change). The
implementer correctly chose to EXTEND the pre-existing
`execution/verification.py` (1314 lines pre-11g — the canonical
home for the `VerificationGraphAttempt` + 17 Pydantic models +
`stable_digest` cluster + the 6 normalize/merge/build/map
factories) rather than create a new module; the doc-11 row for
`execution/verification.py` ("Model verifier orchestration,
expanded lenses, bounded verify context, verifier outcome
typing") names a single module that owns this domain, so an
additive extension is the structurally-correct choice.

- **MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/
  verification.py` (was 1314 lines pre-11g; now 1751 lines; net
  delta +437). The 19 helpers land at `:1357-1700` (appended
  before `__all__`). A module-level alias
  `verify_graph_stable_digest = stable_digest` at `:299`
  preserves the impl.py-local rename so the moved digest-
  cluster bodies remain byte-identical to their pre-extraction
  form. An `ImportError`-tolerant import of
  `dispatcher_stable_digest` from `.dispatcher` at `:29-38`
  preserves the fallback semantics of the pre-11g impl.py-local
  `try/except` (the same shape that wrapped the digest-cluster
  before the move). The pre-11g surface (the
  `VerificationGraphAttempt` + 17 Pydantic models +
  `stable_digest` + the 6 normalize/merge/build/map factories +
  the 3 RuntimeError subclasses `TypedFailure` /
  `GraphApprovalError` / `GraphIdempotencyConflict`) is
  preserved byte-for-byte. New imports added:
  `ImplementationResult` + `ImplementationTask` added to the
  `models.outputs` import; `DagVerifyLensSpec` added from
  `.types`; `to_str` added from `iriai_compose`; the
  `ImportError`-guarded import of `dispatcher_stable_digest`.
  `__all__` extended with the 19 new names +
  `verify_graph_stable_digest`. NO import from `..phases.
  implementation` or any phase module (compatibility arrow
  points IN, never OUT — locked by a back-import guard test in
  `test_verification_extraction.py`).

  The 19 moved primitives (grouped by sub-cluster):
  - **Parser / key (2)**:
    `_dag_verify_stage_from_projection_key(projection_key) -> str`
    (pure splitter, returns the stage iff the prefix is
    `"dag-verify"` and arity is 3 else `"unknown"`),
    `_dag_verify_graph_artifact_key(group_idx, stage) -> str`
    (pure f-string formatter returning
    `f"dag-verify-graph:g{group_idx}:{stage}"`).
  - **Pydantic dispatcher (1)**: `_pydantic_json(value) ->
    object` (pure 4-liner; dispatches to
    `value.model_dump(mode="json")` if the attribute is
    callable, else `to_str(value)`).
  - **Digest cluster (4)**:
    `_synthetic_verification_verdict_id(projection_key, verdict,
    *, suffix="") -> int` (pure factory computing a
    deterministic int via the `verify_graph_stable_digest or
    dispatcher_stable_digest` chain with sha256 last-resort
    fallback);
    `_dag_verify_graph_digest(value) -> str` (pure digest with
    the same fallback chain);
    `_dag_verify_graph_store_payload_digest(payload) -> str`
    (pure composer of
    `_dag_verify_graph_digest(_dag_verify_graph_payload_
    without_durable(payload))`);
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_digest_for_proof(payload) -> str`
    (pure variant that also strips `proof.proof_digest` and
    `proof.graph_payload_digest` before digesting).
  - **Payload helpers (4)**:
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_covers_projection(payload_text,
    key) -> bool` (pure predicate composing
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_for_projection`);
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_has_durable_projection(payload)
    -> bool` (pure dict-shape predicate);
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_without_durable(payload) ->
    dict[str, Any]` (pure JSON round-trip that strips
    `durable_projection`);
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_for_projection(payload_text, key)
    -> dict[str, Any] | None` (pure JSON parser + shape
    predicate).
  - **Metadata walkers (2)**:
    `_dag_verify_graph_projection_metadata(result) -> dict[str,
    Any]` (pure attribute/dict walker over `result.row` +
    `result.projection_links` + `result.graph` /
    `result.evidence_graph`);
    `_dag_verify_graph_durable_metadata_from_reload(result,
    verified) -> dict[str, Any]` (pure walker composing
    `_dag_verify_graph_projection_metadata` + the
    `verified["graph"]` + `verified["required_edges"]` +
    `verified["projection_links"]` dict shapes).
  - **Int / edge (2)**:
    `_dag_verify_graph_int(value) -> int | None` (pure
    try/except int conversion);
    `_dag_verify_graph_edge_ids(value) -> list[str]` (pure
    normalizer composing `_dag_verify_graph_int` per element).
  - **Ref / lineage (2)**:
    `_dag_verify_graph_ref(value) -> dict[str, Any]` (pure
    attribute/payload-dict walker extracting a stable set of
    id/digest fields);
    `_dag_verify_graph_lineage_payload(*, feature_id,
    projection_key, dag_sha256, group_idx, stage, tasks=None,
    results=None, contracts_by_task_id=None,
    workspace_snapshots=None) -> dict[str, Any]` (pure lineage
    builder composing `_dag_verify_graph_ref` +
    `_dag_verify_graph_digest`).
  - **Lens prefix (2)**:
    `_prefix_lens_issue(spec, issue) -> Issue` (pure 1-line
    factory returning
    `issue.model_copy(update={"description": f"[{spec.label}
    Lens] {issue.description}"})`);
    `_prefix_lens_gap(spec, gap) -> Gap` (pure 1-line factory
    mirroring `_prefix_lens_issue` for `Gap`).

- **MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/
  implementation.py` (line count: 33503 → 33252; net delta -251
  lines). The 19 helper definitions were removed in place at
  their pre-11g source locations; the Slice-11a shim block at
  `:321-354`, the Slice-11b shim block at `:368-382`, the
  Slice-11c shim block at `:383-400`, the Slice-11d shim block
  at `:401-427`, the Slice-11e shim block at `:428-463`, and
  the Slice-11f shim block at `:464-511` are UNCHANGED; a NEW
  parallel sibling Slice-11g block was inserted at `:512-577`:
  a header banner at `:512-556` documenting the extraction +
  the boundary contract + the deferred PORT surface, followed
  by the re-import `from ..execution.verification import (...)`
  at `:557-577` listing all 19 newly-moved names alphabetically.
  Nothing else in `implementation.py` was edited — every method
  body, every routing function, every `ImplementationPhase`
  stage is byte-for-byte preserved; the phase-level
  verification PORT surface (the 16 async runner+feature/store/
  recorder-coupled orchestrators + the 4 env-coupled lens
  helpers + the `_json_object_from_text`-coupled blocker-route
  + the rename-alias-coupled `_validate_dag_verification_graph_

**WHAT LANDED — tests.** One new file, purely additive. No
existing test was edited or removed.

- **NEW** `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_verification_
  extraction.py` (810 lines, **60 tests**). The doc-11 § "How To
  Use This Map" four-question contract is locked in code:
  - **Parametrized shim identity (19 tests).** For every name
    in the moved-names list, asserts `getattr(implementation,
    X) is getattr(verification, X)` — proving the shim re-
    exports the SAME object, not a copy. The `is` identity
    (rather than equality) is the structural property that
    keeps `monkeypatch.setattr` working: a patched attribute on
    `implementation_module` is the SAME binding the canonical
    module serves.
  - **Parametrized canonical `__module__` rebinding (19
    tests).** For every moved callable, asserts the canonical
    function object's `__module__` is
    `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.verification`
    (not `…phases.implementation`) — proving the definition
    genuinely moved rather than being re-aliased from the old
    module.
  - **19 behavioral smoke tests** covering cross-cutting
    behavioral invariants the moved primitives embody:
    `_dag_verify_stage_from_projection_key` canonical routes
    (3-arity `dag-verify:gX:stage` → `stage`; non-3-arity →
    `unknown`); `_dag_verify_graph_artifact_key` format;
    `_pydantic_json` dispatch to `model_dump(mode="json")` for
    Pydantic instances + `to_str` fallback for non-Pydantic;
    `_synthetic_verification_verdict_id` deterministic int from
    digest; `_dag_verify_graph_int` int round-trip safety;
    `_dag_verify_graph_edge_ids` list normalization;
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_has_durable_projection`
    predicate over the dict-shape signature;
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_without_durable` JSON round-trip
    strip; `_dag_verify_graph_digest` falls back via the
    `verify_graph_stable_digest or dispatcher_stable_digest`
    chain;
    `_dag_verify_graph_store_payload_digest` composes the
    durable-strip + digest;
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_digest_for_proof` additionally
    strips proof self-refs;
    `_dag_verify_graph_payload_for_projection` JSON parse +
    shape predicate; `_dag_verify_graph_payload_covers_
    projection` predicate; `_dag_verify_graph_ref` canonical-
    attr extraction; `_dag_verify_graph_lineage_payload`
    canonical lineage shape; `_prefix_lens_issue` /
    `_prefix_lens_gap` prepend `[<label> Lens]`;
    `_dag_verify_graph_projection_metadata` typed-row state
    walker; `_dag_verify_graph_durable_metadata_from_reload`
    persisted shape.

    the 19 names land in the canonical `execution/
    verification.py` module and not in any other `execution/`
    sibling; 19-name shim-block completeness probe verifying
    the Slice-11g re-export at `implementation.py:557-577`
    covers exactly the 19 moved names; AND a back-import guard
    asserting `execution/verification.py` never imports from
    `implementation.py` (the compatibility arrow points IN,
    never OUT).

**DOC-11 FOUR-QUESTION CONTRACT (per reviewer).**

- **Q1 — What behavior moved?** **NONE.** 19 pure helpers
  (parser/key + Pydantic dispatcher + 4 digest-cluster + 4
  payload-cluster + 2 metadata walkers + int/edge + 2 ref/
  lineage + 2 lens-prefix) moved byte-for-byte from
  `implementation.py` to `execution/verification.py:1357-1700`.
  No logic change; no signature change; no validation added or
  removed; no return-value-shape change; no string-literal
  change. The module-level alias
  `verify_graph_stable_digest = stable_digest` (at
  `verification.py:299`) preserves the impl.py-local rename so
  the moved digest-cluster bodies remain byte-identical; the
  `ImportError`-guarded `dispatcher_stable_digest` import at
  `verification.py:29-38` preserves the byte-for-byte fallback
  chain semantics of the pre-11g impl.py-local `try/except`.
- **Q2 — What shims still work?** **All 19 names.** Re-exported
  at `implementation.py:557-577` (a parallel sibling block of
  the Slice-11a shim at `:321-354`, the Slice-11b shim at
  `:368-382`, the Slice-11c shim at `:383-400`, the Slice-11d
  shim at `:401-427`, the Slice-11e shim at `:428-463`, and
  the Slice-11f shim at `:464-511`). Every existing `from
  iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import
  X` for any of the 19 moved names resolves to the SAME object
  as `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.
  verification import X` — proven by the 19 parametrized
  identity assertions. External consumers in `tests/workflows/
  test_dag_expanded_verify.py` (6 call sites that exercise the
  verification-graph cluster via the implementation-module
  attribute path) continue resolving through the shim
  identity-equivalent.
- **Q3 — What targeted tests prove the new facade + the shim?**
  `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_verification_
  extraction.py` — **60 tests** (19 parametrized identity + 19
  parametrized `__module__` rebinding + 19 behavioral smoke
  tests + 3 structural tests: the cluster-ownership pin, the
  19-name shim-block completeness probe, AND the back-import
  guard against `verification.py` importing from
  `implementation.py`). Locks both the structural identity and
  the canonical behavior of each moved primitive.
- **Q4 — Why is the PR refactor-only?** Only pure verification-
  domain primitives moved; bodies preserved byte-for-byte. The
  phase-level verification PORT surface stays in
  `implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule: (a) the 16
  async runner+feature/store/recorder-coupled orchestrators
  (`_get_artifact_text`, `_load_verified_dag_verification_
  graph_projection`, `_recover_dag_verification_graph_payload_
  from_store`, `_persist_dag_verification_graph_payload`,
  `_record_dag_verification_graph_artifact`,
  `_put_dag_verify_artifact`, `_record_dag_verifier_runtime_
  failure`, `_run_checkpoint_required_dag_verify_lenses`,
  `_require_dag_verification_graph_approval`,
  `_verify_and_fix_group`, `_run_expanded_dag_verify_lenses`,
  `_verify`, `_verify_enhancements`,
  `_single_rca_fix_verify`, `_record_dag_verification_graph_
  workflow_blocker`, `_validate_dag_verification_graph_
  rejected_payload`) close over `runner` + `feature` + `store`
  + the recorder; (b) the 4 env-coupled lens helpers
  (`_dag_expanded_verify_enabled`, `_dag_verify_lens_specs`,
  `_dag_verify_required_lens_slugs`, `_dag_verify_read_budget_
  for_projection`) depend on the env-flag scan + the impl.py-
  local Role bindings (`verifier`, `security_auditor`,
  `regression_tester`); (c) the `_json_object_from_text`-
  coupled `_dag_verification_graph_blocker_route_payload` and
  the rename-alias-coupled `_dag_verification_graph_attempt_
  from_payload` + `_validate_dag_verification_graph_payload`
  depend on impl.py-local utilities (the `_json_object_from_
  text` parser used by 5+ callers; the
  `VerifyEvidenceNode`/`VerifyEvidenceEdge`/
  `VerificationGraphAttempt` rename aliases); (d) the dedup-
  key-coupled `_merge_dag_expanded_verify_verdicts` depends on
  impl.py-local `_is_approved` / `_issue_dedupe_key` /
  `_gap_dedupe_key` utilities (none in the move set per the

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11g independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3**. All six vectors PASS + all
four doc-11 questions are answered satisfactorily. The reviewer
additionally judged the verification PRIMITIVE/PORT split as
**ACCEPTABLE** — mirroring the Slice-11d sandbox + Slice-11e
dispatcher + Slice-11f gates precedents exactly. The pure
verification-domain primitives move into `execution/
verification.py` alongside the existing pre-11g
`VerificationGraphAttempt` + 17 Pydantic models +
`stable_digest` + 6 normalize/merge/build/map factories; the
phase-level verification PORT (the 16 async runner+feature/
store/recorder-coupled orchestrators + the 4 env-coupled lens
helpers + the `_json_object_from_text`-coupled blocker-route +
the rename-alias-coupled validators + the dedup-key-coupled
verdict merger) stays in `implementation.py` because that port
exists to adapt phase-level state (`runner` + `feature` +
`store` + the impl.py-local utility chain + the
`InMemoryEvidenceRecorder` instance + the env-flag scan + the
Role bindings + the rename aliases + the dedup-key utilities)
to the pure verification-graph primitives — which is the
structurally-correct boundary per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API
contracts" (the named API for `execution/verification.py` is
`VerificationService.verify_group` / `run_lenses` /
`load_bounded_context` — the PRIMITIVE; the adapters that
satisfy these ports from `implementation.py`'s runner+feature+
store state are by definition phase-level). The narrow 11g is
therefore a defensible, surgical extraction that locks 19 pure
primitives via the now-standard shim + targeted-test pattern
while preserving the prompt hard rule.

Reviewer specifics:

- **V1 (doc-11 acceptance criteria for an extraction PR).** All
  four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions are answered
  above; the extraction order constraint (types via 11a, then
  git_service via 11b, then task_contracts via 11c, then
  sandbox via 11d, then dispatcher via 11e, then gates via 11f,
  then verification via 11g) is satisfied; the compatibility-
  arrow direction (extracted module never imports back into
  `implementation.py`) is verified by the back-import guard
  test in `test_verification_extraction.py`. `execution/
  verification.py`'s import set is the existing pre-11g set
  plus the 4 new sibling-module imports
  (`ImplementationResult` + `ImplementationTask` from
  `..models.outputs`; `DagVerifyLensSpec` from `.types`;
  `to_str` from `iriai_compose`) plus the `ImportError`-
  guarded `dispatcher_stable_digest` import; no `from ..
  phases.implementation` import.
- **V2 (source additivity + no-behavior-change).** Reviewer
  spot-checked the 19 primitive bodies byte-for-byte against
  `git show HEAD:…implementation.py`; every body is byte-for-
  byte equal to its pre-extraction form modulo whitespace/
  indent. The pre-11g surface in `execution/verification.py`
  (`VerificationGraphAttempt` + the 17 sibling Pydantic models
  + `stable_digest` + the 6 normalize/merge/build/map
  factories + the 3 RuntimeError subclasses) is preserved
  byte-for-byte; the 19 new helpers land at `:1357-1700`; the
  module-level alias `verify_graph_stable_digest =
  stable_digest` at `:299` + the `ImportError`-guarded
  `dispatcher_stable_digest` import at `:29-38` preserve the
  byte-for-byte fallback-chain semantics.
- **V3 (test honesty + shim coverage).** All 60 tests are real
  assertions (no `pytest.skip`; no `pass`-stubs); the 19
  identity assertions genuinely fail if the shim is removed;
  the 19 `__module__` assertions genuinely fail if the moved
  name is re-aliased rather than re-imported; the 19
  behavioral smoke assertions exercise the parser/key
  formatters, the Pydantic dispatcher, the digest-cluster
  fallback chain, the durable-projection predicate + strip +
  digest, the proof self-ref strip + digest, the int/edge
  normalizer, the ref / lineage walker, the lens-prefix label
  factories, the typed-row state walker, and the reload-
  metadata persistence shape. The reviewer verified the shim
  covers all 19 names by reading `implementation.py:557-577`
  and matching against the test file's `MOVED_NAMES`. The
  back-import guard test reads `execution/verification.py` as
  text and asserts no `from …phases.implementation` line
  exists.
- **V4 (fail-closed edges).** Each pure primitive's defensive
  path is structurally exercised: the parser returns
  `"unknown"` on non-`dag-verify`-prefixed keys or non-3-arity
  shapes; the `_pydantic_json` dispatcher falls back to
  `to_str(value)` when `model_dump` is not callable; the
  digest chain falls back through `verify_graph_stable_digest
  or dispatcher_stable_digest` to sha256 last resort; the
  payload predicates return False on missing fields; the int
  converter returns None on non-int values; the edge-id
  normalizer drops None entries. No exception swallowing in
  any primitive.
- **V5 (cross-component contract).** Only `implementation.py`
  (the 19 helper removals + the extended shim block) and
  `verification.py` (the 19 helper additions + the
  `verify_graph_stable_digest` alias + the
  `dispatcher_stable_digest` ImportError-guard + the
  `__all__` extension + the 4 new imports) were modified for
  source. Imports from `verification.py` are confined to (a)
  the shim at `implementation.py:557-577` and (b) the new
  test file's identity assertions; no other consumer imports
  the moved names directly from the new module. External
  consumers in `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py`
  (6 call sites) continue resolving via the `implementation.
  py` shim identity-equivalent.
- **V6 (docs / ledger).** This journal entry + the JSONL
  acceptance line + the STATUS.md pointer advance constitute
  the V6 surface for 11g; the reviewer verified the journal
  entry's claims match the on-disk state.

The reviewer also explicitly confirmed the verification
PRIMITIVE/PORT split is defensible — the 16 async runner+
feature/store/recorder-coupled orchestrators + the 4 env-
coupled lens helpers + the `_json_object_from_text`-coupled
blocker-route + the rename-alias-coupled validators + the
dedup-key-coupled verdict merger each have a named non-pure
dependency that justifies keeping them in `implementation.py`
until the owning sub-slice or utility-cluster sub-slice lands.
This mirrors the Slice-11d sandbox PORT + the Slice-11e
dispatcher PORT + the Slice-11f gates PORT precedents
exactly.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11h STARTING

The eighth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the repair-domain primitive surface — is
beginning. Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/repair.py`: "`RouteExecutor.build_route_request`,
`build_repair_request`, `build_retry_request`, `RepairService.execute`,
`RepairService.resume`, `RepairService.summarize_outcome`. Concrete
`RepairRequest`/`RepairOutcome` and `RetryRequest`/`RetryOutcome`
execution for product, contract, canonicalization, workspace, commit-
hygiene, dispatcher retry, verifier retry, merge retry, sandbox
capture retry, and cleanup routes." The pre-11h repair surface (the
`RouteExecutor` + `RepairRequest`/`RepairOutcome` Pydantic models +
`RetryRequest`/`RetryOutcome` Pydantic models + `RouteExecutorError`
+ 8 deterministic scope/budget/idempotency-key builders +
`_authorized_direct_source_verdict` +
`_authorized_product_repair_source_verdict` + `stable_digest` +
JSON-able utilities) is already in `execution/repair.py` (889 lines
pre-11h) — that is the canonical home for the typed route-to-repair
request builder surface. Slice 11h EXTENDS that module with 24 pure
repair-domain helpers + constants currently in `implementation.py`
that depend only on stdlib + `models.outputs`
(`Verdict`/`Issue`/`ImplementationResult`/`ImplementationTask`) +
the cluster-mate `DagDirectRepairRoute` from `.types` — WITHOUT
dragging in any `WorkflowRunner`/`Feature`/`logger`/`Path`-coupled
adapters or the env-coupled `_env_flag_enabled` / impl.py-local
`_dedupe_preserving_order` / `_safe_context_stem` /
`_workflow_blocker_text` helpers.

**SCOPE TO MOVE (24 pure helpers + constants).** Each one is byte-
for-byte self-contained; only stdlib + cluster-mates + canonical
exports from sibling modules are needed; no runner/feature/store/
logger/io dependencies.

**Route constants (7)** — referenced 58 times internally in
`implementation.py`; no external test/source consumer references
them directly under the impl.py-local name.

1. `_COMMIT_HYGIENE_ROUTE = "commit_hygiene_focused"` — string
   constant.
2. `_MANIFEST_FORBIDDEN_CLEANUP_ROUTE = "manifest_forbidden_product_cleanup"` —
   string constant; note the existing `repair.py`
   `_DIRECT_CONTRACT_PRODUCT_REPAIR_ROUTES = frozenset({"manifest_forbidden_product_cleanup"})`
   uses the same literal value.
3. `_REPO_HYGIENE_ROUTE = "repo_hygiene_operator"` — string
   constant.
4. `_NORMAL_VERIFY_ROUTE = "normal_verify_repair"` — string
   constant; the existing `repair.py`
   `_DIRECT_PRODUCT_REPAIR_ROUTES = frozenset({"normal_verify_repair"})`
   uses the same literal value.
5. `_MANIFEST_FORBIDDEN_MARKER = "manifest-forbidden product cleanup"` —
   string constant for issue-text scan.
6. `_OPERATOR_REQUIRED_MARKER = "operator_required=true"` — string
   constant for issue-text scan.
7. `_DAG_CONTRADICTION_MIXED_REPAIR_KIND = "mixed_repair"` — string
   constant for contradiction resolution-kind.

**Direct-route classification cluster (8)** — depends on
`Verdict`/`Issue` from `models.outputs` + `DagDirectRepairRoute`
from `.types` + the 6 route constants moved above + stdlib `re` +
`hashlib`.

8. `_commit_failure_issue_kind(issue) -> str` — `implementation.
   py:1151`. Pure 16-liner; lower-cases `issue.description` +
   `issue.file` and returns one of the four route names.
9. `_is_deterministic_dag_preflight_issue(issue) -> bool` —
   `implementation.py:1170`. Pure 12-liner over the same lower-
   cased text; True iff a manifest-forbidden, repair-stale-task-
   metadata, or programmatic-DAG-preflight marker hits.
10. `_direct_route_target(issue) -> str` — `implementation.py:
    1184`. Pure 6-liner returning `issue.file` or
    `f"{issue.file}:{issue.line}"`.
11. `_direct_route_issue_operator_required(issue) -> bool` —
    `implementation.py:1192`. Pure 1-liner over
    `_OPERATOR_REQUIRED_MARKER`.
12. `_normalize_direct_route_signature(verdict, route) -> str` —
    `implementation.py:1196`. Pure 15-liner returning a sha256 hex
    digest over the sorted+normalized concern descriptions.
13. `_classify_dag_direct_repair_route(verdict) -> DagDirectRepairRoute` —
    `implementation.py:1213`. Pure 130-liner; the canonical direct-
    route classifier consumed by 11 test sites in
    `tests/workflows/test_dag_expanded_verify.py`.
14. `_direct_route_target_paths(route) -> list[str]` —
    `implementation.py:764`. Pure 12-liner returning sorted unique
    paths from `route.target_files`.
15. `_direct_route_failure_pair(route) -> tuple[str, str, str]` —
    `implementation.py:778`. Pure 10-liner mapping route name to
    `(failure_class, failure_type, source)`.

**Pure artifact-key classifiers (5)** — depend only on stdlib +
each other; do NOT touch `_dedupe_preserving_order` (so the
text-scanner cluster `_dag_artifact_repair_refs_from_text` etc.
STAYS in `implementation.py`).

16. `_normalize_dag_artifact_repair_ref(ref) -> str` —
    `implementation.py:20623`. Pure 9-liner regex+strip
    normalization.
17. `_is_dag_artifact_repair_path(path) -> bool` —
    `implementation.py:20591`. Pure 30-liner over artifact-marker
    substrings + path prefixes.
18. `_is_dag_artifact_repair_key(ref) -> bool` —
    `implementation.py:20634`. Pure 50-liner; depends on cluster-
    mate `_is_dag_task_artifact_key` (also moved).
19. `_is_derived_dag_artifact_key(ref) -> bool` —
    `implementation.py:20685`. Pure 6-liner.
20. `_is_dag_task_artifact_key(ref) -> bool` —
    `implementation.py:20694`. Pure 5-liner.

**Repair-misc primitives (4)** — depend only on stdlib +
`DagContradictionResolution` from `.types`.

21. `_post_dag_repair_group_idx(source, attempt_number) -> int` —
    `implementation.py:6641`. Pure 2-liner sha256 digest.
22. `_dag_contradiction_needs_artifact_repair(resolution) -> bool` —
    `implementation.py:21569`. Pure 5-liner predicate.
23. `_dag_contradiction_fix_guidance(resolution) -> str` —
    `implementation.py:21599`. Pure 9-liner format string.
24. `_dag_product_cleanup_ready_for_artifact_repair(report) -> bool` —
    `implementation.py:27185`. Pure 12-liner over a skipped-
    reasons set.

**LEAVE BEHIND (phase-level, deferred).** Each one is named with
its dependency class.

- `_dag_parallel_repair_enabled()` /
  `_dag_preflight_repair_enabled()` — env-flag wrappers over
  `_env_flag_enabled` (impl.py-local).
- `_dag_artifact_repair_refs_from_text(text)` /
  `_dag_artifact_repair_refs_from_planned(planned)` /
  `_dag_artifact_repair_target_refs(resolution, *, planned)` /
  `_dag_artifact_repair_paths_safe(planned)` /
  `_dag_task_artifact_refs_from_planned(planned)` /
  `_planned_non_dag_artifact_refs(planned)` /
  `_safe_dag_task_artifact_refs(refs)` /
  `_dag_task_artifact_refs_from_reported_result_text(text)` —
  all depend on impl.py-local `_dedupe_preserving_order`.
- `_planned_needs_source_artifact_repair(planned)` /
  `_planned_needs_dag_task_artifact_repair(planned)` /
  `_dag_metadata_only_repair_candidate(planned)` /
  `_dag_blocked_result_should_reroute_to_artifact_repair(planned,
  result)` /
  `_dag_artifact_repair_resolution_from_planned(planned, *,
  reason, blocked_result)` /
  `_dag_task_artifact_refs_from_rca(rca, *, extra_text)` /
  `_rca_needs_dag_task_artifact_repair(rca, *, extra_text,
  target_refs)` — depend on the text-scanner cluster + impl.py-
  local `_dedupe_preserving_order`.
- `_dag_artifact_repair_synthetic_result(group_idx, retry,
  planned, result, record)` /
  `_dag_repair_task_failed_result(group_idx, retry, gid, exc)` —
  depend on impl.py-local `_safe_context_stem` +
  `_workflow_blocker_text`.
- `_repair_task_for_rca(repair_id, repo_id, affected_files)` /
  `_normalize_repair_contract_path(raw_path, *, repo_id,
  repo_root)` / `_repair_contract_for_paths(...)` — depend on
  impl.py-local `_dedupe_preserving_order` /
  `_sandbox_blocker` / `Path` operations + factories
  (`TaskFileScope` etc.).
- `_repair_sandbox_required(runner, feature_root)` /
  `_bind_repair_sandbox(runner, feature, *, ...)` /
  `_dag_artifact_repair_workspace(runner, feature, feature_root)` /
  `_dag_artifact_key_for_repair_file(runner, feature,
  reported_path)` /
  `_dag_artifact_repair_target_path(runner, feature, target_ref,
  feature_root)` /
  `_apply_dag_artifact_repair_updates(runner, feature, result,
  feature_root)` /
  `_run_dag_artifact_repair_lane(runner, feature, group_idx, ...)` /
  `_attempt_parallel_dag_repair(runner, feature, ...)` /
  `_attempt_dag_authority_gate_repair(runner, feature, ...)` /
  `_classify_dag_repair_path(reported_path, roots, feature_root,
  alias_map)` /
  `_sanitize_dag_repair_result(result, roots, feature_root,
  alias_map)` /
  `_sanitize_dag_repair_results(runner, feature, ...)` /
  `_contract_guard_results_for_repair(results,
  contracts_by_task_id)` /
  `_dag_manifest_repo_prefix_for_config(config_path, scan_root)` /
  `_deterministic_dag_artifact_repair_group(group_idx, retry,
  verdict, group_tasks, verifier_path_problems, feature_root)` —
  all `runner`/`feature`/`Path`/`async`-coupled or depend on
  larger impl.py-local helper chains.
- `_record_dag_direct_repair_route(runner, feature, ...)` /
  `_record_dag_checkpoint_direct_repair_route(runner, feature,
  ...)` — async runner+feature-coupled.

The non-pure repair-orchestration PORT surface stays in
`implementation.py`; the doc-11 ports
`RouteExecutor.build_route_request` / `RepairService.execute` /
`RepairService.resume` / `RepairService.summarize_outcome` are
the PRIMITIVE that the future repair-service refactor will adapt
this PORT into. Slice 11h is the additive extraction of the
pure direct-route classification + pure artifact-key classifier
+ pure repair-misc primitives.

**ESTABLISHED PATTERN.** Slice 11h mirrors the Slice-11a +
Slice-11b + Slice-11c + Slice-11d + Slice-11e + Slice-11f +
Slice-11g pattern exactly:

1. EXTEND the pre-existing `execution/repair.py` with the 24
   helpers + constants moved byte-for-byte; preserve the entire
   pre-11h `RouteExecutor` + request-types surface.
2. Add a NEW parallel sibling shim block at `implementation.py`
   head (sibling to the Slice-11a through Slice-11g blocks at
   `:321-577`) that re-imports every moved name from
   `..execution.repair`.
3. Remove the 24 helper definitions in place at their pre-11h
   source locations in `implementation.py`.
4. Write a NEW targeted test file `tests/workflows/develop/
   execution/test_repair_extraction.py` (sibling of the prior
   extraction-test files) asserting identity-equivalence,
   `__module__` rebinding, behavioral smoke + a back-import
   guard against `repair.py` importing from `implementation.py`.

`tests/supervisor/`,

`tests/test_public_dashboard.py`,

PRIMITIVE/PORT SPLIT JUDGMENT: **ACCEPTABLE** — mirrors the
Slice-11d sandbox + Slice-11e dispatcher + Slice-11f gates +
Slice-11g verification precedents exactly. The pure repair-domain
primitives move into `execution/repair.py` alongside the existing
pre-11h `RouteExecutor` + request-types surface; the phase-level
repair PORT (the runner+feature/store-coupled async orchestrators
+ the env-flag wrappers + the impl.py-local
`_dedupe_preserving_order`/`_safe_context_stem`/
`_workflow_blocker_text`-coupled text-scanner / factory clusters +
the impl.py-local `_classify_dag_repair_path` path-classifier
chain + the `_repair_task_for_rca` / `_repair_contract_for_paths`
factories + the `_repair_sandbox_required` /
`_contract_guard_results_for_repair` runner-services-coupled
helpers + the `_deterministic_dag_artifact_repair_group` Path-
coupled factory) stays in `implementation.py` because that port
adapts phase-level state (`runner` + `feature` + the
`InMemoryEvidenceRecorder` instance + the env-flag scan + the
impl.py-local `_dedupe_preserving_order` / `_safe_context_stem` /
`_workflow_blocker_text` utility chain + the `WorkspaceAuthority`
+ `WorkspaceManager` + `ArtifactMirror` services) to the pure
repair primitives — which is the structurally-correct boundary
per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" (the named API for
`execution/repair.py` is `RouteExecutor.build_route_request` /
`build_repair_request` / `build_retry_request` /
`RepairService.execute` / `RepairService.resume` /
`RepairService.summarize_outcome` — the PRIMITIVE; the adapters
that satisfy these ports from `implementation.py`'s
runner+feature+store state are by definition phase-level).

**Slice 11 sub-slice plan (recap).** Per the Slice 11 STARTING
journal entry, the dependency-ordered plan is: 11a types (DONE)
→ 11b git_service (DONE) → 11c task_contracts (DONE) → 11d
sandbox (DONE) → 11e dispatcher (DONE) → 11f gates (DONE) →
11g verification (DONE) → 11h repair (DONE) → 11i
failure_router → **11j merge_queue (P3-6 fold-in PRIMARY OWNER)**
→ 11k regroup_overlay → 11l post_dag_gates → 11m post_test_guard
→ 11n slice-end SIX-VECTOR review + formal Slice 11 ACCEPTANCE.
**Next: 11i failure_router** (per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md` § "Proposed Module Boundaries" —
`execution/failure_router.py` is the next surface after
`execution/repair.py`). The 11i implementer should: inventory
the existing `execution/failure_router.py` (it already exists
from Slice 07); identify failure-router helpers in
`implementation.py` (search `_failure_router_*`, `_typed_*`,
`_route_decision_*`); apply the same shim + targeted-test
pattern as 11a + 11b + 11c + 11d + 11e + 11f + 11g + 11h.

Slice 11h is closed. Slice 11i (extract
`execution/failure_router.py`) is next.


## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11i STARTING

The ninth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the failure-router consumer surface — is
beginning. Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/failure_router.py`: "`FailureRouter.decide(failure_id) ->
RouteDecision`. Typed failure taxonomy, retry budgets, deterministic
route selection, quiesce/escalation decisions. Must not own: running
repairs, mutating git, scanning artifact bodies." The pre-11i surface
(the Slice-07 `FailureRouter` + `RouteDecision` + `FailureObservation`
+ `InMemoryFailureRouterPort` + `ROUTE_TABLE` typed taxonomy + the
`_route` policy factory + the 53-row route table + the budget
exhaustion + idempotency-key builders) is already in
`execution/failure_router.py` (1838 lines pre-11i) — that is the
canonical home for the typed failure router. Slice 11i EXTENDS that
module with 2 pure typed→legacy `RouteDecision`-to-dict adapter
helpers currently in `implementation.py` that depend only on stdlib
+ `getattr` over a `RouteDecision`-shaped duck-typed input — WITHOUT
dragging in any `WorkflowRunner`/`Feature`/`runner.services`-coupled
phase-level adapters.

**INVENTORY (failure-router surface in `implementation.py`).**

Pre-11i `implementation.py` `_failure_router_*` / `_route_decision_*`
/ `_typed_*` / `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` cluster + the
runtime_provider retry-route adapter at `:13190`:

- `_failure_router_for_runner(runner)` — `implementation.py:729`.
  Resolves the failure router from `runner.services["failure_router"]`
  / `runner.services["failure_router_port"]` (the dict-services
  branch) OR `runner._failure_router_port` attribute (the attribute
  fallback) so the in-memory in-process router is reused across DAG
  dispatches and across the merge-queue drain. **PHASE-LEVEL —
  STAYS.** Runner-coupled via `runner.services` + the `setattr`
  fallback; not a pure function.
- `_route_decision_retry_budget_payload(decision, *, action,
  max_attempts=None) -> dict[str, Any]` — `implementation.py:757`.
  Pure 20-liner; reads a `RouteDecision`-shaped object via
  `getattr` and returns a flat dict with the legacy `retry_budget`
  keys (`route`, `budget_key`, `max_attempts`, `max_retries`,
  `remaining_attempts`, `idempotency_key`, `reservation_ordinal`,
  `budget_exhausted`). `max(0, int(...))` clamps negative
  remaining/ordinal to 0; `max_attempts` is derived from
  `remaining + ordinal if ordinal else remaining` when `None`.
  Stdlib-only (`getattr`/`int`/`max`/`str`/`bool`). **MOVE.**
- `_route_decision_compat_payload(decision, *, failure_class,
  failure_type, max_attempts=None, legacy_route="",
  legacy_failure_type="") -> dict[str, Any]` —
  `implementation.py:779`. Pure 43-liner; reads a `RouteDecision`-
  shaped object via `getattr` and returns the legacy full payload
  with `failure_id`/`typed_failure_id` dual aliasing,
  `signature_hash`/`stable_signature_hash` dual aliasing,
  `action`/`route` dual aliasing, the
  `retryable=action.startswith("retry_")` string-derived flag, the
  `operator_required=action == "operator_required"` value check,
  the deep-copied `repair_scope` dict, the materialized
  `required_evidence_ids` list, and the nested
  `retry_budget` dict (delegated to
  `_route_decision_retry_budget_payload`). Optional tail keys
  `legacy_route` / `legacy_failure_type` emitted only when non-
  empty (truthiness check). Stdlib-only + the moved sibling
  `_route_decision_retry_budget_payload`. **MOVE.**
- `_typed_direct_route_payload(runner, feature, group_idx, retry,
  route, *, dag_sha256, source_verdict_key, status)` —
  `implementation.py:827`. **PHASE-LEVEL — STAYS.** Takes `runner`
  + `feature` + `DagDirectRepairRoute` and calls
  `_failure_router_for_runner(runner)`; orchestrates the typed
  `FailureObservation` build + `record` + `decide` +
  `mark_route_started` chain. Runner-coupled.
- `_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS` (`implementation.py:5049`)
  + `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure(runner, feature, *, ...,
  item_id, failure_class, ...)` (`implementation.py:5063`) —
  **PHASE-LEVEL — STAYS.** Runner+feature-coupled; tightly tied
  to the merge-queue drain path. Per the Slice-11j fold-in plan
  (recorded in the Slice 11a STARTING entry), this is properly
  Slice 11j territory (merge_queue.py is the producer-side
  owner of the apply-failure routing surface). Leaving it here
  keeps the P3-6 producer + consumer changes inside the SAME
  atomic 11j chunk.
- The `runtime_provider` retry-route adapter at
  `implementation.py:13190` (the `_dispatch_runtime_failure_
  route_payload`-tail that builds a typed `RouterFailureRouter()`
  + `RouterFailureObservation` for the dispatcher-retry path)
  — **PHASE-LEVEL — STAYS.** Tightly coupled to the dispatcher
  retry orchestrator and the `VERIFY_RETRIES` cap; not a pure
  adapter.

**SCOPE TO MOVE (2 pure helpers).** Each one is byte-for-byte
self-contained; only stdlib + `getattr` over a duck-typed input
needed; no runner/feature/store/logger/io dependencies.

1. `_route_decision_retry_budget_payload(decision, *, action,
   max_attempts=None) -> dict[str, Any]` —
   `implementation.py:757-776`. The flat-dict retry-budget
   factory.
2. `_route_decision_compat_payload(decision, *, failure_class,
   failure_type, max_attempts=None, legacy_route="",
   legacy_failure_type="") -> dict[str, Any]` —
   `implementation.py:779-820`. The full legacy-shape
   `route_decision` payload factory; calls (1) for the nested
   `retry_budget` dict.

**LEAVE BEHIND (phase-level, deferred to Slice 11j or stays).**

- `_failure_router_for_runner(runner)` — runner.services-
  coupled. STAY.
- `_typed_direct_route_payload(runner, feature, ...)` —
  runner+feature-coupled. STAY.
- `_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS` + `_route_merge_queue_
  drain_failure(runner, feature, ...)` — merge-queue drain
  surface; properly Slice 11j territory (the P3-6 fold-in
  primary owner). STAY.
- The runtime_provider retry-route adapter at `implementation.
  py:13190` — dispatcher-coupled, tightly tied to the
  `_dispatch_runtime_failure_route_payload` orchestrator +
  the `VERIFY_RETRIES` cap. STAY.

The non-pure failure-router-PORT surface stays in
`implementation.py`; the doc-11 port `FailureRouter.decide(
failure_id) -> RouteDecision` is the PRIMITIVE that the
future failure-router service refactor will adapt this PORT
into. Slice 11i is the additive extraction of the 2 pure
typed→legacy `RouteDecision`-to-dict adapter helpers.

**P3-6 FOLD-IN BOUNDARY.** Slice 11i is the CONSUMER half of
the P3-6 fold-in pair. The producer-side contract change
(extending `MergeApplyResult` with a structured `escaped_paths:
list[str]` field that lets `FailureRouter._allows_product_
repair` preserve the `contract_violation` route instead of
downgrading to `quiesce`) is OWNED by Slice 11j (`execution/
merge_queue.py`) and lands inside the SAME atomic chunk as
the merge_queue extraction. 11i is purely refactor-only;
no `MergeApplyResult` shape change, no router contract
change, no behavior change. The two pure decision-payload
adapter helpers move byte-for-byte.

**ESTABLISHED PATTERN.** Slice 11i mirrors the Slice-11a +
Slice-11b + Slice-11c + Slice-11d + Slice-11e + Slice-11f +
Slice-11g + Slice-11h pattern exactly:

1. EXTEND the pre-existing `execution/failure_router.py` with
   the 2 helpers moved byte-for-byte; preserve the entire
   pre-11i Slice-07 `FailureRouter` + `RouteDecision` +
   `FailureObservation` + `InMemoryFailureRouterPort` +
   `ROUTE_TABLE` surface.
2. Add a NEW parallel sibling shim block at `implementation.
   py` head (sibling to the Slice-11a through Slice-11h
   blocks at `:321-636`) that re-imports both moved names
   from `..execution.failure_router`.
3. Remove the 2 helper definitions in place at their pre-11i
   source locations in `implementation.py:757-820`.
4. Write a NEW targeted test file `tests/workflows/develop/
   execution/test_failure_router_extraction.py` (sibling of
   the prior extraction-test files) asserting identity-
   equivalence, `__module__` rebinding, behavioral smoke + a
   back-import guard against `failure_router.py` importing
   from `implementation.py`.

Targeted regression after the chunk:
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/`, `tests/workflows/`,
`tests/supervisor/`,
`tests/test_execution_control_store.py`,
`tests/test_public_dashboard.py`,
`tests/test_execution_control_startup.py`. Hygiene gate:
`python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py`,
`git diff --check`, JSONL parse.


## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11i COMPLETE

The ninth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) is done. MODIFIED
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/failure_router.py`
(was 1838 lines pre-11i — the canonical home for the pre-11i
Slice-07 typed-failure-router surface: `FailureRouter` +
`RouteDecision` + `FailureObservation` + `FailureRecord` +
`RouteRecord` + `RetryBudgetState` + `InMemoryFailureRouterPort` +
`FailureRouterPort` + `FailureRouterError` + `IdempotencyConflict`
+ `UnknownFailurePolicyError` + the 27-class `FailureClass`
`Literal` + `FAILURE_CLASSES` + `FailureSource` + `FailureSeverity`
+ `FailureType` + `FAILURE_TYPES` + `FAILURE_SEVERITIES` +
`FAILURE_SOURCES` + `FailureTypePolicy` + `FAILURE_TYPE_POLICIES` +
`FailureRoutePolicy` + `RouteAction` + `ROUTE_ACTIONS` +
`CLASS_RETRY_BUDGETS` + the 53-row `ROUTE_TABLE` + the budget-
exhausted-`quiesce` rewrite + the idempotency-key builder + the
`_route` policy factory; extended with **2 pure typed→legacy
`RouteDecision`-to-dict adapter helpers** at `:1826-1889` byte-
for-byte moved from `implementation.py`; now 1927 lines / +89).
The pre-11i Slice-07 surface (the FailureRouter + RouteDecision +
ROUTE_TABLE + the typed taxonomy + the policy factory) preserved
byte-for-byte; `__all__` extended with the 2 new names.

The 2 moved helpers:

- **`_route_decision_retry_budget_payload(decision, *, action,
  max_attempts=None) -> dict[str, Any]`** — the flat-dict
  `retry_budget` factory; reads a `RouteDecision`-shaped object
  via `getattr` (stdlib-only: `getattr`/`int`/`max`/`str`/`bool`)
  and emits the legacy 8-key shape (`route`, `budget_key`,
  `max_attempts`, `max_retries`, `remaining_attempts`,
  `idempotency_key`, `reservation_ordinal`, `budget_exhausted`).
  `max(0, int(...))` clamps negative `remaining`/`ordinal` to 0;
  `max_attempts` is derived from `remaining + ordinal if ordinal
  else remaining` when `None`.
- **`_route_decision_compat_payload(decision, *, failure_class,
  failure_type, max_attempts=None, legacy_route="",
  legacy_failure_type="") -> dict[str, Any]`** — the full
  legacy-shape `route_decision` payload factory; emits the
  `failure_id`/`typed_failure_id` dual aliasing, the
  `signature_hash`/`stable_signature_hash` dual aliasing, the
  `action`/`route` dual aliasing, the
  `retryable=action.startswith("retry_")` string-derived flag,
  the `operator_required=action == "operator_required"` value
  check, the deep-copied `repair_scope` dict, the materialized
  `required_evidence_ids` list, the nested `retry_budget` dict
  (delegated to the sibling helper). Optional tail keys
  `legacy_route` / `legacy_failure_type` emitted only when non-
  empty (truthiness check). Stdlib-only.

Both helpers are duck-typed adapters: they read the
`RouteDecision`-shaped input through `getattr` with safe defaults
and never call back into `runner` / `feature` / `store` / any
async surface. The duck-typed shape is what made the 11h
implementer's "STAY" misclassification recoverable — see the
**Slice 11h misclassification correction** note below.

NO import from `..phases.implementation` (compatibility arrow
points IN, never OUT — locked by a back-import guard test).

MODIFIED `implementation.py` line count 32982 → 32975 (net delta
-7 lines; the 50-liner shim block at `:637-686` is the net of
removing the two helper definitions while adding the explanatory
banner). The Slice-11a shim block at `:321-354`, the Slice-11b
shim block at `:368-382`, the Slice-11c shim block at `:383-400`,
the Slice-11d shim block at `:401-427`, the Slice-11e shim block
at `:428-463`, the Slice-11f shim block at `:464-511`, the
Slice-11g shim block at `:512-577`, and the Slice-11h shim block
at `:578-636` are UNCHANGED; a NEW parallel sibling Slice-11i
block at `:637-686` has a header banner at `:637-682` + the
re-import at `:683-686` listing both newly-moved names from
`..execution.failure_router`. NEW
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_failure_router_extraction.py`
(770 lines, **20 tests**: 2 parametrized identity + 2
parametrized `__module__` rebinding + 12 behavioral smoke tests
(max_attempts derivation from `remaining + ordinal`; explicit
override; negative `remaining` / negative `ordinal` clamp;
`budget_exhausted` flag propagation; dual-aliasing of
`failure_id`/`typed_failure_id`, `signature_hash`/`stable_
signature_hash`, `action`/`route`; the `retryable=action.
startswith("retry_")` string-derived flag for all retry-* /
quiesce / operator_required / run_product_repair actions;
operator-required action flips the flag and clears `retryable`;
deep-copy of `repair_scope`; materialized `required_evidence_ids`;
legacy_route / legacy_failure_type empty-string suppression; the
Slice 10c-2 `_typed_retry_budgets` canonical-shape persistence
contract that the runtime_failure_context evidence row depends
on) + 4 structural tests: cluster ownership pin against the 8
sibling execution modules (no sibling owns either name); 2-name
shim-block completeness probe + pre-existing Slice-07 alias
preservation; back-import guard against `failure_router.py`
importing from `implementation.py`; `__all__` export probe).

All four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions answered:

**Q1 (what behavior moved)** — 2 pure typed→legacy `RouteDecision`-
to-dict adapter helpers byte-for-byte preserved. Both are pure
`getattr`-driven duck-typed adapters; no logic / signature /
validation / return-value-shape / string-literal change. The
adapter contract surface is unchanged — every existing caller
gets identity-equivalent return values, including the 10c-2
`_typed_retry_budgets` source-of-truth read off the persisted
`runtime_failure_context` evidence row.

**Q2 (which shims still work)** — both names re-exported at
`implementation.py:683-686` resolve identity-equivalent. The
2 internal callers in `implementation.py` (`:865` typed-DAG-
direct-route producer + `:13255` dispatcher retry-route adapter)
continue resolving via the Slice-11i shim to the SAME object as
`from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.failure_router
import X`. No external importers exist (a project-wide grep
returned only the two `implementation.py` call sites and the new
extraction test file).

**Q3 (which targeted tests prove the new facade + shim)** —
`test_failure_router_extraction.py` is the facade + shim proof.
20 tests: 2 parametrized identity (`is`-equivalence between
the impl.py shim binding and the canonical
`..execution.failure_router` binding) + 2 parametrized
`__module__` rebinding (proves the definitions genuinely live
in `failure_router` so a re-aliased binding cannot silently
pass) + 12 behavioral smoke + 4 structural (cluster-ownership
pin against the 8 sibling execution modules; 2-name shim-block
completeness probe + Slice-07 alias preservation; back-import
guard; `__all__` export probe).

**Q4 (why the PR is refactor-only)** — only pure typed→legacy
`RouteDecision`-to-dict adapter primitives moved. The phase-
level failure-router PORT surface
(`_failure_router_for_runner(runner)` runner.services-coupled
router resolver at `implementation.py:729`;
`_typed_direct_route_payload(runner, feature, group_idx, retry,
route, *, ...)` runner+feature-coupled FailureObservation
orchestrator at `implementation.py:827`;
`_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS` +
`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure(runner, feature, *, ...,
item_id, failure_class, ...)` merge-queue drain surface at
`implementation.py:5049-5063` — properly Slice 11j territory
per the P3-6 fold-in plan; the runtime_provider retry-route
adapter at `implementation.py:13190` tightly coupled to
`_dispatch_runtime_failure_route_payload` and the
`VERIFY_RETRIES` cap) correctly stays in `implementation.py`.
The **P3-6 `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` producer-side
contract change is reserved for Slice 11j (NOT in 11i)** — see
the dedicated note below.

**Slice 11h misclassification correction.** The Slice 11h
finalizer's "Left behind" ledger at `implementation-journal.md:
29297-29305` erroneously bundled `_route_decision_retry_budget_
payload` and `_route_decision_compat_payload` into the
`_record_dag_direct_repair_route` / `_record_dag_checkpoint_
direct_repair_route` / `_typed_direct_route_payload` /
`_failure_router_for_runner` STAY cluster with the blanket
rationale "async runner+feature-coupled or runner.services-
coupled". That bundling is wrong for the two adapter helpers:
they are pure `getattr`-driven duck-typed adapters with ZERO
runner / feature / services / async coupling. They merely read
a `RouteDecision`-shaped object's attributes through `getattr`
with safe defaults and emit a flat dict. The Slice 11i
implementer recovered them correctly by inspecting the source
locations (`implementation.py:757-820` pre-11i) and confirming
the pure-function character through a clean compile + the new
test file's behavioral smoke coverage. This correction is
recorded here for the historical record and to prevent the
same misclassification from being inherited by a future
extraction implementer. The 11h finalizer's other STAY
classifications (the runner+feature-coupled async orchestrators
+ the path-classifier chain + the env-flag wrappers + the
`_dedupe_preserving_order`/`_safe_context_stem`-coupled
clusters) all hold as judged.

**Clean PRIMITIVE/PORT split rationale.** Slice 11i mirrors the
Slice-11d sandbox + Slice-11e dispatcher + Slice-11f gates +
Slice-11g verification + Slice-11h repair precedents exactly —
a structurally-honest separation, not work-dodging. The 2 pure
typed→legacy `RouteDecision`-to-dict adapter helpers move into
`execution/failure_router.py` alongside the existing pre-11i
Slice-07 `FailureRouter` + `RouteDecision` + `FailureObservation`
+ `InMemoryFailureRouterPort` + `ROUTE_TABLE` typed taxonomy +
the 53-row route table + the `_route` policy factory + the
budget-exhausted-`quiesce` rewrite + the idempotency-key
builder. The phase-level failure-router PORT
(`_failure_router_for_runner` runner.services-coupled resolver +
`_typed_direct_route_payload` runner+feature-coupled
FailureObservation orchestrator + the merge-queue drain surface
which is properly Slice 11j territory + the runtime_provider
retry-route adapter at `implementation.py:13190`) stays in
`implementation.py` because that port adapts phase-level state
(`runner.services["failure_router"]` /
`runner._failure_router_port` attribute fallback + the in-
memory in-process router reuse + the `WorkflowRunner` retry
budget reservation + the `Feature` instance + the typed-row
runtime_failure_context evidence persistence) to the pure
typed-failure-router primitive — which is the structurally-
correct boundary per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts"
(the named API for `execution/failure_router.py` is
`FailureRouter.decide(failure_id) -> RouteDecision` + "Typed
failure taxonomy, retry budgets, deterministic route selection,
quiesce/escalation decisions. Must not own: running repairs,
mutating git, scanning artifact bodies." — the PRIMITIVE; the
adapters that satisfy this port from `implementation.py`'s
runner+feature+services state are by definition phase-level).
The 2 typed→legacy `RouteDecision`-to-dict adapter helpers are
on the PRIMITIVE side of the boundary because they read a
`RouteDecision`-shaped duck-typed input and emit a flat dict
without consulting any runner / feature / services state.

**P3-6 contract change reserved for Slice 11j.** Slice 11i is
the CONSUMER half of the P3-6 fold-in pair, but the actual
producer-side contract change (extending `MergeApplyResult`
with a structured `escaped_paths: list[str]` field, threading
it `_ApplyFailure` → `MergeApplyResult` → the 08e-3a drain →
the router observation payload `repair_scope` / `target_paths`,
and updating `failure_router._allows_product_repair` to read
the new structured field instead of downgrading
`contract_violation` to `quiesce`) is the PRIMARY OWNER of
Slice 11j (`execution/merge_queue.py`). Both producer + consumer
updates land inside the SAME atomic 11j chunk so the
`MergeApplyResult` contract change is applied end-to-end
together. Slice 11i is purely refactor-only — no
`MergeApplyResult` shape change, no router contract change, no
behavior change. The two pure decision-payload adapter helpers
move byte-for-byte. The P3-6 fold-in is therefore correctly
SCHEDULED for 11j, not carried in 11i.

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11i independent
review returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3** on the first pass.
All six vectors PASS. PRIMITIVE/PORT SPLIT JUDGMENT:
**ACCEPTABLE** (mirrors the Slice-11d sandbox + Slice-11e
dispatcher + Slice-11f gates + Slice-11g verification +
Slice-11h repair precedents exactly).
