  the runner.services lookup. STAY.

**Slice 11 sub-slice plan (recap).** Per the Slice 11 STARTING
journal entry, the dependency-ordered plan is: 11a types
(DONE) → 11b git_service (DONE) → 11c task_contracts (DONE) →
11d sandbox (DONE) → 11e dispatcher (DONE) → 11f gates (DONE)
→ 11g verification (DONE) → 11h repair (DONE) → 11i
failure_router (DONE) → **11j merge_queue (P3-6 fold-in
PRIMARY OWNER)** → 11k regroup_overlay → 11l post_dag_gates →
11m post_test_guard → 11n slice-end SIX-VECTOR review +
formal Slice 11 ACCEPTANCE. **Next: 11j merge_queue (P3-6
producer fold-in)** (per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md` § "Proposed Module Boundaries" —
`execution/merge_queue.py` is the next surface after
`execution/failure_router.py`; much of the canonical merge-
queue surface already lives there from Slice 08, so 11j is a
mix of extracting any remaining `_merge_queue_*` /
`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` helpers from
`implementation.py` AND the legitimate P3-6 contract reopening
of `MergeApplyResult` to add `escaped_paths` + the threading
through the 08e-3a drain into the router observation payload
+ the matching consumer-side
`failure_router._allows_product_repair` update). The 11j
implementer must run extra regression on the merge-queue and
failure-router test surfaces to ensure the contract change
doesn't break Slice 08 / Slice 09 acceptance criteria.

Slice 11i is closed. Slice 11j (extract
`execution/merge_queue.py` + P3-6 producer fold-in) is next.


## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11j STARTING

The tenth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the durable-merge-queue surface — is
beginning. Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/merge_queue.py`: "`MergeQueueService.enqueue`, `claim`,
`apply_patch`, `run_post_apply_gates`, `commit_and_checkpoint`,
`recover_expired`. Canonical patch apply, queue leases, commit,
no-dirty proof, checkpoint projection. Must not own: runtime
dispatch, sandbox patch generation, task completion authority from
`dag-task:*`." The pre-11j surface (the Slice-08 `MergeQueue` +
`GroupMergeCoordinator` + `MergeApplyResult` + the `_ApplyFailure`
internal signal + `LeaseToken` + `RepoApplyInput` / `RepoApplyOutcome`
+ `MergeGateResult` / `MergeCommitResult` + the `MergeQueueReadiness`
verifier + `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` + `_checkpoint_coverage_
digest`) is already in `execution/merge_queue.py` (1247 lines
pre-11j) — that is the canonical home for the durable merge queue.

**SLICE 11J IS THE MOST COMPLEX SUB-SLICE IN SLICE 11.** It is BOTH:

1. **PART A — pure refactor extraction (like 11a–11i)** — find any
   merge-queue helpers in `implementation.py` that are still pure
   and not yet in `execution/merge_queue.py`. Most of the
   merge-queue surface already lives there from Slice 08, so Part
   A may be small or empty.
2. **PART B — the P3-6 PRODUCER-side fold-in (THE CRITICAL CHANGE)**
   — a GENUINE CONTRACT CHANGE that reopens the accepted Slice-08
   `MergeApplyResult` contract. This is legitimate inside Slice 11j
   because the contract change is what fixes P3-6 (the
   durable-merge-queue drain `contract_violation` → `quiesce` route
   downgrade).

**P3-6 ROOT CAUSE (recap from STATUS.md / refactor-map).** When the
merge-queue drain encounters a `contract_violation` failure
(`apply_candidate` raises `_ApplyFailure("contract_violation", ...)`
when patch paths or applied paths escape the lane contracts), the
typed failure is routed through `failure_router._allows_product_
repair`. But the router doesn't have structured information about
WHICH paths escaped the contract — the merge-queue drain's
`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` observation payload only carries
the unstructured legacy free-text `detail` field. Without structured
`target_paths` (which `_repair_scope` reads from the payload) and
`target_contract_ids` (likewise) in the observation payload,
`_allows_product_repair` conservatively returns `False` and the
caller downgrades the route to `quiesce` (operator intervention),
instead of attempting product repair where appropriate.

**P3-6 FIX (PART B, producer+consumer atomic chunk).**

1. **Extend the Slice-08 `MergeApplyResult` contract** with a new
   field `escaped_paths: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)`.
   ADDITIVE — default empty list so every existing construction
   still works. Slice-08 acceptance criteria must still hold.
2. **Thread the structured field through the producer chain:**
   - Extend `_ApplyFailure` (the internal failure signal in the
     merge-queue worker at `merge_queue.py:135`) to accept an
     optional `escaped_paths: list[str] | None = None` parameter.
   - In `apply_candidate` (`merge_queue.py:181`), populate
     `escaped_paths` when raising `_ApplyFailure` for the two
     `contract_violation` paths (the patch-path-outside-contract
     escape at `:287-292` and the applied-path-set escape at
     `:320-325`); both already compute the sorted `outside` /
     `escaped` lists for the detail string — pass them through to
     the structured field too.
   - In `_fail` (`merge_queue.py:648`), accept the optional
     `escaped_paths` from the `_ApplyFailure` and propagate it to
     the returned `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths`.
3. **Thread the structured field through the drain payload builder:**
   - Extend `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure(runner, feature, *,
     ...)` (`implementation.py:5056`) with a new keyword-only
     `escaped_paths: list[str] | None = None` parameter; when
     provided, populate `target_paths` (and conditionally
     `target_contract_ids` from the queue item's `contract_ids`)
     in the observation payload. The escaped paths surface to
     `_repair_scope` via the existing `payload` → `target_paths`
     reader.
   - Extend `_drain_one_merge_queue_lane`'s inner `_fail_result`
     helper (`implementation.py:5265`) to extract
     `apply_result.escaped_paths` (when populated) and forward to
     `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure`. Likewise pass the queue
     item's `contract_ids` so the router observation can carry
     `target_contract_ids`.
4. **Update the consumer side** —
   `failure_router._allows_product_repair`
   (`failure_router.py:1739`) — currently reads
   `repair_scope.get("target_paths")` and
   `repair_scope.get("target_contract_ids")`. The repair_scope is
   built from the observation payload by `_repair_scope`
   (`:1670-1737`) which already reads `target_paths` /
   `target_contract_ids` from the payload. So once the producer
   side populates these, the consumer logic already does the right
   thing — **no consumer-side LOGIC change required**. The
   consumer just needs the structured signal it currently lacks.
   **The fix is therefore ENTIRELY producer-side**, BUT the
   producer-side change MUST land in the same atomic chunk as a
   test that exercises the consumer end-to-end (so the contract
   is locked end-to-end).
5. **Both producer and consumer changes land in the SAME atomic
   chunk** — and the P3-6 fix test exercises the full end-to-end
   chain: `_ApplyFailure(contract_violation, escaped_paths=[...])`
   → `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` → `_route_merge_queue_drain_
   failure` payload → `FailureRouter.decide` →
   `_allows_product_repair` returns `True` →
   `RouteDecision.action == "run_product_repair"` (not the pre-11j
   `quiesce` downgrade).

**INVENTORY (merge-queue surface in `implementation.py`).**

Pre-11j `implementation.py` `_merge_queue_*` / `_drain_*` /
`_recover_*` / `_route_merge_queue_*` / `_checkpoint_durable_*`
cluster:

- `_MERGE_QUEUE_DRAIN_FAILURE_PAIRS` (`:5042`) + `_route_merge_
  queue_drain_failure(runner, feature, *, ..., item_id,
  failure_class, ...)` (`:5056`) — **PHASE-LEVEL — STAYS in
  `implementation.py`.** Runner+feature-coupled (uses
  `_failure_router_for_runner(runner)` + `feature.id` +
  `RouterFailureObservation`). The drain-failure router adapter
  Part B EXTENDS this with the new `escaped_paths` /
  `target_contract_ids` keyword arguments to enable the P3-6 fix.
- `_merge_queue_post_apply_gate_decision(item)` (`:5151`) —
  **PHASE-LEVEL — STAYS.** Calls `merge_queue_git_service.run_git`
  + reads `item.repo_targets`; returns a `MergeQueueGateDecision`.
  Async + I/O-coupled. Could be argued to belong in
  `merge_queue.py`, but it's properly part of the drain
  orchestration (it's the inject-as-gate-runner closure
  `build_gate_runner` wraps); leaving it in `implementation.py`
  preserves the drain orchestration's locality.
- `_drain_one_merge_queue_lane(runner, feature, worker, item, *,
  dag_sha256, stage)` (`:5223`) — **PHASE-LEVEL — STAYS.**
  Runner+feature+worker-coupled async orchestrator. Part B
  EXTENDS the inner `_fail_result` helper to forward the new
  `escaped_paths`.
- `_recover_one_crashed_merge_queue_lane(...)` (`:5393`) +
  `_recover_one_crashed_checkpointing_group(...)` (`:5567`) —
  **PHASE-LEVEL — STAY.** Runner+feature+worker-coupled async
  recovery orchestrators.
- `_drain_durable_merge_queue_for_feature(...)` (`:5797`),
  `_checkpoint_durable_merge_queue_group(...)` (`:6013`),
  `_resume_recover_durable_merge_queue_group(...)` (`:6315`) —
  **PHASE-LEVEL — STAY.** Runner+feature+worker-coupled async
  drain orchestrators.
- `_merge_queue_connection(store)` (`:4558`) — **PHASE-LEVEL —
  STAYS.** Async asyncpg connection context manager; trivial but
  store-coupled.
- `_resolve_merge_queue_lane_inputs(...)` (`:4606`),
  `_enqueue_durable_merge_queue_for_results(...)` (`:4790`) —
  **PHASE-LEVEL — STAY.** Runner+feature-coupled enqueue
  orchestrators.
- `_PENDING_DURABLE_MERGE_QUEUE_NOTE` (`:4495`),
  `_result_requires_durable_merge_queue(result)` (`:4505`),
  `_pending_merge_queue_marker_key(task_id)` (`:4509`),
  `_durable_merge_queue_blocker_for_results(results, *,
  enhancement=False)` (`:4513`),
  `_parse_patch_evidence_ids_from_notes(notes)` (`:4530`) — these
  are pure-ish but tightly coupled to `ImplementationResult` (a
  Pydantic model in `models/outputs.py`) and
  `_workflow_blocker_text` (impl.py-local). **Pure helper
  classification follows the Slice-11h precedent**: helpers
  coupled to impl.py-local `_workflow_blocker_text` or
  `ImplementationResult` consumers correctly stay in
  `implementation.py`. STAY.

**PART A SCOPE — ZERO PURE HELPERS TO MOVE.** Every merge-queue
helper in `implementation.py` is either runner/feature/worker/
store-coupled (the bulk of the drain + recovery orchestration), or
impl.py-local-`_workflow_blocker_text`-coupled (the
`ImplementationResult`-readers). Slice 08 already extracted the
canonical merge-queue PRIMITIVE surface (the `MergeQueue` worker +
`MergeApplyResult` + `GroupMergeCoordinator` + `_ApplyFailure` +
the `MergeQueueReadiness` verifier + `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body`
+ `_checkpoint_coverage_digest` + `_path_allowed`). There is no
further pure-helper extraction surface for Slice 11j.

**PART B SCOPE — THE GENUINE CONTRACT CHANGE.**

The Slice 11j contract change is intentionally narrow and tightly
scoped:

1. **`MergeApplyResult` gains `escaped_paths: list[str] =
   Field(default_factory=list)`** — ADDITIVE; every existing
   construction in `merge_queue.py` works unchanged.
2. **`_ApplyFailure.__init__` gains an optional `escaped_paths:
   list[str] | None = None` parameter** — ADDITIVE; the two
   `contract_violation` raise sites pass their already-computed
   `sorted(outside)` / `sorted(escaped)` lists; every other
   `_ApplyFailure` raise (the 5 other `merge_conflict` / `stale_
   projection` / `checkpoint_contradiction` raises) leaves it
   `None`. `_fail` reads `getattr(failure, "escaped_paths", None)`
   and forwards.
3. **`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` gains two keyword-only
   parameters: `escaped_paths: list[str] | None = None` and
   `target_contract_ids: list[int] | None = None`** — ADDITIVE;
   when non-empty, surface them into the observation payload as
   `target_paths` and `target_contract_ids` respectively. The
   `_repair_scope` reader (`failure_router.py:1670-1737`) already
   harvests these keys from the payload — no consumer-side
   logic change required.
4. **`_drain_one_merge_queue_lane`'s `_fail_result` extracts
   `apply_result.escaped_paths` and the queue item's
   `contract_ids`** and forwards both to
   `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure`.
5. **No change to `failure_router._allows_product_repair` logic**
   — the consumer already does the right thing once the producer
   provides structured `target_paths` + `target_contract_ids`.

**Why no consumer-side logic change is needed.** Re-read
`failure_router.py:1739-1777`:

```python
def _allows_product_repair(self, record, route, repair_scope):
    if route.action != "run_product_repair":
        return True
    if record.observation.failure_class == "product_defect":
        return bool(... and repair_scope.get("target_paths"))
    if (
        record.observation.failure_class != "contract_violation"
        or record.observation.failure_type not in
        _SCOPED_CONTRACT_PRODUCT_TYPES
    ):
        return False
    return bool(
        repair_scope.get("target_paths")
        and (
            repair_scope.get("target_contract_ids")
            or self._authorized_direct_source_verdict(...)
        )
    )
```

For the merge-queue drain `contract_violation`:
- `failure_class == "contract_violation"` ✓
- `failure_type == "outside_allowed_paths"` (mapped at
  `implementation.py:5045`) ✓ — IS in
  `_SCOPED_CONTRACT_PRODUCT_TYPES`
- needs `target_paths` (escaped_paths from MergeApplyResult) AND
  `target_contract_ids` (from the queue item) — once the producer
  supplies these in the payload, `_allows_product_repair` returns
  `True`.

The bug today is that the producer never supplies `target_paths`
or `target_contract_ids`, so `_allows_product_repair` returns
`False` and the route degrades to `quiesce`. The Slice 11j
producer-side fold-in supplies both, completing the end-to-end
chain.

**ESTABLISHED PATTERN.** Slice 11j mirrors the Slice-11a +
Slice-11b + Slice-11c + Slice-11d + Slice-11e + Slice-11f +
Slice-11g + Slice-11h + Slice-11i pattern with one key difference:
**Part A is empty (no pure helpers to extract)**, but **Part B is
a GENUINE CONTRACT CHANGE** — not just refactor. The shim block
pattern doesn't apply because nothing moves (Part A is empty); the
journal AFTER entry will document the contract change and the
producer-side threading. A new targeted test file
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_extraction.py`
covers (a) the additive `escaped_paths` contract on
`MergeApplyResult`, (b) the producer-side threading from
`_ApplyFailure` through `apply_candidate` and `_fail`, and (c) the
P3-6 fix scenario end-to-end (a `contract_violation` failure with
populated `escaped_paths` + `contract_ids` → router decides
`run_product_repair`, NOT `quiesce`).

**Targeted regression after the chunk:**
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue*.py` (the
  Slice 08 baseline — must still pass byte-for-byte).
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_failure_router*.py`
  (the consumer side — must still pass byte-for-byte).
- `tests/workflows/develop/execution/`.
- `tests/workflows/`.
- `tests/supervisor/`.
- `tests/test_execution_control_store.py +
  tests/test_public_dashboard.py +
  tests/test_execution_control_startup.py`.
- Hygiene gate: `python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2
  dashboard.py`, `git diff --check`, JSONL parse.


## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11j COMPLETE + P3-6 RESOLVED

The tenth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) is done. **Slice 11j is the most complex sub-slice
in Slice 11**: PART A (pure refactor extraction) is intentionally
EMPTY — Slice 08 already extracted the canonical merge-queue PRIMITIVE
surface (the `MergeQueue` worker + `MergeApplyResult` +
`GroupMergeCoordinator` + `_ApplyFailure` + `LeaseToken` +
`RepoApplyInput`/`Outcome` + `MergeGateResult`/`MergeCommitResult` +
`MergeQueueReadiness` + `_reconstruct_checkpoint_body` +
`_checkpoint_coverage_digest` + `_path_allowed`), and every remaining
merge-queue helper in `implementation.py` is phase-level
(runner+feature+worker+store-coupled or
`_workflow_blocker_text`-coupled per the Slice-11h precedent). **PART
B is the GENUINE CONTRACT CHANGE** — the P3-6 producer-side fold-in
that resolves the durable-merge-queue drain `contract_violation` →
`quiesce` route downgrade. This is **NOT a pure refactor**; it is a
legitimately-reopened contract on the accepted Slice-08
`MergeApplyResult` and is intentionally scoped to land inside the
Slice 11 merge-execution refactor per
`docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md`. Both producer and
consumer changes land in the SAME atomic 11j chunk so the contract
is applied end-to-end together.

**GENUINE CONTRACT CHANGE — legitimacy + additivity.** The Slice 11j
contract change reopens the accepted Slice-08 `MergeApplyResult`
contract. The reopening is legitimate inside Slice 11 because
`docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md` § "Proposed Module
Boundaries" assigns the durable-merge-queue surface to
`execution/merge_queue.py` and the P3-6 fold-in plan recorded in
Slice 09 / Slice 10 / Slice 11i STARTING explicitly slates the
producer-side `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` extension for the
Slice 11j merge-execution chunk. The contract change is **PURELY
ADDITIVE**: `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths: list[str] =
Field(default_factory=list)` — every existing construction in
`merge_queue.py` (and in the Slice-08 test fakes / fixtures) works
byte-for-byte unchanged because the field defaults to an empty list
via the Pydantic default-factory. The Slice 08 acceptance baseline
**153 merge-queue tests** pass byte-for-byte unchanged (proof that
the contract reopening did not break the Slice 08 acceptance
criteria).

**SOURCE CHANGES — producer side.** MODIFIED
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/merge_queue.py` (1247
lines pre-11j → 1312 lines / +65):

- **`MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths: list[str] =
  Field(default_factory=list)`** at `:89` — the ADDITIVE field that
  carries the sorted list of contract-escaping paths from the
  apply-step `contract_violation` raise sites. Default-factory empty
  list preserves byte-for-byte construction for every other
  failure-class path. The docstring at `:81-88` documents the
  cross-module contract — the field surfaces to the drain payload
  as `target_paths` so `FailureRouter._allows_product_repair` no
  longer downgrades the `contract_violation` route to `quiesce` when
  the escapes are classifiable as product-repair targets.
- **`_ApplyFailure.__init__(failure_class, detail, escaped_paths:
  list[str] | None = None)`** at `:165-174` — the internal failure
  signal in the merge-queue worker gains the optional
  `escaped_paths` keyword. ADDITIVE: every non-contract-violation
  raise (the 5 other `merge_conflict` / `stale_projection` /
  `checkpoint_contradiction` raise sites) leaves it `None`; the
  default-factory contract on `MergeApplyResult` yields an empty
  list on the consumer side.
- **Two `contract_violation` raise sites in `apply_candidate`
  populated**: at `:330` (`escaped_paths=sorted(outside)` — the
  patch-path-outside-contract escape) and at `:369`
  (`escaped_paths=sorted(escaped)` — the applied-path-set escape).
  Both already computed the sorted lists for the legacy `detail`
  free-text; passing the same lists through to the structured field
  is byte-for-byte additive. The Slice 11j P3-6 fold-in comments at
  `:321-325` and `:360-364` explicitly document the producer →
  consumer thread for both raise sites.
- **Catch-and-forward at `:419-432`** — the `_ApplyFailure` catch
  block forwards `failure.escaped_paths` to `_fail` via the new
  `escaped_paths=failure.escaped_paths` keyword argument.
- **`_fail(item, token, failure_class, detail, *, escaped_paths:
  list[str] | None = None)`** at `:700-752` — accepts the optional
  `escaped_paths` and forwards to `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths`
  via `list(escaped_paths) if escaped_paths else []` at `:751` (the
  defensive copy + truthiness check yields an empty list on
  `None`/empty input, matching the default-factory contract).

**SOURCE CHANGES — drain payload + router observation side.**
MODIFIED
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`
(32975 lines pre-11j → 33046 lines / +71):

- **`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure(runner, feature, *, ...,
  escaped_paths: list[str] | None = None, target_contract_ids:
  list[int] | None = None)`** at `:5056-5160` — the drain-failure
  router adapter gains two keyword-only parameters. When both
  signals are present + non-empty, the observation `payload` carries
  `target_paths = sorted({str(p) for p in escaped_paths if p})`
  (deduplicated, string-coerced, sorted) and `target_contract_ids =
  sorted({int(cid) for cid in target_contract_ids if cid is not
  None})` (deduplicated, integer-coerced, sorted). When EITHER is
  empty/missing, the corresponding payload key is OMITTED entirely
  (truthiness check at `:5141-5146`) — the fail-closed
  `quiesce`-downgrade safety net is preserved when the producer
  cannot supply the structured signal.
- **`_drain_one_merge_queue_lane`'s `_fail_result` closure** at
  `:5303-5391` — snapshots `initial_contract_ids = list(item.
  contract_ids)` at `:5304` so the inner closure captures the
  authoritative contract id list at lane-start (decoupled from any
  later worker-side row reload). Accepts the keyword-only
  `escaped_paths: list[str] | None = None` parameter at `:5310` and
  gates `contract_ids_for_route` on **both** `escaped_paths AND
  initial_contract_ids` being truthy at `:5324-5326` (fail-closed:
  if either signal is empty, `contract_ids_for_route = None` and
  the router payload omits both keys, downgrading to `quiesce`).
  Forwards both signals to `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` at
  `:5327-5338`.
- **The apply-step caller** at `:5372-5386` — when
  `not apply_result.applied`, extracts
  `apply_escaped_paths = list(getattr(apply_result, "escaped_paths",
  []) or [])` (defensive against pre-11j fakes that may lack the
  field) at `:5379-5381` and forwards
  `escaped_paths=apply_escaped_paths or None` to `_fail_result` at
  `:5385`. Non-contract-violation failures carry an empty list (the
  default) and the fail-closed `quiesce` path is preserved.

**CONSUMER SIDE — NO LOGIC CHANGE REQUIRED.** The Slice-07
`FailureRouter._repair_scope` reader at
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/failure_router.py:
1670-1737` **already** harvests `payload["target_paths"]` +
`payload["target_contract_ids"]` from the observation payload into
the typed `RouteDecision.repair_scope` mapping. The Slice-07
`_allows_product_repair` consumer at `:1739-1777` **already** gates
the `(contract_violation, outside_allowed_paths)` route on BOTH
`repair_scope.get("target_paths")` AND
`repair_scope.get("target_contract_ids")` being truthy when
`source == "merge_queue"`. **The bug was purely producer-side
missing data, not consumer-side logic.** Once the producer surfaces
the structured signal, the consumer's existing gate correctly
returns `True` and the route stays `run_product_repair` instead of
being downgraded to `quiesce`. This is the cleanest possible fix
shape for P3-6: zero consumer-side risk; the Slice-07 typed router
contract is byte-for-byte unchanged.

**THREE INDEPENDENT FAIL-CLOSED SAFETY NET GATES.** The producer
side preserves the conservative `quiesce` fallback when either
signal is missing through three structurally-independent gates:

1. **`_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` truthiness check** at
   `implementation.py:5141-5146` — `if escaped_paths:` and
   `if target_contract_ids:` independently gate the payload-key
   insertion. An empty list, `None`, or any non-truthy value
   omits the key entirely, so the consumer-side `_repair_scope`
   reader returns `None` for the absent key and
   `_allows_product_repair` returns `False`.
2. **`_fail_result` `contract_ids_for_route` gate** at
   `implementation.py:5324-5326` — `contract_ids_for_route =
   initial_contract_ids if (escaped_paths and initial_contract_ids)
   else None`. The `AND` ensures BOTH signals must be truthy for
   the contract-id list to be forwarded; either signal missing or
   empty sets the variable to `None` and the router payload omits
   both `target_paths` and `target_contract_ids` together.
3. **`_allows_product_repair` consumer check** at
   `failure_router.py:1739-1777` (Slice-07, unchanged) — requires
   BOTH `target_paths` AND `target_contract_ids` (modulo the
   authorized-direct-source-verdict alternative which is not
   relevant for `source="merge_queue"`) to return `True`. An
   absent or empty list on either key returns `False` and the
   route downgrades to `quiesce`.

The three gates are mutually reinforcing: even if a future producer
change accidentally surfaces one signal but not the other, gate 2
catches the asymmetry; even if gate 2 is bypassed, gate 1's
independent truthiness check protects the payload; even if gate 1
is bypassed, the Slice-07 consumer-side gate 3 refuses the route.

**TEST CHANGES — ADDITIVE ONLY.** NEW
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_extraction.py`
(1014 lines, **20 tests**):

- **3 contract additivity unit tests** —
  `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` default empty list construction;
  explicit list construction; sorted/deduplicated list preservation.
- **3 `_ApplyFailure.escaped_paths` plumbing tests** — default
  `None`; positional + keyword acceptance; non-contract-violation
  raise leaves the field `None`.
- **3 `apply_candidate` producer integration tests** — the two
  `contract_violation` raise sites populate
  `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` with `sorted(outside)` /
  `sorted(escaped)`; non-contract-violation raises leave the field
  the default empty list.
- **3 `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` signature tests** —
  observation payload carries `target_paths` +
  `target_contract_ids` when both signals are non-empty; omits both
  keys when either signal is empty; the existing pre-11j signature
  (no `escaped_paths` / `target_contract_ids`) still works
  byte-for-byte.
- **3 P3-6 router-layer tests** — the fix succeeds
  (`_allows_product_repair` returns `True` when both signals are
  present + non-empty); 2 fail-closed-when-either-signal-missing
  tests (one for `escaped_paths` missing, one for
  `target_contract_ids` missing).
- **2 end-to-end drain tests** —
  `test_drain_contract_violation_routes_to_run_product_repair_when_paths_escape`
  locks the actual P3-6 fix end-to-end (a `contract_violation`
  failure with populated `escaped_paths` + `contract_ids` →
  `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` payload → `FailureRouter.decide`
  → `RouteDecision.action == "run_product_repair"`, not the pre-11j
  `quiesce` downgrade); `test_drain_merge_conflict_still_quiesces_
  unchanged` pins that the scope is contract-violation-only (a
  `merge_conflict` failure with empty `escaped_paths` still
  downgrades to `quiesce` exactly as before).
- **3 structural tests** — `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` schema
  shape (`list[str]` with `default_factory=list`); `_ApplyFailure.
  escaped_paths` attribute exists and is `list[str] | None`;
  back-import guard against `merge_queue.py` importing from
  `implementation.py`.

All four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions answered:

**Q1 (what behavior moved)** — NONE (Part A is empty; Slice 08
already extracted the canonical merge-queue PRIMITIVE surface).
The Slice 11j change is a **GENUINE CONTRACT CHANGE** (Part B): the
ADDITIVE `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` field + the producer-side
thread `_ApplyFailure` → `MergeApplyResult` → the 08e-3a drain →
the router observation payload `target_paths` + `target_contract_
ids`. NOT pure refactor.

**Q2 (which shims still work)** — every existing
`MergeApplyResult(...)` construction works byte-for-byte unchanged
(the `default_factory=list` on the new field preserves existing
positional + keyword construction); every existing
`_ApplyFailure(failure_class, detail)` construction works
byte-for-byte unchanged (the new `escaped_paths` parameter defaults
to `None`); every existing `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure(...)`
call site works byte-for-byte unchanged (the new keyword-only
parameters default to `None` and the payload keys are omitted when
not supplied). No shim block needed in `implementation.py`
because nothing MOVED — Part A is empty.

**Q3 (which targeted tests prove the new facade + shim)** —
`test_merge_queue_extraction.py` with 3 contract additivity + 3
`_ApplyFailure` plumbing + 3 `apply_candidate` producer integration
+ 3 `_route_merge_queue_drain_failure` signature + 3 P3-6
router-layer + 2 end-to-end drain + 3 structural tests (20 total).
The Slice 08 baseline `test_merge_queue_*.py` files pass
**153 passed** byte-for-byte unchanged.

**Q4 (why the PR is refactor-only)** — the PR is **NOT pure
refactor**; Part B is a GENUINE CONTRACT CHANGE that reopens the
accepted Slice-08 `MergeApplyResult` contract. The reopening is
LEGITIMATE inside the Slice-11 merge-execution refactor per
`docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md` § "Proposed
Module Boundaries" (assigning the durable-merge-queue surface to
`execution/merge_queue.py`) and the P3-6 fold-in plan recorded in
the Slice 09 / Slice 10 / Slice 11 STARTING entries. The contract
change is purely ADDITIVE (`Field(default_factory=list)`); the
consumer side requires zero logic change (the bug was producer-side
missing data); three independent fail-closed safety net gates
preserve the conservative `quiesce` fallback when either signal
is missing. Both producer + consumer changes land inside the SAME
atomic 11j chunk so the `MergeApplyResult` contract change is
applied end-to-end together; no half-landed contract change; no
intermediate state where the producer emits `escaped_paths` but
the consumer ignores it (or vice versa).

**P3-6 RESOLVED.** P3-6 (originally raised in Slice 09 and
re-deferred to Slice 11 with PRIMARY OWNER Slice 11j) is now
**RESOLVED** by the producer-side fold-in landing here. The
durable-merge-queue drain `contract_violation` route is no longer
downgraded to `quiesce` when the apply-step `_ApplyFailure` raise
sites populate structured `escaped_paths` and the queue item
carries `contract_ids` — the consumer-side `_allows_product_repair`
correctly returns `True` and the route stays `run_product_repair`.
The end-to-end test
`test_drain_contract_violation_routes_to_run_product_repair_when_paths_escape`
locks this behavior; the sibling
`test_drain_merge_conflict_still_quiesces_unchanged` pins that the
scope is contract-violation-only (a `merge_conflict` failure with
empty `escaped_paths` still downgrades to `quiesce`).

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11j independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3**. The P3-6 fix was judged
**GENUINE** (the structured `escaped_paths` signal solves the
exact failure mode P3-6 documented; the three fail-closed safety
net gates preserve the conservative `quiesce` fallback). The
Slice-08 contract reopening was judged **ACCEPTABLE** (purely
additive; default-factory empty list; **153 baseline merge-queue
tests pass byte-for-byte unchanged**). The PRIMITIVE/PORT split
JUDGMENT: **ACCEPTABLE** — Part A is empty because Slice 08
already extracted the canonical merge-queue PRIMITIVE surface; the
remaining phase-level merge-queue helpers (drain orchestrators,
recovery orchestrators, enqueue orchestrators, `_workflow_blocker_
text`-coupled `ImplementationResult`-readers) correctly stay in
`implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11k STARTING

The eleventh extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the regroup-overlay validator surface — is
beginning. Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/regroup_overlay.py`: "`RegroupOverlayService.validate`,
`activate`, `resolve_active`, `rollback_before_first_wave`. Derived
DAG overlay validation, active marker resolution, activation and
rollback records. Must not own: scheduler speed decisions that
bypass dependencies/write sets, queue commits."

The pre-11k surface in `execution/regroup_overlay.py` (418 lines,
created by Slice 09a) is the canonical home for the typed overlay
schema + models + deterministic identifier derivations:
`OverlayStatus`, `RegroupActiveMarkerStatus`, `OverlayBarrierSource`,
`SchedulerGroupMetricState`, `SchedulerFeedbackDataQuality`,
`SchedulerFeedbackConfidence`, `OverlayCompatibilityKeys`,
`OverlayBarrier`, `OverlayTaskSpeedMetadata`,
`RegroupActivationContract`, `RegroupRollbackPlan`,
`RegroupActiveMarker`, `RegroupOverlay`, `SchedulerGroupMetric`,
`SchedulerFeedback`, `derive_overlay_id`, `derive_overlay_slug`,
`derive_metric_id`. The Slice 09 family additionally produced
`execution/regroup_overlay_validation.py` (the deterministic 13-step
`validate_overlay`), `execution/regroup_overlay_activation.py`
(atomic activate / before-first-wave rollback), and
`execution/regroup_overlay_resolver.py` (the fail-closed
`RegroupOverlayResolver`). Slice 11k EXTENDS the canonical
`regroup_overlay.py` module with the remaining PURE
`DerivedDAGArtifact`-validator cluster currently in
`implementation.py` that depends only on stdlib + the
`models.outputs` types (`DerivedDAGArtifact`, `ImplementationDAG`,
`ImplementationTask`) + the pre-existing Slice-11h
`execution/repair.py` `_is_derived_dag_artifact_key` predicate +
a lazy import of `..dag_regroup`'s `_barrier_for_task` /
`semantic_lane_for_task` (already module-level in
`workflows/develop/dag_regroup.py`).

**INVENTORY (regroup-overlay surface in `implementation.py`).**

The regroup-overlay-domain helpers fall into two clusters:
phase-level resolver/observation orchestrators (STAY) and the pure
DerivedDAGArtifact-validator cluster (MOVE).

PHASE-LEVEL — STAY (runner/feature/store/async/I/O coupled):

- `_resolve_active_regroup_before_group_dispatch(runner, feature,
  dag, *, group_idx)` — `implementation.py:15561`. Async; takes
  `runner: WorkflowRunner` + `feature: Feature`; calls the typed
  resolver and the legacy marker path. **STAYS.**
- `_resolve_active_regroup_typed_overlay(runner, feature, dag, *,
  group_idx)` — `implementation.py:15620`. Async; uses
  `_execution_control_store_for_runner` +
  `_merge_queue_connection` + `RegroupOverlayStore` +
  `RegroupOverlayResolver` + `_write_typed_regroup_observation` +
  `_typed_regroup_active_overlay_probe`. **STAYS.**
- `_write_typed_regroup_observation(runner, feature, feature_id,
  group_idx, observation)` — `implementation.py:15730`. Async;
  uses `runner.artifacts.put` and `_log_feature_event`. **STAYS.**
- `_typed_regroup_active_overlay_probe(store, feature_id)` —
  `implementation.py:15777`. Async; opens a typed
  `_merge_queue_connection` and runs `SELECT 1 FROM
  execution_regroup_overlays WHERE feature_id = $1 AND status =
  'active' LIMIT 1`. Store-coupled. **STAYS.**
- `_typed_regroup_active_overlay_offset(runner, feature_id)` —
  `implementation.py:15807`. Async; uses
  `_execution_control_store_for_runner` +
  `_merge_queue_connection` + a typed-overlay-table SELECT.
  Runner+store-coupled. **STAYS.**
- `_resolve_active_regroup_legacy_marker(runner, feature, dag, *,
  group_idx)` — `implementation.py:15858`. Async; reads
  `DAG_REGROUP_ACTIVE_KEY` via `runner.artifacts`, calls
  `_dag_artifact_record_for_key` + `_log_feature_event` +
  `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` (the moved one).
  **STAYS.**
- `_load_regroup_validation_context(runner, feature, parsed)` —
  `implementation.py:22176`. Async; uses
  `_dag_artifact_record_for_key`. **STAYS.**

PURE — MOVE (zero runner/feature/store/async/I/O coupling):

1. `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update(artifact_key, content, *,
   base_dag=None, base_dag_artifact_id=None, base_dag_sha256=None,
   boundary_checkpoint_exists=False,
   require_regroup_context=False) -> tuple[DerivedDAGArtifact |
   None, str, list[dict[str, Any]]]` —
   `implementation.py:23920-24058`. The synchronous parser +
   validator entry-point. Uses `_is_derived_dag_artifact_key`
   (already imported from `..execution.repair`),
   `DerivedDAGArtifact.model_validate_json`, and the moved
   sibling `_derived_dag_write_set_conflicts` +
   `_validate_regroup_against_base_dag`. Stdlib + Pydantic
   only. Called from 3 sites in `implementation.py` (legacy
   marker path at `:16038`; the artifact-update validator at
   `:22264`+`:22282`), 1 site in `..dag_regroup` (late import
   at `dag_regroup.py:558`), and 12+ test sites via
   `monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module,
   "_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update", …)` /
   `implementation_module._validate_derived_dag_artifact_update(…)`.
2. `_validate_regroup_against_base_dag(parsed, *, base_dag,
   base_dag_artifact_id, base_dag_sha256,
   boundary_checkpoint_exists)` —
   `implementation.py:24061-24341`. The pure regroup-vs-base
   contract validator. Uses
   `_regroup_task_definition_for_compare`,
   `_derived_dag_task_write_sets`,
   `_derived_dag_write_set_conflicts`,
   `_regroup_hard_barrier_by_task` (all moved siblings).
   Stdlib + Pydantic only.
3. `_regroup_task_definition_for_compare(task, *,
   remaining_task_ids)` — `implementation.py:24344-24352`. Pure
   `ImplementationTask` `model_dump` projection minus the
   `dependencies` field. Stdlib only.
4. `_regroup_hard_barrier_by_task(parsed, *,
   task_definitions_by_id=None)` —
   `implementation.py:24355-24393`. Pure barrier-by-task index
   builder. Carries a function-body lazy
   `from ..dag_regroup import _barrier_for_task,
   semantic_lane_for_task` (already module-level at
   `workflows/develop/dag_regroup.py:181, :204`); the relative
   import points "across" within the same `workflows/develop/`
   package and survives the move byte-for-byte (the destination
   `workflows/develop/execution/regroup_overlay.py` has a sibling
   `..dag_regroup`). Otherwise stdlib only.
5. `_derived_dag_write_set_conflicts(parsed, *,
   task_write_sets=None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]` —
   `implementation.py:24396-24417`. Pure pairwise overlap
   builder. Uses the moved sibling
   `_derived_dag_task_write_sets`. Stdlib only.
6. `_derived_dag_task_write_sets(parsed, *,
   task_definitions_by_id=None) -> dict[str, set[str]]` —
   `implementation.py:24420-24440`. Pure write-set aggregator.
   Uses the moved sibling `_regroup_task_declared_write_paths`.
   Stdlib only.
7. `_regroup_task_declared_write_paths(task) -> set[str]` —
   `implementation.py:24443-24461`. Pure declared-write-paths
   reader off an `ImplementationTask`. Stdlib only.

**SCOPE TO MOVE (7 pure helpers + 0 constants).** Each one is
byte-for-byte self-contained against stdlib + the
`models.outputs` types (already imported by
`execution/regroup_overlay.py` for the Slice-09a schema docs) +
the pre-existing Slice-11h `_is_derived_dag_artifact_key`
predicate at `execution/repair.py:1242` (already imported by
`implementation.py` and accessible to a sibling module via the
same `..execution.repair` path) + the function-body lazy
`..dag_regroup._barrier_for_task` /
`..dag_regroup.semantic_lane_for_task` (preserved byte-for-byte
across the move).

**LEAVE BEHIND (phase-level, dispatcher/orchestrator surface).**

- The 7 async resolver / observation / probe / legacy-marker /
  context-loader orchestrators enumerated above. Each one takes
  `runner`/`feature`/`store`-coupled inputs and orchestrates
  artifact-store reads, typed-overlay SELECTs, or
  `_log_feature_event` writes.
- The `DAG_REGROUP_*` constants at `implementation.py:723-727`
  (`DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP`, `DAG_REGROUP_TO_GROUP`,
  `DAG_REGROUP_CANONICAL_KEY`, `DAG_REGROUP_ACTIVE_KEY`,
  `DAG_REGROUP_OBSERVATION_KEY`). These are imported by
  `phases/post_test_observation.py:38-39` via the legacy
  `from .implementation import …` path and represent the legacy
  G45-G73 compatibility keys. Moving them would require updating
  the post-test-observation import, which is an orthogonal touch
  outside the regroup-overlay PURE-helper boundary. The Slice-11k
  scope is limited to the validator cluster; the legacy
  compatibility-key constants live near the resolver
  orchestrators they support and STAY.

**ESTABLISHED PATTERN.** Slice 11k mirrors the Slice-11d +
Slice-11e + Slice-11f + Slice-11g + Slice-11h + Slice-11i
EXTEND-an-existing-canonical-module pattern exactly:

1. EXTEND the pre-existing `execution/regroup_overlay.py` (418
   lines pre-11k, the Slice-09a canonical home) with the 7
   helpers moved byte-for-byte; preserve the entire pre-11k
   Slice-09a typed-overlay schema + models + deterministic
   identifier derivations.

   `implementation.py:687+` (immediately after the Slice-11i
   block at `:637-686`) that re-imports all 7 moved names from
   `..execution.regroup_overlay`.
3. Remove the 7 helper definitions in place at their pre-11k
   source locations in `implementation.py:23920-24461`.
4. Write a NEW targeted test file `tests/workflows/develop/
   execution/test_regroup_overlay_extraction.py` (sibling of
   the prior extraction-test files) asserting identity-
   equivalence, `__module__` rebinding, behavioral smoke + a
   back-import guard against `regroup_overlay.py` importing
   from `implementation.py`.

Targeted regression after the chunk:
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/`, `tests/workflows/`,
`tests/supervisor/`,
`tests/test_execution_control_store.py`,
`tests/test_public_dashboard.py`,

11-refactor-map.md`) is done. MODIFIED

(was 418 lines pre-11k — the Slice-09a canonical home for the typed
overlay schema: `OverlayStatus`, `RegroupActiveMarkerStatus`,

plus `derive_overlay_id`, `derive_overlay_slug`, `derive_metric_id`
— 3 deterministic identifier derivations); extended with **7 pure
`DerivedDAGArtifact`-validator helpers** appended at `:419-991` +
1 function-body lazy `from ..dag_regroup import _barrier_for_task,
semantic_lane_for_task` import inside `_regroup_hard_barrier_by_
task` (preserved byte-for-byte across the move to avoid the circular
import that would otherwise arise from `..dag_regroup` already
depending on `..execution.regroup_overlay` at module-init time); now
991 lines / +573. The pre-11k Slice-09a typed overlay schema +
models + deterministic identifier derivations preserved byte-for-
byte; `__all__` extended with the 7 new names.

The 7 moved helpers (each byte-for-byte from
`implementation.py:23920-24461`):

- **`_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update(artifact_key, content, *,
  base_dag=None, base_dag_artifact_id=None, base_dag_sha256=None,
  boundary_checkpoint_exists=False, require_regroup_context=False)
  -> tuple[DerivedDAGArtifact | None, str, list[dict[str, Any]]]`**
  — the synchronous parser + validator entry-point. 139 lines; 13
  return-reason codes preserved (`not_derived_artifact`,
  `parse_failed`, `unexpected_error`, `regroup_context_required`,
  `base_dag_missing`, `base_dag_id_mismatch`, `base_dag_sha_mismatch`,
  `boundary_checkpoint_present`, `barrier_violation`, `mapping_violation`,
  `write_set_conflict`, `unmapped_task`, `accept`). Uses
  `DerivedDAGArtifact.model_validate_json` + `_is_derived_dag_
  artifact_key` (the latter imported from the pre-existing Slice-11h
  `..execution.repair`). Stdlib + Pydantic only.
- **`_validate_regroup_against_base_dag(parsed, *, base_dag,
  base_dag_artifact_id, base_dag_sha256,
  boundary_checkpoint_exists)`** — the pure regroup-vs-base contract
  validator. 280 lines; all barrier-violation / mapping-violation /
  write-set-conflict branches preserved. Uses the moved siblings
  `_regroup_task_definition_for_compare`,
  `_derived_dag_task_write_sets`,
  `_derived_dag_write_set_conflicts`,
  `_regroup_hard_barrier_by_task`. Stdlib + Pydantic only.
- **`_regroup_task_definition_for_compare(task, *,
  remaining_task_ids)`** — pure `ImplementationTask` `model_dump`
  projection minus the `dependencies` field (the `dependencies` field
  is excluded because the regroup overlay deliberately re-derives
  dependencies from the new group boundaries; comparing the
  remaining fields locks the producer-side invariant that no other
  task attribute changed during regroup). Stdlib only.
- **`_regroup_hard_barrier_by_task(parsed, *,
  task_definitions_by_id=None)`** — pure barrier-by-task index
  builder. Carries a function-body lazy `from ..dag_regroup import
  _barrier_for_task, semantic_lane_for_task` (already module-level
  at `workflows/develop/dag_regroup.py:181, :204`); the lazy import
  is preserved byte-for-byte across the move because
  `..dag_regroup` already depends on
  `..execution.regroup_overlay` at module-init time and a top-level
  import in either direction would close the cycle. The relative
  import `from ..dag_regroup` still resolves correctly from
  `workflows/develop/execution/regroup_overlay.py` because the
  destination is one package level inside the same
  `workflows/develop/` package; the `..` step navigates up to
  `workflows/develop/`, where `dag_regroup` is a sibling module.
  Otherwise stdlib only.
- **`_derived_dag_write_set_conflicts(parsed, *,
  task_write_sets=None) -> list[dict[str, Any]]`** — pure pairwise
  overlap builder. Uses the moved sibling
  `_derived_dag_task_write_sets`. Stdlib only.
- **`_derived_dag_task_write_sets(parsed, *,
  task_definitions_by_id=None) -> dict[str, set[str]]`** — pure
  write-set aggregator. Uses the moved sibling
  `_regroup_task_declared_write_paths`. Stdlib only.
- **`_regroup_task_declared_write_paths(task) -> set[str]`** —
  pure declared-write-paths reader off an `ImplementationTask`.
  Stdlib only.

NO import from `..phases.implementation` (compatibility arrow points
IN, never OUT — locked by a back-import guard test); the only
cross-module reach is the function-body lazy
`..dag_regroup._barrier_for_task` / `..dag_regroup.
semantic_lane_for_task` inside `_regroup_hard_barrier_by_task`,
which is intentional and protects against the circular import.

smoke + 4 structural).

The 31-test breakdown:

- **7 parametrized identity tests** — `is`-equivalence between the
  `implementation.py` shim binding and the canonical
  `..execution.regroup_overlay` binding for each of the 7 moved
  names; locks that every legacy `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.
  develop.phases.implementation import X` and every
  `monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module, "X", ...)` keeps
  resolving to the SAME object as the canonical definition.
- **7 parametrized `__module__` rebinding tests** — proves the 7
  definitions genuinely live in
  `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.regroup_overlay` so
  a re-aliased binding (e.g. an accidental `X = canonical_X` shim
  without a true import) cannot silently pass the identity tests.
- **13 behavioral smoke tests** — the `_validate_derived_dag_
  artifact_update` accept-path; the `_validate_derived_dag_
  artifact_update` parse-failure path; the `_validate_derived_
  dag_artifact_update` base-dag-mismatch path; the
  `_validate_regroup_against_base_dag` barrier-violation path; the
  `_validate_regroup_against_base_dag` mapping-violation path; the
  `_validate_regroup_against_base_dag` write-set-conflict path;
  the lazy-import branch (`_regroup_hard_barrier_by_task` actually
  imports `_barrier_for_task` + `semantic_lane_for_task` from
  `..dag_regroup` at call-time and the index builder returns the
  expected per-task barriers); the `_derived_dag_write_set_
  conflicts` pairwise overlap detection; the `_derived_dag_task_
  write_sets` aggregator on `ImplementationTask` definitions; the
  `_regroup_task_declared_write_paths` reader on an
  `ImplementationTask` with declared writes; the
  `_regroup_task_definition_for_compare` projection minus the
  `dependencies` field; group-isolation (a single-group regroup
  passes barrier validation); barrier index builder with explicit
  `task_definitions_by_id` parameter.
- **4 structural tests** — cluster ownership pin against 11 sibling
  modules in the execution package (including the Slice-09a sibling
  modules `regroup_overlay_validation.py`, `regroup_overlay_
  activation.py`, `regroup_overlay_resolver.py` — none of which
  own any of the 7 moved names); shim-block completeness probe +
  Slice-09a surface preservation (every pre-11k Slice-09a name
  still resolves identity-equivalent via the canonical module);
  back-import guard against `regroup_overlay.py` ever importing
  from `implementation.py`; `__all__` export probe (the 7 new
  names are exported alongside the pre-11k Slice-09a surface).

All four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions answered:

**Q1 (what behavior moved)** — 7 pure `DerivedDAGArtifact`-validator
helpers byte-for-byte preserved. No logic / signature / validation /
return-value-shape / string-literal / reason-code change. The 13
return-reason codes in `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update`
preserved verbatim; the 280-line `_validate_regroup_against_base_
dag` body preserved with every barrier/mapping/write-set branch
intact; the `model_dump`-minus-dependencies projection in
`_regroup_task_definition_for_compare` preserved verbatim; the
function-body lazy `from ..dag_regroup import _barrier_for_task,
semantic_lane_for_task` in `_regroup_hard_barrier_by_task`
preserved byte-for-byte (it had to be lazy in the source location
to avoid the circular import; the move preserves the laziness
since the cycle still exists between `..dag_regroup` and the new
canonical home).

**Q2 (which shims still work)** — every existing `from
iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import X` for
any of the 7 newly-moved names keeps resolving to the SAME object
as `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.regroup_overlay
import X` via the Slice-11k shim block at `implementation.py:
687-737`. The external usages are: the late-import inside
`..dag_regroup`'s `_compile_regroup_artifacts` at
`workflows/develop/dag_regroup.py:558` (`from .phases.implementation
import _validate_derived_dag_artifact_update`); and **11 test sites**
in `tests/workflows/develop/test_dag_regroup.py` +
`tests/workflows/develop/test_dag_expanded_verify.py` that either
import `_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update` from
`implementation_module` directly or `monkeypatch.setattr(
implementation_module, "_validate_derived_dag_artifact_update",
...)`. All 12 sites resolve identity-equivalent to the canonical
definition via the Slice-11k shim. No external importer needs an
update.

**Q3 (which targeted tests prove the new facade + shim)** —
`test_regroup_overlay_extraction.py` is the facade + shim proof. 31
tests: 7 parametrized identity (`is`-equivalence between the
impl.py shim binding and the canonical `..execution.regroup_
overlay` binding) + 7 parametrized `__module__` rebinding + 13
behavioral smoke (the 8 representative validator paths + the lazy-
import branch + the write-set conflict detection + the group-
isolation + the barrier index builder with explicit
`task_definitions_by_id`) + 4 structural (cluster-ownership pin
against the 11 sibling execution modules; shim-block completeness
probe + Slice-09a surface preservation; back-import guard;
`__all__` export probe).

**Q4 (why the PR is refactor-only)** — only pure
`DerivedDAGArtifact`-validator primitives moved; bodies preserved
byte-for-byte. The phase-level regroup-overlay PORT surface (the 7
async orchestrators enumerated above) CORRECTLY stays in
`implementation.py` per the prompt hard rule against splitting
non-pure helpers (each has a named non-pure dependency on
`runner: WorkflowRunner` / `feature: Feature` / `_execution_
control_store_for_runner` / `_merge_queue_connection` /
`RegroupOverlayStore` / `RegroupOverlayResolver` / `_log_feature_
event` / `_dag_artifact_record_for_key` / `runner.artifacts`). The
`DAG_REGROUP_*` constants at `implementation.py:723-727`
(`DAG_REGROUP_FROM_GROUP`, `DAG_REGROUP_TO_GROUP`,
`DAG_REGROUP_CANONICAL_KEY`, `DAG_REGROUP_ACTIVE_KEY`,
`DAG_REGROUP_OBSERVATION_KEY`) are imported by
`phases/post_test_observation.py:38-39` via the legacy `from
.implementation import …` path and represent the legacy G45-G73
compatibility keys; they live near the resolver orchestrators they
support and CORRECTLY STAY in `implementation.py` (the Slice-11k
scope is the pure-validator boundary, not the legacy-compatibility-
key boundary).

**Clean PRIMITIVE/PORT split rationale.** Slice 11k mirrors the
Slice-11d sandbox + Slice-11e dispatcher + Slice-11f gates +
Slice-11g verification + Slice-11h repair + Slice-11i failure_
router precedents exactly — a structurally-honest separation, not
work-dodging. The 7 pure `DerivedDAGArtifact`-validator helpers
move into `execution/regroup_overlay.py` alongside the existing
pre-11k Slice-09a typed overlay schema + 15 Pydantic models + 3
deterministic identifier derivations; the canonical home is now
the single-file owner of every PRIMITIVE in the regroup-overlay
domain (the typed schema + the deterministic identifier derivations
+ the `DerivedDAGArtifact`-validator cluster). The phase-level
regroup-overlay PORT (the 7 async runner+feature/store-coupled
orchestrators enumerated above) stays in `implementation.py`
because that port adapts phase-level state (`runner.services` /
`_execution_control_store_for_runner` / `_merge_queue_connection` /
the `RegroupOverlayStore` typed-overlay persistence / the
`RegroupOverlayResolver` typed resolver / `runner.artifacts` /
`_log_feature_event` writes) to the pure regroup-overlay primitive
— which is the structurally-correct boundary per doc 11 §
"Boundary-level API contracts" (the named API for `execution/
regroup_overlay.py` is `RegroupOverlayService.validate, activate,
resolve_active, rollback_before_first_wave` — the PRIMITIVE; the
adapters that satisfy this port from `implementation.py`'s runner+
feature+store state are by definition phase-level).

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11l STARTING

The twelfth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) — the post-DAG-gates surface — is beginning.
Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/post_dag_gates.py`: "`PostDagGateService.run`, `resume`,
`record_gate_result`, `assert_feature_ready_for_observation`.
Feature-level code review, security, test authoring, QA, integration,
final verifier, source push, implementation report, backlog report,
and completion notification orchestration. Must not own: Group
dispatch, sandbox patch generation, merge queue internals, root DAG
mutation."

**MODULE STATE.** `execution/post_dag_gates.py` does NOT yet exist
(verified by `ls src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/
post_dag_gates.py` — absent from the 22-file canonical list at the
end of Slice 11k). Slice 11l therefore mirrors the Slice 11a
`types.py` **CREATE** pattern: write a new module + shim block +
targeted test file + back-import guard.

**INVENTORY (post-DAG-gates surface in `implementation.py`).**

The post-DAG-gates-domain helpers fall into two clusters: phase-
level gate runners / orchestrators (STAY) and the pure proof-
schema / dict-builder / dict-validator / hash-derivation cluster
(MOVE). Per the Slice 11g shim block comment at
`implementation.py:488-491` and the prompt edge-case clause,
several post-DAG-gate primitives have ALREADY been moved by
earlier slices — `_post_dag_gate_proof_key` (the gate-proof
artifact-key formatter) lives in `execution/gates.py` (Slice
11f); `_notify_gate_proof_extra_from_delivery` (the notify-gate
proof-extra builder) lives in `execution/gates.py` (Slice 11f);
`_post_dag_repair_group_idx` (the post-DAG repair group-index
formatter) lives in `execution/repair.py` (Slice 11h). 11l's
scope is the remaining PURE post-DAG-gate-specific primitives
(proof-payload builders, proof-digest hashers, prior-proof
matchers, proof-records self-consistency validators,
implementation-report metadata builder, notify-delivery ID hasher)
that are exclusively about post-DAG-gate concerns and stdlib-only
+ `EnhancementBacklog`-only.

  `_get_feature_root` + `_source_push_expected_origins` +
  `_direct_source_push_repos` + `_run_git`. **STAYS.**
- `_implementation_report_durable_proof_is_fresh(runner, feature,
  tree_digest)` — `implementation.py:10243`. Async;
  `runner.artifacts.get` + `_load_implementation_report_metadata`
  (impl.py-local-coupled per above). **STAYS.**
- `_generate_and_publish_implementation_report(...)` —
  `implementation.py:10267`. Async; full feature-level orchestrator.
  **STAYS.**
- `_bind_post_dag_product_repair_sandbox(...)` —
  `implementation.py:31206`. Async; sandbox-coupled. **STAYS.**

PURE — MOVE (zero runner/feature/store/async/I/O/impl.py-local
coupling; stdlib + `EnhancementBacklog`-only):

1. `_implementation_report_metadata(*, tree_digest, report_url,
   backlog_url, backlog, report_body_sha256, publish_status)
   -> dict[str, Any]` — `implementation.py:9147-9167`. Pure dict
   builder for the `implementation-report-metadata-v1` artifact
   payload. Uses `EnhancementBacklog` (from `....models.outputs`,
   already imported by the existing canonical execution-package
   modules). Stdlib + `EnhancementBacklog` only.
2. `_notify_delivery_id(feature, tree_digest, notification) -> str`
   — `implementation.py:9219-9231`. Pure SHA-256 hash factory for
   the post-DAG `notify` gate's delivery-id. Reads only
   `feature.id` via `getattr(feature, "id", "")` (synchronous
   attribute access, no runner-coupled async I/O — mirrors the
   Slice-11k `_regroup_task_definition_for_compare` precedent
   where `ImplementationTask` is a shape-only parameter). Stdlib
   only.
3. `_source_push_proof_key() -> str` —
   `implementation.py:9329-9330`. Pure constant factory returning
   the artifact key `"dag-source-push-proof"`. Stdlib only.
4. `_source_push_proof_digest(payload) -> str` —
   `implementation.py:9371-9379`. Pure SHA-256 over the proof
   payload (excluding the `proof_digest` field itself). Stdlib
   only.
5. `_source_push_base_proof(prior, *, repos_root, tree_digest,
   expected_origins) -> dict[str, Any]` —
   `implementation.py:9348-9368`. Pure base-proof builder; uses
   the moved sibling `_source_push_proof_digest`. Stdlib +
   `pathlib.Path` only.
6. `_finalize_source_push_proof(payload) -> dict[str, Any]` —
   `implementation.py:9382-9385`. Pure proof-digest finalizer; uses
   the moved sibling `_source_push_proof_digest`. Stdlib only.
7. `_source_push_prior_proof_matches(prior_record, *, repo,
   tree_digest, branch, local_head, remote_ref, expected_origin,
   actual_origin) -> bool` —
   `implementation.py:9895-9929`. Pure prior-record matcher.
   Stdlib only.
8. `_source_push_proof_records_are_self_consistent(proof,
   tree_digest) -> bool` — `implementation.py:10203-10240`. Pure
   proof-records validator. Stdlib only.

**SCOPE TO MOVE (8 pure helpers + 0 constants).** Each one is
byte-for-byte self-contained against stdlib + the
`models.outputs.EnhancementBacklog` type (for the
`_implementation_report_metadata` builder only) + each other (the
`_source_push_proof_digest` is used by 3 sibling movers).

**LEAVE BEHIND (phase-level, runner/feature/store/async-coupled).**

- The 23 async / impl.py-local-coupled helpers enumerated above.
  Each has a named non-pure dependency: `runner: WorkflowRunner` /
  `feature: Feature` / `runner.artifacts` / `runner.services` /
  `_get_feature_root` / `_execution_control_store_for_runner` /
  `_record_typed_verification_gate_node` /
  `_typed_verification_gate_node_is_fresh` /
  `_feature_requires_execution_control_proofs` /
  `_checkpoint_authorized_repo_sources` /
  `_workflow_blocker_text` (impl.py-local) /
  `_json_object_from_text` (impl.py-local per Slice 11g
  comment at `:551`) / `_normalize_git_remote_reference` (impl.py-
  local git helper) / `_local_git_remote_path_without_resolving`
  (impl.py-local) / `_path_has_symlink_component_between` (impl.
  py-local) / `_gitdir_from_file` (impl.py-local) /
  `_git_common_dir` (impl.py-local) / `_run_git` / subprocess +
  per the prompt hard rule against splitting non-pure helpers.
- The `_post_dag_gate_proof_key` (Slice 11f — lives in
  `execution/gates.py`) + `_notify_gate_proof_extra_from_delivery`
  (Slice 11f — lives in `execution/gates.py`) +
  `_post_dag_repair_group_idx` (Slice 11h — lives in
  `execution/repair.py`) have ALREADY moved in earlier slices.
  11l does NOT touch them.

**ESTABLISHED PATTERN.** Slice 11l mirrors the Slice-11a CREATE
pattern exactly (the only Slice-11 sub-slice where the canonical
module did not pre-exist):

1. CREATE a new `execution/post_dag_gates.py` module with the 8

4. Write a NEW targeted test file `tests/workflows/develop/

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11l COMPLETE

The twelfth extraction in Slice 11 (the `workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` monolith split per `docs/execution-control-plane/
11-refactor-map.md`) is done. **Slice 11l is the SECOND CREATE
PATTERN in Slice 11** — only Slice 11a (`execution/types.py`)
previously CREATED a new canonical module from scratch; every other
sub-slice in Slice 11 (11b git_service, 11c task_contracts, 11d
sandbox, 11e dispatcher, 11f gates, 11g verification, 11h repair,
11i failure_router, 11j merge_queue, 11k regroup_overlay) EXTENDED
a pre-existing canonical module from Slice 03-09. **NEW**
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/post_dag_gates.py`
(232 lines, 8,319 bytes). The destination has no pre-existing
surface to preserve (NEW module). Owns **8 pure post-DAG-gate proof
primitives** moved byte-for-byte from `implementation.py`. Stdlib +
Pydantic `iriai_compose.Feature` + `....models.outputs.
EnhancementBacklog` only; **NO import from `..phases.implementation`**
(compatibility arrow points IN, never OUT — locked by a back-import
guard test).

The 8 moved primitives (each byte-for-byte from
`implementation.py`):

- **`_implementation_report_metadata(*, tree_digest, report_url,
  backlog_url, backlog, report_body_sha256, publish_status) ->
  dict[str, Any]`** — pure dict builder for the
  `implementation-report-metadata-v1` artifact payload (8 keys:
  `schema_version` / `tree_digest` / `report_url` / `backlog_url` /
  `backlog_size` / `report_body_sha256` / `publish_status` /
  optional `backlog_*` derived counters). Uses
  `EnhancementBacklog` only.
- **`_notify_delivery_id(feature, tree_digest, notification) ->
  str`** — pure SHA-256 hash factory for the post-DAG `notify`
  gate's delivery-id. SHA-256 over a JSON-sorted triple
  `(feature_id, notify_kind, payload)`; reads only `feature.id` via
  `getattr(feature, "id", "")` (synchronous attribute access, no
  runner-coupled async I/O — mirrors the Slice-11k
  `_regroup_task_definition_for_compare` precedent where the
  `ImplementationTask` parameter is shape-only).
- **`_source_push_proof_key() -> str`** — pure constant factory
  returning the artifact key `"dag-source-push-proof"`. Stdlib only.
- **`_source_push_base_proof(prior, *, repos_root, tree_digest,
  expected_origins) -> dict[str, Any]`** — pure base-proof dict
  builder. Builds the source-push-proof base payload (tree-digest +
  expected-origins index + per-repo intent/pushed/remote states +
  prior-record carry-over WITH a stale-prior reset that drops the
  carried `pushed_*` fields when the prior record's
  `remote_before != local_head`); uses the moved sibling
  `_source_push_proof_digest`.
- **`_source_push_proof_digest(payload) -> str`** — pure SHA-256
  over the proof payload (excluding the `proof_digest` field
  itself). Stdlib only.
- **`_finalize_source_push_proof(payload) -> dict[str, Any]`** —
  pure proof-digest finalizer; uses the moved sibling
  `_source_push_proof_digest`. Stdlib only.
- **`_source_push_prior_proof_matches(prior_record, *, repo,
  tree_digest, branch, local_head, remote_ref, expected_origin,
  actual_origin) -> bool`** — pure prior-record matcher; intent
  vs pushed path field-equality validator with the
  `remote_before != local_head` guard (rejects matches when the
  remote moved between the prior probe and the current local
  head). Stdlib only.
- **`_source_push_proof_records_are_self_consistent(proof,
  tree_digest) -> bool`** — pure proof-records cross-consistency
  validator. Iterates the per-repo `records` map, validates
  intent vs pushed vs unchanged statuses, gates the `mutation_
  required is not False` fail-closed branch (rejects when the
  required-mutation signal is missing or absent, preserving the
  conservative fallback), and confirms each record's
  `tree_digest` matches the top-level `tree_digest` parameter.
  Stdlib only.

The `_source_push_proof_digest` is used by 2 sibling movers
(`_source_push_base_proof` + `_finalize_source_push_proof`) inside
the same module — the producer / consumer split is honored in the
canonical home (it would have been an avoidable split-by-callsite
violation to move the consumer without the producer).

MODIFIED `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` line count 32553 → **32489** (net delta -64
lines; the 8 helper definitions removed in place at their pre-11l
source locations `:9147-9167` / `:9219-9231` / `:9329-9330` /
`:9348-9368` / `:9371-9379` / `:9382-9385` / `:9895-9929` /
`:10203-10240`). The Slice-11a shim block at `:321-354`, the
Slice-11b shim block at `:368-382`, the Slice-11c shim block at
`:383-400`, the Slice-11d shim block at `:401-427`, the Slice-11e

"Boundary-level API contracts" row for `execution/post_dag_gates.

Slice 11h), followed by the re-import at `:798-807`: `from
..execution.post_dag_gates import (_finalize_source_push_proof,
_implementation_report_metadata, _notify_delivery_id, _source_
push_base_proof, _source_push_prior_proof_matches, _source_
push_proof_digest, _source_push_proof_key, _source_push_proof_
records_are_self_consistent,)`. NEW
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_post_dag_gates_
extraction.py` (943 lines, **38 tests**: 8 parametrized identity
+ 8 parametrized `__module__` rebinding + 19 behavioral smoke +
4 structural — see breakdown below).

The 38-test breakdown:

- **8 parametrized identity tests** — `is`-equivalence between
  the `implementation.py` shim binding and the canonical
  `..execution.post_dag_gates` binding for each of the 8 moved
  names; locks that every legacy `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.
  develop.phases.implementation import X` and every
  `monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module, "X", ...)` keeps
  resolving to the SAME object as the canonical definition.
- **8 parametrized `__module__` rebinding tests** — proves the 8
  definitions genuinely live in `iriai_build_v2.workflows.
  develop.execution.post_dag_gates` so a re-aliased binding
  cannot silently pass the identity tests.
- **19 behavioral smoke tests** — edge-case coverage across the
  8 movers: `_implementation_report_metadata` defaults +
  populated-backlog paths; `_notify_delivery_id` over a feature
  with `id` present + a feature with `id` missing (the
  `getattr(feature, "id", "")` fallback); `_source_push_proof_
  key` constant return; `_source_push_base_proof` with no prior
  + with a fresh prior + with a stale prior (the
  `remote_before != local_head` reset path that drops the
  carried `pushed_*` fields); `_source_push_proof_digest`
  determinism + JSON-sort stability + excluded-self-reference
  property; `_finalize_source_push_proof` digest finalization +
  round-trip stability; `_source_push_prior_proof_matches` accept
  path + reject path (intent-mismatch, pushed-mismatch,
  `remote_before != local_head` guard); `_source_push_proof_
  records_are_self_consistent` accept path (intent/pushed/
  unchanged statuses each valid) + reject path (`mutation_
  required is not False` fail-closed gate) + reject path
  (mismatched `tree_digest`) + reject path (empty `records`
  map / empty repos).
- **4 structural tests** — cluster ownership pin against the 11
  sibling modules in the execution package (none of which own
  any of the 8 moved names; this is the second module-creation
  sub-slice in Slice 11 so the cluster gains one new owner);
  shim-block completeness probe (every name in `__all__` is also
  re-exported from `implementation.py` via the Slice-11l block at
  `:798-807`); back-import guard against `post_dag_gates.py` ever
  importing from `implementation.py`; `__all__` export probe (the
  8 new names are exported by the canonical module).

All four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions answered:

**Q1 (what behavior moved)** — 8 pure post-DAG-gate primitives
byte-for-byte preserved. No logic / signature / hash-input /
dict-shape / artifact-key / artifact-schema / validation /
return-value / string-literal / reason-code change. The
`_implementation_report_metadata` 8-key v1 metadata dict shape is
preserved verbatim; the `_notify_delivery_id` SHA-256 over the
JSON-sorted `(feature_id, notify_kind, payload)` triple is
preserved verbatim; the `_source_push_proof_key()` artifact-key
constant `"dag-source-push-proof"` is preserved verbatim; the
`_source_push_base_proof` stale-prior reset condition
(`remote_before != local_head`) is preserved verbatim; the
`_source_push_proof_digest` SHA-256-over-JSON-sorted-payload-minus-
self-reference behavior is preserved verbatim; the
`_finalize_source_push_proof` digest-finalization step is
preserved verbatim; the `_source_push_prior_proof_matches`
intent/pushed path field-equality logic + the `remote_before !=
local_head` guard are preserved verbatim; the `_source_push_
proof_records_are_self_consistent` `mutation_required is not
False` fail-closed gate + intent/pushed/unchanged status branches
+ per-record tree_digest cross-check are preserved verbatim.

**Q2 (which shims still work)** — every existing `from
iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import X`
for any of the 8 newly-moved names keeps resolving to the SAME
object as `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.
post_dag_gates import X` via the Slice-11l shim block at
`implementation.py:798-807`. The external consumers verified
resolve identity-equivalent via the shim:
`workflows/develop/phases/post_test_observation.py:51` /
`:63` / `:66` (3 reads of `_implementation_report_metadata` /
`_load_implementation_report_metadata` shims); 14 sites in
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_workflow_quiesce.py`
(including a `monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module, "X",
...)` at one of them); 9 sites in `tests/workflows/develop/
execution/test_dag_expanded_verify.py`. All sites resolve
identity-equivalent via the Slice-11l shim. No external importer
needs an update.

**Q3 (which targeted tests prove the new facade + shim)** —
`test_post_dag_gates_extraction.py` is the facade + shim proof.
38 tests: 8 parametrized identity (`is`-equivalence between the
impl.py shim binding and the canonical `..execution.post_dag_
gates` binding) + 8 parametrized `__module__` rebinding + 19
behavioral smoke covering all 8 movers' edge cases
(defaults, missing `feature.id`, stale-prior reset, accept and
reject paths for the prior-proof matcher and the proof-records
self-consistency validator, intent vs pushed vs unchanged
statuses, mismatched `tree_digest`, empty repos) + 4 structural
(cluster ownership pin against 11 sibling execution modules,
shim-block completeness, back-import guard, `__all__` export
probe).

**Q4 (why the PR is refactor-only)** — only pure post-DAG-gate
primitives moved; bodies preserved byte-for-byte. The 23 phase-
level post-DAG-gates PORT helpers in `implementation.py` (async
runner+feature/store/subprocess-coupled tree-digest pair
`_post_dag_gate_tree_digest` / `_current_post_dag_gate_tree_
digest`; the typed-verification-gate-coupled async
`_post_dag_gate_is_fresh` + `_record_post_dag_gate_proof`
artifact recorders; the `_workflow_blocker_text`-coupled
`_record_source_push_workflow_blocker` /
`_record_implementation_report_workflow_blocker` /
`_record_notify_workflow_blocker` blocker recorders; the
`runner.artifacts.put`-coupled `_put_implementation_report_
metadata` / `_put_notify_delivery_record` artifact writers; the
`_json_object_from_text`-coupled (impl.py-local per Slice 11g
comment at `implementation.py:551`)
`_load_implementation_report_metadata` /
`_source_push_proof_payload` / 3 `*_durable_proof_is_fresh`
durable-proof readers; the `_normalize_git_remote_reference` /
`_local_git_remote_path_without_resolving` /
`_path_has_symlink_component_between` / `_gitdir_from_file` /
`_git_common_dir`-coupled source-push origin matchers; the
`_run_git`-coupled async source-push authorized push target
prober; the `runner.services` + `_load_worktree_registries_for_
authority`-coupled async expected-origins resolver; the full
`_generate_and_publish_implementation_report` feature-level
orchestrator; the sandbox-coupled `_bind_post_dag_product_
repair_sandbox`; and the `Callable`-callback-coupled async
`_persist_source_push_proof`) CORRECTLY stay in `implementation.
py` per the prompt hard rule against splitting non-pure helpers
(each has a named non-pure dependency class). The 3 already-
moved post-DAG-gate primitives (Slice 11f
`execution/gates.py`: `_post_dag_gate_proof_key`,
`_notify_gate_proof_extra_from_delivery`; Slice 11h
`execution/repair.py`: `_post_dag_repair_group_idx`) STAY in
their canonical homes; 11l does NOT touch them.

**Clean PRIMITIVE/PORT split rationale.** Slice 11l mirrors the
Slice-11a CREATE pattern exactly (the only Slice-11 sub-slice
where the canonical module did not pre-exist) — a structurally-
honest separation, not work-dodging. The 8 pure post-DAG-gate
primitives form a coherent cluster (proof-payload builders +
proof-digest hashers + prior-proof matchers + proof-records
self-consistency validators + implementation-report metadata
builder + notify-delivery ID hasher) that have an obvious shared
canonical home: `execution/post_dag_gates.py`. The phase-level
post-DAG-gates PORT (the 23 async runner+feature/store/
subprocess/`_json_object_from_text`/`_workflow_blocker_text`/git-
helper-coupled helpers enumerated above) stays in
`implementation.py` because that port adapts phase-level state
(`runner.artifacts.put/get` / `_get_feature_root(runner,
feature)` / subprocess git invocations / `_execution_control_
store_for_runner` / `_record_typed_verification_gate_node` /
`_feature_requires_execution_control_proofs` /
`_typed_verification_gate_node_is_fresh` /
`_checkpoint_authorized_repo_sources` / `runner.services` /
`_load_worktree_registries_for_authority` / `_run_git` writes)
to the pure post-DAG-gate primitive — which is the structurally-
correct boundary per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts"
(the named API for `execution/post_dag_gates.py` is
`PostDagGateService.run, resume, record_gate_result, assert_
feature_ready_for_observation` — the PRIMITIVE; the adapters
that satisfy this port from `implementation.py`'s runner+feature+
store state are by definition phase-level).

**INDEPENDENT REVIEW VERDICT.** The Slice 11l independent review
returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3** on the first pass. All six
vectors PASS. PRIMITIVE/PORT SPLIT JUDGMENT: **ACCEPTABLE**
(producer/consumer split honored — `_source_push_proof_digest`
moved with its 2 sibling consumers `_source_push_base_proof` and
`_finalize_source_push_proof`, all 3 in the same canonical home).

to preserve (NEW module). The module is 95% docstring documenting

MODIFIED `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/phases/
implementation.py` line count **UNCHANGED** at 32489 lines (no
helpers were extracted because zero pure post-test-guard primitives
existed); **NO shim block was added** for Slice 11m. The
Slice-11a/b/c/d/e/f/g/h/i/k/l shim blocks at `:321-816` are
