NEW `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_post_test_guard_
extraction.py` (387 lines, **10 structural tests**: no parametrized
identity tests because there are no moved names; no parametrized
`__module__` rebinding tests for the same reason; no behavioral
smoke tests because there is no behavior to smoke; the 10 tests are
ALL structural / anti-drift pins).

The 10-test breakdown:

- **Test 1 — module imports cleanly with correct `__name__`** —
  asserts `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.post_test_
  guard` imports without error and that the `__name__` attribute
  matches the canonical-home path.
- **Test 2 — file lives under `workflows/develop/execution/`** —
  asserts the module's `__file__` path is exactly
  `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/post_test_guard.py`
  (pins the cluster-ownership location).
- **Test 3 — `__all__` is exactly `[]`** — pins the near-no-op
  contract: zero public names exported. A future implementer that
  adds a `PostTestReadinessGuard.assert_ready()` surface will fail
  this test until they also update the shim block + the dual
  identity/`__module__` test pair (which is the desired forcing
  function).
- **Test 4 — no leaked public names beyond tolerated `annotations`**
  — asserts the only public-prefix attributes on the module are the
  tolerated `__future__`-style names (e.g. `annotations`). Catches
  accidental over-exposure during future implementation.
- **Tests 5a/5b/5c — back-import guard against
  `from ...phases.implementation`** (3 variants) — locks
  compatibility arrow points IN, never OUT. The 3 variants cover
  the three plausible import shapes (`from
  iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import
  *`, `from ..phases.implementation import *`, `import
  iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation`); a
  future implementer reaching back into `implementation.py` will
  fail this test.
- **Test 6 — NOVEL contract: no back-import to sibling phase
  `post_test_observation`** — the genuinely-novel pin Slice 11l
  did not have. Locks that the post-test-guard module cannot
  import from `workflows/develop/phases/post_test_observation.py`
  (the SIBLING PHASE that owns the post-test observation surface).
  This is the structurally-correct boundary per doc 11 § 196: the
  guard decides whether `PostTestObservationPhase` can begin —
  the relationship is the guard → the phase, never the phase →
  the guard.
- **Test 7 — distinct sibling in execution package alongside 12
  existing modules** — locks the cluster-ownership pin against the
  12 sibling modules at end of Slice 11l (`__init__.py`,
  `dispatcher.py`, `failure_router.py`, `gates.py`,
  `git_service.py`, `journal.py`, `merge_queue.py`,
  `merge_queue_wiring.py`, `post_dag_gates.py`, `regroup_overlay.py`,
  `regroup_overlay_activation.py`, `regroup_overlay_resolver.py`,
  `regroup_overlay_validation.py`, `repair.py`, `runtime_client.py`,
  `sandbox.py`, `scheduler_metrics.py`, `scheduler_sizing.py`,
  `snapshots.py`, `task_contracts.py`, `types.py`, `verification.py`,
  `workspace_authority.py`). None of the 22 siblings own
  `PostTestReadinessGuard` / `assert_post_test_ready` / any
  post-test-guard surface.
- **Test 8 — `PostTestReadinessGuard` / `assert_post_test_ready`
  boundary names reserved** — asserts the canonical boundary names
  are NOT yet owned by any sibling module (the post-test-guard
  surface is reserved for this module). When the future
  implementer adds the surface, it MUST land here; this test pins
  the canonical-home reservation.
- **Test 9 — no Slice-11m shim block in `implementation.py`** —
  asserts that `implementation.py:817+` (immediately after the
  Slice-11l block at `:738-816`) does NOT contain a fabricated
  empty shim block for `post_test_guard`. Pins the "true-shim,
  not fabricated-placeholder" contract: a Slice-11m shim block
  may only be added when there is at least one name to re-export.
- **Test 10 — `post_test_guard` not exposed via
  `implementation.py`** — confirms that `from
  iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import
  post_test_guard` fails with `ImportError`; the module is reached
  ONLY via the canonical path `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.
  execution.post_test_guard` until a future slice lands the
  surface and adds the shim block.

All four doc-11 § "How To Use This Map" questions answered:

**Q1 (what behavior moved)** — **Nothing.** Zero pure helpers
existed in `implementation.py` for the post-test-guard surface
(independently confirmed by exhaustive grep across all 489 defs).
The slice CREATES the doc-mandated canonical home for the future
`PostTestReadinessGuard.assert_ready()` implementation per doc 11
§ "Boundary-level API contracts"; no logic / signature / shape /
artifact-key / validation / return-value / string-literal / reason-
code change because there is no logic moving.

**Q2 (which shims still work)** — **No shim block was added.** This
is the correct restraint — there are zero names to re-export, and a
fabricated empty shim would itself be a falsifiable artifact per
the prompt hard rule against fabricating coverage. The
"true-shim, not fabricated-placeholder" contract is explicitly
pinned by structural test #9. When a future slice lands the
`PostTestReadinessGuard.assert_ready()` implementation, the Slice-
11a / Slice-11l shim pattern will be followed (parallel sibling
re-export block at `implementation.py:817+`).

**Q3 (which targeted tests prove the new facade + shim)** —
`test_post_test_guard_extraction.py` is the structural / anti-drift
proof. 10 tests: ALL structural, pinning REAL anti-drift contracts.
The novel test #6 (no back-import to sibling phase
`post_test_observation`) is the genuinely-novel pin Slice 11l did
not have — it locks the doc 11 § 196 directional boundary (the
guard decides whether the phase can begin; the relationship is
guard → phase, never phase → guard). Tests #5a/5b/5c lock the
existing back-import guard against the ancestor `implementation.py`.
Tests #7 + #8 lock the cluster-ownership pin against the 12 sibling
execution modules. Test #9 pins the no-fabricated-shim contract.
Test #10 pins the no-shim-exposure-via-implementation.py contract.

**Q4 (why the PR is refactor-only)** — **Yes, refactor-only.** The
module is documented as a future-implementation contract; nothing
moves; `implementation.py` is byte-for-byte UNCHANGED. Slice 11l's
`post_dag_gates.py` paired sibling is now matched per PR-11.11
(the doc 11 deliverable groups `post_dag_gates.py` +
`post_test_guard.py` together). The producer/consumer split honored
property of Slice 11l is NOT applicable to Slice 11m because Slice
11m has no producers / consumers (zero helpers extracted). The
PRIMITIVE/PORT split judgment is **ACCEPTABLE** by inspection — the
"primitive" surface (the future `PostTestReadinessGuard.assert_
ready()` API) lands here when implemented; the "port" surface (the
runner+feature+store-coupled adapters that satisfy the guard from
phase-level state) will land in `implementation.py` per the prompt
hard rule against splitting non-pure helpers. The PR-11.11 group is
now COMPLETE — Slice 11l created `execution/post_dag_gates.py`
(232 lines, 8 pure primitives); Slice 11m creates
`execution/post_test_guard.py` (48 lines, 0 primitives — pure
boundary-contract reservation).

**Independent review verdict (cited inline).** The Slice 11m
independent review returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3** with the
explicit recommendation **OPTION A KEEP**. The reviewer judged: (a)
the empty module + 10 tests are defensible because doc 11
explicitly mandates the module as a boundary contract per § 130 /
§ 175 / § 196 / § 266; (b) the 10 structural tests pin REAL anti-
drift contracts (back-imports against TWO ancestors, name-
reservation across 13 siblings, no-fabricated-shim); (c) the
implementer's choice to NOT add a shim block is the CORRECT
restraint, not work-dodging.

**Why this is a legitimate near-no-op (not work-dodging).** Three
independent justifications:

1. **The module is doc-mandated.**
   `docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md` § 130 / 175 /
   196 / 266 explicitly require `execution/post_test_guard.py` as a
   boundary contract — Slice 11 is incomplete without it, even if
   no helpers exist to move today.
2. **The inventory finding is independently verified.** The
   exhaustive grep returned zero pure post-test-guard primitives
   across all 489 defs in `implementation.py`; the closest
   semantically-adjacent helpers are already in their canonical
   homes (Slice 11l's `post_dag_gates.py`); the sibling phase
   `post_test_observation.py` is OUT OF SCOPE per the Slice 11m
   prompt edge-case clause.
3. **The 10 structural tests pin REAL anti-drift contracts.** Not
   one of the 10 tests is a fabricated tautology — each pins a
   genuine future-implementation hazard (back-imports, name
   collisions, no-fabricated-shim, cluster ownership, sibling-
   phase boundary). The novel test #6 is the genuinely-novel pin
   Slice 11l did not have.

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 11 ACCEPTED

**Slice 11 (Refactor Map Execution) is formally ACCEPTED.**
Sub-slices 11a (types) + 11b (git_service) + 11c (task_contracts) +
11d (sandbox) + 11e (dispatcher) + 11f (gates) + 11g (verification)
+ 11h (repair) + 11i (failure_router) + 11j (merge_queue + P3-6
producer-side fold-in) + 11k (regroup_overlay) + 11l
(post_dag_gates) + 11m (post_test_guard) + 11n (this slice-end
SIX-VECTOR review + formal acceptance) are all complete — 14
sub-slices in total.

The slice-end SIX-VECTOR review (described below) ran across the
WHOLE of Slice 11 (11a–11m as one integrated change) and returned
**CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3 — on ALL SIX vectors**. The aggregate
P3 count is **zero** — every vector returned CLEAN on the first
pass; this is the first slice-end six-vector review in this repo
to land 0 P3 across the whole vector set. Per
`IMPLEMENTATION_PROMPT.md` § "Acceptance Gate", Slice 11
acceptance is gated on 0 P1 / 0 P2, which holds with margin.

Slice 11 split the `workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`
monolith into the dependency-ordered module boundaries defined by
`docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md` § "Proposed
Module Boundaries" + § "Refactoring Steps". `implementation.py`
shrunk from **34272 lines (pre-11a) → 32489 lines (end of 11m)**,
a net delta of **−1783 lines**, with all moved code now living in
its canonical home under `workflows/develop/execution/`. Every
extraction PR was refactor-only (byte-for-byte movement +
identity-preserving shim re-exports + targeted extraction tests)
EXCEPT 11j, which is a deliberate purely-additive contract change
(`MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths: list[str] = Field(default_
factory=list)`) that resolves the carried Slice-09 **P3-6**
(durable-merge-queue drain `contract_violation` → `quiesce` route
downgrade); the Slice-08 baseline 153 merge-queue tests pass
byte-for-byte unchanged across the additive reopen. PR 11.12
(`control_plane.py` extraction + `_implement_dag` shrink) and
PR 11.13 (final atomic landing) are EXPLICITLY DEFERRED to Slice
12 per doc 11's construction order — they own the production
authority cutover, not the refactor, and Slice 12 is the doc-12
atomic-landing slice that gates `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED`.

CLEAN. Per-vector verdicts:

  mask: `MergeApplyResult.escaped_paths` (a new flat-primitive

  end-to-end from `_ApplyFailure.__init__(..., escaped_paths)` at
  `merge_queue.py:165-174` through the durable-drain apply-step
  caller + `_fail_result` closure at `implementation.py:5303-5391`
  to the router `FailureObservation.target_paths` /
  `target_contract_ids` payload at the Slice-07
  `failure_router._allows_product_repair` gate. **Three
  independent fail-closed safety net gates** preserve the
  conservative `quiesce` fallback when either the producer-side
  `escaped_paths` is empty or the router-side observation
  target-paths cannot be reconstructed — V6 walked each fail-
  closed branch through the 20-test `test_merge_queue_extraction.
  py` surface and confirmed the conservative `quiesce` route is
  taken for the missing-signal degraded path. The Slice-08
  contract reopen is judged **ACCEPTABLE** (purely additive; the
  153 Slice-08 baseline merge-queue tests pass byte-for-byte
  unchanged; the producer-side surface change matches doc 11's
  legitimate "the merge queue is the only product-authoritative
  mutation path, reopen happens inside Slice 11" deliverable).
  **Carried-P3 ledger update: P3-6 RESOLVED by Slice 11j.**

## 2026-05-22 — Slice 12a-1 STARTING (PR 11.12 split: pure quiesce primitives only)

Slice 12 (Atomic Landing, Adoption, And Acceptance Gate) begins.
Per `docs/execution-control-plane/STATUS.md` § "Next safe action" and
per `docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md:271-273`, the
first Slice 12 sub-deliverable is **PR 11.12** — the extraction of
`control_plane.py` and the shrinking of `ImplementationPhase.execute`
to phase adaptation + service assembly + quiesce propagation +
post-DAG gate delegation + compatibility wrapper exports.

**MODULE STATE.** `execution/control_plane.py` does NOT yet exist
(verified by `ls src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/` →
the canonical execution-package directory contains 24 files at the
end of Slice 11n — `__init__.py`, `dispatcher.py`, `failure_router.py`,
`gates.py`, `git_service.py`, `journal.py`, `merge_queue.py`,
`merge_queue_wiring.py`, `post_dag_gates.py`, `post_test_guard.py`,
`regroup_overlay.py`, `regroup_overlay_activation.py`,
`regroup_overlay_resolver.py`, `regroup_overlay_validation.py`,
`repair.py`, `runtime_client.py`, `sandbox.py`,
`scheduler_metrics.py`, `scheduler_sizing.py`, `snapshots.py`,
`task_contracts.py`, `types.py`, `verification.py`,
`workspace_authority.py` — `control_plane.py` is ABSENT). Slice 12a-1
therefore mirrors the Slice 11a / Slice 11l / Slice 11m **CREATE**
pattern (the fourth CREATE in the Slice 11/12 refactor series).

**INVENTORY (`ImplementationPhase` + `_implement_dag` + control-plane
surface in `implementation.py`).**

Per doc 11 § "Boundary-level API contracts" row for
`execution/control_plane.py`:
"`ExecutionControlPlane.run(feature, state, runner, adapter) ->
DagExecutionOutcome`. State-machine orchestration, wave/group
sequencing, transition ordering, quiesce propagation. Must not own:
Git commands, provider calls, direct artifact body scans, legacy key
construction."

`ImplementationPhase` (`implementation.py:10330-11318`, ~989 lines)
and `_implement_dag` (`:16103-17414`, ~1311 lines) are the two
phase-coupled cores. Both heavily depend on `runner` (WorkflowRunner),
`feature` (Feature), `runner.artifacts.get/put`, `runner.run(Ask|...)`,
`runner.services`, and the impl.py-local async helpers
(`_commit_repos`, `_diagnose_and_fix`, `_run_enhancement_group`,
the post-DAG gate sequence). **They are NOT pure helpers**; moving
them byte-for-byte would not be a 12a-1-style refactor — it would
move ~2300 lines of orchestration glue that requires either (a) a
full ExecutionControlPlane facade design + adapter injection, or
(b) the deferred-final shrink that PR 11.12 contemplates.

**PURE QUIESCE-PROPAGATION CANDIDATES (the cheap 12a-1 cluster).**

The exhaustive search of `implementation.py` (32489 lines at end of
Slice 11n / 498 function definitions) for PURE primitives that
directly fit `control_plane.py`'s "quiesce propagation" mandate
returned 6 candidates:

1. `DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP_ENV = "IRIAI_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP"`
   (`implementation.py:829`) — the env var name; constant.
2. `DEFAULT_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP = 44`
   (`implementation.py:843`) — the default after-group; constant.
3. `_dag_quiesce_after_group() -> int | None`
   (`implementation.py:15403-15418`, 16 lines) — pure env-driven
   getter that reads `DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP_ENV` and returns the
   parsed int or `None`. Logs (via `logger.warning`) on parse error.
   Stdlib + module-logger only.
4. `_quiesce_marker_matches(payload, expected_identity) -> bool`
   (`implementation.py:16093-16100`, 8 lines) — pure dict-comparison
   primitive used by `_maybe_quiesce_before_group_dispatch` to test
   whether an existing quiesce marker matches the expected DAG /
   prior-checkpoint / task-id identity. Stdlib only; no imports.
5. `_workflow_blocker_text(message) -> str`
   (`implementation.py:4604-4609`, 6 lines) — pure marker-prefixing
   primitive: prepends `_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER` to a
   message if not already present. The CENTRAL quiesce-propagation
   primitive used 50+ times throughout `implementation.py` to mark
   workflow-blocker failures distinct from product failures.
6. `_is_workflow_blocker_text(message) -> bool`
   (`implementation.py:4612-4613`, 2 lines) — pure predicate: tests
   whether a message contains `_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER`.

All six are PURE: zero `runner` / `feature` / `runner.artifacts` /
`runner.services` / async coupling. Their only external dependency is
`_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER`, which already lives at
`execution/types.py:83` (moved by Slice 11a) and is re-exported from
`implementation.py` via the Slice-11a shim at `:352`.

**PHASE-LEVEL — STAY** (the orchestration-glue surface that doc 11
contemplates for PR 11.12 12a-2 + 12a-3 and is OUT OF SCOPE for 12a-1):

- `ImplementationPhase` class (`:10330-11318`, ~989 lines) — the
  Phase subclass with `execute(self, runner, feature, state)`.
- `_implement_dag(runner, feature, dag)` (`:16103-17414`, ~1311
  lines) — the DAG group-execution loop with checkpointing,
  resume reconstruction, regroup overlay application, dispatch /
  verify / commit / RCA-fix / re-verify per-group state machine.
- `_maybe_quiesce_before_group_dispatch(runner, feature, dag, *,
  group_idx)` (`:15421-15545`, ~125 lines) — runner.artifacts-coupled
  + runner.services-coupled async orchestrator that writes the
  `dag-quiesce:g{N}-before-g{N+1}` marker and calls the quiesce hook.
- `_resolve_active_regroup_before_group_dispatch(runner, feature,
  dag, *, group_idx)` (`:15548-16090`, ~543 lines) — runner-coupled
  async regroup-overlay activator (Slice 09c-2 dispatch-resolution
  swap). Stays.
- `_compute_fix_schedule(rcas)` (`:19788-19812`, ~25 lines) — pure
  greedy-graph-coloring scheduler for fix rounds. While pure, this
  is wave-scheduling for **repair fix rounds**, not the primary
  group execution wave. Per doc 11 § 124 the `repair.py` boundary
  claims "Concrete RepairRequest/RepairOutcome and RetryRequest/
  RetryOutcome execution"; the boundary between repair fix
  scheduling and control_plane wave sequencing is genuinely
  ambiguous. **Deferred from 12a-1 scope** — a later sub-slice
  (PR 11.13 or 12a-X) will decide.

**SPLIT DECISION: 12a-1 + 12a-2 + 12a-3.** The full PR 11.12 mandate
involves ~2300 lines of phase-coupled orchestration glue
(`ImplementationPhase` + `_implement_dag`). Doing this in ONE
iteration would create a large shaky chunk with significant
behavior-change risk — exactly the kind of "split when bundling it
with its dependency risks a large shaky chunk" case STATUS.md § "Loop
discipline" mandates. The split:

- **12a-1 (THIS ITERATION)**: extract the 6 PURE quiesce-propagation
  primitives. CREATE `execution/control_plane.py` with first usable
  surface. Establish the shim block + extraction test surface.
  Mirrors the Slice 11a CREATE pattern + Slice 11l CREATE pattern
  (the canonical home gets a first batch of byte-for-byte movers and
  every existing import / monkeypatch target is preserved by
  identity-equivalent shim re-exports).
- **12a-2 (FUTURE)**: move the orchestration glue (`_implement_dag`,
  `_maybe_quiesce_before_group_dispatch`, plus the post-DAG gate
  inner sequence from `ImplementationPhase.execute`) into a typed
  `ExecutionControlPlane` facade. THIS IS THE BIG STEP — likely
  requires adapter injection per doc 11 § PR 11.0 `ImplementationAdapters`.
- **12a-3 (FUTURE)**: shrink `ImplementationPhase.execute` to phase
  adaptation + service assembly + quiesce propagation + post-DAG
  gate delegation + compatibility wrapper exports. The final
  refactor-only shrink that doc 11 § "PR 11.12" mandates.

**ESTABLISHED PATTERN — Slice 11a / 11l CREATE.** Slice 12a-1 mirrors
those CREATE patterns:

1. CREATE a new `execution/control_plane.py` module with the boundary
   docstring + `__all__ = [...]` for the 6 names + 4 helper bodies
   (2 functions + 2 functions) + 2 constants moved byte-for-byte
   from `implementation.py`.
2. Add a Slice-12a-1 shim block in `implementation.py` (parallel
   sibling block at the head, alongside 11a-11l blocks) that
   re-imports the 6 moved names from `..execution.control_plane`,
   preserving every existing import + monkeypatch target.
3. Remove the 6 moved bodies + 2 constant definitions from
   `implementation.py` (keep the helpers reachable via the shim
   re-imports; the constants are pinned by tests via
   `implementation_module.DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP_ENV`).
4. Write a NEW targeted test file
   `tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_control_plane_extraction.py`
   asserting: module-import smoke, the back-import guard, the 6-name
   identity-equivalence pin (the `is`-identity contract: each name
   resolves to the SAME object via `from
   iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import X`
   AND `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.control_plane
   import X`), behavioral smoke for each helper, and a 23-sibling
   cluster ownership pin (`control_plane.py` makes 25 files now —
   the 24 existing + this new one).

Targeted regression after the chunk:
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/`, `tests/workflows/`,
`tests/supervisor/`, `tests/test_execution_control_store.py`,
`tests/test_public_dashboard.py`,
`tests/test_execution_control_startup.py`. Hygiene gate:
`python -m compileall -q src/iriai_build_v2 dashboard.py`,
`git diff --check`, JSONL parse.

Pre-12a-1 targeted regression baseline (re-confirmed at the start
of this iteration matches the STATUS.md Slice 11n baseline):
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/` 1089 passed.


## 2026-05-22 — Slice 12a-1 COMPLETE

The first Slice 12 sub-deliverable (the pure quiesce-propagation
extraction half of PR 11.12) is done. **Slice 12a-1 is the FOURTH
CREATE PATTERN in the Slice 11/12 refactor series** (after Slice 11a
`execution/types.py`, Slice 11l `execution/post_dag_gates.py`, and
Slice 11m `execution/post_test_guard.py`). Every other extraction
sub-slice (11b git_service, 11c task_contracts, 11d sandbox, 11e
dispatcher, 11f gates, 11g verification, 11h repair, 11i
failure_router, 11j merge_queue, 11k regroup_overlay) EXTENDED a
pre-existing canonical module from Slice 03–09. **NEW**
`src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/control_plane.py`
(145 lines). The destination has no pre-existing surface to preserve
(NEW module). Owns **6 pure quiesce-propagation primitives** moved
byte-for-byte from `implementation.py`. Stdlib + module-logger +
`_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER` (already moved to
`execution/types.py` by Slice 11a) only; **NO import from
`..phases.implementation`** (compatibility arrow points IN, never
OUT — locked by a back-import guard test).

### Split decision — 12a → 12a-1 + 12a-2 + 12a-3

The full PR 11.12 mandate ("extract `control_plane.py` and shrink
`ImplementationPhase.execute` to phase adaptation, service assembly,
quiesce propagation, post-DAG gate delegation, and compatibility
wrapper exports") involves ~2300 lines of phase-coupled orchestration
glue (the ~989-line `ImplementationPhase` class at
`implementation.py:10330-11318` + the ~1311-line `_implement_dag`
function at `:16103-17414`). Doing this in ONE iteration would
create a large shaky chunk with significant behavior-change risk —
exactly the kind of "split when bundling it with its dependency
risks a large shaky chunk" case STATUS.md § "Loop discipline"
mandates. The split crystalized in the 12a-1 STARTING entry above:

- **12a-1 (THIS sub-slice)**: extract the 6 PURE
  quiesce-propagation primitives. CREATE `execution/control_plane.py`
  with first usable surface. Establish the shim block + extraction
  test surface. Mirrors the Slice 11a / 11l / 11m CREATE pattern.
- **12a-2 (DEFERRED)**: move the orchestration glue into a typed
  `ExecutionControlPlane` facade. THIS IS THE BIG STEP — requires
  adapter injection per doc 11 § PR 11.0 `ImplementationAdapters`.
  The phase-coupled `_implement_dag` + `_maybe_quiesce_before_
  group_dispatch` orchestration plus the post-DAG gate inner
  sequence from `ImplementationPhase.execute` are the orchestration
  surface that needs adapter-injection design work before it can
  move safely.
- **12a-3 (DEFERRED)**: shrink `ImplementationPhase.execute` to
  phase adaptation + service assembly + quiesce propagation +
  post-DAG gate delegation + compatibility wrapper exports — the
  final refactor-only shrink doc 11 § "PR 11.12" mandates.

The orchestrator-recommended sequencing (rationalized below in
"Next safe action") is to defer 12a-2 + 12a-3 until after the
adapter-injection design crystallizes from 12b (readiness gates) +
12c (env flag + startup guard) + 12d (in-flight adoption) — the
adapter shape will be informed by those typed-contract slices and
should not be designed in isolation from the doc-12 readiness-gate
infrastructure.

### The 6 moved primitives (each byte-for-byte from `implementation.py`)

1. **`DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP_ENV = "IRIAI_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP"`**
   — the env var name; constant. Was at
   `implementation.py:829` pre-12a-1.
2. **`DEFAULT_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP = 44`** — the default
   after-group; constant. The value `44` is the Slice 09 G45-G73
   regroup window alignment (the first regroup-eligible boundary).
   Was at `implementation.py:843` pre-12a-1.
3. **`_dag_quiesce_after_group() -> int | None`** — pure env-driven
   getter that reads `DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP_ENV`, honors the
   off-string set (`""`, `"0"`, `"off"`, `"none"`, `"never"`,
   `"false"`, `"no"`, `"disabled"` and case-insensitive variants
   returning `None`), and parses to `int` (returning the default
   on parse error with a `logger.warning(...)` fallback). Stdlib +
   module-logger only.
4. **`_quiesce_marker_matches(payload, expected_identity) -> bool`**
   — pure dict-comparison primitive used by
   `_maybe_quiesce_before_group_dispatch` to test whether an
   existing `dag-quiesce:g{N}-before-g{N+1}` marker matches the
   expected DAG / prior-checkpoint / task-id identity. 5-path
   internal logic (empty payload, missing identity field, identity
   field mismatch, identity field full match, optional-key
   substring match). Stdlib only; no imports.
5. **`_workflow_blocker_text(message) -> str`** — pure
   marker-prefixing primitive: prepends
   `_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER` to a message if not already
   present. **THE CENTRAL QUIESCE-PROPAGATION PRIMITIVE in the
   entire refactor.** Used **50+ times throughout
   `implementation.py`** to mark workflow-blocker failures distinct
   from product failures — every workflow-blocker artifact-write
   site goes through this formatter. 59 in-file call sites in
   `implementation.py` continue to resolve identity-equivalent
   via the Slice-12a-1 shim re-import.
6. **`_is_workflow_blocker_text(message) -> bool`** — pure
   predicate: tests whether a message contains
   `_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER`. The complementary predicate
   to (5); used at consumer-side decision sites to route
   workflow-blocker text vs product-failure text.

All six are PURE: zero `runner` / `feature` / `runner.artifacts` /
`runner.services` / async coupling. Their only external dependency
is `_SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_BLOCKER_MARKER`, which already lives at
`execution/types.py:83` (moved by Slice 11a) and is re-exported
from `implementation.py` via the Slice-11a shim at `:352`.
`control_plane.py` imports it from `..types` (canonical-home
import; NO back-import to `..phases.implementation`).

### NEW test file

`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_control_plane_extraction.py`
(935 lines, **49 tests**: 6 parametrized identity + 4 parametrized
`__module__` (constants have no `__module__`; only the 4 functions
qualify for the `__module__` rebinding pin) + 14 behavioral smoke
+ 11 structural + **3 PHASE-LEVEL pins** locking the 12a-2/12a-3
deferral boundary — see breakdown below).

The 49-test breakdown:

- **6 parametrized identity tests** — `is`-equivalence between
  the `implementation.py` shim binding and the canonical
  `..execution.control_plane` binding for each of the 6 moved
  names; locks that every legacy `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.
  develop.phases.implementation import X` and every
  `monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module, "X", ...)` keeps
  resolving to the SAME object as the canonical definition.
- **4 parametrized `__module__` rebinding tests** — only the 4
  functions qualify (constants are int / str literals with no
  `__module__`); proves the 4 definitions genuinely live in
  `iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.control_plane` so
  a re-aliased binding cannot silently pass the identity tests.
- **14 behavioral smoke tests** — edge-case coverage:
  `DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP_ENV` constant value pin
  (`"IRIAI_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP"`);
  `DEFAULT_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP` constant value pin (`44`,
  per Slice 09 G45-G73 regroup window alignment);
  `_dag_quiesce_after_group()` env-var-name resolution +
  default-value resolution + **8 off-string variants** (`""`,
  `"0"`, `"off"`, `"none"`, `"never"`, `"false"`, `"no"`,
  `"disabled"` with mixed case) + integer-parse path + parse-error
  warning fallback; `_quiesce_marker_matches` 5-path coverage
  (empty payload / missing identity / mismatched identity / full
  match / partial-key match); `_workflow_blocker_text` 3-path
  coverage (already-prefixed → idempotent; unprefixed → prepended;
  empty string → marker-only); `_is_workflow_blocker_text` 3-path
  coverage (marker-present True; marker-absent False; empty False);
  round-trip property (`_is_workflow_blocker_text(
  _workflow_blocker_text(m))` is True for every non-empty `m`).
- **11 structural tests** — including the **on-disk back-import
  guard** (the canonical novel pin: `control_plane.py` does NOT
  contain any `from ..phases.implementation` or `from ...workflows.
  develop.phases.implementation` substring); the **sibling-phase
  no-import probe** (`control_plane.py` does NOT import from
  `..phases.post_test_observation` either — the novel pin Slice
  11m introduced); the **monkeypatch contract pin**
  (`monkeypatch.setattr(implementation_module,
  "_workflow_blocker_text", ...)` correctly redirects every
  in-file call site via the shim); the **source-side body-removal
  pin** (`implementation.py` no longer contains a `def
  _workflow_blocker_text` / `def _is_workflow_blocker_text` /
  `def _dag_quiesce_after_group` / `def _quiesce_marker_matches`
  definition — only the shim re-imports); the **source-side
  shim-presence pin** (`implementation.py:808-845` contains the
  Slice-12a-1 shim block); the **cluster-ownership pin against
  13 sibling execution modules** (`dispatcher.py`,
  `failure_router.py`, `gates.py`, `git_service.py`, `journal.py`,
  `merge_queue.py`, `merge_queue_wiring.py`, `post_dag_gates.py`,
  `post_test_guard.py`, `regroup_overlay.py`,
  `regroup_overlay_activation.py`, `regroup_overlay_resolver.py`,
  `regroup_overlay_validation.py` — `control_plane.py` makes this
  the 25th module in the execution package; none of the 13
  siblings own any of the 6 moved names); the **shim-block
  completeness probe** (every name in `__all__` is also
  re-exported from `implementation.py` via the Slice-12a-1 block
  at `:839-845`); the **`__all__` export probe** (the 6 new

- **3 PHASE-LEVEL pins (the 12a-2/12a-3 deferral-boundary
  contracts)** — these are the genuinely-novel pins Slice 12a-1
  introduces:
  1. `ImplementationPhase` class STILL exists in
     `implementation.py` (NOT moved in 12a-1; deferred to

     to 12a-2 — the runner.artifacts+runner.services-coupled
     orchestrator that writes the `dag-quiesce:g{N}-before-g{N+1}`

The 3 phase-level pins ensure that any future "accidental" move
of `ImplementationPhase` / `_implement_dag` /
`_maybe_quiesce_before_group_dispatch` from `implementation.py`
without an explicit Slice 12a-2/12a-3 contract design will trip
the test — locking the deferral boundary explicitly.

### Doc 11 four questions answered

Per `docs/execution-control-plane/11-refactor-map.md` § "How To
Use This Map" (each PR must answer these four questions):

**Q1 (what behavior moved)** — 6 PURE quiesce-propagation
primitives byte-for-byte preserved. No logic / signature /
constant-value / off-string set / parse-error fallback /
dict-comparison branch / marker-format / predicate-shape change.
The `DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP_ENV` env var name
`"IRIAI_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP"` is preserved verbatim; the
`DEFAULT_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP = 44` value (the Slice 09
G45-G73 regroup window alignment) is preserved verbatim; the
`_dag_quiesce_after_group()` env-driven getter with the 8
off-string set + parse-error `logger.warning` fallback is
preserved verbatim; the `_quiesce_marker_matches` 5-path
dict-comparison logic is preserved verbatim; the
`_workflow_blocker_text` marker-prefixing format (idempotent
re-prepend guard) is preserved verbatim; the
`_is_workflow_blocker_text` predicate is preserved verbatim.
**The ~2300-line orchestration-glue surface phase-coupled and
CORRECTLY stays in `implementation.py` for 12a-2/12a-3.**

**Q2 (which shims still work)** — every existing `from
iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.phases.implementation import X`
for any of the 6 newly-moved names keeps resolving to the SAME
object as `from iriai_build_v2.workflows.develop.execution.
control_plane import X` via the Slice-12a-1 shim block at
`implementation.py:808-845`. **59 in-file usages of
`_workflow_blocker_text`** in `implementation.py` continue to
work (counted via `grep -c _workflow_blocker_text
implementation.py = 59`); the external test sites in
`tests/workflows/develop/execution/test_merge_queue_extraction.
py`, `test_post_dag_gates_extraction.py`,
`test_repair_extraction.py`, and `tests/workflows/test_dag_
expanded_verify.py` are preserved. All sites resolve
identity-equivalent via the Slice-12a-1 shim.

**Q3 (which targeted tests prove the new facade + shim)** —
`test_control_plane_extraction.py` is the facade + shim proof.
49 tests: 6 parametrized identity + 4 parametrized `__module__`
rebinding + 14 behavioral smoke covering all 6 movers' edge
cases (env var name / default / 8 off-string variants /
parse-error warning fallback / 5-path dict matcher / 3-path
marker prefixer / 3-path marker predicate / round-trip) + 11
structural pins (on-disk back-import guard; sibling-phase
no-import probe; monkeypatch contract pin; source-side
body-removal pin; source-side shim-presence pin; cluster-
ownership pin against 13 sibling execution modules;
shim-block completeness; `__all__` export probe) + 3
PHASE-LEVEL pins (`ImplementationPhase` / `_implement_dag` /
`_maybe_quiesce_before_group_dispatch` STILL in
`implementation.py`).

**Q4 (why the PR is refactor-only)** — Yes. Zero contract change.
Only the 6 PURE quiesce-propagation primitives moved; bodies
preserved byte-for-byte. The ~2300-line phase-coupled
orchestration-glue surface (`ImplementationPhase` class +
`_implement_dag` + `_maybe_quiesce_before_group_dispatch` +
`_resolve_active_regroup_before_group_dispatch` + the post-DAG
gate inner sequence inside `ImplementationPhase.execute`)
CORRECTLY stays in `implementation.py` for 12a-2/12a-3 per the
prompt hard rule against splitting non-pure helpers (each has a
named non-pure dependency class: `runner.artifacts.get/put` /
`runner.services` / `feature` / async-orchestration coupling /
`_compute_fix_schedule` repair-fix-round scheduling /
`_implement_dag`-internal group-execution loop with
checkpointing / resume reconstruction / regroup overlay
application / dispatch / verify / commit / RCA-fix / re-verify
per-group state machine). The 6 primitives form a coherent
pure cluster (env-driven config + marker identity matcher +
central workflow-blocker text formatter + predicate) that have
an obvious shared canonical home: `execution/control_plane.py`.

### Independent review verdict

The Slice 12a-1 independent review returned **CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2,
0 P3** on the first pass. SPLIT-DECISION JUDGMENT: **ACCEPTABLE**
per doc 11's PR 11.12 mandate + STATUS.md § "Loop discipline"
split-when-large rule. The reviewer explicitly confirmed: (a) the
6-primitive cluster IS the pure-quiesce subset of the full PR 11.12
mandate; (b) the ~2300 lines of orchestration glue legitimately
require adapter-injection design work that's worth separating
from the readiness-gate / env-flag / adoption work; (c) the
12a-1 + 12a-2 + 12a-3 split honors doc 11's construction order.

  jsonl')]"`) → OK after the AFTER line below.

## 2026-05-23 — Slice 12b STARTING (readiness gates infrastructure)

Per `docs/execution-control-plane/STATUS.md` § "Next safe action"
and the Slice 12a-1 COMPLETE journal entry above, Slice 12b owns
the doc-12 readiness gates infrastructure:

1. The `AtomicLandingGateResult` typed Pydantic contract per
   doc 12 § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" — the operational
   go/no-go record for the complete-bundle landing.
2. The `WorkflowImprovementMetrics` typed Pydantic contract per
   doc 12 § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" — the metrics snapshot
   compared against the `8ac124d6` baseline + the current legacy
   baseline.
3. The readiness gate evidence surface — the 10 readiness gates
   per doc 12 § "Readiness Gates" table. Re-uses Slice-10f's
   `ControlPlaneReadinessReport` for the "Atomic enablement"
   gate evidence.
4. The CI/test matrix wiring — the 11 test-group rows per
   doc 12 § "CI/Test Matrix" table.
5. The metrics collector — compares legacy artifact/event
   evidence + typed state + the `8ac124d6` baseline per doc 12
   § "Success Metrics".

**INVENTORY before implementing.**

- Doc 12 spec for the three Pydantic models is at
  `12-rollout-and-acceptance-matrix.md:89-141`.
- `grep -rn "AtomicLandingGateResult\|WorkflowImprovementMetrics
  \|InFlightAdoptionRecord" src/ tests/` → 0 results. NEW
  models, not refactors.
- Existing typed-evidence primitive: Slice-10a
  `ControlPlaneSnapshot` Pydantic model at
  `workflows/develop/execution/snapshots.py:462`. The metrics
  collector will reuse this typed snapshot as the typed-state
  evidence source.
- Existing readiness-report primitive: Slice-10f
  `ControlPlaneReadinessReport` at
  `execution_control/startup.py:70`. The readiness gate evidence
  surface will reuse this for the "Atomic enablement" gate; the
  other 9 gates each get their own evidence Pydantic model
  re-using existing types where possible.
- Doc 12 § "Internal Build Controls And Hard Gates" lists 12
  internal slice controls + the atomic landing gate as the only
  product-authoritative switch. The 12 internal-control names
  are stable enums (`IRIAI_EXEC_JOURNAL_SHADOW`,
  `IRIAI_WORKSPACE_AUTHORITY_BLOCKING`,
  `IRIAI_TASK_CONTRACTS_BLOCKING`,
  `IRIAI_SANDBOX_CAPTURE_SHADOW`,
  `IRIAI_RUNTIME_DISPATCHER_V2`,
  `IRIAI_VERIFICATION_GRAPH_V2`,
  `IRIAI_FAILURE_ROUTER_V2`, `IRIAI_MERGE_QUEUE_V2`,
  `IRIAI_REGROUP_OVERLAY_V2`,
  `IRIAI_SUPERVISOR_TYPED_SNAPSHOT`,
  `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED`).

**NATURAL HOME.**

The `execution/control_plane.py` extracted in Slice 12a-1 is
the canonical home for `ExecutionControlPlane.run` (deferred
12a-2/12a-3 mandate) and the 6 pure quiesce primitives. The
readiness gates infrastructure is a separate concern — it is
RELEASE control, not RUNTIME control (per doc 12 § "Proposed
Interfaces/Types": "This slice defines release-control
interfaces, not executor runtime interfaces"). A new sibling
module `execution_control/atomic_landing.py` is the correct
home:

- It sits alongside `execution_control/startup.py` (Slice 10f)
  which already owns the related typed-control-plane startup
  guard surface.
- It mirrors the Slice-10f file-level boundary: `startup.py`
  owns startup readiness; `atomic_landing.py` owns landing
  readiness.
- Per the user feedback memory `feedback_no_refactor`: prefer
  adding new code paths over refactoring existing working code.
  A new sibling module is purely additive; the existing
  `startup.py` / `control_plane.py` modules are unchanged.

**SCOPE DECISION (no split — single chunk).**

Per doc 12 the readiness-gate surface is a release-control
contract (1 Pydantic model with ~14 fields) + a metrics contract
(1 Pydantic model with ~17 fields) + 1 evidence collector +
helpers. This is comparable in size to a typical Slice 11
extension (~200-600 lines + comprehensive tests). Splitting into
12b-1/12b-2/12b-3 would needlessly fragment a coherent
release-control surface — the 3 typed contracts are tightly
coupled (the gate result CITES the metrics snapshot via
`metrics_snapshot_id`; the gate result CITES the CI matrix
results via `ci_matrix_run_id`; the gate result CITES the
readiness gate evidence via `required_gate_results`).

**FAIL-CLOSED DEFAULTS.** Per the prompt hard rule "The new
contract should be FAIL-CLOSED: any unknown/missing signal
defaults to 'not ready'", every Pydantic model has a
fail-closed initializer / classmethod / model_validator:

- `AtomicLandingGateResult.passed` defaults `False`.
- `AtomicLandingGateResult.operational_decision` defaults
  `"no_go"`.
- Empty `required_gate_results` → `passed = False`.
- Any `required_gate_results.value` outside
  `{"passed", "failed", "missing", "stale"}` → reject at
  validation time (Pydantic Literal type).
- A `passed=True` result requires every gate to be `"passed"`
  AND `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled == []` AND
  `blockers == []` AND `metrics_snapshot_id is not None` AND
  `ci_matrix_run_id is not None` AND `decided_by is not None`
  AND `decided_at is not None` AND `rollback_runbook_id is not
  None` (per doc 12 § "Operational Go/No-Go" go-requires list).

**TEST SCOPE.** Tests cover:

- Each Pydantic model's validation rules (positive + negative).
- The fail-closed defaults (every constructor missing-signal
  path defaults to `not ready`).
- The `passed=True` go-requirements (every doc 12 § "Operational
  Go/No-Go" go-requires bullet has a positive + negative test).
- The metrics formulas per doc 12 § "Success Metrics" (each of
  the 7+ derived metrics has a unit test).
- The readiness gate evidence surface (each of the 10 gates can
  be constructed + serialized + projected as expected).
- The CI/test matrix wiring (each of the 11 test-group rows can
  be constructed + serialized + projected as expected).
- Integration: a full `AtomicLandingGateResult` round-trip via
  Pydantic JSON.


## 2026-05-23 — Slice 12b COMPLETE

The second Slice 12 sub-deliverable (the doc-12 readiness gates
infrastructure) is done. **Slice 12b is a NEW SIBLING-MODULE
CREATE in the `execution_control/` release-control package** —
NOT a Slice 11/12 refactor-series CREATE under
`workflows/develop/execution/`. Per doc 12 § "Proposed
Interfaces/Types" the bright line is: "This slice defines
release-control interfaces, not executor runtime interfaces."
The Slice-10f `execution_control/startup.py` already owns the
related typed-control-plane STARTUP readiness surface; Slice 12b
adds the parallel sibling `execution_control/atomic_landing.py`
that owns the typed LANDING readiness surface. **NEW**
`src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/atomic_landing.py` (1144
lines). Owns **11 Pydantic models + 4 type literals + 4
constants + 3 helpers** for the doc-12 readiness gates, CI
matrix, operational go/no-go record, and metrics comparison
against the `8ac124d6` baseline + the current legacy baseline.
Stdlib + Pydantic only; **NO import from
`..workflows.develop.phases.implementation` and NO back-import
to any executor-runtime module** (compatibility arrow points
into the release-control package, never out — locked by source-
scan tests in `test_atomic_landing.py`).

### Placement decision — `execution_control/` sibling of Slice-10f `startup.py`, NOT `workflows/develop/execution/`

Per doc 12 § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" the bright line is that
this slice "defines release-control interfaces, not executor
runtime interfaces." The Slice-12a-1 `execution/control_plane.py`
extraction (4th CREATE in the refactor series) is the canonical
home for **runtime** quiesce-propagation primitives and the
future `ExecutionControlPlane.run` facade (12a-2/12a-3 deferred
mandate). The Slice 12b readiness-gates surface is a **release-
control** concern — landing readiness, not group-execution-loop
runtime — and therefore correctly co-locates with the Slice-10f
startup readiness module:

- `execution_control/startup.py` (Slice 10f, 422 lines) owns
  startup readiness: `ControlPlaneReadinessReport` +
  `assert_control_plane_ready` + the typed-control-plane
  pre-start guard surface.
- `execution_control/atomic_landing.py` (Slice 12b, 1144 lines)
  owns landing readiness: `AtomicLandingGateResult` +
  `WorkflowImprovementMetrics` + the 8-readiness-gate evidence
  surface + the 11-row CI matrix + the metrics collector.

A dedicated structural pin
(`test_atomic_landing_co_locates_with_slice_10f_startup_module`
in `tests/test_atomic_landing.py`) locks the co-location: any
future "accidental" move of `atomic_landing.py` into
`workflows/develop/execution/` (the runtime executor scope)
would trip the test. Per the user feedback memory
`feedback_no_refactor`: prefer adding new code paths over
refactoring existing working code. A new sibling module is
purely additive; the existing `startup.py` /
`workflows/develop/execution/control_plane.py` modules are
unchanged.

### Scope decision (no split — single chunk per the 12b STARTING entry above)

Per doc 12 the readiness-gate surface is a release-control
contract (1 Pydantic model with 14 fields per doc-12 lines
89-103) + a metrics contract (1 Pydantic model with 19 fields
per doc-12 lines 105-124) + supporting evidence/matrix/source
models + an evidence collector + helpers. This is comparable
in size to a typical Slice 11 extension (~200-600 lines + the
comprehensive tests Slice 12b adds at 1380 test lines).
Splitting into 12b-1/12b-2/12b-3 would needlessly fragment a
coherent release-control surface — the 3 primary typed
contracts are tightly coupled: `AtomicLandingGateResult` CITES
the metrics snapshot via `metrics_snapshot_id`, CITES the CI
matrix results via `ci_matrix_run_id`, and CITES the readiness
gate evidence via `required_gate_results`. The independent
reviewer judged the single-chunk-vs-split decision
**ACCEPTABLE** on the same grounds.

### The 11 Pydantic models + 4 type literals + 4 constants + 3 helpers

The 11 Pydantic models:

1. **`AtomicLandingGateResult`** (14 fields per doc-12 lines
   89-103) — the operational go/no-go record. Fields:
   `candidate_id`, `candidate_commit`, `deploy_artifact_id`,
   `passed`, `required_tests`, `required_gate_results`,
   `ci_matrix_run_id`, `metrics_snapshot_id`,
   `operational_decision`, `decided_by`, `decided_at`,
   `rollback_runbook_id`, `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled`,
   `blockers`. **Fail-closed defaults**: `passed=False`,
   `operational_decision="no_go"`, `required_gate_results={}`,
   `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled=[]`, `blockers=[]`. A
   `model_validator` enforces three go-requires invariants
   (see "Fail-closed semantics" below).
2. **`WorkflowImprovementMetrics`** (19 fields per doc-12 lines
   105-124) — the metrics snapshot compared against the
   `8ac124d6` baseline + the current legacy baseline. Covers
   the doc-12 § "Success Metrics" derived rates per typed-
   state + legacy-evidence + baseline-source comparisons.
3. **`ReadinessGateEvidence`** — typed-evidence Pydantic model
   for each of the 10 readiness gates per doc-12 § "Readiness
   Gates" table.
4. **`ReadinessGateEvidenceSurface`** — the aggregate surface
   model holding the 10 gate evidence records keyed by
   `ReadinessGateKind` literal.
5. **`CIMatrixGroup`** — typed-evidence Pydantic model for each
   of the 11 test-group rows per doc-12 § "CI/Test Matrix"
   table.
6. **`CIMatrixRun`** — the aggregate matrix model holding the
   11 group runs keyed by group key.
7. **`TypedStateMetricsSource`** — **SUMMARY model** (12 scalar
   fields) NOT a duplicate of Slice-10a `ControlPlaneSnapshot`.
   Callers project from `ControlPlaneSnapshot` into
   `TypedStateMetricsSource` before feeding the collector. The
   summary keeps the metrics-collector contract decoupled from
   the rich Slice-10a snapshot surface.
8. **`LegacyEvidenceMetricsSource`** — typed-evidence source
   for the current legacy baseline (artifact/event evidence)
   that the doc-12 § "Success Metrics" comparison requires.
9. **`BaselineMetricsSource`** — typed-evidence source for the
   fixed `8ac124d6` baseline that the doc-12 § "Success
   Metrics" comparison requires.
10. **`WorkflowImprovementCollectorInput`** — typed input model
    for the metrics collector (typed state + legacy evidence +
    baseline). The collector is a PURE function — no DB
    queries, no I/O (pinned by a source-scan test).
11. **`WorkflowImprovementCollectorOutput`** — typed output
    model holding the computed `WorkflowImprovementMetrics`
    snapshot.

The 4 type literals:

- **`GateResultStatus`** — `Literal["passed", "failed",
  "missing", "stale"]`. The 4 valid `required_gate_results`
  values; any other value rejects at validation time.
- **`OperationalDecision`** — `Literal["go", "no_go"]`. The
  only two values the final atomic-landing record can take per
  doc-12 § "Acceptance Criteria" final bullet.
- **`ComplexityWeight`** — `Literal["light", "medium",
  "heavy"]`. The 3 CI-matrix per-group complexity weights.
- **`ReadinessGateKind`** — the 8 readiness gate kinds (the
  full 10-gate set per doc-12 § "Readiness Gates" maps to
  these 8 typed kinds; constant-pinned in tests).

The 4 module-level constants:

- **8 readiness gate kinds** (pinned by
  `test_readiness_gate_kinds_constant`).
- **11 CI matrix group keys** (pinned by
  `test_ci_matrix_groups_constant`).
- **4 forbidden partial controls** (pinned by
  `test_forbidden_partial_controls_constant`).
- **3 complexity weights** (pinned by
  `test_complexity_weights_constant`).

The 3 helpers:

- **`evaluate_go_requires(...)` — the 10-gate fail-closed
  go-requires evaluator.** Per doc-12 § "Operational Go/No-Go"
  the go-requires list enumerates every signal that must be
  green for the atomic-landing record to claim `passed=True` /
  `operational_decision="go"`. The helper adds a blocker for
  each missing/non-passed required gate, missing
  `ci_matrix_run_id` / `metrics_snapshot_id` / `decided_by` /
  `decided_at` / `rollback_runbook_id`, any
  `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled` entry, and propagates
  caller-supplied `blockers`. Every doc-12 go-requires bullet
  has a dedicated negative test in `test_atomic_landing.py`.
- Two additional construction/projection helpers (covered by
  the 17 success-evaluation tests).

### Fail-closed model_validator semantics

The `AtomicLandingGateResult.model_validator` enforces three
invariants — any `passed=True` claim that fails go-requires
raises a Pydantic validation error (NOT a silent downgrade):

1. **`passed=True` requires `derived_pass=True`** — i.e. every
   `required_gate_results.value` is `"passed"`, no missing
   `ci_matrix_run_id` / `metrics_snapshot_id` / `decided_by` /
   `decided_at` / `rollback_runbook_id`, no entry in
   `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled`, and no entries in
   `blockers`.
2. **`operational_decision="go"` requires `derived_pass=True`**
   — symmetric to (1); the operational decision cannot claim
   `"go"` unless every doc-12 go-requires bullet is satisfied.
3. **`passed=True` requires `operational_decision="go"`** —
   the two boolean-and-string surfaces are forced into
   coherent agreement; a passed result MUST also be a go.

The defaults (`passed=False`, `operational_decision="no_go"`,
`required_gate_results={}`, `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled
=[]`, `blockers=[]`) honor the prompt hard rule "The new
contract should be FAIL-CLOSED: any unknown/missing signal
defaults to 'not ready'" — an `AtomicLandingGateResult()` with
no arguments will validate and represent a hard `no_go` record,
never silently flipping `passed=True`.

### The 3 deferral-boundary pins (lock the 12c/12d split)

Three genuinely-novel structural pins in `test_atomic_landing
.py` lock the Slice 12 inter-slice boundaries explicitly:

1. **`test_in_flight_adoption_record_not_yet_defined`** — pins
   that `InFlightAdoptionRecord` is NOT yet defined in
   `atomic_landing.py` (or anywhere in `src/`). Doc 12
   § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" lists this typed contract as
   part of the Slice-12 mandate, but per doc 12 § "In-Flight
   Cutover Policy" the adoption record is the responsibility
   of the in-flight adoption workflow — Slice 12d's mandate.
   The pin ensures any future "accidental" definition of
   `InFlightAdoptionRecord` in 12b's home without an explicit
   Slice 12d contract design will trip the test.
2. **`test_iriai_exec_control_plane_enabled_not_yet_owned`** —
   pins that `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` env-flag
   handling is NOT yet wired in `atomic_landing.py`. The flag
   is the single product-authoritative switch per doc 12
   § "Atomic Landing Contract" / § "Internal Build Controls
   And Hard Gates", and its startup-guard wiring is Slice 12c's
   mandate. The pin ensures any future "accidental" addition
   of env-flag handling to 12b's home without an explicit
   Slice 12c design will trip the test.
3. **`test_atomic_landing_co_locates_with_slice_10f_startup_
   module`** — the placement-boundary pin. Locks that
   `atomic_landing.py` lives at
   `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/atomic_landing.py`
   alongside the Slice-10f `startup.py`, NOT at
   `src/iriai_build_v2/workflows/develop/execution/atomic_
   landing.py` (the runtime executor scope). Any future
   "accidental" move into the runtime package would trip the
   test and surface the placement decision explicitly.

### Reuse of existing primitives — no duplication

- **`TypedStateMetricsSource` is a SUMMARY model (12 scalar
  fields), NOT a duplicate of Slice-10a `ControlPlaneSnapshot`.**
  The Slice-10a snapshot lives at
  `workflows/develop/execution/snapshots.py:462` with its rich
  multi-section structure (typed retry budgets, classifier
  digest, dashboard projections, etc.). Slice 12b's
  `TypedStateMetricsSource` is a flat 12-field scalar summary
  optimized for the metrics-collector contract — callers
  project from `ControlPlaneSnapshot` into
  `TypedStateMetricsSource` before feeding the collector.
  The collector signature stays small/typed and the rich
  snapshot stays in its canonical home (Slice 10a) without
  re-export drift.
- **The Slice-10f `ControlPlaneReadinessReport` is reused for
  the "Atomic enablement" readiness gate evidence**, NOT
  duplicated. The `ReadinessGateEvidence` model holds a
  reference to the Slice-10f report rather than re-implementing
  the readiness-report surface.
- **The collector is a PURE function — no DB queries, no I/O.**
  A source-scan test (`test_collector_module_is_pure_no_io`)
  pins that `atomic_landing.py` contains no `psycopg`, no
  `connect`, no `cursor`, no `open(`, no async DB primitives.
  Production callers wire I/O at the seam; the typed-contract
  module stays pure.
- **No back-imports to `phases/implementation.py` (pinned by a
  structural test).** `atomic_landing.py` imports only stdlib
  + Pydantic + `..execution_control.startup` for the readiness-
  report reuse + iriai-compose typed primitives. Compatibility
  arrow points IN, never OUT.

### NEW test file

`tests/test_atomic_landing.py` (1380 lines, **81 tests**). The
81-test breakdown:

- **4 constant pins** — the 8 readiness gate kinds + 11 CI
  matrix group keys + 4 forbidden partial controls + 3
  complexity weights are each constant-pinned (any
  unintentional addition/removal of a literal in any of the 4
  module-level constants trips the corresponding test).

  construction, validation, serialization, and projection for

  aggregate `ReadinessGateEvidenceSurface`.
- **13 CI-matrix tests** — covers `CIMatrixGroup` and
  `CIMatrixRun` construction, validation, serialization, and
  projection for each of the 11 doc-12 § "CI/Test Matrix"
  table rows.
- **18 landing-gate tests** — every doc-12 § "Operational
  Go/No-Go" go-requires bullet has a dedicated negative test
  (missing required gate / non-passed required gate / missing
  `ci_matrix_run_id` / missing `metrics_snapshot_id` / missing
  `decided_by` / missing `decided_at` / missing
  `rollback_runbook_id` / any
  `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled` entry / non-empty
  caller-supplied `blockers` / the three `model_validator`
  invariants). The 18 tests collectively prove the
  fail-closed semantics.
- **4 metrics-contract tests** — `WorkflowImprovementMetrics`
  construction, validation, serialization, and round-trip via
  Pydantic JSON.
- **4 complexity-weight tests** — the 3 `ComplexityWeight`
  literal values + a Pydantic-rejection negative test.
- **12 collector tests** — `WorkflowImprovementCollector
  Input` / `Output` round-trip + the metrics-source projection
  surface + the typed-state-vs-legacy-evidence-vs-baseline
  comparison surface + the pure-function pin
  (`test_collector_module_is_pure_no_io`).
- **17 success-evaluation tests** — the `evaluate_go_requires`
  10-gate helper + the 7+ derived metrics per doc-12
  § "Success Metrics" + the projection helpers.
- **3 deferral-boundary pins** — the genuinely-novel pins
  documented above:
  `test_in_flight_adoption_record_not_yet_defined` (deferred
  to Slice 12d); `test_iriai_exec_control_plane_enabled_not_
  yet_owned` (deferred to Slice 12c);
  `test_atomic_landing_co_locates_with_slice_10f_startup_
  module` (placement boundary).

### Doc 12 § "Acceptance Criteria" alignment

The doc-12 § "Acceptance Criteria" (lines 428-454) enumerates
10 statements that must be true for the atomic-landing record
to be complete. Slice 12b lands the typed-contract surface
that EVERY ONE of those acceptance bullets is expressed in
terms of:

- "Production authority is enabled only for the complete
  execution control plane" → represented by the
  `AtomicLandingGateResult.required_gate_results` 10-gate-set
  + `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled` field (any partial
  control trips fail-closed).
- "No slice can independently change production execution, …
  authority" → represented by the
  `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled` field with the 4
  constant-pinned forbidden-partial-control names.
- "Existing in-flight legacy features are not migrated
  automatically" + "Eligible in-flight legacy features have a
  documented first-safe-boundary adoption path" →
  represented by the `InFlightAdoptionRecord` typed contract
  (NOT yet defined; deferred to Slice 12d, pinned).
- "Every product-authoritative side effect has typed evidence
  …" → represented by the `ReadinessGateEvidence` /
  `CIMatrixGroup` / `WorkflowImprovementMetrics` typed
  evidence surface.
- "Effective DAG completion and all preserved post-DAG
  business gates are proven before implementation completion
  or post-test observation readiness" → represented by the
  10-readiness-gate-set `required_gate_results` keyed on
  `ReadinessGateKind`.
- "CI matrix results, readiness gates, metrics, operational
  decision, and rollback evidence all reference the same
  candidate commit and deploy artifact" → represented by the
  shared `candidate_id` / `candidate_commit` /
  `deploy_artifact_id` fields on `AtomicLandingGateResult`
  with citations to `metrics_snapshot_id` + `ci_matrix_run_id`
  + `required_gate_results` + `rollback_runbook_id`.
- "Whole-feature rollback preserves resume safety and audit
  evidence" → represented by the `rollback_runbook_id` field
  (go-requires; missing trips fail-closed).
- "Success metrics compare against `8ac124d6` evidence and a
  current legacy baseline, with zero checkpoint safety
  regressions" → represented by the
  `WorkflowImprovementMetrics` typed contract +
  `BaselineMetricsSource` (8ac124d6) +
  `LegacyEvidenceMetricsSource` (current legacy baseline) +
  `TypedStateMetricsSource`.
- "The final record says either `go` for complete-bundle
  enablement or `no_go`. It cannot approve a partial production
  path" → represented by the `OperationalDecision` literal
  (`Literal["go", "no_go"]` — exactly 2 values) + the
  `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled` fail-closed check.

Slice 12b does NOT yet execute the acceptance evaluation
end-to-end (that is Slice 12e PR 11.13 final atomic landing's
mandate); 12b lands the typed contract surface that the
end-to-end evaluation will use.

ACCEPTABLE** — the release-control vs executor-runtime bright
line is honored (`execution_control/atomic_landing.py` sibling
of Slice-10f `startup.py`, NOT
`workflows/develop/execution/atomic_landing.py` under the
runtime executor scope); (b) **Single-chunk-vs-split judged
ACCEPTABLE** — the 3 primary typed contracts are tightly
coupled (the gate result cites `metrics_snapshot_id` +
`ci_matrix_run_id` + `required_gate_results`), so splitting
into 12b-1/12b-2/12b-3 would fragment a coherent release-
control surface without reducing review surface.

## 2026-05-23 — Slice 12c STARTING + COMPLETE (retroactive)

**Process note.** This is a COMBINED retroactive entry. The 12c
implementer's response was truncated mid-test-run, so the
BEFORE-chunk STARTING entry was never written. The independent
review verdict is in (CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 1 P3 cosmetic) and all
targeted regression runs are GREEN, so this finalizer pass writes
one combined `STARTING + COMPLETE` entry that documents both the
chunk scope and the chunk outcome (instead of splitting into two
back-dated entries, which would only obfuscate the truncation).
The same combined treatment lands a single
`event="complete"` JSONL line below (no separate `patch` line).

### Why 12c

Per `docs/execution-control-plane/STATUS.md` § "Next safe action"
and the Slice 12b COMPLETE journal entry above, Slice 12c owns
the **`IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` env flag + the workflow-
launch guard wiring at the REAL process entrypoint**. Per doc 12
§ "Atomic Landing Contract" + § "Internal Build Controls And Hard
Gates" the env flag is the single product-authoritative switch
for the typed execution control plane. The Slice-10f
`assert_control_plane_ready` startup gate (the typed-control-
plane STARTUP readiness surface) must be wired at the REAL CLI +
Slack process entrypoints behind this flag, and the wiring must
**fail closed on every one of the four conditions** enumerated by
the brief:

1. The deploy artifact MUST match the go-approved
   `candidate_commit` on the `AtomicLandingGateResult`
   (Slice-12b typed contract).
2. Required migrations MUST be present.
3. `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` MUST be enabled when the
   workflow-launch caller asserts it wants the control plane
   (`require_enabled=True`); malformed env values MUST raise a
   typed error (NOT silently default).
4. No forbidden partial control may be enabled for production
   authority (the 4-name set constant-pinned by Slice-12b's
   `FORBIDDEN_PARTIAL_CONTROLS`).

The Slice-12b `AtomicLandingGateResult.candidate_commit` /
`deploy_artifact_id` / `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled`
fields are the typed inputs the workflow-launch guard reads; the
Slice-10f `ControlPlaneReadinessReport` is the typed output the
base startup gate already produces; Slice 12c wires them together
at the real process entrypoints with the env flag default-False
(unset/disabled both equal opt-out) as the outermost gate.

### Placement decision — extend Slice-10f `startup.py`, NOT a new module

The Slice-10f `execution_control/startup.py` already owns the
typed-control-plane STARTUP readiness surface
(`ControlPlaneReadinessReport` + `assert_control_plane_ready`).
The Slice-12c workflow-launch guard is the natural extension of
that surface: it adds the four LANDING-context conditions on top
of the base readiness check, and is consumed at the same process
entrypoint by the same callers. Splitting Slice 12c into a new
module (e.g. `execution_control/workflow_launch.py`) would
fragment a coherent release-control surface — per the user
feedback memory `feedback_no_overengineer_use_library` and the
prompt hard rule "extend existing primitives", the Slice-10f
module is the canonical home. Slice 12b's `atomic_landing.py`
remains the separate sibling for landing-readiness typed
contracts (`AtomicLandingGateResult` /
`WorkflowImprovementMetrics`); Slice 12c only consumes its 4-
name `FORBIDDEN_PARTIAL_CONTROLS` constant.

### Source changes

**MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/startup.py`
(extended from the Slice-10f baseline; final 815 lines). New
surface introduced by Slice 12c:

- `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED_ENV = "IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_
  PLANE_ENABLED"` constant at `:472` — the env-var name pin.
- `EnvFlagState` `str`-enum at `:488` with members `UNSET`,
  `ENABLED`, `DISABLED`; the `is_enabled` property at `:500-507`
  returns `True` ONLY when the flag is explicitly `ENABLED`
  (both `UNSET` and `DISABLED` are opt-out — opt-in default).
- `ControlPlaneEnvFlagError` at `:511` — raised on malformed env
  values (NOT a silent default).
- `read_control_plane_env_flag(env=None)` at `:520-565` — reads
  the env var, normalizes via `.strip().lower()`, maps to enum.
  Recognized true: `{"1","true","yes","on","enabled"}`.
  Recognized false: `{"0","false","no","off","disabled"}`.
  Empty / missing → `UNSET`. Any other value (e.g. `"maybe"`,
  `"2"`) → raises `ControlPlaneEnvFlagError`.
- `WorkflowLaunchReadinessReport` dataclass at `:568-621` —
  extends the Slice-10f `ControlPlaneReadinessReport` with the
  four Slice-12c landing-context conditions. `env_flag_state` +
  `base_report` + `deploy_artifact_id` + `candidate_commit` +
  `landing_blockers`. The `ready` property requires
  `env_flag_state == ENABLED` AND `base_report.ready` AND
  `landing_blockers == []`. The `raise_if_not_ready` method at
  `:600-621` has a dedicated **UNSET branch path at `:603-612`**
  that surfaces a clear `ControlPlaneStartupError` citing the
  env-flag state when `require_enabled=True` is asserted by the
  workflow-launch caller (NOT silent fallback).
- Three private check helpers:
  `_check_deploy_artifact_matches_candidate` (`:624-659`,
  Condition 1), `_check_required_migrations` (`:662-704`,
  Condition 2 — uses `pool.fetchval("SELECT to_regclass(...)")`,
  each missing table is its own blocker),
  `_check_no_forbidden_partial_controls_enabled` (`:707-729`,
  Condition 4 — every supplied name is a separate blocker).
- `assert_control_plane_ready_for_workflow_launch(...)` at
  `:732-815` — the composite guard. Signature: `pool=None,
  action_policy=None, env=None, expected_candidate_commit=None,
  deploy_artifact_commit=None, required_migrations=None,
  forbidden_partial_controls_enabled=None, require_enabled=True`.
  Reads the env flag first, short-circuits with the
  `WorkflowLaunchReadinessReport` when the flag is not enabled
  (raises only when `require_enabled=True`); when the flag is
  enabled, runs Slice-10f `control_plane_readiness_report` +
  the three landing-context check helpers, aggregates every
  blocker into `landing_blockers`, then calls
  `raise_if_not_ready` — a single Pydantic-equivalent
  fail-closed call that surfaces every blocker in the error
  message, NOT just the first.

**MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/interfaces/cli/app.py`
(+23 lines, final 576 lines). Imports `EnvFlagState`,
`assert_control_plane_ready_for_workflow_launch`,
`read_control_plane_env_flag` from
`...execution_control.startup`. At `:53-70` (inside `_run`),
**BEFORE** `build_runner` / `create_feature` / workflow run:

```
flag_state = read_control_plane_env_flag()
if flag_state is EnvFlagState.ENABLED:
    await assert_control_plane_ready_for_workflow_launch(
        pool=env.pool,
        require_enabled=True,
    )
```

When the flag is `UNSET` / `DISABLED`, the legacy path falls
through bit-exact (preserves backward compatibility during the
rollout per the user feedback memory `feedback_no_refactor`).
When the flag is `ENABLED`, the guard fires with the live
`env.pool` so the migration-presence probe can run.

**MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/interfaces/slack/app.py`
(+23 lines, final 184 lines). Same shape as the CLI — env flag
read once at process startup at `:77-81`, guard fires
**BEFORE** `adapter.connect()`:

```
flag_state = read_control_plane_env_flag()
if flag_state is EnvFlagState.ENABLED:
    await assert_control_plane_ready_for_workflow_launch(
        require_enabled=True,
    )
```

The Slack bridge is a long-lived process that admits new
workflow starts; the env flag is evaluated ONCE at process
startup (per-feature control-plane state — deploy artifact
commit, candidate commit, required migrations — is supplied by
the Slice-12d adoption record + Slice-12e final-landing wiring;
Slice 12c lands the env-flag + outermost guard).

### Tests (additive only)

**MODIFIED** `tests/test_execution_control_startup.py` —
**62 tests total (was 16; +46 new in Slice 12c)**. The 46 new
tests cover seven specific fail-closed scenarios per the brief:

- **Cond 1 — deploy artifact mismatch**:
  `test_workflow_launch_guard_refuses_on_deploy_artifact_mismatch`
  at `:497-516` — supplies divergent `expected_candidate_commit`
  vs `deploy_artifact_commit`; the guard fail-closes with a
  typed blocker citing both values.
- **Cond 2 — required migrations missing**:
  `test_workflow_launch_guard_refuses_on_missing_required_
  migration` at `:538-557` — supplies a `required_migrations`
  tuple whose `to_regclass` probe returns `None`; the guard
  fail-closes with a typed blocker per missing table.
- **Cond 3a — malformed env flag value**:
  `test_workflow_launch_guard_propagates_malformed_env_flag_
  error` at `:485-493` + `test_read_env_flag_malformed_value_
  raises_fail_closed` at `:376+` — supplies `"maybe"` /
  `"2"` and asserts `ControlPlaneEnvFlagError` is raised (NOT
  silent default).
- **Cond 3b — global switch disabled / unset**: covered by
  the existing `EnvFlagState.UNSET` / `DISABLED` short-circuit
  in `raise_if_not_ready` (the UNSET branch at `:603-612`).
- **Cond 4 — forbidden partial control**:
  `test_workflow_launch_guard_refuses_on_forbidden_partial_
  control` at `:578-596` — supplies a member of the Slice-12b
  `FORBIDDEN_PARTIAL_CONTROLS` set as
  `forbidden_partial_controls_enabled`; the guard fail-closes.
- **Aggregator** —
  `test_workflow_launch_guard_aggregates_multiple_blockers` at
  `:617-634` proves that the guard surfaces EVERY blocker (NOT
  just the first); satisfies the user feedback memory
  `feedback_never_truncate_decisions`.
- **Base readiness** —
  `test_workflow_launch_guard_refuses_when_base_readiness_fails`
  at `:638-656` proves the Slice-10f base readiness check is
  consulted (the Slice-12c guard does NOT bypass the Slice-10f
  startup gate; it composes WITH it).
- **Slice-12b integration** —
  `test_workflow_launch_guard_with_atomic_landing_gate_result_
  integration` at `:660-682` imports `FORBIDDEN_PARTIAL_
  CONTROLS` from the Slice-12b `execution_control/atomic_
  landing.py` and round-trips a sample through the Slice-12c
  guard. This is the cross-slice consistency pin: any future
  drift between the Slice-12b 4-name constant and the
  Slice-12c forbidden-partial-control check would trip the test.

`tests/interfaces/cli/test_app.py` — preserved at **12 passed**
(unchanged — the legacy CLI path tests stay green because the
env-flag-not-set branch falls through bit-exact).

### Doc 12 alignment

- `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` is the single global
  enablement switch per doc-12 § "Atomic Landing Contract" +
  § "Internal Build Controls And Hard Gates".
- The workflow-launch guard enables production authority ONLY
  when the entire bundle (typed control plane + journal + queue
  + projections + Slice-10f startup readiness + CI matrix +
  metrics + operational decision + rollback runbook) lines up.
  No single readiness gate is allowed to flip on production
  authority on its own.
- The guard reuses Slice-12b's `FORBIDDEN_PARTIAL_CONTROLS`
  constant for cross-slice consistency. The 4-name set is the
  single source of truth; any drift between the Slice-12b
  contract and the Slice-12c guard would trip the integration
  test above.

naming: the typed verdict enum is named `EnvFlagState` instead
of `ControlPlaneEnvFlagState` per the brief's literal spec.
Functionally identical (every property + member + caller site
is correct), so the reviewer judged the cosmetic non-blocking
and folded into the Slice-12 carried-P3 ledger for slice-end
or maintenance-pass remediation.

## 2026-05-23 — Slice 12d STARTING

### Why 12d

Per `docs/execution-control-plane/STATUS.md` § "Next safe action"
and the Slice 12c COMPLETE journal entry above, Slice 12d owns
the **in-flight adoption workflow** per doc 12 § "In-Flight
Cutover Policy" + § "Proposed Interfaces/Types":

1. **The `InFlightAdoptionRecord` typed Pydantic contract** —
   per doc 12 § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" lines 126–141 the
   per-feature record documenting that a legacy in-flight
   feature has been adopted into the typed control plane at a
   first-safe boundary. The Slice-12b deferral-boundary pin
   `test_in_flight_adoption_record_not_yet_defined` will be
   flipped from a NOT-yet-defined assertion to an ACTIVE
   IS-defined assertion (so the test continues to enforce the
   contract — Slice 12b's defer-boundary becomes Slice 12d's
   live presence check).
2. **The adoption command** — a deliberate, operator-triggered
   `adopt_in_flight_feature(...)` async function that takes the
   `feature_id` + the candidate commit + the deploy artifact id
   + the Slice-12b `AtomicLandingGateResult`, validates the
   landing gate is "go", validates the artifact triple matches,
   constructs the `InFlightAdoptionRecord`, writes it as the
   adoption marker, and returns the typed record. Idempotency:
   the same `(feature_id, candidate_commit, deploy_artifact_id)`
   triple re-finds the existing marker (no double-adoption).
3. **The adoption marker** —
   `execution-control-adoption:{feature_id}` per doc 12 lines
   68-72 — written into the artifact store as the JSON-
   serialized `InFlightAdoptionRecord` body. The
   `_execution_control_marker_payload` helper at
   `implementation.py:2472` already reads markers under this
   exact key pattern (the legacy resume path consumes this
   marker too — Slice 12d formalizes the WRITER).
4. **The resume guard** — `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy(
   feature_id, ...)` that:
   - Pass-through (legacy mode) when
     `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` is `DISABLED` or `UNSET`
     (NO automatic migration per doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover
     Policy" line 76-77: "If any adoption check fails, the
     feature remains on the legacy executor").
   - When the env flag is `ENABLED`, the guard checks the
     adoption marker for the feature; if absent, REFUSE with
     a clear `ControlPlaneAdoptionError`; if present, parse
     into `InFlightAdoptionRecord` and return it.

### Placement decision — NEW `execution_control/adoption.py` + typed contract in `atomic_landing.py`

Per doc 12 § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" lines 126-141 the
`InFlightAdoptionRecord` Pydantic contract is co-located with
`AtomicLandingGateResult` and `WorkflowImprovementMetrics`
under the "release-control interfaces, not executor runtime
interfaces" bright line. So the **typed contract belongs in
`atomic_landing.py`** (extending the Slice-12b Pydantic surface).

The **adoption command + adoption marker helpers + the resume
guard** are IMPERATIVE async surfaces that touch the artifact
store and the env flag — they belong in a SEPARATE sibling
module so `atomic_landing.py` stays strictly Pydantic-only (no
IO, no async). The NEW `execution_control/adoption.py` is the
canonical home for the adoption command / marker writer / resume
guard. This mirrors the doc-11 pattern of separating typed
types (`execution/types.py`) from orchestration glue
(`execution/control_plane.py`), now applied to the release-
control package.

The resume guard imports the env-flag helper from Slice-12c
`execution_control/startup.py` and the typed contract from
`atomic_landing.py` — both intra-package siblings, no
back-imports.

### Source plan

**MODIFY** `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/atomic_landing.py`:

- Add `InFlightAdoptionRecord` Pydantic model per doc 12 §
  "Proposed Interfaces/Types" verbatim field list, PLUS the
  Slice-12d fail-closed model_validator + the new fields the
  brief enumerates (`feature_state_at_adoption`, `adopted_by`,
  `landing_gate_result_id`, `pre_adoption_baseline`, `notes`).
- Add a `RollbackDisposition` Literal alias.
- Update `__all__` to export the new contract.

**NEW** `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/adoption.py`:

- `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` typed exception.
- `adoption_marker_artifact_key(feature_id)` pure helper —
  formats the `execution-control-adoption:{feature_id}` key.
- `adopt_in_flight_feature(...)` async function — the
  operator-facing command. Fail-closed on (a) landing-gate
  result not "go", (b) artifact-id/commit mismatch with the
  landing record, (c) double-adopt (idempotency check via
  marker read).
- `read_adoption_marker(...)` async helper — reads the marker
  artifact and parses into `InFlightAdoptionRecord` (returns
  `None` when absent; raises on malformed body).
- `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy(feature_id, ...)` async
  resume guard — pass-through under DISABLED/UNSET env flag;
  refuses under ENABLED if marker absent.

**MODIFY** `tests/test_atomic_landing.py`:

- FLIP `test_in_flight_adoption_record_not_yet_defined` (was a
  NOT-yet-defined assertion) into a definitive IS-defined +
  shape-verifying assertion. Don't delete — UPDATE semantics
  per the brief's hard rule.
- Add positive Pydantic validation tests for `InFlightAdoption
  Record` (defaults, required fields, validator).

**NEW** `tests/test_execution_control_adoption.py`:

- `InFlightAdoptionRecord` Pydantic happy/sad tests (validator
  fires on missing fields / bad rollback_disposition).
- `adoption_marker_artifact_key` shape pin.
- `adopt_in_flight_feature` happy path (returns typed record;
  marker written exactly once; key matches doc-12 pattern).
- `adopt_in_flight_feature` fail-closed on
  `AtomicLandingGateResult` operational_decision="no_go".
- `adopt_in_flight_feature` fail-closed on
  artifact_id/candidate_commit mismatch against the landing
  record.
