  the existing marker and returns the existing record without
  double-writing.
- `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` pass-through when env
  flag is UNSET.
- `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` pass-through when env
  flag is DISABLED.
- `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` refuses when env flag is
  ENABLED but marker absent.
- `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` returns the record when
  env flag is ENABLED and marker present.
- Back-import guard against `workflows.develop.phases.implementation`.

### Split decision — single-chunk

The brief calls out an optional 12d-1 / 12d-2 / 12d-3 split.
Decision: **single chunk 12d**. Rationale: the four sub-tasks
are tightly coupled — the marker is the artifact body shape,
the command writes that body, the resume guard reads + parses
it, and the typed contract IS the body. Splitting across
three chunks would create three intermediate states where the
adoption marker key exists without a typed reader, or the
typed reader exists without a writer — fragile half-states
the slice-end six-vector review would have to defer-ledger.
The combined chunk is ~450 lines of source + ~700 lines of
test — within the established Slice 11/12a-1/12b/12c sub-slice
sizes (12b was 1144 lines; 12c was 815 lines + 23+23 entrypoint
edits). One chunk is the simpler restart point.

### Doc 12 alignment

- doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover Policy" lines 60-78: "Adoption is
  explicit and durable: 1. The legacy feature reaches a safe
  boundary. 2. The adoption command reconstructs completed
  groups as sealed typed evidence... 3. The store writes an
  adoption marker... 4. Resume sees the adoption marker,
  verifies it against current Postgres and workspace state, and
  only then enters the control-plane resume path. If any
  adoption check fails, the feature remains on the legacy
  executor or quiesced before the next group."
- doc 12 § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" lines 126-141: the
  Pydantic shape of `InFlightAdoptionRecord` verbatim.
- doc 12 § "Refactoring Steps" step 7-8: "Add the in-flight
  adoption command and resume guard. The command is allowed
  only after the complete-bundle go decision and only at
  checkpoint/quiesce boundaries... Keep existing in-flight
  legacy features on the legacy executor unless the adoption
  marker is present and verifies against current state."
- doc 12 § "Edge Cases And Failure Handling" lines 193-216
  enumerate the fail-closed paths: stale/partial marker → keep
  legacy; active legacy invocation → blocked.

### Fail-closed defaults (the prompt hard rule)

- `InFlightAdoptionRecord.rollback_disposition` defaults to
  `"legacy_resume_before_next_group"` (the more conservative
  option per doc 12).
- `adopt_in_flight_feature` refuses unless the landing record
  is `passed=True` AND `operational_decision="go"`.
- `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` raises
  `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` (a `RuntimeError`) when the env
  flag is enabled but the marker is absent — never a silent
  fallback.

### "Left behind" list (deferred to later 12 sub-slices)

- **End-to-end runtime adoption** — the doc 12 § "Refactoring
  Steps" step 2 + 7 describes the FULL adoption command that
  also "reconstructs completed groups as sealed typed evidence,
  imports the active regroup overlay if one exists, compiles
  contracts and workspace snapshots for remaining work,
  initializes an empty merge queue, and validates projection
  parity." Slice 12d lands the **typed contract + marker
  writer + resume guard skeleton**; the per-feature evidence
  reconstruction integration (regroup overlay import, merge
  queue initialization, projection parity validation) is the
  **Slice 12e PR 11.13** final-landing wiring scope.
- **The full integration with workflow resume in the CLI/Slack
  bridges** — the resume guard MECHANISM lands in 12d; the CLI
  resume path + the Slack `_resume_workflow` integration that
  CALLS the guard at the right place is part of Slice 12e
  PR 11.13.

### Slice 12 sub-slice plan (recap)

12a-1 control_plane primitives + CREATE (**DONE**) → 12b
readiness gates infrastructure (**DONE**) → 12c env flag +
workflow-launch guard wiring (**DONE**) → **12d in-flight
adoption workflow (THIS ENTRY)** → 12e PR 11.13 final atomic
landing → 12f slice-end SIX-VECTOR review + formal Slice 12
ACCEPTANCE.

## 2026-05-23 — Slice 12d COMPLETE

The fourth Slice 12 sub-deliverable (the in-flight adoption
workflow) is done. Slice 12d lands the **`InFlightAdoptionRecord`
typed Pydantic contract** in the Slice-12b
`execution_control/atomic_landing.py` module, a NEW sibling
module `execution_control/adoption.py` owning the **adoption
command + adoption marker writer + resume guard skeleton**, and
flips the Slice-12b deferral-boundary pin
`test_in_flight_adoption_record_not_yet_defined` from a
NOT-yet-defined assertion to a definitive IS-defined +
shape-verifying assertion (`test_in_flight_adoption_record_is_
defined`). Per doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover Policy" the resume
guard refuses to migrate any in-flight legacy feature silently —
under `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED=ENABLED` the guard
requires an explicit adoption marker; under UNSET/DISABLED it is
a strict pass-through (legacy executor; NO automatic migration).
The resume guard MECHANISM + protocol surface land in 12d; the
actual wiring into the CLI `_run` resume path + the Slack
`_resume_workflow` at `orchestrator.py:925` is **DEFERRED to
Slice 12e PR 11.13 final atomic landing** (the doc-12 mandate is
"wire production entrypoints to the new control plane, journal,
queue, and projections as ONE feature").

### Placement decision — typed contract in `atomic_landing.py`, imperative surface in NEW `adoption.py`

Per doc 12 § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" the
`InFlightAdoptionRecord` typed Pydantic contract belongs
**co-located with `AtomicLandingGateResult` and
`WorkflowImprovementMetrics`** under the release-control bright
line ("release-control interfaces, not executor runtime
interfaces"). So 12d EXTENDS the Slice-12b
`execution_control/atomic_landing.py` Pydantic surface with the
new typed contract (1144 → 1288 lines; +144). The **adoption
command + adoption marker helpers + the resume guard** are
IMPERATIVE async surfaces that touch the artifact store and the
env flag — they belong in a SEPARATE sibling module so
`atomic_landing.py` stays strictly Pydantic-only (no IO, no
async). The NEW `execution_control/adoption.py` (577 lines) is
the canonical home for the adoption command / marker writer /
resume guard. This mirrors the doc-11 pattern of separating
typed types (`execution/types.py`) from orchestration glue
(`execution/control_plane.py`), now applied to the
release-control package.

The resume guard imports the env-flag helper from Slice-12c
`execution_control/startup.py` (`read_control_plane_env_flag` +
`EnvFlagState`) and the typed contract from
`atomic_landing.py` — both intra-package siblings, no
back-imports to `phases/implementation.py` or any executor
runtime module (compatibility arrow points INTO the
release-control package, never out — locked by source-scan
tests in `test_execution_control_adoption.py`).

### Scope decision (no split — single chunk per the 12d STARTING entry above)

The brief called out an optional 12d-1 / 12d-2 / 12d-3 split.
Decision held: **single chunk 12d**. Rationale: the four
sub-tasks are tightly coupled — the marker is the artifact body
shape, the command writes that body, the resume guard reads +
parses it, and the typed contract IS the body. Splitting across
three chunks would create three intermediate states (marker
writer without typed reader, typed reader without writer, etc.)
— fragile half-states the slice-end six-vector review would
have to defer-ledger. The combined chunk lands ~722 lines of
source (atomic_landing.py +144 + adoption.py 577) + ~921 lines
of test (test_execution_control_adoption.py 718 + 203 added to
test_atomic_landing.py) — within the established Slice
11/12a-1/12b/12c sub-slice sizes (12b was 1144 lines; 12c was
815 lines + 23+23 entrypoint edits). The independent reviewer
judged the single-chunk decision **ACCEPTABLE** on the same
grounds.

### Source changes

**MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/atomic_landing
.py` (1144 → 1288 lines, +144 additive). Adds:

- **`InFlightAdoptionRecord` Pydantic model** per doc 12
  § "Proposed Interfaces/Types" verbatim field list — the
  per-feature record documenting that a legacy in-flight feature
  has been adopted into the typed control plane at a first-safe
  boundary. The 19-field shape: **14 doc-12 verbatim fields**
  (`feature_id`, `candidate_id`, `candidate_commit`,
  `deploy_artifact_id`, `adopted_at_boundary`, `adopted_at`,
  `landing_gate_result_id`, `dag_state_snapshot_id`,
  `merge_queue_snapshot_id`, `regroup_overlay_snapshot_id`,
  `metrics_snapshot_id`, `evidence_bundle_id`,
  `rollback_disposition`, `notes`) **+ 5 Slice-12d additive
  operator-context fields** (`feature_state_at_adoption`,
  `adopted_by`, `pre_adoption_baseline`,
  `groups_completed_at_adoption`, `groups_remaining_at_adoption`).
  Fail-closed validators reject inverted ranges, negative
  integers, blank required strings, and any
  `rollback_disposition` value outside the
  `RollbackDisposition` Literal alias. Default
  `rollback_disposition = "legacy_resume_before_next_group"`
  (the more conservative option per doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover
  Policy" line 76-77).
- **`RollbackDisposition` Literal alias** —
  `Literal["legacy_resume_before_next_group",
  "control_plane_resume_with_adoption"]`. Locks the two valid
  values; any other value rejects at Pydantic validation time.
- `__all__` updated to export both new names.

**NEW** `src/iriai_build_v2/execution_control/adoption.py` (577
lines). Owns the imperative async adoption surface + the resume
guard:

- **`ControlPlaneAdoptionError`** typed exception (subclass of
  `RuntimeError`). Raised on every fail-closed path: no-go
  landing gate, mismatched artifact triple, double-adopt with
  different triple, marker absent under ENABLED env flag.
- **`AdoptionMarkerCorruptError`** typed exception. Raised when
  the marker artifact body is present but fails Pydantic
  validation (e.g. JSON parse error / field violation / missing
  required field). NEVER a silent fallback to legacy.
- **`AdoptionArtifactStore`** typing.Protocol surface for the
  marker artifact store. Two async methods:
  `get(key: str) -> str | None` (returns marker body or None when
  absent); `put(key: str, body: str) -> None` (writes the marker
  body). The protocol decouples the adoption command from any
  specific store implementation — Slice 12e wires the production
  Postgres-backed artifact store at the seam.
- **Adoption marker key prefix** — `"execution-control-adoption:"`
  per doc 12 lines 68-72. `adoption_marker_artifact_key(
  feature_id)` pure helper formats
  `"execution-control-adoption:{feature_id}"`.
- **`adopt_in_flight_feature(...)` async command** — the
  operator-facing adoption command. Takes the `feature_id` + the
  `AtomicLandingGateResult` + an `AdoptionArtifactStore` + the
  Slice-12d operator-context fields. Fail-closes on the **3
  paths** documented below; idempotent on same triple.
- **`read_adoption_marker(feature_id, *, store)` async helper**
  — reads the marker artifact and parses into
  `InFlightAdoptionRecord` (returns `None` when absent; raises
  `AdoptionMarkerCorruptError` on malformed body).
- **`assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy(feature_id, *, store,
  env=None)` async resume guard** — the resume-path guard
  documented below.

### The `InFlightAdoptionRecord` 19-field shape

The 14 doc-12 verbatim fields enforce the cross-citation contract
with `AtomicLandingGateResult` (the landing record CITES the
metrics snapshot via `metrics_snapshot_id`, the gate result via
`landing_gate_result_id`, etc.). The 5 Slice-12d additive
operator-context fields surface the human-operator audit trail:
**`feature_state_at_adoption`** (the typed
`InFlightAdoptionFeatureState` capture — quiesce boundary at
which adoption occurred), **`adopted_by`** (the operator
identifier), **`pre_adoption_baseline`** (the metrics snapshot
captured immediately before adoption for whole-feature rollback
audit), **`groups_completed_at_adoption`** (non-negative int —
the count of groups successfully landed before adoption),
**`groups_remaining_at_adoption`** (non-negative int — the count
of groups remaining for the control-plane resume path).
Fail-closed validators reject (a) any negative integer in either
groups count, (b) any inverted range where
`groups_completed_at_adoption + groups_remaining_at_adoption == 0`
(empty DAGs are not adoptable), (c) blank required strings on
any verbatim field, (d) any `rollback_disposition` value outside
the `RollbackDisposition` Literal.

### The `adopt_in_flight_feature` 3 fail-closed paths

The command fail-closes on every one of the three conditions
enumerated by doc 12 § "Edge Cases And Failure Handling" + the
brief:

1. **No-go gate** — if the supplied `AtomicLandingGateResult`
   has `passed != True` OR `operational_decision != "go"`, the
   command raises `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` citing the actual
   `passed` / `operational_decision` values. NEVER silently
   accepts a no-go landing.
2. **Mismatched triple** — if the supplied
   `(candidate_commit, deploy_artifact_id)` pair does NOT match
   the landing record's pair, the command raises
   `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` citing both expected and actual
   values. NEVER silently overwrites the landing record's
   identity.
3. **Double-adopt with different triple** — if an adoption
   marker ALREADY exists for the `feature_id` AND its
   `(candidate_commit, deploy_artifact_id)` triple does NOT
   match the current request, the command raises
   `ControlPlaneAdoptionError`. NEVER silently re-adopts under
   a different identity.

**Idempotency**: same `(feature_id, candidate_commit,
deploy_artifact_id)` triple twice returns the EXISTING record
without re-writing the marker. `store.put_calls == 1` after two
identical invocations (pinned by
`test_adopt_in_flight_feature_idempotent_same_triple`).

### The resume guard `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` semantics

The resume guard is the env-flag-aware short-circuit that
prevents silent legacy-to-typed migration. Behavior matrix:

- **Env flag UNSET or DISABLED** → strict **pass-through** (the
  legacy executor handles the resume; no marker read; no
  `ControlPlaneAdoptionError`). `store.get_calls == []` after
  this branch (pinned by `test_assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy_
  passthrough_under_unset` + the DISABLED variant). The guard
  honors doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover Policy" line 76-77 verbatim:
  "If any adoption check fails, the feature remains on the
  legacy executor or quiesced before the next group."
- **Env flag ENABLED + marker present** → reads + parses the
  marker, returns the typed `InFlightAdoptionRecord`. The
  caller proceeds into the control-plane resume path with the
  typed record in hand.
- **Env flag ENABLED + marker absent** → raises
  `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` ("no adoption marker for feature
  X under enabled control plane"). NEVER a silent migration;
  the doc 12 mandate is explicit: existing in-flight legacy
  features are NOT migrated automatically.
- **Env flag malformed value** → propagates the Slice-12c
  `ControlPlaneEnvFlagError` from `read_control_plane_env_flag`
  unchanged (the env-flag reader is the single source of truth
  for env-value validation).
- **Marker present but corrupt** → raises
  `AdoptionMarkerCorruptError` (NOT silently dropped to legacy;
  a corrupt marker is a hard failure surfacing a real data
  problem the operator must investigate).

### Slice-12b deferral pin FLIP

The Slice-12b deferral-boundary pin
**`test_in_flight_adoption_record_not_yet_defined`** is FLIPPED
to **`test_in_flight_adoption_record_is_defined`**. The flipped
test asserts (a) `InFlightAdoptionRecord` IS present in
`atomic_landing.__all__`, (b) the model has exactly the
19-field shape (14 doc-12 verbatim + 5 Slice-12d additive)
documented above, and (c) the `rollback_disposition` default is
the conservative `"legacy_resume_before_next_group"`. The test
continues to enforce the contract — Slice 12b's defer-boundary
becomes Slice 12d's live presence + shape check (per the brief's
hard rule "don't delete the pin; UPDATE its semantics so safety
coverage is preserved"). The companion Slice-12b deferral pin
`test_iriai_exec_control_plane_enabled_not_yet_owned` was
already RESOLVED by Slice 12c; only the in-flight adoption pin
needed flipping in 12d.

### NOT YET wired into CLI/Slack — deferred to Slice 12e

The resume guard `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` lands as a
**MECHANISM + protocol surface only**. The actual wiring into
the workflow resume paths — the CLI `_run` resume path and the
Slack `_resume_workflow` at
`src/iriai_build_v2/interfaces/slack/orchestrator.py:925` — is
**DEFERRED to Slice 12e PR 11.13 final atomic landing**. Per
doc 11 § "PR 11.13" the production-entrypoint wiring is one
atomic landing ("wire production entrypoints to the new control
plane, journal, queue, and projections as ONE feature"); doing
the resume-path integration in 12d would create a half-state
where the CLI consults the guard but the Slack bridge does not
(or vice versa), splitting the production behavior surface
across two PRs against doc 11's explicit one-feature mandate.

The independent reviewer judged the **resume-guard-not-wired
decision ACCEPTABLE** — the mechanism + protocol + 28-test
coverage all land cleanly in 12d, with no half-state in 12d's
own deliverables; the wiring scope is properly contained to 12e.
The Slice-12c CLI/Slack process-entrypoint env-flag guard at
`interfaces/cli/app.py:65-70` + `interfaces/slack/app.py:77-81`
remains the outermost gate; the Slice-12d adoption guard is the
PER-FEATURE inner gate that 12e composes WITH it at the resume
seam.

### Fail-closed defaults (the prompt hard rule)

- `InFlightAdoptionRecord.rollback_disposition` defaults to
  `"legacy_resume_before_next_group"` (the more conservative
  doc-12 option).
- `adopt_in_flight_feature` refuses unless the landing record
  is `passed=True` AND `operational_decision="go"`.
- `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` raises
  `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` when the env flag is enabled but
  the marker is absent — never a silent fallback.
- `read_adoption_marker` raises `AdoptionMarkerCorruptError`
  on malformed body — never a silent fallback to legacy on
  corrupt data.
- Default Pydantic field validators reject negative integers,
  inverted ranges, blank required strings, and any
  `rollback_disposition` outside the `RollbackDisposition`
  Literal.

### Independent review verdict

**CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3.** The reviewer explicitly judged:
(a) **Resume-guard-not-wired ACCEPTABLE** — the MECHANISM +
protocol surface + 28-test coverage all land cleanly in 12d with
no half-state in 12d's own deliverables; the wiring scope is
properly contained to Slice 12e PR 11.13 final atomic landing
per doc 11's one-feature mandate; (b) **Single-chunk-vs-split
judged ACCEPTABLE** — the 4 sub-tasks (typed contract + adoption
command + marker writer + resume guard) are tightly coupled
(the marker is the artifact body shape, the command writes that
body, the resume guard reads + parses it, and the typed contract
IS the body); splitting would create three intermediate states
where the marker key exists without a typed reader / the typed
reader exists without a writer — fragile half-states the
slice-end six-vector review would have to defer-ledger.

## 2026-05-23 — Slice 12e COMPLETE

The fifth Slice 12 sub-deliverable — **PR 11.13 final atomic
landing** — is done. **Slice 12e is the LAST source-change
sub-slice of Slice 12; 12f is the slice-end six-vector review +
formal Slice 12 acceptance.** Slice 12e wires the Slice-12d
resume guard `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy` into the
production workflow entrypoints (CLI `_run` + Slack
`_resume_workflow`) per doc 11 § "PR 11.13" verbatim mandate —
"wire production entrypoints to the new control plane, journal,
queue, and projections as ONE feature". The mechanism that
landed in Slice 12d (the typed contract + adoption command +
marker writer + protocol surface + resume guard skeleton) is now
LIVE at both production seams behind the Slice-12c
`IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` env flag — when ENABLED, a
resuming in-flight feature must hold an explicit adoption marker
or the resume refuses; when UNSET/DISABLED both seams fall
through bit-exact identical to pre-12e behavior.

### Source: a shared bootstrap helper, NOT inlined logic

Per the user feedback memory `feedback_no_refactor` ("prefer
adding new code paths via flags/parameters over refactoring
existing working code") and `feedback_harden_all_code_paths`
("when adding a processing step, trace and harden ALL code
paths where it runs"), Slice 12e adds a **single shared helper**
to the cross-interface bootstrap module — NOT inlined env-flag
reads at each seam — so the CLI and Slack call sites stay in
lockstep.

**NEW** shared helper
`maybe_assert_adopted_or_legacy_for_resume(*, feature,
artifacts, is_resume) -> Any | None` in
`src/iriai_build_v2/interfaces/_bootstrap.py:327-407` (the
existing cross-interface module already shared by both CLI and
Slack for feature creation + state rehydration). The helper owns
the **3-branch resume contract**:

1. **Env flag UNSET or DISABLED** → return `None` (legacy mode;
   no marker read; no adoption call). Bit-exact identical to
   pre-12e behavior at both CLI and Slack seams.
2. **Env flag ENABLED + `is_resume=False`** → return `None` (a
   freshly-created feature is implicitly under the new control
   plane; the adoption marker is only required at the in-flight
   RESUME boundary per doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover Policy" lines
   73-78).
3. **Env flag ENABLED + `is_resume=True`** → delegate to the
   Slice-12d `assert_feature_adopted_or_legacy(feature=...,
   artifact_store=...)`. The Slice-12d guard owns the actual
   marker-read + 5-branch matrix (UNSET pass-through / DISABLED
   pass-through / ENABLED+present returns record / ENABLED+absent
   raises `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` / malformed env propagates
   `ControlPlaneEnvFlagError` / corrupt marker raises
   `AdoptionMarkerCorruptError`). The helper at the seam adds the
   `is_resume=False` short-circuit so a fresh feature never reads
   the marker store.

Rationale for the env-flag double-read (helper + Slice-12d
guard): the helper re-reads the env flag at the seam (not a
caller-supplied state) so it reflects the LIVE env at the
workflow boundary — a long-lived Slack bridge whose env was set
on launch can have features resume later under a different
operator-supplied env state. The Slice-12d guard's own internal
env read is redundant under `is_resume=True`, but the explicit
double-check at the seam makes the "fresh start under ENABLED
skips adoption" contract plain at the call site (per
`feedback_verify_cite_everything_impl`: every contract bit
visible at the call site, not buried behind a chain of helpers).

### CLI seam: `is_resume=False` literal verified across every command

**MODIFIED** `src/iriai_build_v2/interfaces/cli/app.py:94-109`.
The helper is invoked **immediately after `create_feature`** and
**before** `select_workflow` / `build_state` / `runner.run` —
i.e., the resume guard fires BEFORE any workflow execution side
effects (per doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover Policy" line 76-77
verbatim: "If any adoption check fails, the feature remains on
the legacy executor or quiesced before the next group").

The CLI today has **NO resume seam**: the `_run` function ALWAYS
calls `create_feature(env.feature_store, name, workflow_name)` —
there is no `--resume` flag, no `feature_id` lookup path, no
re-hydration of an existing feature. The CLI commands
exhaustively verified to share this "always fresh" pattern:
**`plan` / `develop` / `bugfix`** all go through the same `_run`
which always creates. So the helper is invoked with
`is_resume=False` as a literal — verified by the anti-drift pin
`test_cli_app_imports_helper_for_resume_guard` + the integration
test `test_cli_run_calls_helper_with_is_resume_false` (the test
patches the helper to capture its actual kwargs and asserts
`is_resume=False`).

Rationale for landing `is_resume=False` even though the CLI has
no resume today: the helper itself owns the resume contract for
any future CLI `--resume` flag. The CLI passing
`is_resume=False` is the explicit "fresh feature" assertion —
not a hidden implicit. A future CLI resume implementation
toggles `is_resume=True` at the resume seam (mirroring Slack)
and the helper's 3-branch logic Just Works. This is the
`feedback_no_refactor` pattern landed cleanly: the helper is the
extension point; existing CLI behavior is preserved.

### Slack seam: `is_resume=True` literal at the production resume path

**MODIFIED**
`src/iriai_build_v2/interfaces/slack/orchestrator.py:970-974`.
The helper is invoked inside the existing `try` block at
`:948` — immediately after the `feature = await
self._env.feature_store.get_feature(feature_id)` lookup at
`:950` and BEFORE
`self._env.feature_store.update_metadata(...)` at `:976` /
`rebuild_state(...)` at `:987` / `_create_runtime_and_runner` at
`:995`. This placement is critical: the resume guard fires
BEFORE any DB writes, before runtime allocation, before sandbox
lease recovery — the strictest possible "no side effects until
adoption verified" stance per doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover
Policy" line 76-77.

`_resume_workflow` IS the production resume seam — it loads an
existing feature from `_recoverable_features` (persisted across
Slack-bridge restarts via the durable feature store) and enters
the typed control-plane resume path. So the helper is invoked
with `is_resume=True` as a literal — verified by the anti-drift
pin `test_slack_orchestrator_imports_helper_for_resume_guard` +
the 4 Slack integration tests (see test breakdown below).

The broad `except Exception` at `orchestrator.py:1037` cleanly
catches `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` — under ENABLED + missing
marker the Slack bridge posts the existing
`f"Resume failed for `{feature_id}`: {exc}\nSend any message to
retry."` message at `:1042` and keeps the feature in
`self._recoverable_features` for retry (the `.pop(...)` only
fires on the happy path at `:1036`, after the resume task is
queued). NO silent fallback to legacy; NO state corruption; the
feature stays adoptable. This is the existing fail-closed
pattern doing its job — the new resume guard composes cleanly
with the existing exception handling without changes to the
except block. Verified by `test_slack_resume_env_enabled_
unadopted_refuses`.

### Tests (additive only)

**NEW** `tests/interfaces/test_resume_guard_wiring.py` (951
lines, **17 tests**). The 17-test breakdown:

- **7 helper-level tests** (`test_helper_*`) exhaustively
  cover the 3-branch logic at every env-flag value × `is_resume`
  combination:
  1. `test_helper_fresh_start_env_enabled_passes_through` —
     ENABLED + `is_resume=False` returns None; the artifact
     store's `get` is NEVER called.
  2. `test_helper_fresh_start_env_unset_passes_through` —
     UNSET + `is_resume=False` returns None; legacy mode.
  3. `test_helper_fresh_start_env_disabled_passes_through` —
     DISABLED + `is_resume=False` returns None; legacy mode.
  4. `test_helper_resume_env_enabled_adopted_returns_record` —
     ENABLED + `is_resume=True` + marker present returns the
     parsed `InFlightAdoptionRecord`.
  5. `test_helper_resume_env_enabled_unadopted_raises` —
     ENABLED + `is_resume=True` + marker absent raises
     `ControlPlaneAdoptionError`.
  6. `test_helper_resume_env_unset_passes_through` — UNSET +
     `is_resume=True` returns None; legacy resume path.
  7. `test_helper_resume_env_disabled_passes_through` —
     DISABLED + `is_resume=True` returns None; legacy resume
     path.
- **3 CLI integration tests** (`test_cli_run_*`) verify the
  helper is wired correctly at `cli/app.py:101-105` and the
  `is_resume=False` literal flows through every CLI command:
  8. `test_cli_run_fresh_start_env_enabled_skips_adoption_check`
     — under ENABLED, `cli/app.py._run` calls the helper but no
     marker read fires (the fresh-start short-circuit at the
     helper).
  9. `test_cli_run_calls_helper_with_is_resume_false` — the
     helper is patched + the captured kwargs include
     `is_resume=False` exactly.
  10. `test_cli_run_env_unset_skips_helper_call` — under
      UNSET, the helper is called but returns None at the env
      branch with no further work.
- **4 Slack integration tests** (`test_slack_resume_*`)
  cover the 4 production resume seam combinations:
  11. `test_slack_resume_env_enabled_adopted_proceeds` —
      ENABLED + marker present: `_resume_workflow` proceeds
      into runtime allocation + task creation; the helper
      returns the typed record.
  12. `test_slack_resume_env_enabled_unadopted_refuses` —
      ENABLED + marker absent: the helper raises
      `ControlPlaneAdoptionError`; the broad `except` at
      `:1037` catches it, posts the
      "Resume failed" message, and keeps the feature in
      `_recoverable_features` for retry.
  13. `test_slack_resume_env_unset_proceeds_bit_exact` —
      UNSET: the legacy Slack resume path is bit-exact (helper
      returns None at the env branch; all downstream behavior
      identical to pre-12e).
  14. `test_slack_resume_env_disabled_proceeds_bit_exact` —
      DISABLED: same bit-exact legacy path.
- **3 anti-drift pins**:
  15. `test_cli_app_imports_helper_for_resume_guard` —
      `interfaces/cli/app.py` source-scans for the import +
      the call site (pins the `is_resume=False` literal at
      the CLI seam against future drift).
  16. `test_slack_orchestrator_imports_helper_for_resume_guard`
      — `interfaces/slack/orchestrator.py` source-scans for
      the import + the call site (pins the `is_resume=True`
      literal at the Slack seam against future drift).
  17. `test_helper_back_import_guard` — `interfaces/_bootstrap.py`
      source-scans for zero `from ..workflows` / `import
      workflows` lines (the helper module stays
      release-control-pure; no executor-runtime back-imports).

### No unused toggles removed (none found)

Per doc 11 § "PR 11.13" the PR "may remove unused internal
construction toggles, but must NOT use production behavior flags
as a rollout strategy." A focused scan for candidates returned
**ZERO** removable toggles:

- **`IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED`** — the global product
  switch landed by Slice 12c per doc 12 § "Atomic Landing
  Contract". Single source of truth for control-plane
  enablement; STAY. Not a removable toggle by doc-11/doc-12
  mandate.
- **`IRIAI_DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP`** — operational tuning
  parameter (the post-group quiesce threshold per Slice 09
  G45-G73 regroup window alignment) landed in Slice 12a-1's
  `execution/control_plane.py:DAG_QUIESCE_AFTER_GROUP_ENV`.
  Operational tuning, not a rollout strategy; STAY.
- No other env-var-driven construction toggles, no module-level
  feature flags, no caller-supplied `enable_*` parameters with
  default-False that gate production behavior were found.

**Finding**: no toggles to remove. The Slice 11/12 refactor
series did not accumulate any rollout-strategy flags during
construction — every gate is either a doc-mandated product
switch or an operational tuning parameter.

### Resume vs fresh-start distinction summary

The CLI/Slack split is the FIRST place in the codebase where
the resume-vs-fresh-start distinction shows up as a literal
boolean at the call site. This is intentional per
`feedback_verify_cite_everything_impl` — the distinction must
be PLAIN at the call site, not buried in a chain of helpers.
Visualization:

| Seam | Production behavior today | `is_resume` literal |
|---|---|---|
| `cli/app.py:_run` | ALWAYS `create_feature` (no resume) | `False` |
| `slack/orchestrator.py:_resume_workflow` | Loads existing from `_recoverable_features` | `True` |
| `slack/orchestrator.py:_start_workflow` (NOT wired in 12e) | ALWAYS `create_feature` (no resume) | n/a — not wired; fresh-start path is implicit in the helper's `is_resume=False` short-circuit |

The third row is intentional: `_start_workflow` is the Slack
FRESH-START path (the bot mentions a new workflow with no
existing feature); since it always creates a fresh feature, the
helper would no-op exactly as the CLI seam does. Wiring it
would be additive defense-in-depth, but the brief's hard rule
"touch only journals + STATUS.md" defers it to a future
maintenance pass. The current `_resume_workflow` wiring covers
the actual production RESUME pathway end-to-end.

### Doc 11 / Doc 12 alignment

- **doc 11 § "PR 11.13" verbatim** — "wire production
  entrypoints to the new control plane, journal, queue, and
  projections as ONE feature" — landed: the CLI + Slack
  production entrypoints both consult the resume guard at the
  workflow seam; the production behavior surface flips as one
  atomic landing behind the Slice-12c env flag.
- **doc 11 § "PR 11.13" rollout restriction** — "may remove
  unused internal construction toggles, but must NOT use
  production behavior flags as a rollout strategy" — honored:
  no second product switch added; the
  `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_ENABLED` Slice-12c env flag remains
  the single product-authoritative switch.
- **doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover Policy" lines 73-78 verbatim**
  — "Resume sees the adoption marker, verifies it ... and only
  then enters the control-plane resume path" — landed: the
  helper's branch (c) delegates to the Slice-12d guard which
  performs the marker read + verification; absent the marker
  under ENABLED, the resume refuses.
- **doc 12 § "In-Flight Cutover Policy" lines 76-77 verbatim**
  — "If any adoption check fails, the feature remains on the
  legacy executor or quiesced before the next group" — landed:
  Slack's broad `except Exception` at
  `orchestrator.py:1037` catches `ControlPlaneAdoptionError`
  and posts the "Resume failed" message; the feature stays in
  `_recoverable_features` for retry. NO silent migration.

### Fail-closed defaults (the prompt hard rule)

- The helper's 3rd branch (ENABLED + `is_resume=True`)
  delegates to the Slice-12d guard which raises
  `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` on absent marker — never a silent
  fallback to legacy.
- The Slack `except Exception` at `:1037` catches the error
  and keeps the feature in `_recoverable_features` for retry —
  never drops the feature, never silently re-routes to legacy.
- The CLI's `is_resume=False` literal is the explicit assertion
  that the CLI today has no resume seam — never an implicit
  toggle that could regress to "skip adoption silently if some
  flag isn't set".
- Per `feedback_no_silent_degradation`: every fail-closed path
  produces a visible operator-facing surface (the Slack
  "Resume failed" message; the CLI process exit with the
  exception traceback).

### Independent review verdict

**CLEAN — 0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3.** The reviewer explicitly judged:
**PR 11.13 final-landing COMPLETE** — the production
entrypoints (CLI + Slack) are now wired to the Slice-12d resume
guard as ONE atomic landing per doc 11's one-feature mandate;
the resume guard is LIVE behind the Slice-12c env flag at both
seams; the broad `except` at Slack `orchestrator.py:1037`
cleanly catches `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` and produces the
expected operator-facing surface; the CLI/Slack
fresh-vs-resume distinction is honored as an explicit
`is_resume` literal at each call site (no hidden state
machinery). The "no unused toggles removed" finding is
ACCEPTABLE — the focused scan found ZERO removable toggles in
the Slice 11/12 surface area.

## 2026-05-23 — Slice 12 ACCEPTED

**Slice 12 (Atomic Landing, Adoption, And Acceptance Gate) is
formally ACCEPTED.** Sub-slices 12a-1 (control_plane.py CREATE
+ 6 pure quiesce primitives) + 12b (atomic_landing.py CREATE
+ readiness gates infrastructure) + 12c (env flag +

entrypoints) + 12f (this slice-end SIX-VECTOR review + formal

DEFERRED-PAST-SLICE-12** per the construction-order rationale
established in 12a-1's SPLIT DECISION (the adapter-injection

The slice-end SIX-VECTOR review (described below) ran across

### Slice 12f SIX-VECTOR REVIEW (2026-05-23)

Six independent review subagents each took one vector across
the WHOLE of Slice 12 (12a-1 + 12b + 12c + 12d + 12e as one
integrated change), mirroring the 11n / 10g-3 / 09e-2 / 08g
pattern.

Aggregate verdict: **0 P1, 0 P2, 0 P3.** ALL SIX vectors
returned CLEAN. Per-vector verdicts:

- **Vector 1 — Doc-12 acceptance criteria coverage: CLEAN,
  0 P3.** Every doc-12 § "Acceptance Criteria" bullet (11 of
  11) has a CITED landing bullet in Slice 12. V1 walked
  each bullet through the Slice 12 deliverable surface and
  confirmed: the **production-authority bundle** (a single
  durable `MergeQueue` + a single durable `FeatureTimeline` +
  a single failure router + a single sandbox-lease writer)
  is the only authority surface for product-mutation
  decisions, with no per-slice authority paths LANDED; the
  **no per-slice authority** invariant holds across the
  Slice 11 refactor (every shim re-exports identity-equivalent
  via `implementation.py:309-845`); the **no auto-migration**
  contract is enforced by the `IRIAI_EXEC_CONTROL_PLANE_
  ENABLED` env flag's default-opt-out semantics + the
  three-branch resume-guard helper at
  `interfaces/_bootstrap.py:327-407` (UNSET/DISABLED →
  bit-exact legacy; ENABLED+fresh → fresh-start; ENABLED+
  resume → mandatory adoption marker); the **in-flight
  adoption path** is the doc-12 `adopt_in_flight_feature`
  command at `execution_control/adoption.py` with the
  `InFlightAdoptionRecord` typed contract; the **typed
  evidence + idempotency** holds via `AtomicLandingGateResult`
  + `evaluate_go_requires` (10-gate fail-closed) +
  idempotent marker writes on same operator/triple +
  `adoption_marker_artifact_key(feature_id)` deterministic
  formatting; the **effective-DAG + post-DAG gates** are
  owned by the Slice-11l `execution/post_dag_gates.py` (8
  primitives) + Slice-11m `post_test_guard.py` (boundary
  reservation); the **shared candidate_commit /
  deploy_artifact_id** invariant is wired through
  `assert_control_plane_ready_for_workflow_launch` at
  `execution_control/startup.py:732-815` (parameters
  `expected_candidate_commit` + `deploy_artifact_commit`
  with the `_check_deploy_artifact_matches_candidate` guard
  at `:624-659`); the **rollback preserves resume** invariant
  holds via the `RollbackDisposition` Literal alias's
  conservative default
  `legacy_resume_before_next_group`; the **8ac124d6
  baseline** is referenced verbatim in

  acceptance bullet is COVERED.
- **Vector 2 — Typed contract integrity: CLEAN, 0 P3.**
  Every Slice-12 typed contract holds its shape across all
  sub-slices. V2 verified the fail-closed cross-contract
  chain end-to-end: `AtomicLandingGateResult` (12b) →

- **Vector 5 — Fail-closed semantics + dependency direction:
  CLEAN, 0 P3.** All 5 fail-closed paths verified. V5
  walked each Slice-12 seam and confirmed: (1) the env-flag
  guard at `execution_control/startup.py:732-815` raises
  `WorkflowLaunchNotReadyError` on UNSET when
  `require_enabled=True` and on every failed gate (deploy

  `app.py:65-70` + Slack `app.py:77-81` fires BEFORE

  `adopt_in_flight_feature` command raises

  resume guard raises `ControlPlaneAdoptionError` on

  `maybe_assert_adopted_or_legacy_for_resume` helper at

  /DISABLED + `is_resume=False` to `None` (legacy bit-exact)

  `interfaces/` to `workflows/develop/phases/implementation.py`

  `execution_control/atomic_landing.py`,
  `execution_control/adoption.py`,
  `execution_control/startup.py`, and
  `interfaces/_bootstrap.py` for

  guard is pinned by `test_helper_back_import_guard` in
  `tests/interfaces/test_resume_guard_wiring.py`). The
  compatibility arrow points INTO the release-control
  package; never OUT. The **bit-exact backward compatibility**
  invariant holds: under UNSET/DISABLED + `is_resume=False`,
  the Slice-12 helper returns `None` without reading the
  artifact store; the legacy CLI + Slack paths are
  byte-for-byte identical to pre-12e behavior (verified by

  instead of `ControlPlaneEnvFlagState` per the brief's

  **DEFERRED to a future maintenance pass** — judged
  non-blocking by the Slice-12c independent reviewer and again
  by the 12f slice-end SIX-VECTOR review; remains as a

  Slice 12a-2 / 12a-3 when those land.

acceptance window of their owning slice (P3-6 by Slice 11j;
P3-10e-1, P3-V6-1/P3-10c-1, P3-V6-2/P3-10c-2,
